description:
Description from the publisher:

A little bit slide puzzle, a little bit word find, Word Ravel is a puzzely word game with an ever changing tableau.

In the spirit of the classic word games, Word Ravel is a casual affair of rotating rows and columns of letter in order to spell words.

Strato Checkers is a retro futuristic view of what Checkers could become. Made by the same folk that brought us Strato Tac-Tics. It's made in the same acrylic plastic with oversized board.

Strato Checkers differs from regular checkers in that there are stacking and unstacking rules. Lower pieces in the stack also effect movement of the stacked piece, helping pieces move forward, backwards, or sideways.

It's about building your own pirate ship. You can build cards, or steal them from other players.

Everyone has a hand of cards that they choose to play one card from simultaneously. The cards are different parts of a ship e.g. sail, cannon, hull, etc. Or they can steal other players' cards they have played. The cards are all worth different amounts of gold.

Description from the publisher:

Game of Trolls is a card game centered around trolling and testing the limits of your friendships. Players take turns attempting to attack one another, forcing them to either defend or draw a mountain of cards. The game's unique event cards get the entire group involved. It's an awesome family game and an epic party game that'll ensure you'll have fun at the expense of others.

The longer the round, the more dynamic the game becomes as Gold cards stack on additional rules, keeping everyone constantly on their toes. The first player to discard all their cards becomes the King of Trolls, acquiring game-changing perks for the next round. The loser also gets powers too because let’s face it, it sucks to suck.

boardgame: No Lo Testeamos Ni Un Poco: Drinking
description:
This is an expansion for converting the game 'We didn't playtest this at all' into a drinking game.
Same rules as the original games applies but it also includes new drinking cards.
family: Game: We Didn't Playtest This At All

description:
Another mole is introduced in the expansion. As a member of terrorist organization, he/she can share the information of terrorist organization and has chances to make bombs.

The objectives of both forces remain the same with EOD BASIC: Police Force shall defuse all 3 bombs, while if any one of bombs explodes (assume moles are not suicide bombers), the terrorist force will be the winner.

Mysterium: Secrets & Lies consists of six new characters, six new locations, six new items, and 18 story cards. This latter group of cards can replace the object cards in the game, with each card representing one way in which the ghost died. Now instead of trying to get each investigator to identify an object — a singular item that wouldn't have much nuance to it — the ghost must instead use the dream cards to convey the story of their demise, something far more challenging.

The Frogdog expansion, is filled with a ton of great early campaign content.If you've fought your share of white lions, this is an excellent expansion to mix things up.  The monster has two armor sets, making it a both a great early game and late game target.  Just don't forget your noseplugs!

Includes:

1 x Frogdog miniature (100mm base)
2 x Cess Pond survivor miniatures
2 x Pond Scum narrative armor sculpts
2 x Frogdog armor narrative sculpts
4 x Cesspond board game tiles
120 x game cards
2 x settlement locations
2 x settlement events
16-page rulebook
60 x gear cards
....and more!

boardgame: Kingdom Death: Monster – Nightmare Ram Expansion
description:
The Nightmare Ram, with its claustrophobic garden dungeon, function together to make one utterly insane fight.  Survivors will be impaled, rammed into spikes, crushed in avalanches, and get doused with boiling water (from the Boiling Water bug minion of course!).  The Nightmare Ram has full control of its garden dungeon and will often re-arrange it during the showdown!  Survivors risk being buried alive deep in the inverted mountain.

This expansion adds something totally new to Kingdom Death: Monster...a dungeon!  It includes a dungeon deck, dungeon tiles, minion spawning cards and more.  Get ready to die in a whole new way!

Includes:

1 Nightmare Ram Miniature
4 Narrative Sculpture alpine armor survivors
8 Boiling Water Bug minions
26 page rulebook
1 Weapon Mastery
1 Armor Set card
35 gear cards
30 AI cards
1 basic action card
8 Dungeon cards
5 Disorders
8 Dungeon resources
16 Treasure cards
12 Dungeon tiles
4 Egg Charge cards
8 Fighting Arts
16 Hit Locations cards
8 Hunt events
3 Innovations
13 Minion cards
5 Rare Gear cards
17 Monster resources
1 Settlement location
5 Secret Fighting Arts
3 Terrain cards

boardgame: Kingdom Death: Monster – Oblivion Mosquito Expansion
description:
The Oblivion Mosquito is a great second quarry.  Or, if you're playing aggressively enough, a great higher tier first quarry.  The power of its gear and armor set can well prepare survivors for their first nemesis encounter or their third quarry.  Going after the Oblivion Mosquito early though, can certainly backfire as too many survivors become living incubators, which can lead to an early game-over.
The Oblivion Mosquito expansion introduces a new game mechanic - Encounters!

The Pariah expansion adds a new nemesis to your campaign!  This intelligent and malicious monster slaughters the survivors in two completely different ways.  First on the ground, at the doorstep of the settlement.  And then again, high on the inverted mountain in a daring upside-down battle!  The Pariah's toe-strength is so great that it can effortlessly scale the sheerest of inverted surfaces with ease.  Running through mazes of iron ring pitons that the Pariah has secured over many lantern years as desperate survivors dangle above death.
The Pariah will halt your campaign until defeated!

Contains:

1 x Pariah miniature (50mm)
1 x Serious-Mode Pariah miniature (50mm)
4 x upside-down narrative sculpts survivor miniatures
3 x Innovation cards
3 x Fighting cards
1 x Secret Fighting art card
1 x Inverted Mountain rings token sheet
120 x game cards
1 x Settlement location
3 x Settlement Events
20-page rulebook
20 x gear cards
...and more!

This expansion includes a new campaign, People of the Nest!

Includes:
1 x Gryphon (135mm base, our biggest!)
4 x People of the Nest narrative sculpts
4 x Gryphon narrative sculpts
6 x Innovation cards
5 x Fighting Arts
2 x Secret Fighting Arts
5 x Disorders
120 game cards
1 x Settlement location
3 x Settlement events
30-page rulebook
60 gear cards
...and more!

User summary
World Wide Travel is a light push your luck card game for 2-4 players about travelling the world.

Players take turn in a clockwise order. On a players turn, the player can take actions as follows.

(1) Open a card from deck. If you draw the same location twice, you bust and take only the last card.
(2) Get cards. Decide to stop and take all revealed cards.

If you went once through the deck, the round ends and points are scored. Whoever has the most travel days is the winner of the round and scores one victory point. Play several rounds until any player collects two victory points and wins the game.

The game uses artwork from public domain.

Publisher's summary
10分間で世界旅行が体験できる、簡単バースト系ゲームです。各プレイヤーはカードを好きな枚数めくり、同じカードが出なければ全てのカードを、出れば最後のカードを獲得します。カードの滞在期間を合計し、最も多いプレイヤーが1点を獲得します。2点獲得したプレイヤーが勝利します。

boardgame: Kingdom Death: Monster – Black Knight Expansion
description:
In addition to being integrated into the main game, this expansion includes an alternate short-format campaign (5 year campaign), playing as squires that service the black knight in secret!

Includes:
1 x Black Knight (50mm base)
4 x Squires narrative sculpts
3 x Black knight armor narrative sculpts
6 x Innovation cards
5 x Fighting Arts
2 x Secret Fighting Arts
5 x Disorders
60 x game cards
1 x Settlement location
3 x Settlement events
30-page rulebook
60 x gear cards

boardgame: Kingdom Death: Monster – Red Witches Expansion
description:
This expansion can be integrated into the main game, and also includes an alternative 3-Lantern Year campaign.

The red witches are a nemesis added to the campaign, composed of a three-monster fight!  The content has planned interactions with Fade, the White Speaker and White Secret story event and Story Telling innovation and the Pariah expansion; a target they are hunting down.

Includes:
3 x Red Witch miniatures (50mm base)
5 x Red Cloaks (30mm base)
4 x Black and Red armor narrative sculpts
3 x Innovation cards
3 x Fighting Arts
2 x Secret Fighting Arts
120 x game cards
1 x Settlement location
2 x Settlement events
20-page rulebook
20 x gear cards
...and more!

Description from the publisher:

Defection is a pick-up and deliver, ship customisation, space exploration and dice combat game set in a deep and developed sci-fi universe. 
Unwilling to fight in yet another war, you defect from your factions army in favour of transporting your civilians to safer planets. However, as with nearly all things, in all worlds, conflict and competition follow you. You will soon realise the business of saving lives can be as cutthroat and deadly as the business of taking them.

Pick-Up and Deliver

Your civilians are caught in the crossfire of vengeful violence in which you no longer wish to partake. 
Load civilians, aid packages and other cargo on your ship and set out for safer planets to re-settle and colonise with your faction. 
Dominate other planets with your factions civilians and supply these planets to make them more valuable.

Ship Customisation

Whether you are in the game to save civilians or in the game to take contracts and destroy others, there is the perfect ship for you.
Hire crew, buy ships and upgrade them from the market. Will you stick with the base ship and pay to upgrade and hire new crew? or purchase bigger ships with unique abilities and upgrade those? Either strategy has its advantages. Whatever you decide, there are multiple ways to mod your dice pool and give yourself the best chances of success.

Space Exploration

Travelling through space is a risky business, flipping and resolving hex tiles as you move. Each tile refreshes after use, making space constantly changing with potential danger at every turn.

Dice Combat

Using the stats you have modded with crew, ships and upgrades, gather your dice and shoot. With upgrades and loot cards etc. allowing you to mitigate poor dice rolls and a flee system giving you the chance to avoid the worst, the combat system gives you a good balance of strategy and luck.

Gain Legend

Legend is gained from winning battles, dominating colonised planets and completing contracts/events. Will your faction come out on top before the war on Keleressa meets its inevitable end?
The Magnates: A Game of Power – Betreyal is a promotional card for The Magnates that allows the owner to change position of the conflict cards before playing family cards.

boardgame: Crisis In The Caucasus: Struggle For Nagorno-Karabakh
description:
Description from the publisher:

Crisis in the Caucasus: The Struggle For Nagorno-Karabakh is a game representing a modern day war between Armenia and Azerbaijan. The game takes 90-120 mins to play. This is the third release in the Modern Crisis Series. In this game we are releasing updated system rules that will include a new system for logistic management of your forces. This allows for more realistic gameplay as well as better representing the harassment of enemy supply lines during war.

The game is divided into game phases, such as movement, combat logistics, and the consolidation phase. The conclusion of these phases marks 1 game turn. This is a great intermediate war-game meant to bring new players to the hobby as well as being a challenging play for the veteran wargamer.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Parent and child of Kalgamo is a good friend. 
Always walking together, it is the same when crossing the road. 
If, If you wait too long and the stray, 
ducklings can not cross the road. 
Then, there are a lot of danger on the road ... be careful!

User summary
Roll the snowball roll over the zombies! 
To the peaceful village shut in by the snow, the zombies suddenly come and approach! 
The villagers used the last hope "Snowball" to challenge the zombies ...!

雪玉ゾンビ (Yukidama Zombie) in English means 'Snowball zombies'. This is a strategic action game using tilted boards. As a villager, the player rolls snowballs (balls) from the top of the hill and repels the zombies that are approaching. As a player's action, you can roll snowballs or drive piles into the board. The player who beat the most zombies and became the hero of the village is the winner!

Publisher's summary
雪玉ゾンビは、傾いたボードを使った戦略的アクションゲームです。
プレイヤーは村人として、丘の上から雪玉（ボール）を転がして迫りくるゾンビを撃退します。
プレイヤーのアクションとして、雪玉を転がしたり盤面に杭を打ったりすることができます。一番ゾンビを倒して村の英雄となったプレイヤーが勝者です！

Hiragana Sentence is a mix of boggle and bingo. Each round a new theme will be drawn and hiragana characters drawn bingo style. Players will fill their 5x5 sheet with the characters in any way they want, after all of them have being filled up players try to form a word based on the theme. Players vote for the best word of the round and a new round is played.

Description from the publisher:

It is a communication game to make sentences that fit the theme by connecting hiragana well. She turns over the cards with hiragana written and fills the letters in the favorite squares of the sheet in that order. When everyone can write, last letters are connected by a line and announced.

WORLD'S FIRST BOARD GAME FOR DEVELOPERS AND MANAGERS

You are working for one of the most promising tech startups in Silicon Valley. Everybody expects the release of v2.0 of your product. This could be a huge leap for the whole company, or it might destroy it if the deadline is not met. The CEO has offered $100 000 to the team that contributes most for the new release. This will be either the developers or the PM team claiming the credit.Who will be the hero of the day? And which team will be remembered by history? It’s all up to you and your friends to decide!

Developers vs Managers is an entertaining / educational board game. The game is designed for IT Specialists by IT Specialists. The purpose of the game is to improve the soft skills of everybody involved. Developers vs Managers is ideal for play when you are on a team building event, having fun in the office or just breaking the ice with new people.

How to play:
First - gather 5 to 10 friends or colleagues
Draw your role and keep it secret until the end of the game! Are you a developer or a manager? The others will judge you by your actions, not just by what you are saying!
Pick-cards
Then: You are the one in charge of who is taking part in the release or not! But you also need to win the trust of the rest of the players that you are leading them to the win...
Pick-a-team
Now vote! The twist of the game is executing the sprint. Is it successful thanks to the effort of the development or management team?
Hint: developers trust only other developers. managers know someone needs to "manage the resources"
If the developers execute 3 successful sprints they win! Unless! The managers guess correctly who the CTO is. Then they win!

The gameplay is extremely addicting because the longer you play, the more you fall into it and the more intense the game gets.

Description from the back of the box:

Literati is a new word game that combines obscure words and quotations with your own creative and often hilarious definitions. No genius is required, merely the ability to outwit your opponents. Advance around the board by simply identifying correct definitions and/or tricking your opponents into believing bogus definitions which you have created…"

Literati contains 400 word cards with categories and sample words, including: GENERAL: uxorious – excessively fond of one’s wife; SCIENCE: piblokto – a psychic disturbance seen chiefly among Eskimo women marked by attacks of hysteria and running naked through the snow; FOREIGN: kater (German) – hangover; SLANG: dismal ditty – a song sung by a criminal just before his death at the gallows; and, QUOTE: "...And I don’t like parties of any kind." ~Dick Clark

Game play is similar to Balderdash, and other Fictionary/Dictionary-type games.

Description from the publisher:

"The game where you insult your friends"

Each player is dealt 10 cards and 5 lives. The object of the game is to use your cards to create hilarious insults that score damage points that remove the "lives" from your opponents. The last player left with lives wins.

Each card has two words are phrases on it and an associated point value with each one. Play proceeds clockwise starting with the player left of the dealer.

Each turn the player can either play an insult on anyone at the table OR exchange any amount of cards from their hand for new cards. An insult is 4 or more cards that when read aloud, form a complete sentence that insults another player. Example:

YOUR JACKED, BUCK-TOOTHED GRANDFATHER LIKES TO SPOON WITH A NEUROTIC, TINY CHICKEN!

The player lays down the cards that forms their insult, points to a player they are insulting, and reads the sentence aloud. You imply the punctuation with how you speak the sentence. So say it like you mean it!

You then total the points on the read side of each card in the insult (upper right corner).
If this total is 5 or more, the insulted player loses 1 life.
If the total is 10 or more, the insulted player loses 2 lives
If the total is 15 or more, the insulted player loses 3 lives, AND the insulting player takes one of the lost lives and adds it to their own lives.

There are "Reversal" and "Redirect" cards that the insulted player can play to deflect an insult. The insulting player can play a "BOOM" card at the end of their insult to make it unreversable and unredirectable.

Argle Bargle is a casual game, but it takes more than luck to best your opponents. It requires creativity and strategy.

There are no dirty or offensive words in this version.

boardgame: Arkham Horror: The Card Game – Blood on the Altar: Mythos Pack
description:
Description from the publisher:

Blood on the Altar is the third Mythos Pack in The Dunwich Legacy cycle for Arkham Horror: The Card Game.

In the scenario from Blood on the Altar, your investigations lead you to the village of Dunwich, where you suspect a series of recent disappearances may be related to the events in Arkham. But the more you explore, the more you get the impression the townsfolk aren't exactly pleased to see you. There are secrets in this town that its inhabitants don't wish to share…

With its sixty cards (including a complete playset of each new player card), Blood on the Altar challenges you to delve the secrets of Dunwich. You'll need to travel throughout the town in search of clues, stopping to investigate many of the locations infamously identified in H. P. Lovecraft's classic tale, The Dunwich Horror.
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable
family: Collectible: Living Card Game (Fantasy Flight Games)
Game: Arkham Horror – The Card Game
Game: Arkham Horror TCG – The Dunwich Legacy
Series: Arkham Horror Files (Fantasy Flight Games)

description:
This set contains 600 questions and answers about Dinosaurs on 100 cards from the following categories:

Traits
Pop Culture
Friends, Foes and Families
Discoveries
Habits
Name-it

This set can be used as an expansion along with any Trivial Pursuit base game or played stand-alone with the enclosed rules and customized die.

A wargame based off the anime "Girls und Panzer der Film". This game use same the tank units as the earlier game Panzer Vor!, but uses alternate character units and new game engine because focus to more larger tankery battle,

Each player has 30 tanks and some leaders. In the set-up, players organize their platoons from 1 to 4 tanks.
In the game, players alternate resolve an action, movement, attack, re-organize, card play or pass.

Victory conditions are defined for each scenario, the basic purpose is to destroy opponent tanks.

The game include 2 scenarios for first part and second part of Oarai vs University team. These two scenarios can be play continuously as a campaign game.
And also include 2 scenarios for "Panzer Vor!" based off first part of "Girls und Panzer der Film".
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

boardgame: South Korea (fan expansion for Ticket to Ride)
description:
This map is an unofficial fan made expansion. Symbols & Graphics Copyright © 2004-2015 Days of Wonder, Inc. Produced with permission of Days of Wonder, for non-commercial use only.

This follows the principles of the basic Ticket to Ride game on a special board with special cards and rules. The object of the game is to gain the most points by travelling various routes and outwitting other players. You will need the train pieces and the train cards from either the US or Europe base games (or you can buy them from the Days of Wonder shop.

There is an extra set of Tourist cards for the cities, each with a value to be earned by visiting that city. These cards are dealt/picked up alongwith tickets in the same quantity and with the same 'keep/dispose' rules.

There are also spanners along some tracks which may help or hinder you. The board does not have any grey tracks or parallel tracks - but there are some options in the spanner pack.

Mountain railway excursions and 9 bonuses give you extra points.

There are many new options in the rules: exchange all your cards at the start but keep all the new ones: discard 5 cards and pick up 5 new ones, as your turn: earn double points: and many more. You can also call time when your wagon stock reaches 3, if you want, giving you the advantage of stopping other players completing tickets.

Description from the publisher:

Limpeh Says is a card game for we the citizens of Singapore, containing 550 unique, localised cards revolving around the culture we've built here in our little country.

Guided by the ideology of being 'morally wrong but politically right', it includes topics from food, to National Service, and everything else in between.

The game is as simple as it gets. Each round, one player draws a Red Card. Everyone else answers with their best white card. The funniest combination wins.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
A new weapon promo card for Adrenaline that was included in German magazine Spielbox, issue No. 7/2016.

When used in basic mode, the player chooses 1 target within sight. That target receives a mark.

In alternate fire mode, the player can choose 1 or 2 targets within sight. One target receives 2 damage, the other target receives 1 damage.

Reloading the Laser Magnum costs no ammo. When you play the Laser Magnum, it becomes unloaded, just like any other weapon, even though reloading it during the reload step costs nothing.

A boardgame for two tired parents trying to keep their household running. Fighting against the clock and the little overlord of chaos, will they manage to have any happiness points by nightfall?

Two parents play one day or 17 hours against one little toddler who moves randomly in the house creating mess each turn.Each hour is one turn in which the parents have to spend action points to move around the house to clean, to do the household chores and toe spend time on 'events', some more chores or unexpected chaos or activities with the toddler, leading to more mess but also to happyness.

The mess arrives in a spot due to an event, a chore not managed or the little toddler moving around. If this is not removed before more mess arrives at the location, it becomes harder to clean and in a next level breaks that location so no chores or events can take place.

A clean house, rest and fun activities give happyness points, a too messy house and not managing the chores leads to less happyness. So both parents have to work together to plan the best schedule, when to be where in the house, who does which activity and who runs after the toddler to balance the keeping the house in order and to score happyness points.

boardgame: 100 Swords: The Gardenin' Elm's Dungeon Builder Set
description:
Description from the publisher:'

100 Swords is a sword based dungeon crawling micro deck builder! Load up with as much as you can carry and run head first into the dungeon, exploring rooms and revealing monsters, all while keeping track of hidden treasures and swords!

During play, you must efficiently play the 5 cards in your hand for any combination of movement, strength, or energy. Movement advances you deeper into the dungeon allowing you to peek at or reveal the cards along the way. Monsters you find can only be defeated with strength. New items (and swords!) are acquired by spending cards in your hand for energy. Many items have effects that will further expand your tactical options as your deck grows.

As you defeat monsters and pick up loot, do your best to remember where everything is hidden. Treasures and slain monsters are worth gold, so get collecting! Once the dungeon has been thoroughly ransacked, or the boss is defeated, whoever has the most gold wins!

You can use Dungeon Builder Sets to spice up your dungeon. Each set contains 15 cards (including a new Boss) that can be slapped onto a standalone dungeon to lengthen your game and add new challenges and puzzles to solve! The Gardenin Elm's Dungeon Builder Set features a unique take on card vaporization. It has an interesting variety of new monsters, items, and obstacles.

boardgame: 100 Swords: The Heads of the Hydra Dungeon Builder Set
description:
100 Swords is a sword based dungeon crawling micro deck builder! Load up with as much as you can carry and run head first into the dungeon, exploring rooms and revealing monsters, all while keeping track of hidden treasures and swords!

During play, you must efficiently play the 5 cards in your hand for any combination of movement, strength, or energy. Movement advances you deeper into the dungeon allowing you to peek at or reveal the cards along the way. Monsters you find can only be defeated with strength. New items (and swords!) are acquired by spending cards in your hand for energy. Many items have effects that will further expand your tactical options as your deck grows.

As you defeat monsters and pick up loot, do your best to remember where everything is hidden. Treasures and slain monsters are worth gold, so get collecting! Once the dungeon has been thoroughly ransacked, or the boss is defeated, whoever has the most gold wins!

You can use Dungeon Builder Sets to spice up your dungeon. Each set contains 15 cards (including a new Boss) that can be slapped onto a standalone dungeon to lengthen your game and add new challenges and puzzles to solve! The Heads of the Hydra Dungeon Builder Set features a battle against hydra head monsters. For each head of The Hydra you have defeated, the weaker the boss becomes. It also has an interesting variety of new monsters, items, and obstacles.

"Yokai" is a class of supernatural monsters, spirits, and demons in Japanese folklore. Some are malevolent and others are mischievous, or occasionally a few of them even bring good fortune to those who encountered with them. The name of the game, Hyakki-Yagyo (百鬼夜行), meaning one hundred Yokais' night parade, is based on a popular style of lore where people get together and take turns to tell Yokai (or ghost) stories one by one. When the hundredth story is told, it was believed that a real Yokai is summoned and appears in front of the folks.

In this game, players are Yokai storytellers. But you tell your own Yokai stories by collecting different and beautiful Yokai cards and place them as separate sets based on their suits and numbers. In each round, each player take turns to draw from open Yokai cards into their hands and place a set of cards (for example, four cards in sequence with the same suit, three cards of the same number with different suits, etc.) Each set of cards represents different ghost stories. A game goes until one of the players score 10 points in total by repeating rounds, just like Yokai stories goes on and on all through the night...

boardgame: Epic Card Game: Uprising – Kark's Edict
description:
Captain Marcus' tyrannical grip on the people of Covenant has sparked an uprising. With the city in chaos, Chamberlain Kark has issued an edict declaring martial law. As the Watch make scores of arrests, popular support for the uprising grows even stronger. Will justice prevail?

Epic Card Game: Uprising is an expansion for the Epic Card Game that's packaged in four non-random booster packs (Flames of Scarros, Kark's Edict, Velden's Wrath, and Will of Zannos), with each booster pack containing twelve new game cards and a double-sided token.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

boardgame: Epic Card Game: Uprising – Will of Zannos
description:
With the city of Covenant in the midst of a savage uprising, a dark force is growing in the shadows. Zannos, the Corpse Lord, is using the casualties of the conflict to provide more soldiers for his army of plague zombies. Can the will of Zannos be stopped before it's too late?

boardgame: Epic Card Game: Uprising – Flames of Scarros
description:
For years, the wilds around the city of Covenant have been terrorized by the mighty Draka. Now, there are whispers that the tyrannical dragon may have been defeated. boardgame: Mare Nostrum: Empires – Heroes of the Bible
description:
Description from the publisher:

This new expansion set is fully compatible with Mare Nostrum: Empires. Add new Heroes inspired by the Bible with new powers to explore that affect Military, Culture, and Trade!

The set includes 5 characters:

Daniel – Choose one trade good an opponent must trade.

David – Add +1 to land battle die rolls when at a disadvantage.

Jesus – Temples in your province and adjacent opponent provinces do not double Coin Resources.

John the Baptist – Remove an opposing Legion from yours or an adjacent province.

Joshua – Opposing fortresses do not ignore the first hit.

Description from the designer:

On the surface, Colbridge is a thriving medieval city, ruled over by a council of three wise nobles. But beneath the calm façade the council is locked in a deadly battle for mastery. Your reputation for resourcefulness has seen you hired to help elevate one of the councilors by acts of charity, bribery, or violence. The only problem is that the agent who hired you was recently found stabbed under a hedge, and hadn't yet got around to mentioning which of the three councilors is paying you. Can you use your wits to deduce who your patron is and elevate him or her to rule the city?

The Council of Colbridge is based on a unique combination of imperfect information, deduction and constrained action. Each player knows the patron of at least one other player, but not their own. Each turn players select actions (charity, bribery or violence) and how these balance with the previous actions of the other players determines which councillor advances in power. By watching and analysing the actions of others, players may deduce who their patron is and push them to power. But what if someone was bluffing? The resulting atmosphere of calculation and suspicion is reminiscent of a political thriller.

boardgame: Ridiculous Last-Minute Christmas Shopping Family Brinkmanship
description:
Every Christmas the whole family goes to Grandma's big house to celebrate. But this year Granny went to the Bahamas! Panic-buy your way to a happy holiday and invite relatives who have nowhere else to go - you have to show everyone in the family that you had the best Christmas!

Players take the role of families doing their last-minute Christmas shopping in order to lay on meals for guests on Christmas Day and Boxing Day, and buy and decorate a tree. The board consists of a number of shops; each shop has a separate queue, and each space in the queue offers a different reward - from food items to trees to decorations. On a player's turn they first move all of their limited number of pawns one space down the queues they're in, then choose to either start a new pawn at the top of a queue or retrieve a single pawn from a queue and take the item they've reached. The player must take exactly one of these two actions, meaning they have to carefully plan ahead to make sure they don't run out of pawns before they reach their goals, and don't end up with more than one pawn on a desired space in the same turn.

The task is complicated by the opportunity to bump other players' pawns around in queues or make trick telephone calls that distract your opponents from their shopping, and the juicy bonus points on offer for inviting various relatives to come and have dinner with you... so long as you don't offer anything they don't like!

Ridiculous Last-Minute Christmas Shopping Family Brinkmanship is printable as a single A4 or US-Letter page, intended to be included in a Christmas Card; to play 4 coloured pawns, a pen or pencil and a piece of paper for each player are also required.

description:
Die Jokerbohnen is an expansion for Bohnanza. It was released in the Spiel doch! magazine 2/2016 as an additional add-on, which was only available in magazines in a Thalia bookstore. 
It contains six cards with the "Jokerbohnen". The "Jokerbohnen" can be placed on every other bean card.

Card, Board & Strategy Game.

The objective of the game is to get rid of all weapons on hand in series of attacks.

The first player who gets rid of all his weapons wins the 1st place, the second player - the 2nd place and so on.

The last player left holding cards takes the last place.
 Each weapon has its own power units that determines its ability to attack and defend.

All weapons are ranked by colors - an attack of a weapon can be beaten by a weapon of the same color.

boardgame: Expulsion: Jewish Life in Spain from the Golden Age to 1492
description:
With each roll of the dice, move your piece through spaces outlining the major Jewish events in medieval Spain.

Pull Explusion or Daily Life cards, as directed by the space you land on. These cards might cause you to be reincarnated (Gilgul space) as a different historical person (Nefesh/Soul cards) or put you in danger of excommunication (Herem space).

Along the way, those cards might also earn you tokens for: Torah (learning), Avodah (service), Gemilut Hasadim (showing kindness), and Emunah (faithfulness).

Once you have collected 18 tokens (the same number as the Hebrew word for life), go to the 7th Heaven (Aravot) and pick a judgement (Din) card. Sometimes the King of Demons has tricked you into a lower heaven. But often your deeds are judged as Holy - and you WIN!

description:
A push-your-luck game involving donkeys.  The object of the game is to move your donkeys to the feeding spaces and take the best food.  Every turn each player will anounce how many spaces the would like to move.  Then they will throw the die and if the result is equal or higher than their declaration, they will move that number of spaces.

However, if the result is less, they will stay in the same space, an also they can move backwards if the result is a -1. The neat part of the game is that a donkey lands on a space with another donkey, they pile them, and the player on the bottom must obey the orders of the higher donkey.

Has nothing to do with other games called Huckepack, and is suspiciously similar to Die Heisse Schlacht am Kalten Buffet. No author is credited on the box.

Description from the publisher:

A multiplayer (2-5) card game on power politics in the fictional Latin American country of Virtualia, after the departure from power of its strongman leader Jesus Shaves (pronounced “hay-sus sha-bezz“)... actually a thinly-disguised post-Chavez Venezuela (though when first designed in 2013, it wasn’t post-Chavez yet).

The game is semi-cooperative and semi-competitive, and plays up the constant tension between these urges. As players vie to create the largest and most durable personal power base (scored periodically throughout the game), the card deck delivers more and more crises that players must deal with collectively (and collect small rewards immediately), or the country will collapse. Coups d'etat provide another quick way to score!

The free PnP version consists of 90 cards, 230 counters, and the usual rules and play aids.

Players attempt to achieve victory by moving their combat units across the game map to attack their opponent’s combat units and occupy as many objectives as possible.

description:
User summary
煙血鋼撃 (EnKetuKouGeki) is a military-themed game for two players.

At the beginning of game, players have six units of infantry and six armored vehicles each, from which they form a squad of four infantry and an armored vehicle.

Each turn, they choose an command card from their hand and reveal them simultaneously. Each card has a speed factor and card effects. Resolve them in the order of speed factor, and try to damage opponents' squad.

You win the game if your opponent loses all four infantry, or opponent's vehicle is destroyed.

Publisher's summary
歩兵と機体を編成し、敵部隊の撃退を目指す
ミリタリーテイストな対戦ゲーム。

編成した部隊に命令カードで指示を与え、
様々なスキルを駆使して敵部隊に損害を与えたり、
自部隊の支援を行います。

最目標は、敵部隊の歩兵を殲滅あるいは敵部隊の機体の撃破。

User summary
武当掌門 (ButouShoumon) is a game for three players with a theme of power struggle in a Taoist temple in ancient China.

At the beginning of the game, players are dealt four cards each. One of these cards is the spy, meaning the player holding the spy card has to be in second place to win the game.

During the course of the game, players place their workers and exchange their cards to train themselves (increase their statistics), and gain victory points when you have enough statistics. Note that the spy card can be exchanged so you should keep track of it.

At the end of the game, if the spy player is at the second place, the spy wins. Otherwise the non-spy player with higher victory points wins the game.

Publisher's summary
武当山の道士達が次期掌門を巡って
争うゲームです。
そして、道士の中には朝廷のスパイが潜んでいます。

道士達は日々修練し、時には下山して外界の事件を解決し
名声を高めます。
最終的に名声が最も高い道士が掌門となり勝利しますが、
スパイが2位になると武当山の情報が朝廷につつぬけとなり
スパイの勝利となってしまいます。

User summary
ウサギの政略結婚 (Usagi no Seiryaku Kekkon) in English means 'Rabbits' Political Marriage'. It is a two-player co-operative game. Two rabbit nations, which have been hostile to each other, try to form a marriage between their prince and princess before the hostility leads to a war.

Five event cards are placed face-up on the table, so you know what happens in the next five turns. Events can be positive or negative, and some events even raise hostility level under a certain condition. Each turn, a player play an action card. An action card has two options. One is to gain a positive effect, such as gaining resource, or spending resource to collect victory points; and the other is to avoid a bad event, such as changing event order to avoid a hostile condition. You cannot always take positive effects. How you accept negative events is the key to victory.

If the hostility level is raised to seven, citizens' anger leads to a war and both player lose the game. Before that, players have to collect six victory points (intimacy level) to win the game.

Publisher's summary
仲の悪い2つのウサギの国が
戦争を回避するために、
互いの国の王子と姫君が政略結婚をするゲーム。

民衆の対立度が最大に達する前に、
王子と姫君たるプレーヤーが協力し、
物資を蓄えたり需要を回したりして
勝利点に変換し、政略結婚を成功させることを目指します。

boardgame: Valeria: Card Kingdoms – Expansion Pack #05: Monster Reinforcements
description:
This expansion adds 13 new monsters (One new monster for every existing stack of monsters) to add a new level of difficulty to Valeria: Card Kingdoms!

This expansion was the $100k stretch goal for the 2016 Valeria: Card Kingdoms – Flames & Frost Kickstarter, however the funding goal was not reached.

It next became available as an add-on in the Pledge Manager for that campaign around Dec'16/Jan'17.

boardgame: Valeria: Card Kingdoms – Expansion Pack #04: Peasants & Knights
description:
This new expansion comes with new Peasants and Knights to add variety to every game of Valeria: Card Kingdoms. (6 Peasants & 6 Knights)

Description from the publisher:

The Peasant from the Valeria: Card Kingdoms base game has had just about enough of goblins attacking his farm and the Royal Guard taking food from the mouths of his children. You've promised him riches if he helps you become the next ruler of Valeria. His hoe is his only weapon and a barrel lid is his only armor. His meager carrot harvest still rests in his coat pocket as he marches off to battle.

Within the shadow of Mt. Ostendaar lives a warrior race that has honed the craft of alchemy. Their Knights have joined the battle against the hordes of fiery demons that are spilling out of the once dormant volcano. This eager young Knight wields a sharp spear and spiked chain mace against the foes of Valeria. Potion "grenades" tied to her armor are her secret weapon.

This expansion was included in the Kickstarted expansion, Flames and Frost.

In Haspelknecht: The Ruhr Valley, an expansion for Haspelknecht, coal mining has now become an inherent and lucrative business for farmers in the Ruhr Valley. The tunnels are dug deeper and longer through the use of iron. Water channels are implemented to help drain the pit water. Most of the population earns their living exclusively by mining coal and delivering the black gold to peddlers and the first coal barges cruising the river, which gives its name to the valley and the entire area — the Ruhr.

This expansion features two new challenging modules and 21 new development tiles for you to explore!

boardgame: Villages of Valeria: Deluxe Kickstarter Edition
description:
The deluxe edition comes in a double tall box to accommodate sleeved cards, a plastic insert with separate card & bits sections, foam spacers to keep cards in place, foam cover for bits control, 5 tabbed divider cards, an action selection tile, a tableau tracker, and a box sleeve.

In addition, it comes with the Kickstarter Bonus Content Pack, Guild Halls expansion, and Monuments expansion

description:

     Protective Amulet: given to first player to build four huts. 



     The Spring: Affects druids crossing the great river. 


These promo items are contained in the HABA catalog that was given away for free at Essen 2016. Rules can be found on the publisher's website.

Cuba and El Presidente released together in one box.


Game description from the publisher:

Cuba prior to the revolution: Under turbulent circumstances, the villages of the island strive for independent wealth and influence. Who can buy and sell his products and goods on the domestic market profitably or take in the most on the trading ships? Who can send the right delegate to parliament in order to influence the government legislative process, or erect distilleries, hotels and banks at the right moment to the benefit of his village?

Whoever has accumulated the most victory points in Cuba by the end of the game wins. Players earn victory points by shipping merchandise from the harbor, but also by erecting and using buildings, and by abiding by the law.

El Presidente:

The expansion to be used by 'Cuba'. An additional game board, more and different ship cards, laws, buildings, new character cards to be used with the other characters, and a whole new phenomenon: Cuba - the Arrival of the President!
In their turn, players may take and play a character from the 'El Presidente' board: the worker, dancer, attorney, warden, revolutionary or musician. They all have different effects.
The worker puts your goods into the warehouse; all your goods are safe this turn. The revolutionary gives you one victory point, the musician two money. The player with the dancer card becomes immediately the new start player; the old rule for determining the new start player is no longer in effect.

With the attorney a player may use a building even when his supervisor is not on the row or column of this building, paying one money. The warden may change two adjacent ships in position. There are two mule cards being used when playing with less than five players; these cards cannot be chosen. When all players have chosen a card, the car of the president moves to the position of the remaining card; remember, there are six cards that each turn are shuffled and randomly placed.

Under each card on the board are symbols that come into effect when the car of the president stops there. The first makes all law proposals go into effect; the second makes it possible to move the supervisor anywhere on a player board in the next round. The next symbol also is in effect for the next round: it allows you to pay one money and overbuild a building with another; the difference in resources must be paid as well. One symbol makes the leading player to go back two points on the score track; another allows players to purchase market wares at discount prices. Players must now not only consider which role they would like to take, but also which position they likely want to be visited by the president.

There are 2 additional laws in each of the four categories, so that the game can now last up to 8 rounds instead of just 6.

LiveBoardGame makes watching LIVE sports more fun for everyone. When participants play along with the game live on TV, everyone gets involved. Suddenly the party gets a lot more exciting. Live Board Game is entertainment designed to bring families and friends together to watch live sports. As you watch a live sporting event, you and your friends play along using the game board, which presents many possible outcomes for the next play. Before the next live play begins, participants predict the play’s outcome by placing chips on the board. The participant who predicts the next play correctly collects the losers’ chips. The player with the most chips at the end of the game wins.

Description from the publisher:

What is a triangle? How does a 4 look? What is 5 + 2? The children solve mathematical problems in the tiptoi game "The Hungry Numbers Robot" and feed the hungry Robby! Depending on the level of difficulty they learn to know forms or numbers. For real computing experts, the figure can be expanded to 20. The intelligent tiptoi pen calculates and checks the children's solutions. When the young players solve Robby's tasks correctly, they tap the chip with the tiptoi pencil and place it in Robby's mouth - and he has already disappeared in the belly of the hungry robot. Mjamjam! Whether alone or together, with the tiptoi pen, the children interactively select what they want to do next. If you do not want to count yourself, choose the control symbol for the experience level "Discover", the eye. Then Robby also tells a joke, his belly will be a pocket calculator for the archenemies, and when the children feel like, Robby also sings a song with them.

Description from the publisher:

A board game about parliamentary politics.

Partitocracy focuses on the relationship between the parties, the voters, represented in the game by voter groups, and their interactions, both through legislation and media outlets. Each player is in charge of a Party, decides what gauge of the ideological spectre they want to represent, fight with other parties to be the preferred option for their voters and all of it while establishing alliances to reach government and develop their own policies.

This is a game for 2 to 5 players, and a regular game should last between 2 and 3 hours. Players handle a party by spreading its influence among the voter groups they want to represent, and from which they will gather the voters from. By playing actions and presenting laws in favour of these groups they will collect victory points, but at the same time it will grant voters to the groups affected negatively by those laws by altering the status quo, thus with every law the government pushes in their interests, the more likely that the opposition manages to gather a majority in the next election.

Turns represent a year, and in it several rounds are played. The main resource for the players come in the form of cards, and they can use it for either pay for actions or laws, to trigger the event the card includes or to fire up or cool down the public opinion. Every four turns (or earlier if the government's budget is turned down at the end of every turn) an election round is called and the players have to assemble a new government.

The other pivotal element is the stability of the country. Player's actions and random events modify the stability and how well the country as a whole works (unemployment, security, social integration, etc). This bar is bracketed between two values: Welfare on the low end (how bad things can get before people start rioting on the streets) and high end (how much bureaucracy and government intervention can cap the growth in good times). The stability can never get past the Red Tape value, and if it falls below the Welfare the board advances in the revolution track. If it's not properly handled, it's possible (though not easy) to trigger a civil conflict that ends the game and the winner is decided by which of the most extremist factions got more popular support.

The game is not so much a pure simulator but one that pretends to replicate the flow and the narrative that you can perceive in any country with a parliamentary government environment, and to such extend we believe we have achieved so.

Abstract strategy game.

Glass board, 3 metal circular pawns:
Blue = player A
Yellow = player B
Black = base
The colour on these pieces is only marked on a quarter of the circular top of the playing pieces.

Players try to touch the common black base piece with their own piece by sliding and turning their piece on a 5x5 grid (excluding the corners) from a set starting position to win the match.
Players either slide orthagonal along the dots or turn their piece 90 degrees in their turn.
Touching has to be done between the coloured parts of your playing piece and the common base (hence the importance of turning).
An extra turn can be earned by ending your move on one of the four open dots on the board.
Jumping over other pieces is not allowed.

Kids game in which players "feed the pig" by alternating turns in which they flip a card representing different kinds of food.
For each card flipped, the player flipping the card turns the dial a number of times according to the number on the card (the less healthy the food, the higher the number on the card).
Each turn of the dial increases the pressure on the pig's stomach (a balloon).
First player to make stomach/balloon pop looses the game.

Compete with friends and family members to become the most successful successor to Mads Skjern's business empire! A game of trivia questions based on the successful Television series "Matador". Special questions are available to children playing the game with little or no prerequisites for answering Matador specific questions.

This game builds on questions previously used in other games with the same theme, but has a new different gaming mechanics:

All players start out with a suitcase with which they travel round the board. Players earn money by answering questions of varying difficulty.  Special fields on the game board, allow the players to advance from the suitcase to a small house, eventually reaching the 3 storage warehouse that Mads Skjerns ends with in the television series as well as allow the players to interact by moving the player tokens on the board, stealing money from players etc.

The winner is the first player with a 3 storage warehouse to pass the starting field on the board.

Grab a spoon and watch as it magically changes color. Then, dig into the cereal serving bowl and scoop up pieces of that color. Cross your fingers for a color flash free-for-all! Collect the most cereal pieces and you win!  (From the box)

Crazy Cereal is a children's game for 2 players. Each player gets a spoon which when turned on, lights up with one of 4 colors.  Players try to scoop cereal pieces in the color matching their spoon's light as it changes, one piece at a time. When a spoon starts flashing, that player gets a "free-for-all" and must scoop 2 pieces of a time in any color. Players can also scoop orange colored pieces which are only available during a "free-for-all".

The player with the most cereal pieces in their bowl when the serving bowl is empty is the winner.

A humorous Parscheesi-like game produced by the Lotus Development Corporation in 1987, with a tongue-in-cheek nod to tech office life and many behind-the-scenes happenings during the company's heyday.

Description from the publisher:
The purpose of the Lotus Game is to ship and sell product around the world by obtaining approval from the 4 corresponding departments of manufacturing, finance, research and development, and marketing/sales.  Along the way, you will encounter everything from Lotus celebrations to Lotus mishaps.  You may find yourself phone-mailed or e-emailed away to meetings.  And beware of reorgs that can change everyone's status in the game.

description: In The Queen and Shoe Makers, players are challenged to combine upper cards and heel cards to make nice-looking shoes for the Queen.

The Fun Family Race Game!

In Ooops!, players attempt to move their pawns from Start to Home in this Pachisi / Sorry! type game. Movement is determined by turning over a card from the draw pile, then following the instructions shown on the card.

Some of the main differences between Ooops! and Sorry! are:

The game crafting beermats for lovers of good beer. In the game teams compete magical characters who are strong, but not on the battlefield, and in the tavern for a drink.

boardgame: The Ultimate Trivial Pursuit Question & Answer Book
description:
A book containing more than 2,500 questions and answers in the six standard Trivial Pursuit categories:


     Geography
     Entertainment
     History
     Arts & Literature
     Science & Nature

The format is similar to the question cards of a standard Trivial Pursuit game, with six questions on one page and the answers on the backside of that page.

boardgame: A Feast for Odin: The Norwegians
description:
The first large expansion for A Feast for Odin, The Norwegians, includes a viking-sized number of additions to the game, adding new strategies and puzzle-tiles.

Renewed action board: The largest of the new additions, made of three double-sided pieces that can be flipped to show different actions based on the number of players, opening up new action paths and increasing the chances of players getting in each others' way. It contains new opportunities like butchering, elk-hunting, fishing, and thievery, along with changes for some old actions. It's now easier, for example, to play an animal strategy, because you can now get two of the same animals in one action. There is also a new fifth column with (better) actions that require only one viking. For example, you can now forge a grey special tile with a sword value of 8 or less by using an ore and only one viking! Another totally new 1-viking action is to do a "small emigration" using a whaling boat, which covers one space at the banquet table instead of two but is worth 7VP and costs no silver to do so. And as a bonus, you can send two vikings to this column instead of one to play an occupation card in addition to the action. The catch is, once you use an action from the fifth column, you can't place any more vikings for the rest of this round.

New puzzle-tiles: 2x5 rectangle (horse/pregnant horse/leather/vadmal) and 5 spaces in a "U" shape (herbs/pig/antlers/tools). The red versions of these tiles feature two new animals, horses (worth 6VP) and pigs (worth 1VP and breed every round as opposed to every other round) respectively, adding yet more paths for an animal strategy. There are also five new grey special tiles such as a pan, a hauberk and an anvil, most of which can be forged with ore. Also included is a third goods box to hold all the new tiles.

Four new exploration boards: Islands on the frontside (Isle of Man, Isle of Skye, Islay, Outer Hebrides) with Irish coastal viking-settlements on the backside (Waterford, Wexford, Cork, Limerick), where people from Norway came to stay through the winter, to trade at, and to settle nearby. Also included are renewed versions of the four exploration boards from the base game, with updated iconography and some with adjusted VPs.

New buildings: Also totally new is the idea of a random start-building. Everybody gets one of the six double-sided "artisan shed" boards, and whenever a player could build a shed during the game, they may instead build the front or backside of their artisan shed. Just like the stonehouse and longhouse, the artisan sheds offer new spaces to place orange and red tiles to earn bonus goods, silver and VP, and many of them require specific tiles to be placed in specific spaces. So everybody has another different start-opportunity - like the occupation cards.

VP tokens: Another new option is that, whenever a player would play an occupation card into the game, the player may instead discard the card for a VP token, which are worth 4, 3 or 2VP at the end of the game. A nice little competitive element, as there are a limited number of the 4VP and 3VP tokens.

Other stuff: The expansion also includes another mountain strip, more two-silver coins and more beans/mead and oil/runestone tiles.

The expansion includes another almanac about the new game-elements and their viking-history. But again it was written by Gernot Köpke as a "historical-science"-journalist and learned local newspaper editor and the main elements will be published on BGG at Expansions-Almanac / Almanach für Erweiterungen.

—adapted from the description from the designer
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
Publisher's summary
GoRoGo is new Go variant game for two players played with each 10 ordinary black and white pieces and 5 additional  neutral ones. Each player has two of the neutral pieces, and one starts on the game board.

Like Go, the game is played on a grid, but the board has only a 5x5 grid with 25 intersections and 40 paths. The purpose of the game is to obtain more of your opponent's pieces, not to claim an area majority.

Since there are only 25 pieces, the game is over after 24 moves at most. A player cannot pass, nor play a neutral piece last. If they don't have a legal move, they lose.

The neutral pieces are both black and white. That is, on Black's turn, a neutral piece is a black piece. Then on White's turn, it is a white piece. This allows moves that are normally prohibited in Go, such as 'Kou'. A player can attack into an area that in Go would be protected.

GoRoGo is a GO game but not GO. It means that the tactics are GO tactics but the strategy isn't GO.

boardgame: MASSIVE! Attack of the Tyrant (マッシヴ!Attack of the Tyrant)
description:
User summary
マッシヴ!Attack of the Tyrant (MASSIVE! Attack of the Tyrant) is a game of a battle between two monsters as seen in monster films like Godzilla and Gamera. Players control their own monster to defeat their opponent. No tricks are needed. Just slam it, kick it, and blow it away!

Players take five parts-cards of their monster. Cards represent their body parts: two arms, two legs, and body. They place these parts to form their monsters.

Each turn, you draw an action card, or when you think it's the time to act, you play any number of action cards to approach and attack your opponent at once.

Whenever you move or attack, the physical position of the parts-cards is important.

Walk: Move one of your legs by one step. You may rotate your leg in any direction as long as both of your leg cards touch at an appropriate point (do not overlap your leg cards). Adjust your body and arms accordingly.

Dash: Walk twice.

Punch: Place an area-of-effect card from one of your arms. If the card touches the opponent's body, the body is pushed back, and if this cause the body to be detached from its legs, the opponent loses its balance and falls prone.

Kick: Same as punch, except that an area-of-effect card is placed from one of your legs.

By combining action cards, you may make a super attack such as slam, stomp, and smash.

You win the game if your opponent falls prone three times.

Publisher's summary
男子なら誰しもが経験したであろう怪獣ソフビやアクションフィギュアでのごっこ遊び、ブンドド…
そのブンドドを完全再現したテーブルゲームが、このマッシヴ！シリーズです。
ゴジラやガメラといった怪獣映画でお馴染みの巨大怪獣同士の喧嘩をイメージし、「殴れ、蹴飛ばせ、吹っ飛ばせ！！」をテーマにした、これまでのボードゲームの常識を覆す、全く新しいプレイ感覚のゲームです。

Total War Cards: Punic Wars is a two-player card game which takes place in the ancient Punic Wars between Rome and Carthage.

Gameplay: Both players pick an army and fight over different regions to gain score cards for the end of the game. Every turn you can overpower your opponent in a region by placing better army cards than them or gain distinct bonuses by advancing through the technology tree to gain an edge.

Winning the game: Points are scored according to the combinations of Territory each player captures. After six turns of play, the scores are added up, and a winner declared.

The game features a little bit of strategy, bluffing, foresightedness and luck and is very simple to play with children.

Includes 6 different colored shooting marbles of 1" glass (one per player) and one straight block of wood with 5 arches one next to the other.

Place the wooden arches on a flat surface a distance away from the players, based on player skill. Each player takes turns shooting their marble towards the arches, from the same spot and distance away. Marbles that make it through an arch earn points based on the value marked on the the top of the arch.

A player with more skill can take a 'handicap' by shooting from further away.

The first player to 50 points wins.

This theme deck Includes:
1 60 card Pokemon deck
1 metallic coin
1 rulebook/card list
1 playmat
1 sheet of damage counters
1 Deck box

boardgame: Gridiron Football: The Realistic Football Card Game
description: This is a simple, luck-based game designed to simulate the play of American football. Using a deck of 78 cards, players select from among eight play types, including special teams options, then turn over the topmost card to determine the result. Two plays through the deck constitute a complete game. In addition to the card deck, the game contains a rules pamphlet and a pegboard for marking field position, down, and distance to go for a first down.

boardgame: Sails of Glory: Ship Pack – San Juan Nepomuceno 1766 / San Francisco de Asis 1767
description:
Description from the publisher:

The San Juan Nepomuceno was a Spanish ship of the line built in 1765 in Guarnizo in Cantabria, on design of Francisco Gautier, along with other similar ships. She was used for many years for many different missions, many of them in the Caribbean. In 1793 she fought against Revolutionary France, especially in the occupation of Toulon. In the Battle of Trafalgar, she achieved glory under commander Don Cosme Damian Churruca and provided an incredible example of Spanish heroism, fighting British ships after the loss of over 400 officers and crewmen. Ultimately, she was captured and renamed by the Royal Navy.

This ship pack can also be used to represent the Spanish Nepomuceno class “San Francisco de Asis 1767.”

Ship pack contains a new ship with base, ship card, ship log, and specific deck of maneuver cards.

boardgame: Sails of Glory: Ship Pack – San Agustin 1768 / Bahama 1783
description:
Description from the publisher:

The San Agustín was a 74–gun ship of the line built at the royal shipyard in Guarnizo (Santander) in 1768 by Francisco Gautier. After fighting during the American War of Independence and in the Battle of Cape St. Vincent (1780), she took part of the Napoleonic wars, where she fought at the Battle of Algeciras in 1801 and the Battle of Trafalgar in 1805. In Trafalgar, under the command of Felipe Jado and Cagigal with 711 men aboard, she was surrounded by several ships of Nelson’s squadron. They fought for hours, but after the third approach, she was after being dismasted and with 180 dead and 200 wounded, the British offered terms to surrender. The ship was burned on October 29.

This ship pack can also be used to represent the Spanish 74-gun ship-of-the-line class “Bahama 1780.”

Ship pack contains a new ship with base, ship card, ship log, and specific deck of maneuver cards.

boardgame: Sails of Glory: Ship Pack – HMS Bahama 1805 / HMS San Juan 1805
description:
Description from the publisher:

The Bahama was a Spanish ship of the line, built in Havana in 1780. Originally designed as 64–gun by Ignacio Mullan, she left Havana for Cadiz on 1785. Here she was rebuilt and completed as a 74–gun, following the project of Francisco Gautier, the designer of Nepomuceno class. As a 74–gun ship of the line, she was armed with 74 cannons and a designed crew of 640. They were armed with 28 32–Pounder guns in the lower deck, 30 18–Pounder guns in the upperdeck, 8 32–Pounder guns and 6 12–Pounder guns in the quarterdeck, and 2 12–Pounder guns in the forecastle.

The ship was part of the Spanish fleet that participated in the Battle of Trafalgar, where she was hit by the intense fire of British ships, with 75 killed and 65 wounded. Her commander, Commodore Galiano, died after being struck by a cannonball. After capture by the British fleet, Bahama became HMS Bahama, following repairs in Gibraltar. She sailed to England and was used as a prison ship.

This ship pack can also be used to represent the Spanish Nepomuceno class “HMS San Juan 1805.”

Ship pack contains a new ship with base, ship card, ship log, and specific deck of maneuver cards.

boardgame: Sails of Glory: Ship Pack – Protee 1772 / Eveille 1772
description:
Description from the publisher:

Protée was built in 1772 and designated as flagship of a convoy directed to India, departing with other ships as escort, transporting troops and ammunition. On 23 February, off Spain, the convoy met Rodney's fleet; Duchilleau, commander in charge, tried to lure the British by continuing on the same bearing, while the convoy escaped, escorted by the other ships. The British fleet chased and captured Protée.

This ship pack can also be used to represent the French Artésien Class ship-of-the-line “Eveillé 1772.”

Ship pack contains a new ship with base, ship card, ship log, and specific deck of maneuver cards.

boardgame: Sails of Glory: Ship Pack – HMS Prothee 1780 / HMS Argonaut 1782 Ship Pack
description:
Description from the publisher:

After being captured off the coast of Spain by Lord Robert Manners, the Protée was commissioned in the Royal Navy as the third rate HMS Prothee. She was converted to serve as a prison ship in 1799, and broken up in 1815. Eight of her small cannons were purchased by John Manners, 5th Duke of Rutland and are currently at Belvoir Castle, Leicestershire. The cannons are still fired on special occasions, such as weddings and the Duke's birthday.

This ship pack can also be used to represent the British Caton Class ship-of-the-line “HMS Argonaut 1782.”

Ship pack contains a new ship with base, ship card, ship log, and specific deck of maneuver cards.

boardgame: Super Mario Kart: The Card Game
description:
Description from the back of the box:

Super Mario Kart - The Card Game!

A fast running card game, players cannot reach the goal before items such as bananas and shells jam your kart and prevent victory! Mushrooms and Stars help you get in first place and achieve victory! Two game modes are included with this game! Grand Prix and Battle Mode!

In the Super Mario Kart Card Game, there are two games you can play: Grand Prix and Battle Mode.

In Grand Prix, players use numbered movement cards as well as item cards to navigate through the pack of racers from the back of the line to the front of the line. When players play movement cards, the numbers are compared to determine placement of one's kart in that round. However, players can also use item cards to assist themselves or hurt other players in their quest to win the race!

In Battle Mode, players are equipped with three balloons (like the video game) and players must manage their hand as well as anticipate the moves of other players to try and outmaneuver their attacks while landing their own. The last player with balloons is the winner!
family: Video Game Theme: Super Mario Bros.

boardgame: Sails of Glory: Ship Pack – HMS Leander 1780 / HMS Adamant 1780
description:
Description from the publisher:

HMS Leander, launched at Chatham on 1 July 1780, served on the west coast of Africa, West Indies, and the Halifax station. During the French Revolutionary Wars, HMS Leander participated in the Battle of the Nile and was captured by French, then captured again by Russians and Turks. On 25 April 1805 cannon fire from Leander killed an American seaman with the resulting “Leander Affair” that contributed to the worsening of relations between the USA and Great Britain. In 1813 Leander was converted to a hospital ship under the name Hygeia.

This ship pack can also be used to represent the British Portland Class “HMS Adamant 1780.”

Ship pack contains a new ship with base, ship card, ship log, and specific deck of maneuver cards.

boardgame: Sails of Glory: Ship Pack – HMS Leopard 1790 / HMS Isis 1774
description:
Description from the publisher:

HMS Leopard saw service during the French Revolutionary and Napoleonic Wars, and was active in Europe, East Indies and Egypt. In 1807, a group of British sailors – some of American birth – deserted and joined the crew of USS Chesapeake. Captain Humphreys, commanding Leopard, requested permission to search for the deserters. Chesapeake’s captain refused, and Leopard opened fire, capturing the American ship. Humphreys took back four deserters and hanged the British–born ones. This incident was one of several episodes that increased the tension between the British Empire and the United States, finally exploding in the War of 1812.

This ship pack can also be used to represent the British Portland Class “HMS Leopard 1790.”

Ship pack contains a new ship with base, ship card, ship log, and specific deck of maneuver cards.

boardgame: Sails of Glory: Ship Pack – Petit Annibal 1782 / Leander 1798
description:
Description from the publisher:

Petit Annibal was originally a British ship, the HMS Hannibal, built by Adams of Bucklers Hard and launched on 26 December 1779. She was subsequently captured by the French ship Héros off the coast of Sumatra, in 1782. She was called Petit Annibal to distinguish her from the eponymous 74–gun Annibal, which was in the same fleet. Admiral Bailli de Suffren took her into his fleet, and she saw service in his five battles against British Admiral Sir Edward Hughes in 1782 and 1783. She was decommissioned in 1787 and used as a hulk from 1792.

This ship pack can also be used to represent the French Portland Class “Leander 1798.”

Ship pack contains a new ship with base, ship card, ship log, and specific deck of maneuver cards.

boardgame: Sails of Glory: Ship Pack – Real Carlos 1787 / Conde de Regla 1786
description:
Description from the publisher:

Real Carlos was a 112–gun three–decker ship of the line, built following plans by Romero Landa for Santa Ana. She served in the Spanish Navy during the French Revolutionary Wars, but was destroyed during the Second Battle of Algeciras, with the loss of many of its crew. After participating in the capture of San Pietro Island for Spain and Sant'Antioco for France, in July 1801, she was in Algericas battling against the British fleet. The HMS Superb, cutting through the rearguard, maneuvered between Real Carlos and San Hermenegildo. When the Spanish ships opened fire, they struck each other and then collided, with fire spreading out of control. Both exploded and more than 1700 men were killed in the blast.

This ship pack can also be used to represent the Spanish Meregildos class “Conde de Regla 1786.”

Ship pack contains a new ship with base, ship card, ship log, and specific deck of maneuver cards.

boardgame: Sails of Glory: Ship Pack – Principe de Asturias 1794 / San Hermenegildo 1789
description:
Description from the publisher:

The Príncipe de Asturias was another of the Spanish three–deck 112–gun ship of the line, based on the Santa Ana design by Romero Landa. She fought with a British squadron on 14 February 1797 in the Battle of Cape St Vincent, with 10 killed and 19 wounded. At Trafalgar, the ship suffered 50 killed and 110 wounded. After the battle, she had to be towed by the French frigate Thémis and undergo major repairs in Cadiz. She returned to fight in the Peninsular War, capturing many French ships. She crossed the Atlantic to Havana with Santa Ana to avoid capture by the French. In 1814 she struck a rock and was scrapped three years later.

This ship pack can also be used to represent the Spanish Meregildos class “San Hermenegildo 1789.”

Ship pack contains a new ship with base, ship card, ship log, and specific deck of maneuver cards.

boardgame: Sails of Glory Ship Pack: Artesien 1765 / Roland 1771
description:
Description from the publisher:

The Artésien was built in 1765 as part of a series of 12 ships of the line built to compensate losses endured by the French Royal Navy during the Seven Years' War. The Artésien took part in the American Revolutionary War, departing in 1781. A fine 1/28th scale model of the ship was used to instruct Louis XVI in naval studies and is now on display at the Musée de la Marine.

This ship pack can also be used to represent the French Artésien Class ship-of-the-line “Roland 1771.”

Ship pack contains a new ship with base, ship card, ship log, and specific deck of maneuver cards.

In this board game adaptation of Mario Golf 64 from the Nintendo 64, players play through a full course of golf (18 holes), or the half-course (9 holes). Players assemble large cardboard tiles to make each of the 18 holes as well as assemble the plastic putting green.

Once the hole has been assembled, players in turn take control of a 4 inch tall mechanical Super Mario figure. Players choose the golf club (drivers/irons/putter) they wish to use for each stroke on each hole.

Once the appropriate club has been placed in Super Mario's hands, players pull back a lever to make Super Mario hit the small golf ball down the cardboard course. Players take turns on each hole, choosing their club for each stroke, and hitting their golf ball towards the green. The player with the lowest stroke count at the end of the game is the winner!
family: Video Game Theme: Super Mario Bros.

description:
Quiz: Historia Polski (which means Quiz: History of Poland) is a trivia game about Polish history.

Every turn a player throws a die and moves her pawn the corresponding number of spaces. The space that the player lands on determine the following action which may be:

     a general question from a card of a color corresponding to the color of space that the player landed on
     a historically important date question
     the catapult challenge in which a player will try to shoot the ball into the cup using a catapult


Each good answer and each scored catapult is worth 1 point.
The first player to score 50 or 100 points (depending on what players agreed on) is the winner.

The fate of four empires rests in the hands, and the reins, of the men and women in charge of the Baggage Trains.  They race their fleet of wagons against their rivals, striving to reach the battlefield and resupply their armies.

Players draw cards which help them to scout the field ahead or sabotage their rivals, and then calculate their advancement towards the battlefield.  Random events affect the players progress, and relics may be gained which will invoke the favour of the gods granting bonus victory points.

However, as a Baggage Train is always on the move, resources are scarce.  You can only use what you can carry with you, and the more you carry, the slower your progress will inevitably be.

The player with the most victory points at the end of the game is the winner  Points are earned for the cargo carried in your baggage train, as well as for having the most scouts or the most saboteurs.  The first player to reach the battlefield is awarded a generous victory point bonus, but this alone will not win you the game.

This is the baggage train, where getting there is half the battle.

Description from the designer:
German korps, Russian army level ... generally. 31 German-Axis and 42 Russian units/pieces. Ferdinands, 503th and 505th Tiger Abs/Bns, 10th Panzer (Panther) Brigade. Panzerkeil panzerkorps formation statuses (unless/until they are Disrupted). 4 Russian Artillery Preparation Korps. No Retreat! and Pressed Attack options. 26+mis/42kms per hex. 10 5-day turns enabling Russian counter-offensive. For Panzerkeil formations possible post-DE second attack after tactical advance.  Blitzkrieg/Proriv/Penetration movement phases.

For Kursk, quite fast and simple.  Good 4-player game if larger map used.

Another of my free print-and-play games on Coatney History ... while it lasts - while *I* last - I am 70 now.

description:
This is a 2-player paper and pencil game played on a grid of 6 squares by 6 squares. The first turn is slightly different from the following turns: on the first turn the first player writes his initial in any square, as does the second player (though this could not be the square initialed by the first player).

On the second and subsequent turns each player 1) fills in the square in which he had previously put his initial and 2) initials another square which is a queen's move from the square which he has just filled in (initialed and filled in squares are barriers to this process).

A player loses when he is no longer able to play.

Challenge your rival!
Learn & Mater 44 Knots, win points.
Knot Tying cards range in difficulty from 1 to 5 points.
Play it as a game for 2-4 players, of brush up on your Knot Tying skills.
Each player selects one length of tying rope and one tying bar.  From a deck of cards, one card is turned face up and players race to be the first to tie the Knot.  The first player is given the knot tying card with its point value.  Cards are played, one by one, until all the cards are used.  The winner is the player with the highest sum of points.

Pixl Adventure: Dungeon Quest is a dungeon crawl/hack-n-slash card game for 2-4 players. Setup takes less than 2 minutes and gameplay takes around 30-60 minutes. Suitable for ages 8- infinity!

The game is played by selecting your hero and relevant health tokens, drawing starting equipment, then drawing monsters/traps/chests from the dungeon deck until this has been exhausted and both bosses have been killed. It is a co-op game that engages all players every turn.
