description:
A collection of 4 challenging Puzzle-Games. Rules to play a co-op game with each puzzle is included in every package.

Egg Cetera
The basic solution is an Egg Shape but as the pun in the title suggests, there are many other solutions to try. We offer some such as Faces, Foods, etc. & then you can create your own. Play alone or with as many as l2. 24 pieces, 8x10 finished. 8 to Adult.

Messages
The pieces are collections of signs, symbols and pictures. Put them together and a message appears. We have a variety of solutions, but the beauty of the puzzle is you can create your own messages - if you are clever enough! Play alone or as a group. 21 pieces. 7 to Teen.

Turnovers
Co-operative Tic Tac Toe. Try to get 3 pieces of the same color in a row. Sometimes you have to turn them over and colors change. Sometimes pieces get piled on. Keep your wits about you or you soon have a tangle. A challenge indeed for a young thinker! Play alone or with as many as six. Playboard 5 x 5 in, 12 tokens. 8 to Adult.

The Blot
The pieces start as an ink blot. Rearrange them carefully and you can make animals, faces, letters, numbers, birds, ships, etc. We provide a booklet of 60 silhouetted shapes to try. Do ours, then create your own. Play alone or as a group. Rules for a game included. 7 pieces. 8 to Adult.

Description from the designer:

A print & play board game (using components from Finca) for 2-6 players.

In Mallorca Express each player represents a Majorcan farmer growing fruits and trying to sell them for as much profit as possible.

Regarding game mechanics, Mallorca Express is influenced by games like Alien Frontiers and Troyes and their use of dice for worker placement.

For buyers at the Tokyo Game Market Dec 2016, an additional tile "Thunder" was added as a give away to create even more variety.

Rules:
The Thunder tile is flipped over and shuffled to the other 9 tiles, so the starting grid changes from a 3x3 grid to a 3-4-3 grid.
When you turn over the Thunder tile, the player to your left may randomly draw and keep one of your completed rainbow cards (unless you only own one card, or none).
If more then 3 cloud cards were flipped over, they are shuffled and put back to the other cloud cards keeping the general layout.
If 3 or less cloud cards were flipped over, these are shuffled with additional randomly chosen cloud cards in a total of 4 cards and then all put back to the general layout.

The Forbidden Sciences expansion includes all-new Division Cards for the Psychogenics Lab and Grafting Research divisions, as well as five all-new items for both Divisions, adding a wealth of new variety to your games!

Bring out the untapped potential of the human mind with the Psychogenics Lab!

Turn your Troops into horrifying (but effective) alien/human hybrids with the Grafting Lab!

Tap into the untold power of The Forbidden Sciences!

CARDS LIST

PSYCHOGENICS LAB - Items

    Precognition (x5)
    Psionic Adaptation (x5)
    Psionic Enhancement (x5)
    Psionic Feedback (x5)
    Psionic Juggernaut (x5)
    Psychogenics Lab Division (x1)


GRAFTING RESEARCH - Items

    Corpse-Larva Symbiote (x5)
    Deathripper Adrenal Gland (x5)
    Lasher Tendrils (x5)
    Ravager Carapace (x5)
    Siphoning Claws (x5)
    Grafting Research Division (x1)


DIVISIONS

    Psychogenics Lab (x1)
    Grafting Research (x1)

This bingo-style game has a spinner with the letters of the alphabet, and 10 player cards, each bearing the names of three '30s Hollywood stars, one with 5 letters, one with 6 and one with 7.

After each spin, players put tokens on top of all examples of that letter on their card. The first to cover all letters on two of the names on a card shouts "Hollywood". The names are checked and if correct, that player wins; otherwise that player is out of the game and spinning continues. The rules say players may agree to play until all three names on a card are covered.

The cards are a who's who of Depression-era stars: Astaire, Garbo, Chaplin, Gable, Cagney, Hepburn, Karloff, Jolson, Ruggles and many more.

This expansion for Mare Nostrum: Empires adds six new heroes, that may be used in place of the original Starting Heroes. All additional rules are contained on the back of the tiles.

The new heroes provided include: Alexander, Brutus, Gadeirus, Khufu, Mago I, and Xerxes.

Description from the publisher:

In Wizard’s WARdrobe, you take the role of an apprentice wizard who has busted in to the Grand Wizard’s magical WARdrobe to battle other wizards for epic loot. Each player starts with 99 Hit Points, two learned spells in hand, and a wizard inventory playmat with 8 color & number-coded body locations for items (2 feet, 2 body, 2 hands, 1 head & as many accessories [trinkets or familiars] as you want). During your turn you allocate 3 actions to cast spells, use items, and grab items from the 8 face-up WARdrobe piles. The more powerful the effect, the more actions it costs, so keep an eye out for boots and familiars that can give you extra actions.

Gear Up!
The more items you are wearing, the harder you are to hit. The innovative hit roll system supports the theme nicely: When a wizard deals damage to other wizards, they roll an 8-sided hit die and refer to the hit location on each affected players’ playmat. If the wizard has an item in that location, it is moved to the junk pile and no damage is dealt. If the wizard does not have an item in that location, then the damage is rolled and dealt to the wizard’s HP.

Throw Down!
There is a vast repertoire of offensive and defensive effects in the base game (over 200 cards), with more to come in the preset expansion boosters. Once your wizard is suited up with synergetic buffs, you are ready to use your spells and items to attack your opponents and defend yourself. The last wizard standing wins!

Wizard’s WARdrobe is quick to set up and very easy to learn: the game pieces laid out almost teach the game itself. It is fast-paced (you can start slinging right off the bat) yet still highly strategic (with dozens of spells and 8 WARdrobe piles to choose equipment from, there are many lines of play). So far, in the hundreds of private and public playtests at conventions and stores, almost every game has been unique and exciting. The few games that weren’t we used to refine the game to its current 5th version, which is running super smooth, fun, and balanced.

Description from the back of the box:

Players frantically roll their dice at the same time and quickly try to place them on the gameboard, in numerical order.  Keep rolling until the right number is rolled  The player to place all of his or her dice on the board first wins.

The "out of sight" game to play. For groovy gamers of all ages.

The object is to collect as many marbles as possible without uncovering any of the red dots that are "out of sight"

Marbles are set on all open circles so the red dots underneath cannot be seen. Then a spinner is spun that changes the position of the red dots to unknown areas.

The players spin the "Fickle Finger of Fate" spinner to tell them how many marbles to remove. If a red dot is under the marble, the player must return as many marbles as it takes to cover all the holes.

If the player who uncovered a red dot cannot cover all the open holes, then the game is over and the player with the most marbles wins.

A new of its kind strategy board game for 2 to 6 players. Take your viruses and battle your opponent's occupied cells, but be careful, the cure syringe is also part of the game. Take risks and dominate the game. Challenge your friends and let them Infected.

Featuring built-in neodymium magnet characters and fully magnetized board.

Play Infected in single mode or in team mode with your friends. 
Each player takes at the role of one color virus and battles his opponent's occupied cells with the purpose of infecting other player's head viruses to win the game. Avoid the cure miracles, make the right moves to infect cells, take risks and dominate the game. Let your friends Infected.

Infected: The Board Game is not like other games. Here, players don't start in the same place each game played. Infected randomize the way to start each game by spinning the Infected spinning disk and throwing the dice at the beginning of the game.

All viruses start in each player's head virus, so protecting your head virus is something to always keep in mind.

A great art exhibition is celebrated. For the first time it has managed to gather in a great exhibition numerous objects, jewels and artworks that until now it had not been possible to contemplate together. This exhibition is assembled for the first time in a museum enabled in The Alhambra Palace (Seville, Spain). Incredible safety deployment, protection systems have been carefully designed. cAmong all the pieces featured are five valuable jewels coveted by the world's most famous thieves, who will meet there in an attempt to prove who is the best of them all. They will all try to steal them without being trapped.cNo one can enter without jumping the system. No one? ... Nothing can be removed from the museum without being detected. Nothing?...

Penetrate into the Palace of the Alhambra, getting as many jewels as possible and escaping without being discovered is the challenge of the game. Each player becomes a thief for the duration of the game, and must use all his ingenuity to avoid escape the strict security measures that have been put in place to protect the jewels. All the tools and weapons necessary to carry out the stole have been buried in the Palace Gardens: the Arrayanes's Garden and the Lion's Garden, months before the Opening of the Exhibition, and so during the first tour for these two gardens, simulated to be innocent visitors, the players are collecting the necessary equipment.

The only escape route is through the Mosque located in the northwest corner of the Palace, as the Alhambra is surrounded by walls around its perimeter. There are a powerful off-road vehicle that will take the triumphant thief down the mountain with the precious booty. If you get it, you will have won and the first pages of the international press will open their editions with "Scandal! Theft of the Century".

Goals of the Game:

The players decide when starting the game which game mode they will play:


     Win the first player to steal a jewel and escape.



     All jewelry must be stolen, and that player who gets the most value by adding the points of the jewels obtained is the one that wins. In case of a tie, the thief who holds the Recesvinto's Crown will win.
family: Theme: Fine Art and Art Museums

description: Bring the iconic 80’s slasher movie to life in this intense, immersive board game. The innovative story-board mechanism puts you in front of the camera as the scenes unfold, leading you and your fellow cast members through a terrifying night in a variable wilderness setting. Use cunning to survive until dawn or stand and fight to stop the Killer once and for all. Gather Plot Points to maintain your Courage in the face of horror as your Supporting Cast is slaughtered around you. Will you be able to reveal the Killer’s vulnerabilities and face him (or her) down before the mayhem of Act 3 begins? Or will you simply run for your life, never knowing where terror will strike next? Either way, the Story Board will track the choices you’ve made and tell your tale of woe (or heroism) to all who dare to watch the Silver Scream...

What did the tiger get as a great gift? And why did the penguin bring the same six gifts? Whoever can count the points correctly gets to unwrap the wacky gifts. Here we go! Throw the dice, count, receive gifts. A crazy game for 2-4 players ages 4-99 that helps develop counting and math skills!

Malaysianised card game based on the same engine as Cards against Humanity.

START
Choose a ‘Boss’ (the judge)
Boss deals all players 8 cards

MATCH
Boss displays and reads aloud a Yellow card
All players (except the Boss) match a Blue card within their hand to the Yellow card

BODEK
Players bodek (persuade) the Boss to choose their card
Boss picks a winner

A children's space themed board game played by moving playing pieces along numbered squares till they reach the moon. Starting from earth, the first to get to the moon is the winner. Along the way players will encounter picture squares which have either set back or speed up their travel.

Playing pieces are simple counters and the board has graphics very similar to the British Dan Dare space caricature. Published about 1957 by Emu Series in the U.K.

The purpose of this spin and move vintage space exploration game is to travel to as many planets as possible on the playing board starting at the rocket launching platform spot. The planets are located around the edge of a Monopoly style layout. Each player receives the celestial currency called Celestros upon visiting a planet. Players can encounter setbacks and benefits along the way by landing on specific squares. The game is over when all players return to the Launching Platform. The player with the greatest amount of currency wins the game.

The game has beautiful box graphics typical of the 50s era. The board has the spinner built into it and the playing pieces are colored plastic rockets very similar to that produced by the Lido corporation and seen in the board game Rocket Race to Saturn (1953). Rules are printed on the inside of the box lid and the game was produced by the E-Z game Manufacturing Corporation, New York. Another version of the game is all but identical except the title was changed to 'Race to Planets'.

boardgame: Lanterns: The Harvest Festival – Snowflake Promo Tile
description:
Originally available in Santa's Renegades 2016

Remove one other random tile and replace it with this tile. Follow other regular setup rules.

boardgame: Space: A Race to the Moon
description:
A roll and move electronic space race board game starting at the Woomera rocket range in Australia. The first to reach the moon is the winner. Two players each have a wired playing piece (electrode) that they move by the roll of the die. The electrode piece advances along small holes in the board towards the moon passing planets along the way. Each player has to follow a separate route to the moon made of these small holes. At certain contact points along the way the playing piece sets off one of a series a colored warning lights on the board that indicates a penalty sending them back to a planet. On reaching the moon a yellow light in the moons center indicates the winner.

Unknown publisher but most likely Australian or English considering the unique launch zone where rocket testing was conducted during the 1950s

A space exploration spinning top board game. The object of the game is to spin and steer four capsule travel tops into the solar system game board and attempt to land on planets to win points. To win, tally up all points to make the highest possible score.

Each player spins three yellow tops. The yellow capsule tops spins on the playing board and the player tilts the board to make the tops travel into the solar system and stop in a circular hole representing a planet and score points. This is repeated with one red top to score double points. Trouble if you land on the sun, either losing half your points if it was a yellow top or all points if your red capsule stops on the sun. The player having the highest score after spinning all four tops is the winner.

Description from the game insert:

During the same sitting, you can find yourself at the Casino, at the race, at an auction, or playing a friendly game of poker... all the while scratching cards to find out if you have a chance to win or if you have lost your bid.  Scratshow starts with a roll of the dice telling you where your pawn will land.  Are you on a Poker square? Take a scratch card marked Poker and discover your hand.  Place your bet, bluff, raise... Your pawn takes you to the races? Place your bet, scratch a card and find out if your horse is in the Win, Place or Show category.  The first player who gets to be a millionaire wins the game.

Description from the box insert:

A game where the scholar is not necessarily the winner. A game where wits provide for hilarity. The Great Dictionary Game is a fun-filled search for the definition of a word picked out of the dictionary and starting with one or more of the letters appearing on a card chosen at random. Proposed definitions can be absolutely accurate, a figment of the imagination, incoherent, plausible, misleading or humorous. The winner is the player with the highest score of points obtained either from his bets on accurate definitions or from votes of players.

The B-26 has arrived in the European Theater of Operations. It has now joined the B-17, B-24 and the Lancaster over the skies of war torn Europe...

B-26: The Marauder Strikes! is a solitaire game set onboard a Martin B-26 Marauder medium bomber during World War Two in the European Theater of Operations from July 1943 until the end of the war in May 1945. It is a comprehensive game with several bomber models, mission details and a large selection of targets.

The game reflects the twin engine B-26 Marauder and situations and events which crews experienced on their missions. If you add the advanced rules you will find “B-26” to become deep and detailed and you will find yourself in situations where you may have to make decisions that will affect not only the success of your mission, but the fate of your bomber and crew. Historical accuracy has been an important guideline throughout the development of this game, though there are also some ”what-if,” fictional, aspects added.

You can begin your campaign flying missions from bases in England then move to the continent later. You will find your station either in England, France, Belgium or the Netherlands, depending on when you fly your campaign. Put together a crew, name your B-26 and fly missions over France, Belgium, the Netherlands, Luxemburg, Germany, Austria and Czechoslovakia!

description:
"A long time ago Erloth used the scraps of his trashed barrow to build a stall. He sold mead and wine there and after some time it developed into a little hut - The 'Drunken Troll Tavern.' Merchants, voyagers, and heroes frequented it freely and Gilda, Erloth's granddaughter, was well-known and liked.

Many times creatures ranged Andor but neither had they attacked it nor would anybody believe this the case. Until that fateful morning, as a farmer approached the heroes and gasped:

'Come quickly! The Drunken troll ravages the tavern!'"

This legend was designed by the users of the official German-language Andor forum from May to September 2014. Made up of over 70 cards it is one of the longest legends of Andor, with high variability, and it uses components from New Heroes and The Star Shield in new ways. Thank you to all the fans who contributed ideas and opinions.

This cat is soooo moody! 
He'll switch from happy to angry in one step.

In this game you play as a mischievous alley spirit on a mission to earn silver feather points by affecting Moody Cat's mood as he's prancing down your alley. As a spirit you are forbidden to affect the cat’s mood directly so you'll have to enlist help from other creatures. Keep in mind that there are other alley spirits competing for the points and will try to outsmart you in their efforts to earn more points than you. So unleash your spirit power to meddle with the Moody Cat.

Typically played as a set number of game rounds with score keeping after each game. The winner is determined after the last game in a set.

2 players.

Materials
A hex board having 37 cells, 4 hexes to a side, and 37 stones in a single color.

Setup
Place a stone, in each of the 37 cells of the board.

Definitions
Group - one or more adjacent stones.

Object
The player with the most stones wins.

Play
Choose a starting player, turns alternate

On a turn remove 2 non-adjacent stones from a line of adjacent stones. Removed stones are used for endgame scoring.

If a group is split into 2 or more groups, the player that caused the split takes the smallest group, but if the smallest groups are of equal size, the player then decides which one to take.

The game ends when a player cannot take his turn.

Description from the publisher:

In the Yahtzee-like dice game Dice'n Score! Farm, players get to throw the dice up to three times on their turn while attempting to make high-scoring sets of animals. Choose carefully which ones to score as each line of animals can be scored only once! If all the possible choices for a roll have been filled, then you must score zero points in another box of your choice.

If you score 18 or more points on the upper half of the score sheet, then you receive 10 bonus points. Roll five identical items, and you get a 10-point Big Farm bonus. Whoever has the highest score when all the score sheets are filled wins!

Description from the publisher:

Starlight & Vengeance is the expansion to Shroud of the Shadow Demon. Starlight & Vengeance includes two entirely new decks, represented by heroes, Melevanie and Ingduhl.

Like in Shroud of the Shadow Demon, your goal is to collect more Ember Stones than your opponent. The expansion offers new cards and strategies for you and your friends to play with.

Sunk! is a skill and action game in which the task is to add water — one drop at a time — to the cup floating in the water-filled tank without causing it to sink. You're not simply pouring water, though, as the game involves challenge cards as well. Can you pour with only one eye open? What about when you're hopping on one foot?

Description from the publisher:

Nibbled is a fast-paced, easy-to-learn game that starts with kids clipping four plastic fish onto themselves. Guess a color, then flip a card. Guess correctly, and you unclip one fish and clip it onto an opponent. Whoever first unclips all of their fish wins!

Originally offered through Dice Tower's Season 13 Indiegogo campaign (Promo Pack E) as well as at Gen Con 2017

Comes with 7 identical copies of Cybernetics Limb - a new trait card.

Text:
Negates a defensive trait when attacking. 
Carnivores receive one fewer food when attacking this species

Dice Tower Season 13 kickstarter on Indiegogo

Provided x5 Socialist cards that can replace the Ambassador role.

boardgame: The Lion Guard: Gut gebrüllt, Kion!
description:
From the publisher:

An exciting race with the heroes from the TV series "The Guard of the Lions".

The little lion Kion is leader of the "Guard the Lion", they protect the sacred land. From his vantage point on the King's Rock, Kion discovers some wandering hyenas, which always cause trouble. With loud roar, he calls his friends for help! Who will win the exciting race and be the first at Kion?

From the Publisher:

"I'm packing into my suitcase ..." - and the number of things being remembered is getting longer! Now two games in one: classic with travel luggage or even witty with crazy grimaces and movements!

"I pack in my suitcase ..." and the series of memorized things is getting longer. Now two games in one: classic with travel luggage or even witty with crazy grimaces and movements! 
The classic is even more fun now!

Description from the publisher:

Monopoly, the legendary game of risk taking, deal making, and owning it all, and The University of Texas at Austin, the celebrated university of academics, research, and national championships, team up to create a classic board game for Longhorm fans everywhere. Take a spin around the board to find the best loved athletes, coaches, buildings and scenes of one of America's great universities. And be prepared to pay big bucks if you land on the Tower or Littlefield Fountain-the University's most recognized building and beloved spot on campus don't come cheap!

Grab your friends and family put on your Longhorn sweatshirt and enter the halls of UT at Austin for the game of your life. The Texas Union, Show Band of the Southwest, Darrell Royal,  Littlefield Home or 99 other Longhom properties can all be yours.  Whether a student, alumnus or fan, can you resist the chance to buy Texas' finest?

GAME INCLUDES:  Game Board Property Cards.  Play Money and Bankers Tray.  2 Dice 10 Game Tokens.  Chance Cards Community Chest Cards Package of Houses.  Package of Dormitories
Two monsters have escaped from the monster party! Let's go searching for them in Monster Wanted! If you can remember who vanished and who's still at the party, you could win the game.

boardgame: Mauseschlau & Bärenstark: Rund um die Natur
description:
From the publisher

A memorabilia and collecting game with mouse squeaky and bearish actions!

With Mauseschlau & Bärenstark It's in the nature! There the players learn local animals, flowers, tree and berry fruits. In addition, they can show how well they can represent animals and imitate animal voices. If you also show some skill in gyros and a good memory, the first to collect 6 picture cards and win
Subsidiary expansion cards for use with Trivial Pursuit: The 1980's Master Game.

Description from the publisher:

You are a young Captain, in charge of your own fleet of ships, seeking fame and fortune.

In Commanders of Land and Sea you take the role of the Captain of a fleet of ships that seeks to expand its domain, your goal is to take over as many islands as possible, placing your boats in them, claiming their treasures and increasing your power; At the same time you must eliminate the other players, destroying their ships and taking possession of their main ports.

You have found an area of the ocean where there are several islands that are not under any kingdom or country, it is your duty to land on them and reclaim them along with their wealth as your own. But be careful, other corsairs, have also established themselves in nearby ports, and are also in search of new domains, you must face them and prove to them who is the most powerful, using your ships and wealth to eliminate their ships and drive them away.

What are you waiting? Have fun sailing your ships, conquering islands landing in their ports and increase your wealth; face your opponents and if necessary send them flying by exploding your traps. Who will be the most successful Captain? You will have to play to find out.

Add new strategies and surprises to Illimat with new Luminaries and Okus tokens inspired by The Crane Wife. The Loom allows you to craft at a cost. The Island is a place of isolation, while The Boat bridges the divide between distant fields. Will you pull off The Perfect Crime, or will The Butchers strike down a Luminary before you can claim it?

This expansion includes six Luminaries and a booklet with variant rules. The first edition also included two Okus tokens and a cloth bag.

Eclectica is a word-based game for two or more players. Teams compete to decipher clues, get words, and phonetically rearrange them to find answers. The first team to identify the correct answer wins the point!​​

Clues are based on all subjects - ranging from art to mathematics to idioms in the English language - so there’s plenty of room for everyone to play!

The Lend-Lease program saw thousands of tanks supplied to the Soviet Union during the war. Amongst these were a small number of M10 tank destroyers that somehow found their way to the Eastern Front. While not as heavily armed or armored as some Soviet designs, the turret meant it was one of the few self-propelled guns that could not easily be outflanked.

Description from the publisher:

Charms is a fun, easy to learn party game where each player takes a turn as the Quester by asking a question from the Quest Cards (or their own question). Everyone else finds their inner sage and tries to write the wisest answer based on the Quester's roll of the charms, their meanings, and where they land on the board. The Quester then picks the winning answer. The player with the most winning answers wins the game.

Understand the game in 4 basic steps.

1. Draw a card, read a question to the group, and roll 9 random charms onto the board. Remove "out of play" charms.

2. Use the KEY CARD to review charm meanings. Other players quickly write an answer to your question based on the charms, where they landed on the board, and what this could mean for you.

3. The answers are read aloud. You pick the one that best answers your question, and the author is revealed as the winner of the round.

4. Pass the charms to the left and play again until everyone has asked a question. The player who writes the most winning answers is crowned the Charms Champion of Insight!
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
Does the stupidest farmer have the biggest potatoes? Not in this game! This is because the clever cultivation of beds, which is about the meaningful juxtaposition (mixed culture, plant friendliness) and succession (fruit sequence), leads to a sumptuous harvest and thus to win the game.
Gameplay: Players systematically grow vegetables, fruit and herbs. They plant according to the garden calendar and promote the growth of their plants. However, a lot has to be taken into account. Are they low or flat rooted, weak, medium or heavy consuming? Does the plant have a long or short growth period? In addition, weather caprioles, beneficial insects and pests, the neighbor or other imponderables can influence the tranquil gardener life.
Game objective: The players try to "harvest" as many self-planted as also by other players placed plant cards. Whoever has the greatest harvest yields is the winner of the game.

Using the Napoleonic version of the March into Battle system, this game portrays the Battle of Quatre Bras in a furious, fast moving two turn game. Part of a group of mini-games on the battles of the Waterloo campaign, with Ligny and La Belle Alliance.

In this system as in reality, units will become ineffective from fatigue and disorder long before they run out of men - unless you, in command, can manage them effectively and maintain their edge. A turn lasts as long as the typical historical time to arrange and execute an assault - every move counts.

Map scale: 1km per hex
Unit scale: divisions, 1000 men per strength point
Time scale: 3.5 hours per turn

In Contact, players take turns as the Spy whose goal is to keep their chosen word secret. They reveal the first letter of their secret word to begin the round. Other players take turns interrogating/asking a question of the Spy who must truthfully answer, thus revealing what the secret word is not in the process. Their answers must also begin with the revealed letter(s). If a question stumps the Spy the other players may Contact the Interrogator to provide their answer. If the answers are the same then the Spy reveals the next letter in their secret word, thus making it harder to keep their word secret.

boardgame: 1831 For our freedom and yours: Battle of Grochow
description:
Board wargame 1831 For our freedom and yours: Battle of Grochow represents one of the most famous battles in the history of the Polish army. Players have historical forces and must act, given the historical advantages and limitations of their armies. Also important are factors such as terrain and weather conditions. Of course you can not skip the random factor.

All these aspects are described in rules which are not complicated.

Among the four scenarios it is introductory scenario - attack of Rosen’s IV corps on alder grove. This is the perfect introduction to a large battle. Players have limited forces and learn basic
rules and characteristics of the terrain. Thanks to this, the experience gained can be used in the course of the scenario for the entire battle.

Game mechanics based on random activations. However, the proper marker set reflects a better command structure of the army of Field Marshal Dybicz. Moreover, this solution provides an exciting game in which each side can take the initiative to surprise the enemy.

Three types of weapons: infantry, cavalry and artillery act according to the common rules, but each of them has its own characteristics. Success on the battlefield provides the appropriate use of the advantages of all kinds of weapons at the right moment. This is obviously the goal pursued by both players. Coordination of their own troops, and respond to your opponent moves makes Battle of Grochow a challenging game, even for experienced players.

boardgame: Myanmar (fan expansion for Ticket to Ride)
description:
This map is an unofficial fan made expansion. Symbols & Graphics Copyright © 2004-2015 Days of Wonder, Inc. Produced with permission of Days of Wonder, for non-commercial use only.

This follows the principles of the basic Ticket to Ride game on a special board with special cards and rules. The object of the game is to gain the most points by travelling various routes and outwitting other players. You will need the train pieces and the train cards from either the US or Europe base games (or you can buy them from the Days of Wonder shop.

There is an extra set of Tourist cards for the cities, each with a value to be earned by visiting that city. These cards are dealt/picked up alongwith tickets in the same quantity and with the same 'keep/dispose' rules.

There are also chance cards which may help or hinder you. The board does not have any grey tracks or parallel tracks - but there are some options in this pack.

There are 36 possible mountain railway excursions which give you extra points and a big bonus for the most trips.

There are many new options in the rules: exchange all your cards at the start but keep all the new ones: combined points for two journeys: discard 5 cards and pick up 5 new ones, as your turn: earn extra points: and many more. You can also call time when your wagon stock reaches 3, if you want, giving you the advantage of stopping other players completing tickets.

boardgame: Rum & Bones: Second Tide – Deep Lords Heroes Set #1
description:
This Hero Set contains five all new Heroes to join the ranks of the Deep Lords.

Captain: Triton    
Brute: The Elder Below
Gunner: Euryale
Quartermaster: Tick Tock    
Swashbuckler: Spyridon

boardgame: Rum & Bones: Second Tide – Marea de la Muerte Heroes Set #1
description:
This Hero Set contains five all new Heroes to join the ranks of the Marea de la Muerte.

Captain: Don Juan
Brute: Felicia
Gunner: Cabo Morales
Quartermaster: Senor Espadero
Swashbuckler: Renaldo Guitarra

boardgame: Rum & Bones: Second Tide – Sea Monsters
description:
Sea Monsters Box contains resculpted miniatures for the sea monsters available in the base game (Sea Dragon and Kraken) and three new monsters.

The new monsters are:
Siren
Avatar of Poseidon
Shell Cracker

boardgame: Paw Patrol: Sports Day Challenge Path Game
description: Players choose one of three pups (Chase, Marshall, or Skye) and take turns racing along a path.  A spinner determines the distance moved.  Certain squares along the path earn the player trophies (points), or send a player backwards a short distance.  Along the way, players choose to take a longer and safer path, or a shorter path that risks giving their trophies to the player with the least amount.  Players that reach the finish line earn an additional trophy on their turn.  The game ends when the last pup crosses the finish line.

Description from the publisher:

This game allows two players to game an alternate history where Germany and Japan have won WWII.  Three years after that victory in 1948 they square off against each other for a final battle for world domination.  The map of the game joins along the equator to depict the entire world.

Game mechanics recreate dilemmas and challenges inherent in grand-strategic warfare and the issues involved with controlling the entire planet.  Movement switches rapidly back and forth between players keeping you fully involved in the game with no long waits between players.  In any order you want, you can: enter reinforcements; bring back into play previously eliminated units; move an army, air force, fleet or elite corps on the map via land, sea or air; launch a conventional attack or launch a nuclear attack. Both conventional and nuclear attack options are available to players.

Complexity: Medium
Time Scale: Varies 1yr/turn
Map Scale: Global Area Movement
Unit Scale: Armies/Fleets/Air Forces
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Description from the publisher:

It's the fun and fast-paced game you love, now featuring your favorite DC Super Hero Girls! Players race to get rid of all their cards by matching a card in their hand with the current card shown on top of the deck and in this version, there's a special wild card with new DC Super Hero Girls rule and two customizable wild cards. Maybe you'll draw the Save the Day card! That lets you make another player immune from penalty cards until another wild card is played. Score points by being the first to get rid of all the cards in your hand. First player or team to 500 wins. When you're down to one card, don't forget to yell "UNO!" Contains 112 cards plus instructions. For 2 to 10 players, ages 7 and older. Easy to pick up, impossible to put down.
family: Versions & Editions: Versions with IP after the original release

description:
Description from the publisher:

Get ready for a thinking player's game. Dogs Night Out Poker School will not only make you think about your plays, but you will have fun leaving your money off the table. Whether you are new to Texas Holdem or a long time player, your're going to gain an understanding of the game that you have never had before. Gambling is not required.

Two complete games! OUT CARD (the more challenging game) and FIVE BALL POKER (the simpler game). Play with friends, teach friends, play solo. No Matter how you choose to play, you will continuously be improving the game.

Out Card
An entertaining, innovative approach using a combination of tools to analyze a poker game. A visual representation of a poker hand makes it easier to see winning possibilities. Numbering Out Cards in priority order is a challenge that will improve your poker game. This game can be played with friends as a challenge or teaching tool, or play solo for individual learning.

5 Ball Poker
Learn to analyze the top current and best potential hands at five different analysis points represented by Pool balls in a street map format. A simple, yet important tool for your poker game, showing the road to the money hand.

Szyfr Pana Twardowskiego is a simple roll and move racing game designed as promotional material by Bydgoszcz City Hall (Polish city).
Game apart from simple roll and move mechanic uses trivia card with  questions related to Bydgoszcz (architecture, geography, history, etc).

boardgame: Kingdom Death: Monster – First Hero Expansion
description: In the First Hero Expansion, players take the role of 4 veteran survivors who have no settlement to call their own. They find and invade a settlement where they face the First Hero, a survivor so powerful, they do all the hunting by themselves! The purpose of this expansion is to help players start more advanced campaigns at Lantern Year 12 or 20. Instead of simply providing complicated rules to generate later settlements, your showdown with the First Hero will create the advanced settlement that you can take over.

boardgame: Kingdom Death: Monster – Abyssal Woods Expansion
description:
The Abyssal Woods campaign, of course has an Abyssal Woods Campaign! It's fully devloped re-imaging of the People of Bloom campaign in the Flower Knight Expansion!

Develop a civilization in the Abyssal Woods!  Worship the Flower Knight.  Battles Disciples of the Witch!  Hunt the Dragon Goblin, Confront the Flower Witch!  Craft awesome armor, roll around Spider Balls and more!!!

In addition to the Core Game.  You'll need:
Flower Knight expansion
Spidicules Expansion.

The Dung Beetle Knight and Honeycomb Weaver expansions have optional special interactions with the Campaing too!

boardgame: Kingdom Death: Monster – Ivory Dragon Expansion
description: The Ivory Dragon extends your campaign by 5 years, establishing a final new threat after the Gold Smoke Knight.  It gives the players an avenue to use their experience with the Gold Smoke Knight for further development and gear.

While seeking to take control of The Nightmare Forest, two of the Alien factions have decided to have a competition at a nearby dairy farm to see who can abduct more of these tasty cow creatures.

Objective:
Players are trying to collect the most cows from the Pasture, which is made up of cards indicating how many cows are in each space. Players take turns peaking at the cards to determine where the most cows are hiding, deviously moving the cows around, and strategically placing their tractor beam cards along their 2 sides of the pasture. Once all the tractor beams are in place, players compare the power value of the beams that intersect on each cow. The player with the higher total power value pulls that cow into their ship.  The player who collects the most cows is the winner.

Description from the publisher:

Dicecapades! 2nd Edition is the same great game as the original version but now with more cards! Think you can stack dice on your face? Will you dominate in arm wrestling? How about obscure facts? With a mixture of dice games, physical challenges, artistic creations, and trivia questions that will exercise both sides of your brain, you'll see why DICEcapades isn’t just the luck of the dice - it’s the smarts of the roller!

Need more cards?  No problem, get the DICEcapades Expansion Packs. Each expansion pack comes with 45 new cars to boost your Dicecapades! 2nd Edition game.

Expansion pack 1 is kids themed and includes 45 more cards for Dicecapades! 2nd Edition game.

Throw dice and go from water lily leaf to another and a frog shows how the body whirl. Stretching, balancing, jumping and various dynamic conditions are important for the development of the growing child.

Spinner surprise by accelerating or slowing down the going onwards on leaves.

Include: 18 track tiles, die, spinner and four frogs pawns.

This is a small box that contains several tear-off pads of sheets of several types, as well as 48 numbered cards with samples of famous people's handwriting on one side, and three choices as to whom the handwriting is on the opposite side. Also included is a booklet that goes into some detail about how handwriting analysis works, what can (and cannot) be deducted from it, as well as rules  for three variants of games that can be played, two of which use the cards included.

An unsavoury crime has been committed in Mintsby and you, the amateur sleuth Sherbet Holmes, aided by your dear friend Dr Winegum, are charged with solving the case.
You must move around the randomly generated playing area, search locations and memorise the suspects you encounter.
For, when you have eliminated the innocent, whoever remains, however improbable, must be the culprit.
A glance at your pocket watch shows you only have 12 hours remaining. Come, Winegum, the game is afoot...

The game is a take on a 'find the pairs' memory game. Find the pair of tiles of a suspect to eliminate them from your enquiries.
However, there are also other tiles which can help or hinder your investigation.
And you must solve the case against the clock.
Dice are used to move to and from locations so your choice of where to search may be restricted - fortunately Dr Winegum is there to help you.

Elemintary was an entry in the 2016 Mint Tin Design contest and it did rather well:

2nd place (equal): Best Overall Design
1st place: Best Artwork & Graphic Design
2nd place: Best Written Rules
4th place: Best Theme

boardgame: Naturally Disastrous: Discoverable Items Promo Pack
description:
This promo pack was available during Kickstarter.com campaigns and at conventions.  It contains the following:

Cloaking Device- The owner of the device cannot be seen by natives unless they are the only Alien not KO'd or Mutated.  If the Yellow Alien is in play, and all other Aliens are KO'd or Mutated, the cloaking device has no effect.

M.R.E. (Microwavable Ready Earth-food)- The Medical officer of the crew figured out that if you Microwave a lemon filled doughnut    allows you to heal all your wounds.  It is then discarded.

Malfunctioning Transporter- This functions similarly to a transporter but you have to roll the D10 and D8 die to decide where you end up.  You can also discard it to move a Native or Mutated Alien instead of an Alien.

The playable bookmark "Bookmark of Death" was part of the "Gloom: Digital Edition" kickstarter project.

Description from the kickstarter: Sour friendships and turn the tables with the playable bookmark, usable in a game of physical Gloom.

Bookmark Text:
Front:
During your turn, you may perform wither of the following actions as a free play.
Take one Untimely death card from the discard pile and add it to your hand.
Or...
Discard any number of Untimely Death cards from your hand.

Back:
After using the Bookmark of Death, pass it to the player on your left. Each player may only use the Bookmark of Death once per game.
At the end of the game, it is returned to its original owner.

boardgame: Carry On: Gear Collecting Card Game
description:
Description from the publisher:

Carry On is a gear collecting card game for the end of the world. Players compete to stash food and weapons, and seek aid from other survivors.

Each player starts with a "stash" of four cards. Each turn you can play a card from your stash. Different cards have different Actions. After an Action resolves, replace the card played with the top card from the Draw Pile. However, most Actions allow you to Replenish your stash in several strategic ways.

Different cards/items have different Actions, or rather different items cause different things within the game to happen. Tools like the Compass and Shovel allow players to search the Draw Pile and Discard Pile for a card to replenish their Stash. Weapons, like the Tactical Knife and Hand Gun let you intimidate opponents into giving you cards.

In addition to Items, there are also other Survivors within the deck that can you help you on your quest for survival gear. The Minimalist allows you to discard additional cards. The Pacifist gets rid of your opponents Weapons. The Anarchist let's you straight up steal from your opponent. And The Generalist allows you to swap out cards with an opponent.

To win, be the first player to collect all three pieces of radioactive protective gear - Hazmat Boots, a Hazmat Hood, and a Hazmat Suit.

Be the first player to answer four cards correctly to win. The game contains 96 question cards (18 Hard Ass and 26 each Who, What, and Where cards) with instructions. The reader announces four items that all appear to have a correlation. Players then choose which item they feel does not belong. Each player gets only one try at answering each card. The first player to yell out the correct answer earns the card. If no player answers correctly, the reader wins the card. The player to the left of the reader becomes the new reader.

The objective of the game is to obtain the maximum income from the factories and to apply the profits by investing in Villas or in the stock market.

In each round the investor:

     Rolls two dice and moves that number of spaces around the board (if the player is using the Spectrum app, he moves the number of spaces accordingly)
     Exports merchandise (if desired)
     Makes all the transactions he want.


The game ends when the time limit (decided by the players) is reached or the deck of cards is empty.

The winner is the investor with the most capital.

Description from the designer:

In these days of recession, it's not that easy to scrape an honest living. But there's always a neighbour’s lawn waiting to be mowed.
Fortunately you have inherited your grandfather’s MowMaster 2000, in it’s day the height of grass removal technology. Unfortunately that day was about 100 years ago and mowing with it now is tricky, due to the machine’s somewhat limited functions.
Mind you don't smash it into those trees, because it'll cost you for repairs and watch out for that dog. Above all try to steer clear of the customers' flower beds and prize vegetables as they get very upset at the sight of beheaded daffodils or mashed marrow.

Mower Mania is a mix of puzzle/strategy (working out best routes through the gardens), resource management (your mower functions) and luck (dice for movement and randomness of garden tile layout).

When you land on a long grass tile, you turn it over to reveal it nicely mowed. Landing on vegetables or flowers, however, results in loss of points (money). Land on a pot, or go too fast into a tree or garden feature, and you'll also sustain damage to your mower.

The amount you earn will be based on how much of the lawn you've successfully mowed (a minimum amount required to progress to the next garden) and how much damage you've caused. You'll then need to pay for mower repairs and to replenish mower functions you used.
.

Mower Mania was an entry in the 2016 Mint Tin Design Contest and did exceptionally well:

1st place: Best Overall Design
1st place: Best Theme
2nd place: Best Artwork & Graphic Design
3rd place: Best Written Rules
Top pick: of Guest Judge, Caroline Berg 
.

Thanks to all those at BoardgameGeek who played the game, and gave feedback and suggestions, during the contest, especially:
John Kean, lelo Arrowsmith. Scott Allen, GreenTea532, Laura Blachek, G. Uitz, Janine Viglietti, Marianne Waage, Benjamin Wack, Andrew Orel, Niklas Hook, Alex Johns, melbards and James Ronald Lo.

boardgame: Epic Resort: Villain's Vacation with Upgrade Kit
description:
Epic Resort: Villain's Vacation, the first expansion for Epic Resort, introduces villains, new tourists, new workers, and an epic monster!

This version was offered during the Kickstarter campaign for Epic Resort: Villain's Vacation.  Inside the box is an upgrade kit for 1st edition backers of Epic Resort to bring it up to the second edition.

The upgrade kit will be available via the publisher's website.
Retail copies of Epic Resort: Villain's Vacation will not have the upgrade kit included.  To be sure, on the back of the box there is a UPC sticker which indicates if it includes the upgrade kit.

boardgame: Vault Wars: Chromatic Cavern Promo Card
description:
Promo card for Vault Wars

Text:
You may keep this as a Gem Item.
When scoring, choose one Gem type and score victory points as if you have one more of that type

Cannot be sold, traded or exchanged. Storage Fees must be paid.

Description from the publisher:

The Destination Fun Combo Pack features three full expansions for each game in AEG's Destination Fun series: Trains, Planes, and Automobiles.

Trains: Coastal Tides features all-new cards and strategies for you to build the best rail system in Japan. This expansion not only features new cards, but also three entirely new boards. Two of the boards are designed specifically for two-player games, while the Okayama map is a new challenge for 2 to 4 players. Route bonus cards are also included, allowing you to score additional points for being the first to connect specific stations.

Planes: Round Trip features two new boards, providing more airports to explore. An entire deck of new playing cards allows players to experience the game with new actions and goals. New planes tokens gives players more options to customize airport boards with endless variety.

Automobiles: Racing Season includes three new race tracks, including two that are tighter for two-player games. New garage, pit, handling, performance, and engine cards offer more options for customizing your race car. Season campaign rules allows players to race a series of tracks to determine an overall winner, complete with unique driver skills and wealthy sponsors.

boardgame: Kingdom Death: Monster – Campaigns of Death Expansion
description:
CAMPAIGNS OF DEATH

HARDCOVER CAMPAIGN BOOK!

A hardcover book that has refined rules and guidelines for creating custom campaigns from the core game and our 12 current expansions.

Includes 3 x 30 Lantern year campaigns, each with their own timelines, and a sprinkle of unique innovations, story events,
settlement events, and new gear.

Also includes 2, 3-5 lantern year short format campaigns.

These campaigns will serve as examples of our new node system
and help teach you how to build your own campaigns, where its
appropriate to shift timeline events to prevent overcrowding, how to keep the difficulty balanced at the KD level and when is ok to add a boon type story event (like Hands of Heat).

Includes details about the nodes and power levels of all the current monsters (from the core game to the currently released expansions *NOT NEW EXPANSIONS).

Also includes my personal take on creating custom campaigns.

Content Pitch:
l x Hard cover Book (Est. 60-100 Pages)
1 X Ancient Butcher Monster (50mm or 100mm base)
3 x New Hybrid Armor Set Narrative Sculpts
80 x Game Cards
10 X Gear Cards
3 x campaign Specific Settlement Record Pads
1 x Blank Settlement Record Pad
2 x Character Record Pads
Generous Fistful of new Story Events!

The upgrade kit is for those with the first edition of Epic Resort to bring it up to speed with all the changes included in the second edition

It was originally offered in the Epic Resort: Villain's Vacation Kickstarter campaign. The upgrade kit was contained in the expansion box for those that backed at that level. There was a UPC sticker on the bottom of the box indicating it included the upgrade kit

The upgrade kit will be available from the publisher

Includes:
Workers - Bouncer (4), Journeyman (4), Squire (4), Versatilist (4)

Heroes - Ranger (2), Rogue (2)

Attractions - Tiki Hut (4), Dragonflame Tub (2), Timberline Trail (2), Street Parade (2), Gallery of Conquests (2), Canopy Zipline (2), Razorback Roast (2), Island Regata (2), Twilight Masquerade (2), Slaughter Coaster (2)

Other - Harbormaster Card (1), Attack! Cards (2), Monster Cards (30)

boardgame: Time Management: The Time Management Game – Department Of Company Time Or Relativity Promo
description:
A set of 2 promo cards for the Time Management draw deck included as part of the Meta Games II Kickstarter campaign.

The cards are both wild backs (so can be past, present or future).  When picked up, they are immediately played to the office, the player gains an hour and a new card.  Any project completed using the wild office cards are worth 1 hour less.

boardgame: Trick-Taking: The Trick-Taking Game – Clairvoyant promo
description:
Promo pack of 7 Clairvoyant cards for Trick-Taking that were included as part of the Meta Games II Kickstarter. These should be put in the deck at the start of the game and 7 random cards removed after shuffling.

When played, the Clairvoyant card effect allows the player to look at three random cards from another players hand.

boardgame: Traitor Mechanic: The Traitor Mechanic Game – Alien Spacecraft
description:
A single job card for Traitor Mechanic, shuffle into the Job deck at game start.

Originally a promo item included for backers of the Meta Games II Kickstarter

Maha Yodha was designed as a fun and quick 2 player game that also features tactical maneuvering and hand management. It's an epic battle-themed strategic card game based on ancient stories from Hindu Mythology.

In expansion cards, we have introduced weapon of mass destruction and artifacts. An artifact is a powerful force multiplier the is permanently deployed onto the battlefield.

A Weapon of Mass Destruction cards requires a specific Maha Yodha scroll cards. These are single use cards.

boardgame: Kingdom Death: Monster – Honeycomb Weaver Expansion
description: The Honeycomb Weaver expansion comes with 2 forms.  The 10 year version is a node 1 monster that can be fought lantern year 1 of the campaign!  The 100 year form is a node 3 monster that provides a much more powerful set of armor and weapons.

Holiday Special 2016

During the darkest times, mysterious objects occasionally appear throughout the galaxy. Fragments of old chronicles suggest that these forebode the return of the Elders. However, the stashes quickly flicker away from the base reality again. Only the quickest civilizations can benefit from their rewards...

Eclipse: Anticipation of the Elders is a print and play expansion.

Player materials:
24 Elder Stash Tiles

Preparations:
Shuffle the Stash Tiles face down (showing the round numbers). Take one random tile of each number and place them face down on the respective spaces (2-9) on the Round Track. Discard the rest without looking at them.

Rules:
First player to pass in the Action Phase immediately draws the tile on the next round space on the Round Track. He resolves the immediate effect and discards the tile. The round then continues normally.

Plunder! is a fast-paced card game with a lot of "take that" action. Your goal is to steal treasures and pirate crew members from your fellow pirates by playing cards to attack other players and to defend yourself from attacks.

The game has a simple card-playing mechanic at its core, but outside of your turn you can play reaction cards. These influence cards played by other players. Play them at the right time, so you can benefit from the other pirates' actions!
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
Two tradesmen vye for business on ladders of ever increasing height.

Play cards to expand your ladder and score points, or to hinder your opponent.

A card can only be played if the number of spots on its edge matches the card that it joins.
A card is placed so that it either joins the top card of a ladder or overlaps it half way. Some cards may be rotated before being placed and those with creatures on have special actions.

.
Ladders was an entry in the 2016 18 Card Microgame Contest

User summary
天秤にかけろ (Tenbin ni Kakero) in English would mean 'Take a Balance'.

The whole nation is divided by two sides: republicans and monarchists, and it's time to determine which side will rule the nation. You have some connections with national magnates who have a certain extent of influence on both sides. Your future will be bright if you have strong connections with the winning side.

In the game of Tenbin ni Kakero, players secretly vote for one side or two, and when the winning side is determined, a player who has the card with the most influence on that side wins the game.

At beginning of the game, players have several magnate cards and backing cards. Each magnate cards has a different number (1-20) to indicate how much influence that magnate has on which side. Backing cards are either vote card or special cards. Vote cards are numbered 0-3 to indicate vote values, and special cards have a specific power to manipulate votes in play or magnate card in one player's hand.

During your turn, you may place one vote card on one side face down to add some votes, or play one special card for a special effect.

When all backing cards have been played, reveal all vote cards and determine the winning side. The victory goes to the player who has the magnate card with the most influence on the winning side.

Actually, the two sides are not necessarily the republicans and the monarchists. This game has two blank cards so that players may name two sides by their preference, such as pro-Brexit and anti-Brexit, cat person and dog person.

Description from the publisher:'
今この国は「君主制派」と「共和制派」に分かれて争っている状況です。(これは説明の為です。実際は「離脱派・残留派」「犬派・猫派」等、グループで楽しめるものにしてください)
・「有力者カード」は1〜20の数字が振ってあり、1が共和制派の最有力者、20が君主制派の最有力者となります。
・「共和制派」は1に近い方がより強力な有力者ですので、共和制派が実権を握れば、20は最弱となります。
・「君主制派」は20に近い方がより強力な有力者ですので、君主制派が実権を握れば、1は最弱なります。
・ゲーム開始時に配られた「有力者カード」を見て、どちら側の有力者と繋がっているかを確認してゲームをプレイします。
・どちらかの陣営に「数字カード」を裏向きで置くことで、その陣営を支援（投票したと考えると分かりやすいかも知れません）をしていきます。
・全員のすべての手札がなくなったら、「数字カード」を集計して合計が多かった陣営が実権を握ります。
・「実権を握った側の有力者カード」で最も有力なカードを持つプレイヤーが勝利です。
・例えば「君主制派」が実権を握った場合は、20に近い有力者カードを持っているプレイヤーの勝利となります。
・ゲームに変化を持たせるために、「リムーブカード」と「ニューリレーションカード（鞍替えカード）」が用意されています。

boardgame: Hikari to Yami to (光と闇と)
description:
User summary
光と闇と (Hikari to Yami to) in English is 'Light & Darkness'.

In the game of Hikari to Yami to, players play cards to score points, and the highest scorer wins the game.

At the beginning of the game, players have the same number of cards. Each card belongs to one of two sides: light side or dark side, and players' sides are determined by majority of card by side. If you have more light cards than dark cards, your side is the light. You are neutral if you have the same number of cards for both sides.

Players take turns playing a card from their hand, and the card affects all appropriate players written in the text. Most cards refer to the side of players, so you should be conscious of your side. The affected players lose or gain points by the card effect. Then the played card is discarded, which may cause the player to change their side.

The game ends when all players have played all but one card. Apply the remaining card effect only to you, and the highest scorer wins the game.

Publisher's summary
・手札となるカードには「光のカード」と「闇のカード」があります。
・どちらのカードが手札に多いかによって自分の属性が決まります。
・「光のカード」が多ければ、「光の者」です。
・「闇のカード」が多ければ「闇の者」です。
・「光と闇のカードが同数」の場合は「中立の者」となります。
・カードを使用すると効果が発生し、同じ属性のプレイヤーすべてに影響します。
・例えば「光の者」に効果があるカードが使用されると、自分だけでなく他のプレイヤーも含めて、すべての「光の者」に効果が及ぶことになります。
これを繰り返していく、とてもとても簡単なカードゲームです。だけど、ちょっとしたジレンマも発生します。
「自分だけ」に得点を入れようとしても、他の同じ属性のプレイヤーにも得点が入ってしまったり。
他のプレイヤーが何気なく使用した効果で、自分が大きな得点を得たりと、もどかしい感じのゲームになっています。

The team-based social deduction game Spy King includes three royal families as well as a family of spies who wants to defeat the royals. If the spies defeat all the kings, they win; if the spies are defeated, then the family with the highest value wins.

A moderator is needed to play Spy King, but this player can also be part of a family.

User summary
キャベツ鑑定 (Kyabetsu Kantei) in English is Cabbage Appraisal.

Cabbage Appraisal is card game where players bid for the right to appraise opponents' cabbages.

At beginning of the game, each player has 200 coins and the same deck of five cabbage cards, each of which is numbered -30, -20, -10, +10, or +20, indicating the quality of the cabbage.

Each round, players take a random cabbage card from the deck, and place it on the table face-down. Only the player who placed the card may look at its face value. Then they bid for the right to appraise opponents' cabbages.

The appraiser (highest bidder) may reveal an opponent's card. If its value is negative, the appraiser takes that many coins from that opponent as a fine for distributing bad cabbages. If positive, the appraiser must pay the opponent that many. The appraiser may repeat revealing until +20 is revealed or they wish to stop.

After the tenth round, when the deck runs out twice, the player with the most coins win the game.

Description from the publisher:
『キャベツ鑑定』はルールの簡単な「競りゲーム」です。
キャベツ農家になって、ガッポリお金を稼ぎましょう！　ただし、あなたが市場に出荷するキャベツは、良いものばかりとは限りません。
悪いキャベツを出荷したことが分かったら、罰金を払わなくてはなりません！
上手くキャベツを売り抜けるのはどのプレイヤーになるのでしょうか？

・ルール
ゲームは10ラウンド行ないます。各ラウンド、キャベツの鑑定権を競ってください。10ラウンド目が終了した時点で、一番多くコインを持っているプレイヤーが勝利者です。
designers: Ryota Miyagawa (宮川 リョウタ)
publishers: Rommel Games (ロンメルゲームズ)

description: In this exciting tower building game, players take turns rolling the dice to determine what pieces will be added.  The player who makes the tower fall loses the game!  Crafted from sustainably forested beechwood, this game is suitable for children over 3 years old, but is fun for the whole family!

User summary
をかしや (Okashiya) in English is a 'Confectionery Store'.

In the game of Okashiya, you are running a confectionery store, and your goal is to score the most victory points by delivering confectionery to customers.

In a square grid board (town map), there are several wholesalers and customers. In order to deliver confectionery to customers, you first visit wholesalers to buy ingredients, then visit customer and spend required ingredients, and finally you can score victory points.

Each turn, you have five action points. You may spend one action point to
1) Move: Move to adjacent square.
2) Buy: If you are in a wholesaler square, spend 2 gold to buy an ingredient.
3) Deliver: If you are in a customer square, spend required ingredients to deliver your products, then gain victory points and some positive effect.
4) Build a shop: Spend some gold to build a shop. It grants you some movement advantage, and you score mid-game bonus if you have built the most shops in a certain district.
5) Income: Gain 3 gold.

At the end of game, the highest scorer wins the game.

Publisher's summary
「をかしや」は、手番で限られた数のアクションを行い、
材料を購入して和菓子を届け、勝利点を得るボードゲームです。

手番では下記の中から複数のアクションを組み合わせて行うことができます。
・移動・材料の購入・注文の達成・店コマの配置・収入

これらを実行し、和菓子の材料をあちこちのお店を巡って集め、公開されている共通の注文カードを他プレイヤーよりも早く達成すると、ボーナスや勝利点がもらえます。
また、ボードに自分の店コマを置くことによって、ゲーム中に3回ある決算での勝利点の獲得や、プレイヤーのコマが移動するときに一気に移動できたり、臨時収入を得るチャンスがあります。

boardgame: The Red Dragon Inn: 2016 SlugCrew Minor Reward
description:
Adds 3 drinks to the drink deck.

Golden Stout (also appears in The Red Dragon Inn Smorgasbox Collector's edition)
Prognosticating Punch (also appears in The Red Dragon Inn Smorgasbox Collector's edition)
Timewarp Tonic (also appears in The Red Dragon Inn Smorgasbox)

Description from the publisher:

Land of Medievals is a Medieval Fantasy card board game with mechanics such as combat, dice rolling and improvement cards.

Fight against fantastic creatures and opponent players, siege enemy castles and become the one and only King of the Land.

Inspired by ancient mythologies the game offers a unique gameplay, you will need to earn Army Units and Improvement cards for your faction, kill creatures at the outside encounters and make intelligent strategies using your cards to defeat opponent players.

Dark Dealings: Dwarven Delve takes players underground as the dark lords of subterranean dungeons and fortresses. The heroes are bent on chasing the dark lords from their lands, sending their mightiest dwarves to evict you from your lair! You have to use your new goblin- and troll-powered defenses to hold back the siege, or get evicted (again) — and let's be honest, moving is a pain.

This expansion contains 54 new cards, including new pesky dwarven heroes with a new ability called "TOUGH", and goblin- and troll-themed defenses with tricks up their sleeves!

User summary
In Japan, a buffet-style restaurant is called "Viking Restaurant". ケーキバイキング! (Ke-ki Baikingu) means 'cake baking' but it is phonetically equivalent to "Cake Viking" and Japanese would understand the pun if you pronounced it that way. The designer suggests an English title Dessert Buffet!.

In the game of Dessert Buffet!, players are a group of girls who have come to a all-you-can-eat cake buffet. Actually, all of them are on a diet so they don't want to eat too much. That's why they began to foist fattening cakes to one another!

Each player initially has three cake cards. Each turn, you pass a card from your hand to any other player. If it causes that player to have a pair of the same cake, that player has eaten the cake and takes the calories written on the card.

Some cards are special. They can make you or another player gain or lose calories. When you draw such a card, follow the instruction written on it.

At the end of each turn, players with fewer than three cards must fill their hand up to three cards. If that causes a pair, more calories are added as well.

When the draw pile runs out, the player with the lowest calories eaten wins the game.

The game is packaged like a cake, 10cm round and 4cm deep.

Description from the publisher:
プレイヤーはケーキ食べ放題の店にやってきた女子グループになります。
しかし、全員こっそりダイエット中なので、あまりカロリーを摂取したくありません。
手札（皿）にある高カロリーのケーキカードを、他のプレイヤーに押し付けましょう。
同じカードが二枚揃ったプレイヤーはそれを食べたことになり、カードを場に出します。
山札がなくなった時点で一番摂取カロリーの低いプレイヤーが勝者です。

In this  fast playing, opponent-crushing card game featuring The United States’ most popular political figures, players take turns strategically building their presidential asset sets until someone completes three sets and wins the game. 
The deck contains event cards used to destroy your opponent’s reputations and press cards used to leverage your assets and steal voters! Don’t forget to watch your own back as your opponents can do the same to you and your destinies will change in an instant!

boardgame: Sherlock Holmes and the Case of the Silver Bullet: Murder Mystery Game
description:
The players sit around a table for dinner (a real dinner) and each one is assigned a role within the game, and is given an envelope with a letter indicating something that only his character knows, as well as some instructions in the form of a menu that indicates how the dinner will be taking place. The game includes a DVD that seems to contain some instructions on events that will be happening during the dinner.

As the dinner progresses, players have to find out who has been the killer of a girl.

Everyone's a suspect when murder is on the menu. And with a Classic Detective Murder Mystery you have everything you need to serve up a perfect murder dinner party. First the invites, you choose which of your friends play which devious character. Then the shopping list, menu suggestions and recipes. Plus of course .....a really tasty murder to solve. We guarentee that it will be drop dead fun, and by the end of the evening one (or more) of the suspects, your 'friends' will be caught out as the murderer.

The night of March 1905 saw a full moom rise over the remote Hampshire village of Thor Bridge. Here there are still some prepared to celebrate the coincidence of the old pre-Christian festivals of Eostrara and Lady Day. For weeks the village has been rife with rumours of pagan rituals, tales of the spitits and demons that would stalk the Earth.

But even on this haunted and mysterious night, no one could have predicted the form that evil was to take. Until the body of Maria Gibson was discovered. The Brazilian wife of the millionaire who lives in the big house, she has been shot dead with a single silver bullet.

Only one man can solve this bizarre case that seems shrouded in the folklore of three continents. And now Sherlock Holmes has gathered the suspects together to uncover the truth of what happened that terrible night.

The Suspects are:

J Neil Gibson - The American gold minig tycoon who moved into Thor Place five yeasrs ago with his exotic wife.

Mary-Lou gibson - J Neil's daughter from his first marriage.

Rev Marlow Bates - The vicar of Thor Bridge, an authority on the old religion and it's most implacable foe.

Grace Dunbar - The beautiful young governess who looks after the two children of the Gibsons.

Chastity Darling - A chilhood friend of Grace who has come to visit her in the old manor house.

Juan Pinto - The younger brother of the murdered woman, he is visiting England from his home in Brazil.

Countess Von Orlock - A Hungarian noblewoman who has know J Neil Gibson for many years.

Jack E Jackson - A South African gold miner who has made and lost his fortune many times over.

The system from Nobunaga's Most Dangerous Time and Frederick's Most Dangerous Time comes to the Chinese Three Kingdoms era.


Issue game of Game Journal #58
family: Theme: Romance of the Three Kingdoms

description:
Storm Over the Japanese Homeland was originally published in Japanese in Game Journal #60. Translated and republished in Special Ops #8 as Avenge Pearl Harbor.

It simulates Operation DOWNFALL, the hypothetical invasion of Japan in November 1945. The game uses the same basic system as What Price Glory?, Operational cards are used on both attack and defense.

Game components include one 22" x 34" map, 200 counters, and 32 cards.

(adapted from MMP website)
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
Simulate the entire war of the Imperial navy and army in Asia and the Pacific. Attempt the heretofore impossible, such as forcing a decisive fleet battle in the East Pacific without the Pearl Harbor attack, or focusing your war effort agains the Soviet Union or United Kingdom. Rise to the challenge!

Using a variant of the Paths of Glory CDG system, GEAW covers the Second World War in the Pacific and Asia from July 1941 to December 1945 in seasonal turns, with four scenarios.

Description from the designer:

Chibi Quest! is an exciting semi-cooperative game for 1-6 players, where you play as a Chibi, going on quests to save your lands from monsters! After your Chibi is prepped for battle, it’s up to you to save your lands from as many monsters as you can! Of course, no quest is complete without an epic boss fight! You must work together to defeat it! By the end of the adventure, one of you will be known throughout the lands as the CHAMPION CHIBI!

How to play overview :


     Equip! You quickly search through the available armor in market, paying for any armor you wish to keep! Some armor will protect you and give you lots of hit points, some will give you better dice to fight with, and others will give you special powers! But you must decide quickly... because you only have 1 minute to equip your Chibi for battle!



     Battle! Then you battle monsters! You do this by collecting all the dice shown on your armor cards. Some monsters are stronger than others and you must meet the sword requirements to beat the monster, otherwise they damage YOU! If you defeat a monster you then follow the actions listed on that monsters card. Sometimes they give you gold, sometimes they cause you to loose armor, and sometimes they become your sidekick and help you fight!



     Shop until you drop! Or in this case, fight until you faint! The first Chibi to cover up all his or her hit points faints! At this point you finish out the round so everyone has an equal number of turns.



    BOSS FIGHT! Then everyone will have another minute to equip their Chibis with fresh, undamaged armor.  Afterwards, you must fight together to defeat the BOSS!



     WIN or LOSE! If you defeat the BOSS you are all winners! But there is only ONE Champion Chibi. You count up all your points, and whoever has the most is known throughout the lands as the Champion Chibi!

boardgame: Kharnage: The Dark Rampage – Army Expansion
description:
The undead have come to join the fight for the hill and haunt the other armies.

Kharnage: The Dark Rampage – Army Expansion is an additional army for use in Kharnage, which allows up to five players to compete all at the same time — or even more players should you have additional armies on hand.

"The Sex Romance Game for Two"

GAME RULES:
Shuffle the deck of 10 cards and place them face down. Players take turns rolling the die, moving around the board and carrying out actions based on the spaces he or she lands on.

To win you just have to reach the last spot: "The Sexy Rendez vous" first!

The game includes a 6 3/4 in. x 9 1/2 in. gameboard, 10 Rendez vous scenario cards, 2 game markers, a die, and easy-to-follow instructions.

boardgame: I Can't Even With These Spectacular Supers
description:
I Can't Even is a series of card games that all share one rule: The player with the highest odd score wins! For example, if the game ended with players at 9, 16, 20, and 22 points, the player with 9 points would win! Each game can be played as a standalone item or shuffled together with one or more other games in the series.

I Can't Even With These Spectacular Supers has 49 hero cards: Crusher, Dr. Chance, Ra, Reactor, Scrapper, Sensor, Vigilante, each appearing seven times.
family: Organizations: Game Designers of North Carolina

boardgame: I Can't Even With These Marvelous Supers
description:
I Can't Even is a series of card games that all share one rule: The player with the highest odd score wins! For example, if the game ended with players at 9, 16, 20, and 22 points, the player with 9 points would win! Each game can be played as a standalone item or shuffled together with one or more other games in the series.

I Can't Even With These Marvelous Supers has 49 hero cards: Dash, Faultline, Gargoyle, Mega-Mite, Obsidia, Silver Star, Sun Spear, each appearing seven times.
family: Organizations: Game Designers of North Carolina

boardgame: Munchkin: Playmat – The Flower of Love
description: Munchkin Playmat: The Flower of Love is a 28"×14" playmat that showcases Flower conversing with a love-struck shark. It includes spots to keep track of your Class, Race, level, and all your monster-slaying gear, and it features an in-game bonus: Once per game, you may play a monster from your hand to add its Level to your side in combat by discarding the number of Treasures printed on its card.

This 1923 publication was designed to transform Babcock Rules Mah-Jongg into a card game. (As the rules book notes, Mr. J. P. Babcock "originated the name Mah-Jongg, adapted all that was best of the Chinese forms of play, and added the Arabic numerals".) The rule book credits Douglas Lawson for having "correlated" the Babcock rules with card play.

The 164-card deck replicates the mix of tiles in the classic game. It includes four cards for 1-9 for each of the three suits (Dots, Bamboo, Characters), as well as four each of the North, South, East and West winds and Red, Green and White Dragons, plus the 8 Flower cards. As in the classic rules, players try to collect sets or 3 or 4 of a kind or in a sequence, with bonuses for various combinations and special tiles.

Instead of ivory or bone scoring sticks, the card game uses thin colored wooden rods for scoring. The game includes 40 short green sticks worth 2 each, 32 medium-length yellow sticks worth 10, 36 longer red sticks worth 100, and 8 long blue sticks worth 500.

While the rule book focuses on the Standard and Royal games for four players, it does include variant rules for 3, 5 and 6 players. (The Babcock rules were largely superceded in North America in the 1930s by American Mah-Jongg rules.)

The Brood evolves yet again! This Xenoshyft card expansion brings a host of new threats and challenges to the game, adding even more danger and excitement. With 5 new enemies per wave of terror and 3 brand new horrifying bosses to savage your team, the Brood has truly reached a new level of lethality.

WAVE 1 ENEMIES [4x of Each Basic Enemy, 1x of Each Boss]
•  Mist Tracker
•  Marsh Scavenger
•  Moss Pincer
•  Deathripper Stalker
•  Acid Worm
•  The Deathbloom [BOSS]
WAVE 2 ENEMIES [4x of Each Basic Enemy, 1x of Each Boss]
•  Poison Host
•  Gloom Devil
•  Corpse-Larva Swarm
•  Mist Ambusher
•  Ironhide Spawn
•  Razormaw Monstrosity [BOSS]
WAVE 3 ENEMIES [4x of Each Basic Enemy, 1x of Each Boss]
•  Razorspine Charger
•  Ironhide Behemoth
•  Strangleroot Lasher
•  Mind Reaver Drone
•  Eclipse Horror
•  The Blood Harvester [BOSS]

This set contains exclusive stretch goals from the Xenoshyft: Dreadmire Kickstarter campaign. It contains the following:

18 x Full-Art Brood Boss Cards
20 x Legendary Item Cards (Metallic-Framed)
14 x Exclusive NorTec Hero Cards to upgrade your Dreadmire troops (Metallic-Framed)
36 x Exclusive NorTec Troops (4 types, 9 of each)
8 x Over-sized Division Cards
2 x Exclusive Division Cards
1 x Rules Reference Card
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

boardgame: Dead Panic: Laser Sight Promo Card
description:
Make your shots in Dead Panic count for more with a Laser Sight.
