Play this promo card when using a Rifle, Pistol, or Shotgun to deal one additional point of damage to the target Zombie.

In Executive Producer, players take control of a Hollywood movie studio, buying Scripts and hiring Actors and Directors to appear in them with the goal of earning box office profits, critical acclaim and prestigious awards! Players can also use their unique studio abilities to gain an edge, or play Hollywood News cards to improve their movies - or sabotage opponents!

boardgame: Shimmer and Shine: Genie Friends Forever Game
description:
Shimmer and Shine Genie Friends Forever Game is based on an excitingly-new, animated preschool series on Nick Jr.

Players join in on magical adventures that sometimes don't go quite as planned!
It's up to the three girls to work collaboratively to solve problems as they discover their way and learn from their mistakes

The game encourages counting and social interaction. No reading required

Catan: Seefahrer is an expansion for Catan that includes many water hexes — as well as many different water-hex-heavy variant set-ups — in which players can build shipping lanes, which function similar to roads.

Catan: Seefahrer – 20 Jahre Jubiläums-Edition includes four new scenarios packaged under the title "Die Legende der Seeräuber".

From the publishers site (unofficially translated from German), announced January, 30th 2017:
For 20 years the most popular Catan expansion "Seafarers" adds with a few additional rules endless new possibilities and permutations to play. For the anniversary this May this fascinating expansion will now be released with additional bonus material.

The Anniversery edition includes additionally four new scenarieos: „Legend of the Sea Robbers“. The intense narrative tells, how the sea robbers came to Catan and is connected by an ongoing plot. Therefore, a new way to play is introduced to the CATAN universe.

The scenario expansion „Legend of the Sea Robbers“ will be available as well as a separate box without the content of the Seafarers expansion in selected stores as well as the Catan Shop.

It's time for an exciting duck race on the lake!

Each player in Quack Quack gets a headband with a wobbly duck, then sits down. The start command "Quack Quack!" starts all players moving at the same time. If one moves with the duck dangling from their headband near the eggs, the magnet on the duck attracts the eggs, which the player must then deposit as quickly as possible on their own water lily. If no eggs of a color are left in the pond, the player with that color has won this crazy race!

boardgame: Steal The Show! Act II: Theatrical Party Card Game
description:
Are you ready for Act II? Play as iconic characters as you challenge players to a Battle of Strength or a Battle of Wits using Shakespearean insults and the art of bluffing!

Steal The Show! is a race to collect five comedy masks, and the first player to do so wins. If a player receives five tragedy masks first, however, they're eliminated from the game. In more detail, each player takes on the role of a character. Characters have unique gameplay abilities as well as a strength value (used in a Battle of Strength) and a wit value (used in a Battle of Wits). One location card is always in play and changes throughout the course of the game; location cards modify the rules of the game.

Action cards (Insults) are used during battle to enhance the strength and wit value of the character as well as insert some trickery to mix! Don't forget bluffing! Players can use bluffing techniques to assist with a numerical boost as well!

Steal The Show! Act II: Theatrical Party Card Game is a standalone expansion for Steal The Show! that features new characters, new locations, and new actions/insults that can be played on their own or in combination with the first game.

boardgame: A Game of Thrones: The Card Game (Second Edition) – Watchers on the Wall
description:
Description from the publisher:

Travel north and join the Night’s Watch with Watchers on the Wall! Within this expansion, the Night’s Watch explores new and existing themes, as you find new ways to defend the Wall, new ways to conscript your opponent’s characters, and plenty of rewards for focusing on the three branches of the Night’s Watch: Rangers, Stewards, and Builders. Along with the nameless men who swell the ranks of the Night’s Watch, you’ll find new versions of iconic characters from throughout the saga, including Jon Snow, Old Bear Mormont, Maester Aemon, and Samwell Tarly.

And although the focus of this expansion rests on the Night’s Watch, you’ll find two new non-loyal cards for every other faction, expanding your options for any alliance. You’ll also receive an assortment of neutral cards portraying the Night’s Watch’s most common enemies, the Wildlings. With seven new plots (one loyal to the Night’s Watch) rounding out the expansion, Watchers on the Wall offers new pathways to victory for every deck.
family: Books: A Song of Ice and Fire
Collectible: Living Card Game (Fantasy Flight Games)
Game: A Game of Thrones – The Card Game (LCG)

description:
Description from the publisher:

You and your fellow adventurers have completed what you thought was a typical dungeon crawl. With the dungeon cleared and boss defeated, the treasure is yours for the taking... or so you thought. There's a disagreement as to who gets what. Talking leads to arguing. Arguing leads to yelling. Suddenly, there's a twinkle of light and a flash of steel. Everyone reactively jumps back, weapons at the ready. Each person eyes the other as they all stare back knowingly. There's no turning back now. It's now become a Dungeon Brawl.

Dungeon Brawl is a customizable, take-that, free for all for 3 to 5 players. In this 30 to 60 minute game, players draft 1 of 5 classes then customize it by constructing a deck consisting of weapons, armor, items, and abilities. Draft your treasure, then drop into the dungeon with your so-called "friends". Your cards determine what actions you can take. Deal out damage in order to give your opponents Wounds. Wounds are added to your deck when it runs out, slowing you down by impeding your hand size. Traps will effect the dungeon every round, helping, but mostly hindering you. Once the Wound deck runs out, the game ends and the adventurer with the least amount of Wounds takes all the treasure and is declared the winner!

Description from the designer:

GRENADE! is a quick card and dice game for 2-4 people.

All players have a hidden card on their hand that matches one on the table. By roling and moving dice they try to eliminate the other players.

Description from the publisher:

This game is based on one of the most unconventional ideas ever to have come out of international warfare – dazzle ships! Inspired by the shock and awe of Cubism and Vorticism, the dazzle ships of the First World War used a striking form of bold camouflage to confuse enemy attackers.

Use this wonderfully oddball idea to confuse your opponent and evade attacks on your fleet with this new version of the ever-popular battleships game. Includes two 64-page map-and-grid books for playing the game and a 16-page booklet explaining the history of the dazzle ships and the artistic inspiration behind them.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Bored Room is an easy-to-play, but hard-to-win party game pitting you against your co-workers in a series of challenges taking place during a work meeting! That's right, your company is going to be paying you while you play this game. That's definitely a win-win! *cough*

Three dice are rolled before the meeting, comparing the results to the list of activities. All players attend the meeting and complete as many of the rolled challenges as possible to score points. After the meeting ends, players reconvene to tally up scores and declare a winner.

With multiple modes of gameplay and included badges of honor, you'll be sure to liven up your stuffy work environment in no time!

Be quick to trap the zombies that have invaded the garden. Observe, be alert and use the hunters to help you trap the zombies. To win the game, accumulate as many game cards as possible.

Goal of the game: be the player who has accumulated the most cards at the end of the game. In turn, each player places a card on the central stack of cards. As soon as a player returns a
zombie hunter, the next player must provide the same number of cards as indicated by the hunter. The player who has returned the zombie hunter picks up the whole central stack and puts it after his own stack of cards. Keep an eye open, because as soon as two identical cards follow each other, the first player to hit the central stack picks it up and deposits it in his prison. These cards belong to him for the rest of the game.

Bloody Mary, a promotional character card for BANG! included with Spielbox #6 of 2016, has this unique ability: Each time your BANG! card is Missed!, draw one card from the deck.

This card is also contained in the expansion BANG! Armed & Dangerous, although with a different artwork.

boardgame: Star Wars: X-Wing Miniatures Game – C-ROC Cruiser Expansion Pack
description:
Description from the publisher:

Safeguard your fortunes with the C-ROC Cruiser Expansion Pack for X-Wing! Designed for use in the game's Cinematic Play and Epic Play formats, the C-ROC Cruiser Expansion Pack contains one C-ROC Cruiser miniature, one M3-A Interceptor miniature with an alternate paint scheme, seven ship cards, thirty upgrade cards, and all the maneuver dials, damage decks, tokens, and game pieces that you need to fly your C-ROC Cruiser and its escort to battle. Additionally, the expansion includes a new campaign for Cinematic Play in which your C-ROC draws the ire of either the Galactic Empire or Rebel Alliance.
family: Game: Star Wars X-Wing Miniatures Game

description:
These promo cards for Dragon Whisperer function as a mini-expansion. They function similarly to Dragon Rage cards and feature unique artwork and new icons.

From the errata:
"The Promo Cards are used like the Dragon Rage cards. The difference is that, when used, the person who plays the last promo card wins the trick instead of it just cancelling the round."

These promo cards were available to certain tiers of backers during the Kickstarter.

Detective Boso has been murdered! Suspicious falls on some of the main characters of his previous investigations.
Every player will have to gather as much clues as he can to solve the riddle and bring the suspected to the trial: you choose randomly the color of your suspect and noone knows it. Every players receive four cards with three cubes random coloured: every cube is a clue that lead to the suspected of the same color. You choose to play the card that maximize the gathering of clues for your suspected pay attention not to be discovered and minimizing the clues for the suspected of other colors.
The game is based on a mix of strategy and luck, but mostly on bluffing skills.

Help the detective Boso, convincing him of the correctness of your hypothesis emerged from your investigation: move (every move is a clue) your token towards the case solution as you gather clues that prove that the character that you suspect is the murder. Every player will have to gather as much clues as he can to solve the riddle and bring the suspected to the trial: you choose randomly the color of your suspect and noone knows it. Every players receive four cards with three cubes random coloured: every cube is a clue that lead to the suspected of the same color. You choose to play the card that maximize the gathering of clues for your suspected pay attention not to be discovered and minimizing the clues for the suspected of other colors.

You are detectives and have 24 turns (every turn is one hour from the title) to found all clues and witness to stop the villains. Every turn you will take a SMS card that will do something with game. Sometimes it could help you - you can have extra steps to go in this round. Sometimes it can force you to find some clue again or to go to some specific place to recover something. You win if you will find all 16 clues/witness, before the ending of last turn.

Co-operative and very random game. Good to play with kids.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description: A fun, abstract family game for 2-4 players that adds a multiplayer twist to the traditional peg solitaire game. To win Campo Bello, remove your pieces from the board first. Pieces are arrayed in four triangular home areas that surround a center diamond. Pieces are removed by jumping - jumping one's own piece causes the jumped piece to be removed. Jumping an opponent's piece allows you to remove any of your pieces from the board.

As a Pokemon themed version of rummy, the object of the game is to be the first player to earn 100 points by making matches and runs. The game features round pokeball-backed cards that are numbered and suited like traditional cards and can be used for other card games. It comes with rules for a Pokemon variant of Rummy in which runs are created with different evolution stages of Pokemon characters, aces are wild cards, and jokers allow you to take a card from another player's hand. These four joker cards feature characters from the first season of the Pokemon TV series: Ash, Misty, Brock, and Team Rocket and the represented Pokemon are all from the Kanto region: Bulbasaur, Ivysaur, Venusaur, Charmander, Charmeleon, Charizard, Squirtle, Wartortle, Blastoise, Pidgey, Pidgeotto, Pidgeot, Pikachu, Raichu, Ekans, Arbok, Clefairy, Clefable, Jigglypuff, Wigglytuff, Meowth, Persian, Koffing, and Weezing.

Description from the publisher:

The Fastest Card Game!! (maybe). MANBOU(Sunfish) x MAMBO!! High speed battle in 60 sec. >

You must place your cards quicker than other players on the rules to the field.
And you bring up a MANBOU(Sunfish) from an EGG to an ADULT FISH.
You win if your card’s points are less than other player’s when GAME is OVER.

Description from the publisher:

Gem Duel is one of the card game to duel with your opponent, using variety beautiful cards of sealed creatures into GEM.
These creatures are from myths around of the world.
You and your opponent summon each 3 creatures one by one, sort each summoned creatures , and battle at the same time.
You win by direct attack to your opponent after destroying all of his shields.

Note : You can destroy the shields and attack directly in same color creature’s battle only.

Orc Office Party is a quick card game for 2-4 players per deck with games lasting an average of 15-30 minutes wherein players compete to survive in an office environment managed by dastardly orc supervisors.
In this card game, players take on the role of an employee scrambling to survive the most hostile work environment, a company run entirely by orcs! Along with surviving the dangerous traps and time consuming assignments, players compete to see who can slack off the most and still remain employed at the end of the work week. Players begin with timecards, each one representing an hour they can slack off and still get paid.

The game plays 2 to 4 players with the base deck. It only takes about 5 minutes to learn, and only about 15 minutes for a single game.

Description from the designer:

The Most Brilliant Mage is a short CCG-like game for two players that can be played with a standard deck of cards.  In the game, the players are Mages casting spells and summoning the elements to fight for them.

Each turn, players cast spells and/or summon elements in an attempt to have the most power in play.  Players are fighting a tug-of-war for points, similar to games like Twilight Struggle, and if a player gets to 3 points, she wins.

The game was designed to mimic the more meta-decisions involved in CCGs while being able to be played with just 18 cards.  Strategies like Aggro and Control are present in the game.  There are also options for pre-built decks and drafting.

While it does not have the depth of true CCGs, The Most Brilliant Mage is a brain-burner that can scratch that same itch.

boardgame: Star Wars: Destiny – Spirit of Rebellion Booster Pack
description:
Description from the publisher:

“This is a rebellion, isn’t it?”
   –Jyn Erso, Rogue One

The Star Wars saga has always centered on the timeless struggle between good and evil. At different points throughout the saga, evil may be lurking in the shadows or controlling an oppressive, galaxy-spanning Empire. Yet there must always be balance in the Force, and whenever the dark side rises, a spirit of rebellion begins to light the way forward.

With the new Spirit of Rebellion Booster Pack for Star Wars: Destiny, 160 brand-new cards enter the game, incorporating elements from every movie in the saga, but with a special focus on the newest Star Wars movie: Rogue One. You’ll find plenty of characters from Rogue One within this set, including Jyn Erso, Chirrut Îmwe, Mon Mothma, and Director Krennic, alongside other iconic characters from across the Star Wars story. The spirit of rebellion has ignited, so prepare to bring the feverish battles and kinetic action of Rogue One to your games of Star Wars: Destiny!

Every booster pack includes five randomized cards and one premium die, broken down into three common cards, one uncommon card, and one rare or legendary card with a corresponding die. All cards that feature a corresponding die are either rare or legendary cards. With every booster pack, you’ll find new characters, supports, upgrades, or events, along with new ways to enter the Star Wars galaxy and fight for your chosen side!

boardgame: Dead of Winter: BGG Crossroads Promo Cards
description:
Dead of Winter: A Crossroads Games - BGG Crossroads Promo is a pack of 5 crossroads cards generated by the BoardGameGeek community, and officially printed with the kind help of Plaid Hat Games. The cards included are:


     Board Games (created by BGG user Exoow)
     The Other Colony (created by BGG user MarissaDanielle)
     Fourth Wall (created by BGG user spitfire23bc)
     Damsel Dilemma (created by BGG user BeardedOne)
     Sherlock (created by BGG user Winter_Neko)


Part of the Dead of Winter series.

boardgame: Operation Musketeer: The '56 War in the Middle East
description:
Operation Musketeer is a wargame based on the assumption that the 1956 Anglo-French-Israeli invasion of Egypt turned into a major superpower confrontation in which the forces of the Soviet Union and United States became involved. 
Historically, the invasion was ended because both the USSR and USA opposed the Allied invasion. But, the assumption here is that, instead, both Moscow and Washington were drawn into the conflict and deployed combat forces to support their respective camp.

The game system emphasizes command control. Each side is composed of different contingents. Players activate their contingents through the use of Command markers. This models the historical command-control factors involved in multi-national operations during the 1950s. Players command ground and air forces, and there is the possibility of nuclear war breaking out. There are two players: the Allies (including France, Britain, Israel and the US); and the East Bloc (Egypt, USSR).


Published with Modern War #32, Nov-Dec 2017

Slaughterville II brings to the never quite peaceful town 6 brand new Locations and 3 new Villains! This stand-alone expansion can be easily integrated into the original game.

Locations:
The Mall
The School
The Museum
The Sewers
The Ski Lodge
The Mines

Villains:
Doctor Frankenstein
Cthulhu
Dracula

boardgame: Cold Start: The Next India-Pakistan War
description:
Cold Start: The Next India-Pakistan War is a strategic-level, two-player wargame of low-intermediate complexity that covers the campaign that could occur if the Indians decide to try for the big win over long-time opponent Pakistan. 
The title comes from the fact “Cold Start” is the phrase used by the Indian military to describe their officially declared policy of eschewing the first use of nuclear weapons in any future war with Pakistan.

The game’s sub-systems are crafted to present a supreme-commander’s-eye-view of such a war. It’s therefore almost fully strategic in its perspective, with only the most pastel of operational and tactical undertones added to enhance its tone and texture in those regards. Cold Start takes two experienced players only about two hours to complete, and it’s adaptable for solitaire play.


Published with Modern War #36

Originally released as part of Hull Breach!'s Corporate Wars box set, this individual deck has been revamped and improved for the current edition of the game.

Commander, nobody can compete with the industrial might that stands behind you. Xeros Engineering produces ships and heavy weapons better than anybody else can – we expect you to leverage this production to further the company's aims.  Most of your enemies will have difficulties supplying and maintaining battle groups with heavy combatants out on the frontier, so put your trust in the captains of your heavy-hitters. They and their Marines won't let you down.
Hotshots: River Tile is a single tile for use with the Hotshots base game that provides a firebreak due to the river that ends to three of the six edges.

This mid-1920s game can be used in two ways. The board has six concentric tracks, one per player, each named after a popular radio set or circuit brand: Neutrodyne, Armstrong, De Forest, Crosley, Radiola and Super-H. There also are six D6, marked with the starting letters of the six brands. The board is divided radially into 16 spaces, each with the call letters and city of a major radio station.

The first game is a simple roll-and-move affair. Each player gets a small metal radio horn or "loud speaker". All the dice are thrown at once, and each player moves ahead as often as his or her starting letter shows up. First player to complete the circuit wins. (This appears to be closely replicating one of All-Fair's top sellers of the period, 1922's Spe-Dem Auto Race Game, which - tellingly - also features a board with parallel colored paths labeled in brand names, as well as an identical method of play.)

The rules booklet also suggests a second game called Toon-In Radio Golf. This requires an actual radio set as well as a copy of the game for each player. Players have to use their radios to try to tune in each of the game's stations in turn, waiting until an announcement of the call letters to verify that he has it right. The first person to successfully tune in all of the stations wins.

The game includes the call letters, city, wavelength and power of each signal. (Even the biggest stations used only 1,000 watt0s!) The rules note that in those days, listening to radio was like golf, with all sorts of hazards like static, amateur transmitters and lightning interfering with clean reception.

You're the Pirate Captain, and you live or die by your Ship. Your field is the deck of your Ship. Your opponents' fields are theirs.  Each player draws cards to form a hand consisting of crew, cannons, field effects, and outside influences, and attempt to use the cannons to sink the other ships.  Crew can fire the cannons, shoot pistols at opposing crew, repair the ship, and bandage wounds.  Using each crewmembers special skills and talents, you have to keep your ship afloat while doing your best to send everyone else to the bottom of the sea.

boardgame: Ninja Camp: Participation Ribbon Promo Card
description: The first player to pass their turn receives the Participation Ribbon worth victory points at game end.

This is a light party game in which players take it in turns to read out phrases drawn from a deck of cards while wearing one of the included "Mouth Traps" which impede the player's speech. Their teammates attempt to guess the most phrases being read within the time limit. The team or player (in a 3 player game) with the most correctly guessed phrases wins and the loser must perform a forfeit drawn from a deck of cards.

In Clank! Sunken Treasures, an expansion for Clank! A Deck-Building Adventure, players explore two partially flooded maps with new dungeon deck cards, a new persistent monster to defeat, new environmental challenges, and more as they venture in the watery depths!

The challenge to prove your thieving skills has moved to new environments. You’d better know how to swim as several rooms you'll face have been completely flooded with water. Plus, of course, there's always an angry dragon to avoid...
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Description from the publisher:

As the sun sets on another day of defending the Burbs, you can't help but ask yourself, "Is this all there is? Are we truly doomed to live out our days in this suburban purgatory?"

Players brave enough to venture out beyond their white picket fences and take to the highway in Zpocalypse 2: Wasteland, an expansion for Zpocalypse 2: Defend the Burbs, will find themselves greeted by mutants, bandits, mad scientists, and more.

Featuring a four-part campaign adventure, new survivors, weapons, locations, craftables, and terrain, the Wasteland expansion adds hours of blood curdling fun for the whole family.

Players collect various types of resources—money, time, special gear, tour guides, etc.—that allow them to travel to cities in Asia and purchase travel experiences, which have point values.  Each player starts with a randomly dealt hand of five (5) unique Experience cards, each detailing a particular travel experience in a particular city. Players are able to draw other Experience cards through the course of the game.  Players purchase Experience cards from the five (5) in their hand, or from Experience cards that have already been played by other players.  The five (5) Experience cards in your hand do not count for points until they have been purchased by traveling to that particular city and exchanging the necessary Resource cards for it.  Only one player may “claim” a particular city and any experiences they purchase in that city.  There are five different categories of Experience cards:  Adventure, Sightseeing, Romance, Rest & Relaxation, and Culture & Service
 The game ends when a player has purchased at least one (1) of each category of Experience card and plays their Go Home card.  Players may choose to keep traveling and purchasing additional Experience cards if they wish, before playing their Go Home card to end the game.  Players use the Scoring Values card to calculate their total points. The player who plays their Go Home card first to end the game scores an additional 10 points.  The player with the highest point total wins.

boardgame: Les Quatre-Bras & Waterloo 1815: The Empire's Final Blows – Davout in Command, 18 June 1815: What if? Scenario
description:
This scenario offers players an alternative to the historical conditions of Waterloo.  What would have happened if Davout had been present at Waterloo.

This scenario was included in C3i magazine, issue #29.

The contents are the two sided scenario card and the single Davout counter on the magazine countersheet.

MNM the music battle is a Musical Trivia Game with an app, an internet connection is required to play, the app has sound effects, asks the questions and keeps the score. You get a wristband to identify your color and set your name in the app.

Your aim is to collect 1000 points, by answering questions about 4 categories of music. The big MNM 1000, 90 and nillies hits, sing your song and the big hits. You get 3 cards, one red, light blue and dark blue (which are the colors of the MNM logo) and those get recognized when pressing them on the screen. It's really easy, and works extremly well. At 300, 600 and 900 points you get to pick a new category out of the 4 aviable by showing a LP shaped cardboard to the camera. Which is always identified.

Some are just questions, somtimes you need to find a cover hidden in a ton of images, or watch a clip and tell which music it is, or bleeped words out of the clip or audio clip, so it has a good diversity and also plenty of questions so you don't get them repeated often.

So the theme is music, the aim of the game is to win and show your knowledge and the whole thing is an enjoying time with friends.

A simple car-racing game with electric lamps from the `70s. If you step on a red lamp, you have to back to the start line. If the yellow lights up it means you miss a turn, but if it`s the green, you can roll the dice again.

Description from the publisher:

After previous Data Packs have explored the game's cyberstruggles with the settings of Luna, SanSan, Mumbad, and a New Angeles wracked by economic chaos, Daedalus Complex, the first Data Pack in the Red Sand Cycle for Android: Netrunner, turns our attention to Mars. There, terraforming is already well underway, and millions of humans live alongside clones and bioroids in a handful of cities and nodes protected by transplas domes.

With sixty cards (three copies each of twenty different cards), Daedalus Complex explores the ways that life on the Red Planet is markedly different than life on Earth. On Mars, it requires a certain ruggedness to live and work. Simply traveling outside of the protected cities requires breathing assistance and protective gear. The shared hardships of survival have led to the de facto authority of the clans, and even though the Martian Colony Wars have ended, the tensions between the clans, the Martian Colonial Authority, and the corps remain high.

Mine these conflicts for change or profit with Daedalus Complex. Mars very much remains a semi-lawless frontier… and any given spark has the potential to ignite a much larger flame.
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable
family: Collectible: Living Card Game (Fantasy Flight Games)

description:
Terraforming Mars: Hellas & Elysium, the first expansion for Terraforming Mars, consists of a double-sided game board presenting two new areas of Mars:

• Elysium takes players almost to the opposite side of Mars' equator, with vast lowlands for oceans in the north and a dry, mineral-rich south. Place a tile on Olympus Mons, the highest peak in the solar system, to gain three free cards!

• Hellas, the southern wild, includes Mars' south pole and the enormous seven-hex Hellas crater that just begs to become a giant lake. Building around the pole gives you new placement bonuses in the form of heat and possibly even water.

Each of these maps consists of new sets of milestones and awards with relevance for that particular map. Place three tiles around the south pole to be a Polar Explorer, for example, or race to have the most estates beside water on Elysium!

Description from the publisher:

This one is pretty simple… try to keep up.
Stack the blocks up three by three to build a tower.
A player starts by removing a block from the tower and choosing to either answer the truth or do the dare on the block.
After answering the truth/doing the dare, the player stacks the block on the top of the tower. Then the next player goes.
If a player refuses to do the truth or the dare on the block, that’s ok, they can put that one on top of the stack and pick another (note: you can choose to set a limit to the number of times a player may skip before starting the game).
BEFORE STARTING THE GAME you need to decide what the punishment will be for the person who knocks over the tower. We tend to go with spanks.

NOTE: There’s a good chance that a block will get pulled twice in the same game (because you put the blocks back on top after pulling them, genius). When this happens, whoever pulls the block the second time must choose the truth/dare that wasn’t done the first time the block was pulled. Once a block is pulled twice in one game, don’t stack it back on top, just take it out and don’t use it for the rest of the party.

boardgame: London Dread: Essen 2016 Character Item Promos
description:
This promo pack is published and made available by the game publisher Grey Fox Games.  These promos were originally available at Essen 2016.

The pack consists of six additional character-specific item cards,  giving players more ways to manage investigations during a game.

The six cards are:


     Maria's Bible
     Murphy's Gloves
     Stürmer's Mausers
     Milton's Tome
     Basil's Pocket Flask
     Jacqueline's Cigarette Case


The promo items as personal starting items or you can shuffle them in with the remaining items. It is recommended playing with more than one starting item per character if you find the game dreadfully difficult.

Sex is a vital part of a healthy lifestyle but, as with many things in life, there can be downsides: broken hearts, jealousy, or (drum roll please) the potential for contracting a sexually transmitted disease.

Quickie:The STD Card Game is a fast-paced, easy-to-learn party game dealing with sexually transmitted diseases and real-life sexual encounters. The game consists of 95 yellow cards (DISEASE/NON-DISEASE) and 55 red cards (ACTION).
Game play is simple. Each-player is dealt eight yellow cards. On each turn, players draw one action card and follow the instructions. The objective of the game is to rid one's hand of all disease cards. 
Action cards may either benefit or negatively affect players.

Examples:
MONOGAMOUS RELATIONSHIP: Choose 1 other player. You each discard 1 DISEASE card. 
SHARE A DIRTY NEEDLE: Choose 1 other player. You each draw 1 YELLOW card.
There are also special ability cards (NON-DISEASE). Example: STAY HOME AND MASTURBATE which protects a player from having to draw a disease card.
Each round takes approximately 10-15 minutes to play. Play as many rounds as desired. Optional scoring rules are also included.

Ciudadano Ilustre is a trivia game about six subjects: politics, health, natural resources, education, population and work (all about Argentina). The questions do not evaluate only academic knowledge, but the ability to derive the right answer. There are also cards that can alter the rules of the game.

Players receive secret objectives at the beginning of the game (e.g., to have 3 points in politics) that give extra points. Players take turns answering, until one of them gets 12 points.

Monsters, Don’t Treat Them Gently! incorporates the Monsters and Protagonists from the short stories of the 19th century to early 20th  century celebrated English ghost writer, M. R. James. The game is set in the fictional parish of Barchester and features 20 typical locations from James’ stories. The game is introduced by means of a mysterious letter which informs the reader that they must lead a team of courageous people to fight back against the malevolent forces that threaten to overtake Barchester. The game can be played with basic rules or with a host of tactical rules as player’s become used to the flow of the game. A player can play a solitaire game where they control the Protagonists with the aim of banishing the Monsters to claim victory (the Monster being played by A.I. mechanics in the game). Alternatively a 2 player game can be played where one side takes control of the Protagonists and the other side the Monsters. Both sides choose a starting team of 15 from their decks; each card has 4 attribute statistics. Each turn take place at a different location, which may allow counters to be played or benefits to accrue. The Protagonist cards can be equipped with Boon cards that can be found at the various locations. The turn has 4 phases: organise, initiate, engage and resolve. The Protagonist and Monster players take alternate turns to hold the location and decide the type of encounter, which is kept secret from their opponent. The responding player must try and guess what strategy is being used and play their hand accordingly. The controlling player can see the card images but not the statistics, so uses their knowledge to try and play the best cards to win the encounter. The cards are then revealed and the statistics compared. An encounter table is consulted to determine the outcomes. The Monster cards can also be played as a stand-alone trump-style game, “Monsters & Miscreants.”

boardgame: A Game of Thrones: The Card Game (Second Edition) – Tyrion's Chain
description:
Description from the publisher:

The War of the Five Kings has reached its tipping point. Stannis Baratheon marches on King’s Landing by land and by sea, sending his powerful navy up the Blackwater Rush to seal off any escape. Tywin Lannister moves south with all speed, leaving the north to House Stark and House Greyjoy, but his mighty armies will never arrive before the city is besieged. In the end, the man who sits the Iron Throne at the end of the battle may be determined by nothing more than the ingenuity of Tyrion Lannister’s defenses.

With the Tyrion’s Chain Chapter Pack for A Game of Thrones: The Card Game, the War of Five Kings cycle approaches its dramatic conclusion. Here, you’ll find plenty of new support for Summer and Winter decks, inviting you to draw your strength from the warm zephyrs of summertime or the howling gales of winter. As the final chapters of A Clash of Kings play out in the card game, you’ll also find iconic characters entering the game for the first time, including Qhorin Halfhand, Salladhor Saan, Podrick Payne, and Ser Arys Oakheart. It’s time for you to rally behind your King and enter one of the greatest battles in the game of thrones!
family: Books: A Song of Ice and Fire
Collectible: Living Card Game (Fantasy Flight Games)
Game: A Game of Thrones – The Card Game (LCG)

description:
Description from the publisher:

Native Wars is a competitive, two-player card game, where players manage their tribe decks to capture the most territories.

This game features unique card and dice play. Players' cards are numbered in which you have to roll two dice to activate them by the total sum of your roll. Their card effects guide your tactical actions in the game through manipulating cards in play, attacking the enemy, or granting war power.

Players will need to recruit the best tribe members by collecting copper and paying for their costs. You then need to decide which tribe members will go into certain territories to take on your opponent's tribe. Be the first team to capture two out of the five territories to win the game!

In Captain Sonar, up to eight players take on roles manning a submarine in a fierce battle against rival players. Captain Sonar: Upgrade One adds new elements to the base game, including five new scenarios, two new weapons, eight erasable markers, and different ways to play the Captain and Radio Operator roles.
Roll dice and move piece board game.

Description from the publisher:

Penguin SLAP! is a last-man standing, penguin-slapping, family-friendly card game! Players assume the roll of penguins who slap each other with fish! (Slapping in the sense of playing a card, not physically slapping your opponents or the table) The last penguin holding fish is the winner!

The game consists of 2 decks of cards, Penguin Personas cards and fish cards. Each player assumes the roll of one of the Penguin Personas cards. These personas are comical and give them special abilities in the game.

There are 4 different colors (or suits) of fish cards. Red fish contain attack cards, yellow fish are counter-attack cards, blue fish help you draw more cards, and green fish are items and equipment. The fish cards are colored on both sides so you can see what color (or suit) of fish your opponents have, but you don't know exactly what *kinds* of fish they have. (Is that a red SLAP fish, or a red STEAL fish?) Each player also has two randomly-shuffled draw piles to choose from, so they can strategically pick which colored fish to draw depending on what's available.

Players take turns catching and slapping each other with fish, but have to be careful! Their opponent could counter their slap back to them, increasing it's power! With 4 different colors of fish you can strategically build your hand to defend or attack!

In the fast and luck-free game Dinosaurus, players compete to reign in Pangea with their dinosaurs.

A 4 x 4 grid of terrain cards is placed, then players take turns to place their dinosaurs (herbivores, carnivores, flying, etc.) of different sizes at the sides of the square. Then, by turns, the players move one of their dinosaurs to an adjacent space or eat an adjacent card: herbivores can eat plants, carnivores can eat herbivores or smaller carnivores, etc.

When no more moves are possible, the game ends and whoever has the largest stack of cards eaten by their dinosaurs wins.

In the two-player abstract game Cerrojo, which has some similarities to Nine Men's Morris, players take turns to move their sculpted figures in a star-shaped board in order to lock the opponent's main piece.

Different pieces have different movement abilities, and the initial set-up is done by each player alternatively placing their pieces.

The game is themed around a battle between two witches and their familiars. Players take turns either placing one piece on the board or moving an already placed piece; the Witches are the first pieces each player must play. All pieces are initially placed on the outer circle of the board, in any free junction. The game ends when one player cannot move her Witch because it's locked in by her opponents' pieces, or even by her own pieces.

Each player controls one Witch, three Wolves, two Owls and one Raven. Witches can move up to three junctions (they need not be aligned) and may jump over one piece of its own colour. Wolves move the same way, but only up to two junctions. Owls can only move one junction at a time, and therefore cannot jump over other pieces. Ravens may move any amount of junctions in a continuous line, without jumping over any pieces; the outer circle counts as a continuous line.

Description from the publisher:

Who would win in a fight between an elephant and a rhino? What if both of them were standing on an iceberg? What if there was an octopus involved somewhere? What if the octopus was being worried by a tortoise?

In Beastly Bust-Up, a gang of irascible animals from all corners of the globe come together to duke it out for the coveted elephant head trophy. Players wrangle their teams of fighters, trying to send the right beasts to the right places to win the most points.

Battles take place in various habitats, each worth a number of points. Players take it in turns to dispatch fighters of various ranks to contest those habitats, hoping to have the highest total rank in each habitat at the end of the game and claim the points.

This would all be straightforward enough, but each animal has its own special strengths and weaknesses to throw into the mix. Fierce ones can scare others off, cowardly ones need babysitting, agile ones can avoid being squashed, speedy ones can slip in unnoticed... from the mighty lion to the lowly mouse, every fighter can contribute something.

Beastly Bust-Up is a scaleable game that can fill a ten minute coffee break or launch a multiplayer tournament across several hours.

boardgame: What The F*ck: I Never Have
description:
Description from the publisher:

Hours of outrageous fun! The classic ?I Never Have? game with 552 What the F*ck? styled statements. Get your friends to admit the most f*cked up things they have ever done! When you believe players are not admitting to something f*cked up that you know they've done, you get to coerce them into taking their drinks. For 3 or more players.

Cinnamon, Star Anise, and Peppercorn add extra spice to Herbaceous's flavorful play!

				
				
					Rules
    When a flavor card is drawn, all players perform the action, if they can, starting with the player who drew the flavor card.
    After the actions are performed, the player then discards the flavor card from the game and continues their normal turn.


Note: The flavor card does not count as a drawn herb.

Buriiri is an arrangement type board game where all the players cooperate and fill with the character [hiragana] to which 5 × 5 squares are distributed [hiragana]
The player first decides the number, the first person freely places one letter
The game master randomly distributes two letters to the player who is turning the turn
The active player selects one of the two characters and fills it in the square

Filling conditions
1, it is in contact with the character already existing in the square
2, it is established as a word of two letters together with the character in contact
3, It is possible to explain the meaning of the word to the game master
If conditions are met, you can fill in that square
Repeat as you decided this, if you can fill all the trouts is victory
Unusable vocabulary
Proper noun including place name, family name, character name, etc.
Foreign languages and dialects
There is an item of reading aloud and kun reading in the meaning of the dictionary

boardgame: Hoplite: The Great Battles of Sparta's Persian Expedition
description:
Two scenarios and 26 counters included in C3i Magazine, Issue #29.

The Battle of Meander 397 BC.
 Designer Notes. The Spartan army has just halted and faced the rear to deploy.  The Persians have also hastily deployed from column.  As the battle is about begin, Xenocles' ambush force has just emerged from hiding in the off-map woods to appear in the rear of the Persian Army.

The Battle of Sardis, 395 BC.
The Persians are favored.  The Spartan army withdraws when unit with Route Points totaling at least 70 have been eliminated.  The Persian Army withdraws when units with Rout Points totaling at least 85 have been eliminated.
family: History: Greek & Persian conflicts of the 4th century BC
Series: Great Battles of History (GMT Games)

description:
Description from the publisher:

Your battles for an insignificant hilltop are about to become even more chaotic and dangerous with the Kharnage: Tricks & Mercenaries – Army Expansion! Within this army expansion, you'll find new mercenary units to supplement each of the four armies in the base set, leading new dwarves, humans, goblins, and orcs storming onto the battlefield — and if new units alone weren't enough, this army expansion offers plenty of new trick cards, including a new type of card that can give you an unexpected advantage in the midst of battle.

In short, Kharnage: Tricks & Mercenaries – Army Expansion contains 18 tricks and eight mercenaries for use with the Kharnage base game.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Description from the publisher:

Kingmaker: The Reluctant Coronation is a turn-based, tile drafting, anti-set collection game where the goal is NOT to become king, but to instead be responsible for forcing another player to the throne. Being the leader of this cantankerous village is difficult work, best left to someone else. Still, the time has come for a new monarch to be crowned and as the wisest elders still around, one of you will surely become the new person in charge/scapegoat to blame for all the kingdom's problems. To decide who will ascend to this awesome responsibility, you'll present the accoutrements of power to your opponents hoping to ensnare one of them (ANY OF THEM!) with all five: the ring, the robe, the sceptre, the crown and the throne. Be careful! Each item given to another player also gives them a powerful advantage in a future turn. If they survive the round, revenge will come swift and hard.

Every round, each player presents one gift of power to another and then in turn, each player can assert the powers granted by their previously acquired artifacts. These powers can refuse gifts, trade them, introduce new ones or shuffle them around. When a player is bestowed the fifth item in the set and is unable to avoid accepting it, they become king and the player who gave them that item becomes the kingmaker and wins the game.

In this game you travel between the different municipalities in the Central Uusimaa region of Finland (poorly defined, but roughly the area immediately north of Helsinki and it's immediate environs) by rolling a d6. You cruise around the municipalities and do tasks which are given to you by cards. Tasks may include, for example, moving to a certain town on the map. After the first player has visited four out of the six municipalities in the game, and defined one of the six as an end goal destination, the first player to get there wins the game.

(The name Munkki translates roughly as Donut in this context, but instead of driving in small circles to leave skidmarks, in the local slang it refers more to cruising ie. driving some popular short repetitive route, such as driving around the block. Often in the company of other such drivers - a pastime that has always been popular among the youth of this region.)

This game has been sponsored by the museums of the local area: Hyvinkää City Museum, Järvenpää Art Museum, Kerava Museum, Mäntsälä Museum Department, Nurmijärvi Museum and Tuusula Museum.

Do you see the little mole?
While playing on the meadow or at the sea, making a picknick or working in the garden? But only if you look carefully at the pictures you'll recognize all the details to be able to identify the correct tiles for your board.

Ravensburger children's game. Among 32 different tiles those have to be found which are matching the details on your cardboard.

The rule booklet offers four different variants of playing the games to provide challenges for children from 3 to 6.

In the Ivory kingdom, an old portal has been activated due to the abuse of magic. Ancient creatures threaten to destroy the kingdom, but warriors of all Ivory's places come to the call of the king to try to recover the peace.

Mythsgate is an asymmetric game in which a player controls the creatures laying waste to the kingdom while everyone else tries to stop the carnage. During the game, the warriors have to improve their equipment and increase their power reserve to be able to defeat the ancient creatures and close the portal.

This 5 card expansion for Pax Renaissance, designed exclusively for BGG by Phil Eklund, contains new explorers, nomads, cossacks, and Transylvanian mass murderers.

The cards are simply added to the west and east decks, and do not require any extra rules.

User summary
毒ガス塹壕戰 (Dokugasu Zangousen) in English means 'Poison Gas and Entrenchment'.

A Government kidnapped unemployed, NEET, and homeless people, and sent them onto a deserted island. These people were given outdated firearms, and were forced to kill one another. There were two reasons for the Government to do this: one was to collect battle data through the experimental battles, and the other was as an easy way to dispose such troublesome people.

Dokugasu Zangousen is a three-dimensional chess-like board game. In a 7x7 square grid board, you may make a hump (place a cube) or dig an entrenchment (remove a cube) to avoid lines of fire or to hinder enemy's movement. But entrenchment is vulnerable to poison gas which tends to go downward into holes.

Players initially have two soldiers each. At the beginning of each round, players place two action cards face-down in front of them. Then first player takes all actions they have placed.

During your turn, you may do two of the following  actions as you have programmed:
1) Move: Move one of your soldiers one square orthogonally, or advance the soldier two squares forward.

2) Turn: Turn the direction of one of your soldiers.

3) Construct: Place a cube on a square adjacent to one of your soldiers. Or remove a cube from in front of the soldier.

4) Fire: Fire a gun from one of your soldiers orthogonally to kill an enemy or destroy a cube. Or throw a bag of poison gas to kill soldiers within an affected area, which is determined by wind direction.

5) Dig: Dig an entrenchment at the exact square of one of your soldiers.

6) Item: One of your soldier wears or takes off a gas mask. While wearing the mask, the soldier gains protection from poison but is unable to advance two squares, construct, or move cubes. Or one of your soldier takes a drug for two extra actions.

7) Camp: Retrieve one or all action cards you have played so far.

You score one point when you kill one of your enemies, or when you move one of your soldier outside the board across the opponent's border. You win the game if you score two victory points.

Publisher's summary
政府は無職、ニート、浮浪者などを拉致して無人島に送り込み
旧式武器を支給し、殺し合いをさせていた　生身の人間による有益な戦闘実験データを入手できて、犯罪者予備軍をも駆逐すできて一石二鳥だからである

多人数による立体的なチェスです
積み木で高低差を表現。それらは自由に回収したり設置が可能。
銃撃したり風向きに影響される毒ガスをばらまいて戦います
遮蔽物（立方体）を置いて敵の進行を阻んだり、攻撃から身を守ることが出来て、塹壕を堀り（地面の立方体を取り除く）、中に潜んでいれば相手からの銃撃を避けつつ相手を射撃することができる

カードを裏向きに提出し、
コマの行動をプロットして動く
使用したカードは捨札になり、カード回収行動するまで使えない
publishers: Offkaio ga Tsukutta Game (オフ会王が作ったゲェム)

description:
Spider Pete's Treasure is a children's skill and action game for two to four players.  You've caught Spider Pete burying his hoard of ill-gotten goods and it's up to you to steal his treasure using your spinning mechanical hook before he jumps at you. The first player to steal five rings wins the game.

One player controls Spider Pete and the other player acts as the pirate thief.  The thief begins by rolling the treasure die to find out how many rings he must steal this turn.  He then winds up the mechanical hook and shouts go and begins trying to fish out the appropriate number of rings from the plastic tray.  The other player immediately begins rolling and re-rolling the spider dice, hoping to get two spider icons to show.  If the thief steals the correct number of rings first, the round is over and he keeps the rings.  If the Spider Pete player rolls two spider icons first, he slaps the lever on the back of the box to cause Spider Pete to leap out!  If this happens, the thief player must return any rings he stole this turn to the tray.  Players take turns being the thief and Spider Pete until someone manages to collect 5 rings and win the game.

boardgame: Star Wars: Imperial Assault – Jawa Scavenger Villain Pack
description:
Droids are common throughout the galaxy, but to the Jawas of Tatooine, they're something special. Once taken as salvage, droids can be broken apart for parts, rebuilt and sold, or even incorporated into the Jawa's scavenging operation. As the Jawa Scavengers join the galaxy's Mercenaries in this Villain Pack, you'll also find ways to improve the droids fighting by your side.

This Jawa Scavenger Villain Pack offers a single Jawa scavenger figure, alongside all the Deployment cards you need to field normal or elite versions in your campaign or skirmish games. Your campaigns may take you to the wastes of Tatooine as you interfere with a Jawa salvage operation, but a Jawa Scavenger can also be recruited into your skirmish team, especially if you're fighting in the new Mos Eisley Back Alleys map. Whether you're trawling the Jundland Wastes for salvage or headed elsewhere in the galaxy, a few Jawa Scavengers would rarely go amiss in your team.

boardgame: Star Wars: Imperial Assault – BT-1 and 0-0-0 Villain Pack
description:
First introduced in the Darth Vader comics, BT-1 and 0-0-0 serve the interests of the Galactic Empire with a special relish for destruction. 0-0-0 is a protocol droid specially equipped to interrogate and eliminate captives, while BT-1 possesses enough hidden firepower to destroy an entire squad of enemy soldiers. With their unique skills, you can field these two droids in any Imperial strike team with the release of the BT-1 and 0-0-0 Villain Pack.

No matter where your Imperial Assault campaigns take you in the Star Wars galaxy, the Rebel heroes will have reason to fear to glowing red displays of BT-1 and 0-0-0. A new campaign Agenda mission challenges the heroes to rescue prisoners from an Imperial interrogation facility on Kessel and you'll find a new skirmish map with two unique missions, giving you the opportunity to battle through the twisted corridors of the Tarkin Initiative Labs.

boardgame: Star Wars: Imperial Assault – Hera Syndulla and C1-10P Ally Pack
description:
Though 0-0-0 and BT-1 serve the malicious purposes of the Galactic Empire and Jawa Scavengers are only looking to make a profit, some beings in the galaxy fight for a cause larger than themselves. Hera Syndulla and her loyal astromech C1-10P are stoking the fires of resistance in a galaxy that's choking under the heel of the Empire. With the Hera Syndulla and C1-10P Ally Pack, you can bring the will to rebel into your games of Imperial Assault.

In your campaigns, Hera may lead you to Tatooine in a desperate attempt to rescue Chopper from Imperial imprisonment, but Hera can prove herself as an adept leader in any mission, calling the shots to keep your entire strike team on point. Chopper is also more than capable of handling himself in battle, using a variety of tricks and tools to knock your enemies back and keep you on top of any engagement.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

boardgame: Lock 'n Load Tactical: Hell Frozen Over
description:
In June of 1942, the Imperial Japanese Army seized the island of Attu in the Aleutian Islands of the United States’ Alaskan Territory. The United States, fearing the Japanese would use this strategic location as a base to control the northern Pacific Ocean’s sea lanes, launched an assault in May of 1943. The 2,500 Japanese soldiers, mostly of the 301st Independent Infantry Battalion, were dug-in in the high mountains to defend against the Americans of the 7th and 32nd Infantry Regiments of the 7th Division. An arduous, nearly three-week campaign fought in brutal arctic conditions over bleak, rugged mountainous terrain ensued, climaxing in one of the largest Banzai charges of the war.

Hell Frozen Over is an expansion module for Heroes of the Pacific. Play nine scenarios on a unique 22”x34” Map that accurately depicts Attu’s harsh terrain. The terrain varies over eight levels in height and features boot-sticking Muskeg, rock-strewn Rough, precipitous Cliffs and jagged Razorbacks crossing the high peaks of the unforgiving, mountainous landscape. Through fog and unexpected high winds, known to the Aleuts as “Williwaws”, both sides struggle to hold and take ground. Clevesy Pass, Cold Mountain, Point Able, Engineer Hill, the Sarana Valley and Prendergast Ridge are all fought over. The fighting is hex by hex, yard by yard, bullet by bullet, hand to hand; and it won’t end until the last Japanese soldier is vanquished.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
User summary:
進め！ユグドラ探検隊 -First Dig Edition- (Terra Caravan -First Dig Edition-) is a card game based on the smartphone game of the same name. Players are excavators who dig the Earth to obtain treasures below.

Players are initially on the surface of the Earth, and big treasures lie at five-card-length below the surface. Each turn, you roll a die to determine your movement direction and move your pawn, then reveal the area card under your pawn if it is not explored yet. Depending on the card revealed, you may find an item or a small treasure, or you may be forced back to where you were. You may use items you have found for special effects, such as to destroy an impassable area, or to dig deeper than usual. When you reach a big treasure, you score big points and the round is over.

Play three rounds and the highest scorer wins the game.

Description from the publisher:
あなた達は、地底に眠るはかりしれない価値を秘めた財宝を求める発掘者。
一気に掘削できるスキルや、行く手を妨害することもできるボスブロックを駆使して最深部を目指そう！
世界の中心を目指し、全力で”Let's DIG”！

■ゲームの目的 
3ラウンドを1ゲームとし、ゲーム終了時に採掘ポイント（=TP）の合計が最も高かったプレイヤーが勝ちとなります！

■遊び方（1ラウンド） 
(1)ブロックカードを並べる 
カードを人数に応じて並べます。

2~3名：横5 x 縦４ 
4名：横６x縦４

(2)アーティファクトカードを並べる 
ブロックカードの一番下の列に、アーティファクトカードを1列並べます。 
並べたブロックカードは上から1列目を「地表」と呼び、一番下のアーティファクトの5列目を「最深部」と呼びます。

(3)プレイ順の決定 
一番最近地下深くに降りた人から時計回りの順番で進めます。 
2ラウンド目以降は、最深部より遠かった順に手番を進めます。

(4)ファーストディグ 
ラウンドの1番最初の手番を、特に「ファーストディグ」と呼びます。 
1列目（地表）のブロックカードのどれかにコマをおき、カードをめくりましょう。 
※ファーストディグでは、他のプレイヤーがすでにコマを置いている場所に置くことはできません。 
※ファーストディグでは、スキルの使用はできません。

(5)ディギング 
2回目以降の手番では、以下のことができます。 
・サイコロを振り、コマを進める。 
→サイコロの面に描かれた方向に一マス進むことができます。 
・スキルを使用する。 
→スキルが発動できるトレジャーカードを消費して、そのスキルの効果を使用します。 
※サイコロの出目の方向に進めなかった場合は、その場に留まります。ブロックカードの効果が発生するものは、移動できなくても発動します。

(6)アーティファクト獲得！ 
最初の一人が最奥のアーティファクトカードに到達したら、そのラウンドは終了になります。 
到達したプレイヤーはそのアーティフェクトカードを手に入れます。 
その後、空いたスペースに新しいアーティファクトカードを補充してください。

TIPS! [ボスブロックに三方（左右下）を阻まれたら] 
サイコロの目が「ランダム」が出たとき、上にも回避することができます。 
※ゲーム終了後、一緒に遊んだ他のプレイヤーに優しくしてあげると、次回からこういうことが起こりにくくなるかもしれません。

(7)ゲーム終了と勝敗 
(1)~(6)を3回繰り返したらゲーム終了です。 
獲得しているトレジャーカードとアーティファクトのTPを合計し、最も高かったプレイヤーが勝ちとなります。

公式サイト： 
http://talosil.com/games/yggdra/

KaleidoCards is a game system. It consists of a deck of 100 unique coloured cards which are used to play many varied abstract games which are quick to learn and play.

The cards use five 'colours’: Red, Blue, Green, Black and White.  Each card has three ‘aspects’:  Word, Background and Font Colour.  The Word is the name of one of the five colours, the Background of the card is one of the colours, and Font that the word is written in is also one of the colours.

KaleidoCards comes with rules for 7 different games and the KaleidoSpeed Challenge:

• KaleidoShout - a fast and loud trading game; 4-5 players; 2-3 minutes.
• KaleidoClash - a fast-paced game of frantically grabbing cards; 2-8 players; 2-3 minutes.
• KaleidoLine - a turn-based game where players play tactically to get rid of all their cards; 2-8 players; 5-10 minutes.
• KaleidoCry - a quick colour perception game where players call out which colour appears most among multiple cards; 2-8 players; 10-15 minutes.
• KaleidoSeek  - a thought-provoking deduction game; 2-8 players; 5-15 minutes.
• KaleidoCombo - a mind-bending perception game where players look for pairs of cards which make a 'combo' of all five colours; 2-8 players; 10-15 minutes.
• KaleidoGrid - a cooperative or solitaire puzzle game of placing cards strategically to score points; 1 or more players; 5-10 minutes.

• The KaleidoSpeed Challenge - a colour perception speed challenge influenced by the pyschological 'Stroop Test'; 1 or more players; 1 minute/player.

There are many other KaleidoGames which can be played with KaleidoCards, making it a very versatile game system.

Only limited editions of KaleidoCards are currently available.  Its due to be launched into mainstream distribution in 2020.
Place the Loggerheads on the game board, let the others memorize the order and make a switch. Who can see what has changed? The player with the most tokens at the end wins the game.

boardgame: Pathfinder Adventure Card Game: Mummy's Mask – "Mummify Self" Promo Card
description:
Mummify Self
Type: Spell

Traits: Magic, Divine

Check to Acquire: Wisdom / Divine 10

Powers: 
Display this card next to a character's deck. While displayed, add 1 die to her checks that invoke the Mummy trait, and reduce Poison damage dealt to her to 0; she may bury a card to add 1d6 and the Cold trait to her combat and Wisdom checks.

At the end of the scenario, if you do not have the Divine skill, banish this card.
