description:
Description from the box:

A New Map Game.

An exciting world race and one which will teach the players -
Trade within the Empire. Exports and Imports.

The object of the game is to complete a voyage to the largest of the British Dominions and land imports and take away exports, and the winner is the one who first gets home to his starting point.

Description from the publisher:

Pacific Submarine. Germany's U-boats are better known, but the US Navy's submarine fleet achieved the greater victory, bringing the Japanese Empire to its knees by hollowing out its merchant fleet. In this solitaire game, you the player represent the skipper of a submarine. Your task is to conduct patrols against the Japanese by supporting the US fleets in combat, ambushing Japanese warships, carrying out covert operations in Japanese territory, and sinking merchant ships and tankers. You can conduct one of several individual patrols, or fight the whole campaign, with a goal of promotion to Captain—if you survive.

Patrols are assigned by a die roll against a possible set of missions, but the set changes through the course of the war. In each, you move your boat across the situation map, that covers a quarter of the map, at 375 miles per hex, to the chosen island, ports, or shipping route. Some of the counters represent Japanese naval forces and installations, which you either seek or avoid depending on your mission. Each action covers 1-to-3 hexes and may result in enemy contact.

You decide whether to engage, and choose your boat's actions—depth, speed, course, and weapons—to hit the juiciest targets and avoid their escorts. At the end of the patrol, your tally is based on ships sunk and other mission parameters, less the damage to your boat. High scores result in promotion; low scores leave you beached.

Components: One 22" x 34" map & 280 counters

Magazine: Strategy & Tactics #311

Description from the publisher:

Windhoek: Campaigns in Southwest Africa 1914-15 is an Operational level wargame of the campaign in which British Empire forces, mainly from the Union of South Africa, conquered the German colony of Southwest Africa. In the original campaign, the British won within ten months, but the campaign could have gone differently, as von Lettow-Vorbeck demonstrated in German East Africa. This is a two-player game, in which one side commands the Germans, and the other Allied forces (including South Africa, British and Portuguese colonial). The Allied player must win the game quickly, while the Germans win by delaying the Allies beyond the historical time frame. The central game mechanism is Action Points (AP). These are a quantification of each side's command and logistical capabilities. Players expend AP to recruit units and conduct various military operations. The game has two levels: The Standard Rules, which give a quick wargame, and the Advanced Rules, which give more realism and complexity. Windhoek includes 176 5/8 inch counters and a 22 x 34” map with each hex representing approximately 25 miles .

S&T #313, Nov-Dec 2018

description:
User summary
あてっこついたて (Atekko Tsuitate) is a guessing game.

At the beginning of the game, a theme is randomly determined. Each player write down an answer that relates to the theme on a small white-board, and passes the board to another player. That player stands the board so that only other players can see the answer.

Each turn, you may ask a yes/no question about the answer given to you, and other players respond to it with a thumb up/down. When you are sure what your answer is, you may call out your guess. You win the game if you correctly guessed your answer before everyone else does.

description:
Description from the publisher:

Grey Duck is a lively, fast-paced game. The custom pyramid deck structure is a unique break from the 52-card deck paradigm.

Grey Duck is played in tricks where players follow patterns of sets of cards (forms). Grey Ducks are cards that can be played out of turn to extend previously played forms.

Points are scored based on the order in which players plays all the cards from their hands.

The person with the highest score after 12 hands is the winner.

In this tile-based board game, you are tasked with bringing life back to the countryside. Cut down trees to clear pathways through the forest, plant trees to entice animals back, and strike a balance between humanity and nature.

description:
Traveling Animals is a multi-difficulty trivia game in which players answer questions about animals or world environmental conservation.  At the beginning of the game, each player independently selects whether they will play at the easy (tourist), medium (pathfinder), or hard (native) difficulty level.    The goal is to correctly answer questions and be the first player to reach the "Global Animal Congress" (located off the east coast of Australia) with five collected trivia cards.

Traveling by dice-roll, players move their tokens along paths defined by real-world animal migration routes; side routs and branching paths allow for strategic movement.  Upon landing on a question space, players get to answer a question from either an Animal or World card.

The 75 Animal cards each have a custom piece of artwork on the front depicting the animal in question and one trivia question for each of the three difficulty levels (usually in the form of true/false, multiple choice, and direct answer, respectively).  A correct answer awards the card to the player; some spaces on the board can later cause cards to be lost or stolen.  The 15 World cards work similarly, although they all share identical artwork.

Special spaces on the travel paths allow players to jump around the board, steal cards, lose cards, and gain/lose a turn.  The rule book contains variant instructions.

Description from the publisher:

Northampton 1460 is a 1-2 player game of the Battle of Northampton that took place on the 10th July 1460. It is a play-at-home version of the very popular participation game of the same name used by the Northampton Battlefield Society on its display stand at game shows and conventions.

The games has similar mechanisms to W1815 but with added non-military options. The players take alternate turns using one of their character cards. These enable the players to take historically plausible decisions whilst still retaining the ability to influence events to go in a different direction to the historical outcome.

The game enables the players to develop a clear narrative of the course of the battle , understanding the options available to the commanders, why the battle turned out as it did and how it might have had a different result. The victory point system also gives an explanation of how history would have changed if the battle had ended differently.

The game usually plays in less than 30 minutes and it is expected that players will alternate roles and compare their relative performances. Following the hard copy book selling out, the game has now been made available as a pdf download from Wargames Vault.

The game name, which translates to "Corrupt people have money" is a pun on Karl Heinz Grasser, infamous former finance minister of Austria, who passed an action for injunction regarding this game, but his suit was rejected by the Supreme Court.

The game is basically a roll and move game with a 12 sided die on a Monopoly resembling board, but includes decks of action cards ("Power and Intrigue") and negotiation cards to gain money ("Corruption Cases"). All cards resemble actual Austrian scandals of recent years. Game ends, when the bank (named "treasury") is broke, at which point the richest player wins.

2 players Black and White

Materials    
A hex board having 91 cells, 6 cells per side and a supply of black and white stones

Tendril - a group of connected like color adjacent stones

Object
Player having the largest “Tendril” at endgame wins

Rules of Play
Board starts out empty

Black starts turns alternate

On black's first turn he places one stone, hereafter on a turn players place two stones into empty cells

A stone cannot be placed in a cell which does not have 4 adjacent empty cells

When no more moves are possible, the game ends

Description from the publisher:

War in the Pacific 1941-1945 allows players to replay the conflict between the Empire of Japan and the Allies in the Pacific Theater of World War II. WIP is a campaign game on a grand strategic scale. Random event cards reflect the major political and technological changes and events which occurred during the War; depots are used to stress the crucial importance of logistics; and there is a mechanic that generally effects simultaneous movement (campaigning). The heart of WIP is the use of Resource Points which players spend to conduct their campaigns. The Japanese player starts with more RPs and is his challenge to use them effectively in the first months and years of the war before the immense resources of the Allies kick in and begin to be felt.

description:
Description from the designer:

Galactic Explorers is a traditional roll and move game that is anything but traditional.  Players design their ships using parts and technologies to fit their particular strategy.  Once their ship is built, its time to start rolling and exploring the galaxy!

There are three types of ship parts, Cargo, Colony, and Engine, and four technology trees that can be used to specialize your ship.  At the start of the game players have a budget they spend to build their ship.  Each ship part is represented by a different d6 and how far a ship moves each turn depends on the role of the dice.

The goal of the game is to amass the most credits.  Players earn money by discovering new locations, delivering miners to worlds they can mine, earning income from their colonists and delivering goods back to the Galactic Hub.  Credits can also be used to improve player's ships, either adding more pieces or upgrading technologies.

The board is a spiral galaxy with six arms connecting the center to the outer rim.  The number of players determines which arms are used.  The game ends when certain parts of the board have been completely discovered or a turn limit, whichever comes first.

A fast crossword game.

Each player tries to form a crossword pattern with 10 letter cubes.  A dice shaker and sand timer are used to roll the letter cubes and form a crossword pattern using all 10 letter cubes in the time the sand timer empties.  If a complete crossword pattern is accomplished before the timer empties, the player may place all the cubes back in the shaker and try again.

After the timer empties the shaker and letter cubes are handed to another player.

Scoring is determined by words and the amount of times a complete pattern is accomplished.

Description from the publisher:

Professor Frankindex has given the monsters Zeropi and Decamo an impossible assignment; they must feel how many numbers are in the bag. But monster claws are much too big to recognize little objects. That’s why they desperately need your help! The players use their sense of touch to feel how many cubes are in each little bag. Who can whisper the correct eight or more numbers to Zeropi and Decamo? A monstrous numbers and quantities learning game.

boardgame: Nations at War: Tigers in the Snow
description:
Description from the publisher:

Tigers in the Snow depicts the nail-biting combat during the Battle of the Bulge in our Nations At War series. In December of 1944 Hitler launched Wacht am Rhein (Watch on the Rhine) catching the Allies completely by surprise. The plan was to surprise the Allies with an encirclement attack which would force the allies into a peace treaty with terms favorable for the Axis. The end result would what would be the last German offensive of World War 2.
 Designed and developed by veteran Sean Druelinger, Tigers in the Snow will recreate some of the famous battles fought around Eisenborn Ridge in the north to Bastogne in the south, during the Battle of the Bulge. In the game, you can expect six gorgeous map boards with new terrain. Four counter sheets of 3/4" counters and various player aid cards. Special module rules covering limited fuel, German Commandos, and weather to name a few.

This is the 5th core game in the Nations At War series.

Stalin’s Triumph uses the newly developed v2.0 Core Rules Edition and also includes our new design your own scenario generator. This will add hours of play to your game.

description:
Description from the publisher:

Dungeon Mountain is a tabletop dungeon crawler for two players.

It is published as a play book, containing the rules, a space for custom rules and over 50 game boards.

A free, printable game board template and pocket rule book is also available.

The object of the game is to escape the other player's dungeon.
Each player creates a dungeon on an 8x8 grid. This contains a starting point and exit point. A class system can be used to predict the difficulty of a dungeon allowing for fair play or else to create a handicap for games between players of different experience.

Players place several items throughout the dungeon. These are Big Monsters, Small Monsters, Traps, Hammers and Gems. The position of these items does not change during gameplay.

Combat with Monsters is decided with attack points that are assigned when the monsters are placed.

On a second blank grid the players are given the starting coordinates. Players then navigate their opponents dungeon by calling out directions. Players continue their turn until they attempt a move into a wall or certain items. As they progress the players mark their progress on the grids.

The winner is the first player to exit the dungeon.
Number of lives used, monsters defeated and items collected can also be counted as points.

Description from the publisher:

The Exosuit Commander Pack adds 30 super-sized (55 mm tall) Exosuit miniatures to the Anachrony base box, plus the Pioneers of New Earth expansion, and the Guardians of the Council module.

Upgrade your Exosuit tokens to 24 detailed, super-sized miniatures, with a designated cockpit slit for your workers.

The Guardians of the Council adds 6 new Exosuit minis and a whole new module to Anachrony. You may also use the Guardians as the Chronobot's own Exosuit army - or an alternative to yours!

With the Pioneers of New Earth Module, you can upgrade your Exosuits to explore the vast and dangerous Outback. Enhance your Exosuit model with resources or even Breakthroughs, and use the new Adventure action space to uncover secrets of the past, confront hostile lifeforms, and more.

You'll even be able to use your Exosuit's power against your opponents in the race for the best Action spots.

Description from the publisher:

Spin-a-roo is a fun, fast-paced sorting and counting game with two ways to play! Early learners will enjoy the sorting game - a race to match the most numbers and colors on their card! Advanced learners will love the counting game- a race to collect the most tokens by counting up and down from one to ten! With a device that makes colorful tokens appear like magic, Spin-a-roo keeps young learners excited about practicing their sorting and counting skills. There are two ways to play:


    The Sorting Game (Green Side)*


The goal is to collect the most Tokens by making color and number matches on your card. A player is chosen to turn the Spinner Handle and fill all seven slots in the Spinner Tray. The chosen player should crank the handle around in a circle until it is back on the starting position. When any player sees a token that matches any color or number on his/her card, he/she calls out the number, takes the token, and places it on the matching space on his/her card. When no player can make a match, or the seven slots in the tray are empty, the next player (moving clockwise) cranks the handle a full turn to reload the slots. When there are no tokens left in the spinner handle and no more matches can be made by any player, players stack all the tokens they have collected. If you are the player with the tallest stack--YOU WIN!


    Counting Game (Purple Side)*


The goal is to collect the most tokens by counting up and down from one to ten! A player is chosen to turn the handle and fill all seven slots in the spinner tray. They should crank the handle around in a circle one full turn until it is back on the starting position. When any player sees a token that comes before or after any number on his/her card, he/she calls out the number, takes the token, and places it on the correct space on his/her card. Any number may be placed on the "WILD" space to start. When no player can make a match, or the seven slots in the Tray are empty, the next player (moving clockwise) cranks the handle a full turn to reload the slots. If your stack reaches 10, clear that stack and start over. The tokens that you have cleared will be counted toward your total points at the end of the round. When there are no Tokens left in the Spinner Handle and no more matches can be made by any player, players stack all the Tokens they have collected. If you are the player with the tallest stack--YOU WIN!

boardgame: Van Gogh Walks into a Bar
description:
Description from the publisher:

It's the XIX century and famous Vincent Willem Van Gogh is up to party. Players will define his movement from bar to bar and will try to make him pass through their food stands to earn points. A game created in México during the Global Game Jam 2017.

During the set up, each player will place 6 food stands on the map, which is compounded by 3 sectors of 5 tiles x 6 tiles. Then, players will take turns to define 15 of Van Gogh's erratic movements. Each round, one of the players will play secretly so the other players won't know for sure where Van Gogh is located.

Players can get points if Van Gogh passes through one of their Food Stands or if Van Gogh wavelike movement matches one of the Path Cards that the players have. If Van Gogh would leave the table, players may lose points.

The game ends after each player had the chance to play secretly during a round. The player with the most points wins.

September 30, 1938. British Prime Minister leaves Munich with no agreement and no piece of paper to wave at a adoring crowd. In Prague, President Benes meets with his government and the decision to resist is made.

This game covers a hypothetical invasion of Czechoslovakia in October of 1938 after a failed meeting in München.
The Game OB's has all forces present in Oct of 38 plus those that appeared up until Dec of 38.

Contents:
560 counters
2 maps
Rulebook
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

boardgame: 7s 11s: A Fast Paced Dice Game of Chance & Risk Taking
description:
Description from the publisher:

7s 11s is a dice scoring game where players take turns rolling three dice to make combinations that add to 7 or 11. A player's roll determines who contributes chips to the pot. Players take chances to improve their roll as they try to accumulate 100 or more points while avoiding the unlucky "triple", the same number on all three dice.  Win the game and the chips are yours!

Description from the publisher:

Fate Foretold is a tarot-inspired strategy card game where players race against one another to take control of Fate itself. Fate Foretold is honed and crafted to inspire hours of fun and epic gameplay for you and your friends. Gather around the table and take a turn at tempting fate. Use your wits and all the luck you can gather to make it through victorious! Pledge allegiance to kings, gain a spell from a witch, heed warnings in constellations, and use the power of the Four Horses of the Apocalypse to bring ruin to your enemies and emerge as the victor! Fate Foretold is a brilliantly illustrated creator-owned card game designed for 2 to 6 people.

boardgame: StoryCards: Die drei ??? – Buch der Finsternis
description: StoryCards: Die drei ??? – Buch der Finsternis is a storytelling game of sorts in which a player or team is confronted by a situation that presents them with two options for a journey. Which way should they go to solve the mystery? Once they choose, they lay down the card, then search for the appropriately numbered card that they chose. The more cards they lay down, the more they score. If a card is no longer available, however, then the game ends and the detective trio has failed.

boardgame: StoryCards: Die drei ??? Kids – Spuk im Schloss
description: StoryCards: Die drei ??? Kids – Spuk im Schloss is a storytelling game of sorts in which a player or team is confronted by a situation that presents them with two options for a journey. Which way should they go to solve the mystery? Once they choose, they lay down the card, then search for the appropriately numbered card that they chose. The more cards they lay down, the more they score. If a card is no longer available, however, then the game ends and the detective trio has failed.

boardgame: StoryCards: Die Drei !!! – Labyrinth der Spiegel
description: StoryCards: Die Drei !!! – Labyrinth der Spiegel is a storytelling game of sorts in which a player or team is confronted by a situation that presents them with two options for a journey. Which way should they go to solve the mystery? Once they choose, they lay down the card, then search for the appropriately numbered card that they chose. The more cards they lay down, the more they score. If a card is no longer available, however, then the game ends and the detective trio has failed.

Try to score in Sokieba by throwing five soft colorful dice at the white target dice, which are placed two to five meters away from the throwers. You'll score based on the numbers indicated on the die you hit and the die you threw, so throw accurately and hope for the best!

In Feuerwehrmann Sam: Wasser marsch!, based on the television character "Feuerwehrmann Sam", players need to mend the broken firehose in order to save the inhabitants of Pontypandy. To do this, though, they need to find the matching parts that lie hidden underneath the firetruck Jupiter, and whoever finds the most matches wins.

Description from the publisher (translated):

Preserving life is our priority. Life on earth comes to an end. We must find a new planet capable of welcoming us as a species, allowing us to live in it and to which we can call home. For this, you will serve as Valkyries, arcane-technological ships that have been developed thanks to the discovery of some alien planes. To this day, these ships are already irreproducible. They are our key to the future. They are our last hope.

W.O.R.L.D.S. is a cooperative / competitive card game in which 2 to 4 players will try to be the first to find a planet fit for life, the new cradle of humanity.

In the course of the game, players will pilot their ships, the Valkyries, and will play planetary characteristics on the explored worlds, collaborating to find one that is totally suitable. Now, you have to be the first to achieve it... In addition, you have to get it before the earth loses all hope of being saved.

W.O.R.L.D.S. is composed of 24 world tarot size cards, with the aid of which we will travel with our Valkyries to find a planet where all humanity can be transferred. To check if a planet is fit for life, we will have 60 planetary characteristics counters. And to direct our Valkyrie we will use command panel letters and navigation cubes in order to achieve our goal. But do not lose sight of the fact that the planet Earth does not have much time left and that we have to reach our goal before the asteroids end all trace of life.

Description from the publisher:
Preservar la vida es nuestra prioridad. La vida en la tierra llega a su fin. Debemos encontrar un nuevo planeta capaz de acogernos como especie, que nos permita vivir en él y al que podamos llamar hogar. Para ello, os serviréis de las valquirias, naves arcano-tecnológicas que han sido desarrolladas gracias al descubrimiento de unos planos alienígenas. A día de hoy, estas naves son ya irreproducibles. Son nuestra llave hacia el futuro. Son nuestra última esperanza. 
W.O.R.L.D.S. es un juego de cartas cooperativo/competitivo en el que de 2 a 4 jugadores intentarán ser los primeros en hallar un planeta apto para la vida, la nueva cuna de la humanidad. 
En el transcurso del juego, los jugadores pilotarán sus naves, las valquirias, y jugarán características planetarias sobre los mundos explorados, colaborando para hallar uno que sea totalmente idóneo. Ahora bien, hay que ser el primero en lograrlo... Además, hay que conseguirlo antes de que la tierra pierda toda la esperanza de ser salvada. 
W.O.R.L.D.S. está compuesto por 24 cartas de mundo hallado de tamaño tarot, gracias a las que viajaremos con nuestras valquirias para encontrar un planeta donde se pueda trasladar toda la humanidad. Para poder comprobar si un planeta es apto para la vida, contaremos con 60 fichas de características planetarias. Y para dirigir nuestra valquiria utilizaremos cartas de panel de mando y cubos de navegación para poder conseguir nuestro objetivo. Pero no hay que perder de vista que al planeta tierra no le queda mucho tiempo y que hay que alcanzar nuestro objetivo antes de que los asteroides acaben con todo rastro de vida.
Si queréis saber más sobre W.O.R.L.D.S. seguid atentos. Iremos publicando todos nuestros avances por encontrar un planeta que sea apto para acogernos.

boardgame: The World of SMOG: Rise of Moloch – The Mekasylum
description:
Description from the publisher:

The Mekasylum, an expansion for the game The World of Smog: Rise of Moloch, introduces new mechanics and brings a new three-mission campaign to Rise of Moloch and comes with:

3 Heroes (Archibald Argrave, Miss Liddel and Little Alice)
3 Agents (Signore Gepetto, Pinokio and Little Orphan Lily)
8 Minion Miniatures (Killer Toys)
Tiles to explore the Unicorn Club 
20 Plastic coins (KS bonus)

Be the first to build an awesome sandwich. But beware of the mice, they want your cheese!

Each player has a sandwich with six ingredients they need to collect to complete the sandwich. Players start with three random ingredients and then on each turn, they can either choose a random ingredient from the pile in the center or they can ask another player if they have a certain ingredient. If you draw an ingredient, check to see if you can add it to your sandwich, if not put it behind your fridge. If you draw a mouse, it eats a piece of cheese from your sandwich(if you have one). The mouse is then removed from the game and the cheese goes back in the center pile.

Play continues until someone finished their sandwich.

boardgame: The Powerpuff Girls: Saving Townsville Before Bedtime!
description:
Watch out! Townsville has been taken over by villains and only the Powerpuff Girls can help!

This is a cooperative game where the players will work together to move the Powerpuff girls around the board to uncover villains to fight. Each Powerpuff Girl can fight alone (using the dice with erasable sides) or can work together to fight stronger villains. But after each turn, time will pass and the professor will move along the time track (leading to bedtime). Defeat 5 villains, including Mojo Jojo, before bedtime and you win!

Description from the designer:

An abstract game for 2 players. Players decide to either act as the Spirit of the Sun God or as Noble Explorers mistaken for Grave Robbers. While the Explorers work to find each other, the Spirit works to bring down the temple upon them.

Each turn consists of the two players selecting a certain number of spirit stones in secret. After the selection is complete the combined number of spirit stones of the two players determines the action for the turn.

The goal of the game is to escape if you are the Explorers, or stop the escape if you are the Spirits.

boardgame: Dust Tactics: Steel Guard Tesla Squad – "Steel Lightning"
description: The Tesla Squad of the Steel Guard is one of the most feared combat unit on the planet. Nobody ever faced them that wasn’t unchanged after this experience. In combat it uses some of the most powerful infantry weapons known to man and is equipped with the new version of the Steel Guard armor. The squad is presented here in desert colors, as used in the Middle-East, circa 1947.

boardgame: Dust Tactics: Steel Guard Anti-Tank Squad – "Steel Thunderbolt"
description: The Anti-Tank Squad of the Steel Guard is one of the most feared combat unit on the planet. Nobody ever faced them that wasn’t unchanged after this experience. In combat it uses some of the most powerful infantry weapons known to man and is equipped with the new version of the Steel Guard armor. The squad is presented here in desert colors, as used in the Middle-East, circa 1947.

Steel Guards are some of the most formidable fighters on the planet. Their half-robotic bodies makes them very hard to kill, allowing them to gain incredible combat experience. And among them are a few that are even more formidable: Master Sergeant Guaï-Lo Moloï is one of them. The third human on the planet (and last one alive) to ever be encased in a Steel Guard armor, he has seen action in every part of the World. His armor has been heavily modified to his specifications, making him a fearsome sight on the battlefield.

The youngest rising star in the SSU, Leytenant (Lieutenant) Eva Koroutchenko is already a legend. She is famous throughout the bloc for her emerald eyes and long hair, every young man in the SSU keeps a picture of her near his heart for good luck and to protect him from harm. Eva is a hell of a pilot who has already scored more than 30 air victories in her short career. Axis pilots on the Eastern Front have already placed a large bounty on her head: they need her down as soon as possible!

Eva flies a unique helicopter modified to her specifications: the Red Star. She is very capable of taking the reins of any other flying vehicle in the bloc, but prefers her helicopter over anything else. Eva and the Red Star are currently stationed outside Baku. From there she flies many specials operations for Spetsnaz forces in the region.

Steel Guards are some of the most formidable fighters on the planet. Their half-robotic bodies makes them very hard to kill, allowing them to gain incredible combat experience. Thanks to their inhuman strength, each one carries weapons a team of three could barely lift.
The Steel Guard Battlegroup contains the following units:

     Steel Guard Command Squad 
     Steel Guard Sniper Squad 
     Steel Guard Anti-infantry Squad
     Steel Guard Assault Squad


Everything you need to start a new glorious Steel Guard Faction Army is included in this Battlegroup!

Promotional card offered by "Dice Tower Season 13 Indiegogo".

SCYLLA (Many Headed Sea Monster):

Your Move: If your Worker moves from a space that neighbors an opponent's Worker, you may Force their Worker into the space yours just vacated.
Bullyparade: Der Film – Das Kartenspiel is a game. More details to follow.
Bullyparade: Der Film – Das Brettspiel is a game. More to follow.

Description from the publisher:

Extinction Crisis is a Co-operative board game for 1-6 players. Working together as a team, players must stabilize the global populations of amphibians, birds, mammals, and reptiles before time runs out or the damage done to the planet’s biodiversity is beyond repair.

Using unique character skills, combined with team strategy and a bit of luck, players travel the globe encountering real-world destruction events and conservation strategies as they try to bring an end to the worldwide Extinction Crisis. The overall health of the global animal populations is tracked on the Population Growth Index (PGI) chart on the board. For victory, each animal population must be “stabilized” by raising its PGI marker above the zero value. The primary method to do this is for a player to successfully complete a Reaction card during their turn. These scientifically accurate cards are a major determining factor towards the team’s overall success.

However, at the start of each player’s turn an Occurrence Card is drawn. These cards guide the story and introduce real-world events and challenges that must be overcome. But be careful… Just when you think you’re in the clear you could be hit with a Destruction Event, causing an entire species to become endangered or a local habitat to be destroyed. Other actions allow players to meet these challenges and progress towards their final objective. Players must also pay attention to the health of the world’s waterways, invertebrates, and plants. Neglect any of the 3 main charts (PGI, IP, and Ocean) for too long and the consequences could be disastrous: a smart team balances the needs of all 3 charts continuously throughout the game to achieve victory.

Extinction Crisis presents players with a daunting task and requires them to find solutions, adapt to challenges, and overcome the very real crisis facing the planet.  With only 10 turns each, how will you rise to the occasion?

Wonderword is a game with the aim to guess words formed by other players, starting from the last letter of the word. Points are awarded both to players who guess right and to those who form challenging words and succeed in revealing as many letters as possible, before a successful guess is made. The first player to reach 24 points wins the game.

boardgame: Mythic Battles: Pantheon – Rise of the Titans
description:
They are here! Four of the largest models you’re ever likely to see on a game board, and all in the same box!

All four of these powerful characters are classes as Titans in the game, though as some of you will know they aren’t always regarded as Titans in the background. Regardless of their technical classification, and call them what you will, on the battlefield they are bigger and more powerful than Gods!

Remember that Titans normally give you 2RP less to recruit your army with than a God would. Typhon is an exception to this: he gives you -4RP instead of -2RP. You’ll see why.

Echidna and her consort Typhon are responsible for many of the most famous Monsters in the mythology. We already have Cerberus and the Lernean Hydra in the core box, the Nemean Lion as a Stretch Goal, and now this box brings you a few more of their malevolent kin.

boardgame: Corinthia: A Conan / Mythic Battles – Pantheon Crossover
description:
This is a dual-use box, with loads of exciting content for both Conan and Mythic Battles: Pantheon players.

Originally, way back in the mists of time, Corinthia was planned as a purely Conan expansion. Although the Conan game has a mountain of models, there were two specific things that some people (including us) felt we missed out. The first of these was villager models. Some Conan scenarios include villagers, and we provided tokens for them which work fine. But with all the other great models in the game, we thought that it would be even better if we could replace them with actual miniatures. So this is what we’ve done.

The other missing miniature was the winged ape. This has become something of a celebrity in its absence, as well as a bit of a running gag.

This expansion pack includes some of the most famous Heroes and Monsters that have been associated with the Goddess Hera over the centuries. It also includes a flexible campaign in which you can revisit some of the 12 Labours of Heracles.

This expansion adds Hephaestus to the game in a brilliantly crafted tale of loyalty, reward, and treachery. This narrative campaign lets you pit the Gods against each other in a struggle for the fate of humanity itself. Literally. The fate of humanity. That’s important.

The campaign can be played with 2, 3 or 4 players. You decide at the start how many will be involved, and carry on like that through to the end. The author has playtested the scenarios hundreds of times, so they run smoothly and are well-balanced. It’s a great example of how you can take the characters, relationships, and stories from ancient myth and build a whole new story around them.

boardgame: Mythic Battles: Pantheon – Oedipus Vs. the Sphinx
description:
This Kickstarter Exclusive expansion contains one of the most unhappy characters in Greek myth, illustrated by one of our favourite pieces of art from all the many pieces we have had done for Mythic Battles: Pantheon.

This expansion contains just two miniatures: Oedipus and the Sphinx, but they are potent forces in the game. They both have unique playing styles, which add an interesting twist whether they’re being used in their dedicated campaign or in skirmish mode. Lots of replayability squeezed into these two.

That dedicated campaign I mentioned is a special 3-scenario mini-campaign, built around the conflict between these two characters: The Riddle of the Sphinx.

Of course, the pack also includes all the cards and dashboards you need to play these characters in the game.
This add-on brings the terrifying Manticore to your game along with all of the cards necessary to play with it. There is also a new scenario.

boardgame: Mythic Battles: Pantheon – Judges of the Underworld
description:
The Hell Judges box is the models of the 3 Judges, plus all the dashboards and cards you need to play them. But who are these Judges?

When Hades left the Underworld to fight alongside his fellow Gods, he left his wife, Persephone, in charge. She, in turn, enlisted the aid of Aeacus, Minos, and Rhadamanthus, who had all been famous lawgivers in life. They would help her to decide the correct treatment for each soul. That, at least, was the plan, and it worked for a while. Unfortunately the gates of the Underworld were open, and the dead began to escape back up to the realm of the living. Eventually the Judges followed suit, and now fight alongside the Gods, among the ruins.

boardgame: Mythic Battles: Pantheon – Heroes of the Trojan Wars
description:
The story of the Trojan Wars is one that all Greeks know well, and the fabled exploits of the many Heroes who fought in it are taught to every child. Now, in the time of such catastrophe, when the Gods are fallen from Olympus and walk the Earth as mortals, these legendary Heroes return to seek glory and honour once more.

The Mythic Battles: Pantheon core box already includes Achilles, and it seemed a shame not to bring some of his contemporaries to the table. So we have.

This add-on brings some of the Heroes of the Trojan Wars to a gaming table near you.

This expansion contains new models, tokens, cards, and more!
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

boardgame: Mythic Battles: Pantheon – Paolo Parente's Guest Box
description: This Kickstarter Exclusive box is intended to be a bit of fun rather than core content. It includes models and cards that you don’t actually need to play the game, so if you don’t like them then you can happily ignore the box and lose nothing.

The Thaug/Ketos miniature comes on its own, without any card pieces. The dashboard, activation cards, and Devour tokens necessary for play come in the Corinthia expansion. This miniature is added as a service to those non-Conan players who wanted to use all 3 huge sea monsters at the same time in their crazy navies.

User summary:
Players use sets of heroes cards in order to defeat villains that come from a separate villains deck. There is also a joker and two trump cards.
An optional companion app keeps interrupting play, asking players to make impressions and find specific cards. All cards have printed codes to accomplish this.

Description from the publisher:
"Os vilões do Reino de Ooo estão à solta! Ajudem Finn, Joke, Beemo, Princesa Jujuba e Marceline a capturá-los. Enfrente vários desafios e descubra quem é o vencedor.
Junte seus amigos e divirta-se com essa nova experiência!"

One of the fiercest Spetsnaz in the whole SSU, Ivan the Butcher earned his nickname thanks to his brutal methods in the field and as he trained a whole generation of SSU special forces. A ruthless soldier, those who serves under him fear him as much as his enemies. Ivan's arrival in the sands of Babylon will surely make them red with blood...

Even if he is still young by some standards, Podleytenant (Sub-Lieutenant) Vasily ‘Red Banner’ Kiritchenko is already a legend among the Spetsnaz. Kiritchenko has fought countless battles all over the planet in the name of the Motherland. He has spent the last few years with the 5th Guards Separate Spetsnaz Brigade, always at the forefront of special operations in dangerous places.

He came up with the idea of the Red Banner during the bloody battle around the Allied Embassy in Lima, Peru. After days of intense combat, the defending Spetsnaz were faltering in the face of attacks by ASOCOM heavy rangers. Remembering the revolutionaries of 1918, Kiritchenko grabbed a large red table cloth as a makeshift banner. Rallying locals and the surviving Spetsnaz around his position, he lead a charge that won the day for the SSU.

Even if it’s unique among special forces to carry a flag, Kiritchenko has done so ever since, earning him the nickname ‘Red Banner’, only now he carries the official standard of his regiment. Kiritchenko continues on the path to victory for the glory of the Sino-Soviet Union and the World Marxist Revolution.

A legend among the GRU Spetsnaz, “Iron Joe” leads all SSU special operations in Africa and the Middle-East at the same time. Which is pretty much all the Soviets can afford in the region, since they fight on too many fronts for their own good. Definitely one of the craftiest generals on the planet, Kameron makes the best out of it and maintains the pressure with the limited amount of troops he has. But these are the most elite of soldiers, and their general makes sure that every operation is carefully planned: they make up their number in efficiency.

boardgame: Robbi, Tobbi und das Fliewatüüt: Die Roboterprüfung
description:
Description from the publisher:

In Robbi, Tobbi und das Fliewatüüt: Die Roboterprüfung, Robbi and Tobbi are ready for the next robot test and now have to find new mysterious and hidden places and persons. Sitting in the helicopter cockpit, the players set course and head off on their journey — but is the triangular castle in the north or rather to the east? With skill, luck and good memory, the players will discover the places they seek and move the Fliewatüüt in the right direction.

Description from the publisher:

Creature Quest is the board game that teaches kids how positive & negative numbers work.  Just pick up a card, follow its instructions, and go.  Be the first to reach the treasure in the castle - but don't get turned to stone by Medusa!

An Unofficial Cards Against Humanity expansion.

Includes 112 NEW cards (90 White cards & 22 Black cards)

Professionally printed Casino Quality Linen Finish cards, 100% compatible with Cards Against Humanity.

Long ago, in a remote village at the edge of the kingdom, lived a Troll. This Troll roamed the streets demanding a toll from any traveller he met; if they refused to pay, he would steal their trousers. One day the Troll stopped a merchant travelling along the road. As usual, he demanded the merchant pay the toll in order to pass undisturbed. The merchant, who was very cunning, made the Troll a proposal…

Trade & Troll is an easy to play game as the rules and the main mechanism can be learned in about 20 minutes. The real challenge lies in how to best suit each player’s strategy to the developing situation on the board.

Players represent a merchant whose goal is to arrange an efficient production line from the collection of basic resources to their manufacturing and consequent trading in market stands. Each turn players can build resource/stall/market tiles, cobble a road or collect money from tiles they already possess.

At the end of the game, when the board representing the developing village is completely constructed, the player who owns the most developed market web, combined with the victory points collected differently during the game, wins.

Unfortunately, things become more complicated as a troll arrives in the village! The control of this character swaps each turn after a fierce auction between players, and the one who finally gets it has the opportunity to block the opponents’ trades until the end of the turn.

Trade & Troll is a European-style game for 2 to 6 players created by Good & Evil Games. It features a double-sided board map of T&T kingdom, 120 high-quality illustrated tiles, 73 wooden meeples, and numerous illustrated tokens.

—description from the publisher

In 1942, Japanese forces invaded the island of New Guinea and started a bitter, three-year campaign against allied Australian and American forces. Fought in dense jungles and across rugged mountaintops, the grueling fight pushed men to their very limits and forced commanders to adopt new strategies and tactics for the harsh island terrain. Filled with new rules, scenarios, and unit types, this supplement for Bolt Action provides players with all of the information they need to set their games in this unforgiving battlefield.

Regarded by many within the clan as the most fearsome shadow crow in battle. Katsumi is a weapons master, with a relish for utilizing any at his disposal to spill the blood of those that oppose the Kage Kaze Zoku. He became an adept at an incredibly young age, his squad formed with little of the meticulousness of others. His fellow worms intimidated into joining him and in truth they weren’t ready. Katsumi led them in an open and frontal attack on an adept, of some experience, his bloody assault lasting mere moments as the members of his squad looked on. They didn’t last long as adepts, their weaknesses obvious to the worms that remained. Katsumi however was never the subject of an attack by a worm squad, his fearsome reputation well-earned and unquestioned.

Blister contains one model, one 30mm base and one full colour profile card.

Rin is swift as she is deadly. Her ability to combine speed whilst remaining as quiet as a mouse with ferocious fighting skills makes her a real asset to the clan. Her twin katana blaze with the fury of her House and leave a trail of dead in her wake. Her lust for battle is matched only by that of Katsumi, also of the Bleeding Moon.

Blister contains one model, one 30mm base and one full colour profile card.

Yuto relished and excelled in training involving the use of a range of equipment, he believes that often some well-placed caltrops to thwart pursuers attempts to catch him or his squad or a smoke bomb to throw off their senses can change the course of a mission. His successful string of missions suggests there may be something to his claims. It is certain that members of any squad he is part of are glad to have him and his carefully selected assortment of items along with them.

Blister contains one model, one 30mm base and one full colour profile card.

Shizuka is a master of the shadows, her cautionary personality ensures her ideal mission is played out clean and without contact with the enemy.

As a worm Shizuka waited until the day before her twelfth birthday to launch an attack on an adept. She had been courted for a number of worm squads over the years and as time passed many thought she wouldn’t have the stomach for it. They assumed she was destined, despite her promise, to join the ranks of the Genin. Little did they know Shizuka was merely biding her time. Her waiting game paid off, she spent the time learning the strengths and weaknesses of her peers and assembled a squad she could be sure of.

Blister contains one model, one 30mm base and one full colour profile card.

From where he came from and who he is a mystery to all in the Kage Kaze Zoku save for the grand master. He never speaks and is never seen without his mask, it seems to the rest of his fellow shadow crows that he neither has need to eat nor sleep, spending most of his time on the training fields. None would ever consider questioning his place amongst them though. The word of the grand master is unquestioned and beside that fact the man fights like a demon.

Blister contains one model, one 30mm base and one full colour profile card.

Kouhei, like many from the House of the Long Shadow, prefers to operate from the darkness. His eyes, as sharp as an owl’s, give him a distinct advantage in the night. Even as an adept Kouhei’s ability to hide and move silently were noted by his superiors and more than one worm squad’s attack on him ended when they simply couldn’t find him. Having honed his skills on the dark side of ‘The Nest’ Kouhei completes most tasks equally well in light or darkness. For those that do come into contact with Kouhei, death is often quick and from the shadows.

Blister contains one model, one 30mm base and one full colour profile card.

Some rely on stealth, others guile or speed, however few on raw power such as Kerasu. Kerasu is an unstoppable machine focused on the annihilation of his enemies, cutting down foes as a farmer scythes wheat to get to his target.  He is a master with the bladed claws his favors as his weapons of choice. The speed and accuracy on his blows slicing veins and arteries in a blur of frenetic carnage.

Blister contains one model, one 30mm base and one full colour profile card.

Like all novices of the Kage Kaze Zoku, Ujimushi is mercilessly and relentlessly drilled in the arts of the Kage. Although adept in all Zoku martial weapons he has chosen the short bow as his preferred weapon. On missions he strives to master the skills of the veteran ninja with whom he serves, mimicking and practising their movements to aid in his own training.

Blister contains one model, one 30mm base and one full colour profile card.

Description from the publisher:

Sicaliptic is an erotic game for two players. (+18)

During the game the players propose each other a combination of an action and a body part from the cards of their hand.

Then, players thow the die. Depending on the number obtained, the player who chose even or odd numbers previously, wins.

The winner throws the die again and the number obtained will be the number of times (or seconds) that the other player must do the action proposed.

The winner will add a point in his own score indicator.

Each player will level up as they win rounds, and the exposed body parts will become more intimate as they do so.

The game will end when one of the players arrives to the end of the score indicator and the loser one becomes completely naked.

A promo Activity card for Chez Geek, featuring the web-series TableTop hosted by Wil Wheaton.

Description of the card:

- Activity (TV)
- Slack 1
- Cost 0

"This week on TableTalk, we play a board game and I get pwned! Like always!"

Description of the game:

In this light, humorous, and fast-paced card game that parodies geek culture and co-habitation, players are roommates just trying to get through life with as little work as possible.

At setup each player is dealt a Job card which lists their free time, income, a special ability that they possess and their Slack goal. Players are also dealt five Life cards. The space directly in front of a player is their Room, where cards will be played during the course of the game. Slack can be represented using any available chit or counter, but every player begins with a Slack total of zero.

Floh am Po is a vacation-themed kids’ roll-and-move game of fleas clinging tightly to the fur of big wooden dogs (thanks to Velcro).

Each player has their own dog that they want to be the very first to reach the destination. The more fleas a dog is carrying the faster it will run. If it's your turn, you roll colored dice hoping that fleas will jump off the other dogs and onto yours.

Advanced rules are also included.

Everything comes together when players strategically place pieces onto the magnetic board. Create the most circles to score points while sabotaging your opponents' scoring opportunities. With single player and multi-player options, the fun is endless with Zircles!

Contents: 48 pieces, 32 scoring markers, magnetic travel case with board, and instructions

- Easy-to-learn puzzle game where players strategically place pieces on to the board to score points
- Each piece must be placed adjacent to pieces of a different color - make your move to score or to sabotage your opponents
- Magnetic puzzle pieces to keep them from shifting around the board
- Great reply value with solo play for high scoring
- For 2 - 4 players ages 10 and up, with average game time of 30 minutes per game

Normandy, June 9, 1944. You have survived the brutal invasion of Omaha Beach, but your mission is not complete. You have been given new high-priority orders from General George C. Marshall to locate a missing paratrooper, Private First Class James Francis Ryan, and bring him home to safety.

Private Ryan’s whereabouts are unknown. Rumors place him directly in the middle of enemy-occupied territory laden with snipers, minefields, deadly tanks, and heavily fortified villages.

You have been made Captain and placed in charge of this mission. With limited intel and an even smaller chance of success, you gather a team, ready your equipment, and set off to bring Private Ryan home.

'D-Day Express: Saving Private Ryan' is a solo wargame played using a single deck of double-sided cards. There are no counters and no record-keeping is needed to play. Cards are used to represent terrain, enemies, friendly units, damage, mines, debris, and hills, as well as used to generate random events, direct enemy fire, manage close combat engagements, and even determine whether snipers are successful in hitting their targets.

'D-Day Express: Saving Private Ryan' is a portable, compelling wargame experience that is easy to set up, quick to take down, perfect for travel and gaming in tight spaces, and most importantly, is fun to play! Just shuffle the cards and play!

Description from the publisher:

Who will control Mars? Every day, the Big Four insinuate more of their products, services, management, and agendas into every aspect of Martian life, and in Earth's Scion, the third Data Pack in the "Red Sand Cycle" for Android: Netrunner, we watch as they spread more fully across the whole of the red planet. This leads to heightened tensions between Martian Corps, Runners, and Clans, and the sixty new cards (three copies each of twenty different cards) from Earth's Scion reveal just how far they may go in order to see their competing visions come to pass.

boardgame: A Game of Thrones: The Card Game (Second Edition) – All Men Are Fools
description:
Description from the publisher:

In the aftermath of the Battle of the Blackwater, a moment of peace falls across Westeros — but schemes are hatching in the darkness. All Men Are Fools is the first Chapter Pack of the "Blood and Gold" cycle for A Game of Thrones: The Card Game, and following the lead of the previous two cycles, this cycle focuses on the next book of the series, A Storm of Swords. At the same time, you'll find a new focus on your gold with a brand-new keyword and recurring events.
family: Books: A Song of Ice and Fire
Collectible: Living Card Game (Fantasy Flight Games)
Game: A Game of Thrones – The Card Game (LCG)

description: Valtaa hansalaiva is Finnish and translates into capture Hanseatic Ship. The game is part of Finnish Public Television's (YLE) historical educational series covering the pirates on the Baltic Sea in the early 15th century. Players are divided in two crews led by historic pirates Klaus Störtebecker and Birgit Fleming. The game is won by the crew that is able to first capture half of the 13 cabins and the captain's cabin. You are capturing cabins by rolling 5 or 6 with D6 while in the conquer space of a cabin. Member of the opposing crew in the same room can counter your capture attempt by rolling a 1.

From the publisher

The intuitive gameplay, different levels of difficulty and different game modes offer a satisfying puzzle experience. The independent Emoji 2 also provides for many new funny puzzles. Thus, unsuspecting players soon become uncontrolled puzzles, which they can assemble with the new cards themselves.

boardgame: Dust Tactics: Spetsnaz Command Squad – "Task Force Command"
description: The Spetsnaz SSU special forces have infiltrated deep in Mesopotamia. The newly formed chaos of Operation Babylon will be a new hunting ground for these ruthless men... The Spetsnaz Command Squad is a special unit of field officers whose skills are invaluable. They are equipped with automatic rifles, a machine gun and a flamethrower, and their presence on the battlefield can change the entire course of battle, thanks to their invaluable skills. A wise general should take care to protect these experienced officers.

The Spetsnaz have arrived in the Middle-East! Deployed alongside any SSU force, the Spetsnaz Assault Squads provide intelligence and are able to communicate efficiently with fire support units should they encounter any threat. These are even sneakier and deadlier Spetsnaz than the rest!

Spetsnaz is an acronym of Spetsialnogo Naznacheniya, or ‘Special Purpose Force’. These are soldiers capable of accomplishing special missions that regular units cannot. The ‘Killers’, the Spetsnaz kill squads, are just that. These men can overcome any situation they are put in, no matter how dangerous or desperate.

When the Spetsnaz Kill Squad is called into action, it’s to make sure that there are no survivors left, no building still standing, and no enemy vehicle in one piece - and to leave no evidence of who did it. They are the most ruthless soldiers in the SSU, with the possible exception of the famed Steel Guards, many of whom are former members of a kill squad anyway.

The Killers use regular Sino-Soviet Union weapons and equipment, mixed with a few extras of their own, but it’s the way they use them that makes the difference. Their preferred weapon is the Avtomat Kalashnikova 45 (AK-45) assault rifle, the future weapon of all SSU infantry. The Killers back these up with Sulphur Jet flame-throwers, DPM machine-guns, and two RPG-1 anti-tank launchers. Every Killer is also armed with a standard-issue Spetsnaz machete, the signature weapon of these troops for close-in and personal killing.

The Spetsnaz Kill Squad primary’s objectives are eliminating enemy infantry and leaders, light and heavy vehicles. They excel at these jobs. As long as it involves spreading mayhem, you can count on these men!

The Spetsnaz have infiltrated the Middle-East! Unlike other Spetsnaz units, these men wear a light body armor, to give them a little more protection. They have to get quite close to their targets, usually tanks and walkers, to be in good range of their powerful weapons. They are presented here in their Babylon camo, as used by SSU forces in the Middle-East, circa 1947.

Today the missions of the Spetsnaz Saboteur Squad have changed a bit, but the basics are the same: making big explosions that will cripple the enemy. Like other Spetsnaz forces, the Saboteurs use the AK-45 as their main weapon, though they don’t use it much - when they open fire it’s because they have been discovered, or it’s too late and they already have planted their charges.

The Spetsnaz have infiltrated the Middle-East! Nothing as efficient as a good artillery barrage to keep the enemy on its toes... Thanks to these observers, regimental and division artillery pieces can deliver the most powerful punch to crush the enemies of the SSU.
A roll and move set collection game where players race to assemble a team of players (and a manager/coach). The game incorporates cards that can be collected and traded.

boardgame: Dust Tactics: KV-47K (TSH) Light Tesla Walker – "Anatoly"
description:
Another tropical variation based upon the classic KV47 series, the L model, or Anatoly, mounts two very efficient Tesla guns, derived from the Red Lightning support weapon. The Anatoly’s capacitors allow it to fire both Tesla weapons in rapid succession, but this leaves it defenceless until the capacitors recharge. Being a Tesla weapon, it incapacitates its targets, even if it does not destroy them outright.

Using pack tactics, the KV-47L Anatoly can take on much larger walkers. Like its cousin the Aleksei, the Anatoly’s small size makes it perfect for special operations. It can easily be air-dropped, put on a landing craft, or travel through a dense jungle with equal facility.

boardgame: Dust Tactics: KV-47K (TSH) Light Gatling Walker – "Aleksei"
description: Special men with special missions need special equipment. The KV-47K (TSH - Tropicheskiy Shagokhod or ‘Tropical Walker’), codenamed ‘Aleksei’ is the perfect example of this maxim, as it was developed specifically for Spetsnaz units. Thanks to the walker being open-topped, its pilot suffers much less from the heat and has a better view and understanding of his surroundings. It sure makes him more vulnerable, but at least he’s not completely cooked at the end of the day. It also makes the walker lighter, which greatly increases its capacity to be transported to remote places.

boardgame: Blood Bowl (2016 Edition): The Dwarf Giants – Dwarf Blood Bowl Team
description:
A Dwarven expansion team with an out-of-the-box team value of 1,000,000 gold pieces. Ideal for playing against either Orcs or Humans in the base game.

Contains:
- 6 Blockers
- 2 Runners
- 2 Blitzers
- 2 Troll Slayers
- 2 team token/coins
- 2 unique blood bowl balls
- 1 team stats and assembly leaflet

boardgame: Dust Tactics: KV-47G (TSH) Light Assault Walker – "Mikhail"
description:
For years the Soviet army didn't care much for losses. The enemy was at the gate and had to be pushed back at all cost. If things could be easily salvaged or repaired, fine. But any more than that was just left to rot on the battlefield. This whole philosophy changed with the first vehicles derived from VK technology: even destroyed these were still very valuable. And even if nothing could be taken from the wreckage, it had to be properly destroyed so nothing would fall into enemy hands. This kind of job is much more seriously done outside of the battlefield.

Hence the creation of a new generation of KV47 with specialized task and equipment. With these, heavy SSU regiments could now take care of their precious vehicles directly during combat. And some of these models proved quite efficient to blow things up as well. The guys piloting these things are among the best mechanics on the planet. They already knew how to make pretty cheap but effective repairs with what they had at hand. But they know how to pilot a walker too.

boardgame: Blood Bowl (2016 Edition): Grak and Crumbleberry – Star Player Duo
description: A Forge World event or Warhammer World exclusive Star Player ogre/halfling combo team, with a unique special move: Kick Team Mate.

boardgame: Blood Bowl (2016 Edition): Special Play Cards – Hall of Fame Pack
description:
A set of additional Special Move cards that expands the tactical options provided by those included in the core game.

A ton of weird, wonderful and bizarre events can and will happen on and off the pitch during games of Blood Bowl, and they’re represented brilliantly by the Hall of Fame cards. Building on the cards included in the boxed game is this pack of 26 cards – split into two decks, the 10-card Magical Memorabilia and 16-card Heroic Feats decks – which bring out the anarchy of this Sport of Champions in fine style. Roll a dice at the beginning of your game to see how many each player can wield; a well-placed card can swing the tide in your favor. (from publisher's website)

boardgame: Dust Tactics: KV-47D (TSH) Light Anti-Tank Walker – "Nina"
description: One of the most common variant of tropical KV47 in use, with its huge RPG, Nina has a very interesting weapon system when facing armored targets. Its piercing shells are very efficient at piercing anything solid covered in steel.

boardgame: Dust Tactics: KV-47B (TSH) Light Gun Walker – "Natasha"
description: The most common variant of tropical KV47 in use, with its dual Howitzers Natasha has a very interesting weapon system when facing elite infantry. Its explosive shells are very efficient against any cover behind which enemy soldiers could hide.

The new game adventure of the Miki the Moth.
Straight from the Filobiblonia, the lend of books and games lovers. SPRINTO - is the favorite board game of bookworms, Grace and aunt Zgryzelda. "The day without Sprinto play with a cup of rose tea, is a lost day" - according to the Miki the Moth.
Let's try to play in the really loony board game, invite your freinds and family. As soon as possible move pawns on yours route to the end. Do you think, it's easy? No way!
You can lose all your pawns and give them back to the beginning or... with frozen pawns idly watch how the other wins!

boardgame: Rising Sun, Tumbling Bear: A Solitaire Game of the Russo-Japanese War (1904-1905).
description:
BACKGROUND: RISING SUN, TUMBLING BEAR is semi-historical simulation of the Russo-Japanese War (1904-1905) at the dawn of the 20th Century. The Game begins shortly after the Japanese surprise naval attack on the Russian warships docked in Port Arthur. The Russians are now building up their forces and gathering their fleets to kick the Japanese out of Manchuria and Korea. The Japanese must capture a number of key Russian controlled settlements before Russia is able to effectively mobilize an overwhelming force into the region or the United States President, Theodore Roosevelt, signs the Peace Treaty of Portsmouth.

OBJECTIVE: As the Commander of the Japanese Naval and Land Forces, you must gather your land and naval forces to capture a number of Key Russian Controlled Settlements before the Russian Baltic Fleet reaches Port Arthur or President Theodore Roosevelt signs the Portsmouth Peace Accord. The Japanese must control all 7 of the Key Russian Settlements in order to win. If the Baltic Fleet reaches Vladivostok, they win.

RISING SUN, TUMBLING BEAR features many colorful Unit and Ship Counters and fast and furious Combat systems for both land and naval combat.

boardgame: The World of SMOG: Rise of Moloch – OHMS Gentlemen Set
description: A kickstarter-exclusive expansion to The World of SMOG: Rise of Moloch, consisting of six additional Gentlemen - Suzanne Taylor, Colonel Steel, John Brown, Sir Daniel Home, Master Fox and Parvin Khan - each with their own dashboard and card.

The Glass Peak is a party game that draws from inclusive ideas that the authors appreciated in games. The main idea is simple: roll a die, pick up a card, perform the task. First player to reach the last square on the board wins. The Volcano Cup and your own friend's shenanigans will make this a night to remember!

While the game is a party game and does encourage folks to indulge it prioritizes having a good time over anything else.

The Kitsune has broken from the ranks of her people by allying herself with the Temple's cause. Her mobility on the battlefield greatly improves the Temple's ability to meet their objectives.

Blister contains one Kitsune miniature, one Fox form miniature, two 30mm bases and two full colour profile cards.

The Temple of Ro-Kan gain some real punching power, the mighty fists of a Gorilla. Fiercely protective of Aiko, the Gorilla is a fearsome opponent at the best of times, when he is imbued by Aiko’s blessing he becomes almost invincible.

Blister contains two miniatures, one 30mm base, one 40mm base and two full colour profile cards.

The guardians of the temples, these silent sentinels have stood for eons outside many a shrine or holy place. Now with the realised threat of another wave they have been reanimated from their long slumber. Always fighting in pairs, the links between the two run right to the very core of their being. In this time of strife, the return of the Shisa is a timely relief.
 Blister contains two miniatures, two 30mm bases and full colour

A favourite of the temple elders and new initiates alike, Riku personifies what it means to be a Monk. His mastery over water allows him to achieve awe inspiring feats on the battlefield, creating walls of water out of the very air to hinder his opponents.

boardgame: Mystic Vale: Das Tal der Magie & Das Tal der Wildnis
description:
Mystic Vale: Das Tal der Magie & Das Tal der Wildnis consists of two expansions in one box.

In Das Tal der Magie, the druids have mysterious new spells at their disposal, but their special powers can quickly destroy them. In Das Tal der Wildnis, the druid players meet mysterious leaders who can help them fight the curse on the land.

Three different "city" tiles, which produce and accumulate resources (these are not removed during clean up): either location cards,  workers or ammunition. Cities are considered shared grounds, multiple players can make a deal with them, in which case, they get in each production phase the given resources. One player can also raze a city, in which case s(he) receives all accumulated resources and the city tile is removed from the game.

Was initially part of the 2016 released pre-order promos for 51st State Master Set, but released separately in the webshop later on. Rules do not come in printed form, but have to be looked up on the Portal website.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
2 new factions for the base game. Texas gives an extra worker in production and converts all three resources on a ratio of 1:1 to contact tokens. (But you are allowed to choose one of these three actions a second time per round.) Hegemony gives an extra wild contact token in production and converts grey and blue resources on a ratio of 1:1, red resources 1:2. (As usual, each only once per round.)

Was initially part of the 2016 released 51st State: Master Set – Pre-Order Promos, but released separately in the webshop later on.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
5 new location cards for 51st State Master Set, labled "Promo Set 1" in the bottom right corner.

"Wrecked Airplane" differs only in title and artwork from "Wrecked Tank" of the base set. The other cards provide new and unique actions or features, without introducing particularly new mechanisms.

boardgame: Basket of Deplorables: The Board Game
description:
This is a light-hearted, humor-filled, liberal-leaning game about the state of politics from 2016 to 2020. The good guys (player pieces) are Bernie Sanders, Elizabeth Warren, Barack Obama, and Hillary Clinton. They're vying to take on Trump in 2020 and filling their Basket of Deplorables along the way. The Deplorables in this game are not meant to be an insult to Trump supporters, rather they are ghoulish caricatures of people in the Trump inner circle, i.e. Steve Bannon, Rudy Giuliani, Vladimir Putin, etc.

The players navigate the campaign trail and participate in debates, compete in primaries/caucuses, navigate scandals, seek endorsements, confer with pollsters, decide whether to run as an independent, etc. Deplorables can be drained from The Swamp and placed in each player's Basket.

boardgame: Shadows of Malice: Promo 2016 Card Set
description: A set of ten playable cards which can be incorporated into their respective decks. There are six new treasure cards (Black Tesseract, Blue Tesseract, Colorless Tesseract, Green Tesseract, Red Tesseract, Yellow Tesseract), four fate cards (Crumble to Dust - instant, Curse of Umbral - lasting, Essence Shift - invoked, Mirrorlight - invoked), rules reference card and also a title card.

boardgame: Kingdom Death: Monster – Anonymous Survivor Promo
description:
Anonymous Survivor is now officially Wanderer Aeneas!

Wanderer Aeneas arrives looking for a snack but stays to tell her story at the dinner table.

Wanderer Aeneas is packaged in a uniquely designed graphical box and includes:
- 1x Aeneas illustrated rulebook
- 1x Aeneas hard plastic miniature
- 1x Aeneas hard plastic scenic base insert
- 1x Kingdom Death 30mm base
- 3x Knowledge cards
- 2x Secret fighting art cards
- 3x Indomitable resource cards
- 1x Universal trait card
- 1x Divider
- 1x Rare gear card
- 1x Innovation card
- 1x Fighting art card
- 1x Dual Modes tile

boardgame: Kingdom Death: Monster – Goth Amy, the unreveling Crossover
description:
The ultimate survivior, Goth Amy always narrowly escapes death.

She endured the great fall, scrambling in mid-air over plummeting, screaming survivors to land on top of the less fortunate broken bodies below.

She escaped the Dark Seamstress, stealing a blade ond chopping off its grasping hand. Amy found several stubbed bracelet trophies wrapped around its stump.

Even as she awoke falling to her doom, Goth Amy felt an inkling that she belongs. Casting aside fear and compassion, she relished a place where the last threads of her humanity could be cast aside with glee.

This chrossover expansion contains:
1 Goth Amy Miniature
1 Goth Amy Small Booklet
Story Events (est 1-2)
Unique Base Insert
Psychopath Philosophy
1 Secret Fighting Art
3 Gear Cards

Amy is classified as a "Wanderer" which is a new compaign-related system introduced in the Advanced KDM rulebook.

boardgame: Dust Tactics: IS-5D Heavy Tesla Tank – "Aleksandr Vasilevsky"
description:
For hundreds of millions of citizens of the Sino-Soviet Union, Iosef Stalin is the Supreme Leader, whose wisdom will guide them to victory. For the enemies of the SSU, the name means death. Many of the SSU’s early heavy tanks were named after Kliment Voroshilov, the Defense Commissar. When it was time for a new and improved series, who better to name it after than the Supreme Leader himself?
The “Aleksandr” is the latest version of the IS-5, mounting two impressive Tesla Guns that fire on the same target. The result is devastating again any type of target but particularly against armor. The vehicle is presented here in its Babylon pattern camouflage, as observed around Mesopotamia circa 1947.

boardgame: Dust Tactics: IS-5C Heavy Assault Tank – "Iosef Stalin"
description:
For hundreds of millions of citizens of the Sino-Soviet Union, Iosef Stalin is the Supreme Leader, whose wisdom will guide them to victory. For the enemies of the SSU, the name means death. Many of the SSU’s early heavy tanks were named after Kliment Voroshilov, the Defence Commissar. When it was time for a new and improved series, who better to name it after than the Supreme Leader himself?

The Iosef Stalin super-heavy tank is armed with an efficient combination of weapons, each of which has been proven on other tanks. Its main weapon is a 183mm (7.2”) D-47T gun. Although not designed to destroy vehicles, the mass of this weapon’s round can knock out any tank, no matter how heavy. Its powerful explosive shell is overkill against infantry, but is death to bunkers.

The secondary weapon is an ATO-45B flamethrower mounted alongside the main gun. It is ideal for destroying infantry in buildings and strongpoints. That’s if there are still some in range when the Iosef Stalin gets there. Usually there’s only wreckage left, or the enemy has fled.

boardgame: Kingdom Death: Monster – Death Drifter Crossover
description:
Legend claims that there was once a man whi willed himself back from in-existence, event the Phoenix's time manipulation couldn't stop him. With no memory of his time, the Drifter wonders the stonescape for the Hooded Knight, as he recognizes it's sword to be the key to understand when he is.

Contains:
1 x Death Drifter hard plastic miniature
1 x Death Drifter scenic base insert
1 x Kingdom Death 30mm base 
5 x Knowledge cards 
1 x Fighting art card 
5 x Arc survivor record sheets 
1 x Divider
4 x Rare gear cards
1 x Glossary card
1 x Strain milestone card

Drifter is classified as a "Wanderer", which is a new campaign-related system introduced in the Advanced KDM rulebook.

boardgame: Dust Tactics: ISU-203 Heavy Assault Gun – "Sergei Shtemenko"
description: Since 1943 the ISU format, a heavy self-propelled gun mounted on a tank chassis, has become a staple of the SSU armored forces. Easy and cheap to build, efficient and deadly, these vehicles are now found everywhere on the planet where the SSU fights. The “Sergei” is the latest version and mounts a 203mm D-30S very heavy gun, capable of knocking out any enemy armored target. The vehicle is presented here in its Babylon pattern camouflage, as observed around Mesopotamia circa 1947.

boardgame: Kingdom Death: Monster – Lantern Year 3 Candy & Cola Crossover
description:
Wanderer Candy escaped a deathmatch arena crumbling in flames. When she arrives, choking in smoke, she discovers that memories of a distant past weave a melancholy tale of longing for a brighter world.

Wanderer - Candy is packaged in a uniquely designed graphical box and includes:

1 x Candy hard plastic miniature
    1 x Cola scenic base insert
    1 x Kingdom Death 30mm base
    3 x Knowledge cards 
    2 x Fighting art cards 
    1 x Glossary card 
    1 x Divider
    11 x Rare gear cards
    1 x Innovation card
    5 x Arc survivor record sheets

Wanderers are new to Kingdom Death: Monster with the release of the Gambler's Chest expansion. Any campaign can now add a wanderer, a mysterious stranger that makes your settlement their temporary home.

As they fight alongside your settlement, their own story unfolds. Secrets, skills, and more are shared by your visitor. Cultivate a relationship as the wanderer fights along with your survivors to protect your settlement. When they leave, as they all inevitably do, they will leave an indelible mark of their life on your campaign.
Each additional player expansion for MedioEvo Universalis includes 96 miniatures, 1 player board, 1 player screen and 25 additional metal coins.
