description:
Race to the middle of the board by moving your tiles like chess pieces.  With timely moves, you can place your opponent in a state of DURESS!

Duress is a two-player strategic board game.  It is intended for adults and children ages 8 and up.

Each player has 16 tiles with different letters (A through P).  These same tiles are arranged as Goal Pieces in the middle of the board.  The Goal Pieces are arranged symmetrically, equidistant from the two players.  Once a layout is selected, the Goal Pieces are arranged in a random order.  The two players may also decide to take turns selecting the placement of the individual tiles, which adds strategy to the game.

There are also Movement Pieces corresponding to chess pieces.  In a classic game of Duress, there are 16 Movement Pieces: 8 pawns, 2 rooks, 2 knights, 2 bishops, 1 queen, and 1 king.

Once the Goal Pieces are arranged, the two players take turns.  In a single turn, the player:

a) Selects a Movement Piece at random.

b) Selects one of the tiles to move.

c) Moves the selected tile to a new location based on the Movement Piece.  Other tiles can block the movements of the pieces, creating opportunities for strategic play.

Players score points by landing their pieces on the corresponding Goal Pieces before the opponent can get there.  So, the two A tiles are racing to reach the A in the Goal Pieces.  The same goes for each letter.

A player wins the game by scoring a majority of the points.

There are many opportunities to change the structure of the game:

a) Each game randomizes the order of the Goal Pieces, creating a different strategic challenge for the players.

b) Players can take turns selecting the order of the Goal Pieces, creating additional strategy.  When one player goes first in the selection, and the other goes first in moving pieces, the potential for a first player's bias can be mitigated.

c) The players may create alternative layouts for the board.

d) As an alternative, players may select and hold 3 pieces.  This allows for planning moves in advance.  With each turn, the player will select one of the three movement pieces, move a tile, return this piece to the bag, and randomly select a new one.

With many potential variations, no two games of Duress are exactly alike.

A twelve card, two player, print and play game derived from the first issue of Punks THE COMIC by Joshua Fialkov and Kody Chamberlain.

Players assume the role of either Dog or Skull and take the corresponding 6 cards into their hand.

These include:
2 x Attack Cards
2 x Defense Cards
1 x Nut Puncher
1 x Bummer

Players put down one card each on the count of 3.  Winner of each individual turn gets a point.  Nut Puncher v Nut Puncher = 1 point each.

Defense > Attack 
Attack > Nut Puncher
Nut Puncher > Defense
Bummer = Void Hand

If you both play the same category of card the hand is void.

Winner with most points at end of hand wins the round.
Winner of the most rounds gets a "punch in the nuts" or firm handshake.

boardgame: Kingdom Death: Monster – Inverted Mountain Expansion
description: The Inverted Mountain expansion combines the Frog Dog, Oblivion Mosquito, Pariah, Black Knight, Red Witches, Nightmare Ram, and Gryphon expansions into one campaign!  Complete with unique story events, innovations, gear, hunt event cards, settlement event and all the bells and whistles.  Guide the People of the Mountain.  Struggle with them to survive.  Climb the treacherous Inverted Mountain.  Battle the Man that sits alone, atop it all!

boardgame: Kingdom Death: Monster – The Silver City Expansion
description:
The Silver City expansion dramatically changes up the mid-late game, introducing itself around Lantern Year 12 or 13.  Survivors of the settlement can choose to make trips into the Silver City, for a very different game experience.  This expansion will rewrite a chunk of the game timeline, ending with a climactic battle with the Lion God.

You will encounter bone-eating madmen, wearing silverlight jellyfish skin, old half-working guardians, silverlight jellyfish, leaping octopi, the Lost Knight, and more!
Love Letter: Erweiterung contains ten new characters for use with the Love Letter base game to allow up to eight players in a game.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
Oh, no! The apartment is a mess, with things scattered everywhere — time to clean up! Luckily, Timmy has some children over who can bring everything to the right place.

In Timmy räumt auf, the players take turns rolling dice to move Timmy around the apartment and find items that fit into the color-coded rooms. Each room should have three objects in it, and whoever finds the last missing item in a room earns a point. When the house is tidy, the player with the most points wins.

Bang, pow! Someone didn't think ahead clearly when the animals' balloon party was planned and Sabrina the porcupine was invited.

In Sabrina Stachelschwein, each player has a number of balloon cards on the table in front of them, with the balloons having different values and all of the balloons being placed in a circle. Sabrina wants to say "hello" to all of her friends, so she keeps running around the circle, with her movement determined by a die roll. Wherever she stops, the balloon pops, with that card being placed on a discard pile. Sabrina can circle in either direction, and players can add new balloons to the table as well.

Once one player runs out of balloons, the game ends, and whoever owns the most valuable balloons wins.

Cats love treats, but these kittens are somewhat selective and prefer to eat only certain colors of treats.

In Katz & Schmaus, players need to try to feed the cats the right treats, but they see these treats only at the beginning of their turn, after which they're hidden. Someone attempts to assign a treat to the proper cat by shaking the game boxes. If someone thinks that a cat is getting the wrong treat, though, they can grab the cat figure from the center of the table to call out the shaker and have them open the box. If the doubter was right, they receive the treat as a reward, but otherwise the shaker collects the treat.

In the end, whoever collects the most delicious treats wins.

The midnight hour has arrived, and the ghosts are now pressing their noses against the window — but they're scared of their own shadows, so who can find the right shadow to drive them to flight?

Each turn in Ghost Catchers (a.k.a. Gespenster am Fenster), one of the ghosts arrives at the window, and players compete to find the proper shadow for this ghost to scare it away. Whoever thinks they have the right shape, places the transparent card on the ghost and if they're correct, the ghost flees and the player scores. The shadows are all similar, however, and you can try to scare each ghost only once, so look carefully!

The game can played with children from age 4-1/2 and up, with the number of ghosts in play being adjusted to make the game more difficult for older players.

Okay, don't panic now, but monsters are lying underneath your bed. You're quite sure of this because you've seen them first-hand — but wait? How many were there? What color were they? And couldn't some of them actually have been teddy bears?

In Gute Nacht Monster, players roll six dice each round, with the dice showing monsters and teddy bears in three different colors. All players need to remember these dice as quickly as they can because the dice then disappear under the bed and a task card is revealed, with players being asked, for example, how many green monsters are present. All players answer in secret, and whoever is correct moves their figure one step closer to the light switch. Whoever manages to turn on the light wins!

boardgame: Battle Bots: The Sling Sliders Game
description: Battle Bots: The Sling Sliders Game is a crokinole-style game that has players using an elastic band to shoot wooden discs into the centre of the game board, or into an opponent's area of the game board, to earn points. Get rid of all of your discs by slinging them straight or ricocheting them on your opponent's side of the board.

Koji has been waiting as patiently as he could for his time to protect all he holds dear. Since he turned away a lone ninja Koji has finally been allowed that chance by the temple elders. Individually Koji and his macaques would fall quickly but united they stand. The shared love and loss of Kita has cemented the bond between boy and monkeys to something akin to brotherhood, each willing to fall in place of the next.
 Blister contains four miniatures, four 30mm bases and two full colour profile cards.

Hotaru thanks the Kami’s for guiding her to the Temple of Ro-Kan and Master Ekusa. She has learned to contain her powers but still fears unleashing her full potential. Hotaru’s desire to control her innate abilities provides little comfort for opponents left ablaze by her fiery attacks. The Temple have found a welcome addition to their order in Hotaru.

Blister contains one miniature, one 30mm base and one full colour profile card.

Master Po’s return has caused quite a stir in the halls of the temples of Ro-Kan. His jovial presence has lifted morale of every monk from the initiates, to his fellow masters. Po’s manipulation of Ki belies his rotund appearance allowing him to be deadly in close combat, many have been caught cold as the big man lands hammering blows.
 Blister contains one miniature, one 30mm base and one full colour profile card.

boardgame: Bushido: Minor Kami of Fire and Water
description:
The extent of the unbalance facing the world is highlighted by the emergence of these minor Kamis. While they are a mere shadow in comparison to their godlike brethren they nevertheless have brought their not insignificant powers to bear in the name of the Temple of Ro-Kan. They allow the monks to do what they do best.
 Blister contains two miniatures, two 30mm bases and two full colour profile cards.

As trouble and unrest sweeps across the Jwar Isles the unlikeliest of heroes are emerging to support the Temple of Ro-Kan. Kintaru is one such man, a peasant more at home fishing the rivers and lakes of the mountains than fighting. Nether the less he has given the strength of his arms and his keen eye to protect that which he holds dear.
 Blister contains one miniature, one 30mm base and one full colour profile card.

From DriveThruCards.com

Spy Tactics is a secret agent themed MicroGame for 4-9 players who will form 2-3 teams of agents. Agencies are Chimera, Tachyon, and Vex. Each agent receives an Alignment card, a point tracker card and 8 code cards. (The same 8 code cards are given to each agent.) After examining their code cards, each agent will choose one to discard. The other 7 form a player's hand, though only 6 of these will come into play.

Agents activate their cards simultaneously. Some have repercussions or rewards if other agents activate the same card simultaneously. In addition to scoring points for your agent team, you may attempt to eliminate opposing agents, though there is also a chance of eliminating team mates. In the end, remaining agents on a team will add their victory points. The highest scoring agency wins.

This is a basic roll-and-move game -- similar to Snakes and Ladders -- where the goal is simply to be the first to reach the finish space. The game board consists of a series of spaces, each requiring the player to perform some drinking-related task. "Staggers" allow the player to skip spaces ahead (like the Ladders) and "Pukes" cause the player to lose ground (like the Snakes). Due to the increasing level of intoxication of the players during the game, there is no guarantee that all (or any) players will make it to the final space.

Operation: Overfur is a family friendly card game where players take turns playing cards that match the species or class of the card on the top of the discard stack.

However the game has various special cards that can be played to trip up your friends and family.

Persevere through the special cards and be the first player to place the last character card in your hand on the pile to win the game.

boardgame: Birds and Squirrels: black bird edition
description:
Description from the publisher:

A Kickstarted creation. The second by Scolopendra Games Creator Joseph Pendergast.

Rules of play:
Each person gets a player strip and the cards are placed face down on the table. If you do not have enough strips for all the players just remember to stop at 6 cards. Take turns flipping cards onto your player strips in turn.
Squirrel cards clear your line and you get no points.
Hawk cards clear all lines and no points are collected.
White raven cards are wild and the suggested use is to double the score of a line but, it is up to you.
Once you have successfully filled a line with cards you may choose to keep your score by counting the number of birds up and adding them to what you already have acquired or if any blank cards are present you may try to gain more points by waiting to cover them a turn at a time until you desire to stay with what you have. After a line is successfully cleared draw a new card and play resumes. If a hawk or squirrel clears your line you do not pick up a new card.
Place all used birds in a face-up discard pile.  You may not draw from this discard pile. When you run out of cards shuffle the discard and continue play.
Once the agreed upon score is reached by any player either that player wins after all other players finish their turns or all other players have a chance to overcome the winner before the deck runs out.
This rule card may be used as an optional 4 bird card.
For a quick single player game, try to make it to 30 points in one round through the deck.
The number of points you choose to go to decides how long gameplay will be I generally play to 30 but some people will play to 1000.

boardgame: Star Trek: Attack Wing – Senior Staff Resource
description:
The Senior Staff Resource is a participation prize given to players who participated in the "Yesterday's Enterprise" episode Organized Play Event. It is considered a Resource, and costs a variable number of Squadron Points.

When using this resource, every ship in your fleet can field an additional Elite Talent for every Crew assigned to it.  Each extra talent is assigned to a specific Crew and is discarded if it's associated Crew is discarded.  The total cost of the resource is 1 Squadron Point per extra Elite Talent that you field via the resource.

Poker-Go is based on the rules of normal go.

At the beginning of the game both decks are shuffled and put on the table face downward. During the game the players can choose  at each turn between making a go move or drawing a card.

What can you expect from this expansion?

     Almighty Lord Vortexeios who comes when the Golem is destroyed!
     Heroes have to defeat Lord Vortexeios if they want to escape from the Labyrinth!
     Lord Vortexeios has much Life and on top of that he moves quickly around the board!
     Lord Vortexeios spreads havoc and mayhem by destroying the Labyrinth or changing its layout!
     Keys and magic items help in combat with this powerful creature!


Story:
The Golem has guarded the Labyrinth for ages…
But he was not always as we know him now.

Before his creation, the desert was inhabited by Sand Elementals.
The sole purpose of their existence was turning the whole world into the Great Desert.

It was because the sands gave power that could threaten even the Ancients themselves.
When the Elementals grew in power, their ruler, Lord Vortexeios,
Mustered a huge army and attacked the races created by the Ancients.
Many lives were lost, buried under the sands and thrown into an endless sleep.

It seemed nothing could stop Vortexeios.
But then the Ancients intervened.

They used the most powerful artifacts known to them.
They crushed the army of the Elementals so that only few escaped.
Finally, they defeated Lord Vortexeios and locked him
inside a sentient construct - the Golem.

This creation has been bereaving Lord Vortexeios of power
for centuries so that he can never free himself to plague the world.

However, what will happen if the Golem is destroyed?
Will Vortexeios, Lord of Sand Elementals return?

Avast, me hearties! Get ready to plunder all the booty you can carry in this whale of an expansion for Buck: Legacy!
Features a new and improved Pirate class card!
The pack features 13 cards, adding new items, treasures, dungeons and areas to explore!

boardgame: Dust Tactics: Mercenary Heavy Machine Gun Squad – "Sisters of Mayhem"
description: Heavy firepower and very strong interception capability. Mercenary heavy machine gun squad is a good supporting in your army. Skillful girls team available for hire, all of them from a different country of the Neutral Nations Organization

Description from the publisher:

Out of the Frying Pan and Into the Oven! Things are heating up in the latest expansion in Buck: Legacy!
Features the new race- Nagapon!
The pack features 13 new cards, adding new items, treasures, dungeons and areas to explore!

Out of the Fridge and into the Freezer! Cool off in in this frosty expansion for Buck: Legacy!
Features the new race- Gryphon!
The pack features 13 new cards, adding new items, treasures, dungeons and areas to explore!

For years now women have played a very important role in the war effort of every bloc, as men have been sent to the front. Some of them have even been sent close, in repair workshops or ammo dumps. They are now a common sight in fields where none would be seen just a few years ago. Justine and her team are top mechanics for hire, all from a different country of the Neutral Nations Organization.

boardgame: Kingdom Death: Monster – Screaming God Expansion
description:
The Screaming God Expansion extends your campaign by +5 Lantern Years, pushing back the Gold Smoke Knight.  It allows the players to chase down a new powerful armor set, and if they are lucky, challenge the Lantern Parasite Queen!

Content Pitch:
2 x Screaming God miniature (100mm)
4 x Screaming God Narrative Armor sculpts
1 x Lantern Parasite Queen (50mm)
1 x Illuminated Lady miniature
1 x Illuminated Man miniature
120 x Game Cards
1 x Settlement Location
2 x Settlement Events
16 Page Rulebook
40 x Gear Cards
...and more!

boardgame: Kingdom Death: Monster – Death Armor Expansion
description:
Can you manage to craft it in time to face to Gold Smoke Knight at the end of your campaign?

Made from the bones of only the greatest monsters, the Death Armor is worthy of making it your not-so-secret goal.  Even better than just surviving!

Put a Bone Eater in one of these and watch things get nuts!

Requires pretty much all of your settlement bones to craft.

Contains:
2 x Death Armor Narrative sculpts
1 x Settlement Location
1 x 6 Page Rulebook
25 x Gear Cards
...and more!

boardgame: Kingdom Death: Monster – Pathfinders of Death Crossover Promo Expansion
description:
Description from the publisher:

This crossover projects brings 4 iconic Pathfinder characters into the nightmare horror world of Kingdom Death!

Includes a revamped, more in-depth hero mode, and alternate Pathfinders of Death campaign, where you can take on the role of those heroes as your starting survivors.

Each hero has a unique, "classic class" inspired Age Story Event (for leveling up).  Similar to the Philosophy System, there is a chance that you can share their unique knowledge with your settlement.  But be warned, intelligent monsters are just as capable of learning as survivors are.  Higher level versions of the Butcher, King's Man, and Watcher threaten to crush these heroes before they have a chance to blossom.

Contains:

1 x Rulebook (12-20+ pages)
1 x Merisiel, Pathfinder of Death miniature
1 x Valeros, Pathfinder of Death miniature
1 x Kyra, Pathfinder of Death miniature
1 x Seoni, Pathfinder of Death miniature
1 x Pathfinder of Death Light - Campaign for KDM 1.5
30+ x Game Cards
2 x Settlement Events cards
12 x Gear Cards
...and more!

boardgame: Kingdom Death: Monster – Super Survivors Promo Expansion
description:
If the settlement has a savior, there is a chance they will be having a terrible Nightmare!  In order to save them, the survivors will have to dream her dream of a super-charged battle!  Keep in mind, this is "promo content", so it's going to be a little silly, but a lot of fun!

Includes campaign integration, story events, and Lantern Power Scouter!

Contains:
Perfect Dung Bettle Knight miniature
Super Survivor Erza miniature
Super Survivor Lucy miniature
Super Survivor Zachary miniature
Super Survivor Allister miniature
Rulebook for a Super Nemesis showdown
60 x Game Cards
1 x Settlement Event card
3 x Story Events

Description from the publisher:

Kawa no Rojin stampedes his way across the battlefield trampling any that get in his way. Considered to be a lucky animal by the Monks of the Ro-Kan, his presence drives them on to courageous acts of valour.

Game in The Black Hole Annual 1981, a Disney Corporation film.

A simple race game. The track has 100 spaces. Most spaces are fine. However, if you land on a white space, you find that the gravity force has cut your engine power. You must stay on that space every turn until you throw a 6 which boosts your engine power and allows you to carry on with your journey. If you land on a black space, this represents extra power by the Black Hole and you must return to the Start space and start again on your next turn. The rules say "Care should be taken to avoid the white and black hazard squares". Well, that would be great but seeing you have to move with just a die roll, it is imp0ossible to "take care".

The winner is the first to reach the Clear space at the end of the track.

boardgame: Bushido: Minor Kami of Earth and Air
description:
Description from the publisher:

The Kami can no longer sit by and watch the Balance be thrown further from its natural alignment. Whilst the Temple benefit greatly from the advantages these Kami grant their forces, the stirring of the elements is a further sign of how much work is to be done.

boardgame: Kingdom Death: Monster – False Messengers Promo
description:
Description from the publisher:

Contains:

1 x Messenger of Courage miniature
1 x Messenger of the First Story miniature
1 x Messenger of Humanity miniature
1 x Messenger of the Spiral Path miniature

Also includes newly-developed rules for the False Messengers.

Long have the Temple of Rokan and the peasants lived in symbiotic harmony. In times of great turmoil the two continue the alliance. The Fishermen who make their lives from the great Sune-Kudansu, fishing from her many tributaries now add their talents to fight. Whether slipping through terrain without trace or calling upon of their cormorant allies they stand opposed to all enemies of the Temple.

The Ranger Attack Squad carries a deadly range of weapons. With these they can destroy anything in their path: tanks, walkers, bunkers, etc. Nothing can stop them. They are rightly feared as one of the deadliest unit on the battlefield. A Ranger Attack Squad includes three soldiers armed with a Shotgun and two armed with a portable Flamethrower. They are presented here in their Cerberus Camo pattern, as used in Great-Britain and Northern Europe, circa Winter 1947.

Raise the anchors and set sail as Port Royal: The Adventure Begins... introduces a campaign mode to Port Royal.

At the beginning of the game, someone reads the prologue of the scenario to be played, then before the start of each turn, players reveal order cards from an event deck that might require the collection of certain crew members or trade with certain ships; players might receive a bonus for certain actions  during the turn. Order cards have a time limit, however, after which new cards are played from the event deck, and the end of a scenario depends on which orders have been executed.

Port Royal: The Adventure Begins... can be played either competitively or cooperatively.
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description: The Ranger Attack Squad carries a deadly range of weapons. With these they can destroy anything in their path: tanks, walkers, bunkers, etc. Nothing can stop them. They are rightly feared as one of the deadliest unit on the battlefield. A Ranger Attack Squad includes three soldiers armed with a Shotgun and two armed with a portable Flamethrower. They are presented here in their Cerberus Camo pattern, as used in Great-Britain and Northern Europe, circa Winter 1947.

boardgame: Dust Tactics: First Sergeant James Murphy – "Crazy Jimmy"
description: The Marines have arrived in the Middle-East! And First Sergeant James Murphy of the United States Marine Corps is going to lead his armored force into whatever situation arises, and come out on top as usual. This amazing pilot is a ferocious foe for anyone who stands in the way of the might of the Allied army.

boardgame: Dust Tactics: Master Sergeant Frank Stone – "Quiet Jack"
description: The Marines have arrived in the Middle-East! And Master Sergeant Frank Stone of the United States Marine Corps is going to lead his men into whatever situation arises, and come out on top as usual. This rugged veteran is a ferocious foe for anyone who stands in the way of the might of the Allied arm

boardgame: Dust Tactics: Gunnery Sergeant Q 'Mack' MacDonald – "Machette Mack"
description: Gunnery Sergeant Q 'Mack' MacDonald has been with the United States Marine Corps for more than ten years now. Twice its boxing champion, 'Mack' has unsurpassed martial skills that he puts to good use against all enemies of the Allies, mostly Japanese in the Pacific.

boardgame: Hell in the Pacific: Plan Orange 1931 and 1935
description:
Decription from the publisher:

Before World War II America anticipated a war with Japan and created Plan Orange.

Hell in the Pacific allows owners of the classic Victory in the Pacific to test out a war between Japan and America in 1931 or 1935. It is a conflict unlike the actual Pacific war, dominated by fleets of battleships, with aircraft playing an important, but largely supporting role. France and Britain can be drawn into the war if the Japanese are overly aggressive. Also included are rules for Japanese fuel shortages and optional rules for players of the Avalon Hill classic.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description: Women in Space is an expansion pack for Women in Science, adding 8 female astronauts (a new type of scientist), 4 Discovery cards (protects a lab against Prestige cards) and 4 Prestige cards. These 16 cards are added to the regular Women in Science deck.

The game can be played in a couple of different ways. 1) Draw tiles with illustrations from wellknown tales and songs from the bag and combine to your very own story or song, told in the same order as the tiles you draw. Best story wins. 2) Memory version: find the cards belonging to each tale or song, sort them in the correct order and tell it/sing it.

Ett spel för alla som gillar sagor och att fantisera! Dra bildkorten med illustrationer från välkända sagor och visböcker ur sagopåsen och fantisera sen ihop en ny saga. Sagan ska berättas i den ordning som bilderna plockas fram. Spännande, tokigt eller roligt, spelaren med bästa minnet och fantasi vinner. Innehåller 3 olika spelvarianter.

In the first phase each player chooses a hero and gathers the equipment (memory play) necessary to get through the swamp (second phase). While running through the swamp the heroes must pass seven dinosaurs (dice rolling and card drafting). If you get scared, you loose some of your equipment. The first hero to cross the swamp scores victory points, and all players who crosses the swamp scores points equal to the equipment left. The player with highest score wins.

Välkommen till ett riktigt äventyr – om du vågar. Tillsammans ska ni springa genom träskmarkerna så fort ni kan. Dinosaurierna är efter er, hungrigare än någonsin. Med sju stycken läskiga dinosaurier, en spelplan, kortlek, tärning, så kan fyra deltagare ge sig iväg och börja äventyret. Lycka till!

A board game version of a quiz show on Swedish television. A player can either choose to answer a question themselves (after hearing its category) or choose to give it to someone else (and then score points if they can't answer it).

Description from the publisher:

In the Krilling Field, up to four players compete against each other to become heir to the Blubbulous Throne and ruler of the ocean. It's a card game played on a randomized map, where the object is to discover defensive outposts and fortify them against the onslaught of the Deep Krillord.

Protecting your home is a wonderful thing to do with friends, but King Blubbulous only has one throne to give to a heroic whale, so you might as well sabotage your fellow whales along the way. There are over 60 abilities and organisms to aid you in your quest, from swordfish assassins to deep-ocean ectoplasm. Or, if being a hero isn't your style, aid the Krillord in usurping the Blubbulous throne and bring terror to the Krilling Field in his evil name.

The Krilling Field takes between thirty minutes and one hour to play, and every game has completely rearranged terrain, with players altering the shape, size, and order of the playing field at will. While it is possible (but unlikely) to exhaust the multitude of unique locations in a single game, it takes most players between five and ten playthroughs to discover every card.

The Krilling Field includes:

Five Player Whales
Twelve Blubbulous Pearls
Eight Non-Player Ocean Denizens
Fifty-six Munition Cards
Thirty-six Ocean Cards
Thirty Deeps Cards
One Ocean Indicator Card
One Rule Book

boardgame: Hissy Fit: the Thinking Drinking Game
description:
Description from the publisher:

It's Hissy Fit'', a new kind of party game for adults featuring creativity, vice, and reckless behavior! Oh, and also drinking and gambling.

We present, for your entertainment, a new sort of party game for adults. Fair warning, though, it contains drinking, racing, expenditure of petty cash, and all manner of chicanery. Have you ever wondered if you were the fastest thigh-poker around? Are you confident that you could portray the most convincing withered crone? Do you excel at eloquence while inebriated? Want to win money from your friends? Do you enjoy judging the wit and skills of your peers? If you answered affirmatively to any of these, Hissy Fit is the game for you!

Oh, and it also pays for itself the first time that you win, provided you play for real money. Now that's value!

A game of Hissy Fit consists of players dividing into teams and competing against each other in bizarre and humorous challenges. These can be anything from “First person to reach the Great Outdoors” to “Best Impromptu Facial Hair” to the dreaded “Slurgeon General”, where a player will be forced to navigate a tricky tongue twister. After judgement is passed, losing players pay a dollar in tribute, and the winner flips their coin to determine how the drinks are administered. If they’re lucky, all the losers will drink, but if fortune turns against them, they drink instead. After much competition, the besotted winner gets a huge pile of cash, and the losers go home happy.

What's with the name? Well, in Hissy Fit, outbursts of laughter are both constant and contagious. Naturally,we decided to reward and punish it simultaneously. When the Hissy Fit token is invoked, all laughter is forbidden and only good-humored hissing is permitted. This effect persists only until someone screws up, but it is truly a sight to behold. As it happens, trying not to laugh is in fact more hilarious than just laughing, and the copious hissing adds tremendously to the fun.

It's a game that pits subjectivity against objectivity in winners. Want to win a "Best" challenge? You'll have to convince the judge of your superiority. Want to win a "First" challenge? Well, the only things standing in your way are your own limitations. It's truly the best of both worlds!

boardgame: The World of SMOG: Rise of Moloch – The Baker Street Set
description:
A kickstarter-exclusive expansion to The World of SMOG: Rise of Moloch, consisting of two new Gentlemen - Lord Holmes and John Watson - and two additional Agents - Irene Adler and Colonel Moran - each with their own card and effect.

As a bonus, there are four Gentlemen Dashboards for these Agents, allowing you to field a whole team of the legends of Baker Street!

On turn players choose a letter and try to write down on the block mammal - bird - fish - bug - reptile/amphibian on for each box.
Round is over if first player has filled all 5 boxes in row or players agreed to end the round.
Each word not written by others scores 2 points, each other word scores 1 point.
After 8th round add up the points and the player with the most points is the winner.
family: Series: Travel Games for Smart Kids (Goldmerk)

description:
Arashikage is a mercenary ronin for Ninja All-Stars. Ronin bring potent new abilities to a player’s team that are entirely unique to them. As mercenaries, ronin may be used by any player, on any team. This makes ronin invaluable assets for every team, regardless of clan, since they may be used to supplement a team’s weaknesses in battle or further augment their strengths.

The silent and masked Arashikage has walked Kagejima for seven years on a quest for vengeance. Ever since he witnessed one of his peers apparently murder his Tora master, he has longed to hunt down the killer. For the right price, Arashikage will use his mastery of Fire and Water to help your team in the Moonlight Tournament, but only so long as he feels it brings him closer to his goal of revenge.

Highlights:

Introduces Unique Abilities
    Fully Assembled Model
    Fire and Water Wielding, Revenge-Seeking Ronin

Yobuko is a Clan Ijin-themed Hero for Ninja All-Stars. Heroes bring potent new abilities to a player’s team that are entirely unique to them. Each Hero is themed to a single clan and make must-have additions for players who pick up the clan box.

Tall, strong, and fierce, Yobuko is also young and inexperienced compared to his Ijin counterparts. This blend of natural ability coupled with an, as yet, unrivaled capacity to fight might be the most frightening aspect about him. His power is already so incredible that he is already the rival of many of his masters, and he is also constantly testing his mettle against full-blooded oni twice his size to the amazement of all of his clansmen.

Highlights:

Introduces Unique Abilities
    Fully Assembled Model
    Ferocious and Powerful Ijin Clan Hero

Musashi is a mercenary ronin for Ninja All-Stars. Ronin bring potent new abilities to a player’s team that are entirely unique to them. As mercenaries, ronin may be used by any player, on any team. This makes ronin invaluable assets for every team, regardless of clan, since they may be used to supplement a team’s weaknesses in battle or further augment their strengths.

For years, Musashi has wandered Kagejima, studying, debating, and fighting with masters from every school he could find. After some years, he found that he no longer sought to master the sword; he sought, instead, to master himself. Since the tool no longer mattered, he abandoned his weapon for a pair of mighty oars. Now he travels on, searching for himself, and refining the art of the wooden blades.

Highlights:

Introduces Unique Abilities
    Fully Assembled Model
    Wooden Blade-Wielding, World Traveler Ronin

This makes ronin invaluable assets for every team, regardless of clan, since they may be used to supplement a team’s weaknesses in battle or further augment their strengths.

Marked by the great wicker baskets that cover their heads, and heralded by the haunting sounds of their shakuhachi flutes, Komuso monks bring a calm aura and share wise words wherever they go. Where the basest of men fight for money, Komuso accept only food or supplies. Unburdened by material possessions, they often demand favors, prayers, or offerings at certain shrines from those who seek their assistance.

Highlights:

Introduces Unique Abilities

Fully Assembled Model

Serene, Solitary Mercenary Monk

Karura is a Clan Tanchyo-themed Hero for Ninja All-Stars. Heroes bring potent new abilities to a player’s team that are entirely unique to them. Each Hero is themed to a single clan and make must-have additions for players who pick up the clan box.

Karura is unusual among even his rare, adventurous clansmen, for he feels a greater kinship with the Tanchyo’s cousins—the eagles—than with any human he has met. Although seemingly distant, Karura possess a great inquisitive nature that has led him to tinker endlessly and invent many wonderful things. His latest invention—a contraption of snares, wires, pulleys, and hooks—allows him to fly between trees, cliffs, and rooftops with all the grace and speed of his beloved avian cousins.

Uzumaki is unlike his otherwise reclusive Kitsune kin. Where others recoil at the sight of travelers, Uzumaki is often the first to greet them and offer them warding runes and medicines in exchange for stories of the world beyond his clans’ hallowed forests. Social, skilled, and savvy, he is ill-suited for the more solemn life his clan’s spirit-haunted trees offer. For these reasons, he has been sent to the Moonlight Tournament as their first ever envoy in over a century.

Yokozuna takes great pleasure in his work at the employ of Shojo, a woman notorious for treating work like a party. Cheerful, kind, and incredibly strong, he has risen to a position of leadership amongst Shojo’s dock workers. The Moonlight Tournament, however, is a nice break from the tasks that typically occupy his days on the dock. Those jovial by nature, he is no fool. He is always on the lookout, ready to be in the right place at the right time to stop trouble in Kagejima.

This makes ronin invaluable assets for every team, regardless of clan, since they may be used to supplement a team’s weaknesses in battle or further augment their strengths.

The Kappa are wild, dangerous kami who inhabit Kagejima’s lakes, rivers, and swamps. Wily and withdrawn by nature, they can be found overturning boats and drowning unwary children. Despite this, several clans have successfully coaxed these exceptionally strong and stealthy creatures into performing services…often with the sacrifice of food, livestock, or even the village elder’s firstborn child.

Highlights:

Introduces Unique Abilities

This makes ronin invaluable assets for every team, regardless of clan, since they may be used to supplement a team’s weaknesses in battle or further augment their strengths.

As a mortal, Onibaba would lure stragglers from battles into her traps, finish them off, and sell their possessions. One night, however, she pulled a finely crafted mask from a dark and terrible samurai, and slipped it on over her face. In that instant, the dark power from within the mask touched the festering rot in her soul and consumer her. Soon the mortal hag was gone, replaced by an ageless, devouring horror that has stalked Kagejima’s places of slaughter ever since.

Highlights:

Schooled since birth in the ancient, esoteric arcana of her family, the Moon Princess sometimes tires of merely watching the Moonlight Tournament. She often desires to use her own strange and terrible power against the warriors competing to do her honor. Yet, competing in the tournament exposes her to threats, assassination attempts, and even death. In truth, though, these dangers are part of what makes these outings so appealing to her.

Hanzo has been called many things over his long and storied life: master, teacher, monster, and traitor. Still the Moon Princess has refused to denounce her “wayward hunting hawk”. Some say this is for previous unknown services, others because he still serves the Moon Court.

The Onitama: Sensei's Path expansion consists of sixteen new movement cards that can be shuffled together with those of the Onitama base game in order to have more variety during play.

The captain of the magnificent trading vessel, Tako-Tako, is as famous for her wild ways as for her wondrous wares. Shojo loves a party, and she brings one (or more) with her wherever she makes berth. A fearless navigator, she is equally masterful with the sword, which she hastily employs with typhoon fury—so long as the sake lasts, that is.

Mizaru is a Clan Yamazaru-themed Hero for Ninja All-Stars. Heroes bring potent new abilities to a player’s team that are entirely unique to them. Each Hero is themed to a single clan and make must-have additions for players who pick up the clan box.

At age 14, Mizaru lost his sight due to a witch’s curse. His story might have ended there except that he refused to become bitter. He, instead, focused his anger into overcoming the setbacks his injury initially posed. Honing his senses until he could navigate trails by the sway and sound of his scabbard, identify others by scent, and track any movement by the slightest noise,Mizaru eventually became captain of the Yamazaru’s famed scouts.

Highlights:

Introduces Unique Abilities
    Fully Assembled Model
    Blind Kunai-Weilding Ninja Master

"Battle of Shanghai" reproduces two Shanghai incidents of different nature in completely different game systems.

The "First Shanghai Incident" is a zonal wargame that represents the military scalade that ensued the January Incident, in 1932. One player plays with the japanese army and the other plays the Kuomintang Armed Forces. The game is played in only two turns, one for february and one for march. Each turn both players alternate in taking actions by expending logistic points until they exhaust their points or both players passes consecutively.
The map depicts the countryside north of Shanghai, in a zonal map. The goal of the Japanese is to score enought victory points by occupying some key areas by the end of each turn or else eliminate all KMT troops from around Shanghai. The KMT wins elseway. The counters represent regiments or brigades, with few support counters.

In "Shanghai / Nanjing Operation", is a more classical wargame, that simulates the 1938 thrust to Nanjing. The map represents the region between Shanghai and Nanjing, and it's an hexagon's map. The units are at divisional level for the japanese and at army level for the Kuomingtang.

As mercenaries, ronin may be used by any player, on any team. Some Kitsune are ill-suited for life beneath their clan’s hallowed home of shadowed trees. Instead, they dream of the wider world full of unknown wonders. Masterful, mischievous, and even a bit malicious, Dark Kitsune either venture beyond their homeland or are shun. Employing their clan’s skills in herbalism and magic, such Dark Kitsune rarely have trouble making a living off of warriors willing to pay their sometimes outrageous fees.

Hidden in the forests and groves of northern Kagejima, the Ondori clan are mysterious and almost legend to most of the island. They only occasionally trade with, or even contact, their nearest neighbors. Those who do interact with them describe the Ondori as proud, fiery, and surprisingly well-informed. In the last century, more and more of these enigmatic shinobi have appeared and bargained their services to other clans for the Moonlight Tournament. With flashing claws and a sharp beak, they challenge all who stand in their way with their unique fighting style that baffles opponents and allies alike.

While seldom seen, the Inu are far more widespread than the other minor clans and far more congenial. Inu are very protective of their lands and their neighbors that treat them well, always coming quickly to their aid and fighting fiercely in their defense. Though small and compact, Inu clan warriors are whirlwinds of frenetic energy, and many opponents have retreated from these warriors’ sheer enthusiasm.

Of the many magical creatures and peoples that inhabit Kagejima, the Tengu are among the most enigmatic. For centuries, these strange creatures would barter or trade their magical and martial skills to the competing clans or war bands. Sometimes they did so for gold or shiny baubles, and sometimes for whispers of love from a beautiful woman, the first wail of a newborn, or thirty stems of saffron. Since the start of the Moonlight Tournament, more tengu have offered their services more often, and increasingly for stranger payments. Though many find this curious, or even ominous, few are unwilling to at least try and meet the price.

Ever watchful for weakness, the Sun Empire’s agents can turn up anywhere, and discovering them and sending them politely on their way has become a game among many clans. The Sun Empire Wandering Samurai is but one of the many skilled warriors that has surfaced in Kagejima, offering services to interested parties. Such is the cut-throat nature of Kagejima’s politics—especially the prestige attached to the Moonlight Tournament—that they often find those willing to employ them.

Jorogumo is a mercenary ronin for Ninja All-Stars. Ronin bring potent new abilities to a player’s team that are entirely unique to them. As mercenaries, ronin may be used by any player, on any team. This makes ronin invaluable assets for every team, regardless of clan, since they may be used to supplement a team’s weaknesses in battle or further augment their strengths.

A horror, a cautionary tale, and a powerful opponent, the spider women called Jorogumo are useful, if questionable, allies. They descend from a witch of Kagejima that rescued the unwary or unfortunate from drowning in lakes and rivers. Claiming to rehabilitate them, she actually slowly poisoned and paralyzed them for use in dark and terrible spells. As her magics grew too much, Kagejima itself cursed her with a shape that evoked her horrid nature. This terrible judgment carried to her offspring who came to bear her form and her name. None trust the Joromugo, but a few unscrupulous warriors will employ them for their speed, strength, and enticing song.

Promo for the game Archaeologia, as handed out at Paris est Ludique 2015.

1 New discovery tile with the title Patate d'Or.
Each player starts with 7 X-shaped tiles. On your turn you add a tile. If your tile has two matching borders, you may play another. It's a race to get rid of your tiles first.

Description from the publisher:

Activity has been around for more than 25 years, and Activity Multi Challenge is the latest version of the game, with players winning or losing together!

In this communication game, players on the team need to guess six terms before time runs out, and you need to plan well since 24 cards must be finished in order to win the game. Things to be guessed come in fifteen categories, and in addition to mentioning these categories to help others guess the hidden term, you can draw activity coins in order to give additional clues, with ten different types of activities being possible, from rhyming to alliteration to pantomiming to talking backwards and more.

Description from the publisher:

The dice in Bumpi are unique in that they do not roll on the table; they bounce! This is because the dice are actually five-sided objects in which four of the sides are attached to the fifth, and to "roll" the die, you push it down onto the table, flattening all the sides, then let it spring up to roll around.

Why are you doing this? Because all players are presented with a task card at the start of each round. Sometimes you want to find an animal, sometimes a color, and often both — and you need to keep rolling until someone finds a solution and wins the card. Whoever collects five cards first wins the game!

Description from the publisher:

Caro Cane is a reworked design from Alex Randolph in which young players are presented with cards that show a watercolor illustration of a dog along with two other items — tree, ship, hat, car, cat, moon, ball, girl or boy — and players try to place cards with matching characteristics in the right location as quickly as possible in order to empty their hands.

In Sheep Sheep, players are trying to collect the most sheep of a particular color.

Each turn, a player has 1 minute and 30 seconds to exchange sheep from their own line of 4 with one from the 3x3 common area. Once time's up, they collect all the sheep in a row and/or column that have the same color in the common area and tuck them under their own personal goal sheep. They are then replaced with ones from the draw deck.

At the end of the game, players tally up their score. Every sheep they've collected is worth 1 point but those that match their goal card are worth 2. Whoever has the most points wins.

In Ultraz, you're a team captain. Build your team of 15 Ultraz and defeat your opponent.

On your side, you'll have 6 active cards. The front 3 cards serve as offense while the back 3 serve as defense. With these cards, you'll attempt to pass, black and shoot.

First person to 3 points wins the game.

Coffee Game is a paper and pencil game where there will be all winners and one loser. The loser have to pay a coffee or whatever you want. ;-) A random player, called the writer, write an hidden number from 1 to 999. The player on his left start the game calling a number from 1 to 999 trying to not guess the written number. The writer tell if the number is higher or lower to the written number. The next player have to call a number in the remain range trying to not guess the written number. The writer tell if the called number is higher or lower to the written number. The range will always be lower. If the turn come back to the writer he have to call a -2 or +2 from the last called number. Calls continue in clockwise order until a player guess the number. Who guess the number lost the game.
Example of play. 4 player A, B, C and D. A is the writer. A write 334 hidden. B have to call a number from 1 to 999. B call 562. Higher. C have to call a number from 1 to 562. C call 275. Lower. D have to call a number from 275 to 562. D call 499. Higher. A, the writer, have to call a -2 number from the last call (499). A call 497. Higher. B have to call a number from 275 to 497. B call 330. Lower. C have to call a number from 330 to 497. C call 399. Higher. D have to call a number from 330 to 399. D call 331. Lower. A, the writer, have to call a +2 number from last called 331. A call 333. Lower. B have to call a number from 333 to 399. B call 340. Higher. C have to call a number from 333 to 340. C call 334. Guessed. C lost the game and have to pay a coffee.

Microworld has modular rules that you combine to create several different scenarios. This promo supplies two new additional rule tiles that you can add to the base game to increase the variability of the game even more.

The first additional rule (B1) applies during the setup: it gives a personal secret goal tile to the players, but only the first player who reach the goal will score the points. Moreover, you will find a bonus Goal Tile in the promo.

The second additional rule (D1) applies during the game. It establishes that when players place in outside cells they will score one more point.

Microworld has modular rules that you combine to create several different scenarios. This promo supplies two new additional rule tiles that you can add to the base game to increase the variability of the game even more.

The first additional rule (C1) applies during the setup. Players will have a common special ability during the game. Moreover, you will find a bonus Mutation Tile in the promo.

The second additional rule (H1) applies at the end of the game. If players are able to control a tissue alone, they will score two more points.

User summary
Kidnap Machine is a print-and-play solitaire boardgame. There is a variant Version called Obscura (see below).

You woke up in a dark room, and you feel the presence of something horrible. You must get out of the room using your wisdom and luck.

To play the game, you need a die, two pawns, a pencil, and a printed square-grid play sheet.

You may move two squares in a turn, but must end your turn after you try to enter a new square, in which case you roll a die. You cannot move if the result is less than the movement difficulty designated on the original square. Otherwise, you move to the new square and roll a die to determine the movement difficulty for that square.

Then the monster moves. Basically it moves along the predetermined route, but while you are within the area of its sight, it approaches you.

You win the game if you reach the goal square without being captured by the monster.

The PnP files are on the designer's website (see Weblinks). This includes a second Version on the game called 'Obscura'.

Obscura
Suddenly, the lights went out.
The darkness came one step closer.

"Obscura" is an improved version that allows you to play "Kidnap Machine" without using a pencil by using working tiles.
Enjoy a difficult escape from the labyrinth now clothed in deeper darkness.

Publisher's summary
Kidnap Machineは、Print and Playの一人用ボードゲームです。
あなたは暗闇の空間で目を覚ましました。あなたを捕らえようとする、何か恐ろしいものの気配をすぐそこに感じます。
運と知恵を頼りに、この恐ろしい状況を脱しましょう。

プレイシート以外に用意するものは、サイコロと駒と鉛筆です。
あなたは、決められたスタート地点から出口まで、隣り合うマスへ駒を移動させていきます。
未知のマスに移動するときはサイコロを振り、今いるマスの脱出難易度を超えれば移動に成功します。
その際、移動先のマスの脱出難易度もサイコロで決定します。
すでに難易度がわかっているマス同士の間の移動であれば、１ターンに２回、自由に行なえます。
あなたの移動が済む度に、ボード上で化物が動きます。
彼らは基本的に決められたルートに沿って移動しますが、あなたを視界に捉えると追跡をはじめます。
彼らに捕まってしまったらゲームオーバーです！

Obscura
すぐそこで、明かりが消えた。
暗闇が、また一歩近づいた。

"Obscura"は、タイルを工作することで"Kidnap Machine"を鉛筆を使わずに遊べるようにした改良版です。
より深い暗闇に閉ざされた迷宮での、困難な脱出劇をお楽しみください。

boardgame: Dust Tactics: USMC Observer Squad – "Artillery Scouts"
description: The Marines have arrived in the Middle-East! Nothing as efficient as a good artillery barrage to keep the enemy on its toes... Thanks to these observers, regimental and division artillery pieces can deliver the most powerful punch to crush the enemies of the Allies.

boardgame: Dust Tactics: USMC Demolition Squads – "The Saints"
description:
When the Imperial Japanese Navy advanced through the Pacific, it conquered a vast number of remote islands. Ordered to defend them were a few resolute soldiers who didn’t have much equipment, but had a lot of free time on their hands. So they did as any soldier in the world does when there’s nothing else to do: they dug in.

When the reconquest of the Pacific started, the United States Marine Corps had to deal with fortified positions right away. Some small islands had more bunkers and pillboxes than they had coconut trees! It was quickly pretty obvious that the Corps would need to adapt to get the job done.

The Marines were not prepared for this, as not everyone could carry a flame-thrower, therefore they needed something more practical. The Corps found its solution with the creation of the Demolition Squads. These men would be given basic instructions on how to handle explosives: throw a satchel full of them in roughly the right direction, and run the other way as fast as possible.

It was soon evident that if you were sent into one of these squads, you were soon to meet your maker - whatever you conceived Him to be. With such a high rate of casualties, they called themselves ‘The Saints’. Nevertheless, these Marines are immensely respected in the Corps, and often benefit from slightly more relaxed standards of discipline.

boardgame: Desperados of Dice Town: Tuco Promo Character
description:
This promo character replaces a 'Brain' character from any of the gangs.

When escaped, his powers are:
1 action symbol: Draw and keep the last Wild West card discarded.
2 action symbols: Draw the last 2 Wild West cards discarded and keep one.
3 action symbols: Draw the last 3 Wild West cards discarded and keep one.

This promo was available with issue 72 of Plato Magazine.

boardgame: Dust Tactics: USMC Recon Squads – "Force Recon"
description: The Marines have arrived! Deployed in front of any USMC force, the Recon Squads provide intelligence and are able to communicate efficiently with fire support units should they encounter any threat. These are sneaky, and deadly, Marines!

boardgame: Dust Tactics: USMC Assault Squad – "Hell Blazers"
description: Armed with a variety of powerful short range weapons, these Marines are among the most feared in the Pacific. Their Flamethrowers can blow up anything from the heaviest walker to the bulkiest bunker.

Description from the publisher:

Austria in all its facets is at the center of Challenge Austria. From landscape to music, history to sport, one should not only have their red-white-red knowledge up to date, but also be ready to shine in their negotiation skills since questions are not only answered, but also bought and exchanged. During the game, players have a number of question cards in their possession and can answer only those about Austria as a whole and those about the region of the country in which their game figure is currently located. Thus, you might want to trade cards with others, offer them cash, move to new locations, or pay to return cards that are too difficult to answer. Too pay too much, though, for in the end the first player to collect a previously agreed upon sum of money wins the game.

There aren't many soldiers on the planet brave enough to face five men armed with Browning Automatic Rifles, or they have a serious death wish. The staple of the Marine Corps is this squad, which can be found in numbers wherever the USMC fights.

boardgame: Dust Tactics: USMC Anti-tank Squad – "Hot Shots"
description: The Marines have arrived in the Middle-East! And they bring with them some deadly new weapons, just out of Detroit's arsenals. The recoilless rifles give these soldiers an incredible firepower against anything on the battlefield. And you can be sure Marines will make the most of it! They are presented here in their Babylon camo, as used by USMC forces in the Middle-East, circa 1947.

In Circo Delfino, players want to find the right combinations of colored dolphins as quickly as possible — sometimes even combining primary-colored dolphins to make secondary-colored ones! — in order to collect more cards than anyone else.

In Das Spiel mit dem Essen, players move their pieces around the plate on the game board that's divided into different food groups and attempt to answer food-related questions in order to win chips — and no, not potato chips, but point chips!

boardgame: Alice in Wonderland: A Curious Collection of Puzzles
description:
A trivia game where players earn points by answering themed questions on their turn.

On their turn a player picks a card from one of four difficulty piles. Scoring points for the difficulty of the card if they answer correctly. Play continues until a player accumulates an agreed upon number of points.

Each player has an "Eat Me" and "Drink Me" card which they may play to alter the score of a given card in accordance to the instructions on the "Eat Me"/"Drink Me" card.

The game is a handheld dome containing 15 dice (5 red, 5 yellow, 5 blue) and whose base contains a 4x4 grid of slots for the dice to settle into.

The object is to shake and rattle the dome so that 4 dice of the same color form a straight line or a square.  You score the sum of the numbers on the dice in that line or square.  A diagonal counts double, and a center square counts triple.

The main characters in Dragoonies have discovered a treasure chest and naturally each of them wants to take as much of the gold as possible for themselves — but to do that, they need to solve tasks more quickly than anyone else.

At the start of play, a circle of sixteen dragon cards is formed in the center of the table; each card is different from each other, whether due to color, horns, or small objects with which they're equipped. On a turn, the active player reveals 2-6 search cards — with more cards making for a tougher challenge — then all the players race to determine the characteristics that are most common on the search cards, e.g., the color blue, a key, or three horns. Multiple solutions might exist, and when you find one, you want to cover an unoccupied dragon card that corresponds to the answer. If you do so correctly, you earn a Drachentaler, and whoever collects the most money in the end wins!

Let's go to the amusement park! Instead of simply gawking at the attractions, however, the players in Gho...Gho...Ghosty get to command their own ghost train, competing against others to travel far and nab the most passengers. Players take turns in the role of Mr. Ghosty, collecting two cards from the ghost train card display. When the ghost train reaches a certain length, the train takes off — and when all 75 wagons are gone, the best ghost train boss will be determined based on the passengers they have.

Description from the publisher:

Orange Toss is a fast-thinking, unpredictable adult party game. How fast can you come up with an answer before the Orange Timer 
runs out? Don't get caught with your peel down! The player left holding the Orange Timer when the time runs out collects the card. The player with the fewest cards at the end of 13 rounds wins!

Yikes! All the foliage in the forest has been stirred up by a violent gust of wind, and 36 little critters have lost their shelter. Equipped with the appropriate leaf cards, the players in Insectini need to hide the insects again leaf by leaf as quickly as possible. To do this, they need to spot the tiny details on these critters — a light bulb on a firefly, a grub playing the guitar, a caterpillar munching on a snack — and cover them with a matching leaf in their hand.

King John is having a party with lots of guests. Unfortunately he's terrible at remembering the faces of those guests, so he needs your help.

In the game King John,  four cards are revealed at the table each round, with each card showing some combination of characters. The active player shows these cards to all, then hides them and rolls dice to determine whether the other players are searching for a particular character or the character who appears most (or least) often on the cards.

In Memo-Palace, players will first hide the animal tiles in the castle, but they won't just place the tiles and leave it at that. No, they must tell a story about each animal as they place it, with all the players paying attention to the stories.

Once all the animals have been placed, players must then attempt to find matching pairs of animals in the castle, but they also need to combine the two stories for the two tiles that they find, and if they don't remember the stories, then they must return the tiles. Thus, Memo-Palace combines storytelling and memory in a new way to create a fresh challenge and experience for young players each game.

Similar to The Memory Palace

In Pixbox, the active player lays out six transparent cards at random. These cards show one or more blue squares on them, with the squares arranged in various patterns. The player then lays out a red task card, and everyone must visualize — without touching the blue cards! — how they could arrange one or more blue cards in an overlapping manner, but with only the transparent areas covering one another, so as to recreate the image shown on the red task card.

As soon as a player thinks they have a solution, they yell out and attempt to demonstrate their idea with the transparent cards. If they're correct, they keep the cards they used, and whoever collects the most points wins.

Players in Shop Hop need to visit the eight stores and buy something faster than anyone else, but to move they need to spin the dial to see which of the six colored paths they can move on each turn. Some stores are closed when you first approach them, but if you can enter through the key symbol — or wave a little money at them — then they'll open for business.

In Tigerilla, players can assemble animals in new ways thanks to the velcro on the game tiles.

In game terms, players decide whether they want to assemble animals or search for patterns, then they place the tiles with the desired thing face down. On a turn, a player reveals two of the tiles; if they match (animal or pattern, which is desired), then they turn over a third tile. If that tile matches as well, they earn a point, and whoever earns five points first wins.

Zebulon: Galactic Control is a space themed strategic board game that allows you to create a new layout every time you play using 40 planets on 20 tiles. Complete missions, lay traps and confront other players in battle. Players race around the map by purchasing fuel en route to completing missions. When you finish a mission pick up rewards and upgrade your ship. Use reward cards to attack your foe or help you win the game.

boardgame: Cows Abhor Hamburgers (fan expansion for Cards Against Humanity)
description:
Cows Abhor Hamburgers is a third-party, unofficial, unauthorized expansion card set for Cards Against Humanity.  The cards are somewhat more offensive than the base game.

There has been a single print run of cards, which occurred 2014 and were available by preorder only. Now available via print & play under a creative commons license.
Object is to line up 3 cards of the same colour, and three cards of the same insect.

Description from the publisher:

Critter Combat is a 2 - 4 player battle and capture card game with cool critters that have even cooler abilities. This game is played with one of 4 included 26 card decks, and a set of 18 habitat cards. Play your cards for abilities to set back your enemy or bolster your positions, use them to capture habitats, or take on your opponent in adorable battle.

Your army of fuzzy critters has been pushed too far! Take your cute, furry, and fuzzy animals to war on a no–holds–barred mission to destroy, decimate, and humiliate your enemies. Take back the trees and land that are rightfully, and not so rightfully, yours!

Linage is a finite territory game for two players: Vertical and Horizontal. It is played on the intersections (points) of an initially empty square grid (board). Both players must have access to a shared pool of identical stones.

				
				
					DefinitionsA region is a maximal set of orthogonally contiguous empty points.

A line is a set of three orthogonally contiguous empty points on the same row or column.

A region is owned by Vertical if it contains no horizontal lines, and by Horizontal if it contains no vertical lines. A region is free if it is owned by neither player.

				
				
					PlayVertical plays first, then turns alternate. On your turn, pass or place a stone in a free region. At all times, every region must include at least one line.

The game ends when both players pass in succession. The player with the higher score wins. Your score is the number of points in the regions that you own, plus komi (see below) in the case of Horizontal.

				
				
					KomiKomi is the number of points that is added to Horizontal's score at the end of the game as compensation for going second. Before the game starts, the first player chooses the value of komi, and then the second player chooses sides. To avoid ties, it is suggested that komi be of the form n + 0.5, where n is a whole number.

User summary
独裁者はつらいよ (Dokusaisha wa Tsurai yo) is the card game Dictator wa Tsurai yo (in English 'It's tough being a dictator').

Dictator wa Tsurai yo is a card game with a theme of a dictator in old Soviet Union. This games uses nine sets of four identical cards. Your objective is to score points by completing sets of four cards in your hand.

Each turn, you ask another player to give you a specific card. If that player has any, all of them are given to you, otherwise you draw a card from the deck. If you have a set of four cards in your hand, you display them in front of you and score victory points (similar to Old Maid or Go Fish).

A special effect is activated when a set is made. A set of chancellors has may purge a person (remove one displayed card from the game), a set of generals may kidnap a person (steal one displayed card), and so on.

When a player is betrayed by people, that player takes a horseshit token, which counts as negative points.

The highest scorer wins the game.

Publisher summary
旧ソ連の独裁者を題材にしたカードゲームです。
手札を４枚づつ配り、手番プレイヤーは手札に持っている番号を、任意のプレイヤーに持っているか聞きます。
聞かれたプレイヤーは、それを持っていた場合全て引き渡します。持っていなかった場合、聞いたプレイヤーは山札から一枚ドローします。
これを繰り返し、同じ数字が手元に4枚そろったら公開して得点札になります。
公開したタイミングで、各カードの能力が発動します。
カードの能力で得点札を拉致されたプレイヤーには「馬糞トークン」がなすりつけられます。当然得点はマイナスになります。
より多くの得点を得たプレイヤーの勝利です。

Subterranean Cities is played in four rounds, during which players will take turns placing one of their three crew members in their ship to add and activate ship part tiles. These parts are used to maneuver the ship and collect more parts. Victory points are earned by building your ship, and refurbishing ship parts into structures. The player with the most Victory Points at the end of the final round is the winner.

Early Pacific Battles is an Operational/Tactical level, World War II board-wargame set in the Pacific Theater of Operations.

This wargame kit, self-published by Bruce Moore in 1972, covers all aspects of 1942 battles in the Pacific.  
Air to Air, Air to Surface (fixed installations and naval task forces), Naval Surface and Land Combat are all treated as well as multi-player Operational combat embracing all of the above.  Scenarios are included for the significant engagements as well as several hypothetical meetings - for example, War Plan Orange III battleship duels and surface action off Malaya had Prince of Wales and Repulse escaped air strikes.

The game kit aspect is the supply of hundreds of small coloured blocks to which unit details must be glued.  Torpedo tracks and cloud formations for the Operational game weather handling must be cut out.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

description:
User summary
PORTAS is a two-player card game with a theme of treasure hunting. Players are competing treasure hunters, and at beginning of the game, each player has the same deck of cards, and each card is numbered 1-6. Two treasure cards (valued 11-15) are revealed on the table.

Players take turns placing a card from their hand on either of the treasure cards revealed. When the total value of the cards placed on the treasure equals to the treasure value, the current player takes the treasure and scores points. Then a new treasure comes on the table.

All cards in players' hands are public information except the one they would draw at the beginning of their turn. So you should play cards so that your rival doesn't take a treasure next turn, but sometimes you are forced to play a bad card, and there's some chance the rival would draw the exact card they want.

The game ends when all treasures are taken or both players passes in a row. The highest scorer wins the game.

As long as a child can count to 15, the game is easy enough for the young. The game uses original art and public domain artwork.

Publisher's summary
PORTAS （読み方は「ポータス」。造語。日本語でも英語でも同じです。）

二人のプレイヤーはトレジャーハンターとなり、宝箱に眠る宝を取り合います。
宝箱の底には魔法の鍵があり、宝はその鍵を手に入れないと数分の内に消えてしまうのです。
宝箱の中身の残りの数を考えて、「丁度最後の一個を手に入れる」事を計算しながらより多くの宝を手に入れるのが目的のゲームです。
バケツやバッグ、木箱等の入れ物を使い宝を自分のもとに運び出します。その中で最後の一個である「鍵」を自分が手に入れられるように、計算をしましょう。
相手の手札（バッグなどの入れ物）は全て公開情報です。明かされた情報と、そこから少しだけ絡んでくる運。そして先を読む頭脳で戦うシンプルな「足し算」ゲームです。

This expansion adds spells to base game. All heros can use eight spells after they get enough Power points from special tile of labyrinth.

For ages people believed that the Magic of the Ancients had left with Them.
The most powerful spells were thought to be forgotten forever…
 Things turned out differently, though. The remains of this unthinkable power are still here.
And they still fill the Labyrinth built so many centuries ago!
 Heroes who shall discover magical springs and seize their power…
shall also gain dominance over time itself and learn to use it as no one has done before!
 However, that’s not all! Thanks to the power lying dormant inside the Labyrinth
the Chosen shall be able to bring fallen heroes to a life of servitude.
 There is also “Change of Destiny”, the most potent spell of all, waiting to be released.
It can ruin plans of many heroes fighting for immortality!

When magic items started to disappear from the Labyrinth
And the mages came to steal its ancient power…
The holy swarm struck for the first time.

The Scarabs of the Labyrinth have awoken to guard secrets of their home.
They are extremely small, deadly quick and dangerous beyond imagining…
They will take control over anyone who seeks power of the Labyrinth for himself!

”Heroes delving into the ruins of the ancient Labyrinth…
They were looking for the gift of immortality, with Crystals of Fate as keys to this most noble of gifts.
We do not know if they found this gift. What we know for sure is that they found the Golem - a strange construct build by an ancient race.
But it was not the only creature lurking in the depths of the Labyrinth. There were many more…”
 Explorer of the Labyrinth from Tiphere’eth

How does it work?

     Scarabs lie dormant in corridors hidden in frame pieces...
     They come into life after players take three magic items from the game board.
     Each player may spend 1 action to move a Scarab swarm.
     When a Scarab encounters a character, this character must make a Scarab Check…
     If the check is successful, the Scarab dies and is removed from the game for good.
     If the check is failed, the Scarab transforms the character into a monster!
     Fortunately, any transformed characters can try to return to their own form!

The Ancients who built the Labyrinth millennia ago
Used a race of Saurians, whom they created.

Strong, manlike lizards were their favorites.
They fascinated, amused and surprised the Ancients with their cleverness.

Saurians worshipped the Ancients as their gods,
While the Ancients had devised grand plans for their children.
They did what they could to develop and educate them…
But they failed.

Despite their cunning, Saurians remained savage.
They kept fighting unending bloody wars,
Wars of domination among themselves.
When Saurians were close to extinction, the Ancients chose to keep in the memory of time
A recollection, however fleeting, about their children. Nothing else could be done for them.

From that moment, Saurians have been kept alive by the Power of the Ancients.
They guard their magic buildings and majestic portals.
They do what they have always done best - fight any enemies
Who wish to wrest long-buried, holy secrets from the depths of time.

What can you expect from this expansion?

     Efficient hunters prowling the Labyrinth with inhuman speed!
     Predatory warriors that aid each other and poison their game!
     The Ancients’ Chosen able to come back to life from the underworld!
     Cunning lizardmen who steal keys and magic items!
     Monsters immune to the Golem, traps, magic of the Ancients and events!
     IMPORTANT: Any player may move Saurians once per turn by spending 1 action!
Expansion pack for Monster Rejects.  Add more strategy and fun by giving your monsters weapons, curses, potions and more.  50 Cards to accompany Monster Rejects.

Theme: For the tricky little mouse to get into any one of his three cheese holes without being caught by the fast sly cat
Game Play: Mouse is moved by using magnetic "mouse" wand underneath the game board. Cat is moved by using magnetic "cat" wand across the top of the game board. Start the cat on one of the starting circles, the mouse is placed in the other starting circle. The game starts when the mouse player says "go". Cat scores when he catches (touches) the mouse anywhere on the board. Mouse scores when he eludes the cat and enters any one of the three cheese holes. As mouse and cat score, they advance one square on the scoring board. After each time the cat or mouse score the players switch, mouse becomes cat, cat becomes mouse. First to score 6 times and land on the house on scoring board wins.

Theme: Be the player to put the final piece of the puzzle together.
Game Play: Pick a puzzle friend and place it on the gear space with the same color as your friend. Spin the spinner and move your friend to the color the spinner lands on. If you land on a space with a puzzle piece place the puzzle piece in the puzzle tray. 
Game ends: When the final puzzle piece is placed in the puzzle tray.

Description from the publisher:

Suchiro, is the embodiment of determination and persistence. With a goal or objective in his mind nothing can stop this quiet and thoughtful monk from reaching it. In battle he fights resolutely, anchoring the forces of the Temple and proving to be an unbreakable bastion for the defenders of Jwar. Many strive to understand the riddles of the Kami of Chikyu’s Rock but this monk of the Temple of Ro-Kan has and in doing so brought the teaching to bear on those that would seek to upset the balance. Suchiro’s wrath may not be quick but like the mountains and earth it is inevitable.

Description from the publisher:

The martial skills of the Tengu are legendary across the Golden Empire. Although few in number these avian Yokai long lives give them ample time to gain mastery of not only the martial but also to learn the secrets of the kami who call the mountains their home. Hidden away in their mountain top cities and guarded by the devoted hill tribes few have ever seen these mysterious warriors. What has brought Sojobo down from the mountains and to the aid of the Tengus' old allies the Temple is not known. Sojobo’s skills in combat are equalled by very few. Now he has spread his great wings and left his mountain home the spirits and prospects of the people of the Rokan can only be lifted as a result.

Description from the publisher:

Ritual is a strategic tile-laying game of magical dominance for two to five players. In Ritual you take on the role of a powerful wizard joining with other wizards to perform an intricate ritual of magic. Being a wizard, you must take this opportunity to prove once and for all that YOU are the best wizard by having the most influence over the ritual at its completion.

At its heart Ritual is about three things: tiles, runes and influence. Being a tile-laying game, Ritual’s core mechanic revolves around drawing hexagonal tiles one at a time from a common pool known as the ley line and placing them onto the scroll that serves as the board.

Each tile has three parts know as rune pieces. Throughout the game players try to match up identical rune pieces in groups of three known as runes. Runes players create can be activated to cause powerful effects capable of reshaping the board, but such power comes at a cost: influence. Each player gets a set of colored cubes known as influence cubes. These cubes are not only used to activate runes, but also to determine where you can play on the scroll and to tally your final score. Clever use of tiles, runes and influence is essential to winning a game of Ritual.

Ritual’s simple gameplay, deep strategy and beautiful presentation make it a fun game for beginners and experienced board-gamers alike.

This venerable monk is a seasoned defender of the Temple. Though his sight left him at a young age his senses and awareness of his surrounding remain sharp as a fox. Like most monks of his age there is little left in this world that can cause fear in this wandering warrior of the Temple.
