In battle Tsutsumi carries with him the ancient Guqin, carved from an ancient wood and passed down from monk to monk since the first rays of sunlight rose over the mountains of Ro-Kan. When his lithe fingers strum its strings as he has done for many decades, using the sweet sound and combining it with his deep voice to create powerful prayers, Shomyos. He uses these to inspire his allies, or suppress and distract his enemies. Tsutsumi can also use the Guqin to channel a powerful wave of sound, knocking hapless enemies to the ground.

Description from the publisher:

For centuries the Temple and the people of the Ro-Kan have lived in symbiotic harmony and for centuries the secrets of the Kami and the mountain have passed between these two allies. Misao, like all village elders has for decades made the decisions for his village and acted as apothecary using the secrets passed down from elder to elder. When his special mix of herbs, found on the mountain, are burned in his censers it clears those of aliments, clears the mind or soul. The peasants in his village follow his every word without question and although physically weak his strength lies in the wise words he draws upon to inspire those around him to great feats of combat prowess in the defence of their lands and peers.

Description from the publisher:

Like all monks of the Temple of Ro-Kan, Seiji is following a path and element that he was always destined to. Still in the early years of his training this calm and relaxed monk is already showing signs that he will become a great defender of the Jwar Isles, and an asset in the struggle against the chaos. It is told that Seiji sat under the Grand Father tree for a full cycle in meditation, quietly becoming as one with the forest around him and in a deep communion with the sacred Kami. The secrets he learned now enable Seiji to seek out the help of the natural world around him, it comes to his and the Temple’s aid. Enemies find themselves rooted to the spot, held firm as they are bound and restrained by the very ground itself. Along with this ability Seiji has also achieved a higher level of understanding and attunement with his own body, enabling him to focus his energies and recover from even the most severe of wounds at an astounding rate.

boardgame: Home Alone 2: Lost in New York – "Talking" Electronic Game
description:
Theme: Harry and Marv chase Kevin, with actual situations from the movie.
Game Play: As Harry and Marv get closer, Kevin taunts them by saying 3 different phrases. Draw a card and move characters according to the color spaces and traps on the card. If the burglars catch Kevin they win the round.

boardgame: Two Rooms and a Boom: Necroboomicon Expansion Pack
description:
The Necroboomicon Expansion Pack for Two Rooms and a Boom comes in a foil pack with characters designed as "Hard Mode". Designed with a H.P. Lovecraft Cthulhu theme, players wrestle with fear and suspicion.

Necroboomicon was designed to enhance the experience of Two Rooms and a Boom for smaller groups. Color sharing is now permitted in groups with fewer than eleven players!

A fast paced card game where players try to score the least points at the end of the game. The game ends once a player is down to two cards. Points are the number of cards in each player's hand plus 'It's Not Your Turn' cards. 
     Players take turns either playing the correct card from their hand (if they have it) face-up, playing a card face-down in an attempt to either bluff or reverse-bluff, or being honest about not having the right card and taking a 'It's Not Your Turn' card. Players can even use a house rule where they can try to steal another player's turn(s) by playing certain number cards at the right moment.
     The game has a lighthearted spoof theme of the stereotypical gamer elitist as shown on the different suit cards and on the game box.

The Marines have arrived! The USMC Command Squad is charged with leading the Marine Forces in combat. Like its Ranger counterpart, it brings a whole lot of new options for your army. It changes drastically how you can control your troops, making sure you'll get the best of every unit!

boardgame: Dust Tactics: USMC Heavy Engineer Squad – "Steel Marines"
description: Combat engineers and pioneers always have the most dangerous post in an army: always on the first line at the front. Clearing a path through minefields and barbed wire for the rest of the army to advance is no small feat, and these guys need every advantage they can get. In the most dangerous terrain, the USMC calls on the Heavy Engineers. With their Ground Assault Power Armour they carry weapons no ordinary man could.

boardgame: Dust 1947: USMC War Dogs Recon Squad
description: The United States Marine Corps (USMC) has arrived! The Allies are in North Africa and the Middle East for good, and no-one is going to kick these Marines out! The fiercest fighting force on the planet is not easily scared or defeated, there's not much these guys haven't seen already in this war. The USMC uses dedicated canine squads for reconnaissance missions, but these dogs don't back out of a fight if need be! The squad is presented here in Babylon camouflage pattern, as used in the Middle-East circa 1947.

"The game the world watches you can now play"

Provides entertainment for all soccer fans.

The object of the game is to be the first person to make their way around the board, answering as many questions correct as possible and making the least amount of errors.

Players roll dice and move along a round, soccer ball shaped playing board.  Players then answer soccer based trivia questions, collecting goal tokens for correct answers.  Make a mistake or commit a foul though, and you could end up with a yellow or red card!

Description from the back of the box:

The following questions are an example of what you'll find:

     Who won the World Cup in 1934?
     Which country does Roberto Baggio play for?
     What shape is a soccer ball?


The answers are made up of:

     multiple choice
     true or false
     plus some real brain teasers


Included in V.N.J's World Soccer:

     A round playing board
     6 player tokens
     6 plastic player holders
     1 playing dice
     70 goal tokens
     22 red cards
     22 yellow cards
     330 question and answer cards


This board game is made in Australia by:

V.N.J. World SOccer
Tapleys Hill Road, West Beach
South Australia, 5024, AUSTRALIA

On Planeta Zwierząt ('Animal Planet') you will encounter various species of animals. Try to determine their characteristics and guess on which continent they live. But you have to avoid the hunter. And better not call the wolf out of the woods.

The aim of the players is the assignment of animal cards to the appropriate continents and prioritize them according to the values of their characteristics. The game allows you to explore the characteristics and habitat of 110 species of animals.

Brass Empire: Kickstarter Promo Expansion contains 21 Promo Cards from the original Brass Empire Kickstarter.  The set expands every corporation's identity with powerful corporate ID cards, a gadget, a new faction reserve card and a unique mining platform for all 5 factions (20 cards). It also includes a Single Player Boss Card: The Pirates of the People which is a unique solitaire variant and challenge for the game.

This item was originally only offered to Kickstarter backers and people ordering directly from the publisher.

boardgame: Dust Tactics: M1D Light Assault Walker Anti-tank – "Bushmaster"
description: The Marines love lightning speed attacks and outflank maneuvers. And heavy firepower as well... There aren't many vehicles on the planet that match these three elements as well as the Bushmaster. Based on the already famous Light Assault Walker chassis, this new machine is widely used by the Corps, and soon by the grand Allied Army.

boardgame: Dust Tactics: M3A2 Medium Combat Walker "Mickey (Light)"
description: This variant of the original Mickey, dubbed the ‘Light Mickey’ by the Marines, was created specifically for the United States Marine Corps. Its open-topped turret makes it much more comfortable to operate in some of the environments that these guys fight in. As with Allied troops across the planet, the Marines love this machine. It’s resilient, easy to operate, and packs a lot of punch. Since its entry into service with the USMC, the Light Mickey has been adopted by other branches of the Allied armies fighting in hot climates.

boardgame: Dust Tactics: M3F2 Medium Combat Walker Assault Engineer "Steel Rain (Light)"
description: Maybe the most recognizable walker on the planet today, the Mickey chassis has given birth to a huge range of machines build on its rugged chassis. In the Pacific or the Middle-East, the Allies use a tropical version of the same chassis, whose main feature is an open top turret. The deadliest arsenal of the Mickey family stays the same. The Steel Rain mounts an impressive gun, capable of blowing any ground target on the planet to pieces.

boardgame: Dust Tactics: M3C2 Medium Combat Walker Anti-Tank – "Pounder (Light)"
description: The new Pounder in “light” open top version is commonly used by the USMC wherever the Corps fight. It is presented here in the Babylon camo pattern as used around Mesopotamia in early 1947, with Marines Corps specific markings.

boardgame: Dust Tactics: M3G2 Medium Combat Walker Anti-Tank – "Barking Dog (Light)"
description:
Filling a gap in the Allied armoured arsenal between the M3A2 Mickey (Light) and the M3C Pounder, the M3G2 Barking Dog (Light) is an excellent anti-tank walker and can serve as a close support vehicle. Its six M40 recoilless guns fire in sequence, aimed at the same point thanks to a very advanced targeting system.

The sound of the guns firing a couple of seconds apart resembles a loud dog barking, hence the nickname of ‘Barking Dog’. This dog’s bite is far worse than its bark! Its 106mm rounds will easily destroy enemy fortified positions or blast tanks into smouldering heaps of metal.

The downside is the reload time needed to make this magic happen. All six guns need to be loaded from the outside of the vehicle, one at a time. That’s why you’ll rarely see a Barking Dog alone on the battlefield. It behaves more like a wolf than a dog, either traveling in pack of its own, or with other walkers that complement its role.

The Marines’ Light Barking Dog is similar to the prototype tested by the Army, but has an open-topped turret for better vision and cross-country performance. It is probably the most popular walker in the Marine Corps’ arsenal.

boardgame: Dust Tactics: M7D Heavy Assault Walker – "Devastator"
description:
Phaser technology was created by the Allies in response to the lasers developed by the Axis. Powerful VK generators concentrate heat waves into small tungsten cylinders around the actual barrel and then release them, projecting a phased discharge of raw energy. How Allied scientists managed to develop such a complex weapon in such a short amount of time is still puzzling for spy agencies around the world - almost as puzzling as how it actually works. The whole project is still shrouded in mystery.

When the scientists combined several phaser systems into one massive phaser gun with a nominal initiator power of 480 Watts (twice as powerful as any previous system) they needed a huge vehicle to carry it. The resulting M7D Devastator is built around the largest phaser energy generator ever built. Unfortunately, this VK generator occupies the space originally used as a passenger compartment, so it is impossible to transport any troops inside.

There is nothing on the planet that can survive a direct hit from a Devastator. The temperature at the core of the blast is believed to be as hot as the Sun, but since there are no practical ways to measure the heat effectively, no one can be sure. So far no known material has been able to survive a hit from this formidable weapon.

Description from the publisher:

In Piñata Loca'  we are filling up Piñatas with tasty candies and toys. But sometimes we secretly put useless objects inside the Piñata too. Hit the right Piñata and collect as many tasty candies and toys as possible.
 
Players try to collect sweets and toys (positive points) and give useless objects (negative points) to their opponents, by wisely playing their cards, on columns that represent Piñatas. When a player places the 7th card, then that Piñata and he collects all of its cards. 
 
Your intuition, observation, strategy and logic skills will guide you to the most... 
“PIÑATA LOCA”!

boardgame: Hero Realms: Contract Negotiation Promo Card
description:
The Cityport of Thandar can be a place of chaos and treachery, but that frenetic nature can be your best defense.  Mercenaries from the four lands come together to worship at the altar of coin, and your odds of survival tend to go up when you make certain alliances.  Buy the best swords, spells, and arrows you can, and show your intentions by your alignments.  Success waits for the wise investor, and fortune favors the gold.

Contract Negotiation is the first unaligned Action card for the Hero Realms Deckbuilding Game.  Simply play it, name a faction, and the ally abilities of your cards with the chosen faction will trigger.  At 2 Gold for a cost of 1, this is a solid early-game acquisition.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

boardgame: Mediterranean (fan expansion for Ticket to Ride: Rails and Sails)
description:
This map is an unofficial fan made expansion. Symbols & Graphics Copyright © 2004-2015 Days of Wonder, Inc. Produced with permission of Days of Wonder, for non-commercial use only.

This follows the principles of the Ticket to Ride - Rails and Sails game on a special board with special cards and rules. The object of the game is to gain the most points by travelling various routes and outwitting other players. You will need the train ,ship and harbour pieces and the train and ship cards from Ticket to Ride - Rails and Sails base game.

There is an extra set of Tourist cards for the cities, each with a value to be earned by visiting that city. These cards are dealt/picked up alongwith tickets in the same quantity and with the same 'keep/dispose' rules.

The 9 bonuses give you extra points.

There are many new options in the rules: exchange all your cards at the start but keep all the new ones: discard 5 cards and pick up 5 new ones, as your turn: earn extra points: and many more. You can also call time when your wagon stock reaches 3, if you want, giving you the advantage of stopping other players completing tickets.

boardgame: Star Realms: Rescue Run Promo Card
description:
Since being court-martialed by the Star Empire, smuggler and thief Joan Shengtu has done what she needed to do in order to survive—gaining a reputation along the way. When a new client’s mission goes sideways, Joan finds herself caught in the middle of dueling gambits between the Star Empire and the Trade Federation. Recruited to perform the heist of a lifetime, the fate of the Star Empire rests in her hands.

Rescue Run is an Event card for the award-winning Star Realms Deckbuilding Game, and it is based on the first Star Realms novel, Star Realms: Rescue Run.  Events are shuffled into the Trade Deck, and resolve when they are revealed.  Rescue Run has a powerful effect that gets even stronger as the game progresses and players improve their decks.  Tension builds as the action unfolds, just like in the novel!

boardgame: Beast Masters: The Dice Placement Game
description:
Beast Masters is a Dice Placement Game for 2-5 Players. If you grew up playing any kind of Monster raising simulation game, you will fall in love with Beast Masters!

Take the role of a young, new Beast Trainer seeking fame and fortune! Spend your time training your beast, going on adventure and confronting threats that affect the citizens of Tymuria, all of which come to a head each Season with the Grand Capital Tournament!

In this game you are scouting band members to create cool and exciting bands. You will then help these bands debut, making a name for themselves, before your talent agency runs out of money. Of course, you will also be aiming to get these bands in to perform at the legendary Namidano Festival, on that famed stage that people are always talking about.

Namidano Festival is a band management card game in which you will rely on your assessment of possibilities as well as your luck with timing. Players will take on the role of talent management, working within the limits of their hand of cards, efficiently building bands in order to build the reputation of their talent agency. Along the way, you will be managing your bands, getting them into events, and hopefully the festival itself, scoring points along the way.
publishers: 大気圏内ゲームズ (Taikikennai Games)

description:
Description from the publisher:

Ashinaga Tenagis are usually sited in streams or rivers and it seems that they have a deep connection with the water, able to commune with the water around them to turn the land into a sea of mud. These yokai, long a legend amongst the peasants of the Ro-Kan, exact motivations for joining the fray are unknown but they are a welcome boost to the morale of the villagers they appear to protect, their presence imbuing a sense of belief in the common people enabling them to fight harder in defence of the balance of Jwar. To look at them individually one would think that they lack any combat skill, but once combined, one astride the other, the pair are a tricky opponent. With a snaring net in one hand which they use to either entangle an enemy up close or from afar, in the other a diamond sharp fishing spear handled with great precision.

Description from the publisher:

The legendary Master of the Temple’s slow and thoughtful nature with his students is the exact opposite when on the battlefield. The diminutive Tanuki is a cyclone of energy, flying between enemies in a flurry of blows, dashing from melee to melee. Nigh impossible to target at range the enemy must close in an attempt to deal with this furry thorn. The Temple do not exclude any who hold the needs of the many and protection of the realm above personal gain as their ideals, Master Akari & Kuma are perfect examples of this mentality.

Description from the publisher:

What the peasants of the Ro-Kan lack in weapons and armour they make up for with their knowledge of the lands they are defending and with the ingenuity born from the necessity to survive in these times of war. Kaito’s tenacity on the battlefield is inspiring to those around him, allowing those imbued by his courage to shrug off lesser wounds. Knowing the lands like the back of his hand, allows Kaito and his allies to pass through the foliage unimpeded, meaning the Temple can pick their battles where the terrain is most advantageous.

Description from the publisher:

Unlike the female Kitsune the males of this mysterious and seclusive yokai race cannot shapeshift and instead keep the form of an anthropomorphic fox. Kyuubi is a skilled warrior and hunter of those his people feel are to blame for the current imbalance in Jwar. He serves a swift and decisive punishment to those Shisai and Shugenja abusing the spirits for their own ends. Waiting silently in the cover of the foliage before striking like a vengeful spirit against the enemies of the Temple.

Description from the publisher:

The defence of the oldest temples and most sacred shrines is often entrusted to the great Oki Shisa. Mountains of ever watchful stone, still and silent for decades but always ready. None know how many Oki Shisa there are but these holy wardens are the bane of those who would attempt to manipulate their Ki against the Temple or hold sway over the land. The enchantments that gift the Shisa protection also imbue the stone with a keenness akin to a Masamune blade.

Description from the publisher:

None have managed to attain such a deep and wide understanding of the world as the venerable Master Enos. His body now old but his mind and powers have never been stronger. His knowledge of the elements enable him to exploit the very Kami themselves, drawing on their energy to transform himself into a conduit of nature’s strength. The other Masters however question how much this unique ability damages the balance and relationship the Temple holds with them. It is certainly obvious that Enos’s mastery of the Void has enabled him to unlock knowledge of the element thought lost and forgotten. Some powers though are better left lost.

Description from the publisher:

Yuuki’s quickly found the name of the wind, where others hear only whistle and whisper. Yuuki hears the chatter of excited and boundless Kami. She is an able warrior and much like her element; rarely still, in play she is light and airy like a summer breeze but when called upon in battle she is a direct and relentless howling gale. Her vitality is endless and her speed astounding, her body a blur of motion as she unleashes blow after blow, an unstoppable storm of Rokan’s fury personified.

Description from the publisher:

Kira is an able and astute student, keen in practise and eager to study. Although slight in frame and few with words she is more than capable of holding her own against much larger foes. She spends hours each day in quiet meditation listening to the rocks and the stones and learning their secrets. From this knowledge she has started to unlock the power that lies silent within her, able to change her body for a short time into an impenetrable and unmoving barrier.

Description from the publisher:

Kuma is the rock on which the water breaks the solid and resilient constant of the Temple. With Bo staff or claw he strikes out and floors foes with swift and brutal strikes. Despite his mass it is difficult to pin him down or outnumber him and with a practiced mediation Kuma can reach out to the roots of the earth to bind and hold firm those would overrun him.

Description from the publisher:

Yggdrasill magiA is a dice game with 21 dice. But it is not a dice game as you know it. This game uses cards to change the number of dice. And not only can you change your dice, but also the dice of others.

You have to change the dice to the number you want, otherwise, you can not win this game. Infer other people's hands and make the game you want. This game is a card game that deceives other players.

Description from the designer (Translated):
An abstract board game of both luck and strategy.  Compete to get your pieces out of the board before the other players do.

Ollinkalli has a unique piece movement mechanic.  When you move your own pieces, it's likely you'll be moving your opponents' too.  You should be attentive and see if your moves benefit yourself or somebody else too.  Be careful, maybe you are helping them to win before you do.

Description from the designer (Spanish):
Es un juego de mesa, de azar y estrategia, con un diseño y una mecánica de juego totalmente original.  El objetivo es sacar todas tu piezas del tablero antes que los demas jugadores.

Ollinkalli destaca por la manera particular en que se mueven sus piezas. Cuando tu muevas tus piezas, es muy probable que muevas piezas de tus oponentes al mismo tiempo. Deberás fijarte muy bien si tus movimientos te ayudan solo a ti o también a alguien más. 
Ten cuidado, si te distraes, quizás hasta los ayudes a ganar el juego antes que tú!

boardgame: Broken Peace (fan expansion for T.I.M.E Stories)
description: A print and play expansion for T.I.M.E Stories. The scenario takes place at the Peace Conference after WWI in Paris, France.

Description from the publisher:

GIANTHOBBY original board game "DORASURE" expansion version 7th bullet!
A new game system to reinforce the story elements "Guardian of Magic Dragon" and 1 additional character!

"Guardian of the Magic Dragon" stopped in front of the adventurer who aims to get rid of the dragon. Can you persuade the dark swordsmen to protect the dragon for the mission of the clan and can both challenge the dragon? The new character "Dusk blade" that appears as "guardian of the dragon" can also be used as a normal additional character.
"DORASURE" is a fully cooperative miniature board game.

Get ready to tell stories like you've never told them before. What's that? Why is this different and just not some marketing ploy? Well, I'll tell you internet friend!

In Everything Makes Sense, you'll be telling stories will all your friends, your family, and even your greatest enemies for story glory!

Games last several rounds or until THE END OF TIME ITSELF. Play until your heart's content. Play for laughs, play to win, play to tell the greatest tale. Play to understand the fundamentals of what it even means to play!

It's up to you.

We're pretty confident things will make sense, otherwise, that would just be awkward.

- How to play -

The game is simple. Each player draws two cards from the Character, Setting, and Plot decks up to a maximum hand size of (6) cards. Whenever you play a card, draw the same card type, always drawing back up to six cards.

The group selects a Story Stickler (judge) who reads off the Theme card, enforces any rules of that card, weights in on the direction of the story if needed, and most importantly awards the points.

Each player plays up to three cards per theme, playing one between the Beginning, Middle, and End of each story. The Player who goes first has the freedom to set up the story however they like, whereas the player who goes last has the power to really end with a bang.

The round ends when all players have played three cards between the Beginning, Middle, and End. The Story Stickler then picks whom the winner is based on whatever they liked from that story. A new Story Stickler is chosen and a new Theme is picked, giving way to a new tale.

Oh! I almost forgot. There's also Plot Twists that can be played anytime to override any card in the story with another card from your hand.

As crazy as stories can get, hopefully... Everything Makes Sense!

Theme: From ancient Egypt to the apocalyptic times of the zombie invasions, going through the Middle Ages and the present age, cats have always sought the supremacy of their counterparts.

In this game each player represents a cat leader who tries to drive away the inhabitants of the regions controlled by their enemies, attacking them ... with cats!

A simple but at the same time very exciting card game that you can use in those moments in which you need to activate your mind.

Goal: To be the last cat leader in the game after having won two of three rounds.

Gameplay: After shuffling the deck of cards, five cards are taken and the player closest to his birthday starts the round.
At each turn a player may use a card to either attack, defend or change sides. If a player can not use a card, he simply passes the turn and waits for the result of the round.

At the end of the round if there are active attack cards, each player will receive a damage card for each attack card on the table, except the last player to play a card.

The player who scores nine points with damage cards loses the round and is eliminated from the game, the last player standing wins the game.

Expansion for Escape Room: The Game.

The last few years you traveled with your team from casino to casino, from Las Vegas to Monaco and from Atlantic City to Singapore. No trouble in sight till you get a disturbing phonecall in your shabby hotel room.

Carolyn Castle broke the first rule of your styste: Never get Caught!
family: Series: Escape Room – The Game (Identity Games International B.V.)

boardgame: Perdition's Mouth: Abyssal Rift – Wound Deck
description:
The fans of Perdition's Mouth created such an amazing set of extra wounds that Dragon Dawn Productions felt burning need to print all of them.

Using this set of 9 extra wound cards will make the game more difficult, but also more lively.
Boardgame like Game of Goose, where you have to collect coasters by solving riddles, while racing to the end of the track. Bottle caps are used as player markers.

Description from the publisher:

Paris 1800' invites you to relive the XIXth century in Paris with its great figures, Romantic and Modern, to decide the future planning of the capital. Moderns transform the city by expanding its boulevards, creating monuments in iron and glass and restoring order. Romantics want to preserve the old quarters of bourgeois ambitions using the dissatisfaction of the people of Paris and inspiring them with revolutionary ideas.

Paris 1800' is an assymetrical game in which the objective is to have more victory points at the end of play (1910) or reach 20 VP at any moment. 
In each round the players: 
1 : collect Resources (Inspiration points or Millions of francs)
2 : move character(s), according to the Observatory
3 : perform an action (one per character)

The Romantics score by making Revolutions, finding and stopping the Engineer from transforming districts and writing in Exile. They can also trigger Unrest in the districts (Discontent and Revolt), get inspiration in the Forest and  liberate a Romantic from the Fort.

Moderns score by transforming Faubourgs, Ville and Cité districts. Their actions are draw Projects, obtain/change Permit, Restore order and Imprison a Romantic.

Both camps are able to open/close the newspaper and to change the movement order in the Observatory.

Bribe: When a Prefect tries to put a Romantic in the Fort, he can use one a Bribe marker to avoid being locked up, but the Moderns immediately gain 2 million francs.

Calculation and treason cards: only valid in a three-player situation.

Each player draws two "Calculation and Treason" cards. Between 1825 and 1850, at any moment, every player secretly holds one card in hand. From 1850, if a player is a renegade, he will have to discreetly help the other camp.

After 1850 loyal players can denounce a suspected renegade. If the player is indeed a renegade, he stops play and joins the other camp (the game is still a 3v3 pawns). If he's not a renegade, the camp loses immediately 5 VP.

Le Billard Finlandais is a handmade woodgame with a elastic strap shooting device.

The players use a shooting device to shoot a wooden disk and to try to knock over wooden pins, which are marked with numbers from 1 to 12. The pins are initially placed in a tight group in an upright position. Knocking over one pin scores the amount of points marked on the pin. Knocking 2 or more pins scores the number of pins knocked over. After each throw, the pins are stood up again in the exact location where they landed. The first one to reach exactly 50 points wins the game. Scoring more than 50 will be penalised by setting the player's score back to 25 points. A player will be eliminated from the game if they miss all of the target pins three times in a row.

Another worm to emerge from the perilous, relentless training of the Kaze Kage Zoku, Wamu is a swift and nimble novice assassin. Like her fellow novice Wamu is able to employ the teachings of her mentors to great effect. A competent warrior and a promising student of the ways of the ninja.

Description from the publisher:

The raccoons are out of control. They’ve diversified into many different forms, morphing into all manner of styles. In Raccoon Madness, you’ll have to be good at bluffing in order to get rid of your cards. Will another player call your bluff?

Players that have no cards won!

Every day we have to decide whether to tell the truth or to tell a lie; sometimes we are afraid of being deceived and we feel ashamed whenever we are caught in a lie. To train our intuition and to enhance our emotion reading skills in a fun environment, we have created “Raccoon Madness Game”.

This game was created by three friends. We have been inspired by Tim Burtons art, old USSR game and Texas Holdem Poker.

boardgame: The World of SMOG: Rise of Moloch – The Wheel

description:
With Quadropolis: Public Services, an expansion for the Quadropolis base game, players can add new public service buildings to their cities. As Mayor, will they choose to increase the population by building a maternity ward or protect their citizens with a new police station? Perhaps a reprocessing plant would be a good investment to decrease pollution... Whatever you choose, competition will be fierce, and being able to build the right public service at the right time will not be made easy by the other players.

Each round, a selection of public service tiles are revealed and placed face up next to the board. Players will then be able to build these in their city, with each of these new buildings offering in-game bonuses and scoring options to newly challenge you as Mayor of a modern city.

GAME SYSTEM

    
    
         This entry is to allow for discussion/rating of the game system as a whole. It is not for a specific product or release. Versions will appear on the individual item pages.

    


From the publisher:
"Based on the delightful Basher Science Book Series, Basher Science toys and games show kids that science is fascinating and fun! The Basher Science Card Game is 2 games in 1 — play battle or match with fun science facts! The base game includes 22 colorful cards that teach kids about various topics such as liquid, pollen, and sandstone, from different branches of science, plus a mini-figure and 2 tokens for game play. Basher Science Card Games are available in these science subjects: Rocks & Minerals, Biology, Astronomy, and Chemistry. Collect Basher Science Mini-Figure singles and 3-packs for exclusive game cards with special powers! For ages 6 and up
Features:
• Learning about science is big fun with Basher Science!
• The Basher Science Card Game is 2 games in 1—play battle or match with fun science facts!
• Each character card teaches facts about an aspect of science (such as atom, virus, and supernova) and includes a special power for use during game play
• When you purchase the Basher Science mini-figures, look for exclusive cards that can also be used in the game
• Pack includes 24 colorful cards that teach kids about topics from different branches of science. Also includes a mini-figure and 2 tokens for game play
• Collect the game for each science branch and have a blast as you learn!"

Further details:
The base game is a small box of 22 cards (2 copies of 11 different cards) and a single Shopkins-like figure.  There are four base boxes: Biology, Chemistry, Astronomy and Rocks & Minerals.  Additionally, there are 3-packs and 1-packs.  These come with three or 1 Shopkin-like figures and 2 copies of their associated cards.  Each pack is also themed Biology, Chemistry, Astronomy and Rocks & Minerals.

Battle Game:
Before the game starts, each player builds an 8-card deck.  Each card has a value between 1 and 8, a name, a small fact, and a game-play ability.  Cards from different themes can be mixed.  Players draw a hand of three cards from their decks.  They then simultaneously choose one and reveal it.  The higher number wins the round.  Special powers will break rules and make things interesting.  The player who first wins 4 rounds (short game) or 6 rounds (long game) wins the game.

A small expansion that consists of 5 Expansion Cards.

These five red/orange cards have the "Alternate Attack" ability, and feature the letters M and Z, and intended to replace the "Attack" cards in the original game.

These cards allow players to activate an ability to hurt other players, for example by forcing them to discard cards.

Wargame Japanese History game #27.

The game uses a worker-placement system and abstractly covers the period from the start of the Mibu Rōshigumi (1863) to the end of the Boshin War (1869).

In start, only mild preparing missions can be conducted such as recruiting, financing, connection making.  Then, each turn a new mission is revealed and can be selected. The most VPs are awarded for assassination of enemy roushi and/or success of military campaign.

The game ends after 5 or 6 turns. It can be played with 2,3 or 4 players and within 2 hours.


The Mibu Rōshigumi (壬生狼顛末記) was a police unit formed from many samurais in 1863 to protect the Shogunate government. It was an organization which precede the famous Shinsen Gumi. https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Shinsengumi
family: History: Boshin War & Meiji Era Rebellions

description:
This is a traditional card game from the southern parts of Taiwan and Penghu County. The game uses 10 sets of 12 cards depicting the 12 Chinese zodiacs, namely the Rat, Ox, Tiger, Rabbit, Dragon, Snake, Horse, Goat, Monkey, Rooster, Dog and Pig.

Players are dealt 11 cards each, with the banker dealt an extra card. The goal of the game is to form sets of 3, either in sequence or of the same zodiac. It is also possible to form a set of 12, i.e. all 12 zodiacs. The player who manages to go out first, i.e. 胡牌, receives a certain amount of chips from the other players.

Finally a game to play out your knowledge of spelling against others! This game requires you to draw cards with letters on them. Clues guide you through the spelling of words, like 'it starts with a P' or 'It has 8 letters'.

Who knows the most puzzlewords? Who draws the joker? Who can claim the words with the required elements? Never was a word puzzle so amazing!

Station One is the second Data Pack in the Red Sand Cycle for Android: Netrunner, and it continues the cycle's exploration of the tensions between the Corps, Runners, and clan members of Mars.

With its sixty cards (three copies each of twenty different cards), Station One carries us to the primary hub for Bradbury Railway Co.’s mag-lev inductracks, a site that's crucial to the distribution of food, fuel, and raw materials across the resource-starved cities and nodes of Mars. The Martian colonists scattered across the planet’s surface rely upon its supplies for their survival. The megacorps terraforming the planet—and increasing their control over it—view the station as a vital hub for their financial investments. And the opportunistic runners of Android: Netrunner? They see Station One as a tempting target through which all the secrets of Mars must pass.

You'll find new assets, resources, programs, and barriers. You'll find Clan Aeneas exerting its influence. And you'll find a daring new Criminal runner identity—one willing to "face check" ice if it will help him turn a profit.
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable
family: Collectible: Living Card Game (Fantasy Flight Games)

description:
BEEctionary is a hex tile placement word game. The game allows players to play in 12 various languages - English, German, French, Russian, Turkish, Arabic, Persian, Italian, Spanish, Portuguese, Dutch and Azerbaijani (all letter tiles are included in one box).

The goal is to create in each round the longest word no one can beat by placing only one letter tile each turn. The game starts with bee hex that could be used as any letter during active player's turn. The word can be in any direction as long as all hex tiles to be used are adjacent and in proper sequence. After each round winner of the round takes all hexes he/she used on his longest word. Next round starts with the next player and with all tiles left on the table after winner of previous round gets his tiles.

Player scores 1 point for each letter and 3 points for bee hex. The one who reaches 20 points first is the winner of the game.

boardgame: Gunship Crew: A Solitaire Game of Helicopter Gunship Combat in Viet Nam (1965-1973).
description:
Gunship Crew is a Game where you are a Commander of a Helicopter Gunship in a Helicopter Assault Company in Viet Nam. Your Goal is to manage your aircraft and crew and Complete a Campaign consisting of a series of Missions and score as many Victory Points (VP) as possible to assist the war efforts. Campaigns can be Short (8 Missions), Moderate (12 Missions), or Long (16 Missions). If the Player takes too many losses before their Campaign is complete, the Enemy will gain the initiative and win.

Missions include rescuing downed crews, helicopter assaults, convoy escorts, river patrols, hunter-killer and general fire support.

The Combat system is similar to the one used in Patton's Best by Avalon Hill. This method best captures the feeling of flying a single Helicopter and resolving combat.

GUNSHIP CREW can be found at the following URL:
http://www.berserkergames.net

boardgame: Blood Bowl (2016 Edition): Skaven and Dwarf Pitch & Dugout Set
description:
Play games of Blood Bowl on this excellent double-sided foldout card pitch! Made to the same specifications as the pitch found in the boxed game, it features a filthy, ‘orrible Skaven pitch on one side – the reeking, dank cavern is perfect for scuttling about on – and a much tidier, properly-hewn Dwarven stadium-vault on the other. Of course, the 2 double-sided dugouts follow the same themes.

Supplied with a new Subterranean Weather Table – exclusive rules featuring weather effects appropriate to underground sports brutality!

boardgame: Days of Ire: Budapest 1956 – Kickstarter Promo Cards
description:
Nine bonus cards included with the fulfilment of the 2016 Kickstarter campaign.

- AK-47 (Revolutionary Card)
- Growing Social Discontent (Starting Event)
- Last Stand (Revolutionary Card)
- Remembering Poznań (Starting Event)
- SPA Members Suspected (Starting Event)
- Blue, Yellow, Red, Green Characters

Shell Beach is a treasure trove to the Hermit Crabs that live there, and the bravest crabs scramble between waves to collect the most desirable seashells to bring to the Market.  Some shells are even big enough to make a home out of!  Each player is a daring hermit crab scrapping seashells for sale, trying to upgrade their own shell, keeping one eye on the competition, and even taking each other’s old shells.  Make the most of each tide, before the Market closes for the season!

Shell Beach is played over a series of Rounds, which have a Collection Phase, where cards are dealt and drafted, and a Market Phase, where sets are sold and special shells can be used.

Sets of shell cards can be turned into the Market for tokens, but the biggest ones can be lived in by the Crabs, which will allow them to carry more and better sets back to market.  There are also 4 Magic Seashells that hypnotize a crab into abandoning their home shell, initiating a unique new game mechanic based on real-life crab behavior.

When the market runs out of tokens, each player remaining finishes the current round and the winner is revealed.

Last Chip Standing: Devil's Luck is a fiendishly simple, feather-light game of chance for 3+ players. In this Wild Western game of chance, roll dice, pass and steal loot, and be the last outlaw with chips to win the whole pot.

User summary:

王さまとカフェ (King and Cafe) is a game of speed, where players are vying to fill the most cafe orders for the king.

At the start of the game, one player is chosen as king, while all others take the role of local cafe managers.  Each player draws a hand of seven cards.  There is a pile of order cards for the king and ingredients for the managers.  The king briefly displays all of their order cards and then the managers are allowed to adjust their hand of ingredients.

At the beginning of each round, the king chooses an order card and plays it face up to the table.  All of the cafe managers then race to gather the ingredients from their hand and touch the standee of the king.  The first player to successfully complete the order, receives the order card and its points.

Managers are then allowed to discard and draw until they have a hand of seven cards in preparation for the next round.  After seven rounds have passed, the cafe managers tally up the value of the order cards they completed.  The player with the highest total wins.

BENEATH - an underground adventure for 1-4 dwarves (2-4 players with the core game)

A board game of sometimes-cooperative mining and mayhem in a quest to restore the Dwarven Kingdom. 1-4 players, 60 min. per player.

In Beneath players take on the roles of brave but ruthless Dwarves seeking untold riches and the crown of the Dwarven Kingdom. Mining, trapping, stealing and fighting through the uncharted passages beneath the city of Stonewatch, players race to be the first to find the legendary King’s Gift, a vein of ore so valuable the Dwarf that finds it will be crowned ruler of all the Dwarves, if they can survive both the monsters and their own colleagues...

Only time will tell if you have what it takes to survive what lies beneath.

Beneath is easy to set up. Each dwarf has a unique player board to track their equipment and abilities. Each scenario map in the campaign shows exactly how to set up the challenges your dwarves will face, creating a uniquely 3D board for each step of your adventure.

Once players select their Dwarves, each with unique abilities, they can further personalise their strategy by purchasing weapons, items and skills from their local merchant. Whether you prefer your treasure hacked from the lifeless bodies of your enemies, or thieved from the pockets of your fellow Dwarves, Beneath lets you Dwarf the way you want to Dwarf.

Fully equipped, players they take turns as they journey through the mines, digging through rock and ancient granite, searching for treasure that all Dwarves crave and monsters to slay. A streamlined skills system keeps play dynamic and exciting whilst allowing incredible breadth in how your particular dwarf faces the dangers below.

But what good is treasure if you’re not alive to spend it? Each foray into the world of Beneath is also a race to find your way back out and do so with pieces of ancient scroll that show the way to the King’s Gift.

With enough pieces of scroll any dwarf will win the game, but there’s no honour among dwarves in the dark catacombs, and no crown for second place. Pinching those pieces of scroll from each other? ‘Accidentally’ blowing up your fellow miners, blocking the exit to tie your shoelaces whilst your friend is slowly devoured by a colossal tremor worm? First rule of mining – they can’t submit a formal complaint if they’re inside a giant worm. This is semi-cooperative dungeon crawling at its most semi.

With enough pieces of scroll any dwarf will win the game, but there’s no honour among dwarves in the dark catacombs, and no crown for second place. Pinching those pieces of scroll from each other? ‘Accidentally’ blowing up your fellow miners, or blocking the exit to tie your shoelaces whilst your friend is slowly devoured by a Gigantic Igadon?

First rule of mining – they can’t submit a formal complaint if they’re inside a giant reptilian. This is semi-cooperative dungeon crawling at its most semi.

A dice game where you brew potions out of 5 different ingredients. The lead player takes cubes out of a bag, forms as many groups as there are players, and then each player picks one of the groups. Players then use these cubes to make potions, empower potions and sell them.

The player who most skillfully brews potions, and gains the most money in the end is the winner!

description:
Make those cute little emojis fight for you.
The angel, happy, gritted teeth, sad, mad, devil and more; all your favorite emojis come out to play in this unique game where you set fellow players up to hurt themselves. It is a race to get rid of all your cards first as everyone is trying to make you fail. Unique game play lends itself to an all out battle, where any emoji might be your last.

The Knockout (KO) Expansion
Beyond the cute faces you are used to seeing, there are other emoji symbols that can help you in battle. Throw the time bomb on the table, or place a target on your greatest enemy, then dawn a shield to protect yourself. This expansion adds a collection of extra cards to make your emoji battles even better. Will you be the one to deliver the knockout punch and get rid of your cards first? Only time will tell.

Game Play
Players take turns playing emojis from their hand.  Every card played has consequences based on the last card played before it.  First person out of cards wins the round.  After five rounds game is over.  Scoring based on emojis left in your hand after every round.

Description from the publisher:

The NaW Compendium gives you three campaigns, Normandy, Operations Cobra and Arnhem.  It's a game of risk and reward as players must learn to conserve their forces. The more lives you spend during one scenario, the fewer they'll have to start the next. However, the more objectives players complete earn them more points which they can use to outfit and re-fit their forces.

You receive from the campaigns a combined 13 scenarios plus an additional 15 stand alone scenarios and another 15 scenarios from our Line of Fire magazine for a total 43 scenarios in total as well as two redesigned maps. NaW Compendium Vol. 1 is the joining of two previous released expansions titled Airborne, Operation Cobra and all the NaW scenarios from Line of Fire magazine. We have cleaned up the scenarios, redesigned and updated the maps and put everything into our new NaW series style.
family: Series: Nations at War (Lock 'n Load Publishing)

description:
Omen Quest is a slight trick taking card game based around 5 rounds, where two players or a 4 player co-op on two teams try to out do their opponent with effective card combinations to win each round with the objective to win best out of 3 before the game ties into a death match in the 5th round.

Players maintain 5 cards or 3 cards for 4 players and expense coins to play card combinations. With some luck, and a bit of strategy of how you maneuver through your guild of cards that you burn haven cards to gain better hands, you must avoid burning too many of your haven cards or you instantly lose. Some players take risks, some play conservatively, if you have a good stride with your style it may work for you depending on the player you play against or it may fail you every time.

There are other strategies to win the game that may deplete your opponents coins disabling them from playing effective card combinations to win or playing your hand right forcing your opponent to burn a lot of haven cards. The tone switches drastically from a contemplative 2 player game to a very social 4 player game of banter and psyching as players trade cards and manage wins without doubt of winning the game.

Description from the designer:

GIA Isn't Abstract, is a 1-2 player economic dice placement game played over 4 rounds.

The world of GIA (pronounced "Gaia") revolves around 4 fundamental resources - 'A' (pronounced "Eh"), 'B' (pronounced "Be"), 'C' (pronounced "Se"), and 'D' (pronounced "De"). These resources hold fixed values relative to each other and the world's monetary unit - 'VP' (pronounced VePe). You will play traders in GIA's fundamental resource trading market, and will compete to hold the largest share of VPs at the end of trading.

Print and Play files available here:
GIA Isn't Abstract Game Rules
GIA Isn't Abstract Full Colour Cards
GIA Isn't Abstract Low Ink Cards

GIA Isn't Abstract was a 2017 9-Card Nanogame Contest entry - GIA Isn't Abstract contest entry WIP thread.

boardgame: Star Trek: Attack Wing – U.S.S. Enterprise-D Expansion Pack
description: This ship is the competitive prize for the "Yesterday's Enterprise" episode Organized Play event. It is a Federation Galaxy class ship and provides an alternative Enterprise-D to the starter set version.

boardgame: Dust Tactics: General Erwin Rommel – "Desert Fox"
description:
Now supreme leader of the grand Axis army, General Rommel earned his nickname in the sands of North-Africa. He's back there and this time for good: there won't be any retreat this time. Commanding operations in Africa and the Middle-East at the same time, the Desert Fox proves once again that he's one of the best generals on the planet.

This miniatures com with his own unit card, to use with Dust Tactics and Dust Tactics Battlefield rules and provide new tactic opportunities.

boardgame: Dust Tactics: Wermacht Heavy Flame Grenadier Squad – "Desert Sun"
description:
The unarmoured Afrikakorps troops love to have these guys around. A heavy squad is just the thing for tackling stubborn opposition head-on while the other Grenadiers manoeuvre around the flanks. Seeing the destructive heat of their flame-throwers’ gout of burning fuel, it’s little surprise that they are nicknamed ‘Desert Suns’.

These miniatures come with their own unit card, to use with Dust Tactics and Dust Tactics Battlefield rules and provide new tactic opportunities.

boardgame: Dust Tactics: Major Tina Baumann's – "Tina & Hyane"
description:
Major Tina Baumann is the head of the Abwehr for the Neue Deutsches Afrika Korps, the famed NDAK. She is a master counter-spy. When confronting a suspect, she is known to let her vicious pet hyena, Hyäne, do most of the questioning.
Major Baumann’s headquarters are based in Tripoli, Libya. People who enter her compound without wearing an Axis uniform are never to be seen again. Sometimes, even those who wear the uniform disappear for her wrath knows no boundaries.

These miniatures come with their own unit card, to use with Dust Tactics and Dust Tactics Battlefield rules and provide new tactic opportunities.

The Golden Sentinels are the bodyguards of the Emperor’s clan, guarding the family on their travels, in their homes and in court. They also serve as escorts to his Teishin as they journey his Empire spreading his holy word. If the Teishin of the Imperial court are the Emperors voice in distant more barbaric lands, then the Golden Sentinels serve as his shield, all proven warriors, sworn to protect their charges with their lives if needed. However, such is the size of the Golden Empire that Imperial missions and postings can often take many seasons to complete not taking into account the travel across the great empire and so often the Sentinels can sometimes take on the local customs and in rare circumstances become embroiled in the affairs of locals.

Blister contains one miniature, one 30mm base and three full colour profile cards.

Yukio Koshimori is one of the shrewdest, craftiest minds in the empire. Many question his recent arrival in the Jwar Isles, some suggest his appearance a bad omen; that the Empire’s interest in the isles is piqued whilst others claim it can only lead to prosperity. What is certain is that when Yukio speaks, he speaks with the authority of the emperor and whether he is questioning a samurai’s honour, chastising an underling or barking orders, he cannot be dis-obeyed. Yukio is a representative of the Imperial Court and can issue a decree in their name should those in his presence displease him. This single action leaves those who oppose him with their plans in tatters, their troops powerless, having no choice but to obey the word of the Emperor.

Blister contains one miniature, one 30mm base, three full colour profile cards and an enhancement card.
The first installment for the Prefecture of Ryu, Bikou is a highly trained ninja. Skilled in the arts of stealth and distraction she strives to strike where her enemies least expect her.

Mikio is a moving mountain on the battlefield. Jumo wrestling skills honed in the pits of Jima enable him to push, pull and throw opponents of the Prefecture at will.

Blister contains one miniature, 30mm base and full colour profile card.

Driven on by a purpose few could match, Matsu fights for her lost sister. The shugenja training she has received for years is now being channelled and focussed in ways her mentors never intended. She balks at no fight against any opponent, seeing any battle as an opportunity to hone her abilities. Allowing her to one day take down her real adversary, Itsunagi Ito.
 Blister contains one miniature, 30mm base and full colour profile card.

Once washed up, Daisuke now washes away the pain of battle from his allies. He has become a specialist, channelling his Ki to heal and support the forces of the Prefecture. He is never far from the fray and can often be seen ensuring members of the Takashi family return home.

Blister contains one miniature, 30mm base and full colour profile card.

Mizuchi was recently awoken by the blood of Asama Takashi and he is sworn to protect her twin, Matsu. Swooping into battle he uses tail, tooth and claw as weapons against any who oppose the rule of the Prefecture of Ryu. His ability to quickly and easily traverse the battlefield have already proved the difference in many confrontations. Many within the Prefecture say that Mizuchi is a gift from the Kami’s and a sign of their right to rule.
 Blister contains one miniature, one 40mm base and one full colour profile card.

Tenchi, Seishin na Goei are an ancient order of highly skilled warrior women sworn to protect not only the bodies of the lords of Ryu, but also their souls. Offering their lives in service of their clan, fearless in the face of death and cold steel for those that oppose the Takashi.

The conch blower has made the role his own and is held up as an example of how auxiliary support should operate in the forces of Ryu. Renowned for his ability to reinvigorate the soldiers around him with his rallying calls, Junichi simply states to new recruits ‘When you hear the call, try harder’.

Another daughter of the Takashi clan has taken up the fight. Hagane wields her twin Katanas to devastating effect. More like her uncle Hida than his own son, Hiro. Her ruthless streak has endeared her to those that tread the halls of power at the very top of the Prefecture. Hagane can be relied upon to get the job done.

boardgame: Battle of the Sexes: Additional New Cards
description: 650 additional new cards to the basic game.

When the tide of the battle is changing and the will of the Prefecture’s soldiers is waning, Fujitaka is there waving his banner and rallying the troops. His bravery in the face of impossible odds is well known and his fellow Ashigaru find their own inner strength in his presence. Fujitaka also shares his own strength to enable others to continue the good fight.

boardgame: The Man from U.N.C.L.E. Annual: No Man's Land
description:
The game pits two players in a race to reach the 'safe' farmhouse. Each player rolls a die and moves along his line. You go back and forth until you land on a circle. The circles might send you up a line or down a line. If you land on a red square entitles you to another throw. Land on a green square and you miss a turn.

The game appeared in The Man from U.N.C.L.E. Annual, published in 1969.

Ryoko-Sha is a samurai with distinct and unique skills. His understanding of strategy and tactics has allowed him to channel Ki to control the ebb and flow of battle. He is of utmost importance to the Takashi family, his Temporal Barriers are relied upon to keep family members safe.

Dash Through the Dungeon is a fast-paced game of dungeon exploration for 2-6 players ages 6 and up. Each player takes on the role of a brave adventurer, making their way through a series of dark dungeons. Your goal is to find treasure while avoiding creatures and other obstacles, like traps and locked doors. You’ll have some help along the way in the form of useful equipment and beneficial encounters.

Contents:

60 Dungeon Cards

21 Equipment Cards

Set Up:

First step is to separate the Dungeon Cards and Equipment Cards into two decks and give them both a good shuffle. Next take the Dungeon Cards and deal out the first dungeon, laying 20 cards out face down in a 4 x 5 grid. This will be the first dungeon that the players will take turns exploring.

Next deal out three equipment cards at random to each player. This is your starting equipment. Players are free to look at these cards. You can place them either face up or face down on the table in front of you. Playing with them face down adds a bit of additional suspense to the game since other players won’t know if you have the equipment needed to get past a particular encounter until you reveal it.

Now you’re ready to play.

Taking a Turn:

The game is played in a series of turns. A player starts their turn with 6 action points or APs. Once its decided who goes first, players one begins their dash through the dungeon. They select and flip over any card on the 5 X 5 dungeon grid, then subtract the AP cost of that card from their remaining APs. In some cases, like flipping over the Pit Trap, the card may tell you that you must “lose all remaining AP and end turn” which means you’re turn is done. Some equipment cards, like the Float-Up Potion, can be used to cancel out these cards. A card that’s canceled in this way costs 0 AP. Some equipment cards can also be used to reduce the AP cost of certain cards, like Bandage, or even reduce them to 0, like Freeze Bomb. Your turn ends when your AP reaches 0 or you flip over a Treasure. When your turn ends you take any cards you flipped over, keeping Treasure cards separate from the other dungeon cards.

Then it’s the next player’s turn to explore. Players continue until all the cards of the first dungeon have been flipped over. When the last card is flipped, the AP of the player who flipped it is reduced to 0 and their turn ends.

Another 4 X 5 grid of Dungeon Cards are then laid out and the next player’s turn begins. Play continues in this order until a total of three dungeons have been explored.

Winning the Game:

Once all three dungeons have been explored players add up the value of their treasure cards. They then count up all the other Dungeon Cards they’ve collected and add the amount to the value of their treasure cards. Whoever has the most points wins.

Born to fight, there was never a doubt that these twins would take any path other than that of the samurai. Genji strong and quiet, Eiji quick to anger but equally quick to laugh, on the battlefield when fighting back to back they fight with an unmatched unity.

In times of war the Prefecture of Ryu is able to call upon those loyal to their Takashi Lords, swelling the ranks of the serving ashigaru with Goshi. Madoka wields his long axe in the name of Ryu, hacking down foe with sweeping blows; his courage in battle is soon restored in the presence of a trusted commander.

boardgame: Hull Breach!: Bank of The Galaxy
description:
Originally released as part of Hull Breach!'s Corporate Wars box set, this individual deck has been revamped and improved for the current edition of the game.

Our enemies think their production and their weapons make them strong. We know better, Commander. A well-placed spy can be worth more than a fleet of warships and a well-placed bullet can defeat whole armies of Marines. You have the immense resource of the Bank of the Galaxy behind you. Use them wisely to divide them and turn them against one another. Profit from their stubborn attempts to rule the galaxy through force of arms. Then, when they have been suitably bled, deliver the finishing blow.

Sensei at the Koryu school in Ryu, Katsumoto has for many years passed his exceptional tactical skills on to the sons of the Takashi clan. It is recognised that much of the military success in the Dragon wars was down to Katsumoto’s cunning. Katsumoto spent much of his formative years in the lands of the West, studying in the further flung schools, tirelessly researching the local teachings of warfare, always hungry for knowledge. On the battlefield Katsumoto is the personification of calm, every move is considered, every order weighed. Those who have faced the old warhorse know that there is never an easy victory when Katsumoto Takashi is your opponent.

boardgame: Dust Tactics: NDAK Recon Squad – "Desert Scorpions"
description: The Afrikakorps is finally back to where it belongs! And to open the path to its victorious troops, the Recon Grenadier Squad is perfectly suited to find a way. Stealthy, swift and deadly, the Desert Scorpions, as they are known, are always in front of the rest of the army. They can take out high value targets with their own weapons and even call-in artillery strikes should they encounter heavier resistance. Surely a unit feared by all enemies of the Axis!

boardgame: Dust Tactics: NDAK Tank-Hunter Grenadier Squad – "African Lions"
description:
While the vipers lay in the shade and wait for their prey, the lions roam and hunt for theirs. Generalfeldmarschall Rommel himself started calling the tank-hunter Grenadiers of the Neue Deutsches Afrikakorps the 'African Lions'.

Throughout the history of the Axis presence in Northern Africa, these men have fought isolated, without any support from tanks, walkers or artillery, more often than any other Axis unit. With only an automatic weapon and a trusty Panzerfaust, they have repeatedly defended desperate positions against all the might of the Allied army.

More men coming from the ‘African Lions’ have been awarded the Iron Cross (often posthumously) than from any other formation in the Afrikakorps. Brave and fierce, they surely deserve their nickname.

Many “African Lions” are veterans of the first Deutsches Afrikakorps. They have already been through the worst this war can offer, and many have chosen to join these specialist tank-hunters squads, were you need nerves of steel. Attacking an armoured vehicle at close range is not for the faint of heart, and veterans are more comfortable alongside others equally prepared to tackle the job, leaving younger recruits in the Grenadier Battle Squads, providing covering fire.

boardgame: Dust Tactics: NDAK Observer Squad – "Desert Eagles"
description: The Afrikakorps is finally back to where it belongs! Nothing as efficient as a good artillery barrage to keep the enemy on its toes... Thanks to these observers, regimental and division artillery pieces can deliver the most powerful punch to crush the enemies of the Axis.

boardgame: Dust Tactics: NDAK Command Grenadier Squad – "Fox Cubs"
description: The Afrikakorps is finally back to where it belongs! The NDAK Command Grenadier Squad is a special unit of field officers whose skills are invaluable. They are equipped with submachine guns and a machine gun, and their presence on the battlefield can change the entire course of battle, thanks to their invaluable skills. A wise general should take care to protect these experienced officers.

boardgame: Dust Tactics: NDAK Security Squad – "Tomb Cleaners"
description:
The Afrikakorps is finally back to where it belongs! Below the sands of Babylon lay many untold secrets, that demand brave men to search for. And these need protection from whatever they might find: that's when the Security Grenadiers come in. They are perfectly equipped to deal with what's below.

These miniatures come with their own unit card, to use with Dust Tactics and Dust Tactics Battlefield rules and provide new tactic opportunities.

boardgame: Dust Tactics: NDAK Battle Grenadier Squad – "Sand Vipers"
description:
A dedicated anti infantry squad of the Axis army, the Sand Vipers are devastating to any infantry and soft armored target. They have assault rifles and machine guns that can lay down some serious firepower. These marksmen are a feared sight for any enemy brave enough to face them.

These miniatures come with their own unit card, to use with Dust Tactics and Dust Tactics Battlefield rules and provide new tactic opportunities.

FlipFour is an innovative approach to creating a new four-in-a-row game with great game play, but using just 4 pieces per player (i.e. the absolute minimum number of pieces).

MATERIALS

4 white FlipFlop pieces, 4 black FlipFlop pieces and a 5×5 board.

FlipFlop pieces have a '+' on one side and a 'x' on the other.

RULES

Initial Setup

The board starts empty. Decide which player plays white; the other plays black. Take all 4 pieces of your color.

Goal

The first player to get all 4 of her pieces in an orthogonal or diagonal line wins (It does not matter whether her 4 pieces are +, ×, or some of each).

How to Play

White plays first. Players take turns either placing or moving one of their own pieces on the board, following the rules below:

1) When you place a new piece, it must be placed on an empty space, but you can choose whether to place it +side-up or ×-side-up.
2) When you move a piece, + moves orthogonally, and × moves diagonally, as the lines suggest. Move your piece any distance to an empty space.
3) Pieces can jump over other pieces as long as the destination space is empty.
4) After you move your piece, you must flip it over (+ becomes ×; × becomes +).
5) You are welcome to move your pieces before all of you pieces are on the board. 
6) Passing is not allowed.

Whether with yari or the fabled Arquebus the ashigaru stationed at Ryu are staunchly loyal to the Takashi and will defend their clan to the death. They are trained and drilled relentlessly to ensure that in battle they act and react instinctively, unflinching as they wait for orders from their Gunso or samurai commanders. The Arquebusiers see it as such an honor to be picked into the regiment that it is said that they even take them on their final journey to Hantaisoku. The fear their weapons create is rightly deserved as there is little that can stand up to raw power, steel, wood and fire fused into this fearsome device.

Blister contains one miniature, one 30mm base and one full colour profile card.

A veteran of the Dragon wars, tales of his courage at the battle of Hendai are still told today in the streets of Tsumaga. He had led the life of a fisherman after the first war until the call from Ryu came, summoning all goshi back to service in this new time of strife. From an early age Isamu exuded an air of authority and a calm head in the heat of the moment. It came as no surprise that he was raised to the rank of Gunso quickly during his first term of service to the Takashi clan, a position he quickly settled into. His experience and presence imbuing his soldiers with a confidence and courage to fight on and turn the tide of battle for the Dragon clan.

Blister contains one miniature, one 30mm base and one full colour profile card.

Akio Takashi is a legendary sensei of the finest sword Gakko in Ryu. The majority of the Takashi line has learning under his excellent tutelage. Now in the twilight years of his life and with the need for all able body warriors to answer Hida’s call, Akio has put down his Bokken and stepped forward to offer his life in service to his lord. Still a fearsome warrior but the real advantage he offers any fighting force is his expert knowledge of combat. His ability to instruct the samurai around him with either a point to a chink in a seemingly impenetrable suit of armour to a swift reminder of the techniques he taught them on the dust training ring of the Gakko, he is another strong commander to join the Prefecture of Ryu’s ranks.

Each turn in Vikings Gone Wild — a deck-building, resource management game based on the online real-time strategy game — players can either buy permanent buildings sitting in front of them (resource factories, resources containers, etc) or buy units, towers and special cards that improve their deck and offense/defense capacity.

The only way to win is by successfully attacking each other, and each attack involves interesting bluffing mechanisms in which the attacker doesn't know the defense capacity of their opponent...

This Kickstarter set contains the base Vikings Gone Wild Game as well as  the Guild Wars expansion, It's a Kind of Magic expansion, the 5th Viking expansion, and a Kickstarter exclusive expansion, all within the box.

boardgame: Hold the Line: French and Indian War Expansion Set
description: Include 75+ plastic miniatures with French, American Ranger, and Indians joining the fighting associated with the Hold the Line, The American Revolution game. This expansion comes with 12 new scenarios and new tiles specifically designed for the game.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

boardgame: Champions of Midgard: The Dark Mountains
description:
The Dark Mountains, the first expansion for Champions of Midgard, extends your adventures to distant lands, allowing you to trek into the dark mountains where the Bergrisar live and wage constant battle with the Archer Clans of the North. Fight fearsome mountain giants and liberate archers who join your cause. Once freed, you will find the archers to be worthy allies, rarely missing and being exceptional hunters. Enemies you face will fall easily before their sure shots.

Champions of Midgard: The Dark Mountains adds a ton of new content to the base game, including the ability to play with up to five players.

The expansion includes new land journeys, a new enemy type (Bergrisar), more powerful Trolls and Draugr, as well as additional rune cards, market tiles, and more.

Description from the publisher:

In Valhalla, the second expansion for Champions of Midgard, you will find yourself rewarded when your warriors die in battle. Your warriors' eternal glory becomes your earthly reward as the Valkyries bestow powerful effects, relics, and warriors upon you for use in your battles in Midgard. Some of your fallen soldiers may do more, rising to face the greatest enemies even in the afterlife, defeating epic monsters and bringing you glory from beyond the grave!

Couple these new rewards with fierce new warriors in the form of Berzerkers and Shieldwarriors, and new custom dice and powers for your leaders, and your adventures in Midgard will never be the same!

5 additional Achievement cards for Crash Games version of Backyard Builders Treehouse.

Selfish
Generous

Rule of Plum

boardgame: Fish Frenzy: Events & Goals Promo Pack
description:
10 additional Event/Goal cards for Crash Games version of Fish Frenzy.  Each card has a "P1" promo designation in the upper right corner.

EVENTS (6)

First Loser
Royal Fish
Not so Tasty

So Long and Thanks for ALL the Fish

Axis field commanders know how to recognize when something is good. No matter where it comes from. And after receiving so many shells on their heads from the SSU's 120 mm heavy mortar, they decided that it was time to also have such an efficient weapon in their arsenal. The 12 cm Heavy Mortar - Granatwerfer model 1947 is the latest variation based on the soviet design. It's in use all over the planet by Axis forces and has proven its value countless times both as a defensive and offensive weapon.

A deadly unit, the NDAK Heavy Mortar Squad can attack any type of target. The unit really shines when paired with an observer team: it can then stay out of harm's way and still be as deadly as ever. These two unit combined can block whole areas of the battlefield, threatening to blow up anything that comes in sight. The soldiers are presented here in their desert fatigue, as used by the Neue Deutsches Afrikakorps (NDAK) circa 1947 in North Africa and the Middle-East.

These miniatures come with their own unit card, to use with Dust Tactics and Dust Tactics Battlefield rules and provide new tactic opportunities.

boardgame: Dust Tactics: Leutnant Sammt, Luftwaffe – "Rolf Samt"
description:
Elite officer of the Fallschirmjäger Korps of the Luftwaffe, Lieutenant Rolf Sammt has seen action since Operation Mercury, the invasion of Crete in 1941. Since then he has fought almost everywhere, mostly in the Luftwaffe Field Divisions on the Eastern Front. With the reactivation of large paratroopers formations and airborne operations, he has been recalled to full Fallschirmjäger duty, where his invaluable experience is passed along to younger generations of Axis jump troops.

This miniature come with his own unit card, to use with Dust Tactics and Dust Tactics Battlefield rules and provide new tactic opportunities.

Death from the skies over Babylon! The Fallschirmjäger Tank-Hunter Squad deals with any armored target the Luftwaffe ground forces might encounter. Thanks to their Bazooka and Panzerfaust, they can engage any walker or tank and blow it to pieces in seconds. The squad is presented here in Babylon camouflage pattern, as used in the Middle-East circa 1947.

These miniatures come with their own unit card, to use with Dust Tactics and Dust Tactics Battlefield rules and provide new tactic opportunities.

Death from the skies over Babylon! The Fallschirmjäger Anti-Aircraft Squad deals with any flying target the Luftwaffe ground forces might encounter. Thanks to their Fliegerfaust (Rocket Launchers), they can engage any aircraft and blow it to pieces in seconds. The squad is presented here in Babylon camouflage pattern, as used in the Middle-East circa 1947.

These miniatures come with their own unit card, to use with Dust Tactics and Dust Tactics Battlefield rules and provide new tactic opportunities.

Death from the skies over Babylon! The Raketentruppe Battle Squad deals with any infantry target the Luftwaffe ground forces might encounter. Thanks to their Machine-gun and Panzerfaust, they can engage any enemy and destroy it in seconds. The squad is presented here in Babylon camouflage pattern, as used in the Middle-East circa 1947.

These miniatures come with their own unit card, to use with Dust Tactics and Dust Tactics Battlefield rules and provide new tactic opportunities.

boardgame: Empires: Galactic Rebellion – Faction Power Cards
description: 12 new faction power cards that add another strategic tool to your arsenal! When using the faction power cards, players can either; 1.) Draft these (one per player) in reverse starting order (last player selects first) or; 2.) Blind draw their Powers.

STORY
Your ship has crashed and suffered serious damage. It's up to you and your crew-mates to traverse the corridors to repair the ship's core systems and call for help, but be careful because your ship has been invaded by BRAIN PARASITES!

GAME PLAY
Brain Parasites is a tile based game where you explore your ship to complete the goal. Play as one of 6 crew members each with unique stats. Work as a team until the enemy reveals itself.
Open one of 36 rooms each holding a new surprise as you fight for tech and increase your skills. Will your team survive, or will the alien creature claim the vessel?

The aim of Dino Kontra Dino is to complete as many event cards as possible. 
 The game is played over a number of rounds. At the beginning of each round, a new event is drawn. During a turn, players play one of the three cards from their hand (and then draws a new one) face down. Then, all players reveal their card simultaneously. Event cards are then resolved depending on the icon featured on the dinosaur cards. The game goes on until a player plays the last card from their hand. The player who completes the most events wins.
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
Dicecapades! 2nd Edition is the same great game as the original version but now with more cards!  Think you can stack dice on your face? Will you dominate in arm wrestling?  How about obscure facts? With a mixture of dice games, physical challenges, artistic creations, and trivia questions that will exercise both sides of your brain, you'll see why DICEcapades isn’t just the luck of the dice - it’s the smarts of the roller!

Need more cards?  No problem, get the DICEcapades Expansion Packs. Each expansion pack comes with 45 new cars to boost your Dicecapades! 2nd Edition game.

Expansion pack 2 is movies themed and includes 45 more cards for Dicecapades! 2nd Edition game.
