-Arachne, Campe, Colchidian Dragon, Dragon of Thebes, Echidna, Graeae, Griffon, Lycaon, Nemean Lion, Orion, Phoenix, Python, Stheno the Gorgon, Tityos

1 dice bag, 2 Corinthian Skeleton tiles for Conan (packaging says 1)

boardgame: Ricky Dillon's Twisted Truth or Dare
description:
Social media influencer, Ricky Dillon creates his first ever card game in Twisted Truth Or Dare.

Written and created by Ricky, the game includes 300 unique playing cards - 150 Twisted, 75 Truth, 75 Dare.

Separate your cards into 3 decks: Twisted Cards, Truth Cards, and Dare Cards

1. The card-puller picks a Twisted Card and identifies the specific person in the group it most likely represents.

2. Once that most likely person is selected, he/she pulls a Truth or Dare Card and completes the card.

3. Once the most likely person completes the Truth or Dare, they pick a new Twisted Card and the round starts over.

4. The person to have 10 Twisted Cards loses. Whoever has the fewest Twisted Cards wins.

One month ago, Star-ship union find an asteroid with many historical remains near the SiTan galaxy. One after another, treasure-hunters arrive this ruin star and search the treasure by the loading of symbols on remains' wall.
Now, it's time for you to show up. Players take a role of treasure-hunters. following the guide of map and racing to the finish first.
In the Ruinstar, draft randomly map-cards forming the race track, namely the map in current game. Also, players will take their own meeples and put them on the origin of map. During the game, players draft cards on the center and play them from hands on the front of themselves, acting as a their own exploration-cards colunm. Then, that player can get some symbols on the cards played by him and may use these symbols for other actions,such as destroy others' exploration-cards column or extra advance his/her meeple.
It's the most important and interesting that how to prevent others' plan and use your own symbols for advance on the map with Combos.

My Mighty Crystals is a colorful family board game, with lovely 3D miniatures characters. It's a race for 2-4 players along a path on which you will collect mighty crystals and meet funny creatures that you can control with your crystals. Each player can collect a maximum of three crystals, and more crystals are offered at the end of the race depending on the order of arrival. The player who owns the most crystals wins.

Populations is an insanely addictive game about primitive fantasy races and their struggles to sort out who's land is who's!

Throughout the game, players will explore a vast and mysterious world in order to find useful resources and victory points. The various resources (i.e. stone, wood, textile) are used to grow population, build fantastic castles, and discover useful technologies.

Specifically, on a players turn, they may spend action points to increase and move their population around the map, and to build castles to secure lands. Additionally, players may always explore new lands by drawing new land tiles and adding them to the known world!

Depending on the number of players, a victory point goal is set at the beginning of the game. Once a player has achieved the preset goal on their turn, they win the game. Likewise, if a player eliminates all other players from the game, then they stand victorious!

boardgame: Thailand (fan expansion for Ticket to Ride)
description:
This map is an unofficial fan made expansion. Symbols & Graphics Copyright © 2004-2015 Days of Wonder, Inc. Produced with permission of Days of Wonder, for non-commercial use only.

This follows the principles of the basic Ticket to Ride game on a special board with special cards and rules. The object of the game is to gain the most points by travelling various routes and outwitting other players. You will need the train pieces and the train cards from either the US or Europe base games (or you can buy them from the Days of Wonder shop.

There is an extra set of Tourist cards for the cities, each with a value to be earned by visiting that city. These cards are dealt/picked up alongwith tickets in the same quantity and with the same 'keep/dispose' rules.

There are also choice cards which may help or hinder you. The board does not have any grey tracks or parallel tracks - but there are some options in the choice pack.

The 9 bonuses give you extra points.

There are many new options in the rules: exchange all your cards at the start but keep all the new ones: discard 5 cards and pick up 5 new ones, as your turn: earn extra points: and many more. You can also call time when your wagon stock reaches 3, if you want, giving you the advantage of stopping other players completing tickets.

Quick and agile, the Axis Light Panzer Walker "Heinrich," is a versatile addition to any mission. Its 2 cm FlakVierling gun is more than a match for infantry and air support alike, and its superior reactive fire means it will never be caught off guard.
The “Heinrich” Tropical is presented here in its Babylon pattern camouflage, as observed around Mesopotamia circa 1947. This model is a recent one, built with the new and improved turret with advanced optics designed to help target low flying aircraft.

The PanzerSpähLäufer I-D, nicknamed "Hermann", is an effective anti-infantry and anti-tank light walker. Its two massive Laser-Kanone produces devastatingly powerful rays, capable of crippling enemy tanks in a single shot. The vehicle is presented here in its Babylon pattern camouflage, as observed around Mesopotamia circa 1947.

Even though it has some of the most modern planes on the planet, the Axis has learned the hard way that sheer weight of numbers will usually grant the air forces of the Allies and SSU air superiority. So for any Axis field commander, the constant need for ground vehicles with anti-aircraft capabilities is undeniable.

A new version of the famed Luther chassis was developed for the NDAK to deal with this necessity: the Leopold.

All stories must end. Take the stage one last time as gods battle giants, immortal heroes join your cause, and worlds shift underfoot. Make this tale one for the ages.

Norsaga: Odds and Endings is a 50-card expansion for the puzzle battle card game Norsaga. It introduces 3 new decks of cards: heirlooms and immortals provide permanent bonuses once unlocked, while realms change the fundamental rules of the game. It also includes additional hero cards and updated 2nd Edition basic embellishments which accelerate the flow of the game.

You can integrate any or all of this content with Norsaga and Norsaga: Into the Myths for an endless amount of variety in your games.

The tropical version of this walker is very common in the Axis army today. The Loth can provide invaluable services on the battlefield, in support of other walkers or heavy infantry. Thanks to its improved open chassis, this walker can now be found all over the world, anywhere the Axis fights. Perfectly suited to desert environments, it's now a NDAK staple.

The Bin'Fa Enhancement Set is an aesthetic upgrade for the Bin'Fa: The Tao of War Abstract Strategy War Game.  It includes 6 sets of Army Unit Decals, using artwork from the flags on the box cover, 6 Tetrahedron Terrain Markers (to replace the black checker pieces), 6 Black Vortex Triangles (to replace the white checker pieces), and the weather die, which adds an exciting new rule element to game play.
4 player race game flying around a map of the world. The objective is to fly your plane between connected cities on a map of the world being first to the final stop.

This takes the elements of a JRPG and puts them into an epic co-op tabletop adventure game. Players explore on a mini-map, grinding away as they search for the set-piece encounters - large battles that make or break the players.

The game is set up in Acts, with each act being represented as a box of map tiles and encounters. Each act is meant to be played in a single sitting (1.5-2 hours), and then strung together into an epic 3-act story, concluding with a massive boss fight. That boss is defined by the earlier 3-acts and since the game features lots of different acts, there is lots of replay value.

During the game, players use a bag of tokens that represents their characters "stats." When players use various abilities, instead of rolling dice to check for success, they will draw tokens from their bag and check the various icons and runes to see if they succeed. As players level-up, they will swap in ever-more-impressive tokens.

These token bags are also used by the heroes' enemies. Get set on fire? Put a fire token in your bag and you'll be on-fire until you draw it out. Get poisoned? Put 3 Poison tokens into your bag taking damage each time one comes out until you find a heal poison potion, etc...

To win the game, players must battle through 3 acts and then a mega boss.

Soldier Ball is a tabletop skill and strategy game that can be learned in 60 seconds and enjoyed for hours.

How to Play:

Two dice are rolled to determine the number of soldiers and barricades for the game. Players are then allowed 2 minutes to arrange their home base as they choose using the determined number of soldiers and barricades. Players then take turns rolling the battle ball into the opposing army in an attempt to knock soldiers down. When a soldier is knocked down, it is removed from play. The player with the last soldier standing wins the game.

Description from the designer:

The year is 1492. It is Sunday. The old pope is dead and now the race is on for the election of the new pope. You’re one of the hopefuls in this election, however, not everyone is on your side… yet. But don’t worry, they’ll come around once you show them a little financial appreciation. After all, everyone has a vote, and everyone can be bought. And once the new pope is elected, the richest player wins—just like in real life.

The goal in Habeamus is to amass more money than the other players, while electing a new pope. Every player has one vote, and there are three non-player cardinals with a vote each as well. During their turns, players can place bribes face-down on the non-player cardinals in the middle of the playing field. These cardinals will vote for whomever promises the largest bribe when the vote starts.
Each player also has a vote that they can use for themselves, or sell to other players. Every player can make hidden or open bribe offers to other players. If, during the vote count, a player accepts an offer by another player, that bribe goes into their account. However, if they do not accept the offer, the player making the offer gets the bribe into their account. In this way, players will have to balance their offers to other players and make sure they themselves sell their vote for a good price. To shake things up a bit more, a player can increase the papal salary, or play actions to change the bribes already on offer.
Once all cards have been played, the votes are counted. If a pope is elected, he will receive a salary bonus into their account. Then, every player counts their money and the richest player wins.

A snowbound small village. It quietly approaches and sneaks into the innermost part of a villager's heart. When the night has come and everyone else is sleeping, it quietly wakes up. The brutal murderer wanders around the village and preys on honest villagers. Who is the heinous monster?

Rocca Monster is a hidden role/social deduction game for 3-6 players. Each player is dealt three villagers which are placed on the table in front of them. One of the villagers in the game is secretly the monster. All players close their eyes and count to ten, during which time the player with the monster card places a six sided die on a villager of their choice. After the count is finished all players open their eyes and see who was attacked in the night. That player rolls the die, and on a roll of 1-4, the villager on whom the die was placed is killed. On a roll of 5 or 6, the villager survives the attack. After the murder (or attempted murder) the players discuss who they think the monster is, and then use their voting cards to vote for which villager they want to accuse of being the monster. Once a single villager has the majority of votes their card is turned over and it is revealed whether they were the monster or not. If they were the monster then the other players win! If they were not the monster then their card is removed from the game and play continues. Another night attack occurs, followed by another round of discussion and voting. This cycle continues until either the monster successfully kills half the villagers (at which point the monster player wins the game), or until the players successfully identify the monster (at which point the monster player loses and everyone else wins).

Frog Family Fun-Pack is an educational game for children, with various math and reading modules, all using the same rules and board.  Players take turns choosing a card and attempting to answer the question on the card.  If the answer is correct, they move their pawn on a track the number or spaces printed on the back of the card.  If the answer is wrong, the card is placed back in the middle of the stack.  Extra turn spaces and shortcuts also appear on the track.  The first player to reach the end wins.

The PanzerKampfLäufer IV-F “JagdLoki” is the latest Flak (anti-aircraft) walker in service in the Axis army. The very first vehicles are currently being field tested by the Neue Deutsche Afrikakorps Panzer units. Should these tests be successful (and they seem to be) the "JagdLoki" might soon be found all over the planet. Mounting four 3,7 cm guns this walker can send a hail of explosive shells in the air, literally shredding any airplane to pieces. The vehicle is presented here in its Babylon pattern camouflage, as observed around Mesopotamia circa 1947.

The JagdWotan is one of the deadliest walker on the planet. The latest version is armed with four brand new laser guns that can punch through anything the SSU or the Allies have. This walker will serve the Axis Army for many years to come. It is presented here in its Babylon Camo Pattern, as used in the Middle-East, circa Summer 1947.

The Sonderkraftfahrzeug 202/3 schwer Schützenpanzerläufer VI-G Stummel (Special Purpose Vehicle 203/3, Heavy Infantry Fighting Vehicle VI-G Stummel) is the latest evolution in Axis troop transport. Replacing the famed Sd.Kfz. 251/9 half-track, with which it shared the nickname ‘Stummel’ (Stump) for its short gun, this new vehicle takes it all to the next level by adding walker technology and the capability to carry a full section of infantry as well as a powerful gun. It can carry six soldiers inside its passenger compartment and another six between the hull and the Schürzen armored skirts.

The value of mechanized infantry has been proven numerous times since the war started. It is even more valuable in the desert environment in which the Neue Deutsches Afrikakorps fights - no one wants to cross a desert on foot!

Axis field Commanders are often outnumbered but never outgunned. The whole Axis military industry and engineers make sure that their soldiers have the best vehicles when in battle. Their latest creation is the already dreaded “KönigsLothar”. With its very impressive dual triple Nebelwerfer rocket launcher, this walker can destroy anything in mere seconds. This weapon has proved its usefulness for years now. And with such huge machines capable of carrying that many, it's going to be efficient for many more years: for the Axis, these weapons will win the war. The vehicle is presented here in its Babylon pattern camouflage, as observed around Mesopotamia circa 1947.

New spies arrive to town and with new abilities. Besides Estoril, players will also fight in Lisbon, where they will find new ways to create the best network of spies.

City of Spies: Double Agent will offer players:


     the ability to add a fifth player.
     7 new characters with a new nationality.
     a fantastic new ability [double agent] who will allow new strategies.
     2 new mission tiles.
     2 new locations boards (Lisbon) with new special rules.

boardgame: Team Yankee: Volksarmee – East Germans in World War III
description:
Volksarmee is a 32-page hardbook expansion to the Team Yankee tabletop miniatures wargame introducing the East Germans as the first non-Soviet Warsaw Pact army lists. The new army lists include a T-72M Panzer Bataillon, T-55 AM2 Panzer Bataillon, BMP Mot-Schützen Bataillon, and BTR-60 Mot-Schützen Bataillon.

While the East Germans use much of the same equipment as their Soviet comrades, there are some differences. The expansion introduces new equipment including the T-72M tank, T-55 AM2 tank, BTR-60 IFV, SA-9 Gaskin SAM, and Spandrel AT. The new equipment, as well as East German infantry, are being released as miniatures box sets with East German and Soviet data cards as appropriate.  East German data cards for existing Soviet miniatures are included with the expansion.

The book also includes a fictional history of the East German units during WWIII, painting guides, and scenarios. It can be purchased as a physical hardback book or as a digital download in the Flames of War Digital app for Android and IOS systems.  A copy of the Team Yankee rule book is required to play the game.

Big Hero 6 is a "6 in 1" game - it has 32 cards which are used in six different games, based on classics children's card games.


     Action Game - Win by getting ride of your cards as soon as possible.
     Action Game Snap - Win by getting all the cards.
     Pairs Game - Win by collecting the most pairs.
     Double Pairs Game - Win by collecting the most double pairs.
     Donkey Game - Win by not having the "Donkey" card in your hand at the end of the game.
     Happy Families Game - Collect the most Happy Families.


First published as part of a collection of promotional card games for Morrison's Supermarkets in the UK.

Contents:
32 Playing cards
2 Rules cards

Balance game by Lidl, a wooden dexterity game were you stack wooden cilinders on a tilting turtle.

A big wooden turtle lays on its back forming an unstable half moon shaped base the feet kerp the cilinders from rolling of. You roll one die or both dice and add a cilinder of the rolled color on the turtle. If the cilinders drop you lose. A bit like animal upon animal but without cute animals and a rocking base.

boardgame: Blood Bowl (2016 Edition): Blitzmania Special Play Promo Cards
description:
A set of 5 limited edition Special Play cards provided during the Feb-March 2017 Blitzmania League.


     Heel Turn, 
     It's all to play for! 
     They think its all Ogre?!
     Blitzmania Mania!
     Pampered Players!

The main point of this game is to level up fast through game experience. In this fantasy-themed game, your Hero can go up 21 levels in a single game session!

Players customize the powers of their heroes though rapid leveling up — with every passing round, their heroes gain experience and grow in power.

Heroes use abilities, tons of items, skills, and spells against enemy units in order to defend the Realm and find the Ancient Artifact before it's too late!

Each player will develop their own Hero during the game. .


     There are also extra cards for a Players VS Player version of the game.


During the game:
Players may recruit new characters, take control of mines (which produce gold and resources for the Cities), and most importantly, defeat the enemy units which occupy the map and Dungeon.

Game-play:
On your turn, your Hero (Attack) rolls one dice (or more in higher Level) AND additional black dice for each enemy in your Location. If the number of your Hero rolled dice matches the number of a Item, Skill, Spell Card that your Hero owns, he gets the effect of that Card.
If the number of enemy rolled dice (black) matches the number on enemy Card, it deals damage to your Hero and gets the effect of own Card.

At the most of the time your Hero rolled die will trigger other Players Cards. Yes, a lot of cards may be activated by other Players rolls and the owner of the Card gains the effect.
After combat roll, your Hero may spend Experience points to get new Skill and Spell Cards, and spending gold for new Items (weapons, armors, items, scrolls).

END OF THE GAME
Lose: lost one of the main City OR lost all magic towers/mines OR  then Dungeon population reached the max.
Win:  find the ancient artifact in the Dungeon AND clear the main 3 Cities from the enemies.

Contains

Dice (36x): 

     d4 x15,
     d6 x10,
     d8 x5,
     d10 x3,
     d12 x2,
     d20


Cards (486x)

     Different Hero Cards: 16x
     Different Item cards: 82x + enemy Units rewards 148x
     Different Skill Cards: 156x
     Different Spell Cards: 74x
     Different Attachments Cards: 32x
     Different Enemy Units Cards: 114x 
     Different Locations Cards: 54x
     Different Event Cards: 46x


Large spell books Cards: 20x

boardgame: The Lord of the Rings: The Card Game – The Crossings of Poros
description:
Description from from the publisher:

The Crossings of Poros is the sixth and final Adventure Pack in the Haradrim cycle for The Lord of the Rings: The Card Game.

For several of Middle-earth's heroes, the end to a long and difficult journey through Harad is nearly within sight. These heroes have survived a shipwreck and a desert crossing. They have tamed Mûmakil and allied themselves to a tribe of good-willed Haradrim. They have battled Orcs and Wargs. They have confronted Giant Spiders and wicked Southrons. And they have freed their friends from one of the Dark Lord's dungeons.

But now the friends they have freed are slowing them down. The heroes know the Orcs and Wargs will soon return—and in greater numbers. The Southrons will want their revenge. And there are still many miles to cross before they reach the safety of Gondor.

With its sixty cards (including a new hero and three copies of each new player card), The Crossings of Poros challenges you and your heroes to find your way through the final stretches of Harad. Will you lead your company through the desert? Or will you ask them to weave their way through the rocky hills of the Ephel Dúath? Both paths bring their own dangers, and it falls to you to safeguard your heroes, their allies, and their hope for freedom.
family: Collectible: Living Card Game (Fantasy Flight Games)
Game: The Lord of the Rings – The Card Game (LCG)

description:
A storm has blown through the sleepy, fairy-tail hamlet of Ewetopia, releasing the sheep from their barns and now they’ve all congregated into a massive, mixed-up herd in the middle of the valley! Players step into the role of village farmers, who must use their feisty canine companions to help them herd back the lost flocks.

Ewetopia is charming, fast-paced, easily-to-learn, and hard-to-master. With warm, friendly graphics that have appeal for kids, it will also appeal to older audiences with it’s deceptively tactical game play: A strong denial game, board control, and a playing field that can change with every turn means Ewetopia enjoy plenty of play-time with old and young alike.

Players command their trusty dog around a 5x5 grid filled with tiles containing numerically ranked sheep, pushing rows and columns of them, adding the tiles that get pushed off the grid to their collection. The dogs are very fast and will always move at least 2 squares during their turn, so although they can be made to change direction after their 1st move, they can’t then move back into the square they just came from: Players will need to plan their turn to avoid collecting the wrong tiles or ending up out-of-position.

Opponents’ dogs can’t be pushed and will create obstacles on the board, but if this happens, or if a player wants to create a better move for themselves, fences can be built in the way of the charging sheep, diverting their path in a direction of the player's choice. Since these fences remain in the ground until the end of the game --and act the same for every player-- the result is a maze-like meadow where creative, zigzag moves aren’t just possible, but become the norm.

Adding to the brain-twisting mix are special tiles, including:
Motivating Bones: When collected, can be consumed at any time to “motivate” a dog to move an extra square. Useful to push your dog to get a rare sheep, or to force an opponent to make tough choice if their dog is in a bad position.
Wild Sheep: A mythical breed with technicolor wool, these sheep can count as any rank at the end of the game, making them especially versatile.
Wolves: A snarky, stenchy wolf-pack have sneakily donned sheep costumes in order to blend into the herd. Their own rank, having them at the end of the game will reduce your score by stinking up your other sheep’s wool.
Wood Carts: Contains enough lumber to build an additional fence on the board when traded in, and may be used immediately after being collected.

At the end of the game, points are awarded based on sizes of unbroken, numerical sequences AND sets of same-ranked sheep, allowing players to pursue different paths to victory if their original plans are foiled. Point values scale logarithmically with size, meaning a single big set or sequence will always be better than several smaller ones. After separating their sheep into the largest sets and/or sequences possible, totaling their values --and subtracting points for wolves-- the player with the highest score is the victor!

Jeu de rébus is a French game. Images are shown on a board and you must match them to create as much rebus as you can before the time runs out. A rebus containing 2 syllables counts for 2 points, 3 or more syllables counts for 5 points.

While the game only provides 5 tokens for five players, you may play as much as you want as long as you provide additional tokens. Great party game for everyone!

Official description from Ludik Québec :

20 images sont placées sur une planche de jeu et le sablier est retourné.

Tu dois inscrire le plus de mots possible en te servant des sons représentés par les images retournées.

Plus tu utilises d’images, plus les points marqués seront nombreux.

Mythic Battles: Pantheon is played on different boards each divided into areas. Each of these areas has a terrain type, indicated by an icon. If the ground is empty of 3D terrain, then no rule applies except in special cases (stairs, water, …). If you place a 3D terrain element on an area, the terrain type is then activated, and a small special rule applies.

For example, if you place a tree on a forest zone, the forest rule applies: units in the forest gain a +1 bonus against ranged attacks. If you place a column on a ruin area, then the units in these ruins benefit from the ruin rules and thus gain a +1 bonus against melee attacks. Ruins and forests are examples of the game scenery, and are found on the various boards available in the core box and the expansions.

This airborne operations ready walker is used in the Luftwaffe Ground Divisions. Thanks to its thrusters, it can be parachuted directly on the battlefield, joining the action as soon as the rest of the invasion force arrives. This model is tasked with destroying enemy attack planes and heavy infantry with the help of its powerful long range antiaircraft guns.

The Raupenschlepper Ost mit Laserkanone is a dedicated antitank vehicle in service with the Luftwaffe ground forces. Thanks to its weight, it can be parachuted along with the airborne troops, offering them a much needed fire support during the first phase of an operation. It is presented here in Babylon camouflage pattern, as used in the Middle-East circa 1947.

The Raupenschlepper Ost mit PaK 40 is a dedicated antitank vehicle in service with the Luftwaffe ground forces. Thanks to its weight, it can be parachuted along with the airborne troops, offering them a much needed fire support during the first phase of an operation. It is presented here in Babylon camouflage pattern, as used in the Middle-East circa 1947.

The Raupenschlepper Ost mit FlaK 3 is a dedicated anti-aircraft vehicle in service with the Luftwaffe ground forces. Thanks to its weight, it can be parachuted along with the airborne troops, offering them a much needed fire support during the first phase of an operation. It is presented here in Babylon camouflage pattern, as used in the Middle-East circa 1947.

Description from the publisher:

WHAT IS IT?

Santimanitay: Race to the Stage, is an action-packed board game based around Trinidad and Tobago Carnival. Two to eight players compete to reach their ultimate goal - The Stage. The path to their ultimate goal is filled with challenges; players must avoid Hard Luck spaces, get past Midnight Robbers, take advantage of Warrahoon cards AND display their knowledge of Trinidad and Tobago Carnival. As unpredictable as it is enjoyable, Santimanitay is a must have for anyone interested in Carnival, board games or just having fun.

WHO’S IT FOR?

Santimanitay was conceptualized as a game that captures the spirit and fun of Trinidad and Tobago while preserving important aspects of our history and culture. The game is designed for 2 to 8 players, making it suitable for any situation or occasion, from a quite evening at home to the highlight of a Friday night lime. The ability to choose characters with different attributes can give an advantage to those who may not be as familiar with Trinidad and Tobago’s culture and history, making Santimanitay universally enjoyable. From age 8 to 80, choose a character and start the race!

WHY DO IT?

Known as the greatest show on earth, Trinidad and Tobago Carnival is a festival of beauty, colour, and pageantry. All aspects of Carnival have a rich and storied history; music, costuming, performance. As well-known as the festival may be, not many people know in what year the first Panorama was held, or who was the first winner of the International Soca Monarch competition, or even when the Canboulay Riots occurred in Port of Spain. Santimanitay was developed to help share and preserve this knowledge through engaging game play, memorable characters and challenging questions.

Since the invasion of Normandy by the Allied forces in 1944, the Axis have been in trouble with their opponent's air superiority and the sheer number of planes they can have in the sky at any given time. While they were developing jet powered aircraft, they also worked a lot on ground vehicles that could protect the troops. The Möbelwagen Ausf B (Model B) is the latest generation of these vehicles, built on the venerable Panzer IV chassis. But with six brand new guns. It is presented here in its Babylon Camo Pattern, as used in the Middle-East circa 1947.

boardgame: Dust 1947: Panzerkampfwagen IV Ausf. K – "Panzer IV-K"
description: The Panzer IV has been the workhorse of the Axis Army for years now. Every satellite state, now part of the Bloc, has used it or is still using it. Constantly upgunned and armored, this venerable tank is still very relevant in 1947's battlefields. Every mechanic knows how to work on it, even though it's a very reliable machine. The latest version is armed with a brand new laser gun that can punch through anything the SSU or the Allies have. This tanks will serve the Axis Army for many years to come. It is presented here in its Kondor Camo Pattern, as used in the Middle-East, circa Summer 1947.

As war continues some “older” vehicles have managed to stay on top. Or have not been destroyed yet. It's the case for the still very efficient “Kommandowagen”, officially known as Sd.Kfz 250/3. Its missions have remained the same for a few years now: transport safely the officers of the Panzer Grenadier division and provide covering fire. This vehicle does these two jobs superbly.

The Sd.Kfz. 250/3 “Kommandowagen” is everything you can expect from our Studio release. As usual with our Premium line it is carefully assembled and painted, and everything you see is there: the weathering, the effects, etc.

This is ATS Warfigthing Guide 3 EAGLEBOOK, a 36+ page magazine with a compilation of all known ATS Q & A, formatted and answered by the awesome efforts of Scott 'The Eagle' and Drew 'ATS Guy' Dorman (dec.) A pile of information, fun to read ... just ... so ... much ... info for info junkies!

Also included ATS QUICKSTART BASIC GAME 1 (Ger vs Am1), a complete game for newcomers to ATS with a mini map, three half countersheets of playing pieces and a four sheets of rules. Only one ten-sided die is needed to play a squad-level combat during World War II.

Mottainai: Wutai Mountain, an expansion for Mottainai that you need only one copy of regardless of player count, is a deck of "Om" works. Each such work can gather its own set of helpers, materials, and sales tucked underneath it, and it has a special ability that grants benefits based on those cards. Expect even more exciting and powerful combinations!
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
Smash Up: Sheep is a single faction promotional item that publisher AEG is distributing to people who complete a survey about the Smash Up game line and signed up for the Smash Club newsletter.

The minions in the sheep faction obediently and harmlessly follow other minions around from base to base — then they mercilessly crush their opponents with a wave of minions no one can stop. Baaa!
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
From the game web site:

War to the Death is the second expansion set for Blücher. It is a boxed set of 216 unit cards, covering the armies of the Peninsular Wars and a great many extras. War to the Death is all you need to create Scharnhorst campaigns for the Peninsular theatre or for any game involving the British, Spanish, French, or their allied armies.

From the back of the box:

This set contains unit cards for every unit type in the French, British, and Spanish army lists as well as all the personality cards and the Spanish Guerilla.

boardgame: Sails of Glory: Ship Pack – Santa Ana 1784 / Mejicano 1786
description:
Description from the publisher:

The "Meregildos" were Spanish 1st-rate ships of the line, armed with 112 cannons and with a designed crew of about 1100. They were armed with 30 36-Pounder guns in the lower deck, 32 24-Pounder guns in the middle deck, 32 12-Pounder guns in the upperdeck, and 18 8-Pounder guns in the quarterdeck and the forecastle.

Santa Ana was a 112-gun three-decker ship of the line of the Spanish Navy, designed by Romero Landa. This ship was launched on 28 September 1784 and, after a positive test, became the first of her class. Subsequent Spanish three-deckers would be built following the same plans. At Trafalgar, Santa Ana was the flagship of Alava, suffering 97 killed and 141 wounded, with Alava himself seriously wounded. Initially captured by the British, a squadron succeeded in recapturing her two days later and returning her back to Cadiz. In 1810 she was captured again in Avana by the French and sank in its Arsenal in 1816.

This ship pack can also be used to represent the Spanish Meregildos class "Mejicano 1786".

Description from the publisher:

Sedis is a “generic gaming device”. Much like dominoes or playing cards, Sedis can be used to play and create many different games.

Each Sedis set consists of 60 hexagonal tiles, which can be evenly divided among one to six players. The arrangement of pips and blanks are conducive to patterns and interpretations that can be used to guide each game. These two attributes – the congruent shape of the tiles and the dot patterns unique to each tile – open up a world of board configurations, mechanisms, and ways to play.

Sedis has many different game mechanics which form the backbone of games, including:

Aligning tiles and matching pips/blanks
Rotating tiles
Stacking tiles
“Battle’ mechanics
Trading
Betting/Gambling
Bluffing
Partnering/Communicating
Collaborating
Interpreting pips as numbers
Interpreting pips as Morse code
And more mechanics continued to be discovered

Wild In The Streets is a fast paced miniatures game of straight-edgers, crust punks, goths and more fighting in the streets. With skirmish level, low miniature count gangs and easy to learn rules brutal street brawls can be played in an hour. Unique scenarios like Record Store Day let the gangs fight it out to get the other guys' precious vinyl. Or it could be a simple turf war with punks fighting over control of the local half-pipe. Card-driven alternating activation keeps the combats fresh and cuts out the down time. This game is fast and bloody. With a bottle in one hand and a axe in the other unleash your inner brawler with Wild In The Streets.

True Story is an adult party game where you use cards prompts to inspire stories of extraordinary experiences or legendary lies. One player draw a card, tells a story involving the prompt on the card, and the other players guess whether or not the story is true or false. If the majority of players guess incorrectly the storyteller keeps the card. Play continues until one player has three cards.

Fantasy Fantasy Football is an inventive card game in which you are the Oracle manager of a football team of powerful and gruesome fantasy creatures battling through a season of fantasy football. Go head-to-head against other Oracle managers and battle the elements, manage bye weeks, and use your team's magic powers wisely.

Developed by designers Daryl Andrews and J.R. Honeycutt with art by Rob Lundy, Fantasy Fantasy Football is a roster-building card game in which players must assemble and manage a team through a season full of unexpected challenges using players skills and magical abilities to win games.
family: Organizations: The Game Artisans of Canada

boardgame: My First Memory: What Animals Eat
description:
My First Memory: What Animals Eat is a tile matching game featuring various animals and the foods they eat. Younger children can play it as a simple matching game, finding pairs of identical tiles, while older children might enjoy slightly more difficulty by matching animals to the foods they eat.

Players begin by placing the tiles in rows, picture side down. The first player takes his turn by flipping over two tiles. If they are a match, he keeps the tiles and takes another turn. If the tiles do not match, the player returns them to their face down positions, and the next player takes her turn. Play continues until all of the tiles have been matched and collected. The player with the most tiles wins.

In this 2-player game, players use the cards in their hand to manipulate a function that allows players to trade in their colored cubes for more/different colored cubes in order to complete the goals set out at the beginning of the game. Once a player completes 2 of the 3 goals available, they win!

RULES:
To start the game, shuffle the 5 goal cards and randomly draw 3 of them. Those will be the goals for this game. The other 2 cards can be returned to the box.

Take the 1 Red cube "left" card and the 2 Yellow cubes "right" card from the deck and place them on the table. This is the starting function. Shuffle the rest of the cards and deal three to each player. Then give each player 3 red cubes. The player who was most recently in a math class will go second.

Turns:
Each turn, the current player MUST do the following things
- Play only one card to either side of the equation. 
- Perform the equation if they have the cubes on the left side to trade for the cubes on the right side from the supply. If they do not have the necessary cubes, they MUST take 1 red cube from the supply. 
- Draw one card from the draw pile.
(Play passes to the next player)

As soon as a player meets the requirements for one of the goals, the MUST trade those cubes into the supply and take that goal card. The first player to complete 2 of the 3 goal cards wins the game.

If the draw pile is exhausted, take the top two equation cards off and leave them on the table. Then you can shuffle the remaining cards to reform the draw pile.

If using the function causes a player to trade for a resource that has been exhausted, they do not get that resource and they do not take a red cube.

War has a price and it's time to pay!
War Cash is a quick casual war game where players use Cash Cards to fund battle actions for fighting your opponents or to help you survive.

A 2 - 4 player game where all 12 Cash Cards are shuffled and players draw one Cash Card and bank or fully pay an action.

Any player can fully pay an action and use it - so the player needs to think - should they pay?

This airborne operations ready walker is used in the Luftwaffe Ground Divisions. Thanks to its thrusters, it can be parachuted directly on the battlefield, joining the action as soon as the rest of the invasion force arrives. This model is tasked with destroying enemy strong-points and heavy armor, with the help of its powerful short range rockets.

In this pirate game you have to find the chest which is hiding Jack Pott's treasure and after getting it be the first to make it to the canoe to win the game. For playing the game you need a free app that guided you through the whole game.

When it's your turn you roll the dice within the app and walk with your pirate figure the eyes number on a symbol on the game board. Depending on what figure your move ends you interact with the app which tells you what happens next or what you have to do. The game board shows a pirate ship which is devided in different parts. In each part is a treasure chest which maybe hides the treasure of Jack Pott.

The app uses augmented reality to scan the game board and it shows animations of the different characters which guided through the game

Description from the publisher:

Ready Set Train! is a card game which consists of several exercises which combined will give you a workout (and a lot of fun!) while trying to beat your opponents. Draw cards, finish your exercises and complete your secret mission. But don’t forget to keep an eye out for your opponents. You can use special cards to help you finish your secret mission before your opponents do or draw cards to block them from completing theirs. Don’t forget to keep in mind you might need their help at some point in this game!

boardgame: Ember: Light and Dark – Prismatic Pack
description:
LIGHT AND DARK is a pair of expansions that supercharge your game with twenty new cards across two exciting packs - each packed with game-changing mechanics. There's a lot to deal with in Light and Dark!

The Prismatic Pack boosts your creatures' abilities and adds a whole new collection of magical monsters to Conjure. Prismatic cards are creatures of divine light; each card has two magical abilities.

The Prismatic Pack contains the cards of light, the healers and protectors of the Emberverse. The cards are all designed to protect play, to mitigate card effects and card loss. All the standard cards have two powers - a first in the Ember universe.

Three of the Prismatic cards provide 0 VP (Victory Points) on their own, but their VP are instead dependent on the cards you have in play.

Then you have Iridia, the Elemental Prismatic Dragon, whose powers focus on protection, of increasing your powers, and defending your forces against the powers of the Spectral. Each Elemental Dragon has an legendary power which can be used if you control all three parts of that dragon, in this case, Irridia's ability allows you to give a creature you control +1 VP whenever any other player modifies a creature with a +1 VP or -1VP token. Powerful!

Contains:
6 Creature cards
1 Artifact card
1 Three-part Dragon

boardgame: Ember: Light and Dark – Spectral Pack
description:
LIGHT AND DARK is a pair of expansions that supercharge your game with twenty new cards across two exciting packs - each packed with game-changing mechanics. There's a lot to deal with in Light and Dark!

The Spectral Pack introduces ten creature cards that curse and hinder your enemies with dark magic. Conjure these creatures and steal the Victory Points for yourself, while the cards curse your enemies with powers that range from stripping the cards in their hand to making their creatures weaker. Beware, for what you reap so may you sew, for the owner can choose to Conjure the creature in their turn and send it right back at you!

Spectral cards are creatures of shadow and gloom. They are cursed and bring only darkness to whoever controls them...

The Spectral cards are the powers of evil, of darkness and malevolence. These ten cards will give you the power to strip your opponents of their abilities, to force players to discard valuable cards, and to reduce the VP of their own creatures.

And then you have Sinistra, the Elemental Dragon God of Shadow. Her trio of powers are alone formidable, but when combined, she can affect all opponents with her powers every turn. Now that's nasty! Sinistra is definitely a game changer...

Contains:
6 Creature cards
1 Artifact card
1 Three-part Dragon

SanremoN is a collectible card game. The deck consists of 40 downloadable cards. It celebrates 67 years of events and characters of Sanremo's Festival, setting them in a fantasy universe.

(The Sanremo Music Festival, or "Festival della canzone italiana di Sanremo", is a popular Italian song contest and awards ceremony. It is held annually in the city of Sanremo, Liguria, Italy.)

PURPOSE OF THE GAME: Being able to bring your opponent's life points to 0

HOW TO PLAY

It is played exclusively in two (1 Vs 1) and with a single deck of cards. The two players (Challenger A and Challenger B), after having shuffled the deck, draw 5 cards each.

The remaining cards remain available to Challengers who at the end of each round of play will draw one or more cards, depending on how many will they play, so you always have 5 cards. Each Challenger has 5000 Life Points to start.

In the first round, the Challenger A plays his first card, chosen among the 5 in their possession. The Challenger A is in the process attack, then the ATK (Attack) points of the card played will be those to be taken into account. The Opponent B, vice versa, is located in defense phase, and then play one of 5 cards in his hands, considering the DEF (Defense) points associated with it. If the ATK points of the opponent A will exceed DEF points of the opponent B, the Challenger to win the turn and the difference between ATK of A and B will be the DEF points to be subtracted to the Opponent's Life Points B. If the ATK points are equal to or lower than DEF points opponent, no Life Point will be subtracted from the Challenger B.

At the end of the round, played cards are collected in a new deck (the discard pile), and the two Challengers will fish from the draw pile as many cards as they were played before moving in round next. He will fish for the first Challenger will play in the Attack phase (in our case, it is for the Challenger B).

This continues round after round, until the the deck runs out. Then all the cards collected in the discard pile will be reshuffled and put back into play. Repeat for 5 times at most.

The winner will be the one who will manage to bring the Life Points of the opponent to 0, or the one who at the end of the 5th phase of the game has higher Life Points.

A party game where players divide into 2 teams and give clues to help their teammates guess the categories on the cards, similar to the game show $25,000 Pyramid.

Each team starts with 1 minute on its timer. Players give clues to their teammates to help them guess the category.

Sample categories are "Things people do while sleeping" or "Ice cream toppings". For example, clues such as "Fire Engine", "Apple", and "Stop Sign" would lead to the category, "Things that are red".

Play continues until a team's time runs out. Then the next team takes its turn.  The team that successfully guessed more categories in a round moves its game piece one space on the game board. The first team to win 4 rounds wins the game.

Includes:
280 Cards/560 Categories
2 Game Pieces
Game Board
2 Sand Timers/Free App Timer
Rules

Smash Up: All Stars is a single faction that consists of previously released minions and actions from other Smash Up sets. This deck is included in the Smash Up: All-Stars Event Kit as a prize for players who participate in games and tournaments run by retail outlets that use this kit.

Smash Up: All Stars (as well as Geek and Sundry Geeks ("The Geeks")) faction will also be included in the Smash Up: The Bigger Geekier Box.
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
"For our friend and her new beginning..."

Balloon is a colorful game dedicated to friendship. Balloon features a color-friendly Cards and peaceful feelings with a sky background and lots of balloon!

Balloon has 2 modes of gameplay; party mode and couple mode. Party Mode is the easier and more fun mode of the game, where players need to make a balloon party merrier by adding confetti (flip Balloon Cards from clear side to confetti side) and by doing so they also sabotaging other players in a fun way. Meanwhile, in Couple Mode, players are required to use strategy to make sure they can fill their board faster or get higher Victory Points.

On both Party Mode and Couple Mode, winners are determined by most Victory Points, but how they acquire it is different. On Party Mode, Victory Points are distributed at random but on Couple Mode are based on how they perform on their player board.

There are one unique way to end the game, each time a player trade their resource to a Balloon Card, one resource has to be discarded, if there are 5 cards discarded with same color, the game immediately ends and Victory Points counted!

To impress the king, players in Kwintessens try to be the best alchemist.

In ancient times, all elements were thought to be built of fire, water, earth and water, which were represented by triangles. In the game, each player tries to outwit the others by building four pure elements, i.e., triangles surrounded by triangles of the same element. To do so, the players can and probably will use the triangles played by other players.

Timing is essential in this quick, tactical game, especially since other players can break down a previously built pure element by mutating one of its parts.

One essential part of the game is the "kwintessens" triangle, which can take any shape the players want.

boardgame: Pathfinder Adventure Card Game: Mummy's Mask – "Dance of the Dead" Promo Card
description:
Dance of the Dead
Type: Barrier

Traits: Trigger, Cache, Curse, Magic

Check to Defeat: Dexterity / Charisma 10

Powers:
When you examine this card, shuffle a random monster that has the Undead trait from the box into each open location deck.

If defeated, banish this barrier or display it next to the scenario. While displayed, after you defeat a monster that has the Undead trait on your exploration, shuffle an item from the box into a random open location deck, then you may explore your location.
family: Series: Pathfinder Adventure Card Game (Paizo Publishing)

description:
In the 1990s, a small band of freedom fighting hackers team up to take down an evil corporation. They meet anonymously over computer networks to conceal their identities, but there's a problem -- one of them might be a corporate spy!

Dark Web is a game of hidden player roles and pushing your luck for 3-6 players, comprised of 128 cards. Search the servers for precious data files to incriminate the Moore-Ally-Good Corporation, but be careful who you trust as you may not all be on the same side.

The goal of the game is to download enough data files after 4 rounds to expose the corporation for what it is. On your turn you may play any action scripts in your hand, then decide if you want to bank your points or stay in and draw a card. But be wary of drawing a lock, as too many locks will cause your connection to be shut down and all the data you've found to be lost.

For IT players, do whatever it takes to stop the hackers. But be careful of revealing yourself too early, as once you do the hackers can work around you!

England, 1940. Claire and her little brother James flee the bombing of London to stay in their late grandparents' abandoned mansion in the countryside. During their first night in the house Claire wakes to find James missing. As she searches for him she begins to realize the house is haunted, but luckily the spirits are not all bent on doing harm. The ghosts of her ancestors are there to whisper into her ear the dangers that lie ahead so she that may avoid them and find James.

House of Whispers is a cooperative game in which one person plays Claire and the rest play the friendly spirits. As a spirit, communicate what information you can to Claire. As Claire, you must make the right decisions to avoid the terrors of the house and save your little brother!

At the beginning of the game all players choose one word. The spirits communicate to Claire with facial expressions and by saying their word in such a way to communicate any information they can about the cards they draw. For example, if your word is "beware" and you draw a zombie, you might say "beeewwaarrre" in a slow grating voice while rolling your eyes and fixing a dead stare on your face. Claire may then decide if she wants to run from this threat or push on and try to deal with it as she searches for James.

Search the house for clues as to James' whereabouts and discover items that may help you locate him. You must save James before 8 rounds are up, otherwise he will be lost to the house forever.

Gran juego de estrategia y conquista feudal.

A game of strategy and feudal conquest.
Roll and move to buy properties and build castles. You can buy castles from other players buying battle cards. If you lose your last coin, you are out of the game. The last player with money is the winner.

This game was crowdfunded but never delivered to backers.


A cooperative game based on the popular Korean animation series.

In There She Is!!, you help Doki (the bunny) and Nabi (the cat) with theri relationship to overcome a series of events in a world where interracial relationships are taboo.

The game is played in a series of rounds determined by the number of players. These players must find the ways to achieve the goals of every episode, while solving the events that will often appear.

To solve these episodes and events, players have the option to use their different abilities or character's skills, to search the right card through a deck of more than 100 object cards, and earn positive tokens while trying to avoid the negative ones.

There are several ways to lose, but only one way to win: solve all five episodes, with custom events for each one!

-description from the publisher:
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
From publisher site:

Announcing four new tracks for Grand Prix!

Every race track in the Thunderverse has a historical counterpart. Some are more famous and well known than others. The four newest tracks, specifically designed to enhance Grand Prix, are no different. Adding to the 13 previous series tracks, the Grand Prix Track Pack provides four new tracks based on the most famous F1 courses in the world; Silverstone, Monza, Monaco and Spa-Francorchamps. Each provides new challenges for racers.

Three of the tracks are designed with just Grand Prix in mind. Monza utilizes the historic oval to create three unique experiences, the oval, the road course, and the monster combined course requiring a trip through the oval after completing a circuit of the road course. Each track can be used for both Grand Prix and Thunder Alley.
family: Series: Thunder Alley/Grand Prix engine (GMT Games)

description:
CONTENTS OF THE BOX: 1 rulebook, 86 cards, 6 help cards, 1 Purple 4-sided dice and 1 score papers.

Coles is a fun card game aimed specifically for children for his easy subject and at the same time makes them working memory and the development of the strategy.

It is easy to play, since every player will have to go down letters by order of turn one by one from his hand to the table for it linking types of cabbages in groups. Everything is seemingly easy but we will have to consider some rules at the end of the round: the sum of odd of the same type of cabbage groups not scoring, only scoring the pairs group (as this group of two, four, six...). If you manage to collect one of each type you will have an extra points. On the other hand, there are cards that have a skill modifier since I could throw a die and exchange that number by another rival cards to avoid or undo it groups, and we add them to our convenience, other letters of discard and others bid on the table to the fastest pinch it with your hand over.

The mechanics is go down cards of different types of cabbage from the hand to the table turns and try to form peer groups or quintets (odd no total points).

There are some cards that have a special ability:

"Coles de Bruselas" (Brussels sprouts) --> allow Brussels sprouts that have bids in the center of the table.

"Col blanca" (White cabbage) --> finally the extra cards are double points.

"Repollo" (Cabbage) --> when you throw a card, you can throw another card.

"Lombarda" (Red cabbage) --> it to table you can launch a 4-sided die indicating the number of cards to exchange with another rival.

"Coliflor" (Cauliflower)  --> it's the key for the lombarda ability.

"Col podrida" (Rotten cabbage) --> allows to discard cards to remove points to rivals.

"Comodín" (Joker card) --> it's copy other cabbage.

At the end there will be a count of points noted in a book and win that most points obtained in 2 rounds of play.

ORIGINAL LANGUAGE  (SPANISH):

CONTENIDO DE LA CAJA: 1 reglamento, 86 cartas, 6 cartas de ayuda, 1 dado morado de 4 caras y unas hojas de puntuación.

Coles es un divertido juego de cartas dirigido especialmente para los niños por su fácil temática y al mismo tiempo les hace memorizar y desarrollar estrategias.

Es fácil de jugar, ya que cada jugador tendrá que bajar cartas por orden de turno uno por uno de su mano a la mesa para así ligar a los tipos de coles en grupos. Todo es aparentemente fácil pero tenemos que tener en cuenta algunas reglas al final de la ronda: la suma de impares del mismo tipo de grupos de col no puntúa y los pares sí (grupo de dos, cuatro, seis...). Si se consigue un tipo de cada col positiva, que son cinco, se obtendrá una bonificación de puntos extra. Por otro lado, hay cartas que tienen un modificador de habilidad ya que permiten lanzar un dado y cambiar un número de cartas con otro rival hasta un máximo según el número que salga en el dado para evitar o deshacerse de grupos, y añadimos a nuestra conveniencia, otras cartas permiten hacer a los demás descartes y otras permiten pujas sobre la mesa según se ponga antes que los demás la mano rápidamente sobre la carta.

La mecánica es ir bajando cartas  de diferentes tipos de col de la mano a la mesa y tratar de formar grupos de pares o quintetos (los grupos impares no suman). Hay algunas cartas que tienen una habilidad especial:

"Coles de Bruselas" --> permiten pujar cartas en el centro de la mesa.

"Col Blanca" --> al final de la partida, las cortas sobrantes pares tienen valor doble.

"Repollo" --> al bajar un repollo puedes bajar otra carta de tu mano a la mesa.

"Lombarda" --> cumpliendo un requisito (tener al menos dos parejas de tipos de col diferente y una de ellas ha de ser de coliflor) permite lanzar el dado de 4 caras morado, indicando el número máximo de cartas que se pueden intercambiar con otro rival.

"Coliflor" --> es la llave para activar la habilidad de la lombarda.

"Col podrida" --> permite descartarle cartas a los rivales para romperles sus agrupaciones y estrategias, permitiendo quitarles puntos.

"Comodín" --> copia la habilidad de otra col.

Al final habrá un recuento de puntos en una libreta de puntuación y ganará el que más puntos haya conseguido en 2 rondas de juego.

boardgame: Dice Trek: A Space Battle in a Tin
description:
To boldly roll where no dice have rolled before.

Dice Trek: A Space Battle in a Tin is light and fast 2 player duel, in which you will be trying to be 'last ship floating'. You will collect power and damage your opponent's mother ship by pushing and pulling two custom dice on a five card space battlefield, by deploying cards with units of various strength and influencing both dice simultaneously most of the time.

Designed for 2016 Mint Tin Design Contest

Best Overall Design - 8th
Most Innovative Mechanic - 9th
Best Theme - 8th
Best Artwork & Graphic Design - 3rd

Guest Judge: Caroline Berg - Top 3 Picks
2nd pick
  "Dice Trek: A Space Battle in a Tin, while I'm not a person for two-player games, I couldn't resist the theme. The game is more than just Star Trek: The Dice Game - it mixes in many Sci-Fi tropes, creating a feeling of nostalgic Sci-Fi action. The rulebook is well done, covering all the information the players need, and the graphic design is top notch."

Mowgli is a cooperative tile game based on the characters of the immortal novel The Jungle Book of Rudyard Kipling. It has a cooperative playing mode and a competitive mode.

The game includes different scenarios, inspired of different episodes of the tale. Every player represents one of the main characters of the tale: Baloo, Bagueraa, Kaa or Akela and will try to help Mowgli to hunt, to escape from the Lost City or to fulfill his destiny, banishing the fearsome Shere Khan from the jungle.

The game is based on the principles of the tile games. We will create a template of tiles on the table, which simulate to be the jungle. The players will move their animals by turns, trying to avoid the dangers of the wilderness, to obtain enough food or to move Mowgli to a safe location.

But every turn Shere Khan will be stalking and getting closer to his prey, Mowgli.

"A thrilling dice game for any number of intrepid players! You'll need a dice (sic) and a counter for each gamester - and nerves of steel as you encounter the arch-villains of Gotham! Are you tough and lucky enough to reach the Batman at Wayne Manor?"

A simple dice game. Move along the trach and obey the special squares should you land on them.

Published in the British book called Batman Official Annual 1981.

The Little War is a wargame for two players exploring the short but spirited border conflict between Slovakia and Hungary in March, 1939. The action begins on March 23, 1939. The time covered by an entire game may represent 8-12 days, depending on events within the game.

The game lasts 7 turns. In general terms, both players will draw a number of cards from a deck of ordinary playing cards, then will play them alternately one by one to conduct different activities with their counters on the map.

Each game comes with 30 counters, an area-movement map of a portion of eastern Slovakia, and short rules. Players will also need a deck of ordinary playing cards (with 2 Jokers) and several six-sided dice.

Published by Hollandspiele in 2017 as part of a two-game package, together with Ukrainian Crisis: Ukrainian Crisis & The Little War. The games within the box use separate components, and only share a rulebook.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description: On the table there are delicious-looking cupcakes. Players roll two special dice  with colors on them instead of numbers. The dice rolls allow players to grab cupcakes of the colors rolled. The player who collects the most muffins of a certain set (set collection) wins.

Drop the Puck is a hockey themed board game. Drop the puck has turn based game play. 
On a players turn they take 2 actions with 2 different team members. The turn actions let players move, pass, shoot, steal the puck and perform other hockey related actions. 
The shooting and passing require a player to roll a die and beat a number depending on how far away the shot or pass is and how many opponents are trying to block it. Dice are also used to try and block shots, fight, and attempt penalties. Other actions involve moving team member figures on the board. Players also have cards to play that enhance the actions they take on their turn. After taking their turn the player advances their game clock token to keep track of game length.
After 3 periods of play the player who scored the most goals wins.

Code of Princess Card Game is based on the 3DS console game software of the same name. In this game, each player assumes role of one of characters with various skills and try to get more VPs than other players by winning round battles for 5 rounds.

In a round each player bids his 5 action cubes divided on 4 stats (Mind/Order of skill, chrisma/order of supporter, order of battle, and attack points) behind cover. Then each player builds up his/her power by choosing a pair of skill cards and a supporter card by the order of bids for each stat. And then the round battle comes and each player plays skill cards and earns VPs by its outcome. After a total of five rounds, the player with the most VPs wins the game.

Welcome to Panzer Orders, a World War 2 card game. The game is fast and fun to play and takes around 30 mins. The game includes rules for a two player game and separate rules for the solitaire game.

The game contains two decks of cards - one deck for the Allies and one for the Germans. All of the cards have completely unique artwork with no duplicates. Each card has a points value based on the card characteristics and special rules. The players agree a point based battle and then build their decks, spending points on specific cards.

Each player must form three columns of any mixture of vehicles, tanks, or infantry and then attack the opponents columns. The first player to defeat all three columns or force the opposing player to draw their last card, wins the game.

During the player turn you can choose to dig and defend with your card, moves cards from one column to another or attack with long range or close combat. None of these are without their risks and weighing up the risk vs the strategic gain is part of the decision making elements of the game.

Combat is fast, simple but very brutal with all the dice rolls required on the cards - no charts or looking up in the rules. Damaged cards are suppressed first before being removed from the game and can still make a free saving throw on the dice to keep in the fight.

Publisher's summary
Putzroboter is the German name for a robotic vacuum cleaner.

Divide and clean up the mess of paper clips on the table.

When the game starts, there should be four different colors of paper clips spread out randomly on the table. Deal the paper clip cards to assign each player a particular color of paper clips to clean up. Take turns pushing around the robotic vacuum cleaner to collect the paper clips.

There is a magnetic ball contained within the robot. When the robot comes close to a paper clip, it will be able to pick up the paper clip. Collect your own paper clips but beware of picking up the clips belonging to a color of another player. If you do, you must give them the paper clip.

It’s important to move in a manner in which you pick up only your own color of paper clips as much as possible. Sometimes the robotic vacuum cleaner will move somewhere you didn’t think it would go. It’s difficult to balance your thinking between the degree of strength, direction, and other factors required to progress as planned.

The first player to pick up all the paper clips of their own color is the winner.

Publisher's summary
テーブルの上のクリップを手分けしてお片付けしましょう。
ゲームスタート時のテーブルには4色のクリップがたくさん散らばっています。

ゲームの最初にクリップカードを配り、それぞれどの色のクリップを片付けるかを決めます。

順番におそうじロボットを押して発進させ、クリップを取りましょう。
ロボットの中には磁石球が入っており、ロボット本体にクリップがくっ付くと、クリップを取ることができます。
自分の色のクリップを集めるのはもちろん他の人のクリップもくっ付くと、そのクリップを他の人にあげなければなりません。なるべく自分のクリップだけを取れるように発進させることが重要です。

また、思わぬ方向におそうじロボットが行くことも。向きや力加減のぐあいも思いのほか難しいものです。

いち早く自分の色のクリップをすべて取った人の勝ちです。

OH NUTS! is an exciting and humorous game that is perfect for friends and family. It is both fun for kids (ages 6+) and engaging for adults! Strategy, a sense of humor, and a bit of scheming is all it takes for loads of fun and laughter.

Each deck comes with 108 cards, and the total game time is anywhere from about 10-30 minutes. The goal of the game is simple. Be the first to collect 4 of the same kind of nut and move them from your nut "surplus" (cards in your hand) to your winter "stash" (cards you play on the table). Easy right? Ahhhh, but you need to watch out! The sly fox, ravenous raccoon, sneaky squirrel and stinky skunk are all after your nuts too, and players might play one of these cards on their turn to take a nut from you (or your stash) OR make you lose your turn by switching the direction of play!

--description from the publisher:

boardgame: Disney Die Eiskönigin Mein magisches Wettlaufspiel
description: In this race game you take control of one of the characters from Disney's Frozen and try to get to the Ice Palace first. You move by spinning a wheel and moving to the next spot of the shown colour. But if you spin a snowflake, you move to the next snowflake space, where a slide connects... Will that slide take you back where you came from or bring you you closer to the finish line?

Unicante 2007 is a German card game based on the student's choir Unicante, specifically its concert season 2007 and the choir members of that year. Cards represent singers, songs, events etc.

Players compete to collect cards forming a choir and line-up of songs to gather victory points. They have to manage the balance of the vocal parts in their choir and the overall mood of their group, take into account the abilities of their choir members, and use event cards to improve themselves or impede the other players.

A "concert" card mixed into the lower part of the deck announces the end of the game and victory points are counted for the final tally. The player with the highest total wins.

boardgame: Die Lieder, die Nerven: Ein Unicante-Spiel
description:
Die Lieder, die Nerven is a German board game based on the students' choir Unicante.

Players take on the roles of choir conductors, drafting actions over a course of 24 rounds (placing "helping hands" on the available actions slots). The actions include drawing arrangement cards and placing rehearsal markers on them to collect victory points, but also improving their abilities to get more helping hands, become better at rehearsing etc.

Being the conductor of a large choir can be quite stressful, so keep an eye on your "nerve" ressource.

German introductory text from the booklet

Leite einen Chor durch ein turbulentes Probenjahr. Sammle Arrangements und probe die Stücke. Verbessere die Fähigkeiten deines Chors und deines Teams.
Und vor allem: Nicht die Nerven verlieren!
Dann kannst du am Ende des Chorjahres mit einem Konzert glänzen, das die meisten Siegpunkte wert ist.

Das Spiel wird in einer festgelegten Anzahl von Runden gespielt. Durch das Einstudieren von Arrangements sammeln die Mitspielenden Siegpunkte, was oft Nerven kostet. Ziel des Spiels ist es, am Ende bei den großen Sommernachtskonzerten mehr Siegpunkte als die Mitspieler zu haben. 
In jeder Runde setzen alle nacheinander ihre “Helfenden Hände” auf die Aktionsfelder des Spielplans, um anschließend die entsprechenden Aktionen auszuführen: Neue Arrangements und Proben-Marker zu bekommen, sich mit Fähigkeitsmarkern in den Bereichen Orga, Stimmgruppenleitung und Chorleitung zu verbessern, ihre Teams mit weiteren Helfenden Händen zu vergrößern... und immer ihre Nervenpolster zu pflegen.

Division Zero’s work is never done. Saved the world from the Cupbearer? Already put down Prime? No time to pat yourself on the back, new threats are always on the rise. The FTZ Mission Generator will put you back into the field and push your squad to the limits!

boardgame: Fireteam Zero: Mission Pack – Team Challenge
description: Take on diabolical new challenges with this Team Challenge Pack.  Each team challenge is designed to push your squad's skills to the breaking point with special rules and scenarios!

boardgame: Fireteam Zero: Mission Pack – Team Competition
description: Find out what happens when two Fireteams go head to head for glory and team pride! Saving the world is important, but this is serious business! Claim your victory in four new missions, each with special scoring rules and conditions! May the best team prevail!

This expansion to '65 Squad Level Combat in Vietnam includes a full die-cut sheet (approximately 8.5” x 11”) of United States Marines and ANZAC counters (Two sheets), including rifle squads, heroes, and armored fighting vehicles, such as the Ontos, Centurion, and eight scenarios featuring them (Four each Marines and ANZACs). The most potent squads in the game, the Marines bring devastating firepower, and heroes with iron discipline to the gaming table.

The forces from Australia and New Zealand played a significant role in Vietnam. We’ll let you recreate that role with these ANZAC counters and scenarios featuring their contributions. Centurion tanks, FN-FAL rifles, snipers, and heroes with unique special abilities will all make muster on your gaming table, and provide unique insight into their fight in Vietnam as well as a bucket of tactical-gaming fun.

Comes in a Ziploc bag. Must own the base game, '65 Squad Level Combat in Vietnam, to play the scenarios in the Marine Expansion. Now shipping.

Includes 2 x sheets of die-cut counters and a full-color scenario booklet and rules.
