boardgame: Test of Honour: The Samurai Miniatures Game – Dice & Cards Expansion Set
description:
Including 10 more dice plus 10 unique cards:

2 special Dishonour cards - Public Disgrace and Private Shame - which add an element of chance when you act dishonourably!
    1 new Injury card - Lingering Wound -  means you start the next battle in a bad way!
    3 new Quest cards - including Despicable
    4 new Skill cards - including Fountain of Blood, Red Mist – which makes you more awesome once wounded – and Vengeance – which kicks in if a friendly samurai is cut down!

Inspired by pop culture science fiction like Star Trek®, Farscape®, Battlestar Galactica®, Robotech®, Expanse® and Firefly® - Cheeky Dingo Games and Layman Kingsford bring you a tabletop game adventure unlike any other.  Starship Crewpers engenders the experience of crewing a starship while undertaking missions that will fundamentally effect the plot of an ongoing galactic saga.

In a galaxy where every culture depends upon the Situdel Parliament to maintain the flow of species-specific food, life has been relatively unruffled for centuries. Now, from unknown interstellar origin, comes the galaxy’s first contact with alien life and it is hostile. One solar system has nearly been eradicated and all attempts to communicate with the invaders have proven fruitless.

With the Situdel Fleet allocating the majority of its resources to defending the population on the war front, you will begin your Fleet deployment by taking your small ship on patrol around a remote sector of space. Support from the central government and the rest of the Fleet is thin so you and your crew will be responsible for maintaining order in the region.

This worker placement and polyomino puzzle game has players drawing custom crew meeples from a shared bag to deploy them into custom polyomino tiles. The polyominos - or meepleOminos, if you will - must then be maneuvered across you starship board triggering worker placement slots as they pass over them. Play cards from hand to activate special effects such as repairing your ship, relieving stress and injuries from the crew and manipulating the polyomino tiles to maximize their scoring when they eventually get placed onto the mission grid for victory points.

Full expansion for Ophidian 2360 Futuristic Gladiator Combat Game! Original mechanics, great art, with new keywords, mechanics, and more!

This expansion adds additional Minions, Quick-Hits, Pumps, and more!

Add variety to your games by bringing these new gods to the battle. Turn fallen gods into stone statues with Greek goddess Medusa, or escort fallen Norse gods back to field with the help of the Valkyrie. This expansion comes with two God Cards and two miniatures.

High Heavens: Valkyrie & Medusa includes:


    Medusa Plastic figure (unpainted)
    Valkyrie Plastic figure (unpainted)
    2 god Cards
    Rules

Change the very landscape that the gods traverse with the High Heavens: Terrain set. Players can bring passive and active terrain features to the board for all new tactical play. Ymir’s Bones create two impenetrable zones while the two monsters, Scylla and Charybdis wreak havoc with those gods foolish enough to approach them.

High Heavens: Terrain includes:


    Rules
    4 Plastic terrain figures
    2 terrain cards

Alter your decks with the all-new High Heavens: Bloodied pack expansion. Replace all of your god cards or use these cards to deck-build a custom set up. These Greek and Norse cards contain the original line-up of Norse and Greek gods but present the gods as battle-worn with new art and modified stats.

High Heavens: Bloodied includes:


    Rules for deck building
    12 Greek god Bloodied cards
    12 Norse god Bloodied cards

Description from the publisher:

Geopolitics Boardgame is a game of geopolitical dispute where the newly emerging World Order Cold War and bipolarity, presents a scenario of unimultipolarity, in which a single superpower interacts with significant regional powers (which tend to oppose the main power), and With numerous secondary powers (which, in general, align with the Superpower and oppose the regional power of its geographical area). The significant strength of some regional powers, concerned with the US struggle for global hegemony, has been hampering this purpose.

One of the players will represent the USA and the other regional powers (Russia, China, United Kingdom or Germany and Brazil), each with its own advantages and peculiarities, that will fight for the balanced world, each one trying to conquer to challenge the unimultippality through the war , Trade, politics, diplomacy and technological progression.

After extensive research in the military area, 50 technological advances of 5 different types (infrastructure, naval, air, land and missile) were incorporated.

With a double board, magnificent meeples of military units (army, special forces, APC, tank, artillery, corvette, destroyer, frigate, cruiser, submarine, transport ship, helicopter, fighter, bomber, tactical air transport, SAM, industrial complex ) Plus tokens including cruise missile and nuclear missile, hundreds of cards, custom dice, sideboards, playmates.

Geopolitics Boardgame has a structure of game in rounds with 3 phases:

I) In the Strategy Phase, players choose one of the 8 strategy cards (leadership, diplomacy, politics, production, commercial, war, technology, geopolitics), each of which has an initiative value that determines the order of the game. Each card has a primary skill that will be used exclusively by the player who chooses the card and a secondary skill that can be used by other players at the necessary costs.

II) In the Action Phase, players will be able to use the chosen strategy card, activate territories on the board to move troops and diplomatic leaders to raise influence and resources, build military units, recruit and / or fund weapons and militants from rebel and terrorist groups, To celebrate political-military alliances, to attack opponents, to conclude trade agreements, to propose voting of treaties in international organizations, to research technological advances and to use action letters.

III) In the Status phase players can qualify for 1 geopolitical goals per round, including pipeline control, technological development, military and diplomatic goals.

Win the game that finishes first 10 goals.

The "designer notes" in the rule book humbly recognize the inspiration of such games as Twilight Imperium, Twillight Struggle, Imperial 2030 and Supremacy.

boardgame: Rise of the Elders: A Cthulhu World
description:
A Tabletop Game based on the Cthulhu mythos, featuring:

- RPG-based character creation for every new game: Create your unique character each time using one of the 6 sheets (10 with the kickstarter edition), each with bonuses, maluses and a point on one feature. Will you focus on Strength, Agility or Resistance ?

- Modular Board: Create your own path, each step into the unknown could be your last, but be aware of the empty hexagons you create while building the map. Cthulhu wants them, Cthulhu wants your defeat.

- Storytelling: Each new tile is supported by a new story, each story has two choices and each of them leads to one of 6 outcomes (3 good ones, 3 bad ones) resulting in 1080 astonishing possibilities. Each choice is based on your character's features and your luck.

- Uniquely designed miniature models for every monster in the game (Heir of Cthulhu, Shub-Niggurath, Azatoth and Yog-Sothoth) and for every character.

- Enemies among Friends: In each game you play, you'll never know if the player by your side is a friend or a foe. It starts  as a cooperative game, but one of your "friends" might be a cultist for one of the Ancients.

- Turn Based: You don't have much time, you need to find the site of the ritual trying to bring a Ancient Ones into our world through a portal. You only have a few turns to do it!

- Equip Yourself: As in any RPG game, your character can use weapons, like a Machinegun or an Axe, find marvellous objects, like powerful Talismans, or outlive danger through the use of protective gear.

- The Goal: If you're one of the investigators of the occult, your goal is to close all the Ancient portals, but if you're one of the cultists, your goal is to complete the ritual to bring your Ancient One on Earth.

This lot contains 5 Gladiator cards:

007 Zdantal
008 The Corruptor L1/L2
09 The Corruptor L3/L4
010 QuickRazor
011 Desert Aegis

Description from the publisher:

Make America Debate Again is a non-political card game where you and your friends debate ridiculous, creative topics with logical or outlandish arguments.

Each player takes turns drawing topic cards which they will attempt to win based on a simple majority vote or by winning a debate against one of the other players.

First to five cards wins!

Into The Rubble offers players of Advanced Squad Leader the opportunity to do battle in cities and railroad yards. The scenarios range from 1938 to 1945 with a variety of forces including American, Chinese, German/SS, Hungarian, Japanese, Nationalist Spanish, Republican Spanish, Romanian and Russian.

Into The Rubble 2 builds on Bounding Fire Production's initial offering (Into the Rubble), adding 12 new and exciting scenarios in addition to the 8 which came in the original pack, in addition to new boards, overlays and counters. Everything from the original game is here, all known errata has been incorporated, and some of the original scenarios have been adjusted for play balance.

Into the Rubble 2 includes the following:

- 20 action-packed scenarios in full-color print
- 140 Full color, die cut 1/2" counters
- 88 Full color, die cut 5/8" counters
- 2 8"x22" geomorphic mapboards (BFP A and B) 
- 1 16"x22" geomorphic double-wide mapboard (BFP DW-7)
- 1 8"x12" rubbled city overlay (BFP RC-1)
- 1 large factory overlay
- 1 sheet of debris overlays
- 1 sheet of rubble/rubbled building overlays
- Rules pages describing new terrain, including Railroad Factories, and new unit types.

Ownership of the following ASL products are required to play all 20 of the included scenarios: Beyond Valor, Yanks, Doomed Battalions, Armies of Oblivion, Red Barricades, For King and Country, Rising Sun.

Battle of Souls is a 2+ player trading card game. In Battle of Souls you deploy (summon) warriors of different ranks and warrior sects to drain your opponents soul points (life points) and win the game. The theme of the game carries alone with historical warriors such as Samurai, Mongolian, Roman etc..

In Battle of Souls, each player starts with 8000 life points which in our game is referred to as soul points. The objective of the game, or otherwise stated the win condition is to be the first player to reach 16000 soul points.

This is done by dealing damage to your opponent from the attacks of your deployed (summoned) warriors.

Each time your opponent takes damage of any type, including any cost to activate an effect, you gain that much SP (soul points) and vice versa.

This mechanic insures that there will always be a definite victor and never a draw game. 
The second win condition is if one player runs out of cards to draw from their decks, they lose the game.

GAME FLOW:
Each player starts the game by drawing 5 cards from their deck to their hand. The first play to take a turn draws one additional card at the beginning of their turn. Each turn the player is able to deploy only one fighter (warrior, elite warrior, warlord, legendary warrior) to their side of the field. The first player to take his/her turn may not attack on that turn. The last player to draw is the first that can attack.
The fighter cards are ranked in the following order, along with deployment requirements:
Warrior > No special requirements
Elite Warrior > Tribute a warrior that has 3 kill counters on it (kill counters are gained whenever a fighter destroys another fighter in battle)
Warlord > Tribute an Elite Warrior with 5 kill counters on it
Legendary Warrior > Tribute a Warlord with 7 kill counters on it, in addition to an Elite Warrior and Warrior on your side of the field.

Equipment cards can be played face up or set face down to be used at a later time. Some are one time use and others are continuous

Tactic cards are played face down on the turn you play them from your hand and may be activated by flipping face up on any turn after they were first played as long as the effect conditions are met (if any)

Battlefield cards are played face up and only one Battlefield card can be active on the field at any time. If a Battlefield card is in play when another is played, the previous one is destroyed.

FIELD LIMITATIONS:
On the field, each player can have a maximum of 5 fighter cards in play 1 battlefield, and 6 total equipment or tactic cards

PHASES:
The game phases are as follows for each turn
Draw phase > Player draws a card
Ready phase > All fighters that are currently in rest position (This is the position a fighter is changed to after a successful attack) are switched to attack position
Main phase > Player can deploy, special deploy, activate equipment, battlefield, or previously set tactic cards as well as utilize any effects whose condition is met.
Battle phase > Player sends his/her warriors to fight
Main phase 2: do the same as the first main phase with the exception of deploying a fighter if the player already did so during the first main phase. Special deployments are limitless and can be done any number of times up to the max allows fighters in play.
End phase: player ends their turn and discards a card from their hand if they are carrying 7 or more cards in hand.

The object of this math-game is to stay in the air, avoid the thunderclouds while collecting as many golden points as possible.

On your turn you roll dice to gather lift-points which you need to keep your plane in the air. You´ll have to gain altitude by spending lift points or save points by descending. But beware, if you drop of the board the game is over. It will get increasingly harder to stay in the air. With every level the amount of lift points needed to gain altitude rises.

The goal in Solo play is to beat the game, level 13, with as many golden points as possible. In a multiplayer game, the player who stays in the air the longest time is the winner.

boardgame: The World of SMOG: Rise of Moloch – Kingdom Death Crossover: Steampunk Twilight Knight
description: A kickstarter-exclusive expansion optional add on. It includes the Steampunk Twilight Knight crossover character who is from the Kingdom Death: Monster game. Also included in this expansion is a model of the character as well as her own dashboard and card.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
Description from the publisher:

Smoothie Kaboom is a kickstarter promotional collaboration between Minion Games and Thunderworks Games, combining the components of Blend Off! and Cosmic Kaboom into a third, hybrid game that plays like a combination of the two.

In Smoothie Kaboom, players will race their ships around the galaxy to collect fruit from different planets to create smoothies. Players take turns flicking their ships. If they collide with a planet that is currently producing, they can collect the fruit. When they have the right fruit to complete a smoothie order, they return to their base to cash in for points. The game ends when all smoothie orders have been filled. The winner is the one with the most gold stars.

boardgame: Adventure Time: The Epic Gassy Jake Game
description:
A Gassy twist to the fun game of Hot Potato Game. Soft plush Jake doll with 4 different electronic "pooting" sounds. Great for both indoor & outdoor play. Quickly toss Gassy Jake from player to player and don't be caught holding Jake as he emits his gassy sounds. If you do, your out. There are 4 electronic "pooting" sounds emitted by randomized timer. So you don't know when the sounds are going to be automatically activated. The featured authentic sounds are "as heard on" the actual show episodes. There are several ways to play for different skill levels. WARNING:: THIS GAME IS CAPABLE OF MAKING YOU LAUGH UNCONTROLLABLY.

—description from the publisher

Promotional card that came with the Hero Realms Legion Cardbox (1 copy)

Captain Koska
Champion - Human Mercenary
Cost 4
Unaligned - No Ally Ability
Combat 3 - Guard 4
Expend Ability: Choose a faction. Captain Koska has that faction this turn.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
A Game of Survival in the Zombie Apocalypse

From the introduction:

The apocalypse has come. The dead have risen. Time is running out for the human race. Only a few survive. Against a rising tide of zombies, a small group of stubborn humans spitefully carries on, scavenging the ruins of the city looking for supplies to bring back to their fortified base where they hide from death.

But for how long?

Time, not the undead, is your greatest enemy.

User summary:

The game is played in turns, with each player moving first and the zombies appearing and moving in an end-of-turn phase.  Characters have several tracked attributes.  The map of the area is developed during play by placing cards.  Game time is limited to one hour.  Victory is collaborative and depends on accomplishing certain scavenge and survival goals before time expires.  A separate, optional, rating method is provided to determine "who on your team finishes on top", in the case of victory.

The game document includes several pages of print-and-play components.

The DC Comics Deck-Building Game: Kid Flash oversized hero card was initially available as a promotional card for First Edition versions of Cryptozoic Entertainment's DC Comics Deck-Building Game: Teen Titans. This card can be mixed with any of the other hero cards in the game to give players one more choice when choosing heroes before starting to play.

As with every other hero, Kid Flash grants a special ability to the player, which in this case is: "Whenever you buy or gain a card, you may add the top card of the main deck to the Line-Up."

Description from the publisher:

Go Fish Fitness is a game based around the classic game Go Fish, it is a card game for kids to encourage them to get up, jump around, and add a component of physical exercise to their game in a fun and competitive way. Each card denomination includes a quick physical exercise, and players compete to make a match and force their opponents to do the activity.

In addition to exercises, each card includes an animal or a creature so that younger children, and those with limited mobility can participate by mimicking the shown creature when a match is made.

Go Fish Fitness is based around the same set of cards as a standard deck, and can be incorporated into many other standard card games as well. Keeping fit is about incorporating exercise into everyday life, in ways both big and small, and Go Fish Fitness is a way to introduce a little bit more exercise into a normally passive activity for your kids!

A board game originally published in Games Workshop's White Dwarf magazine (2014 issue #34 of White Dwarf.)

In the dark, unseen dungeons of Commorragh Haemonculus Covens gather to celebrate the perfection of pain in brutal contests against one another. These affairs are brutal and bloody, but for the lords of torture and rejuvenation, every new sensation is one to be cherished.

Symphony of Pain is a game for 2-4 players where each player controls a small team of five Wracks and a Haemonculus in a fight to inflict the most pain on the enemy.

There are two ways to win Symphony of Pain: eliminate all of the opposing models or gain enough pain points to declare yourself the winner.

A board game originally published in Games Workshop's White Dwarf magazine (2014 issue #14 of White Dwarf)

The Defence of Athel Loren is a simple minigame that uses a Treeman and five Warhammer warriors who seek to despoil the home of the Wood Elves.

There are two players in this game, the Defender and the Despoiler. The object of the game is for the Defender to use his Treeman to kill the Despoiler’s minions before they can reach the Sacred Glade of Athel Loren.

This well-designed game will get kids up to speed on telling time. Players move the arms on their own clock faces to show the time described on cards. It is especially good for correlating analog and digital representations of time as well as wording like half past and quarter to. Cards are color coded for the progression of skills in learning to tell time; each color representing time to the hour, the half hour, the quarter hour, in five minute intervals or for all sixty minutes. Points are awarded for setting clocks to the correct time, as well as for correcting clocks to the specified time, making playing a reinforcement of time telling skills.

In the darkness of the winternight the young men gathered around the fire, listening to the old man's tale. He claimed to be one of the men, who once discovered the fabled lands of Vinland. But was it true? Even as the old man left, his stories remained, took root in the dreams and desires of men. Most of them kept dreaming but some brave or foolish enought were ready to leave their lifes behind and become legends, or die trying. 
This is their Story.

In Vinland, the Players take the role of two of four characters from the Vinland Saga, the merchant brothers Helgi and Finnbogi, the fierce shieldmaiden Freydís and her brother Leif Eiríksson, children of Erik the Red and in their shadow, Eyiolf Thorgestsson seeking revenge for a blood depth. Adventures and fame will welcome the brave, if they survive the sea of storms and blood, entangled in a net of betrayal and ambition. 
The first version of Vinland will follow the characters on their travel across Greenland, past the frozen island of bears and beyond the deep forests of Markland. The Game can be either played as a racing game, to reach Vinland first, or as a co-op game on a shared expedition. Vinland will be a 18 Cards game and tries to merge the dense historical references with a light encountersystem.

Coop Game:
The theme resolves around the fabled Continent of Vinland (America) discovered by Vikings way ahead of Columbus. The player have to play two members of an expedition into the unknown West. Its a coop game in which they have to survive Weather on land and sea, encounter Wild Animals and rediscover lost settlements, ruins and gravesides of former expeditions.

The Game ends, when they cross the last of the seven sea(card)s and may reach Vinland. Its not about the arrival in Vinland, but about the Journey of the Vikings to that fabled place.

Steal all the Cake is a 2 to 4 player card game of remarkable thievery where players dispatch hordes of cute mischievous animals to steal mountains of cake from eachother.

Players can eat their cake to harness its mighty powers but if they are not careful they might find themselves with an empty plate.

Each turn players can send one animal and eat one cake, each type of card has various effects that will bring you closer to victory.

The goal of the game is to Steal all the Cake.

If a player runs out of cake they lose and they are out, so they must learn the balance between stealing, hoarding and devouring cake.

The last player standing wins and is crowned the King of Thieves!

Compilation of:
Wild Life  (Ravensburger/Carlit, type B)
Grand Prix  (Ravensburger)
Chess Race (Ravensburger)
Huckepack

Description from the publisher:

Saga: Aetius & Arthur is a supplement for Gripping Beast's Saga game. Saga: Aetius & Arthur is an 88 page hard cover supplement that comes with six additional factions with Battle Boards for the following: The late Romans, The Huns, The Goths, and The Britons, the Saxons, and the Picts. Players will need to have Saga: Dark Ages and/or The Crescent and the Cross rule book to use this supplement. Saga: Aetius & Arthur makes references to some of the battle boards that were included in the Saga: Dark Ages as well as a few from the Northern Fury and Raven's Shadow supplements that can be used with this supplement. Saga: Aetius & Arthur also makes some references to The Crescent and the Cross when detailing special rules for the factions.
family: Theme: King Arthur / The Knights of the Round Table / Camelot

description:
The TCG set is based on the changes between Pokémon Diamond and Pearl and Platinum, including Giratina in both its Altered Forme and its Origin Forme, as well as introducing Shaymin to the Trading Card Game. It also features Team Galactic's Pokémon and various Trainer cards based on them. Platinum also introduces two new mechanics to the Trading Card Game. The first new mechanic is a new type of Pokémon known as Pokémon SP. The second new mechanic to be introduced is the Lost Zone. The Lost Zone is essentially a second discard pile: however, it is not located in the play area. Hence, cards that are moved to the Lost Zone cannot be retrieved by cards which specify the recovery of cards from the discard pile.

Platinum features 130 new cards. Theme Decks feature a full 60 Pokemon card deck with special holographic foil card , metallic coin, and damage counters.

Platinum roughly corresponds to the contemporaneous Japanese-language expansion Galactic's Conquest.

boardgame: Magic: The Gathering – Fourth Edition Core Set
description:
The Fourth Edition of Magic: The Gathering was the tenth set released for the game, and the fourth base set (or "core set"). It contained 378 cards and was the first set to reprint cards from the expansions Legends and The Dark with white borders. Fourth Edition cards have white borders. The set has no expansion symbol.

Fourth Edition was also the first Magic set to be printed in Asian languages, including Japanese, Chinese, Korean, as well as Portuguese, which was printed primarily for the Brazilian market. The Japanese edition was particularly successful and contributed to the burgeoning of Magic's popularity in Japan. Korean and Chinese Fourth Edition cards have been made only with black borders while Japanese, Portuguese, and Spanish cards exist with white and black borders.

The Magic the Gathering 4th Edition Starter Deck contains 60 cards and rulebook. The distribution is 3 rare, 9 uncommon, 26 common, and  22 land.

description:
Description from the publisher:

The stars and the galaxies reveals its mysteries only to the watchful eye. Æther invites you to play and have fun with our notions of the universe and its inception.

In Æther, the players take turns playing cards in a quest to see how the universe takes shape. To do this, the players use cards that represent matter (Nube Molecular) and energy (Campo Gravitacional), stars, galaxies and singularities. To play a galaxy, a player needs to discard 2 stars under his/her control, but to play a star you need to discard both matter and energy cards. The goal of the game is to be the fist player to control two galaxy cards and one star card.
But every card has an effect on the game. From drawing extra cards to stealing from other players or even canceling a card's effect just played from your opponent, using them in the most effective way is crucial for victory.

This supplement to Starfire 3rd Edition was the first publication from the Starfire Design Studio (SDS).  Its rules presage the changes that the SDS introduced in 3rd Edition Revised.  The most important features are a set of rules for combining the unique tech system from the Akelda Dawn expansion with the traditional tech system (for both tactical and strategic/campaign games), and new rules for the campaign system (Imperial Starfire), including an abstracted Commercial Freighter Network (CFN).

Deep Madness: Endless Nightmares is an expansion for Deep Madness. It was originally announced as three separate items, called Fathoms Dark, Fevered Dreams and Expectant Terrors.

There is more to the story. There are other investigators descending to the depths. There are new monsters being birthed from the sphere’s roiling horrors. New chapters are creeping out of the darkness, and endless fresh nightmares are crouching in wait just out of sight.

Endless Nightmares adds a whole new level to your Deep Madness experience. With three new standalone scenarios, six new investigators, nine new types of monsters, three new epic monsters and much more, Endless Nightmares will keep you submerged in Kadath’s depths for countless hours to come. Journey once more below the ocean’s surface. Step again into the uncharted night. They’re ready for you. And they’re so very hungry.

The Emperor's Samurai is a tactical/action game set in ancient Japan. Players control 1 of 4 samurai from competing clans, in an attempt to earn honor through retrieval of the Emperor's stolen items and captives. They must enter the Mongol Warlords dungeon, and maneuver the shifting passageways before the entire dungeon caves in! All while surviving encounters from the opposing samurai and a deadly assassin who watches over the dungeon.

The object of the game is to secure the most honor. Players collect honor by picking up tokens, fighting opposing samurai and entering the vault. When players reach their carry limit, they'll  want to exit out any stairway, thus securing their honor and preventing other players the opportunity to take it!

Opposing samurai can be extremely dangerous but the assassin who watches over the Warlords dungeon can be even more deadly! His movements are quick and unpredictable. Players will be using action cards to evade the assassin, attack opposing samurai, and manipulate the dungeon paths.

As the game progresses, the Warlord begins caving in the stairways and eventually the vault, thus funneling the players and ramping up the action! The game ends when all carried honor is secured.

The samurai with the most honor earns the privilege of becoming a member of The Emperor's Samurai!

Based on the 1950's TV series of the same name. It is a detective game with some similarities to Cluedo in that the aim of the game is to identify the murderer. 
Up to 4 players use a telephone shaped spinner to move around the board collecting clues. Clues are recorded on crime sheets and the first player to correctly identify the murderer is the winner. 
The board comprises of 6 bedrooms, a bathroom, reception and Sabers office. Squares around these rooms allow players to move around the board depending on the number they spin.
There are 4 wooden playing pieces and a spinner. There are 6 card men (one of which is the murderer) and a card body (a woman). The card men are stood in wooden blocks. There are also 6 clues cards (only 5 used in any one game) and 6 cards keys, silvered on one side and with a letter referring to a bedroom on the other.

In Super-GAUdi each player is a boss of an nuclear energy company. Each player starts with 7 barrels full of atomic waste and has to put them to one of the final storages on the playing schedule. The player that gets rid of all his barrels at first, wins the game. The clue is, that the players can either put a barrel to one of the hidden final storages or they can look if a final storage is safe or note safe. During the game each players knows some safe and some unsafe storages. After a player put a barrel with atomic waste to one of the final storage-cards, the other players can say that they don't believe that this final storage is safe. If they are right, the player has to take all the barrels on this card (up to three). If he was honest, he gives one of his barrels to the player that distrusted him.

Flavor text:
"Die Geschäfte laufen wie geschmiert, doch die Chefs der großen Atomkonzerne haben ein Problem: Sie wissen nicht wohin mit dem ganzen Atommüll. Es gilt also endlich ein "Endlager" zu finden, auf Teufel komm raus! Sei es ein gammeliges Bergwerk, ein Naturschutzgebiet oder der Kühlschrank von Mutti - no Risk, no Fun! Bleibt nur zu hoffen, dass der Bluff nicht auffliegt... Gewonnen hat derjenige Atomboss, der seinen ganzen Atommüll als Erster los ist."

boardgame: Carder Harmonies: A Set Building Card Game From La Dispute
description:
La Dispute is enlisting your help in coming up with the ideal setlist. Mirroring the way the band builds performance sets in real life, you'll arrange themed subsections (called Blocks), using the right songs in the right combinations to earn the most Applause Points. However, Broken Strings and Technical Difficulties might disrupt your plans...

Carder Harmonies is a card game for 1-3 players (competitive or collaborative mode available for multiplayer games). Using various Song cards covering the entire discography of the post-hardcore band La Dispute, players use their hand of 3 cards to populate Set Blocks with Songs, abiding of course by that Block's special conditions (a variety of song counts, allowing songs of only certain records, or heavy/soft dynamics). When a Block condition is met, that Block becomes closed and those songs are safe from the banes of Technical Difficulties, Broken Strings, and other immediate action cards in the deck.

New to the game in 2nd edition are Block Modifiers, a type of card to add additional restrictions to a single block, granting bonus applause at the end of the game for achieving the listed goal or costing you the same amount upon failure.

Once all their Set Blocks are closed, players then tally their Applause Points, accounting for a Song's individual worth, any special conditions granting bonus points, and a streak of consecutive Songs from the same record. Solitaire players aim to surpass a given Applause threshold for their chosen difficulty level, while 2 player mode has the players aiming to score higher than their opponent.

Lone Canuck Publishing extends its Ost Front series with the third ASL expansion adding another 6 scenarios to the series.  This time the scenarios are focused around "The German Invasion of the Soviet Union, 22 June 1941."

The Takashi clan have long followed the teachings of Michi and unlike some of the other clans in the Jwar Isles strictly adhere to its teachings when raising the next generation. The Michi priests already offer the support of their military arm in the form of the Seishin na Geoi, but are not opposed to accompanying any believes on their paths, whether that leads them to battle or otherwise. Through years of dedication the Michi priests are able to evoke the protective powers of their ancestors, dousing flame, neutralising poisons or the intrusive commands of others. The presence of the spirits can also be used to distract and block the concentration of enemies.

Blister contains one model, one 30mm base and one full colour profile card.

“Like all the cycles of the world, those within the Golden Empire are no different. From the changing of the cycles to the rise and fall of the great clans that rule, so is the way things are, and so is the way they will remain for they are as constant as rock and as inevitable as the stream that finds the ocean.” Common Subarashi Baransu saying.

All the great clans draw on the lesser clans for support, either on the battlefield, in court or commercially, the Takashi are no different and for many generations the Makoto have enjoyed the protection of the Takashi in exchange for their wisdom and consort. The Makoto family are renowned for their wealth of knowledge and love of the sciences. Kioshi has long been the voice of reason in the court of Ryu, his quiet words hold more weight than Minimoto steel to the heads of the Takashi.

Blister contains one model, one 30mm base and one full colour profile card.

Those that follow the way of Michi will often spend hours in meditation, waiting for a sign from their ancestors of the correct path they should follow or the right choice to make. In times of peril the ancestor spirits have been known to manifest in physical form. Their presence guiding the hands, minds and blades of the living, gifting them their infinite insight and knowledge gathered from their timeless vigil and granting them the courage to stay on the path. Of course this comes at a great price to the spirit for once you have left the heavens you may never return…

Blister contains one model, one 30mm base and one full colour profile card.

From Ugo to Eddo, the loyal servants of the Takashi can be called upon to bolster the ranks of the Dragon’s armies. Their knowledge of the terrain invaluable in giving the Prefecture an added advantage in battle.

Blister contains one model, one 30mm base and one full colour profile card.

The ronin of the Shiho remain a thorn in the Takashis' side. Without a fixed army the Takashi have found it almost impossible to hunt down and destroy the remaining rebels in the field, these cowed dogs hide in the woods and hills of the Dragon's lands. The solution has been somewhat answered in the excellent tracking and scouting skills of Atsushi, a former Satou family retainer now sworn to the Takashi. With his faithful hound Shinobu, rooting out those hiding in the dark, eager to savage any at the utterance of a single word from his master.

Blister contains two models, two 30mm bases and two full colour profile cards.

Lady Oka is as deadly as she is beautiful. Raised in the luxury of Ryu she is a great power in the court and has learned to use the weapons gifted to her just as expertly as those crafted of polished steel and lacquered wooded in the dojo. A nimble and dexterous warrior she wields the traditional weapon of a lady of the samurai caste, with a natural ease and elegance. She moves across the battlefield with the poise and grace of a dancer, avoiding blows and missiles as if they were not even there.

Blister contains one model, one 30mm base and one full colour profile card.

All lords maintain a healthy number of household guards, training warriors to keep them safe from their enemies. The Takashi guard’s body may not be as honed as that of the Ashigaru who constantly train and drill. This does not mean that their eyes are not sharp as blades and their ears keen as hawks, skills developed over the years watching for threats to their lords and ladies.

Blister contains one model, one 30mm base and one full colour profile card.

Samurai culture has both military and feudal culture.  Everyone fits into the social pyramid where a leader had his trusted supporters under his command. They in turn had their own respective supporters under their command, and so on and so on. The Takashi retainers are all capable warriors in the service of a Takashi Samurai of a higher rank, willing to lay down their lives in service. A high ranking lord will always have at least a hand full of retainers watching and guarding them at all times, constantly scanning a crowd or appraising a seemingly harmless peasant seeking council.

Blister contains one model, one 30mm base and one full colour profile card.

Bachiko is as loyal and devoted to her lord as any can claim to be. She lives and breathes to protect the Takashi line as her father and his father before. A masterful swordswoman, hours spent in the service has given her a sixth sense for danger and she alone has foiled many cowardly attacks against her masters.

This model can be used as a unique profile or as the second model for the Takashi Retainer Profile card.

Blister contains one model, one 30mm base and one full colour profile card.

From childhood, the Samurai train to have self-discipline and a sense of duty, together with contempt for material goods, fear, pain and especially death. When combined with an unshakable devotion to their clan and lord they are the epitome of those who truly follow Michi.

Blister contains one model, one 30mm base and one full colour profile card.

Hidden identities with opposing goals compete for trick-taking galaxy supremacy!

Players are given hidden identity cards: The "Envoy" scores points for other people making their bid, the "Saboteur" scores for others failing, and everybody else scores for making their own. The deck size changes depending on the player count (3, 4, or 5) so every game is balanced.

After being dealt their hands and hidden identities, each player bids the number of tricks they will take using gems. Color-coded gems make it easy to see if the bid is under, full, or over. One player may be a saboteur, so you cannot count on every bid being legitimate.

The hand is played out in trick. The Solar suit is always trump. As players take tricks they place their gems on the piles so the status of all bids is apparent to all. After the last trick is taken every player reveals their identity and scores points accordingly. Players move their corresponding cube on the score track until one player makes it to 30 points to win the game.

STORY

Centuries of multilateral conflicts throughout the galaxy are being resolved at the diplomatic conference of the era: The Congress of Zienna. Borderlines will be drawn and the resources of entire systems will be acquired. Empires, alliances, and multi-planetery corporations struggle for power --- not in battle but at the negotiating table.

You are a representative of a galactic power sent to Zienna Prime. Your goal at the negotiating table depends on your hidden identity:

Envoy - Galactic peace alliances benefit from stability and mutual success. The Envoy's goal is to help all parties get what they want.

Saboteur - Hired by nefarious provocateurs seeking conflict, the saboteur is sent to prevent the negotiations from being a success.

Solo Agent - Representing the interests of a single client state or corporation, agents work to maximise acquisitions with no regard for competitors.

A ward is a member of a noble clan who has been taken in by another clan to be raised for a time. The practice varies, with it sometimes being undertaken voluntarily - to help prospective heirs learn the customs of other clans as part of their education. It can also be involuntarily, with the "ward" actually being a "hostage" for his or her clan's good behaviour or until a ransom is paid. Sometimes, as part of marriage alliances, one of the betrothed is taken as a ward so he or she can get to know his or her future spouse. IIju is not Dragon but Hare, although his loyalty to the Takashi is unquestioned, he is the head of the Takashi household guard whom follow his orders without hesitation.

Blister contains one model, one 30mm base and one full colour profile card.

Kenta is a true samurai in every sense of the word. As a boy he knew his path, the one he has walked for many cycles in service to his family and the name Takashi. A veteran of countless battles and campaigns, he is a master swordsman, no foe able to outflank him due to practised footwork and deft movements. Many times enemies foolishly thought they had struck the killing blow but the ancestors favour Kenta amongst all others. Though quietly spoken his presence in battle banishes any fear in his troops, steeling their hearts for victory.

Blister contains one model, one 30mm base and one full colour profile card.

Blindes Huhn extrem: Tauschen is a promotional card for Blindes Huhn extrem that allows you to change your hand with another player.

Designer Michael Schacht released Blindes Huhn extrem: Tauschen as one-half of a postcard Christmas card (the other half containing Maharaja: Reichtum).

Das Vermächtnis des Maharaja: Reichtum is a promotional collection card for Das Vermächtnis des Maharaja that earns a player eight rupees for any two antique cards.

Released as one-half of a postcard with the other half being Blindes Huhn Extrem: Tauschen.

Description from the publisher:

A "core" set expansion that is designed to primarily alter the turn order of the game.  Tired of going around in circles?!  This expansion features Premium Role cards that change the turn order.  How it works?  At the beginning of each round, players secretly choose a Premium Role card.  That will be the role they are using on their next turn.  Players then simultaneously reveal their role and the order is determined from Highest to Lowest ensuring that the game rounds are always different.

In addition to the Premium Role cards, also included are two (2) expansion Scenarios:  Rise of the Monarch and Battle Front.

As per the original one (1) Player Game Sets, the Rise of the Monarch scenario is back!  This scenario brings back the days of early Renaissance and exploration of the Galaxy.  In this scenario, players race to be the first player to earn the right to be the Monarch Supreme of the galaxy.

The newer addition to the expansion is the Battle Front scenario.  This scenario's goal is to accelerate and shorten the time spent playing the Spacewars scenario.  It can be played as a scenario itself OR as another expansion played together with any of the current multiplayer scenarios (Tradewars, Spacewars or Rise of the Monarch).  In this scenario, players race to obtain "retrofits" for their starships which will give them bonus effects to alter the way space skirmishes are resolved.  There are a total of six (6) different "retrofits" available to each player.  Much like the Days of Glory scenario, the retrofits are unlocked as a player earns more credits.

Also as one of the game's Stretch Goals, we've added a Co-op scenario called United Garrison in which up to four (4) players can combat a Derelict starship that is hell bent on destroying all the player's Homeworlds.

This expansion is comprised of 96 cards for 1 to 4 Players.  It can add from 15-30 minutes of additional game time to the "core" game.

Description from the publisher:

A "core" set expansion that is designed to increase the level of strategy by adding Tradeships and Trade Agreements.  It features the Mission cards that were removed from the original one (1) Player Box Set due to complexity and an attempt to "streamline" the original game for a wider gamer audience.

Missions are back and therefore so are Tradeships.  Tradeships allow you to perform a mission which may take 1 to 5 turns to complete, during this time the player on a mission will benefit from a Trade Agreement which will give them bonus credits when banking certain resources.  Modifiers are 2x, +5 and 3x the value of the banked resource (according to the Trade Agreement).

This expansion is comprised of 140 cards for 1 to 4 Players.  It also adds 4 white custom dice to be used as counters for Tradeships.  It can add from 15-30 minutes of additional game time to the "core" game. However, this is offset by higher banking of resources, which ultimately balances the duration of the game.

boardgame: Dragoon: The Rogue and Barbarian Expansion
description:
The dragons believed their might was unrivaled. They were mistaken...

Ever since dragons took over, the humans were left cowering without a gold coin to their name...until now. From the sea arrives a ship commanded by a stoic Barbarian in need of new recruits by fear or by coin. From the island catacombs emerges a mysterious Rogue, rumored to steal by night with cunning equipment. With the ability to play using any combination of characters, including two player Rogue vs. Barbarian, the first expansion to Dragoon offers both fresh variants for returning players and the ability to play with up to six players!

DRAGOON: The Rogue and Barbarian Expansion takes the fun and excitement of Dragoon to new heights by increasing the total player count to 6. Rather than adding more playable dragons, you can now test your mettle with two new human roles: the Rogue and Barbarian!

Each character has a unique play style. While the Barbarian plays similarly to the dragons, they require a more tactical approach to increase their strength and gain access to a unique deck of cards. The Rogue relies on stealth and a suite of new equipment to outwit the competition.

With the ability to play using any combination of characters, the first expansion to DRAGOON offers both fresh variants for returning players and the ability to play with a larger group at game night!

Asymmetric characters with new play styles!

Increases max player count to 6.

Adds 15-30 minutes.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
The Primus GEP was where it all began. A test subject that was developed before the Hikari had set out on their voyage, The Primus acted as the starting genetic code for all of the G.E.P.E.C. programs. It was kept in an unconscious stasis, but since The Fall, massive power fluctuations have left survivors wondering whether The Primus has awoken from its slumber…

The Primus GEP introduces a new boss to Endure the Stars, complete with its own unique Boss deck and mission! The survivors have decided to act quickly and put an end to The Primus before it wakes, but little do they know that it has already woken up!

Pack contents:
Primus card deck - 22 cards
1 Radar token
1 Health token 
1 Primus dashboard
1 Primus miniature
Take that game similar to a 3x3 Golf with card interactions.

User summary
ストイケイア (STOIKHEIA) is a worker placement card game for two players with extended reproduction mechanism.

In this game, players are wizards who collect elements to create staves which grant you efficiency to collect more elements. But their capacity to store elements is limited, so they should increase the capacity by collecting vials.

Nine action cards are placed on the table in 3 x 3 grid. Each turn, you place your familiar on one of these action cards to perform an action, such as collecting or converting elements, gain victory points, create a staff, etc. When three familiars are placed in a row, you collect a vial and a special effect is activated.

At the end of the game, count victory points earned through taking actions and creating staves. The higher scorer wins the game.

Publisher's summary
「ストイケイア」は２人専用の拡大再生産型ワーカープレイスメントゲームです。
プレイヤーは魔法使いとなり、エレメントを集め、杖を生成します。
杖を生成すれば、１度により多くのエレメントを入手できるようになります。
しかし、持てるエレメントには上限があります。上限を増やすには、瓶を入手しなければなりません。

手番では、３×３に並べられたアクションカードに使い魔コマを置いてアクションを実行します。
使い魔コマを縦か横に３つ揃えると、瓶を入手し、さらに特殊なイベントが発動します。

アクションで得た得点と、杖による得点がより多いプレイヤーの勝利です。

boardgame: Jusenkyo Panic: Race to the Top
description:
An insert game from the December Holiday issue of Animerica Magazine. Volume 2, issue 12. Players race to the finish while trying not to land in a pool or get knocked back by other players. Combat is with cards with the highest tally winning a duel.

From the magazine index.

Now everyone has a chance to get their head wet! You've watched the video and read the comic. ..now it's time to play the board game! A hybrid of popular collectible card games such as
Magic: The Gathering and the children's classic Chutes and Ladders, Jusenkyo Panic: Race to the Top! is a wild ride through some very wet country as you fight (and fight and fight and fight) to reach the infamous Chinese Training Ground of Cursed Springs, Jusenkyo, before it's too late.

boardgame: Least I Could Do: The Card Game – Sloppy Seconds
description:
You've played the men, you've played the ladies, now it's time to play the rest. Brave the bars after the gold's been mined and see if you can find a diamond in the rough. Just beware, you might have to get freaky to get their attention.

Fully compatible with the original Least I Could Do: The Card Game, and the Ladies Night! Expansion, Sloppy Seconds lets you play characters like Harry the Homeless Guy and Mick's Mom trying to pick up miscellaneous dates like Llama and the Suck For A Buck girl, using actions like Golden Shower, Movember, and a surprising number of pandas.

Raiders is a game of surviving and gathering resources in order to gain supremacy within a post apocalyptic world of ruin. Each player takes on the role of a Clan who have been surviving within the wastes for the past four years. When a Death Cult set upon eradicating all remaining life within the wastes descend upon the Clans’ homeland, they must gather all the resources they can in order to survive.

The aim of Raiders is to be the last player in the game, or have earned the most points by the end of the tenth turn.
Each Player’s Clan has their own base that they are trying to protect and grow by gathering more resources. Players develop these bases by sending their Raiders to the 11 different locations that represent the wastes. The locations each player’s Raiders are going to that turn are all revealed at the same time, meaning encounters between Raiders are always a surprise. At the end of each phase, those Raiders who survive are able to return to their base with the spoils they have recovered. Players then use the resources they have obtained to build new items upon their base that improve their performance in the rest of the game.

During encounters between Raiders, there are specific rules for Raiders to negotiate rather than simply battling each time. However if players are unable or unwilling to negotiate with one another, a round of combat can quickly break out. Combat rounds are fast paced and easy to follow so as not to overtake the gathering element of the game.

Not simply content with scrounging the wastes for more resources, Raiders can attack enemy bases in an attempt to wipe enemy factions out and gather more resources from their ruins. Each side must therefore be vigilant or risk losing all that they have amassed.

Mont-à-mots Gribouillis est un jeu de dessin où petits et grands doivent faire preuve de dextérité.

Fais tourner le crayon de la roulette et laisse le sort décider de ce que tu devras dessiner : un objet, un personnage ou le contenu d’une carte « Défi surprise ». Dessiner les yeux fermés ou avec ta main la moins habile ou sans lever le crayon sont au nombre des défis à relever. Et sois sans crainte, des cartes de jeu s’adressent aussi aux débutants.

Mont-à-mots Gribouillis is a dexterity drawing game for all ages.

You turn the "Pencil Roulette" and it will give you the type of drawing you will have to do : an object, a character or follow the directions on the "surprise challenge" card. Drawing with your eyes closed or with your non-dominant hand or without lifting the pencil are among the challenges you will have to face. And for the youngsters there's also a set of special cards just for them.
A Slaughterball arena for 2-player only games.

Republica de Bananes is a 3-8 player free-for-all card game, set in a banana republic during the height of the Cold War. Players take on the role of the county’s ruling politicians. Their goal is to achieve a secret Ulterior Motive, while stopping their opponents achieving their own, all while trying to rule the country.

Ulterior Motives include seizing the presidency with the support of a paricular faction, embezzling the nation's budget, manipulating the nation into becoming an outpost of the US or USSR, or causing outright civil war and benefiting from the ensuing chaos.

During a turn each player takes four actions, using cards drawn from the Power deck, and other options based on the political landscape of the nation, such as its current Stability, and the levels of foreign influence. These actions include playing Supporter cards to increase their personal strength, and activating abilities, which can  improve their position or hinder an opponent. Some abilities have broader effects, changing the political climate for all players.

Elections and Coups are a key part of the game. Each turn players jockey for political power, and often must rely on the support of their rivals to gain the power they seek. After negotiations are done players reveal their choices simultaneously. This is often accompanied by a fanfare of cheers, laughs, and cries of anguish from the betrayed.
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
Publisher Blurb

Lucid is a card game where players battle the night using their dreams against the nightmares of their opponents to be the last asleep.

Lucid is a deck of Tarot-sized cards, where players battle the night using their dreams against the nightmares of their opponents to be the last dreamer asleep.

But...

Lucid combines the elements of fast-paced game-play, stunning art, and subtle strategies into a competitive experience unlike anything else. It can be learned in one game and you can be stabbing your friends in the back like a master, by your third. But in the end, the dice decide your fate.

To win the game, you will have to make 3 sequences of 5 cases (horizontally, vertically or diagonally) on the board game.
To do so, you will have 5 cards in hands who will allow you to chose a case on the board.
The used card is discarded and a new one is picked from the pile. The next player do the same and so on...

The rules booklet explain 3 other fun variation games for all tastes.


Pour gagner vous devez bâtir 3 séquences de 5 cases (horizontale, verticale ou diagonale) sur le tableau.
Pour y parvenir, vous aurez 5 cartes en mains qui vous serviront à choisir, à tour de rôle, une des case du tableau.
La carte utilisée est remplacée par une nouvelle carte, le joueur suivant fait de même et ainsi de suite.

Contient également les instructions pour 3 variante de jeux captivantes et pour tous les goûts.

boardgame: Jinmenbana: Chochorina Saibai Keikaku! (人面花 – チョチョリーナ栽培計画！)
description:
User summary
人面花 - チョチョリーナ栽培計画！ (Jinmenbana - Chochorina Saibai Keikaku!) in English is "Human Flower - Chochorina Cultivation Project!".

Chochorina Saibai Keikaku! is a card game with a theme of growing jinmenbana (a flower with human-looking face) called "Chochorina".

To raise the flower, players take turns playing cards, which are either fertilizer (positive growth) or pesticide (negative growth). The active player wins the game when total growth level is exactly seven, but if the flower has been overgrown (more than seven), that player is eaten by the flower and is out of the game, and the next player will be also eliminated unless the growth level is decreased to seven or less.

There are some event cards which make the game complicated and fun.

Two modes of the game are published at the same time. Red Box, where players raise a flower, is for novice gamers. Green Box, where players raise a cactus, is for intermediate gamers.

Publisher's summary
農家となり、人面花である「チョチョリーナ」を咲かせることを目指します。肥料や農薬を使って数値を上げ下げし、「7」ぴったりにした人が勝者です。「7」を越えると農家の方が食べられて敗けです！

User summary
ミソヒトサジ (Misohitosaji) is a Japanese poetry game, for Tanka poems. Tanka is a short Japanese poem consisting of 5 lines with a structure of 5-7-5-7-7 syllables.

The first edition was given a restaurant theme. The cook is a bear named Goro (ゴロー） and the waiter is a rabbit named Nanako （ナナコ）. They appear in the second edition, also styled 短歌カードゲーム　ミソヒトサジ〈定食〉 （Tanka card game MISOHITOSAJI 〈Teishoku〉）, where Teishoku means “set meal”. The second edition enables solitaire play, whereas as the first edition rules are for 2-8 players.

This game enables you to easily craft Japanese Tanka poems. The game consists of cards with 5 syllable and 7 syllable words in Japanese. Mix and match the cards to create a Tanka poem following the 5-7-5-7-7 syllable structure.

There are six different ways to play, including “Utaichimonme" and "ku-meeting". In “Utaichimonme", players exchange cards in a manner similar to Poker in order to craft their poems. In “ku-meeting”, players collaborate to create a single Tanka poem.

In addition, the set contains 40 blank cards which you can use to write your own words to customize your game play. Because of the depth of Japanese text in the game, it would be unplayable without being able to read Japanese.

Publisher's summary
日本の文化「短歌」を簡単に作ることができるゲームです。

ゲームには５文字の言葉と７文字の言葉が入っており、カードを組み合わせて、５・７・５・７・７の短歌を作ります。

ポーカーのようにカードを交換しながら作品を作る「うたいちもんめ」、協力プレイで全員で１首の短歌を相談しながら作る「句ミーティング（ku-meeting）」など、６つの遊び方があります。

また、１セットに４０枚の白紙カードがあり、自分の好きな言葉を書き込んだカードを追加してカスタマイズすることが可能です。
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description: Animals matching game for toddlers.  This matching game is a basic game for young children.  Roll the color die and the animal die and search for the animal block that matches both in that combination, then place it on your board (if you have that animal space available).  First player to fill their animal board wins!

Flicker is a connection game for two players: Black and White. It is played on the intersections (points) of an initially empty square grid (board). The top and bottom edges of the board are colored black; the left and right edges are colored white.

Black plays first, then turns alternate. On your turn, place a stone of your color on an empty point or remove a stone from an enemy weak pair. A weak pair is a pair of like-colored, diagonally adjacent stones with no like-colored stone orthogonally adjacent to both.

You must not place a stone where your opponent just removed a stone from or remove a stone your opponent just placed. Also, you must not remove a stone if either player removed any stone on their single latest turn. Passing is not allowed, but, if you have no legal moves available, your turn is skipped.

You win if there is a chain of orthogonally connected stones of your color touching the two opposite board edges of your color.

If a board position is repeated with the same player to move, the game ends in a draw. This can happen in certain positions with a board full of stones, but no position is known where creating a repetition is the optimal course of play.

To make the game fair, White will have the option, on their first turn only, to swap sides with Black instead of making a regular move.

In Small Planets players take the roll of burnt-out writers which embark on a journey to the distant galaxy in search of inspiration. Planets are unusual: each of them has a distinct theme: western, industrial era etc. Players will send probes, which help them witness amazing things and events. The "richest" player wins the game.

At the beginning of the round players put a counter on each of six planets. Then, active player rolls three dice and puts additional counters on the corresponding planets. Then, players roll their dice and decide which to reroll. After that, active player asks players which planet they want to visit. Players then decide if they put the dice on the planet or not. Player who puts the most dice on the planet takes the counters from them and also gains a favor of planet's inhabitants. When all planets were pointed at by active player, the round ends. Players gather their dice and the next round begins. Game lasts 9 rounds. Player with the most counters at the end of the game wins.

User summary
Nine Planets is a butting game with a science fiction backdrop. 'Butting' is a term used in JP games when more than one player makes the same choice. The action might fall to the next player down, or the players might resolve the demand some way, such as bartering.

Each term consists of purchasing cards to construct your score base whilst reading your opponents and agonizing over different dilemmas.

Players receive points based on the planets to which they are able to successfully warp. If multiple warps to the same planet occur (multiple players choose the same planet), you will not be able to reach your intended destination. However, this does not necessarily mean you will land on a harmful planet (a planet with a negative effect).

Nine Planets is a game for people with the one or more the following characteristics: 
People who like to analyze the current state of the board and make predictions about other players’ behaviour. 
People who like games that require players to adapt quickly to each change. (A game where the situation changes every turn.) 
People who enjoy the thrill of high risk and high return conditions.

description:
Atropos is a mathematical (topological) game based on Sperner's lemma. No mathematical background is necessary in order to play the game. The game starts with a triangulation of a big triangle, whose three nodes are colored (say) red, yellow and green. Players take turns and color one vertex of the triangulation in red, yellow or green. There are two restrictions: (1) whenever possible, each player should color an adjacent vertex of the last-played vertex; (2) when coloring a vertex on an edge of the big triangle, only the two colors of its endpoints may be used, not the color of the opposing vertex.

Sperner's lemma guarantees that the game cannot end in a tie: when all vertices of a triangulation have been legally colored, there will always exist a small tricolored triangle (in fact, an odd number of them).

The mathematical complexity game has been studied and found to be PSPACE-complete in 2010 for the special case of a "regular" triangulation. In general, the game can be played with arbitrary triangulations, and with or without the rule that next moves must be adjacent to the last move.

description:
In Road Warrior: The Tabletop Touring Experience, players start the game by drafting their band members from a pool of near professionals. Based on some of your favorite rockstars, you will find yourself in good company. After assembling their ideal four-piece, they take their act to the road.

Each turn, players traverse a map of America playing shows and building fame. As they travel coast to coast, by drawing event cards they will run into some snags along the way (such as bus breakdowns and cancelled shows), but if luck is on their side they will also encounter some great windfalls (like hit singles or magazine features).

On their turns, players choose from a variety of actions. They will regularly drive around from city to city in their very own tour bus. Performing shows, by a simple roll of the dice, is a steady form of income in the form of points. Failing shows no matter the size will increase the tension within your band, and if you don't keep it under control, it may just lead to your band's demise!

Eventually, each player will find that their growing band has reached a point where they need some help. They can hire a variety of skilled roadies including bus drivers, guitar techs, sound engineers and more.

User Summary

A 2-5 player trivia game where the player who scores the most points wins.  The trivia questions, score, game instructions and game moderator are all contained in the "Guess The Fake" electronic unit that houses everything you need to play the game.

Players will sit at one of the 5 colored stations on the unit and listen for instruction.  The electronic unit randomly picks a trivia question in true/false format and then directs the question to one of the colored stations, which will flash when selected.  The player then presses the button on their color station and then subsequently presses one of two buttons on the middle of the unit.  The green button signifies true, while the red button signifies false.  The player also has the option of challenging another player to answer the question.  Answering wrong equals a two point penalty for the challenged player, while answering correctly nets the player 1 point.

The electronic unit also mixes in "quick" challenges randomly.  In this type of challenge, the electronic will ask a question than any player an answer, but only the player that pushes their color station button first gets to answer the question and score a point.

The winner is the player with the most points, and a typical game is the first player to 20 points.

The year is 2461, mankind has expanded beyond Earth and colonized Venus, Mars and Titan.

Our sun erupted into activity, volatile solar storms raged across our star’s surface while massive solar flares bombard our system, causing havoc with our technologies and knocking down the security protocols of the SysNet.

Zero Day War is a 2-4 player card game. Players take the roles of hackers from different planets who are taking advantage of a security malfunction in the SysNet.

Katra Mpantsaka (mpantsaka from tsaka "limited") is a mancala game played by the Sakalava people, which occupy the Western edge of the Madagascar from Toliara in the south to Sambirano in the north.

The game is typically played on a 4x8 board with 2 seeds in each hole, but it has also been described on 4x4 and 4x6 boards and with 3 seeds in each hole.

On his turn a player takes the contents of one of his holes and distributes them, one by one, either clockwise or counterclockwise into consecutive holes around the two rows of his own side. (For the remainder of the turn the direction of sowing may not be changed during a turn).

If the last stone falls into a non-empty hole, its contents are distributed in another lap continuing in the same direction as before.

The first lap must be started with an occupied hole of the inner row unless the inner row is empty. When the inner row is empty, a player may start from the back row.  Unlike many other manacala games a move may start with a singleton.

The move ends when the last stone is dropped into an empty hole (mandri "to sleep").  However, if the last piece is placed in the inner row, then it captures the opponent's pieces on the inner row and the sowing continues play continues is if the last stone had landed in an non-empty row.

The game ends, when a player has no seeds left.

The player who captured all of the seeds, wins the game. The loser is said to be "lazy (kamo).

The fate of the solar system is at stake as several cosmic factions struggle for supremacy. As a commander of a star fleet, it is your duty to position your forces at the right time at the right planet and claim it for your faction. Beware, the strongest faction will claim the entire planet and there is no space for runners-up in the solar system.

The solar system consists of 12 planets, each of them surrounded by six grid points where battle stations may be placed. Some grid points border several planets, hence making their battle stations more powerful. You claim a planet if you have the most battle stations at the end of a round. You win if you claim 3/4/5 planets (5/4/3 players).

Each round you compete for the next planet in order. You do so by playing cards representing either two forces (black) or one force (white). One black card may be played each turn together with none or one white card. The black cards must follow suit while the white cards may be played together with any suit.

When you pass you take the tricks of all forces with a majority and places one battle station for each trick. Also, an early pass gives you an early pick of new cards from a drawing row. However, it makes the remaining tricks easier to get for the other players.

When the round ends, you claim the planet if you have the most battle stations around it. Also, any previous planets that were tied in previous rounds are reassessed to see if the majority has changed. (Remember that a battle station may reach more planets.) One of the tricks allows you to change the order of the planets to take advantage of well positioned battle stations.

description:
Shhh…it! is a cheerful and foolish card game that will cheer up countless evenings, as it has been doing in Italy for centuries! The game was indeed conceived in the Middle Ages by a group of aspiring artists. In those days, it was customary to throw excrements out the balcony to the square, once the work had ended. One day, an artist was hit by it and his friends slammed their hands on the table and called out “Shit!”. The unlucky artist decided to use it to develop a game that had immediate success since then.

First, two decks of cards are to be prepared. One of the two decks only include characters cards to be dealt to players, the other one includes all the other cards, representing the points, or so called tons of shit!

The second deck has to be positioned at the center of the table so that it is possible for players to easily reach it with their hands.

The game begins with the counter-clockwise cards dealing, 4 cards each player. Players must quickly choose one of the cards in their hand and pass it to the opponent to the right while picking up at the same time the card that the opponent to the left is handing over.

When a player has a four of a kind (poker) hand must immediately pound the hand firmly on the center deck on the table, while shouting: Shit! All the other players must pound their hands on top of the one who pounded the deck first and called Shit!

The last player pounding the deck has to draw a card from it, which value represents the “quantity” (tons) of Shit picked up.

Watch out! The same rules applies if a player accidentally touches the central deck and subsequently has to draw a Shit! card right away. For this reason, it is possible to bluff by shouting Shit! or by pretending to pound the deck, in order to induce opponents to pound the deck too, by mistake, and therefore be forced to drawing the Shit! card. The winner is the Player summing up less “quantity” (tons) of Shit, once the cards central deck is depleted.

Wages of War is a strategy game of medieval siege warfare! Manage resources to produce weapons, and hire soldiers to wield them in battle as you send wave after wave to attack the enemy castle! The player who inflicts the most damage on the castle by the time it is destroyed shall win the game and seize the glory!

In each round, the active player will issue an Order to one of the 5 Encampments surrounding the castle. Each player benefits from the Order based on how many Buildings they have in the Encampment. Use these orders to your advantage in order to muster your army.

Train swordsmen, axemen, and bowmen, and send them to attack the castle with the support of siege towers, trebuchets, and cannons!

Plan your attack wisely, for when the Attack Order is issued you must not only beat the enemy force, but you must be able to sustain the casualties yourself.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
MALU is an abstract strategy board game for 2 or 4 players.

Long ago, high up in the Himalayan mountains, a group of traveler monks known as the Malu, wandered through its snowy peaks. Their days were filled with prayer, meditation, and training. As part of their training, they played a single board game. The game was elegant in its simplicity, yet complicated in its mastery. For generations the game was a closely guarded secret and used by the monks to train new acolytes in the art of traversing the narrow passages and treacherous peaks of the Himalayas.

One day, while the monks were attempting to cross a particularly narrow bit of mountain, a copy of the game was swept away in a snowstorm. It was brought down the mountain by an avalanche and eventually recovered by a small boy from a remote village. The boy was so intrigued by the game that he left his home to wander the mountains, track down the monks and learn the ways of the Malu.

Many years passed and the boy eventually grew to be an intelligent man, possessing all the secrets of the Malu. He eventually returned to civilization and brought with him the game of the Malu. By sheer chance (or perhaps fate), he has deemed you a worthy challenger. It is now time for you to test your skills... Do you have what it takes to out-think and out-maneuver your opponent and cross the mountain pass in MALU?


The objective of the game is to move both your pawns across the map to the other side of the board and exit the map. The first player to have both their pawns exit the map wins.

The mechanics of the game are simple enough that anyone can pick up and start playing, but the depth of skill and many possible strategies to employ, allows for seasoned players to truly be masters of MALU.

Each turn, a player must move their pawn and place a neutral tile onto the board. As you place your tiles, adjoining tiles create paths that your pawns can move along. As your pawn moves, you have the ability to pick up those tiles and reroute them to your advantage... either as a path to the other side or as a means to block your opponents. But be careful... if you get too close, your opponent can snatch those tiles away from you and use them for themselves! You can also place walls of ice and mountain peaks as obstacles or knock down walls for strategic advantages.

Like many other classic games... first one to the finish wins! But in MALU, you will need to muster all your skill and cunning to make it past your opponent and be the first across the mountain pass.

Nobody likes a punch in the face!

Except for the crowds in New Hope City! Because of the demographics in New Hope City the entertainment entrepreneurs wanted a sporting event that would appeal to everyone, both Basics (Humans) and Aliens, so they chose boxing.

Alien Fight Night is game within a game. Not only is it a fast paced stand-alone boxing game, it's a great diversion for your Urban Renewal Stars. They can supplement their income as a fighter or maybe just be a spectator. It's perfect for generating Encounters like those used in New Hope City, PI.

Alien Fight Night can be played solo or head to head against your friends and inside you'll find:

Fourteen building blocks to create your own unique fighter.
Eighty three pre-generated opponents, both humans and alien.
All the Enhancements from Stims, to Loops, to Physical Enhancements to level the playing field.
Rules for wagering on fights.
A full color Ring and eight full color counters to represent your fighters.

The Evil Sect expansion adds a sixth character into the game. Whoever chooses to play as this character will start the game as an evil character and must destroy his opponents mercilessly or lure them to the side of evil!

Includes:
Evil Sect character card
Evil Sect Henchman
Basic Techniques (x3)
Evil Sect Signature Technique
Evil Sect Special Item
Evil Sect Favorite Food (x3)
Evil Sect Quick Reference Card (KS Version Only)
Evil Sect Duel Sleeve (KS Version Only)
Nemesis Token (x4)
Character Standee
Rules sheet

This game was crowdfunded but never delivered to backers.

Galaxy of Trian is a dynamic tile-based game in which players act as commanders of alien races. They vie for control over nebulas and planetary systems, which they level up to gain an advantage over other players. What is at stake is taking possession of the powerful technology left behind by the unequaled race of the Trian. Over the course of the game, players use beautifully illustrated, double-sided tiles to create outer space and fill it with nebulas, planetary systems, and gas clouds. Using their emissaries and ships, they explore and take control of these parts of the galaxy which have hitherto remained undiscovered. With a ﬂeet of vessels at their disposal, they fight for dominance over areas filled with rich mineral deposits. Players can choose from 8 diﬀerent alien superpowers, each characterized by a unique constellation of features and skills. Close your ranks and enter the race for the hegemony over the Galaxy of Trian. What is at stake is taking possession of the powerful technology left behind by the ancient yet unequaled race of the Trian, and conquering the entire Galaxy!

boardgame: The Nebula From Hell: First Expansion
description: The First Expansion adds tons of new cards that change up the pace, add new challenges, and deliver a whole new amount of pain to an already hard game.

The Player Expansion provides 15 additional cards to support 5-6 player games.  Featuring Vikings, Ancient Egyptians and Tiki Characters - cards have been produced to seamlessly integrate to your standard 5ive: King's Court Deck.
