For returning to standard 1-4 player games, 15 cards can be removed from the deck again, ensuring that 3 full 'courts' are removed (3 each of Block, Destroy, Recover, Draw, and Discard).

Winner of the 8th Board Game Design Competition Ciutat de Granollers (2015).

Players move their zeppelins across the board by using their turn actions; they move them, increase or decrease its flight altitude and try to make the most of the events that are being placed on the board. They also use special events against their opponents. The object is to get the 5 mission cards in the desired order.

A player, by spending an action, can place a normal event -Volcano,Festival or Wind- on an allowed terrain section. Normal events, once thrown, remain on the board.A player can link winds.

Special events are mixed with the rest of the events and they also cost an action. All special events are discarded after being used.

The player who manages to land on the fifth missions spending the lesser number of actions will be the winner.

French:
Pilotez un zeppelin à travers le plateau! Utilisez de façon intelligente dans votre propre intérêt et/ou contre autres joueurs des éruptions volcaniques et des vents enchaînables et amusants afin de modifier les routes et être le premier à achever les missions.

Équilibre entre stratégie et amusement. On a l’impression de jouer en 3 dimensions. La difficulté de chercher les routes optimales augmente à fur et à mesure que la partie avance. De l’haute interaction parmi les joueurs qui utilisent événements pour modifier le plateau.

Les zeppelins se déplacent, à tour de rôle, sur un plateau qui, à cause des décisions des joueurs, devient de plus en plus compliqué. L’un des joueurs gagne de manière serrée.

Description from the publisher:

Beat Down Wrestling is a family friendly 2 - 4 player game. It features 10 original wrestler cards.Choose your wrestler and battle your opponent. Take turns rolling the "1,2,3" dice and move through the Beat Down Universe. Land on a "Story" space and pick up one of 40 "Story Cards". A story card may be beneficial or it may be detrimental. You can save some cards, and some cards must be played immediately.

Get Into The Ring
If you land on a "Fight" space, you have the option of challenging your opponent to a match. Take turns drawing fight cards until you have an even amount of cards and some one has reached 10 damage points. The winner will pick up a " Winner Card "and the loser will go back to the Start or the Mid-Card Checkpoint.

To Win
To win you must enter the ring and consecutively roll a 1,2,3. If you roll a 1 but not a 2 your opponent gets a turn and can still challenge you to a fight. On your next turn you must roll a 2 and then a 3. When you do not roll the consecutive numbers you are vulnerable to be challenged to a fight and lose the progress you have made on the board and in the rolling process.

boardgame: Aeon's End: Tabletop Day 2017 Promos – Drown in Flames / Glyph Enigma
description:
ITTD 2017 Promo Cards (Indie Boards and Cards): This limited edition set of promo cards add to your deck for Aeons End. This set includes one new minion, Glyph Enigma, and five copies of the spell Down the Flames, along with the randomizer for the spell.

Drown in Flames - Spell
Cost - 6
Effect - Cast: Deal 4 damage to a minion or the nemesis. You may lose 2 charges. if you do, repeat this.

Glyph Enigma - Minion
HP - 7
Effect - PERSISTENT: Gravehold suffers 1 damage. If the nemesis is tier 2 or higher, Gravehold suffers 2 additional damage

Space Defenders is a tribute to the arcade games of the 1970s and 1980s. Up to four players must defend earth from hordes of attacking invaders from outer space by eliminating all of them before they reach the bottom row of the board.

Description from the designer:

The pod station has suffered a catastrophic system failure. The crew is almost entirely missing, but those who survived the destruction made it to the main hub. There's only enough power left to launch one evacuation pod. You must begin to reconstruct the pod station. Move from pod to pod as the station takes on a new shape. Search through laboratories to find an evacuation key and other survival necessities. Access computers to find evacuation codes and detect problems within the station. Then, get to your evacuation pod to escape before it's too late!

In this modular board game, players must gain access to laboratories and computers in order to collect the cards necessary to evacuate. But, collecting isn't the only goal, as you must safely navigate your way to the correct Evacuation Pod to achieve the win. Fires, hull breaches, and power failures may need to be dealt with along the way.

The game can be played solo as a survival game or with friends as a cooperative or competitive game. There are also several alternate game "scenarios" that alter rules, remove cards, and add more challenging limitations to the game.

Description from the publisher:

This card does something different than every other card introduced so far in the game. It has an Ongoing ability (as indicated by the blue frame and the word "Ongoing" at the bottom-right corner). Contrary to immediate effects which apply once when you play the cards, or Harvest abilities for which you have to pay the cost each Harvest phase, ongoing abilities are always "on". You can take advantage of them as many times as they allow you to!

In this particular case, the Cabbages ask you to build Livestock cards around them. The more you build, the more Food they will generate! (and don't forget those nice 2 victory points at the end).
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
Excavate! is a game for 2-4 players. Each player takes on the role of a unique archeologist assigned to a fossil-rich pit that was recently discovered. Players will use their tactics to manipulate the board and attempt to create the most highly Acclaimed museum. As players continue the game, the play area will be changing as the players dig through the cards and find the sets needed to benefit them the most. The gameplay is about 30-45 minutes, and because of the simple rules, children as young as 8 years old can play it and be a competitor. However, this game offers a deep strategy level so even adults can compete. Easy to play, and very little luck is provided.

Overview of gameplay: players will take 5 actions each turn.  These actions can be any of the following: dig, move, display or sell. When a player takes a dig action, they take one of two adjacent cards to the piece and flip it face up on the other pile.  Any fave up cards can be interacted wroth by a player on their turn.  They can display it in their museum,  sell it for money,  or simply ignore it.  As players display various sets of fossils,  they will increase in the number of Acclaim points.  The player wit the most Acclaim points wins Excavate!

Deck building game about the race to the White House.
Players take on the role of running a campaign to make your candidate the next president of the USA. Each party will recruit strategies to secure states and earn electoral votes.

Candidate Land is a 2 player Deck building game (3-4 player with the expansion). Using the actual Electoral votes as victory points, this is a fresh look at political games. If you are a fan of deck building or political games, this is right up your alley! With over 220 cards, this is the first of its kind and has created many fans of deck building and political games!

Can you outwit, out-strategize, out-slander the other Candidates and become the Next President of the United States?

Legends of the Four Kingdoms is a captivating strategy game. Nature, technology, power, magic! Learn how to tame the Four Kingdoms.

Designed to be played with 2-8 players, you will only need one 40 card deck to play the game.

HOW TO PLAY

Each player is dealt three cards.

Cards in your hand represent the warriors you have control over. After dealing 3 cards to each player, the next card is dealt face up on the playing surface, and the remaining deck is placed face down. This card is the leading/crown Kingdom, and represents the trump suit for the game.

Play one card each going clockwise, player to the left of the dealer starts. After playing a card, draw one more card from the face down pile.

Winning a hand:

In ascending order:  fewest to most soldiers (they are worth 0 points) then the following: Knight (J) with 2 points; War Princess (Q) with 3 points; King (K) with 4; Supreme Warrior (3*) with 10; and the Kingdom's secret weapon worth 11 points.

If any Leading/Crown Kingdom (trump) has been played, the player who played the highest valued trump wins.  If no Leading/Crown Kingdom (trumps) have been played, the player who played the highest card of the lead suit wins.

Players are not required to follow suit.

Once the winner of a round is determined, that player collects the played cards, and places them face down in a pile. Each player maintains his/her own pile. Then, each player draws a card from the remaining deck, starting with the player who won the trick, proceeding counter-clockwise. The player who won the round leads the next hand.

The winner is the player who won the most important battles and has the most points.  There are 120 points total.

Legends of the Four Kingdoms reimplements the traditional Italian trick-taking game Briscola, with modern artwork and new suits.

boardgame: Marvel HeroClix: Nick Fury Agent of Shield Fast Forces
description:
Standalone set for Heroclix, which can be used as a starter set or as an expansion. 
Contains characters from Tv show Agents of Shield: Agent Phil Coulson, Agent Melinda May, Skye/Agent Daisy Johnson(spoiler:)) ,Dr. Jemma Simmons, Dr. Leopold Fitz , Director Nick Furry and stats cards for all miniatures.

boardgame: Marvel HeroClix: Captain America Civil War Starter Set
description: Standalone set for Heroclix inspired by the movie Captain America Civil War. Can be played alone or combined with other HeroClix sets. Contains minis for Captain America, Winter Soldier, Iron Man, Falcon, Black Widow, and Black Panther + character cards.

boardgame: DC HeroClix: Streets of Gotham – Birds of Prey
description: Standalone set for HeroClix. Can be played as a starter set or as an expansion. Includes Oracle, Black Canary, Lady Blackhawk, Hawk, Dove, Huntress and cards for all characters.

boardgame: DC HeroClix: Batman and His Greatest Foes Fast Forces
description: Standalone set for HeroClix. Can be played by itself, as a starter set, or as an expansion. Includes miniatures of Batman, Joker, Penguin, Riddler, Mr. Freeze, Harley Quinn, and character cards.

boardgame: Star Wars Miniatures: The Clone Wars Starter Set
description: Standalone set of Star Wars Miniatures. Contains rules, double-sided map, die, character cards, and miniatures for General Kenobi, 2 Clone Troopers, Count Dooku, and 2 Droids. Can be played by itself or as an expansion.

In this game each player forms a director of a fine art museum. Your task is simple yet so difficult to achieve, collect as many artpieces as you just can with the lowest prices. The box contains 32 cards with various fine art paintings on the front side, and 55 black plastic chips that represent resources.

The exhibition is an auction game, where your goods are represented by the black chips. You can pay a chips to avoid picking up the current face-up card, or you can pick up the current face-up card and reveal the next card from the deck.

The paintings are numbered from 3 to 35, with each card counting for a number of points equal to its face value. Runs of two or more cards only count as the lowest value in the run - but nine cards are removed from the deck before starting, so be careful looking for connectors. Each chip is worth -1 point, but they can be even more valuable by allowing you to avoid drawing that unwanted card.

The winner is who collect the most paintings for the lowest price.

This is a strategic and operational scale game of the central European theaters of the Napoleonic Wars (France, Austria, Prussia, N. Italy, 1805-1815).

Napoleon and his Marshals covers 1805-1815 in France and Central Europe. Other expansions are underway that will add the Peninsular War, the Patriotic War in Russia and maps of Scandinavia and the rest of Italy. The game is strategic and operational. Each strategic turn is two months, broken down into 6 10-day operational turns.Forces are division level (5,000 men) per factor, with artillery, cavalry, Cossacks, and guerillas as additional specialized units. Leaders are included at the National Military Leader (Napoleon, Kutusov, etc.), Army commanders, and marshals/corps commanders. Combat units for 11 nations are included. In the expansions the naval war is added, with Nelson, other British Admirals, along with French, Spanish, and Russian admirals, and the navies of those countries and Denmark and Sweden, along with the armies of Spain and Portugal.

This is the basic game, required to play all other expansions and the campaign game, which will run from January 1805 through the end of 1815 or the final victory. Included is a 31" x 23.5" map, printed on plastic coated reinforced "banner" paper (which is rolled up for storage), and 5 counter sheets of 88 counters, printed front and back, and slightly larger than traditional counters at 0.6" square. Map scale is about 28 miles to 38 miles per hex (depending on where you are on the map).

During the Strategic Turns, nations collect taxes (production) and expend it on organization and units, including leader activation, replacements, new units, mobilization, sea lift capacity, foreign aid, supplies, and other items. Diplomacy may also occur. During the ensuing 6 operational turns, leaders and units are moved, battles fought, sieges undertaken and possibly diplomacy is conducted.

There are six scenarios in the game: 1805 (vs Austria, Britain and Russia), 1806-07 (vs Prussia, Britain and Russia), 1805-1807 (vs all four), 1809 (vs Austria, Russia and Britain), 1813-1815 (vs all four), and 1815 (the 100 days of Napoleon's second reign). A player-turn takes about 5-15 minutes, so along with a strategic turn, a full "season" (two months is the length of a strategic turn and its associated operational turns) takes about 1.5 - 2.0 hours - less if there are no battles, more if there are many big battles.

A fast interactive battle of narration, between 2 to 6 wizard storytellers, on 4 fantasy themed stories.     +

In this game, wizard storytellers compete in forming and completing four different stories. They all have great ideas to propose for each story theme, but who has the best ones? You are a fantastic wizard storyteller: Manage your story cards – place them on the board as parts of stories or discard them. Gather fantasy coins, buy spells and cast them wisely. Your goal is to add parts of stories and complete them, in order to score story points. The more spectacular a story is, the more points it’s worth, but to whom? The best contributors score, but the storyteller with the final word gets extra credit!

boardgame: Napoleon and his Marshals: The Peninsular War
description:
The first addition to Napoleon and his Marshals expands the series to include the wars in Spain and Portugal from 1805-1815, with scenarios for 1807-1814 and 1809-1814. The basic set (Napoleon and his Marshals) is required to play. The complete British army and navy for the basic game is added, along with the Spanish army and navy and the Portuguese army. A 20.75 x 16 inch map of Spain, Portugal, Southern France and the adjacent seas is included along with two 88-counter sheets of 0.6" square game counters, printed front and back. Scale is approximately 38 miles per hex.

Like its parent game, this is a diceless combat system, based on our algorithm for battles in that period. The gameplay is identical to that of Napoleon and his Marshals, with some minor modifications for the scenarios.

Rules are the same as for Napoleon and his Marshals.

boardgame: Star Trek: Attack Wing – Mutiny Resource
description:
The Mutiny Resource is a participation prize given to players who participated in the "In a Mirror, Darkly" episode Organized Play Event. It is considered a Resource, and costs 10 Squadron Points.

When using this resource, you place 3 Mission Tokens on it at the start of the game.  Once per round, during an opposing ship's Perform Action step, if your opponent declares it is performing an action from it's action bar, you may spend a token to prevent it from performing that action or any other free actions for the round.  Once all three tokens are spent, the resource is discarded.

Description from the publisher:

Zero Sum: The Card Game is a competitive, strategy card game set in a futuristic galaxy where seven mega-corporations have taken hold of humanity's future. Deeply embroiled in a not-so-friendly competition, these corporations are looking for every opportunity to gain an advantage over their rivals.

As an agent of one of these corporations, you are tasked with expanding your corporation's foothold across the galaxy. Entrusted with a generous number of funds, you are expected to build up a respectable presence on the remote, mineral-rich planet of Zatheon Alpha 3. As you achieve success, you will be promoted up the ranks, gaining access to new abilities and funds. Of course, you'll desperately need all the help you can get if you are to bend the hostile climate of Zatheon Alpha 3 to your will. Your corporate overlords have the utmost faith in your abilities.

Do not prove them wrong.

Description from the rulebook:

Poltava 1709 is a two player Tactical level block wargame with one player playing the Swedish side and the other playing the Russian side. Each side has a prescribed number of units representing infantry, horse, dragoon, light cavalry, and artillery formations. The game is played on a stylized game board showing the area in which the battle of Poltava was fought.

Poltava 1709 is a promotional introductory wargame that was given to visitors of the Armémuseum Krigsspel Exhibit (Army Museum War Games Exhibit) in Stockholm, Sweden, 2017. It is based on the designer's Fields of Battle game and Mike DeSanto's Postcard from the Revolution game.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

description: Diagonals is played with black and white stones on an 8x8 grid (i.e., the intersections of a 7x7 gameboard). Each turn a player places a stone on an empty space; there are no captures. When a diagonal line is full, the player who placed the last stone on the diagonal line scores the number of friendly stones on that line. The player with the most points wins when all spaces are occupied.

Kechi is an abstract strategy game played with black and white stones on a 13x13 grid. On each turn, a player moves his stones a toral of six cells. Each stone must finish its move on an available empty space or one occupied by an enemy stone (which is then captured). The player who cannot make a move on his turn loses. When a stone leaves a space, that space is marked as unplayable. In this is resembles Hey, That's My Fish! or Amazons.

Kingdom is an abstract strategy game played on a 9x9 board with a King piece and stacks of 1-4 checkers called Knights. Pieces can move to an adjacent empty cell or jump over an adjacent piece. Knights may only jump over a Knight smaller than themselves, except a one-high Knight may jump over a four-high Knight. Pieces capture by moving into an enemy piece's space. Any piece may capture a King, and Kings may capture Knights, but Knights may not capture Knights (they only block each other). The player who captures the enemy king or occupies the enemy king's starting location wins.

Game series has been retitled, 'V-Sabotage'.

Celcius operation for V-Commandos

June 1944

The "Festung Le Havre" is a crucial point in the Atlantic Wall. Its port shelters several offshore minelayers that have already laid too many of their naval explosive devices. The admiral in charge of the area is currently conducting an inspection of the harbour.

« Sabotage the dry docks where the minelayers are being repaired and kidnap the admiral. Run to the blockhaus and reduce the enemies to silence. Then retreat inland. »

boardgame: Ariadne's Thread: Escape the Maze Button by Button!
description:
Each player starts with 5 buttons.When it’s your turn, place a button on the thread if you have one that is the same colour, shape or size, as the last button and one oh the adjacent buttons, when you reach the buttonholes with arrows. If you manage to place a button, then you draw a new one from the bag; otherwise you have to discard one.
Extra buttons can be won during the game as prizes.
The winner is the player who gets out of the maze with the most buttons!
Younger players can play Tower, a game that helps them learn colours and recognize shapes and sizes through touch.

WALL TO WALL – BUILD YOUR VICTORY BRICK BY BRICK!
Two players, one wall, opposing sides, different colours. Who will place the most bricks of the same colour in a row, diagonally and touching?
The bricks and the die of Wall to wall  are made of wood coming from eco-friendly forestry in respect of the natural and social environment. (100% FSC®)
Wall to wall is a safe, eco-friendly game realised with nontoxic paint and made in Italy.
  
COMPONENTS:  
42 wooden bricks with colours on two sides
1 wooden die with 6 sides
1 instruction sheet

INSTRUCTIONS:

HOW TO BUILD THE WALL
The bricks on the bottom row - the one touching the playing area – must be placed adjacent to one another, with a maximum of 10 bricks being used. From the second row up, each brick must be placed half over two different bricks. Unlike the first row, bricks do not need to be placed adjacent to each other. Only bricks that have no other bricks above them can be moved.
RULES
Goal: Place as many bricks as possible in your colour on your side of the wall in a row, diagonally and touching.
PREPARATION AND STARTING
The two players must sit facing each other, with the bricks placed to one side of the playing area in a pile. Each player choses one of the colours: green, fuchsia or light blue (the colour that is not chosen is neutral). Thefirst player to go is the one that, throwing the die, throws their colour first. After this, the players alternate turns. The wall is built between the two players. The bricks are placed such that the two coloured sides of the bricks face the players.
TURNS
The player whose turn it is throws the die and performs the corresponding action:
- Green brick: Take a brick from the pile that has AT LEAST one green side and PLACE IT on the wall.
- Fuchsia brick: PLACE a new brick that has AT LEAST one fuchsia side.
- Light blue brick: PLACE a new brick that has AT LEAST one light blue side.
 - Little girl: PLACE a brick that has NO SIDES in your colour.
- Grey brick: Take a brick from the WALL and MOVE it to where you want on the wall, or simply TURN IT AROUND.
NB: Once a brick has been picked up, that brick is the one that must be moved or turned. If the wall hasn't yet been started, then you forfeit your turn.
- Little boy: CHOOSE one of the actions from the five options. NB: If the action indicated on the die cannot be done (e.g., the die shows fuchsia, but all the bricks with at least one fuchsia side have already been used), then you forfeit your turn.
END OF GAME
The game ends when all the bricks have been placed on the wall. Points are awarded as follows: 10 points for the longest diagonal (in a player's colour on their side of the wall); 8 points for the longest row; 6 points for the most touching bricks. If there is a tie, then thepoints are shared (e.g. if both players have 8 touching bricks, then each one gets 3 points).

crazy palace - Two "crazy" builders with one goal: build the highest palace without it tumbling down!


COMPONENTS: 21 wooden blocks, 1 die, 2 coloured little figures

INSTRUCTIONS:
Distribute 10 blocks each and place the printed block on the game table, laying it with the word LARGE facing upwards: this will be the base of the building. Choose your pawn and decide who starts first by throwing the dice: the biggest size starts (S, M, and L).
 The one who starts throws the dice and, using only one hand, positions a block on any part of the building, placing it so that it rests with the face indicated by the dice (S, M, and L). Each time you position a block you must place the pawn on any part of the block you have just placed and then pass the turn to your rival.
 To position a new block you can move your pawn, taking it temporarily from the building. You can position a new block on one of your rival’s blocks, but without moving your pawn.

SNAKES - Make the most snakes to win!


COMPONENTS:
16 wooden dice:  12 with green snakes, 4 with orange snakes
1 instruction sheet

INSTRUCTIONS:

GOAL
Complete more snakes than your opponent.
PREPARATION
Each player gets 8 dice: 6 green and 2 orange. The youngest player starts by throwing a green die and placing it on the playing surface. This will be the start of the first snake.
TURNS
Each player takes a turn to throw one of their dice and line it up with the part of the snake already on the playing area, thus continuing to create the snake and, hopefully, completing it. A snake is complete when it has a head and a tail. A snake only needs to have two pieces - a head and a tail - to be complete. For each completed snake, the player who placed the final piece earns 1 point. Once a snake has been completed, it is left on the playing area. If you cannot attach the section of the snake obtained from throwing one of your dice, then start a new snake. If there is more than one unfinished snake on the playing area, then the person whose turn it is can decide which snake to continue or complete. After each player has had one turn, it is possible to throw one of the orange dice at any time during the game. If this orange die ends up on one of the standard “head” or “tail” faces, then the rules are exactly the same as for the green dice. If the orange die ends up on a "+", you can take one die (including another orange one, if one is already on the playing area) from the end of a snake in the playing area; you also get to keep the orange die and get to throw again, using the die of your choice, straightaway. If the die that is taken from a snake is removed from a completed snake, then person who originally won a point for completing that snake keeps the point. If the orange die ends up on, you have to give your orange die to the other player and forfeit your turn.

END OF GAME

Every time you complete a snake, you get 1 point. The game ends when all the pieces have been used. The winner is the player with the most points, i.e. the person who completed the most snakes.

THE WINNER IS THE ONE WHO REACHES FIRST THE RAINBOW! THE GAME OF COLOURS

Wooden game. Rainbow is a wooden educational game based on colours and chance. The age is not important: children and grownups playing together have the same opportunities to win.
 COMPONENTS 3 dices, 1 scorekeeper, 2 pawns made of maple wood deriving from eco-friendly forests 
 
INSTRUCTIONS:

Pick the one who throws the dices first, who moves the green pawns and who the light blue one. Both pawns start from the 0 position behind the start. The dices are thrown in turn. To score a point you have to throw one of the winning combinations.
 The one who scores first 5 points and reaches the rainbow with its pawn wins.

WINNING COMBINATIONS:
 3 of the same color
2 of the same color and joker
3 jokers
1 color and 2 jokers

BUILD THE HIGHEST TOWER EVER!

The Colour Towers is a wooden board game based on the colour theory! It is an educational game for children and for the whole family. It is also an unusual gift.
  
COMPONENTS: 34 pawns made of 100% beechwood (100% FSC®) in 6 colours, 1 bag, 1 brochure containing the instructions and the explanation of the colour theory.
 INSTRUCTIONS:

Pick a pawn from the bag. There are three ways to conquer the pawns on the field or on top of towers of the other players: 1. Easy: you conquer the pawns of the SAME COLOUR as the one you picked from the bag (example: if you pick a red pawn you can conquer a red pawn) if there is any free on the field or on the top of the towers of the other players. 2. Average: you can conquer pawns according to the SUM OF COLOURS, meaning that you can conquer pawns of the colours that when mixed together make the colour of the pawn you picked (example: a violet pawn can conquer red and blue pawns). 3. Advanced: you can get the pawns of the COMPLEMENTARY COLOUR of the pawn you picked. If you cannot conquer any pawn, leave the picked pawn on the field. The conquered pawns are to be piled up to form a tower. The player who build the highest tower wins the game. In the package you will find the COMPLETE INSTRUCTIONS AND THE COLOUR THEORY

T - Boats Challenge
NEW BATTLESHIP!

It is a wooden board game for all ages based on strategy: there is no luck, only tactics!


COMPONENTS: 1 wooden game board, 8 wooden ship-pawns in 2 colours


INSTRUCTIONS:

Goal: Eliminate all the rival sheeps from the board.
 Preparation: Arrange all the ships on the board on the starting position (1). Pick the colours. The yellow fleet always starts.

How to move the ships: The game board is composed by 81 spots. One ship must always fill three spots. Play in turns. The player shall move one ship making a 90° rotation in any direction pivoting on one of the three spots filled by the ship or shift it of one spot lengthways. (2) The ship can only fill free spots without crossing other ships. It is allowed to pass over a ship if the ending spots are free. (3) If the player touches a ship is obliged to make a move. 
 How to eliminate the rival ships: The goal of your moves is to build a "T" across one enemy ship by placing one ship perpendiculalry and centerd to the rival one building a "T" (4). In this way the rival ship must be taken from the board. If you build a "T" unintentionally and don't see it, the other player is allowed not to tell and to eliminate your ship at the next turn without making any other move. If with one move you manage to make more "Ts" you can remove all the involved ships. 
 End of the game: If a player can eliminate all the rival ships or prevent them from moving further, he wins the game. If a player gives up the other one wins. The game shall be considered even is both players cannot move further.

ADORN YOUR TREE WITH THE SAME COLOUR CANOPIES TO WIN!

Throw the two dice to get new canopies for your tree, but watch out you might lose some!The first player to complete their tree in one colour wins. 
 COMPONENTS: 
4 wooden trees, bare,
20 coloured wooden canopies (5 for each season),
1 cardboard basket,
2 wooden dice
 INSTRUCTIONS:

The first player to complete their tree with 5 canopies of one season (colour) wins. All of the 20 canopies are placed in the basket and each player takes a bare tree. The youngest player goes first by picking up a canopy and placing it on their tree. The game then continues in a clockwise direction. A player throws the two dice that show the ACTION to perform (e.g. take a new canopy and put it on your tree) and WHERE to take the canopy from (e.g. from one of the other player's trees) or WHAT to take (e.g. a green or pink canopy). The game ends when a player, the winner, completes their tree with all 5 canopies of the same season (same colour).

boardgame: Forgotten Battles: Scenarios for WWII Spearhead Rules
description:
Subtitled 'Scenarios for Spearhead WW-II Rules', these additional scenarios cover the Eastern Front.

Although designed primarily for Spearhead rules, these scenarios are playable with any set of WW-II rules that provides Divisional organizations and uses the Platoon as the smallest combat element.

Description from the publisher:

Most of us take our local Pubs for granted - yet throughout history they have played an important part in Britain's social life and the landscape of town and country. But the names and signs of Pubs have a rich and fascinating story to tell, which is celebrated in this highly original card game. Easy to learn and tremendous fun to play!

Players start with a hand of 5 cards and must play them all to win. To play a card you must play a pub whose symbol matches the last card played. Symbols form suits and there are 11 in the game. There are also some special cards to spice things up for the next player (miss a turn, reverse play direction). A series of 3x cards (eg 3 Barrels) also require the next player to pick up 3 cards. This can be defeated with another 3x card causing the next player to pick up 6 and so on.

When down to the last card, the player must call "time gentleman please" or "last order" or gain a penalty card. When the last card is played, the winner scores the value of all other players remaining cards. Rounds continue until target score (eg 50) is achieved.

Squarriors: The Card Game is a tabletop game based off of the comic book series, Squarriors. In Squarriors TCG, there is no deck of cards to draw from, instead all cards start either in play or in your hand.

Your "deck" (known as your Tribe in S:TCG) consists of 20 points worth of creatures, three domains to defend (your Council, Army, and Vanguard), and 10 Tactic cards that start in your hand; all of which are chosen by the player.

At any given time, each of your creatures are on the battlefield in one of your three domains, and each of these domains have a power variable that will be modified as the game progresses. If a domain reaches zero power it is destroyed, and creatures there must move to one of the remaining domains controlled by that player. When a player has lost two of their three domains, they are eliminated from the game.

Each creature has several base statistics: Blood (hit points), Might (damage), Will (various uses), Upkeep (the amount of power required to keep the creature alive), Tribe Value (the numeric value of the creature) and many various abilities, keywords, and game texts. These creatures can take various actions and make different types of attacks (Dominance, Leadership, Stealth, or Manipulation) depending on what domain they are in.

Tactic cards, which start in a player’s hand, serve two functions. Initially, they can be used as an action, trap, instant etc. But after they have been played, they serve a second function by being added to your strategy. Each of your three domains can host a strategy chain and each chain adds more layers of abilities and enhancements to your tribe. Each card in a strategy chain can gain new abilities or functions depending on the other cards connecting to them in the chain.

On the box "An exciting skill-testing game from start to finish."

Stock trading game, with player influenced moving stock markets. Roll the dice and move around the outer part of the board, picking up lobby cards, paying tax, inheriting money, winning the lottery, or being fined. Get chances to buy stock and enter the stocks markets to influence each stock, stocks can double or even triple in value, they can also halve or be wiped out on the stock board, first player to a million dollars wins.

Game about three batlles of the 1066, where Normans and Vikings attacked England. At Stamford Brige Englishmen managed to defeat the vikings, but they were beated at Fulford. Hastings was decisive batlle, where Normans managed to win.

Game uses the rules for the "Medieval" system, with classic "I move - you move" mechanics. Unitss are infantry and cavalry, light, medium and heavy, along with archers and leaders. Cavalry may charge to make it's attack more devastating.

Morale plays the important role in the mechanics - as he number of the losses increases, the stronger enemy player attacks. Defeated units might be weakened, destroyed or disordered.

The game is about the battle at Peipus Lake, known as the "Battle on Ice", where armies of the Novogrod defeated the Teutonic Knights.

The game uses rules for the "Medieval" system, with classic "I Go - You Go" mechanics. Units are infantry and cavalry, light, medium and heavy, along with archers and leaders. Cavalry may charge to make their attack more devastating. Russians also have some mounted archer Tatar units.

Morale plays an important role in the mechanics - as the number of friendly losses increases, the stronger the enemy player attacks. Defeated units might be weakened, destroyed or disordered.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
The Frostgrave Folio is the complete, single-volume collection of all the previously released Frostgrave digital mini-supplements.

Within these pages can be found "The Hunt for the Golem", "Sellsword", "Dark Alchemy" and "Arcane Locations".

Also included is a new mini-supplement, "The Ravages of Time".

In the super-fast competitive card game Bitcoin Empire, you are a plucky, pioneering entrepreneur trying to collect and keep as many bitcoins as possible in storage. Bitcoins are represented by face down cards. The game ends when the last card is drawn from the deck and whoever has the most face down cards at that point in storage is the winner.

Every player gets a character card. Characters have ranks on three different skills: law, coding and marketing. You also have a bitcoin storage rating which determines how many face down cards can be stored underneath the character.

The main deck of cards is shuffled and each player is dealt two cards. There are several different types. Exchanges can be played down as places to store face down cards based on the bitcoin rating. Hack, Lawsuit and Smear Campaign are attack cards that can be played against an opponents Exchange or character is your ranks in law, coding and marketing respectively are greater than the targets. If successful all the cards in that storage are discarded without looking at them and the Exchange card is taken to the hand of the attacking player. Alternatively these attack cards can be played underneath the character as extra ranks of the applicable icon. Internet Meltdown cards can also be played on the characters to increase their ranks but the Satoshi card will wipe them all away if played. Also, Head Hunter cards can steal an opponents skill cards. Bribe cards can be played to cancel any attack or the Head Hunter.

So keep your wits about you as you take on the fledgling crypto-market and build your empire.

boardgame: Endure the Stars: Survivors Gender Variant
description:
Finding more survivors is the priority. We need to bolster our ranks with as many crew as we can find, the more skilled the better.  The Gender Variant Character Class Pack introduces 6 gender variants of the core game character classes. Each one of these characters also comes with a completely new set of abilities different from those of the core game characters. Better still, these abilities can be interchanged with the core game character’s abilities, unlocking a whole new set of tricks!

Pack contents:
6x Character dashboards 
6x Ability tokens 
6x Health tokens 
6x Resolve tokens 
18 x Injury tokens 
6x Survivor miniatures 
6x Colour matched base clips

Game System

The Midnight Legion is a highly advanced, color illustrated gamebook trilogy in the style of the classic Choose Your Own Adventure series, but with MUCH more control over your character and world—think of an advanced version of SORCERY or LONE WOLF, with deeper character generation. An interactive story is combined with combat, strategy, puzzles, diplomacy, stealth and role playing elements. Collect information, knowledge, and goods from the world around you to advance your character.

2-player Version

In this variation, one player reads the entries like a game master to another player who experiences the world of Midnight Legion. All the rolls for the encounters are handled by the reader and the player handles all of his or her character's dice rolls. This is also a good way for the game master player to introduce the game to someone who doesn't know all the rules.

Game Play

You must choose whether to complete your objectives using combat, stealth or sixth sense and diplomacy. You can solve the puzzles that unlock the base’s secrets using logic, or you can try to recover your lost memories for clues.

Choices

Whatever you decide, your actions change the world around you and determine the encounters you face next. Every game you play will be different – and you will need all of your skills to survive.

Theme of The World Reborn:

More than four centuries have passed since the Devastation engulfed the Earth. The species that remained were fused and reborn in the alchemy of atomic fire. Now their diverse descendants have claimed the planet. You are an agent of the Midnight Legion, tasked with preserving life and rebuilding civilization. Only one other agent remains, and she is somewhere ahead of you, racing for your last remaining base. Unless you can reach it before she does, she will seize the command center and use its ancient weapons to unleash a second Devastation. This time, if she succeeds, nothing will survive. Your chase will lead you to new creatures, lost technologies and possibly even the clues your adversary leaves behind. But beware—there are no established paths across this constantly shifting land, and nothing is what it seems.

boardgame: Sultans of Wind: Relics of Power
description:
Sultans of Wind: Relics of Power adds twelve new cloud tiles to the Sultans of Wind base game. Some of these tiles have pillars on them, with a relic being placed randomly atop each pillar for players to grab during play. Players can typically use these relics to attack or block one another.

Sultans of Wind: Relics of Power also includes new gameplay modes.

Ladies in the government, a new Orator ability, improved abilities for Generals, rules to cast out two-time presidential losers and the ability to try Statesmen for sedition are the new features in this Founding Fathers expansion.

Includes 18 cards and 5 plastic rings.

Fully compatible with Founding Fathers, Founding Fathers Green Edition and Founding Fathers: Offices & Statesmen.

Card Name: Excess Mass
Type: Setback

Place a Super Creep on the Evacuation Pod. Until it is killed Colonists cannot cannot be loaded onto the Evacuation Pod and it cannot be launched. No Colonists in the Pod are converted by this Super Creep.

Lovecraft's Revenge is a tabletop game by Two Hour Wargames that plunges you into the world created by horror writer HP Lovecraft. It's about persons who dug a little too deep, driven mad by knowledge never meant for mortal minds. It's about places with thoughts and souls of their own. And it's about things whose very existence cackles at the laws of nature. These creatures defy accurate description, view people as mere insects, and can drive you out of your mind (sometimes literally) simply by standing before you.

This game can be played by any number of players; either solo, same side, or head to head.

In Lovecraft's Revenge, players control one or more characters in an rpg-like Lovecraftian adventure. Each adventure has a goal and the goal is attained by collecting clues and managing to survive the ordeal with mind and body intact. The game unfolds through a number of scenes at various general locations. Each location will likely have some kind of encounter. Some encounters will surely be of a combat nature, but others might involve solving puzzles or interacting with npc's within the adventure.

Description from the designer:

Ever After: Twisted Realm is a unique objective based Board game where player takes a role as a character from one faction between “Dawnguard” and “Eclipse”. Each player is secretly assigned a team at the start of the game.

There is no player elimination in this game. Player wins or loses with the team by comparing team victory points. Player gains VP (Victory Point) by completing quests, controlling areas and bonus power from identity skill.

The game starts by dealing character card secretly to each player as Identity Card. Then randomly select leftover character cards to make a draft pool for Partner Cards. Each player will have 1 secret Identity card and 1 Face-up Partner card. The partner card will have its ability during game play while the identity card will define your team and guide your play style by its extra score condition.
Player plays the game by moving around the map to complete the quest, conquer more areas, attacking other players or killing world boss. Player will get to know other players identity through rumor system which will let you know some information and guess from their actions.
At the end of the game, all victory points gathered from each player will be summed up as team score and the team with higher score wins the game.

In the 2000s, each player is one of the VIPs of Small City. Each one hides behind a pseudonym of a Roman emperor, based on the special ability that player chose at the beginning of the game. The goal of Brutus: The Board Game is to racketeer the commerces, to rob the residences, and to hire new thugs in the industries. Each turn, you move your Roman empire figure, and can either destroy or burgle a building. Thus you gain more and more influence in the districts. The player who builds up the most influence in Small City wins.

The city is represented on a hex hex grid, and its size depends on the number of players.

There are two ways to play this game in the Small City universe. A book should be included in the box, a hard-boiled novel that deals with the story of the same six emperors, which takes place after the story of the board game. A new concept to expand the board game...

This is a Playmat for Qwirkle with connect special rules.

Before you start draw a tile and put it on each black spot.

You must add blocks to existing tiles. When putting a block on a spot with points you gain that many addiontal points

Math+Op is a card game based on math problem solving together. Each players have number cards. Players choose a rulesmaker at first. Each person get one card from number card deck. Player who gets big number is rulesmaker. Rulesmaker gives ten card at a time face down, beginning with the player on the right. Each player gets 10 cards but rulesmaker 11 cards. The remaining number cards and operations cards are placed face down separately on the table, forming the stock.

Rulesmaker placed at first number card. Player at right of rulesmaker gets number or operation card from stock and placed. Player who placed result number card is winner.

boardgame: Canada 2017 (fan expansion for Ticket to Ride)
description:
This map is an unofficial fan made expansion. Symbols & Graphics Copyright © 2004-2015 Days of Wonder, Inc. Produced with permission of Days of Wonder, for non-commercial use only.

This follows the principles of the basic Ticket to Ride game on a special board with special cards and rules. The object of the game is to gain the most points by travelling various routes and outwitting other players. You will need the train pieces and the train cards from either the US or Europe base games (or you can buy them from the Days of Wonder shop.

There is an extra set of Tourist cards for the cities, each with a value to be earned by visiting that city. These cards are dealt/picked up alongwith tickets in the same quantity and with the same 'keep/dispose' rules.  There are also choice cards which may help or hinder you.

There are 9 challenges which can give you extra points if you complete a particular tour.

There are many new options in the rules: exchange all your cards at the start but keep all the new ones: discard 5 cards and pick up 5 new ones, as your turn: add two routes together: and many more. You can also call time when your wagon stock reaches 3, if you want, giving you the advantage of stopping other players completing tickets.

Bowser sits on top of his tower, but it starts shaking as more and more Yoshis and Toads are invading the tower. Super Mario is trying to climb to the top as well, making things even more shaky.

In this game of dexterity, players take turns rolling a die, to see on which of the colored layers of the tower they have to place one of the Toads or Yoshis. As more miniatures get added to the tower, it gets less and less stable. If Bowser falls off the top, the player whose turn it was loses the round.
family: Video Game Theme: Super Mario Bros.

description:
This is a Playmat for Catan with  special rules.

The desert is replaced a gold field where you can get the resource of your choice.

This is a Playmat for Catan with special rules.

Catan is suffering droughts, cross the desserts to reach the furtile oases.

This game comes with rules in three languages: Dutch, English, and German.

The mat is 61cm x 61cm (42’ x 42’).

This is a Playmat for Catan: Seafarers with special rules.

Catan is divided in multiple islands. On some, pastures are missing, on others forests. How will you be able to make ships? This game comes with rules in three languages: Dutch, English, and German. The mat is 61cm x 61cm (42’ x 42’)

This is a Playmat for Catan: Cities & Knights with special rules.

Defend catan from attacks of barbarians. This game comes with rules in three languages: Dutch, English, and German. The mat is 61cm x 61cm (42’ x 42’).

Description from the publisher (translated):

In Il Principe Ranocchio (The Frog Kiss), each player is a prince turned into a frog by the evil court sorcerer (who wants to marry the Princess with impunity). The objective of each player is return human again, but the only manner do so is to accumulate power by eating insects and then ... kiss the Princess!

The pond is represented by a playmat, on which the water lilies and the insects are placed. The frogs can hop hon themand eat the insects. Each player controls a frog with a rolling tongue, which can easily catch the insects.

During his turn, each frog has two actions (also identical) to choose from the 4 avalilable: jumping on an adjacent free water lily, bouncing on another frog to reach a free water lily, replace a free water lily, or roll the tongue to catch insects /  kiss the Princess. But you can kiss the Princess only if you have collected a sufficient number of insects.

Each frog has its own asymmetric power and, every Princess needs a different collection of insects to be kissed and change the rules in a different way. Adapt your strategy to the actual Princess and to your power, and slow down the leader. May the prince with the longest tongue win!

Description from the publisher:

Ne 'Il Principe Ranocchio', ogni giocatore impersona un principe trasformato in un Ranocchio dal malvagio stregone di corte (che vuole sposare la Principessa impunemente). L'Obiettivo di ogni giocatore è tornare umano, ma per farlo deve accumulare potere mangiando insetti per poi ... baciare la Principessa!

Lo stagno è rappresentato da un tappetino playmat, su cui vengono piazzate le ninfee su cui i ranocchi possono saltellare e gli insetti che devono catturare. Ogni giocatore controlla un ranocchio con la lingua srotolabile, con cui può agevolmente acchiappare gli insetti.

Durante il proprio turno, ogni ranocchio ha due azioni (anche uguali) a scelta fra le 4 disponibili: saltare su una ninfea libera adiacente, rimbalzare su un'altro ranocchio per arrivare su una ninfea libere, ricollocare una ninfea libera, oppure srotolare la lingue per acchiappare degli insetti o ... baciare la principessa. E' possibile baciare la Principessa soltanto se si sono raccolti un numero adeguato di insetti.

Ogni Ranocchio ha il proprio potere ed ogni Principessa richiede una diversa collezione di insetti per essere baciata e modifica le regole in un modo diverso. Adeguate la vostra strategia alla Principessa in gioco e al vostro potere, e rallentate chi è in vantaggio. E che vinca il principe più linguacciuto!

boardgame: Larger Than Life: The Director's Cut – Action! A Scenario Book
description:
Description from the book:

In Larger Than Life – Directors Cut you learned that it all boils down to the Story and we gave you the tools to create them.

In Action we’ve taken it a step farther; now we’ve given you three full blown adventures. Each Story comes complete with pre-generated Stars, Co-Stars, Big Bads, and more, but they will work just as well with your own cast.

STORY #1 – You’re “Duke” Clayton, world traveling adventurer, and you’re girlfriend Leilani, has been kidnapped by a power crazed villain – and what about Hooni, the Great Squid God?

STORY #2 – You’re the Gray Raven – a Vigilante whose current case involves stolen artifacts and murders – not one, but many. You’ll be required to bring your best as you travel south to a Hidden Valley deep in the Peruvian jungle.

STORY #3 – It’s Germany 1935 and there’s been a scientist kidnapped. In our 3rd adventure we’ve given you a classic pre-World War II spy story, but with a couple of twists.

Paris, the city of lights, love and arts. An exotic location with all its glory. Unfortunately, it is also a perfect place for an act of heist! In Le Voleur (the thief), you will gather a team of thieves trying to steal loots from various districts in downton Paris. Be mindful with your steps because not only you have to plan carefully and dodge the patrolling police, but rumor says there are other teams trying to expose you! Be careful with your planning and notes, because even a slightest mistake might ruin your perfectly planned heist!

Le Voleur is a logic-strategic game for 2 to 4 players. There are 2 main mechanics going on: the main board with area control and player board with deduction. On the main board, players need to create patterns by flipping colored tokens. With the right pattern, players can Scout and finally Steal the good. To flip the tokens, players need to use one of the Abilities of their agents, 4 are taken randomly from bag at setup, by turning their Agent's dial to show the desired abilities (shared color with the token on the board). Other players may mark these abilities on their player board, called Headquarter, which also shows the identity of all agents available in the game. Everytime an Agent use their abilities these clue grows and eventually reveal the Agent correct identity.
 The game played in 8 turns. Each turn are consist of several phases and each phase shows how many agents a player can play. If a player have less than 4 agents, he eventually lost all ability to use any actions on that phase and can only recruit new agents. Victory Points are collected by either Steal a Good or exposing other Agents. The first player with 5 Victory Points wins the game.

Learn to recognize and name the 4 basic emotions with this game. The children take turns in rotating the dial. When the dial stops, the child whose turn it is, takes a card with the same face as indicated by the dial and places it on his assignment card. The first to fill his or her assignment card wins the game. The assignment cards are all different. It is possible to do a group activity with the large emotion cards or use the cards for group discussion.

Each player has 10 field players and one goalie. Movement is determined by a die. The direction may be changed once during movement. If a player gets on the same space as the ball he gets an extra move for the ball. The ball can't change direction during movement. The ball may move through occupied spaces. If the movement of the ball ends not on the space of a friendly field player, the opponent gets his turn. If the movement of the ball ends on one of the three numbered spaces in front of the goal, the goalie catches the ball, if he rolls one of the numbers shown on the space the ball is in.

After an agreed on playing time the player who gets the most goals wins the game.

DESCRIPTION:
The air is clear and the water sparkling, as you and a group of friends prepare to go down the river on rafts.  With your skill set high, and the water moving swiftly and dangerously, you are anticipating an exciting run down the river.

Will you end up drenched and ejected from your raft, or will you finish first and victorious?

GAME OBJECTIVE:
Complete your 12 cards before everyone else

GAMEPLAY:
Players can complete up to 5 cards each round, by rolling dice and matching them up with card goals.  You can continue going until you claim these river/downstream cards, but if you roll twice without claiming a card, you BUST and need to reset that round.

GAME ELEMENTS:
72 cards (12 for each player) and 20 dice of three different colors (for multiple simultaneous player turns)

You take the role of an adorable kitty-lover granny.

Your mission is to get as many kitties as possible with the help of your granny friends, avoid the health inspector, suffer diseases and deal with the Death.

The Grannies and the kitties are grouped into five categories.

Each player get 5 Grannies cards.

In each turn appears a kitty and the player who has more compatible grannies with the kitty gets the kitty.

If a player doesn't have any compatible granny card they have to get a disease card.

In case of a tie, the player who doesn't have a disease card compatible with the kitty wins the kitty.

If a tie persists the kitty remains on the table and another turn is played, the winner takes both kitties.

When a Player wins a kitty can use a Health inspector card from their own deck against another player. If this player doesn't have a bribe card they has to give 3 kitties cards from their own pile.

When the death card appears the players can discard their diseases cards, discarding a compatible grannie (both card are discarded).

The game ends when all the kitties cards are gone.

The player who gets more kitties cards wins the game.

After the Battle of Kursk began a large-scale offensive of the Soviet Army. On leaving in 1943 to the Dnieper the Soviet command had decided immediate crossing it. This option does not have any time to the Germans to strengthen the left bank of the river and turning it into an impregnable "East Wall". Due to the haste of the operation craft sorely lacking, so the main forces crossed the Dnieper on improvised means: fishing boats, makeshift rafts of logs, barrels, tree trunks and planks. The entire burden and forcing the first battles on the left bank fell to infantry units. About heavy fights indicates the following fact - for the crossing of the Dnieper in 2438 soldiers were awarded the title Hero of the Soviet Union, which is more than the total amount awarded for the entire previous history of the awards. This title was awarded to not only the commanders of battalions and regiments, but also up to the distinguished junior officers (NCOs). Such mass awarding a single operation was the only one in the history of war. In memory of all the fallen Soviet soldiers dedicated to this game. We remember.
 
The game is designed for two players - one led by the Red Army, the other - Wehrmacht troops entrenched on the banks of the Dnieper. Soviet Player has 6 turns not only to cross the river and take a foothold, but also to keep it to the end of the game.

The game has simple rules and takes 30-60 minutes. Released in the PPP format in the second issue of the magazine Stratagema (Стратагема)

This is a fantasy card game in which players each take on the role of a fantasy adventurer (cleric, ranger, rogue, warrior, or wizard) who starts the game with a randomly selected personality trait, personal motivation, and end goal.

All adventurers are part of a party, and they work cooperatively to reach the Lair Card (where the chosen adventure's Boss Monster is revealed). If the party defeats the boss, they win, and gain an Epic Treasure Card. Otherwise, they die and lose the game.

The objective of the Solo version of the game is to accomplish your character's End Goal before s/he becomes too cowardly, despairing, mad, reviled, or too dead to continue adventuring.

Different End Goals include: Becoming Accomplished (in a certain discipline or skill), becoming Powerful, being Revered as a god, becoming Renowned (for performing feats), or by attaining great Wealth.

To accomplish the End Goal, the adventurer travels from Location Card to Location Card.

When the adventurer enters a Location, s/he can do one of four things: Make Camp (to hunt and rest), Interact with the displayed NPC, do Trade (rolling on a table), or Explore the Location.

To explore, the Location Card is flipped over, revealing an Encounter. Encounters represent monsters, traps, or perilous situations that must be defeated. Successfully defeating these encounters results in gaining XP and Treasure Cards.

The XP can be used to eventually gain up to three Levels. Each new level has three Skill Cards you can choose from. You place the acquired Skill Card below your Adventurer Card.

Each Encounter displays from one to three Impact Icons, which signify how dangerous, horrifying, and taxing the encounter is to resolve. Unless the adventurer immediately avoids (or escapes) the encounter, s/he will be impacted by interacting with it.

The Encounter Impact will add to the character's Personality, which in turn, affects how effective s/he will be during subsequent encounters.

In addition to encounter impacts, there are Event Cards that come into play for different durations that also display Impact Icons on them.

Whenever conditions in play match those specified on active Event Cards, the event impacts the adventurer. The Event Card is then placed under the Adventurer Card, with the Event Card's bottom section visible from the appropriate side of the Adventurer Card.

A possible outcome of the event is that the character becomes mentally afflicted, emotionally disturbed, or physically maimed.
Thus, as the adventure continues, the character may become too emotionally, psychologically, or physically weak to continue to pursue his or her End Goal. In this case, the adventurer loses the game.

Conversely, the adventurer wins the game by enduring encounters and events (some of which are positive), and by attaining his or her End Goal.

Each End Goal lists varying levels of success, so you always try to score higher in subsequent gaming sessions.
A quiz game by Michael Szymanski. 10,000 questions.
A quiz game by Michael Szymanski. 4,500 questions.
A quiz game by Michael Szymanski. 20,000 questions
Special edition by Shell with the most valuable monuments of Greece.

Historia: gra karciana is a card game for 2-4 players who play in either Middle Ages, Modern or Contemporary times epoch and use famous historical personas, symbols or military units to win the outcomes of the historical events.

The goal of the game is to score Victory Points, by:

     winning the events
     generating Culture
     winning wars


The game consists of 4 rounds (3 for 3 players), in each round one historical event is active (events are sorted chronologically). Players manage the resources of their factions (politics, economy, science, culture) in a way similar to euro games and use them to put into play personas, symbols and units. Players perform actions one by one, until they all pass (or are out of resources). Then the winner of the current historical event is determined.

There are 12 factions in the core set, grouped into the following "clashes":

     Polish-Teutonic Wars: Poland vs Teutonic Knights Order
     Hundred Years' War: England vs France
     "Deluge": Poland vs Sweden
     Thirty Years War: Habsburg Monarchy vs France
     Polish-Bolshevik War: Poland vs Soviet Union
     I World War: UK vs Germany

Card game based on Brazilian Banco Imobiliário (Monopoly), but with simpler rules and shorter playing time.
Basically, there are property cards which have distinct color band on the top, signifying the "color-group" to which them belong. The card also displays the property name, the number of cards in its "color-group", and the value of the complete group and each house attached.
The idea of the game is to draw and trade cards and organize them into "color-groups". Players take turns drawing and discarding until one player announces that he/she has a completed hand with three groups of the same color.

The First Crown of Miltania, first among monarchs, is dead. You, the monarchs of Miltania's eight realms, now must choose who among you shall be the next to wear the Eightfold Crown as First Crown. The Chancellor, the First Crown's chief supporter, also wields great power in the new regime. As you wager in this game of diplomacy and deception, never lose sight of the ultimate question: Who Wears the Crown?

Who Wears the Crown is a king-making, social-collusion, partial information, negotiation game of court politics and shifting alliances. In Who Wears the Crown there are two winners the First Crown who has the most Crown points and the Chancellor who has the most of the First Crown's influence. Players play Crown cards to either support or betray their rivals and players negotiate with their influence to score Crown points.

Do you want the Throne or the Power? Who Really Wears the Crown?

—description from the publisher:

boardgame: Submerged Atlantis (fan expansion for T.I.M.E Stories)
description:
Originally released as a print-and-play project by Kevin and Melissa Delp from Tantrum House under the name "Submerged Atlantis", this scenario will appear from Space Cowboys as TIME Stories Revolution: The Hadal Project in 2019.

This scenario takes agents on a journey to the year 2099, and again the agents do not know what to expect — but it seems that underwater adventure might play a role.

西藏夾棋 (Tibetan wedging game) or 青海藏棋 (Qinghai chess) is an abstract strategy game played on a 5x5, 7x7 or 9x9 Alquerque-style board.

Players take turns to place their stones on an empty intersection until the board is full except for the center intersection; then they take turns to move a stone to an adjacent empty intersection.  When a piece of one color is trapped between two pieces of the opposite color, or between an opposing piece and the edge of the board, that piece changes to the opposite color as in Othello.

When one player has no legal move, the player with more pieces on the board wins.
family: Traditional Games: Alquerque / Fanorona (Indian War Games)

description:
Abstract dice-rolling game created by five-year old Holly Stallard, and published by Games 4 Geeks.

Using a single dies, players roll to see who will win, depending on the outcome of the die.

This is essentially a gag game, as the way the designer (who as stated above is five) has created the rules, the youngest player can't fail to win.  
Whether this was originally intentional, or just a lucky accident on the designer's part is currently unknown.

boardgame: 桜降る代に決闘を　幕間：細音雪花 (Intermission to a Duel during the Falling of Cherry Blossoms: Hosooto Snowflake)
description: 桜降る代に決闘を　幕間：細音雪花 (Intermission to a Duel during the Falling of Cherry Blossoms: Hosooto Snowflake) allows owners of the first edition of Sakura Arms published by BakaFire Party to upgrade to the second edition of the game released in 2017. This pack contains all the new cards added to the game for the second edition, as well as all updated cards, updated rules, and the new megami Saine.

boardgame: Bad Actors: Free Your Wild Side
description: Bad Actors is an inhibition-crushing character impersonation party game. Your wicked imagination turns simple questions into hilarious experiences. Players answer questions impersonating characters like geekiest tech nerd, mad scientist or fanatical sports fan.

CWC Durango 2016 Special is a small expansion for Catan, given to contestants at the 2016 Catan World Championship.

The Durango hex is placed in the middle of the Catan board, replacing a pasture hex.

Up to three players may build roads into the center of this hex and settlements on the end of these roads. When the number of this hex is rolled each settlement owner may take a resource card of their choice.

The distance rule does not apply to settlements built inside this hex, and these settlements cannot be upgraded into cities.

How many horrors can be fit into one box? Let’s find out.

Uncounted Horrors contains all of the Kickstarter-exclusive content unlocked during the Deep Madness campaign. It includes the limited-edition investigators, monsters, search cards, items and markers. In addition, it holds the “True Ending” scenario, which includes its own story and a new room tile.

boardgame: Marvel Comics Super Heroes Game Book
description:
A collection of six roll-and-move games in a book format. Each two-page spread is one game. All games use the electronic die that is attached on the top right corner of the book.

Games included in the book:

     The Amazing Spider-Man
     Fantastic Four
     The Avengers
     The Incredible Hulk
     Wolverine
     X-Men

Woomal is an educational cooperative board game published internally by The Guides and Scouts of Finland as part of the 2009-2011 Finnish-Senegalese environmental project of the same name (the word means 'development' in the Wolof language of Western Africa). Although the game contains a lot of snippets of text describing basic environmental problems and solutions around Senegal, the actual game play is language independent producing simple, thematically imaginable situations which the players can use as a node of thought for discussions and learning.

During the game, players move their pawns around a highly abstracted map and use combinations of knowledge cards in three colours to remove corresponding combinations of problem markers from affected areas. Starting with just one card, players can acquire new ones by collecting enough markers from the map or by going to the education center. The three different colours represent three generic categories of issues: water, the natural environment and human activities.

Because the problems are often too complicated or become too large, players need to work together and plan their turns carefully so that their characters can work together in the game and share their knowledge and effort in the most effective way. Due to the randomly allocated problem markers giving players new objectives and blocking movement on the map, the game is constantly presenting small, changing spatial puzzles for players to solve. Separate game sessions can develop in very different ways and provide varied narratives to think about.

Players win the game by removing all the larger problems from the map or by each player collecting enough knowledge cards so that everyone is prepared for the future. The players lose if all the villages on the map are covered with problem markers or if too many larger problems accumulate anywhere on the map. Woomal could be described as a very simple version of Pandemic, played on a grid of hexagons.

A game of words and association of ideas in which interactions create laughter, emotions and teasing.

Each player completes the words of his list secretly by the first word that comes to mind. Which players will have the same answer as you? In turn, you must try to put yourself in the skin of other players to guess what they may think. You score points every time you succeed. Be the first or even the only one to give the right answer and score even more points. Who will say like you?

A Trick Taking Game of Winning Cat Friends and Cat Fights

Play offering cards of different values and houses to earn a range of blessings from the Houses of Elder Cats across multiple seasons

Each season you will strategically play your 5 blessing cards, play one per round, to earn better rewards or deny your rivals resources.

At the end of a season, new offering cards are dealt, repeating the process.

Use these offering cards to secure Lesser and Crown Blessings from the Houses of the Elder Cats to stock your Fiefdom with food, entice wild cats to your Fiefdom's Court, increase your share of Catnip, and gift your food stocks to wild cats to turn them loyal to your fiefdom.

Newly recruited cats can offer significant powers to your fiefdom. Other cats can add new powers when are fed.

Use your newly fed loyal cats to "trump" your opponent's offerings or fight off your opponent's cats to secure your reward.

The first player to feed 3 wild cats and make them loyal to his/her fiefdom wins the game.

NOTES: Offering Cards are now Mini Sized! - not Micro as depicted and also now all cards and player spaces include texture and uv coating!

A German-style Trick-taker about building a glorious garden with gnomes!

For centuries, in the thick cover of the woods, gnomes have settled their quarrels over a game of Wunder Garten. These conflicts may be as serious as a disagreement on a property line or as trivial as to who makes the best mushroom stew. In any case, Wunder Garten has been the go-to game of gloating for gnomes for as long as one can remember.

Wunder Garten is a trick-taking game that uses set-collection for scoring. Essentially players take on the role of a gnome attempting to make the most glorious garden of all. You can score points in a number of ways.

1) Bidding. Before a round begins each player must bid, using elderberry tokens, to determine how many tricks they think they will win. If a player guesses exactly the number of tricks, they will gain 3 points. If they under-bid, they lose 1 point for each bid they are off. If they fail to reach their bid, they gain ZERO points!

2) When you win a trick you may choose to keep either the winning card or a card from the meadow (community pool) to add to your personal garden (tableau). The player with the most total points in their garden at the end of the round gains 2 points.

3) There are up to 5 suits being used during play. The lower the value of the card, the more fruit it produces. This rewards players for winning tricks with lower-valued cards because another way to score points is to collect the majority of each suit type. For each majority you have the highest sum in, you gain a bonus point.

4) Finally, a player may gain an additional bonus point for collecting a card from each different suit into their personal garden.

There are other strategic choices to be made as well. When to use a Raven Card is important as it allows you to take two cards into your garden instead of one.

Before bidding takes place, each player must draft a suit to become their trump suit. Once a player has their cards in hand, and have examined them, they must choose a suit that will be most advantageous for them in that round. The suit become their personal trump suit, meaning that if they cannot follow suit during play, they can trump using any card from their chosen suit. No two players can have the same trump suit, making the game both entertaining and balanced.

boardgame: Touhou nimensou ～　reversible Night (東方二面相 ～ reversible Night)
description:
User summary
東方二面相 ～ reversible Night (Touhou nimensou ～　reversible Night) is a fan fiction / alternate universe card game based on the video game series Touhou Project produced by Team Shanghai Alice.

Each card has 3 suits and effects. A special characteristic of this game is that two cards can be combined into a single double-faced card.

Players should initially be given 2 cards of each of the 9 types for a total of 18 cards. Each player should then proceed to freely combine two differing type cards, back-to-back, to create a total of 9 double-faced cards.

Every card has three suits (Power, Speed, or Brain). The suit that wins or loses the current trick is decided at the exact moment the first player or the winner of the previous trick plays a card. Once the suit has been decided, the card of that suit with the highest value wins the trick.

If there is a tie, all cards with the same highest value are flipped face down and the next highest value is calculated again from the remaining cards in play for the trick. If all players have the same value and the tie cannot be broken, no player is declared as winner for that trick.

The winner of the trick claims the points that were decided at the beginning of the game for each trick. All players, excluding the winner of the trick, may choose to turn over one card in their hand (which gives them a better chance to strategize in secret against their opponents).

Repeat for a total of 9 tricks. At the end, the person with the greatest amount of points is the winner.

A second edition 輝針城編 (Kishinjou edition) was released in 2016, with a new set of cards. Both sets can be mixed together.

Publisher's summary
「東方二面相 ～ reversible Night」は上海アリス幻樂団様制作「東方Project」作品の二次創作カードゲームです。
カード毎に３つのスートと効果を持ち、2枚1組で両面表のカードとして扱うのが特徴です。
プレイヤーはそれぞれ9種類2枚ずつ、計18枚のカードを持ち、種類の異なる2枚の裏を自由に組み合わせて1組にし、両面が表の1枚のカードにします。
カードはそれぞれ３つのスート(パワー・スピード・ブレイン)を持ち、先攻または前トリックの勝者がカードを出すと同時に、このトリックでの勝敗を決めるスートを指定します。
指定スートで数値の大きいカードがそのトリックの勝者ですが、もし引き分けならば、引き分けのカードを全て裏返し、再度数値を比べます。
1度裏返しても同じだった数値のカードは勝敗に含めません(全員が同じ数値だった場合、そのトリックは勝者無しとなります)。
トリックの勝者はゲーム開始時に決めておいたトリックごとの点数を得て、それ以外のプレイヤーは自分の持つカードを1枚裏返すことができます。
9トリック繰り返し、獲得した点数の多いプレイヤーが勝ちとなります。
family: Video Game Theme: Project Shrine Maiden

description:
A fast paced boardless board game – PeltaPeeps is a game for 2 to 6 players. The play combines negotiation and placement. Players take turns placing new game pieces and making connections in the play area. They may also flip or move another player's piece in each turn. Flipping a piece down lowers its scoring. However, a piece that has been flipped face down, may also be flipped up with some help from other Peeps, restoring its value. A piece may also be moved from one area to another. A player may never move their own pieces, and a piece may only be moved if it will not cause a discontinuity in the connected pieces already in play. The player with the most points counted up at the end of the game wins.

Each player has either 8 or 16 unique pieces per team color, with up to 6 colors, depending on game version. The versions are- PeltaPeeps For Two (2 player) or PeltaPeeps Core and Full games, both of which are 4 to 6 player.

Play revolves around the unique connections each piece can make- blocking pieces preventing other players from making favorable connections, leaving open connections that allow you to connect to your own color (giving you bonus points) or creating hubs for future plays. Different playing pieces have varying point values based on being face up or down and who they are connect to as well as what they are. Play continues until no more valid placements are available.

The game combines bluff and negotiation, as players get bonus points for connecting to their own color, but cannot directly reverse the moves other players make against their pieces. Even if players choose not to discuss their moves, the moves, themselves act as a form of diplomacy, potentially forcing other players to react to 3rd parties as play progresses.

The pieces started out as wood, but are now coming to market as UV printed and laser cut Acrylic.

boardgame: Frappé ! A Miniature Wargame of Tactical Maneuver in the Napoleonic Era
description:
A simple set of rules for tactical combat with miniatures set in the Napoleonic era.

Ground scale figure scale 1:10. 
No ground scale given.
Designed to be played on a hexed terrain board.

44 pages of rules and army lists.

From an idea by Duke Seifried, developed by Ray Johnson.

First volume in the Wargamers' Library line.
