Survive obstacles, learn a new language, adjust to a different culture.
The players move on a path via a roll & move mechanic and have to handle events and do tasks depending on which spot they land. 
The player who reaches the finish first is the winner.

boardgame: Masmorra: Dungeons of Arcadia – Kickstarter Exclusives
description:
This is a set of non-addon items for Masmorra that were only available during the Kickstarter campaign.

Among the exclusives there were fourteen new heroes, all have cards which allow the heroes to be used in Arcadia Quest and Arcadia Quest Inferno:
Quan
Baruk
Thereon
Lotus
Tosh
Azure
Blossom
Toghor
Travis
Raffi
Grenouille
Makumba
Hyldir
Zsa Zsa

Among the exclusives there were 10 new monsters among 5 types:
2 Undead Dice
2 Villain Dice
2 Infernal Dice
2 Nameless Dice
2 Rigor Mortis Dice (This kickstarter exclusive box is the only way to get these monster dice. They are not available in the Promo Dice Set)

In addition to the monsters dice and heroes, there was one kickstarter exclusive upgraded component:

5 Treasure Chests

Description from the publisher:

In WordChance, players can easily make large words using the 16 tiles in their hand.  The score for the word is based on the product of the number of letters in the word played and the value on a multiplier chance card selected during the player turn.  The multiplier cards have values of 0 to 10, -5 and -10.  Hence playing a large word may be a good thing, or a bad thing.

The game board has 676 spaces for word forming (26x26 spaces).

There are also other types of chance cards:


     Bonus word cards that allows the player to play a second word during their turn.
     A swap tiles card which causes the players to switch their game tiles with that of the player to their left.
     A bankrupt card which sets the total score of the player to zero.  If chosen early in the game, the player can recover.  This card is only put in the card deck if there are three or more players.


We think that the ability to make larger words and the addition of chance makes WordChance lots fun to play.

boardgame: Phobos Infection: The Lost Journey of X27
description:
Phobos Infection tries to understand what happened in 1959 when the spacecraft X27 was lost during its voyage to the far flung moon of Mars, Phobos. During this trick taking game, you will fight the four viruses of The Red Planet's moon by various means of attempting to not win tricks, trumps, or even certain high value cards.

After each round an infection occurs and the most infected players will spread their viruses. No one is immune. The least infected player at the end of the game (either 9 rounds with 3 players, or 8 rounds with 4 players) will live to tell the story of the lost X27.

Roots to Barbu

This game can be played in 4 different ways.

1.The players have to get their 2 figures to the center of the game board. Movement is determined by a die. If a figure reaches or overtakes an opponent figure, this figure loses it's hat and can't move until the player rolls a 6. At the marked spaces the figures of the same color advance to an inner circle. The colored spaces between circles are safe spaces. The figures may stop there, even if they rolled more than is required to reach that space.

2. The players have to build their figures by rolling a die for the parts they need to assemble their figures.

3. This is a combination of game 1 and 2. The players have to assemble their figures before they may enter the boardgame.

4. The game is played as a pachisi variant with 2 meeples per player.

User summary
どうぶつれんさ (Doubutsu Rensa) in English means 'Animal Linker Card' and is in the position between being a card game and being a board game. While simple rules make it playable for children from the age of four, the game also has strategic aspects that make it just as enjoyable for adults.

Before beginning the game, place cards face-down in rows to create the board. Arrange each row to start and end at the halfway point of the starting and ending cards of the rows above and below in a double pyramid pattern.There should be a total of 7 rows, but players are free to arrange these rows into any shape they please (doesn't have to be a straight line).

Deal four cards to each player to form their hand. The player who doesn’t go first is dealt one additional card. The remaining cards serve as the deck.

The players may then proceed to freely exchange the cards in their hand with cards from the row on the board closest to them to form their own “base”. After the cards making up the base have been chosen, these cards should be placed face up.

During a player’s own turn, they may play a card from their hand that connects with the characters drawn upon one of the cards previously played faced up on the table.

If a face-down card is available where the player places their card, this card is taken into the player’s hand. If a face up card is already placed where a player wishes to play their card, the player simply places their card on top of this card.

A player may chose to either place a card or pass and draw a card from the deck. The first player to place a card on top of their opponent’s base is the winner.

Publisher's summary
「どうぶつれんさ」は4歳から遊べるシンプルなルールながら、大人も楽しめる戦略性もあるカードゲームとボードゲームの中間のようなゲームです。

ゲーム開始前にカードを裏向きに並べてボードを作ります。
列ごとに半分ずつずらすように並べ、おおむね7列で線対象の形であれば自由な形が可能です。

プレイヤーに手札を4枚ずつ配り、後攻になったプレイヤーに1枚手札を多く与えます。残りを山札とします。
次にプレイヤーは自分に一番近いボードの列のカードと、手札を自由に入れ替えて「ベース」を作ります。「ベース」を決めたら表向きにします。

プレイヤーは自分のターンに、手札のカードと既にボードにある表向きのカードに描かれたキャラクターが繋がるようにカードを出します。
　そこに裏向きのカードがあれば、裏向きのカードは手札に加え、表向きのカードがあれば、そのまま上に重ねます。

カードを1枚出すか、出さずにパスをして山札から1枚ドローしたら相手にターンを回します。

先に相手の「ベース」の上にカードを置いた方の勝ちとなります。

Game Features
1. A system like card game and board game intermediate
2. Even small children can play simple games, and adults can enjoy it.
3. The board to use can be set freely.

The player draws an animal drawn on the card Put cards on board to connect,I aim to reach the opponent's position earlier than the opponent.
Board games in which hand weights and read ahead are important,
Card games where luck and strategy decide victory and defeat,
It is a game that combines both good places.

Game flow

① Two people determined the shape of the board to be used this time,I will arrange the cards and make the board.
If the shape seen from the two people is the same, if it is within 45 total
It is decided freely. (If you get lost,Please choose from "board card" and decide.)

② Hand each 4 cards to each player.
I will leave the rest as deck piles.

③ The row closest to you on each player board
Look at the card (base), exchange freely with your hand
Decide the base.

④ Once both players have decided the base,
Turn face up at the same time.

⑤ Mix the card at the top of the deck with the card "Final card"
The one who pulls by two people and pulls the "second card" is the first
The game starts. Those who ordered a normal card
Just add it to your hand.
(You will have 1 more cards in your hand)

⑥ In the player's turn,
I will do one of the following AB activities.

A Cards are put out so that animal is connected.
Look at your face-up card already on board, To connect to a small left and right animal.
I will put out the card. Take it out to a card face down.
If it is, replace it and add it to your hand.
If you put it out on a face-up card, leave it as it is
Overlay. (You will have 1 card less.)
At this time, if you put a card on the base of your opponent
It is a win and the game is over.

B pass and draw 1 card from the deck
When I can not or can not withdraw my card
Draw 1 piece from the deck and turn the turn to the opponent.
If you do not have a deck when you pass, the back of the board
Select one from the card and draw 1 piece. Draw
Where cards are gone,I can not put cards in the future.
Without cards facing the back of the board, It becomes "no drawing without draw".
When two persons are "success without draw" in succession,
The game ends with a draw.

⑦ If you connect the same animal with two cards,
The connected card is called "double"
You can not put your opponent on.
If either of the two foundations is overwritten
"Double" is canceled.
(Double can be used without the age to play)

Publisher's summary
Safari-Dual is a kind of Chess game, using three kinds of pieces; Lion, Elephant and Giraffe. The board and pieces are made of ceramic.

The Lion is the King and the Elephants and Giraffes are the Pawns. The winner is the first to capture the opponent's Lion, or to move their own Lion onto the opponent’s Lion's base area.

Pieces have dual natures; one is their shape and the other is their species.

Players move their own pieces and try to sandwich any of the opponent's piece or pieces. If the player's pieces making the sandwich are of the same species, this is a capture and the captured animals are removed from the board. A Lion can sandwich and capture any opponent’s pieces, using their own Elephant or Giraffe piece as the other end of the sandwich.

But the dual nature of the pieces gives the game more complexity.

For example, a player's own Elephant piece will sandwich and capture the opponent's Giraffes and Lion by using the opponent's Elephant. The same specie will work together! Likewise, the opposing Giraffes will work together to capture the opponent's Elephants and Lion. Only the two Lions will not work together.

This dual system is a unique and very interesting system first invented by the designer Mitsuo Yamamoto in 2008, and the game was first produced in 2011, a small and a large edition. A new edition is being produced in 2017 for a Kickstarter campaign.

boardgame: Clone Wars Adventures Trading Card Game
description: The Clone Wars Adventures Trading Card Game is a trading card game based on the mini-game Card Commander from Clone Wars Adventures MMO. Duel your opponent with cards featuring characters, ships and vehicles from Star Wars: The Clone Wars! When you win a duel, attack one of your opponent's hidden bases. Reveal the base's defense and destroy it! When you destroy your opponent's last base, you win!

boardgame: Catan: Seafarers Scenario – Legend of the Sea Robbers
description:
Catan: Seafarers Scenario – Legend of the Sea Robbers includes four new linked scenarios that tell the "Legend of the Sea Robber" to celebrate the 20th anniversary of Catan: Seafarers. Each scenario can be played individually, with players trying to win the game as usual by collecting ten victory points, but players can also record their scores at the end of each scenario, with effects of one game affecting the next.

Catan: Seafarers Scenario – Legend of the Sea Robbers requires both the Catan base game and the Catan: Seafarers expansion.

Description from the publisher (translated):
"Quiz about Europe - mini" - fascinating game and great fun, during which participants answer questions about Europe. Players can answer 180 questions with 45 cards, and they have 540 answers to choose from. Topics for questions and answers are: history, geography and nature, science and technology and the art and culture of Europe. "Quiz about Europe - mini" is an exciting and exciting entertainment for the whole family, and its participants can expand their knowledge of Europe while playing.

Description from the publisher:
"Quiz o Europie - mini" - fascynująca gra i świetna zabawa, w trakcie której uczestnicy odpowiadają  na pytania o Europie. Gracze mogą odpowiadać  na 180 pytań z 45 kart, a do wyboru mają 540 odpowiedzi. Tematami  pytań i odpowiedzi są: historia,  geografia i przyroda,  nauka i technika  oraz kultura  i sztuka  Europy.  "Quiz o Europie  -   mini"  to  atrakcyjna  i emocjonująca    rozrywka  dla całej rodziny,  a jej uczestnicy mogą  w czasie  zabawy poszerzyć  swoją wiedzę o Europie.
The Greed & Fury add on introduces 2 new monsters, the Draugr and Red Cap, as well as the Shield Maiden and Celt Town Heroes.
This pack introduces some of the most notorious monsters of all time to the castle; the Werewolf and Sandman!

This add on introduces 2 new Monsters and 2 new Heroes:
-Leshy
-Glaistig
-Pathfinder
-Jailer

Your mission: to identify the secret agent and discover the country where he is hiding.

Roll the dice, formulate mathematical equations and get information about the secret agent you're looking for. Filter the information and decode the name of the country where it is hiding.

Secret Agent, from the Mont-à-mots family, promotes learning and developing mental math skills with this suspenseful game where your deduction spirit is strongly sought.

Essentially, each turn, the player rolls 4 dice. If he can formulate a mathematical equation (+ - × ÷) with at least 2 dice to equal one of the numbers on the board, he can look at the related hint. The hint can be the and encoded version name of the country (which can be decoded using a wheel), a clue about the appearance of the person to find or if there is a traitor among the informants. A player win as soon as he guess who among the suspects is the wanted person as well as the country where he is hiding.

boardgame: North Star Games 2017 PAX promo pack
description: PAX East 2017 promo questions for Wits & Wagers

In Good Little Trains, players become train drivers eager to delivery their cargo to the ring of colourful stations connected by an ever-changing layout of tracks.

Every turn you have a chance to switch one piece of track before both your own train and the neutral 'Ghost' train both head out across the network of rail lines. Aim to drop your own cargo at the most valuable stations, while also having an eye on where the Ghost train is headed, and what your competitors are up to!

If at the end of a turn all the stations of one colour have cargo, then those stations are scored. All players can earn points for their cargo, and the player who triggered the scoring gets a coin. Once all 10 coins have been taken, the game ends and the player with the most points is the winner.

Good Little Trains is the first product of a new collaboration between the co-designers of 'Elysium', 'Costa Rica, 'Pyramids and 'Professor Evil and the Citadel of Time'. All these new games will be available as free print-and-play downloads from the Good Little Games website, alongside the existing catalogue of free microgames including the original editions of Pocket Imperium, Empire Engine and Bad Grandmas.

One side of the board has a simple roll and run game. The first player to reach space 120 wins th game. On some spaces events hinder or benefit the players.

The game on the other side of the board can be discribed as "roll, run and collect". On some spaces the players can collect small cards, worth different amounts of points. If a figure gets on an occupied space it robs the cards the other figure is "carying". Cards may be brought to safety by using the shortcuts into the caste along the way.

The game ends when all players are back in the castle, but only if one player has found the card with Ivanhoe's coat of arms. If not, the players have to continue the game till one finds this card and thereby ends the game. The player with the most points wins the game.

Build a party of heroes, explore rooms in a dungeon, slay monsters (or be slain...) and grab the loot.  Improve your survival chances by using your heroes' special abilities and/or some of the cool stuff you grabbed off the monsters you've dispatched.  Make it through seven rooms and you've survived the dungeon - now tally up the gold value of the items you've escaped with.

Gameplay is simple:  take the attacker's attack value, subtract the defender's defense value and then add in a 20-sided die roll (die included!).  If it's over 10 it's a hit, and you subtract the damage.  Or you can use your Special Ability, or one of the items you've picked up from a prior room.  Repeat until there are no monsters or heroes alive in the room.

Play cooperatively, solo or semi-competitively - all in less time than it takes to convince your gaming buddies that playing an Elven Hunter is not cliche.

This is a simple roll and move game. The players have to roll "1" to get from one of the starting circles to space 0 (Zürich). The first player to reach space 100 (back in Zürich) wins the game.

If a player gets passed on a yellow space he has to go back to the last square space. But if a player gets passed on a red space, he has to start again at the beginning. On a space with an arrow the player has to move forward or backward as shown by the arrow.

The game can be supplemented with additional cyclists to form équipes of 3 or more cyclists or for Play with more than 4 players.

Description from the publisher:

In the effort to find the best plot-theme, produce the most beautiful costumes and allegorical floats, each of the players represents the president of their Samba school and should manage their resources, rehearsals, events and hiring samba personalities. For this, each of them has a carnival team to help them.

Folia is a resource management game with worker placement and set collection and supports 2-5 players. Having characteristics of a classic eurogame, and lasts about 35 min/ player, Folia will surely be your main night game.

Players take turns rolling the dice to try to achieve the winning goal. For example, one of the cards wants you to look for any one's but no sixes. That gets you two points. You can keep rolling to build up your score. A losing throw loses you all of your points. Neither winning or losing throw gets you one point.
This pack introduces the English legends of Spring Heeled Jack and Black Annis. Also contains the new Heroes Battle Mage and the Exorcist.

boardgame: The Oracle of Delphi: The Power of Zeus
description:
Zeus himself offers his assistance: Whenever you visit one of his holy places, you receive a reward.

In this small expansion place the 4 Holy Places approximately equidistantly around the game board. Place the Power Tokens on the Holy Places with the corresponding Greek letter.

Whenever you stop on a space adjacent to a Holy Place, immediately take the Power Token from the Holy Place, without payment and without spending an action, if it is still available.

You may spend Power Tokens during phase 2 of your turn (when performing actions). After using the Power Token, return it to the corresponding Holy Place.

We are currently running a developer diary on Facebook. If you want to read about it here, please vote in the forum.

Werewolf vs. Vampire is a tactical game that brings these creatures to life through their unique abilities.
Plan ahead by building paths through this unknown world. But watch out, your opponent is trying to block you just as much as you are trying to block them!

This game was born from wanting to create something both my girlfriend and my son could play and enjoy, while developing their tactical skills.
So, both beginners and advanced students of tactics and strategy can play to win! 
Will you play as the crafty Vampire? Or the vicious Werewolf?

The game supports 2-3 players with the playable clans of Werewolf, Vampire and Hunter.

The story of Werewolf vs. Vampires immerses you into the dark realm of these creatures.
Choose from playing as the Vampire seeking to retrieve the potion that will save their race, or the Werewolf who's tracking down the Vampire.
As the Vampire you'll be able to transform into a bat and cast powerful illusions.
As the Werewolf, you can use your savage bite as well as supernatural speed to gain the advantage.

Each game in Werewolf vs. Vampire is different. There are almost unlimited ways a game plays out, while maintaining the theme of the setting. 
A bit of luck makes the game great for casual gamers, while the skill system will challenge tactical maniacs time and time again.

Description from the publisher:

Defense of the Citadel is a cooperative hero defense board game. Set in a high fantasy world, enemy forces are marching towards your Citadel in great numbers and only a handful of heroes remain to prevent them from achieving their goal.

Mission & Campaign mode

Players can choose to play set missions for a quick game with predefined objectives and events, or a more traditional defense game in campaign where the map and events are all randomized, and the status of the game carries over to the next round until you reach the final objective.

Crafting & Preparation

Players must make preparation before the inevitable battle to even out the odds. A large variety of options are available to players from crafting items, traps, infrastructure, scouting, rest or even restructuring the Citadel!

The Invasion

During the invasion, players can perform special combat skills through the usage of class skill cards. Players can mix and match different classes skills to create a unique battle strategy of their own. The course of the invasion is randomized through the invasion card deck, with many different events.

Cooperation

The enemy units are controlled by the rules of the game, there are no game masters. The game is a fully cooperative game with many game elements to promote working together, such as with crafting, battles, event choices or helping other player characters who were taken out of action to continue fighting.

TPS Pocket Game #4

One of the post-card inclusions by Turning Point Simulations.

This is a Mini-game that simulates Operation: Pedestal which was a Convoy to Malta from Gibraltar in 1942. The game consists of 17 counters and a progress track.

Description from the publisher:

Just you, your opponent and your pieces.
A battle of minds where the board is divided – and your game is actually two.

EXEO Duo is a strategy game for 2 players over the age of 12. 
A game can take 30 minutes to play or an entire, captivating night.
The objective of the game is to exit your 4 pairs, all while attempting to stop your opponent from doing the same. The first player to exit all 4 pairs is the ultimate EXEO Duo master.

boardgame: Star Wars: Armada – Imperial Light Carrier Expansion Pack
description:
Description from the publisher:

The Imperial Light Carrier Expansion Pack introduces one pre-painted Quasar Fire-class cruiser-carrier miniature to your games of Star Wars: Armada. A mobile starfighter base frequently used as a garrison vessel, the Imperial light carrier bristles with laser turrets that allow it to defend itself while its fighter craft are away on crucial missions. It comes supported by two ship cards and a total of ten upgrades, including a dual-slotted Weapons Team / Offensive Retrofit upgrade that allows you to disrupt your opponent’s ships with the game’s first boarding action!

boardgame: Star Wars: Armada – Hammerhead Corvettes Expansion Pack
description:
Description from the publisher:

Brace for impact with the Hammerhead Corvettes Expansion Pack for Star Wars: Armada! Named for its distinctive silhouette, the Hammerhead corvette is officially classed as a transport, but features combat-worthy engines, hull, and armament. Often fielded in groups by the Rebellion, Hammerheads lend themselves to formation tactics with their agility and responsiveness. You’ll be able to explore some of these formation tactics with the expansion’s two pre-painted Hammerhead miniatures, and you’ll find them supported by two ship cards, as well as a full complement of fourteen upgrade cards heavily focused on close-range combat!

Alice in Shroomland is a 1-4 player deck-building card game, with an approximate 30 minute play time.  "Spore Cultivation" is the core game of the Alice in Shroomland game series, which includes 197 playing cards, 8 meeples, and 6 jumbo card dimensions.  Game expansions include, but are not limited to, Spore Fertilization (45 card expansion) and Spore Germination.  Game expansions enhance game play, but are not required to play.

You'll enter a twisted and psychedelic world called Shroomland where you'll attempt to survive an onslaught of approaching enemies in an effort to either save or destroy Shroomland. When you begin the game you'll be given a set of 12 starting cards, called a "tripper’s kit". You'll draw and play the top 6 cards from your Tripper's Kit each turn.  These cards are used as in-game currency and are used to obtain additional cards to slowly strengthen your tripper’s kit.

Encroaching enemies will traverse Shroomland and its many dimensions looking for you. Your job is to defeat these enemies before they get to your stronghold in The Ominous Dark Forest.  The game is a race against the clock; enemies that fight you in the Ominous Dark Forest will add "ouch cards" to your tripper’s kit, thus weakening it. You will be temporarily defeated and labeled as "unfortunately sober" when you draw and play 3 of these ouch cards in the same turn.  Teammates are able to revive defeated players with a Meaning of Life Card.  The game ends when all players are unfortunately sober.

Alice in Shroomland: Spore Cultivation, has 2 game modes, cooperative and competitive. For either game mode your mission is simple… destroy the enemy leader. But beware! You can also win in competitive mode by wiping out the competition and being the last player remaining; sabotage at its finest!

Players who feel like preserving peace will defend Shroomland and play as the Sativaz. Those looking to destroy Shroomland will play as the Indicaz. Playing as the Sativaz is an easier leveled game experience while playing as the Indicaz is meant for more avid gamers looking for a challenge. Take a trip on the wild side in Alice in Shroomland: Spore Cultivation!

This later entry into the Weapons & Warriors series has a single large castle (wall with drawbridge, 2 towers, and keep) that is under siege by an invading army with a Triple Blast Barrier and a Defense Shield.  Weapons include a cannon, a crossbow, and 2 catapults.    Castle Siege has quite a few more units overall than previous games in the series, adding new types including archers, knights, and battle tents.  All opposing army units must be defeated for a player to win.

boardgame: Legendary: A Marvel Deck Building Game – X-Men
description:
The 13th expansion to the Legendary: A Marvel Deck Building Game puts the spotlight squarely on the X-Men. This set will bring to the game many of the X-Men characters that have been overlooked since the inception of the Legendary game.

This big box expansion has X-Men’s biggest heroes, including Psylocke and Banshee, battling their greatest foes. Even Beast makes a return as an all new X-Men team affiliated Hero!

This will be the biggest Legendary expansion to date with 394 cards of new Heroes, Masterminds, Villains, and Schemes straight from the X-Men comics!
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

boardgame: Not My President: The Board Game
description:
Description from the publisher:

What is Not My President?

I bet you’re asking yourself, what is this? Let me start off – I love games. I do. I do. Don’t let anyone tell you otherwise: nobody loves games more than me, and when it comes to games, I love them. I do. I’ve made plenty of games – I’ve provided more games to the American people than Obama ever will, ever has been, will go, games. And this one – it’s the greatest game of all time, mark my words. The best. Anyone that says different – we’re fighting a war here, ladies and gentlemen, a war, against fake news. If this game doesn’t get funded, we’ll know – it’s phony, America. This game is going to be huge. *Small loans of a million dollars are accepted and encouraged.

How It Works?

Based off the popular game Mafia, Not My President is a high-energy, 3-12 player game of deception that lasts 5-10 minutes per round. Using your best poker face, diversionary tactics, and a lot of finger pointing, the goal of Not My President is to either find the Trump Supporters amongst you, or, if you are one, to keep yourself hidden through any means necessary. Lying, spreading chaos, and blaming others are highly encouraged tactics. The game begins when players randomly select and privately view a “role card,” dividing the party into two groups: Trump Supporters and Reasonable People. Players then close their eyes for the election night. During this phase, the free app guides you through the special abilities of your assigned roles. Players secretly swap cards, change identities, and cause more mayhem than a private email server. When everyone has taken their turn, players open their eyes, and the accusations begin. After 5-10 minutes, players vote for who they believe to be the most likely Trump Supporter. If they’re wrong, the Trump Supporters win. If they’re right, it’s probably fake news, and the Reasonable People win.

Narration:

Because this game is so great and since we don't want anyone to feel left out of the party we have made a free downloadable app to narrate the game! Trust me its not Skynet, an extremely credible source told me so. Includes adjustable settings and soundtracks for those of you who like to spice things up a little.
This 5-card Boss Monster: The Lost Levels mini-pack includes Boss Anererak, Monster Room: Wight House, Monster Room: Room 1-1, Monster Room: Shocksquid Pool, and Monster Room: Piranha Tank.

Side stitch is played on board tessellated with hexagonal cells and possessing an odd numbered of sides. The goal is to create a group of stones that touches more sides than your opponent.  (Note that on game boards that have colored borders, each distinct color counts as a distinct "side.")

				
				
					RulesOn each turn, a player plays a single stone of his/her color to any empty cell; once placed, stones do not move. Play continues until the board is full or until both players pass in succession. The pie rule applies (that is, once the first stone is played, the second player can opt to switch colors with his/her opponent).

A group of same-color stones has a value equal to the number of sides it touches. (Note that a stone in the cell where two sides meet touches both of those sides). At the end of the game, each player identifies his/her highest-valued group. The player whose highest-valued group is highest is the winner. In case of tie, the players should ignore the tied groups and compare their remaining groups; the highest value among the remaining groups wins. This process is repeated until the tie is broken.  Draws are not possible.

Honors


     Side Stitch won 2nd place in the Best Combinatorial 2-Player Game Contest of 2017

User summary
DOSUKOI is a game based in the world of Sumo wrestling, using a game board, cards, and chips.

A player acts as the Oyakata of a Sumo-beya (the Master of a Sumo family/group - essentially a training school). The wrestlers must have good Chanko-Nabe from the kitchens (Chanko casseroles, a typical food for Sumo wrestlers). You must recruit new Rikishi (wrestlers), and they visit the Ozashiki (parlours) to gain support from Geisha and Oiran. Ultimately the wrestlers will gain victory at the Dohyo (Sumo ring) by pushing off the opponent, sometimes with support from Ninja actions.

This is a 2 player game of comparing numbers in three areas, the Dohyo, the Kitchen, and the Ozashiki. Each section gives you a different victory bonus which you can use further on. Play RIKISHI cards face-down from your hand against these sections, apply any extra actions (such as revealing cards or moving cards) and then compare strengths. The winner of the section advances their chip and this helps them further.

Winning in the Dohyo is crucial to victory, but without Kitchen and Ozashiki support, you would eventually fail there. Secure your strength, and aim for victory.

Publisher's summary
DOSUKOIは相撲を世界観にしたゲームとなっておりゲームボードとカード チップを使用したゲームになります。

世界観
プレイヤーは、相撲部屋の親方となり、台所で美味しいちゃんこを作り新しい力士をスカウトしたり、
お座敷に通い芸者や花魁の応援をもらいながら、時には忍者を暗躍させ、土俵で勝利し相手プレイヤーを押し出し最強を目指す。

このゲームは土俵と台所、お座敷というそれぞれ勝利ボーナスが違う3つの場所で、手札から出すRIKISHI達の数比べが基本的なシステムとなっております。

土俵で勝つとゲームの勝利に近づけますが、台所やお座敷で自身の相撲部屋を強くしないと負けます。うまく強くしながら、土俵での勝利を目指します。
designers: Naoyuki Matsuzawa (松澤 ナオユキ)

boardgame: Hengen Kaitou J -Fantomu Daiya no Himitsu-
description:
User summary
変幻怪盗J　～ファントムダイヤの秘密～ (Hengen Kaitou J -Fantomu Daiya no Himitsu-) in English is 'The Everchanging Phantom Thief J -Secret of the Phantom Diamond-'. The 怪盗 (Phantom Thief) theme is quite popular in Japan, from Arsene Lupin or Lupin the Third, or recently, Persona 5 from the Persona series in console games.

A card game with a phantom thief theme, the game has hidden role assignment. The cards are placed in a circle on the table, and the mission is to find the appropriate card in there. The Phantom Thief will win when the Heroine is found, or the Cops will win when they find the Phantom Thief.

To win, you must control the Squad card moving on the circle of cards, and execute the "charge in" card at the appropriate position.

Publisher's summary
テーマは怪盗。テーブルの中央に環状に伏せられたカードの中から、怪盗役は「ヒロイン」カードを、刑事役は「怪盗J」カードを探しあてれば勝利です。
勝利条件を満たすには、伏せられたカードの上を移動する「機動隊」カードをうまく制御して、目的のカードの上に「機動隊」カードが来た時に「突入」カードを使います。

boardgame: Espada Negra: Juego de Mesa – Edición Gunear
description:
Expansion for Black Sword the table game that can also be used separately.
This edition incorporates a new element, the berserkers.

Expansión para Espada Negra el juego de mesa que se puede utilizar también por separado.
Esta edición incorpora un nuevo elemento, los berserkers.

1947 is a railroad game inspired by the 18xx Series and is set in India between 1836 and 1947.

This 18XX game introduces some new mechanics:
- Convertible Bonds, to finance your companies
- Savings Shares, so a player can avoid becoming a Company president - however they also reduce your dividend.  
- emigrations 
- temples to reach with your routes for travelling pilgrims
- seasonal climate (monsoon, dry, fertile, intermediate)
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
User summary
'Color Code is a Karuta-like card game. Someone reads the hexadecimal RGB color-code (the kind that is used in web pages), and players aim to be the first to grab the corresponding color card.

In Japan, there is a a 400-year-old card game for two players, named Hyakunin Isshu      (100 Poems by 100 Poets) (Wikipedia: Karuta), for example "Iroha Karuta", or Ogoola Karuta: 50 English Classics. Two players kneel on the floor opposite each other, with cards arranged in front of them. On each card are small pieces of text from classic poetry, and typically with a small illustration.

A separate Reader chooses from a random deck and sings a phrase out loudly. The players must search to find the matching couplet on their cards and either grab or block the appropriate card. Whoever snatches the most cards wins. Play is very swift and physically as well as mentally demanding.

This game, Color Code, is basically the same. The difference is that there are no illustrations on the played cards, but instead a single color is printed on them. The Reader doesn't read a phrase, but calls out an HTML color-code, like #FFFFFF or #FF0000. When the Reader calls a code, the players must know which color it is and make their play. So if the Reader calls "#FF0000", players must grab the red card.

Publisher's summary
WEBページの色を指定する１６進数のRGBカラーコードを読み上げ、該当する色カードをとる速度を競う、かるた風カードゲームです。

boardgame: Star Wars: X-Wing Miniatures Game – Scurrg H-6 Bomber Expansion Pack
description:
Description from the publisher:

The Scurrg H-6 Bomber was crafted with combat versatility in mind. It was armed with powerful shields and a bristling array of destructive weaponry, and one of its prototypes was infamously stolen by Captain Nym. You’ll find not one, but two different ship cards for this notorious pirate in the Scurrg H-6 Bomber Expansion Pack for X-Wing, allowing you to fly him with the Rebellion or the galaxy’s Scum and Villainy. Additionally, the expansion’s pre-painted, small-base miniature starship is accompanied by three other ship cards, plus a hefty complement of ten upgrades laden with ordnance.
family: Game: Star Wars X-Wing Miniatures Game

boardgame: Star Wars: X-Wing Miniatures Game – TIE Aggressor Expansion Pack
description:
Description from the publisher:

The TIE aggressor was designed for long missions, supply chain raids, and tactical flexibility, and in X-Wing, it can lend a great deal of tactical flexibility to your Imperial fleet. Much of this versatility comes through the ship's ability to equip a turret weapon and a pair of missiles. Altogether, the TIE Aggressor Expansion Pack pairs its pre-painted miniature starfighter with four ship cards and five upgrades, plus everything else you need to fly the ship to battle.
family: Game: Star Wars X-Wing Miniatures Game

boardgame: Star Wars: X-Wing Miniatures Game – Auzituck Gunship Expansion Pack
description:
Description from the publisher:

Designed by Appazanna Engineering Works of Kashyyyk and equipped with three wide-range Sureggi twin laser cannons, the Auzituck gunship was known for its impressive firepower and incredible durability. The Auzituck Gunship Expansion Pack brings this ship to X-Wing as a resilient, pre-painted, small-base Rebel starship with three attack, one agility, and six hull protected by three shields and the reinforce action. You’ll find even more ammunition and defensive options among the expansion’s four ship cards and six upgrades.
family: Game: Star Wars X-Wing Miniatures Game

description:
You are running a gang in New York in 1921 at the dawn of Prohibition competing to gain the most Prestige by 1926 to earn an invitation to shape the organization of crime in America at the Atlantic City Conference. You can gain Prestige by expanding your Turf, building a  bootlegging empire, earning Headlines, and completing devious Schemes.

Success will require balancing running your gang like a business and being a ruthless criminal. You can earn Gs with Buildings  that produce and sell Booze, or you can collect Extortion from the hapless denizens of your Turf.

Alternately, you can generate Headlines by ordering your Goons to attack your competition, Steal their resources, Torch their operations, or take over their Turf.

Crushing your foes is going to require that you choose between focusing on completing complex Schemes, or getting a Tactical advantage during Conflict.

However, everything you do will generate Heat. As Public Outrage grows, the more often, and more severely, the Feds will disrupt your operations. You can reduce the pressure by generating goodwill, or when you get busted choose whether to pay a Bribe, Face the Consequences, or Do a Bit in jail.

Good luck!

Four Corners is a casual abstract game for two to four players.  Be the first player to collect rings from all four corners of the board and return home in the center of the board.  But you can only move in the directions the die roll allows.  Four Corners is a race against your opponents that is suitable for the entire family.

Description from the publisher:

We are in the 16th century, after the death of Martin Luther, the world-changing Augustinian friar. Most of his discouraged followers and admirers are still asking each other hopelessly: how to go on without him? What’s next now?

Some of them, however, know very well what is to be done in times like this: to write down the events of the reformer’s earthly journey as detailed and as true as possible. This is the only way to pay due tribute to their friend and professor in order to educate the world and the church. So, they organize themselves into a handful of scriveners and set to writing the great biography. But they might get hard pressed for time!

While playing Luther – Das Kartenspiel we have the chance to slip into the shoes of these scribes. The teamwork, as usually happens, swings into a little rivalry: who will reap the glory, which comes with chronicling certain stages of life? Who will be the one taking the most manuscripts to the printing house? What’s more, from time to time, the diligent work of the scribes is interrupted by the least expected incidents such as: a carefully inscribed sheet of paper burns or changes hands “accidently”, or a crafty fellow scribe filches a page, or mixes foolish rumours into the reliable notes.

Description from the designer:

Revealations is an easy to play party board game which tests your knowledge of Movie, TV show and Song titles in a fun and unique way. The titles have to be guessed by seeing only the initial letters of titles printed on the various category card decks.

Revealations has simple rules, is quick to set up and the game play involves a race to the top of the game board. Players earn points depending on how many initial letters are revealed before the player can guess the title. The game combines knowledge of Movie, TV and Song titles with a hint of anagram/code breaking skills.

boardgame: The Black Badge of the Galaxy
description:
The Black Badge of the Galaxy is a wargame in which each player puts himself in the shoes of a commander sent to one of the thousands of solar systems at war to consolidate the power of his species in him. Tercants, Humans, Hormínids, Talians and Aknumis will fight to the devastation and the death by winning the one that is the greater war in the galaxy in centuries. The war that will forever change the history of and the becoming of all the races of the galaxy.

Conquest, economic and social development, and strategic vision will be fundamental to victory. Who will win? Who knows. What is certain is that everyone will return home with the black badge of the galaxy in their memories.


Es un juego de guerra en el que cada jugador se pone en la piel de un comandante enviado a uno de los miles de sistemas solares en guerra para consolidar el poder de su especie en él. Tercantes, humanos, hormínids, talians and aknumians lucharán hasta la devastación y la muerte por ganar la que es la mayor guerra en la galaxia en siglos. La guerra que cambiará para siempre la Historia de y el devenir de todas las razas de la galaxia.

La conquista, el desarrollo económico y social, y la visión estratégica serán fundamentales para lograr la victoria. ¿Quién ganará? Quién sabe. Lo que es seguro es que todos volverán a casa con la negra badge de la galaxia en sus memorias.

Rocky Road: Dice Cream is a microgame for 2 players.

Collect scoops of ice cream to match the quantities on the demand dice. The more demands you serve at once, the higher your reward!

Game Play:
The player further behind (or on top) along the time track is the active player.

On your turn, do one of the follow two actions:

Collect: Gain 1 to 3 scoops of one flavor of ice cream. Move your player piece that number of spaces clockwise on the track.

or

Serve: Pay scoops to match the die value for one, two, or all three flavors of ice cream. Then score VP and move your player piece clockwise along the track as indicated on the game card based on how many flavors you served. Then re-roll the dice for all of those flavors. If any die value matches any of the unserved dice gain one scoop of the unserved flavor

First player to gain 12 VP wins immediately.
A pack of an additional 10 'Perk' and 10 'Quirk' cards for mixing into the decks of the base Billionaire Banshee game.  All 20 cards are in the 'Sexy' category.

They say no man is an island. We live within communities where we get to interact with other people, engage in various activities, have new experiences and be part of something bigger. The people around us shape who we are and in turn, we shape their lives through our behavior. The actions we take, allow us to become more popular, or slide us into obscurity. How will the community around us affect our constant pursuit for happiness?

The Pursuit of Happiness: Community expansion introduces the concept of the local community. Community cards, a new type of cards, allow you to engage in various community events and choose their outcome, with a new board included on which to place the new cards and keep track of your Popularity score. More Projects, Items, Activities, Jobs, Partners, Life Goals and Child Traits are also included to enhance your game experience, as well as enough material for a 5th player to join your games.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
XOBrainer is advanced type of five-in-a-row that can be played by two persons or two teams in which the object of the game is to get five-in-a-row in either shape (Xs or Os) or color (green or red), using both your pieces and those of your opponents.

One team has 24 green Xs and 8 pink Xs, while the other team has the reverse: 24 pink Os and 8 green Os. Each team places four of their pieces in the line-up area: three of their main color and one in the opponent's color. Each team then takes turns playing the pieces from their line-up area in numbered order (1-4); when the line-up area is empty, the team refills it as before.
 When you achieve five-in-a-row, you earn points based on the placement of the row on the game board. In each square are dots, as on a die, that indicate how many points you score from that square. When a team reaches 20 points, they win a set. The game is won when a team has won three sets. Alternatively a team can win instantly by building a five-in-a-row that passes through one of four "knock out" squares on the game board. Only pieces that both teams can benefit from — pinkXs and green Os — can be played in a knock out square.
 XOBrainer comes with four game variations that can be added and combined freely;


    Random: Instead of playing with the standard color order, play with randomly selected pieces, either by drawing four pieces randomly from the X- or O-bags or using a free WebApp.



    Map: Start the game from a new starting point; each team draws a card and places four pieces as depicted on the card – this way there is a new starting point in each round.



    TimeBomb: Set a time limit for each move with the free WebApp.



    Mission: Using the map/mission cards, each player has a secret mission to place pieces as depicted on the card. Each piece a player succeeds in placing gives the player extra points (if they also achieve five-in-a-row).

In Drakkar: The Card Game, a fast and fun game with a great interaction between players, you are the leader of a Viking village who must collect and steal resources such as goats, weapons, and gold to show you are the strongest Viking on the island! Will you be a Viking worth the favor of the gods?

During each turn, you use your own cards and the ones that you steal from rivals to build your ship, hire your warriors, and store your beer. Now you're ready to sail and pillage!

At the end of the game, each loot card is worth one victory point regardless of the type for a Viking, a goat, a weapon, or a gold coin have the same value! Additionally, you collect one additional point for each trio (goat, weapon, gold). After three complete rounds, whoever has the most victory points wins!

Description from the publisher:

End Days: Atlantis is a cooperative, dungeon crawler deckbuilding game for 3 players. You have been called upon as champions of New Atlantis to save the city from Armageddon. Lost knowledge capable of redeeming the city is kept in a sacred, ancient tome affixed with multiple seals. Each player assumes the role of 1 of 3 characters with unique classes-- Warrior, Witch, or Wizard. Your quest is to break each seal, which binds the ancient tome, in order to reveal the mystical knowledge and save New Atlantis from catastrophe. But it isn't easy. To break each seal, the party must leave the safety of the city and travel to a dangerous land to vanquish the evil inhabitants within. Only then, can the seals be broken,the book read, and New Atlantis restored.

Games may be played from 1 to 5 seals, each seal taking about an hour each.  Each seal contains 5 enemies.  When injured by an enemy, players add a wound card to the top of their deck.  Each class has a certain number of cards and abilities that must be played each turn, and the party is defeated if any player is forced to play a wound.  Special abilities and attacks require charges from crystals, the major Atlantean power source.  Loot and experience are awarded after each battle which can be used to upgrade weapons, crystals, and cards within each player's deck. After each seal is completed, the party can return to Atlantis to receive special benefits such as removing all wounds of one color from their deck, trading in loot, and recharging all of their crystals.

The Big Stream has been taken over by Invasive Species!  Join Olive and her friends from the popular children's book series on an adventure to meet the local stream residents and clear out the invaders!  While it may be based on a book series for kids, the  gameplay appeals to a much broader audience.

In Chuckin' Bugs, you collect an assortment of flies in your "fly box" (your card hand) and make casts to The Big Stream where you encounter 10 species of fish, Invasive Species, and hazards that get in the way of your fishing!  Match fly colors to the fish's preferences and roll dice to reel them in!  You also get to cooperate with other players to get Invasive Species out of the river and prevent them from completely shutting down the fishing.

Gameplay:

On their turn, players draw a card to their fly box (hand) and either make a cast with a fly of their choosing, or play one of several special "helper" cards.  When making a cast, the player places their fly on the table and declares which card in The Big Stream they are casting to.  The Big Stream card is turned over revealing either a fish, invasive species, or hazard.  If a fish is revealed, it shows its fly preferences, which dictates how many dice you get to roll and what number is needed to succeed in catching it.

The game includes two card decks: The Big Stream and Camp Tightloops.  The Big Stream contains fish, Invasive Species, hazards, and weather events.  Camp Tightloops contains three different types of flies in four different colors and special cards to give you an edge when you need it.  Some high points fish in The Big Stream require very specific flies to have a chance at them, just like big fish int he real world!  But, if you don't have the right fly in the box, you have the option of playing your special cards, wild cards, or creating a "wild" out of playing different fly type/color combinations with multiple cards.  Invasive species yield points to both players who cooperate in defeating them, and the player who cleans out the most invasives gets a bonus point token at the end of the game.

Is the best strategy to go for several smaller fish or take a chance on the larger high point fish? Should you go after the growing invasive species threats hoping they will pay off, or go immediately for the easy fish you know are there? Do you take chances on netting another player's fish to split the points with them or hope they fail their roll? Every time you play Chuckin' Bugs, The Big Stream has changed like your favorite fishing river.  You never know what lurks under the surface!

boardgame: Harry Potter: Hogwarts Battle – The Monster Box of Monsters Expansion
description:
Description from the publisher:

Venture into the Forbidden Forest with The Monster Box of Monsters, the highly anticipated expansion to Harry Potter: Hogwarts Battle  Play as Harry, Hermione, Ron, Neville or Luna Lovegood as you face frightening creatures and challenging encounters. Beware of any wrongdoing that might earn you detention! Only working together to defeat the evil forces and protecting iconic locations will make the Wizarding World safe once and for all!
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups
family: Game: Harry Potter: Hogwarts Battle (The OP)

description: Who told you that scientists are nice, harmless eccentrics? In this expansion you’ll find new technologies and new methods to your madness. Be ready for new Inventions with abilities that can be used repeatedly,  new Twists that add new places to send Spies, and even more monstrous Twists that add new Action Cards to the game - Develop, Experiment, and Produce. Let the scientific race frenzy begin! Hurrah!

100 new Perk and Quirk cards for Billionaire Banshee as well as 20 new Dater or Deny cards and 8 Rabid Animal stickers are included.

Works by itself, or with Billionaire Banshee.

boardgame: Tall Tales: The Game of Infinite Storytelling
description:
Description from the publisher:

Studies show that 38-percent of infants and toddlers use smartphones or tablets. And it doesn’t stop there...children ages 8-10 are exposed to an astounding 8 hours of media each day. In an age where electronics are taking over society, Tall Tales brings back the classic magic of imagination in a new and exciting family game. This creative, non-competitive storytelling game is for people of all ages. Players draw random story elements from a bag and twist their best tale using each piece, just like the world used to do before the technology revolution. The only limit is your imagination!

Real 3-D game includes 50 detailed game pieces, 24 full color story cards and a story bag to store game pieces.

5 Ways to Play:

Basic Game: One player reaches into the story bag and draws 8 story pieces one at a time. Player then begins telling a TALL TALE using one of the pieces drawn until all the pieces are included in the story. The story can be as wacky or fun as your imagination will allow. When the story is over, all the pieces go back into the story bag, and the next player draws to start their TALL TALE.
World Tales: Follow the BASIC GAME directions. After drawing 8 story pieces, draw one story place card and use that card as an environment for your TALL TALE. As you tell your story, place your story pieces on the story place card as used.
Taller Tales: Choose a number 9 to 50. One player draws one piece to use in their story. Once that piece is used, that player draws another piece which must be the next piece they use in their story. The story continues until the number of pieces chosen to use is achieved. Story place cards may be added for even more variations.
Group Tales: Each player draws two or more story pieces, making the total number of pieces among all players between 10 and 20 (or whatever amount you desire). The first player chooses one piece they’ve drawn and begins the story. Once the first player uses their piece in the tale, they pass the story onto the second player who continues, using one of their pieces. The story passes from player to player until all players have used up all their pieces. Story place cards may be added for an added challenge.
Marathon Tales: Like TALLER TALES, but the number chosen is always 50 pieces (the entire bag). Have one story teller, or make it like Group Tales where the story travels from player to player. Story place cards may be added for professional storytellers.

Table Air Combat is a fast, simple air combat game that can be played on any flat surface.  All you need are some six-sided dice and some coins to provide weight for the aircraft counters.  Everything else can be printed from this ruleset. 
Each player has a flight of four fighters, represented by two counters.  Aircraft counters move by means of curved “performance rulers” that represent the aircraft’s historical cornering ability and maximum speed.
Each performance ruler is unique to each aircraft, and contains all the speed, cornering, and weapon information needed.
TAC is designed for portability;  The entire game can fit in a plastic bag.  Even the largest battles can take place on a fast-food restaurant table.

This set is a self-contained rulebook that includes miniatures for the B-25 and contains three scenarios that reference other aircraft:
Railyard: Bomb the flak-defended railroad marshaling yards at Littoria, Italy.
Dolittle Raid: Bolster American morale with a daring strike against Tokyo.  Ki-43 fighters defend.
Bridge over River Po:  Cut supply lines by destroying the rail bridge into Piacenza, Italy. The bridge is defended by Bf-109G of the German Luftwaffe

Description from the publisher:

Get ready for one of the most brutal war games, as you take the role of a lord, and fight your way through the Scorched Kingdoms. Choose your clan, build the perfect deck, and create a seamless strategy for the ultimate 1-vs-1 battle to rule the nations of Ethalios. Dominate kingdoms, obliterate armies, and claim the throne as all others bow before you.

You will have to learn countless strategies for every situation, and always stay ahead of your opponent. One wrong move can end entire wars.

Each of the 11 Nations are broken up into multiple kingdoms and clans. They will have entirely different armies and strategies for battle, ensuring that two battles are never the same.

Scorched Kingdoms is a standalone, tabletop card game for 2 players to battle head to head. It takes advantage of many deck building elements, where you create your clan with different characters, equipment, and environment cards, to strategically defeat the opponent’s clan, and capture their throne.

It also has a resource management system, used to collect the essence of the nation, which creates legendary tiered cards. These can include giant mechanized titans, terrifying abominations, or rulers from other realms.

The Poker Expansion adds another fun western themed element to the base game...gambling! The expansion includes a full 52 card poker deck & 6 "All In Poker" insert cards. Add the "All In Poker" cards to the base deck & when pulled, play a round of poker against other players to earn money to spend in the General Store!

Also included are 2 Joker Bandit cards with a whopping 3 "Rep" points. These are the only 3 "Rep" point bandits in the base game!
30 additional encounter cards plus 10 additional treasure cards for Delve. Initially available only as part of the King's Blade pledge level on the KickStarter for Delve in 2017.

Disgruntled Decks is a card game for military service members and grizzled Veterans who enjoy laughing together about their time in the service. With a unique blend of descriptive language and military themes, Disgruntled Decks gives one more reason for service members and Veterans to talk about their shared experiences while having fun in the process.
Our message is simple: Humor through healing, and some Veterans still have a good amount of healing left to do. We hope to provide some of that humor with Disgruntled Decks. First to 7 cards wins.

This is a Cards Against Humanity style game targeted at veterans and members of the military.

Description from the publisher:

Frankenwords is the fast-paced matching game that puts a thrill into making new words!  Stitch two words together to create new meanings and watch vocabulary come alive in amazing ways.  Encouraging kids to be inventive and experiment with compound words, Frankenwords is the exciting word laboratory of electrifying play!

Here's how it works: 1. stack the cards in a grid, 2. race to be the first to find a Frankenword, 3. grab the cards a score a point!

User summary:
First player to 8 points wins.  The cards have words on one side and the same words with a picture for students just learning to read on the other.  Show only words, words with pictures or a mixture of both... choose according to the players' abilities.

Dim Sum Duel (or DSD for short) is a short strategy game for 2 players. In each 5-10 minute round, two players compete to see who can cook and keep the most dumplings.

Each player uses a separate deck and players are encouraged NOT to shuffle their decks but rather to arrange their cards in the order they wish to draw them - thus making the game one of pure strategy and no luck factor involved.

The game incorporates elements of planning ahead, risk assessment and risk-taking, reacting to strategy shifts and figuring out what your opponent is up to.

boardgame: Nine Beasts and Crusaders in Moosingal
description:
Publisher's summary
Nine Beasts and Crusaders in Moosingal (Moosingal no 9 Hiki no Maju to Tennku no Kishidan) is a Japanese card game set in a mythical land, a place where the Makai and the local world meet Moosingal.

There, nine animals were gathered as an Order, to recapture humans who won't grow old. But to protect the area from monsters, the Sky Knight appears in various places.

When the Order joined the Moosingal, they heard a horrible legend. "Do not hunt the Dragon of the Five Heads, the Dragon of the Five Heads will attack as a zombie."

In the battle, the cursed soldiers become monsters, and attack as zombies. Even while in the mercy of the humans turned demonic beasts, the Order must defeat them with the help of angelic humanity.

Jealousy and desire are the most horrible monsters swirling inside humans.

Nine Beasts and Crusaders in Moosingal is a trick-taking card-game in which players must co-operate to help defeat the monsters, but also try to compete for the highest final score. The cards have an Art Nouveau design to them.

Publisher's summary
Régalez is an art game with motifs of heart shaped leaves. Grow trees and score by cutting branches. Luck and intuition and intelligence will decide victory and defeat. It is an art to play Régalez!

During the game, players place tiles showing various patterns in six colours, such as hearts or clovers, good or bad fruit. Players make connections and grow the plants, and then score points by cutting off branches. But avoid taking bad fruit, which will lose you points.

The intent is to be artistic and make patterns while scoring points. Try not to interfere with your opponents with dead leaves or flowers. Deciding where to branch out, and which branches to cut needs intuition, intellect and some luck.

The game can be played solo and even if you don't know the rules, you can still connect beautiful tiles and play!

Come on, Régalez together!

Odd-Y is a connection game played on a hexagonal grid containing an odd number of sides of equal length (thus, an equilateral triangular board, or an equilateral pentagonal board, or an equilateral heptagonal board, etc.).

				
				
					RulesOn each turn a player plays a single stone to an empty cell on the board; once placed, stones do not move.  Corner cells belong to both of the sides that they join.

The Pie Rule applies:  after the first stone is placed on the board, the second player can choose either to play a stone of his/her color, or swap colors with the first player.

A player wins by connecting a set of three sides of which the following is true:  a triangle drawn of straight lines connecting the mid-points of each of the three sides contains the gameboard's midpoint (i.e., has the gameboard's midpoint inside its borders). (That is the fully general statement of the win condition. For the 5-sided board in particular, there is a very simple statement of the win condition: The winner is the player who connects three sides, not all of which are adjacent.)

Draws are impossible in Odd-Y. For each way of completely filling an Odd-Y board with stones, there will always be one -- and only one -- winning group of stones.

				
				
					CommentsWhen played on a triangular board, Odd-Y is also known as 3-Y.  Likewise: 5-Y on a pentagonal board, 7-Y on a heptagonal board, etc.  Note too that 3-Y is equivalent to The Game of Y designed by Ea Ea.  Thus, Odd-Y is a generalization of The Game of Y. Ea Ea himself discovered 5-Y on a pentagonal board, which he called "Star Y" (stating the win condition as described above).  Odd-Y generalizes 3-Y and 5-Y further, to any hexagonal grid board with an odd numbered of sides.

Mankind has long been confined to travel by land and sea, but a new technology has emerged from the greatest minds in Eastern Europa: airships. These steam-driven behemoths sail freely across the sky, aiding their empire's expansion through innovation and confrontation. As the years pass, the world has come to understand that no single faction will rise above the rest for any span of time. In the hope of decreasing the conflict and increasing the peace, leaders of Europa begin to gather each year to declare a new way for the factions to resolve their differences.

Scythe: The Wind Gambit, an expansion for Scythe, adds two new modules that can be played together or separately at any player count and with either the base game on its own or with other Scythe expansions:

• Airships: (1 miniature per faction; 16 tiles) An airship is a new type of unit that is distinctly different from characters, mechs, and workers. Unlike those units, airships never control territories. Airships are moved via a Move action, and rivers and lakes do not constrain their movement. Each airship has two randomly combined abilities (1 aggressive and 1 passive ability; same combo for all players).

• Resolutions: (8 tiles) The resolution module changes the way a game of Scythe ends. If you play with this module, ignore the standard end-game trigger (when a player places their sixth star, everything stops and the game ends). Instead, the resolution tile for the current game will determine when and how the game ends.

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
In CON521: The Game Convention Game, players race to claim seats at 42 game events over the three days of the CON521 gaming convention. The events are represented by hexagonal tiles on a board showing the three levels of the Meepleton Convention Center, as well as four hotels where the players start each day.

Each player's limited stamina restricts the number of games they can attend, but stamina refreshes daily. Claiming a seat at an event requires at least two turns: one to move to that event hex, and one to claim the seat by spending stamina. There is only one open seat per day at each event. Each game event has a type, such as board game or cosplay, each with its own section of the board; within that area, the seven events of that type are distributed randomly for every game. Each game also has a theme, such as zombies or space wars, and one of each theme in each type are spread across the board.

Money is used to pay for hotel rooms and for cards in the exhibit hall. Players can also buy food at food stop locations, with food providing extra stamina. Money does not refresh, and the initial supply must last the entire convention. Exhibit hall cards give bonus actions or points, but the exhibit hall is only open for certain hours each day. The exhibit hall also contains one demo per game type. These events do not have themes. A time track advances one hour per round, from 8 a.m. to midnight, and then from Friday to Saturday to Sunday.
 Players win by accumulating the most "Nerd Cred", which comes from two main sources, scored at the game's end:


    Event Types — The player who claims the most of each type of event scores that many points (0 to 8, including demos.) Two-way ties split the points. Ties of three or more players score no points.
    Themes — All players receive Nerd Cred based on the number of events played in each theme (0 to 7), according to a progressive scale.


A few bonus points can come from exhibit hall cards or unused money. The game includes a scoring sheet for easy calculation.

boardgame: Bushido: Nezumi & Small Swarm of Rats
description:
The latest recruit to the Cult of Yurei, Nezumi is fleeting on the battlefield, transforming into a rat and scurrying away when the time is right.

Blister contains one Nezumi miniature, three small rat swarms miniatures, four 30mm bases and two full colour profile cards.

The latest slave of Yurei, having been raised by Kato ‘the puppet master’ now takes on any and all opponents of the Cult. Enemies that fall before his onslaught become the gruesome tools of his trade.

Blister contains one miniature, 50mm base and full colour profile card.

The orphan cuts a miserable figure; many unwary strangers have rushed to the aid of the seemingly vulnerable and needy little girl. Those that do, more often than not, fail to survive their act of charity. Wrath materialises as if from thin air, channelling its rage, it’s very essence into destroying the adversaries of the Cult.
 Blister contains two miniatures, two 30mm bases and two full colour profile cards.

A man of excesses, Gengo was attracted to the Cult to satisfy his more base urges. He finds the sacrifice of others particularly appealing. One shouldn’t underestimate the difficulty in taking such a big man down.

Blister contains one miniature, one30mm base and one full colour profile card.

Carroting is the first expansion of Carrotia and comes along with the base game. In Carroting the Master Rabbit is trying to avoid the river's currents and find the exit on time but at the same time he must face the fishes.

River's Currents: When the Master Rabbit is moving along with the river's currents nothing happens, the game continues as you know. However, if the Master Rabbit is moving against the river's currents, then for each move you make, you count 2 moves on the Rabbit-Meter.

New Rabbit Characters: In "Carroting" expansion you will discover three new characters. The Steam Rabbit, the Wizard and Ms. Lovely. Each of these characters has a new ability. Example: Ms. Lovely ignores the river's currents.

Updated Quest Cards: The updated Quest cards indicate where you will place the fish tokens as well as the fishing spots. The fishing spots are hidden and you don't know what they hide unless you move the Master Rabbit there and you claim such a token.

Description from the publisher:

In Project Airlock, the crew of a starship must work together to survive the hazardous journey back to their home planet. However, one member of the crew is secretly a saboteur, plotting to destroy the ship. The other crew members must do their best to keep the ship intact and force the saboteur out of the airlock, but if they choose the wrong person, the consequences could be dire.

In this intense game for 3-6 players, the crew must fend off hazards ranging from hull breaches to wormholes, and at the same time try to identify an unknown saboteur. Meanwhile, the saboteur must destroy the ship's oxygen supply while pitting the crew members' suspicions against one another.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
A Mancala game played on a 4x6 array of "holes" (shallow cardboard dishes). Plastic "cowrie shells" are supplied as pieces.

On each turn the player takes all the shells from one of their holes (the twelve holes in the two rows nearest them) and sows them one by one clockwise around their twelve holes. If the last shell lands in an occupied hole, they take all the shells from that hole and carry on sowing. If the last shell lands in an empty hole in the front row (the row nearest the opponent), they capture all shells in the opponent's holes in the same column.

From the publisher's website:

Kids can’t get enough of this TREND original! A favorite of children, parents, and teachers since 1969, TREND Bingo turns skill practice into an exciting game. Our unique 6-way format adapts to all skill levels, keeping play exciting for everyone. Perfect for small groups, the entire class, learners with disabilities, and anyone learning English.

• Ages 4 & up
• 30 pairs of rhyming words
• Photos for real-life learning
• Play 6 ways
• Sturdy cards & storage box
• 36 different playing cards, 6½ inches x 9 inches
• Caller's cards & mat
• Bingo chips
• Answer guide
• 3 to 36 players
• Made in USA

boardgame: Marvel Dice Masters: Spider-Man Maximum Carnage Team Pack
description: Marvel Dice Masters: Spider-Man Maximum Carnage Team Pack contains 24 cards and 16 dice that feature characters from the "Maximum Carnage" Spider-Man storyline, including Venom, Shriek, Spider-Man's Doppelganger, and Spider-Man himself. This team pack can be combined with any Dice Masters start set.

The Elemental Kings need more than generals, they need heroes … to die for their cause.

This game requires the following components from TKG ARENA: The Elemental Kings & TKG ARENA: Brethren of Chaos.

MONSTER DECK:    1
STANDARD DECK (DECK):    1 for each player.
LIFE:    20 for each player (default)

Monsters from the MONSTER DECK can be sourced from TKG ARENA: Brethren of Chaos, this expansion itself or a mix from both games.

The objective of this game scenario is to survive an onslaught from a group of monsters randomly configured and represented by a MONSTER DECK in a cooperative manner.

Players start the game by drawing the top card from their shuffled DECK and the highest number starts first. The drawn top card goes back to the bottom of the DECK. Henceforth, each player takes turns to be the starting player for each cycle of attacks against the monsters in a clockwise manner. Players draw 5 cards from their STANDARD DECK as CURRENT HAND.

During each turn, a specified number of monster cards are drawn from the MONSTER DECK and each player may attack them according to the rules as described in TKG ARENA: The Elemental Kings. All attacks played automatically hits unless they are SOLO attacks that are LOWER than a game variable called the Strike Rate.

The Strike Rate refers to the minimum FACE VALUE of each solo card that needs to be used to remove a monster with 1 LIFE. Other categories of ATTACK HAND will automatically remove monsters according to the damage they deal. E.g. playing a TWINS can remove a monster with 2 LIFE or less automatically regardless of its FACE VALUE. Players may accumulate the damage of their attacks on 1 target but may not accumulate cards from multiple parties to form an ATTACK HAND.

Likewise, all attacks from any surviving monsters will hit automatically and the sum of all damage (based on the POWER of the monster) will be distributed evenly across all players (rounded up). E.g. 5 damage scored against 4 players means each player will suffer 2 damage. Players lose half (round down to the lower of) their remaining LIFE if they deck out and reshuffle for a new DECK. For every 10 POWER worth of monsters a player removes, his/her Strike Rate goes up by 1. When the Strike Rate is 10 for any player, SOLO attacks become ineffective.

The CHARGE BAR can be deployed for this game by increasing the deploying player’s initial Strike Rate by 1, and once deployed lasts till the end of the game. All spell effects and monster special abilities (printed for TKG ARENA: Brethren of Chaos) are not used.

The game ends when all the monsters are killed or when all the players are eliminated. The player who eliminates the most monsters in terms of LIFE wins. When there is a tie, the accumulated POWER of the monsters removed will be considered and the higher accumulated POWER wins.

Other than this new semi-cooperative mode of play, TKG ARENA: Monster Mayhem offers an additional new set of 32 monsters to complement the existing 60 monsters found in TKG ARENA: Brethren of Chaos.

Let the mayhem begin!

This is a book made to celebrate the movie Dragonheart. It includes a summary of the movie, pictures and profiles of major characters, and a set of 24 cards portraying these characters. The book contains the rules for 3 games to be played with these cards: "Battlefield" and "Duel" are simple games of direct combat, and "Siege," which is a solitaire game in which you are trying go through an entire deck of challenges.

A fast paced, friendship bruising player-elimination card game designed to identify who amongst the group (Military or other wise) is the ultimate Blue Falcon (Military slang for a buddy F---r). Players strive to draw as many cards as possible, while limiting the hand draws of their friends/enemies. The goal is to gain the Blue Falcon card and strike when your friend can't defend themselves and are eliminated from the game. The last person standing is the ultimate Blue Falcon and champion of the now friendless masses.

User summary
就カツ！ (Shukatsu!) is an educational game aimed at helping graduates prepare for the job market.

Until recently, it was very unusual for people to switch jobs, as many employers would be very loyal to their staff and effectively provide a job for life (evidenced by the ubiquitous Japanese 'salary-man'). You try to get into a good company on your first go and prosper for life, or get hired by an unknown small company and you are doomed. Changing jobs suggests to employers that there is something wrong with you.

Therefore, for pre-graduates, the process of landing your first job became hugely important, really defining your whole future. This process is called 就活 (Shuukatsu),from two words: 就職 (Shuushoku) meaning 'to get a job' and 活動 (Katsudou) meaning 'activity'.

This game Shukatsu! is a co-operative game to simulate the experience of winning a job offer. The game was designed and published by a professional career advisor. He says "Experience an year of job hunting, in an hour. The game is based on 20 years experience as an advisor, you would have a live experience. You may try to beat it purely as a game, but also it may be used as an job-hunting support tool. In that case, the creator may act as a facilitator to gain the best effects."

The game uses various types of cards, for example, for the job-hunters:
Activity card - study companies, attend ability training session.
Weakness card - don't study for unknown matters, habitual latecomer, impatient.

For the employers:
Company card - industrial newspaper press, internet brokerage.
Hiring requirements - co-operativeness 3+, aspiration 3+.

Also, some cards have Bad Events, for example:
 - too busy on part-time job.
 - suffer burnout.

On the setup, lay out some company cards and assign them requirements cards. These would be the goals of the game for the job-hunters.

Each of the players will be assigned some parameters, and some Weakness cards. By using the Activity cards, players work together to overcome those weakness, and eventually meet the requirements of those companies.

Every once in a while, some Bad Events occur. Players must also work together to beat them too. At the end of the game year, if all of the players have managed to meet the requirements of some of the companies, they are hired and they win. If anyone fails to get a job, they all lose.

Publisher's summary
キャリアアドバイザーが作った「就職内定」のためのゲーム。
1年間の就職活動を、1時間で体験することができます。20年間のアドバイザー経験をもとに制作したので、ゲームとは思えないリアルな体験が得られます。
純粋なゲームとして攻略してもいいですが、制作者がファシリテータとなって、就職活動支援のコンテンツとしていただくことで、より高い効果が得られます。
publishers: Career30.net (キャリアサーティードットネット)

description:
Table Air Combat is a fast, simple air combat game that can be played on any flat surface. Each ruleset is a self-contained game including cut-out miniatures of one aircraft and a set of rules. All you need are some six-sided dice and some coins to provide weight for the aircraft counters.  Everything else can be printed from this ruleset.   Download a free set of the complete rules here: Flight Operations Manual
Each player has a flight of four fighters, represented by two counters.  Aircraft counters move by means of curved “performance rulers” that represent the aircraft’s historical cornering ability and maximum speed.
TAC is designed for portability;  The entire game can fit in a plastic bag.  Even the largest battles can take place on a fast-food restaurant table.

The Il-2 Shturmovik was a heavily-armoured attack plane used by the Soviet Union.  It specialized in low-level attacks, and was occasionally pressed into service as a fighter.
