This set is a self-contained rulebook that includes miniatures for the Il-2 Shturmovik and includes four scenarios that reference other aircraft:
Air and Bread: Attack a bridge on the Berezina river to slow down the invading German army.  This is a standalone scenario using only the Il-2.
Circle of Death:  Il-2s form a defensive Lufbery circle against  Bf-109F fighters.
Anti-Transport: The Luftwaffe tries to supply by air, but has a shortage of transports.  He-111 bombers are loaded with supplies and used instead. The Il-2 aircraft are assigned to shoot down the lumbering aircraft.. The airfield is defended by Bf-109F fighters.
Zementbomber:  Fight the battle of Kursk by attacking German tanks.  Il-2 are escorted by Yak-7 fighters, but the Luftwaffe defends with Fw-190A fighters and counter-attacks Soviet tanks with Ju-88 bombers.

Table Air Combat is a fast, simple air combat game that can be played on any flat surface. Each ruleset is a self-contained game including cut-out miniatures of one aircraft and a set of rules. All you need are some six-sided dice and some coins to provide weight for the aircraft counters.  Everything else can be printed from this ruleset.
Each player has a flight of four fighters, represented by two counters.  Aircraft counters move by means of curved “performance rulers” that represent the aircraft’s historical cornering ability and maximum speed.
TAC is designed for portability;  The entire game can fit in a plastic bag.  Even the largest battles can take place on a fast-food restaurant table.

The Yak-7 was a trainer for the Yak-1 but was developed into a new fighter.  It can also substitute as a Yak-1 or Yak-9.
This set is a self-contained rulebook that includes miniatures for the Yak-7 and contains five scenarios that reference other aircraft:
Barbarossa: Yak-1s defend an airfield against the Luftwaffe's Ju-87 Stukas escorted by Bf-109E fighters.
Teeth of the Bear: Yak-7s ambush a group of  Bf-109F fighters.
586th:  Stalingrad is under attack from Ju-88 bombers and  Bf-109F fighters. An all-female fighter regiment defends the city with their Yak-1 fighters.
Jet Ambush:  A German Me-262 jet fighter is caught unaware by a Soviet aces flying a pair of  Yak-9 fighters.
Yaks vs. Lightnings:  USAAF P-38 fighters mistakenly attack Soviet ground forces in Yugoslavia.  Soviet Yak-9s coutnerattack.

Isle of Domination is a turn-based Strategy-Economy Board Eurogame.

The game starts with random map creation. Several hexes build up the map in any shape. There are also resource-tokens placed on some hexes those add more randomness to the map.
Players can build cities, armies on game map.

Gameplay goes around resources. There are two types of resources: 

     Countable resources like food, wood, stone, ore, test-tube and gold. 
     Special resources like horse, iron, fish, wheat, marble, diamond and mana are used to develop (science) or build (building) something. 


There is no storage for resources, a player needs to have access (by having a city near the resource) to certain number and type of resources to build something. Food is the most important resource, each city, extra population and army eats one food. You need food-surplus to build a city/army/extra population, but no problem if your food production goes under your food production (no starving to death).

Each player has an Economy Page (like character page in RPGs), that shows the state of his empire. Players use small markers for recording various things.

Player's turn have 4 phases:

     Money Phase: Player adds the number of his/her gold production to treasury. (Gold is the only resource in Isle of Domination, that is storaged.)
     Army Moving Phase: Players can move their armies by grid lines of hexes. Combat system is very simple: if attacker's army is stronger than defender army, defender army has to be withdrawn. No die rolling this time. Army power is based on sciences, and have modifiers by terrain, city or buildings.
     Dismiss Phase: Player can dismiss any number of cities, armies and extra population to create the needed food-surplus to build something.
     Building Phase: Player can build a City (on a hex edge occupied by an army), increase Population, build an Army, develop a Science and/or build a Building in this phase.


The goal of this game is to reach one of the six Victory Conditions. It is possible that all other Players have stronger economies, stronger armies and bigger empires, but still you win because you reach one of the Victory Conditions.
There are 6 different types of Victory:


     Territory Victory: You dominate by having a large number of cities.
     Military Victory: You dominate by having a very strong and large army.
     Population Victory: You dominate by outnumbering others in population.
     Science Victory: You dominate by knowledge. (You develop Future Science 2.)
     Cultural Victory: You dominate by your people's cultural influence. (You build Cathedral.)
     Magical Victory: You dominate by magical superiority. (You have a city near every Mana resource.)


Randomized map preparation may make some of the Victory types easier than others. But when several players pursuit the same victory, it will be much more difficult to achieve it.

Description from the publisher:

The Resurgence Expansion Pack for Project Airlock contains 18 new cards, including new characters and abilities, new missions, and powerful new additions to the deck such as Engine Failure and Nullify. It is shuffled into the Project Airlock game deck to add more variety and allow for more complex strategies.

Madrasso, also known as Mandrasso or Magrasso, is perhaps the most popular and widespread card game in Venice and the surrounding region, where it has over the last 50 years practically replaced the older game Scarabocion. In the ranking and values of cards and the presence of a trump suit it is related to Briscola, but because of the necessity to follow suit (as in Tressette) Madrasso offers greater scope for sophisticated card play technique.

Slipstream is a compact card game for 2-4 players in which you take the role of scientists fighting your way through shifting dimensions to reach your control panel to return to your dimension. You are in a race across the 25 laboratory cards between you and your control panels. Each player gets a 'Slipstream Gun' card that allows them to warp themselves or others to different dimensions. Some dimensions contain cards that make the trip across the board easier, while other dimensions make the journey more perilous. Players must balance their urge to race across the board with their need to blast other players into neighboring dimensions. To win, a player must make it across the lab to their own control panel.

Each turn every player gets to perform one movement action and one 'Slipstream Gun' action. The movement action allows the players to move their token to a neighboring card, edging ever closer to their control panel, and victory. For their 'Slipstream Gun' action, players can choose to shoot any player (sending them to another dimension) or they can 'recalibrate' their gun by flipping the card over. This changes what dimension players will go to when shot with the 'Slipstream Gun'.

Players start at one end of a 4x5 card grid. Their control panels are on the opposite side. All of the laboratory cards are face down and as players move on to each card, it is flipped face up and the events on the face of the card are incurred. These can offer benefits like moving forward or gaining an item card. They can also be negative like losing a turn or being sent to the 'Dark World' a dimension in which the cards are much more challenging.

The game ends when any player is able to make it all the way across the board and steps on to the colored control panel that matches their player token.

Based on the card game "War", but with a twist. Take part in the epic battle between gun-toting kitties and power-hungry aliens.

If you have ever played the game of "War", then No Mercy will be instantly familiar. "No Mercy" follows the same game mechanics, in which each player turns over the top card of their deck, and the player with the highest card wins.

But there is a twist: the player with the lowest number has the option to pull the facedown card from the top of the modifier deck; modifiers can add or take away from your card's number, and can also be an action that has a positive or negative result on your hand.

As with war, the winner is the first person to lose all their cards, or, optionally, the player with the largest deck when a time limit is reached.

Each card contains an imaginative drawing by the children's book illustrator and comic artist Travis Hanson.

boardgame: Chocobo's Crystal Hunt: Chocobo's Dungeons & Monsters
description: Receive the blessing of the Crystals and send your brave companions into the depths to defeat legendary monsters in this fun and fast-paced expansion!

Description from the publisher:

Brace for Impact! is a 2-Player micro dice game that requires lightning-quick decisions, dexterity and stamina. As a Naval Submarine Commander your mission is to either gather enough vital intel or destroy your enemy’s crew before your enemy beats you to it. Each Commander controls two dice and a meeple crew aboard a submarine equipped with torpedoes. Surface and dive, load and fire torpedoes and manage your crew to become victorious. Do you have what it takes to win?

How to Play:

THE SETUP: Prepare for your epic battle by separating the black and blue game pieces. Each player receives two dice, 1 chit, 1 torpedo, and 3 Meeples of their color. The 8 intelligence gems and extra meeples are set off to the side within reach of both players.

Ready? START ROLLING! Engage your aquatic rival in a real-time, action-packed battle by rolling your dice. Manage to roll a 7 or doubles and you have a few options:

ON THE SURFACE: 7's on the surface allow you to dive underwater OR add 1 crew member. Doubles on the surface allow you to load your torpedo OR drop depth charges to remove 1 of your rival's gathered intelligence gems.

UNDERWATER: 7's underwater allow you to return to the surface OR gather 1 intelligence gem. Doubles underwater allow you to load your Torpedo OR fire your torpedo (if loaded) to remove 1 of your rival's crew members.

Play continues until a player loses their entire crew or gathers 5 intelligence gems!

Hier kommt die Maus is a simple die-rolling game for children that features the main character of a popular German children's TV program, "Die Sendung mit der Maus" (The Program with the Mouse), which first aired in 1971.

Players take turns rolling the die and moving accordingly. If they end up on a spot showing two arrows, they they have to move forwards or backwards according to the colour of the pips. Additionally, on two spots players can fall back. The first player to reach the last spot on the board – the tv studio – wins!

This game shares its designer with Mausefalle (1980) and re-implements it.
family: TV Shows: Die Sendung mit der Maus

description: The Masked Pursuit is a fast-paced card game for  2-4 people where all players play at the same time. Place your cards as fast as possible and before your opponents. The fastest wins!

Educational game in which players roll and move around the board.  Each space has a stack of cards players read, gaining information about gang activities and how to fight them (and points).  First player to 315 points (or circle the board 5 times) wins.

On The Slopes is a fast-paced skiing adventure for 2-4 players. Each player is a skier or snowboarder racing around a mountain, trying to be the first to visit every lodge.

On The Slopes was one of five finalists in the 2016 Hasbro Game Design Challenge.

On your turn, you have three actions, which you can use to go up a lift, ski down a run, or pick up a stone from a lodge.  But runs and lifts only work in one direction, you must carefully plan your route for the day.

Whenever you pick up a stone, you also get a Snow Card, which can give you benefits like extra action tokens or a one-time-only ability.  Some Snow Cards let you help or hinder other players.

When going down runs, you can choose between easy green runs, intermediate blue runs, or advanced or expert black diamond runs.  The more difficult runs are faster, but you might fall and have to take a Wipeout Card, which can cause bad things to happen.

Advanced players can play with special characters that give each player a unique ability they can use throughout the game.

Stack the monsters to form a giant pyramid! Who can match the dowels with the colors of the monsters and carefully slide them in between each figure without knocking them down?
5 quick games with varying rules and difficulty levels to provide engaging play and stacking fun for toddlers two and up. With entertaining options for older kids as well.
Each game varies slightly in its complexity which means as your child grows, you can advance to harder games or harder versions of the games you already know.
Contents include: 12 stacking monsters, 12 plugs, 1 stacking frame, 4 tiles, 1 die and set of instructions.

Word Burp! is a fast paced word party game that rewards the fastest players each round.  During each round 2 to 4 letters are randomly selected, one at a time, from a pool of letters.  They are then placed, face up, in reverse order, within clear view of all players.  Players are in a race to say a word that contains the letters in the order shown while meeting the requirements of that round (which are chosen randomly).

This battle shows the Estonians fighting the Danish in 1219 on the northern shores of Estonia.

Ownership of a game from A la Charge! series is needed to play this scenario.

The scenario includes 1 new map & 30 new counters.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
Enter the futuristic universe of Antagonism through four distinct scenarios in which only the finest strategists will survive. In competitive or semi-cooperative mode, dominate or destroy your opponents!

The game is portable and magnetic, easy, fast and different every time.

Game overview

     Players choose one of the four scenarios and a group of 15 custom pawns with the same symbol and place them on the corresponding colors. Each scenario is grid-based with a different theme and objectives. 
     Victory conditions: The main objective depending on the scenarios is to hold the base(s) for 3(2) turns or kill the enemy leader(s). A "King of the Hill" variant for three players is available in a scenario.
     On every turn you can move up to five different pawns, one of which should always be the leader (black pawn). Pawns can move up to two spaces in any direction; red pawns can move up to three spaces in any direction. Blue pawns can move through clouds without penalty and use the warps.
     Pawns of the same color can stack up to three pieces high. This increases the strike force value and defense value. Pawns can detach and move on their own from a stack. The bottom pawn acts as a carrier.
     Every single pawn has a strike force Level 1 (L1). The black pawn (the leader) has a strike force L3 but its defense remains at L1.
     To attack an opponent's pawn, you must move your pawn onto a square occupied by the antagonist. By attacking an opponent pawn with a superior level, you execute a suicide-attack; your pawn(s) is(are) sacrificed and the same number of antagonist pawns are eliminated.

The goal of the game is to move your pawn to the finish while moving across a grid reaching numbers on Easter-egg-tiles from 1-24 in the right order. In your turn you roll two dice and move forward if you can. Bunnies are sometimes in your way and by spending coins you can move quicker. Make sure you reach the finish the first and you will win.

A little blush, some lip color, and eyeshadow can perk up even an exhausted look and fill the wearer with confidence and social energy. Yet, makeup can be a complicated thing. Youtube is filled with Internet celebrities who give expert advice, magazines roll out new style guides practically monthly, and everyone has a different face, meaning something that works for one person might not work so well for the next. To give younger girls an introduction to makeup, as well as a few deeper lessons about true beauty, Decker Sisters presents Pretty Pro: The Exciting Makeover Race Game.

To play, each player picks a character from the four playing pieces: Lauren, Izzy, Lexie, and Zoe. These characters come with profile cards that give a little background to them. These do not have any affect on the gameplay; they just allow players to get a little more into the game by picking their favorites. Each has a favorite color, pet, and hobby. They also give tips, both in fashion (“accessorize with scarves”), beauty (“always wear sunscreen when outside”), and internal beauty, described as a PrettyHeart quality (“always have a positive attitude”).

Pretty Pro is a straightforward game suitable for younger players. On a turn, a player rolls the dice and moves her or his character to a new square on the board. The goal is to land on each square necessary for the six components of a makeover: Lipstick, Blush, Eyeshadow, Brows, Earrings, and the PrettyHeart Tiara, which denotes inner beauty. The board also features stylish cities, where players draw cards. Some are good, such as “You are always kind with your words and never say anything hurtful. Find the PrettyHeart Tiara.” Others are not so good, “You went to bed very late last night. Now you feel grouchy and look tired. Lose your next turn.”

Facemask card. Players might be unlucky in landing on a Beauty Block square and missing out on something they need or gain the Facemask, which stops players from adding any new makeup. When a player lands on Beauty Block, she or he may move it to a new space, potentially keeping another player from getting the final square needed to win.

Perhaps the best part of Pretty Pro is the coloring sheets. The set comes with colored pencils and a pad for each character, and extras may be printed off the website. Through the game, whenever a player lands on a space she needs, the player may color in the makeup on the character’s face. This is a great introduction into style, seeing what colors work, what is not enough, and what might be too much.

Pretty Pro is a game for two to four players aged six and up. It is a moderately long game, taking twenty to thirty minutes to play, depending on how quick and lucky the players are. With its randomized dice-rolling play and fun coloring sheets, younger players will enjoy it the most as they eagerly assemble the parts of their makeovers.

Which make-up artist will win the game?  Players use strategy, skill and luck as they race their friends to create the perfect look, but soon learn that inner beauty is needed before any makeover is complete.  Game cards reward kindness and cover fun topics including beauty and fashion tips.  Hurry to be the first to capture the PrettyHeart Tiara and finish the face to win this exciting makeover race!
 Ages 6+, 2-4 players, 15-20 minute play time, reading not required to play.
 Contents:  Game board, 52 card deck, coloring pencils, facemask, beauty block, PrettyHeart Tiara,  set of 4 make-up sketch pads, set of fun info cards, character playing pieces, stands, dice, instructions.
Asteroids is a board game adaptation of the Atari video game from the 1980s.

Clean Up Your Room! is a family friendly card game with a simple goal: score the most points while trying to get all of the objects off your room floor. On each player's turn, she can put an object, such as power tools or a pink fairy armadillo, one of her pieces of furniture. She might also steal an object from a sibling's room floor. Or, she might throw something from her room into another room. Players can also use event cards to mix things up, but sometimes they have unexpected consequences like making animals run from room to room! When one player's room floor is clear of objects, the game ends. All players count up points for objects put away plus any bonus points and subtract one point for any objects left on their room floors.

Water is an introductory deckbuilder based on the water cycle. Game play is symbol based to enable younger children and language independent play.

In Water, players build their deck by purchasing cards from the river using rain and snow tokens. The game ends when all the snow tokens are removed from the cloud, so strategy of when to buy and when to collect comes into play. Other deckbuilding mechanisms such as getting rid of weaker cards help new players lean the basics of this mechanic.

Thematically, cards can move rain or snow between the cloud, a player's reservoir, and other players' reservoirs. Cards can also change the state of the water from gas in the cloud to liquid or solid in the reservoirs.

With a mixture of skill and luck, players will build up their own map of landmarks while trying to stop others from doing the same.

On your turn you will aim to build new landmarks by playing landmark cards, while demolishing other people's landmarks by playing attack cards. Jules the tourist visits each landmark in turn. If you succeed in demolishing an opponent's landmark while Jules is visiting it, that player is eliminated.

To win a player must either build a winning number of landmarks, or eliminate all other players.

East India Companies is a competitive deck-builder where you take control of your shipping empire.  Your fleet of white-winged sailing ships are the collateral you utilize to develop and build your strategy, ensuring your victory against your merchant competitors.  Maritime portraitures of the 17th through 19th centuries give this deck builder a historic feel, and allow for a more intuitive notion of card/effect as play progresses.

Playing in similar fashion to Dominion, each player begins the game with a starter deck of ships (currency) and waterfront property (victory points).  Leveraging their Fleet cards, players determine the best cards and combos from 15 stacks of 25 available with which to best build up victory points until such time as one of several end game scenarios are triggered.  After a medium learning curve, a four player game should progress over the course of about 75 minutes.  Some game mechanics do allow for player vs. player attacks, but will depend on what stacks the randomizer brings onto the table.

The goal of the game is to have the most waterfront property and Trade Routes in your deck once the final round is played.  Any player may trigger the end game condition, but may want to consider what their opponents have been doing over the course of the game.

boardgame: Random Encounter: Seas of the Sea Chicken
description: Random Encounter is a strategic, number-based, pixel-art card game for all ages. Players build encounters made up of various creatures, then try to win as many battles as possible at the table in the pursuit of loot! Each fully customizable deck is made up of basic number creature cards along with several trick creature cards to throw your opponents off guard. Two or more players compete to reach five loot before other players by either winning the most battles or stealing loot from the coffers of their fallen enemies.

An entertaining board game that familiarises you with the Royal Forest of Dean in Gloucestershire and acts like a guide book telling you about the area.  It will help you find your way around and enhances your further visits.

Each player chooses an empty town on the map along with a marker of the appropriate colour.  Each player receives 15 cards appropriate to their starting town and must make their way around the map visiting attractions and avoiding diversions.

Sailing across the ocean from another land, the Dynasty Invasion brings two full new Clans, who will count on their own Seven Lucky Gods and introduce an extra Set of Season cards! This expansion brings a lot of extra variety to Rising Sun, featuring two Clans that forego the allegiance of Japanese Monsters, relying on their own Seven Lucky Gods instead.

The Dynasty Invasion expansion box contains:

27 Miniatures 
       1 Moon Clan Daimyo 
       3 Moon Clan Shinto 
       3 Moon Clan Bushi A 
       3 Moon Clan Bushi B 
       1 Sun Clan Daimyo 
       3 Sun Clan Shinto 
       3 Sun Clan Bushi A 
       3 Sun Clan Bushi B 
       7 Lucky Gods 
    4 Plastic Clan Markers 
    2 Clan Screens 
    1 Political/War Tile 
    8 Stronghold Tokens 
    15 Season Cards 
    7 Lucky God Monster Cards 
    22 Miniature Bases
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
It's the 1940s, and Nazi spies have infiltrated three cities in Chile. Department D.50 of the police is charged with uncovering their evil plans.

One player in this asymmetrical game is the Nazi spy and has to enhance bases in Chile's three major cities, gain control over resources and protect their bases from the investigations of the D.50. The others are the investigators and also have to collect resources, but also 20 evidence points. With these, they can manage to shut down the Nazi bases to win the game.

The game is based on historical events and has been sponsored by the Fondo Nacional para el Desarrollo Cultural y las Artes (National foundation for Cultural Development and the Arts).
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
"The most merciful thing in the world, I think, is the inability of the human mind to correlate all its contents."

-H.P. Lovecraft

The entirety of mankind hefts no consequence, for our world is merely a borrowed rock. Long before ancient life, as indicated by human evidence, winked into existence, the Great Old Ones walked our landmasses, lived in our oceans, and hung in our skies. But most of them eventually left Earth and fell into a death-like sleep. These horrors of the universe render us no more significant than the insects that we crush under our boots as a matter of living.

Rites of Cthulhu offers a new dimension to traditional card play. Players who like face card games like hearts will love this Lovecraftian twist on standard gaming. Players compete against each other as rival cultists in a series of 6 rites (mini games) to win artifacts. Each artifact adds new interest to the game by introducing a new rule that effects play. After playing all 6 mini-games, the player with the most points from artifact cards wins and becomes the grand cultist.

Fantasy Chesslords / 棋幻爭霸

Fantasy Chesslords is a fantasy themed strategy game that combines the gameplay experience of Chess with Customizable Card Battle.

Two players taking turn playing cards and moving pieces until a player wins, either by destroying the opposing LORD, or moving your LORD onto the opponent's PORTAL STONE.

Here is the football match Poland v Germany. Quite small size but has very high rank! The table becomes a stadium, cards—turf and players—team.

Piłka w grze is a simple pattern recognition game combined with very simple soccer match simulation. The game consists of deck of large cards with images associated with football and wooden ball token. Five of these cards laid on a table produce small board—football pitch—combined of five spaces.

The aim of the game is to score three goals. In order to score a goal the player have to move the ball to the utmost space in front of his or her opponent and win the decisive duel.

To move a ball or win a duel players compete, one trying to identify pairs of common symbols faster than their adversary. Ball may be moved from its space to adjacent one only if a player recognizes and names the only image, which these two spaces have in common. Before each move new card is played on board, putting new symbols in play.

Whoever scores third goal, wins the game. Teams may also determine that the game lasts until one team reaches a different, mutually agreed outcome or score more goals in the allotted time.

You and your friends can help Little Red Riding Hood get to her Grandmother's house before the Wolf. Remember which pine tree he is hiding behind in the forest and choose the right pine tree to move forward. The Huntsman, foxes and imps will help you along the way.

A kids' cooperative game roll-n-move without rolling the dice (you pick a card that represents an action) with a classic theme, featuring a memory element.

How to play:

The first player must take a card from the market, turn it and show to others. They must then perform the action indicated on the card. The following player will do the same and so on. Game ends as soon as tree reach the grandmother, once there all you need to do it flip them over and see who is hiding at the bottom:

If the Hunter and Little Red Riding Hood get ahead of the Wolf, then the whole team won and they beat the game!

However, if the first one that came to the grandmother is the gray Wolf, then if the next one who makes it to the grandmother is the Hunter, and not the Little Red Riding Hood then the team can still win as the Hunter can beat the grey wolf.

Different Levels of difficulty
Easy level: During preparation of the game Little Red Riding Hood is necessary to remove the card "Cheeky Fox" from the marked cards.

Intermediate: Before the game, mix all the trees.

Difficult level: During preparation of the Little Red Riding Hood Whoo hop game is necessary to remove the card "Curious Gnome" from the marked cards. Before the game, mix all the trees.

Cards with the characters pictures:
Little Red Riding Hood: You need to make one move forward with the tree, beneath which the Little Red Riding Hood hides.

Hunter: you need to make one move forward with the tree, beneath which the Hunter hides.

Wolf: you need to make one move forward with the tree, beneath which the gray Wolf hides.

Action cards:
All forward: you need to make one move forward with all the tree's.

Curious Gnome: you need to choose one tree and find out who is hiding under it.

Cheeky Fox: you need to swap any of two tree's around so that they stand in each others place.
family: Folk Tales & Fairy Tales: Little Red Riding Hood

description:
You and your monster friends are worried as the villagers have finally had enough of all of your shenanigans and are hunting you all down one by one. It's every monster for themselves in this game of deduction, deception and survival.

In Cry Monster! each player is a classic monster like Frankenstein's monster, The Invisible Man or Dracula. Each round the villagers put up a reward poster for one unlucky monster. Your job is to pierce the disguises of your fellow monsters and determine who is who. Once you know, you have to decide if you throw them to the villagers or help them by deceiving everyone into thinking it's one of the other monsters. Of course, if you are the wanted monster it's time to pull off your greatest act of deception. You have to work fast because the pitchfork and torch crowd is growing quickly and starting to Cry Monster!

The game takes place over one week in a bustling Japanese village. Each day, you will employ Farmers, Carpenters, Peasants and Merchants to harvest rice and construct buildings.

The more daring might send Ninjas to raid the Palace Treasury, or recruit Samurai to prevent the predicted raids. You might even persuade one of the more powerful inhabitants to help you: the Shogun, the Daiymo, or even the Emperor himself!

Ultimately, the player who best uses their cunning to gain the most coins wins the game.

Good Little Ninjas is a card-drafting game for 2-4 players, aged 12+ that takes about 30 minutes to play. It is available as a free print-and-play download from the Good Little Games website.

Flow is a 2-4 player card game set in the fantasy world of Flow.

Goal: Get the highest score by the end of the game. In case of a tie, last player wins.

End Game : Number of the decks equal to the number of players runs out. e.g. 2 players = 2 empty decks, 4 players = 4 empty decks.

Gameplay: Every round, players do 3 available actions in any order they choose. 
A - Activate card abilities
B - Buy
C - Card Draw

boardgame: Fortunate Sons: The Anonymous Battle, March 26 1970
description:
The NVA laid a clever trap for their nemesis, the 1st Air Cavalry Division, in the remote northern region of Tay Ninh Province, not far from the border with Cambodia. Charlie Company of the 8th Regiment, 1st Air Cavalry was dispatched to inspect suspicious activity by the NVA north of Fire Base Illingworth. On March 26th they were ambushed by the 1st Battalion of the NVA's 272 Regiment, 9th Infantry Division. Deep in jungle terrain, Charlie Company was cut-off and unable to call-in accurate artillery or air support. The jungle canopy also made impossible any extraction by helicopter. It looked like the Communists were about to wipe-out or capture over 100 US soldiers.

Four kilometers away was Alpha troop of the 11th Armored Cavalry, along with Alpha Company of the 8th Regiment of the 1st Air Cav. Commander John Poindexter, monitoring the increasingly desperate radio messages from Charlie Company wasted no time in ordering his men to "Saddle Up!" in order to rescue their trapped comrades. Busting through jungle and risking ambush, the rescue and battle fought here in March, 1970 rank among the war's most epic and amazing fights.

Fortunate Sons is the companion “sister” game to Bad Moon Rising: The Battle for Firebase Illingworth. The two games use the same design and scale. Players use a standard deck of playing cards to activate units and resolve some game events, as well as a six-sided die.
family: Series: Standard Deck Activation System (High Flying Dice Games)

description:
Saloon Tycoon: The Ranch Expansion expands your Saloon Tycoon experience by adding a new ranch board for each player. Each turn, you earn a ranch point to be spent on developing your ranch. You can acquire horses and cow meeples, new tiles for your ranch board like the bunkhouse, new animal pens, and new rooms for your ranch home!

New ways to score that supplement building out your saloon add a new dynamic to the game without being needlessly complicated.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

boardgame: GKR: Heavy Hitters – Urban Wasteland Expansion
description:
Transform your GKR: Heavy Hitters gameboard into an urban wasteland with a set of durable plastic buildings and decorations! The add-on pack includes:
- x1 Big Little Buddy Miniature (this was an add-on during the Kickstarter campaign)
- x1 Unit Card
- x3 Weapon Cards
- x5 BLB Sponsor Cards
- x10 Plastic City Buildings
- x10 Rubbles Bases
- x4 Corporation Toppers
- x40 Holo-board Tags
- x3 Building Spires
- x12 Sentry Guns
- x12 Sentry Gun Cards
- x2 Sticker Sheets
- x1 Setup Sheet
- x1 Rulebook

boardgame: GKR: Heavy Hitters – Big Little Buddy
description:
Available as a Kickstarter add-on or as part of the Urban Wasteland Expansion.

Big Little Buddy, a friend you can share! Need a friend? Call on the corporation with money to spend! Enter BUDDYFINDER™ - the premier subscription-based friend rental service. Rental friends starting as low as 12.99 a month!

In sport, there are usually a couple of big sponsors that pay for exclusive primary advertising and presence. In GKR: Heavy Hitters, Big Little Buddy is that guy. He’s made available for the match (as well as serviced and repaired after the match) by his premier sponsor – BUDDYFINDER ™. They’ve got money to burn and they want their name everywhere. IN YOUR FACE.

Subtle, Little Buddy is not.

When Big Little Buddy is deployed to the gameboard, he advertises for whichever Faction has unlocked him for that round. Players can try to claim Little Buddy by playing a special Sponsor Card – a move that can only be deflected by playing a counter-hack.

Once a player controls Lil Buddy, the fun begins. Move him during the Movement Phase, and use him to attack opponents in the Combat Phase with his own unique cards: 3 Weapon Cards and 1 Unit Card. Each round, he can only fire 1 of these weapons in his arsenal BUT he does not require Energy to fire. Making him really rather handy in a pinch. Once the Combat Phase is over, players lose control of him once more.

Like all Units, Little Buddy can be destroyed. Once he’s destroyed, he’s removed from the game completely. Buh bye, Little Buddy.

One important note: Lil Buddy is not considered a “Support Unit” so he’s not bound by Support Unit rules. That also means he can’t tag buildings. Standing at 70mm he’s a mid-size Unit that, we think you’ll find, more than pulls his own weight.

With Little Buddy, you’re not just getting an awesome new mini, 1 unique Unit Card, 5 new Sponsor Cards, and 3 new Weapon Cards. You’re getting a little friend to keep you warm through those long lonely nights.

boardgame: The Walking Dead: All Out War – Prelude to Woodbury Solo Starter Set
description:
The Prelude to Woodbury is a narrative single-player adventure featuring Brian ‘The Governor’ Blake and his exploits from before he rose to power in Woodbury and encountered Rick. The introductory quick-start tutorial will take you through the rules step-by-step as you play out the Governor’s story, and the box contains everything you need to get right into the action. You do not need the All Out War core set to play, but all of the Prelude to Woodbury components are all fully compatible with the core set, so you can use Brian and his equipment in your larger, two-player games.

This set contains 6 unique, collectible miniatures allowing you to add Brian ‘The Governor’ Blake to your games, along with 5 Walkers to battle your way through. It also contains an exclusive ‘Solo’ version of the game’s Event deck.
family: Game: The Walking Dead – All Out War

description:
Color Slap Card Game

The Slap the Splat card game!

Slap your way to victory in this test of color coordination and fast reflexes.

Players take turns placing cards face up in a pile.
Whenever a card showing a splat with matching text is flipped, players must race to Slap the Splat and win the pile!

Easy right? Things get crazy with splat cards with no text and splat cards with non matching text.

Whoever gets the most cards wins!

Description from the publisher:

Based on the Starport Scum scifi rules, Dungeon Scum is a narrative wargame intended to be played by role players and war gamers alike.
Gather your group of adventurers, set out on a dungeon delve and hope you make it back!
Get loot, see the sights, maybe go on an epic quest.

Dungeon Scum sets itself apart in a few ways:

    The combat rules are fast, simple and easy to remember, allowing you to fight multiple encounters in one gaming session.
    Unlike many RPG's, it includes a morale system for more realistic combats.
    It can be played solo, with encounter tables and monster lists for a fully random delve.
    It can be turned into a full-fledged role playing game.
    Characters can generated randomly in a few rolls or simply described with a couple of common-sense sentences.


Your hero is a SWORDFIGHTER with QUICK REFLEXES and a VOW OF SILENCE? Good, that's all we needed to know.

    Loot is well supplied with tons of magical items to find.
    6 types of magic with 10 spells in each system, plus "quest" spells used in campaign play.
    Campaign rules for letting your heroes become stronger..if they don't die.
    While the game is intended to use miniature figures, advice is provided to players who prefer playing without them.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description: Void Pirates presents a Sci-Fi skirmish miniature battle game using a streamlined version of the Goalsystem rules. Use whatever miniatures you own to play out exciting skirmishes in any future world! Build crews with as few as two miniatures, or as many as seven. These rules work in any scale between 10mm - 54mm, and offer flexible and fun character creation, furious combats, and rewarding campaign play. Gather your friends, a handful of six-sided dice, and your favorite miniatures, and blast-off for Sci-Fi fun!

boardgame: Easy Street (fan expansion for T.I.M.E Stories)
description:
A print and play expansion for T.I.M.E Stories.

Salvage Series: The Agency is facing budget cuts. You need to complete a series of covert missions to salvage the resources necessary to keep the operation running.

Easy Street Mission: Getting the largest pile of gold in history makes sense, but why is it in a small town suburb in 1952...?

Wage War in the Steampunk Apocalypse
The world has changed. Over a hundred years have passed after the American War for Independence was interrupted by the opening of the first rift and the Legion of Damned poured though. Humanity was facing extinction at the hands of the demonic and undead. Finally the Angelics arrived and set a stop to the expansion of the rifts and halted the free roaming Legion hordes. Once on the brink of extinction humanity has surged back, forging new countries and technologies. Now after decades of rebuilding the uneasy peace is starting to fracture. Skirmishes are becoming more frequent along the borders and nations turn to warmongering.
Titan Zero Industries LLC presents Ictus, a table top wargame set in the Stempunk apocalypse of 1909. Ictus features high quality miniatures, six unique factions, an all-new stacking rule set and multiple game scenarios including cooperative play. 
In standard Skirmish or competitive game scenarios the goal is to eliminate the other player(s) officers. However, in cooperative play scenarios the goals of the game can vary to include eliminating the enemy, surviving for a number of rounds or achieving a set goal. 
Each unit is a paintable miniature that comes with a corresponding card that details the abilities of that character. While many abilities are set, conflict is resolved by the rolling of six sided dice (D6), with rolls of 5’s and 6’s being considered successful. Stacking is the major game mechanic. The Shield Warden unit, for example, provides a +1 defensive bonus to all allies within 1”. If there are three Shield Wardens within 1” of each other the bonuses stack and each unit would receive a +3 defensive bonus.
There are six different factions to choose from; the British American Colonies, Mercenaries, United American Territories, Legion of Damned, Republic of Cascadia and Empire of Castile. Each faction has a unique look and play style.

"El Dorado discovered in Estado de Para!"

With the discovery of a large gold deposit, Brazil erupted into a frenzied gold rush.  Over ten thousand workers swarmed to become garimpeiro (gold prospectors in Portuguese) and make their millions.  The greed filled desires of the garimpeiro and their patrons bored deep holes into the earth like an inverted pyramid, and the once lush jungles were quickly stripped naked, giving the name of Serra Pelada to the dig site - "the Naked Mountain".

In Garimpeiro, each player becomes a patron of the garimpeiro (gold prospectors) who will lead them to seek fame and gold ore through various actions, in order to become the greatest man or woman in Serra Pelada.  It is a full-fledged worker placement game that simulates the excitement of gold prospecting.

boardgame: Batman Miniature Game: Arkham Knight Campaign Book
description:
Arkham Knight is the first campaign supplement for the Batman Miniature Game. This 132-page, full-colour book is the ultimate toolbox for devising, organizing and running your own campaigns with your friends, transforming your games from one-off encounters to a series of linked adventures, where every result counts towards ultimate victory or crushing defeat.


What is a Campaign?

At its most basic, a campaign is simply a series of linked battles between two or more players. The result of each game has an effect on the next, giving the winner an advantage in the later stages of the campaign. Inside the Arkham Knight Campaign book, you’ll find rules and guidelines for creating and running campaigns – taking your one-shot games and instead playing a series of linked scenarios, with ultimate victory coming to the crew that achieves its long-term goals. Everything from organizing a campaign to forming alliances or playing multiplayer games is covered, making this your one-stop source of campaign material.


Not only that, but within these pages you will find a complete, ready-to-run narrative campaign, recreating the Arkham Knight saga from the thrilling finale of Rocksteady’s Arkham trilogy, and giving you the chance to save or destroy Gotham City. Want to see what would happen if the Arkham Knight defeated Batman? Or if the Scarecrow became the most powerful crime-lord in Gotham? Now’s your chance! Every scenario in the campaign also comes with suggestions for alternative participants – so even if you don’t have the right models to play the Arkham Knight storyline, you can still play the campaign with the models in your collection. You can even use the scenarios as standalone ones if you want to, adding even more variety to your games!


Is it Just About Campaigns, Then?

Not quite. While much of the book is geared towards campaign play (as you’d expect), you’ll also find some awesome new rules that you can use in your regular games. Want to play a Predator Mode game, where a single hero stealthily takes down a mob of Henchmen? Now you can. Want to play four, five, or six-player games? We got you covered. Want character cards for a whole host of vehicles, including Batmobile variants, GCPD vans and Rattler Drones? Look no further.

Oh, and if you still needed an incentive to order this tome, there’s a chance to get your hands on a limited edition Arkham Knight model, with three different head variants, while stocks last.


“Tonight Ends with Gotham’s Fall….”
Aeon's End: The Void is an expansion for the standalone Aeon's End titles that features two new nemeses and two new breach mages.
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

boardgame: The Hobbit: Motion Picture Trilogy – There and Back Again

description:
Chicken Run is a simple collection game that takes strategy,  a little bit of luck, and a whole lot of fun.

Here are the facts: There’s chickens, roosters, and feed. Each player tries to pair four chickens of the same color along with a rooster and feed to complete a Chicken Run.

The game is fast paced– compete against other players to fill your chicken coops and complete your runs- the first to reach ten points wins. But watch out for action cards though as other players can steal or eat your chickens right from your coop.

While Chicken Run is simple, it is full of energy and excitement that develops as players attack one another with varmints, protect their coops from attacks with guard dogs, and trade with other players for the cards they need to complete a Chicken Run.

Two people dueling to try to get the most gems.

Pick cards and steal gems from your opponent to win.
But remember, don’t let the greed take you over.

Gems may be collected several ways, one, play a collect one gem card, or collect 4 gem fragments which you can pick up randomly in the deck.

If you have four of one kind of gem fragment, you can play them to get 6 gems, if you have four different kinds of gem fragments, you will make 4 gems.

You can also steal the enemy gems with cards that your pick up randomly.

You pick one card on the beginning of each turn, you may also play a pick two cards card, then you pick two cards from the deck.

You are only allowed to play one card a turn, but there are cards that you may play in addition to your one card your allowed to play,
such as, skip opponents turn, Pick two cards, Switch hands, etc.

the most important thing is to keep your opponent from getting gems, or he might win when your not paying attention.

Build a factory by hiring specialists, completing jobs, and buying new equipment. Be careful what jobs you finish, though, because everyone can piggyback on your contracts to raise capital for themselves.

Each turn in Piggyback × Factory, players can either add a free specialist to one of their three production lines or take a gamble that their factory can complete an order. If an order does get completed, that production line is reset to zero and all specialists are returned, while the player gets the value of the goods. At that moment, any other player can also piggyback on that order, although earning one fewer good. Players can then spend their money to either get victory points or buy more machines for their factory. At the end of a turn, each player advances all their production lines by one step, preparing more parts to run next turn.

Piggyback × Factory won the 2016 Game Market best game award.
publishers: すまいる120円工房 (Sumeru 120 Yen Studio)

description: Roll and move race game around a map of Australia. The first to Tasmania wins. Along the way are hazards and bonuses. Printed in water-based inks on fabric. The playing pieces are wooden disks stamped with Australian animals.

Night prowler is awarded to the top 4 players in an Evolution tournament.

Night Prowler: Prevent 1 population loss due to heat.  Once per feeding phase, this species can negate one defensive trait when attacking.

CALAS is a fancy and competitive table-top game inspired by the Middle Ages history with our fancy creation added. You can escape from the reality by engaging in this exciting Middle Ages-simulated platform, where you can pick a remarkable team to start your adventure. To end this chaotic period, we need a powerful leader to conquer CALAS, a mysterious city girt by mountains, ocean, deserts and forests.

CALAS is a strategic and network building game for 2 to 4 players.
Each player controls the leader of a faction. To win the game, you must be the first person to reach 60 points.
The main mechanism for players to move is by rolling dice. 
Moreover, there are Dice cards that players may use to change the result of a roll.

In Calas, every step you take leads you to buildings. There are three types of buildings: 
1.Public buildings: Guildhalls
 -When entering guildhalls, players gain 2 Strategy cards;To recruit characters and expand the faction; or Upgrade your cities.
2.Resource buildings: Villages, Mines
 -Occupied villages produce soldiers for its owner’s team.  
 -Occupied mines upgrade cities.
3.Cities
 -Occupied cities produce soldiers and great amounts of Honor points.
  Consequently, every leader seizes and jealously guards their cities.

Players may choose one of the three types of battle when entering a city occupied by an enemy
1.They may start champion warfare. If the attacker wins, they take soldiers from the city. 
2.Players may start an honor fight. The winner gains Honor points as well as reduced soldiers for the opponent.
3.Players may start a war of aggression. If the attacker wins, they may occupy it.
Dice cards apply to all kinds of battle. Using Dice cards wisely is essential to winning the game!

Every Character card has its own unique skills. You can choose your own starting characters corresponding to your own strategy.
Also, Strategy cards make the game more strategic and fun.

In order to win, players have to cooperate and make deals with each other to try gaining advantages without drawing too much attention to themselves or being in last place. Take the initiative to thrive, but step back when the situation is dangerous. These are the best parts of playing Calas!

Description from the designer:

A fun, interactive family board game that teaches you how to get smart about money. Players make real-life investment and career decisions, learn to balance their portfolios and plan for retirement, manage budgets and debt, and answer questions on fundamental principles of economics and finance. No prior experience needed.

Players start out with $100,000 cash, roll the dice, and proceed around the board. Spaces on the board correspond to different life events such as getting paid a salary, going on vacation, or picking from the three decks of cards that sit at the center of the board. The three decks of cards are: news flashes, knowledge cards, and wild cards. Newsflashes provide a news story, and players must buy and sell investments accordingly in order to win or lose money. Users may accept advice from other players for a shared win or loss in the investment. Knowledge cards are multiple choice cards that teach users key finance information that will be used in the game and life. Wild cards are life events that will cause the loser to win or lose money depending on whether or not they own the right assets. To win, a user must double their money and own a home, life and health insurance, and a retirement plan.

There are several levels of game play that allow for beginner users all the way through advanced users to play the game and be continually challenged. Additional card decks are available through our company website as they become available.

Rather than having the standard two cards, a cockpit and a machine, Ignacy Lukasiewicz has a single 1×2 card depicting a cockpit with an oil drilling tower.

Ability: Gain an additional cog and a heat (red) die whenever you discard a drafted card for dice or cogs.
family: Organizations: The Game Artisans of Canada

description:
Carrots Out is an expansion for Carrotia.

In Carrots Out the Master Rabbit must escape the mine and in order to do that he must protect his torch. If the torch lights out, then the Master Rabbit will face a terrible threat, as the enemy is waiting in the dark.

A dark journey is awaiting Master Rabbit in the land of Noscarrotu. This time, Master Rabbit will have to encounter Vampire Rabbits. Walking through a dark maze, Master Rabbit must create a path leading outside the maze in any of the four corners of it. He must collect at least 10 carrots and escape before Noscarrotu awakens!

This task though, will not be easy to achieve. Vampire Rabbits and Noscarrotu have special abilities capable to stop Master Rabbit. Noscarrotu has created a magic meter, the Noscarrotu meter. As long the numbers are decreasing, special effects take place in the game, effects that work in favor of the dark master and his puppets. Master Rabbit will have the chance to succeed having the support of three great heroes, the Psychic, the Priest and the Hunter.

Who will prevail through this dark encounter?

boardgame: Barsoom (fan expansion for Ticket to Ride)
description:
This map is an unofficial fan made expansion. Symbols & Graphics Copyright © 2004-2015 Days of Wonder, Inc. Produced with permission of Days of Wonder, for non-commercial use only.

It is a sci-fi map of Mars conceived in the books of Edgar Rice Burroughs and developed by cartographer Oberon Zell.  It also featured in the Disney film John Carter.

It follows the principles of the basic Ticket to Ride game on a special board with special cards and rules. The object of the game is to gain the most points by travelling various routes and outwitting other players. You will need the train pieces and the train cards from either the US or Europe base games (or you can buy them from the Days of Wonder shop.

There is an extra set of explorer cards for the cities, each with a value to be earned by visiting that city. These cards are dealt/picked up alongwith tickets in the same quantity and with the same 'keep/dispose' rules.

There are also challenge cards which may help or hinder you. The 9 bonuses can give you extra points.

There are many new options in the rules: exchange all your cards at the start but keep all the new ones: discard 5 cards and pick up 5 new ones, as your turn: earn extra points: and many more. You can also call time when your wagon stock reaches 3, if you want, giving you the advantage of stopping other players completing tickets.

Master Rabbit returns and this time must face the powerful and cunning Tuttancarrot. The mighty Egyptian king has ordered his servants to protect well his kigdon. Mummies are walking around to prevent Master Rabbit from achieving his victory. Master Rabbit must fight to keep the Amulet Power-meter at maximum power and at the same time must collect at least 10 carrots in order to defeat Tuttancarrot.

In his mission, Master Rabbit will receive some unexpected assistance from the bonuses that he will find in the Sarcophagus tokens. Yet, the path to the victory will be full of traps as Tuttancarrot demands to build the maze in the formation of a pyramid in order to move freely through it, so he can defeat Master Rabbit once and for all!

Will our hero come victorious through this trial?

Description from the publisher:

In your dreams, you are haunted by monsters. After some sleepless nights, you want to get rid of them. However, this is easier said than done. The monsters are always changing colors and shapes. Above this, when you think that you have gotten rid of them all, they suddenly reappear in numbers.

Monster Boo is a fun fast paced bluffing game, where you try to get rid of the monsters in your dreams by discarding cards from your hand. Will you be able to free your dreams first?

Description from the publisher:

This is a set of new characters that can be added in any game of the 12 Realms Universe.

With this pack you get 8 different heroes, with their minis and boards: Alice, Mad Hatter, Peter Pan, Tinkerbell, Sherlock Holmes, Dr. Watson, Dorothy and Toto.

You can also use these characters with the companions mechanic, described in 12 Realms: Ancestor's Legacy.

Description from the designer:

This simple set of card- and dice-driven “Fast-Play” rules is designed for use in re-fighting naval battles in the ‘Age of Sail’ (early 1700s to early 1800s), involving small or large numbers of ships, in a tabletop situation. There are also a series of Scenarios to accompany this game: In the Spirit of Trafalgar, Chesapeake Bay, Battle of the Nile.

boardgame: Form Line of Battle: Naval Wargames Rules for the Age of Sail 1650 to 1820
description:
Description from the designer:

These rules enable you to take on the role of Hornblower RN, John Paul Jones or even Horatio Nelson. They are prepared in such a way that you can take command of a squadron of Ships of the Line, or on larger games with many players you may be given command of but one vessel. We would recommend that each player should have up to 3 vessels under his command. Experienced players could probably handle more once they have got an understanding of the game system. The basic rules are quick to learn, and there are a host of optional extras to give the feel for the period.

The basic rules are designed for games set around the turn of the 18th to 19th Century, however they are equally able to be used for the Dutch Wars of the 17th Century, and there are rules covering this.

The game sequence is managed using a card system, which randomizes when, and whose, command and action phases happen. There are advanced and more detailed rules for actions using frigates and smaller vessels.

9 Scenarios and 25 typical ship classes are provided to get you into the game, as well as an easy to follow system to create your own ships based on historical research.

• recommended scale 1/600 to 1/2400
• complexity: 6/10
• level of detail: 6/10
• avg game length 2 hours
• dice required: D6

Note on 6th edition:
This is essentially a reprint of the final edition published by A&A Game Engineering. If you have that edition you do not need this one
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

boardgame: Vast: The Crystal Caverns – Bonus Cards
description:
A set of bonus cards for Vast: The Crystal Caverns, originally included as free extras for backers of the Kickstarter for the second printing and miniatures.

This set includes the Silver Gauntlet and Treasure Trove promo cards from the first printing, plus a variety of other cards, including new Sidequests, Treasures, Events, Secrets, Schemes (a new type of Secrets card), and Upgrades for the Thief.

Full Card List:

Sidequests

     Noble Failure
     Prepared


Treasures

     Silver Gauntlet (Original Promo, with revised text to include the Fearsome Foes characters.)
     Treasure Trove (Original Promo)
     Rune Staff
     Cursed Amulet
     Heroic Boots (Revised)
     Javelin (Revised)


Events

     Quake
     Cave Bread (Revised)


Secrets

     Slingshot
     Meat!
     Cave Mushrooms


Monsters

     Ground Kraken


Schemes

     Armory
     War Banner
     Underboss
     Press Gangs
     Ancient Ritual
     Mercenaries
     Eye of Rage
     Cave Oil


Dragon Scales

     Golden Scale
     Silver Scale
     Platinum Scale


Thief Upgrades

     Charm
     Warlock’s Wand
     Disappearing Cloak


Terrain Cards

     Canyon
     Lake
     Magma
     Mushroom Forest
     Pits
     River
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

boardgame: Northern Moat: Wargames Rules to Recreate Naval Warfare on the Great Lakes in the War of 1812
description:
Wargames rules to simulate naval warfare on the Great Lakes in the War of 1812.

Fast play rules for Napoleonic period naval warfare - which Napoleon had nothing to do with!  While Britain and France fought a bitter war to decide the future of Europe, across the Atlantic another war was being fought, borne of American ambition and British disdain.  On the long wilderness border between the United States and British North America, hardy frontiersmen and colonial militia hacked fleets out of the virgin forest, fleets to contest the Great Lakes, fleets to decide the future of a continent.


    Includes the historical background to the war.
    A description of the main campaigns on all three main theatres.
    Detailed rulebook and ready ref sheet.
    Examples of play.
    Ship cards for all warships known to have been active in the conflict (Note these are also sold separately, but this full rules set includes all ship detail sheets).
    Bonus scenario: the battle of Put-in-bay.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
This game is designed for use in primary school, but is also suitable fort originally to play with numbers at home.

The idea behind the game is that when the child playing and having fun, then it is also easier to learn. Therefore, in these games emphasized that it should be both fun and educational at the same time.

Game contains 12 different small games like memory game, where combining "actual numbers" with "number of dots" on the tiles. Dice rolling with addition and/or subtraction. The game focuses on getting familiar with numbers and light addition and subtractions

Leg Lær Læs focuses on the 120 most frequently occurring words in the Danish language. Today it is widely recognized that children who master these words are far easier to learn to read and write.

The game consists of 16 small games and game with increasing difficulty that all strengthens children's language comprehension and increase their reading speed. Leg Lær Læs use different learning styles, so there are games and games for both children who would like to sit still, and those who want to move while they have fun learning.

The Munchkin Halloween Monster Box has room for two thousand Munchkin cards, various Kill-O-Meters, a Level Playing Field or two, Dungeons, Seals, and more. To get you started, it includes twelve holiday-themed Munchkin cards that you can shuffle into any game

Eat brains. Don't get shotgunned.

In Zombie Dice, you are a zombie. You want braaains – more brains than any of your zombie buddies. The 13 custom dice are your victims. Push your luck to eat their brains, but stop rolling before the shotgun blasts end your turn! Whoever collects 13 brains first wins. Each game takes 10 to 20 minutes and can be taught in a single round.

Each turn, you take three dice from the box and roll them. A brain symbol is worth one point at the end of the round, while footsteps allow you to reroll this particular dice. Shotgun blasts on the other hand are rather bad, cause if you collect three shotgun blasts during your turn, it is over for you and you get no points. After rolling three dice, you may decide if you want to score your current brain collection or if you want to push your luck by grabbing new dice so you have three again and roll once more.

Zombie Dice Horde Edition includes the Zombie Dice base game, as well as the Zombie Dice 2: Double Feature and Zombie Dice 3: School Bus expansions, along with a cloth bag and a scorepad to keep track of those tasty brains.

boardgame: Team Yankee: Charlie's Chieftains – British Armored Squadron
description:
Contains five plastic Chieftain Main Battle Tanks, two plastic two Lynx helicopters, six resin tank commander figures, eight rare-earth magnets, two decal sheets, six unit cards and one printed cardboard Artillery & Salvo template.

C Squadron, 17th/21st Lancers, and the Irish Guardsmen supporting them made their last-minute preparations, each man checking his weapons one more time and going through the little rituals that had become talismans against violent death.

After days of hard fighting, they were worn and frayed, no longer so eager to test themselves in battle. Their expressions were grim and hard. The coming battle was not going to be easy.

boardgame: Lookee: The Clue-Solving Scavenger Hunt Card Game
description:
Lookee is a new spin on the scavenger hunt. It allows kids to have fun and learn critical thinking skills at the same time.  The object of the Lookee game is for player(s) to solve clues, the answer to those clues provides player(s) with information about where to find their next clue. You decide how many clues and where the adventure takes the player.  Lookee can be played as a single player, team or multi-player game. Lay out separate “paths” of clues for multiple players, or have everyone work together to solve the same path and win as a group!

Directions

• Determine how many clues and locations your scavenger hunt adventure will have.
• Choose one card to be the “Starting” point.
• Layout the cards based on the answers to the clues; for example, if the answer to the clue is the refrigerator, the next clue should be placed in, on or near the refrigerator.
• Once you’ve placed all your clue cards, direct player(s) to the “Starting” Point. Give player(s) the first clue and send them on their way.

Have ideas for your own clues? Lookee also includes blank cards, so you can write your own. Have fun and get creative!

An educational game about utilizing addition, multiplication, and number color matching.  The game is played in rounds utilizing 5 cards in which each player must follow the color or number prior.  At the end of each round the players add or multiply the cards in front of them and the one with the greatest sum is the winner.  They are given a golden egg which are the victory points.  The one with 3 or the predetermined number of golden eggs is the overall winner.

This is definitely a children's teaching game.

It’s Christmas Eve and Santa needs to make sure all the presents are delivered on time, and to the right addresses. Sounds easy enough, but in this game - you are Santa. You start in the workshop, preparing all the presents, and as soon as you have everything ready, you can start making your way across the rooftops, dropping the correct presents through the letterboxes. There are a few stumbling blocks to look out for, and although there is some strategy involved, the game is mostly relying on luck. With easy to grasp rules, even the youngest players can skilfully play with the older ones. The first player to deliver all their presents is the winner. A wonderful and fun way for the whole family to get into the Festive Spirit, and is sure to be a family favourite year after year
The first expansion to Joking Hazard. Mix these 100 cards in with your Joking Hazard game for extra deck enhancement.

Pacific Ocean naval campaign Spring 1942 through Summer 1945 in 14 seasonal turns.  Capital ships paired.  Simple surface, submarine, and air combat mechanics any kid can quickly master.  Magic intelligence in the early seasons - kamikazes in the later ones.  VIOLENT little game is also somewhat unpredictable making it solitaire fare.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

description:
Jaque Quince: Simple rules and short strategic duels inspired by Bauhaus

The objective of the game is to play in such a way that your opponent cannot respond to the opening play.
 Jaque Quince has 3 different suits. Each suit has a reference value with respect to the other suits (i.e., every suit is bigger than another suit). Each suit has 5 cards, and the bigger number cards have greater value. Aces are higher than 3, 4, and 5, but they are lower than 2. As there are 3 suits each with 5 cards, there are a total of 15 cards.

Each player start's each hand of the match with 7 cards. The remaining card remains hidden to both players. One player opens a round with a card which must be responded to by a card with the same suit. If suit respond cannot be made, then must be responded by the same number. The player which played the greater value card opens next round. If responding player does not have either same suit or number then the opening player is granted points according to how long the hand was (i.e. the number of the round played).

Jaque Quince was part of the 3rd Annual 18 Card Microgame Contest in 2016 and is planned to be published in some microgames compilations during 2017.

From the box:

Cuál? Is an intriguing boardgame of observation and deceiving.
Will you be able to reach victory with your secret piece before your opponent finds it? Which one would be the secret piece of your opponent?

Overview:
Cuál? (translated as “Which one?”) is an abstract strategy game for 2 players in which each one controls 8 pieces in 4 different shapes each shape having unique movement patterns. At the beginning of the match each player choses 1 of their 8 pieces as the secret piece using a deck of 8 cards. Winning may be achieved by reaching the adversary end of the board or by discovering which one is the opponent secret piece.  
During the game pieces may be captured but they don’t leave the game, instead they are positioned by whom captured it. By revealing one of the remaining 7 cards, any player may move 2 pieces or 1 piece 2 times in their turn.

boardgame: Blood Bowl (2016 Edition): The Early Bird Special Play Promo Card
description: Pre-order copies of Blood Bowl (2016 edition) ordered directly from Games Workshop received a copy of a web exclusive promo card while supplies lasted.  'The Early Bird' is a Special Play card which grants a player Pro skill for the first half of the match and a chance to be KO'd in the 2nd half.

boardgame: Blood Bowl (2016 Edition): Go Full Beard Special Play Promo Card
description:
Event Promo - Warhammer World Exclusive

The Go Full Beard promo is a special play card for Blood Bowl given out at the Bugman’s Full Beard Cup tournament held at Warhammer World in Nottingham, England on November 26th and 27th, 2016. The card's effect allows a player to beef up their players with bonus skills for one drive before passing out.

Try-Angle is a fast-paced strategic game for two or three players. In the game, you try to create a large triangle consisting of four smaller triangles in your own color. Because the game makes use of height as an extra dimension in addition to the three natural ones provided by the triangular shape of the tiles themselves, this proves to be a harder challenge than the mere description would suggest.

boardgame: Blood Bowl (2016 Edition): Special Play Cards – Team Titans Pack
description:
Description from the publisher:

A ton of weird, wonderful and bizarre events can and will happen on and off the pitch during games of Blood Bowl. The best way to represent these happenings is with Special Play cards, and this set of 26 Team Titans cards, usable by any and all teams adds a whole load of extra anarchy.

They’re split into two decks – the Benefits of Training and the Dirty Tricks decks – each featuring a wide variety of extra rules that make your games of Blood Bowl that little bit more unpredictable. You don’t have to use them, but you’re missing out if not!

Hostile Takeover is a strategic game for two players. You are a shareholder of a company and you can move executives, managers and workers. The aim of the game is to make the workers of your opponent yours.

The game is played on a 7x7 tile grid. Each player begins with eleven workers (large sized disks), seven managers (medium sized disks) and four executives (small sized disks). Pieces can move independently but managers must always be place on top of a worker and executives must always be placed on top of a manager. The color of the disk on top determines which player owns the piece; the size of the lowest disk moved determines the possible move. Workers move one space orthogonally, managers move one space diagonally, and executives can move one space in any direction.

The first player to control six of the opponent's workers, while still controlling six of their own, controls more than half of the workers on the board and has pulled off a "hostile takeover".

The game comes with some extra or specially marked components allowing for more different games to be played with the same set.

Illegal winemaking is, in whatever age, the best way to make a fortune in the black market. It is also a crime that the police force is eager to solve.

Now within a closed area, there's secret winemaking activity going on. Meanwhile, the police has found out about the activity and is waiting for the winemakers' arrest. Can the winemakers get away with it? Can they make a big fortune out of it eventually?
 In Moonshine Shutdown, players play the winemakers who try to brew as much wine as possible before the police lock down the area. Whoever makes the most wine earns the most points and wins the game.

The players travel around the board revealing hidden animals and then matching them up with the birthday cards on the side. The player with the most collected cards at the end of the game is the winner.

Bamsemor has made birthday cards for Bamsens birthday. All the animals of Hakkebakkeskongen are invited, and you must help Bamsemor to find them.

You'll need to check behind every blueberry bush, and remember where each animal is hidden when you find them.

It's not easy as they keep disapearing back into the forest. Once you find an animal and have the birthday card that matches you can place the card out infront of you.

The player with the most birthday cards at the end of the game wins, and gets to go to the party.


Bamsemor har laget fødselsdagsskort til Bamsens fødselsdag. Alle dyrene i Hakkebakkeskogen er invitert, og du må hjelpre Bamsemor med å finne dem.

Du må lete bak hver en blårbærbusk, og huske hvor dyrene skjuler seg når du finner dem.

Det er ikke alltid så lett, forstadig vekk forsvinner de i skogen. Finner du et dyr og har fødselsdagsskortet som hører til, får du straks legge kortet foran deg.

Spilleren som har flest fødslsdagskort til slut, vinner spillet og får være med på festen.

P.Y.G. or Pretty Young Girl is a card hand management game of romantic affection theme. In this game, he who has the closest numbered card to the open goal card (pretty young girl's heart) number at the end of each round wins that round and takes 2~3 points according to the position. After several rounds the game ends when there is less than 2 untaken points and the player with most point wins the game.
designers: Kengo Tatsugawa (たつがわ けんご)
publishers: オトコマエゲームズ (OTOKOMAE GAMES)

description:
Theme: 
A light card game for 2-6 players about being moonshine bootleggers in the hills of Louisiana, at the height of Prohibition! You'll be establishing your stills, paddocks and businesses, making, moving and selling moonshine, hiring Bootleggers to defend your turf, raiding other player's properties for their goods and trying to keep one step ahead of the Feds. You'll make fast friends to take down stronger players, but beware... those allies you have just made will always have schemes and machinations of their own.

Game-Play:
Turns go quickly around the table, with each player able to take one action. 
Players can choose to build paddocks, stills and businesses (placing these cards in their "Property" playspace), roll a dice to make or sell moonshine (Results of 1 mean a still blows up, or the Feds appear!), steal any building from another player's Property, hire bootleggers to defend their own Property, or even Tip Off the Feds to disrupt a rival's moonshine operation.

Creating an efficient production line for your moonshine operation, defending your property, temporarily allying with other players to cut another rival down to size, and choosing the right moment to sell or strike are keys to success in Still in Business.

Goal:
The ultimate goal is to sell 5 moonshine. Whether you make this yourself, or steal from other players before sale, is entirely up to you and your real-life sense of morality (or lack thereof!)

Also known as "The Little Goat Game".

This is considered the children's version of Um El Bagara, and it originated in Africa, in the area now known as the Sudan.

The board is a 2x3 mancala board, and 3 stones are placed in each of the six playing wells. Players take turns moving stones by picking up all the stones in a well then dropping a stone in each well until they run out, moving either clockwise or counter-clockwise depending on the position of the well. A well with only a single stone cannot be moved.

After your move, starting with the well you last placed a stone into, any wells that have exactly two stones may be placed in your scoring well. If there is a well that you cannot score (it has a number of stone other than two and cannot be your well), then any that come after it in sequence may not be scored either.

The game ends when neither player can make a legal move. Any stones left on a players side of the board are added to those in his scoring well. Whoever has the most stones is the winner.

This is space card game.
Player is God.
Gods fight with strength of star as card.
In this game, God can rob and break down other God's star for advantage.
God with stronger star will win at the end of game.

It’s been 14 long years since the virus took over. You and your only friend are stranded on an island, overrun by zombies. Avoid zombie attacks, backstab other survivors and fight to be the last remaining survivors!

Each player receives seven cards from the Prime deck and two survivors with ten health each. Each round, a player can discard a Set (1, 2, 3, 4, 5 or 6 of a Kind), a Run (3-7 consecutive cards), or Scavenge cards and finish their turn with 7 cards in hand. The next player in turn must respond to the play by matching or raising the Set or Run to avoid a zombie attack (indicated by the amount of zombies visible on the table). If Scavenge cards have been discarded, the players must respond accordingly. When a survivor is killed, they turn into zombies, increasing the amount of zombies in an attack. The game is over when there is only one player with survivors.
