Quickpick: Mutant Dinosaur Park, the second title in the Quickpick games line, challenges players to pay close attention to everyone around the table, with the gameplay combining humor, dexterity, and simultaneous action. On a turn, the imitator draws a dinosaur card and tries to recreate the sound he imagines the dinosaur would create, while the other players try to guess which dinosaur they are acting out. If a player guesses correctly, both guesser and imitator collect a card. Whoever collects the most cards by the end of the game wins.

Relativity is a light abstract game with unique movement mechanics. The only random element occurs during setup.

The players take the roles of time and space and move numbered coins to try to complete tetrominoes (tetris piece shapes) while blocking the opponent from doing the same. After one round the coins are reshuffled and the players swap roles and play again. The winner is the player who completes the most tetrominos.

Triangle Maker is an abstract strategy game in which two to four players compete by forming triangles.  Players use the thirteen pieces to make triangles to score points.  This educational board game is a fun way to hone players’ creativity, spatial awareness, critical thinking, and concentration.

The two movable pieces are the game changers of Triangle Maker.  Clever use of these pieces can tip the balance to your side and name you the ultimate victor!

The goal of the game is to make the most and/or biggest triangles with the pieces available to earn the most points.  Every newly formed triangle adds to your score.  However, there is one rule:  You cannot place pieces where the same colored lines overlap.

There are four different ways to play Triangle Maker:

1. Card Game

a. Shuffle the cards
b. When it is your turn, draw the first card and place the corresponding piece onto the board. (Earn tokens based on the number or size of the triangle[s] made)
c. You can use either of the movable pieces during your turn instead of drawing your next card.
d. Whoever gets the most points wins!

2. Strategy Card Game

a. Shuffle the cards and draw two cards.
b. When it is your turn, use one of the pieces on the drawn cards and place it onto the board. (Earn tokens based on the number or size of the triangle[s] made)
c. During the next turns, remember to draw a card to replace the one used and then repeat the above step.
d. Whoever gets the most points wins!

3. No-Card Game

a. When it is your turn, place any piece onto the board.  (Earn tokens based on the number or size of the triangle[s] made)
b. Whoever, gets the most points wins!

4. Obstruction Game

a. While keeping only the movable pieces, transfer all your pieces to another player.
b. When it is your turn, the other player will choose the piece he/she wants you to place onto the board.  (Earn tokens based on the number or size of the triangle[s] made)
c. When it is the other player’s turn, select the piece that would be most disadvantageous for him/her.
d. You can use either of the movable pieces during your turn instead of accepting the other player’s pieces.
e. Whoever gets the most points wins!

Scoring

1. Small triangle is worth 1 point each.
2. Large triangle is worth 4 points each.
3. Giant triangle is worth 9 points each.

    It is possible to earn more points by forming multiple triangles in one turn.

The players start at "START" and move through spaces 1 to 80 towards the parking lot "P". Movement is determined by a die. On the red spaces events or traffic signs hinder or benefit the players. On each space the player has to explain the pictured traffic sign or traffic situation. The winner is the player, who had to pay the least penalities while traveling to the parking lot.

Butchers vs Orcs! is a two player roll and move game.  The theme of the game is based on in-character rivalry between the Butchers Guild and the Imperial Orcs within the Live-Role Playing world of Empire by Profound Decisions.

Players roll dice to move their pieces (representing the Butchers and the Orcs respectively) in opposite directions along a track on the board, trying to get their own pieces from their opponent's base to the safety of their own base. Pieces are moved and taken if landed upon in a similar way to backgammon, however two key enhancements give it more depth than classic backgammon. Firstly, the middle section of the track is a dangerous no-man's-land where pieces cannot protect each other, forcing players to judge when best to risk making a run for it.  Secondly, each player has a leader which is at greater risk than other pieces, encouraging players to play tactically to protect their leaders and get them to safety.

Six games to learn maths.

Game 1 (find the right amount): Find the card with the right amount of lines on it given a number.

Game 2 (amount matching game): Race to put the same number of tokens next to your card as there are lines on the card (or as the number shown if you use the number side).

Game 3 (sorting task): Sort the numbers from 1 to 10 (or to 20).

Game 4 (dominoes with amounts and numbers): play dominoes with cards that have a number on one end and an amount of lines (or pictures) on the other end.

Game 5 (calculation problems): Cover a board of calculation problems with the solution number cards.

Game 6 (calculation bingo): As above but the solution cards are called out as with bingo, the player who first finds a matching problem on their board gets the card.

The gameboard shows a grid of Dutch fields. On many intersections, there are one, two or three houses. Two playing pieces and a blockade piece are randomly placed on three intersections, and a sand timer or stopwatch counts 60 seconds. In that time, players have to find a route from one playing piece to the other that goes through as many houses as possible, while making no more than four turns. They have to write down that number of houses before the time runs out. 
The player with the highest number shows their path first - if successful, they get that number and everyone else gets whatever they wrote without being checked. If the highest player fails, they get two points less than the lowest player and the second highest shows their path, etc. So bluffing is encouraged, as long as you don't bid too high. 
First player to reach 55 points is the winner.

Travel back to the 1920's to the year of Prohibition. The Golden Age of Moonshine. Secure your still site. Gather your supplies and produce 3 runs of  'Shine and you win. But beware, other competitors will be raiding your supplies or sabotaging your still site, government Revenuers will be stalking the woods looking to destroy your operation. Bribe them, dodge them, just stay ahead of them, because moonshine season is short and the longer you wait the weather is going to get worse. No one ever said being a Moonshiner was easy, but becoming a legend never was!

Collect a set of one of each Supply Cards (Corn, Water, Activators), to exchange for a Makin' Shine Run Card. Along the way, avoid Revenuer Cards and resolve any Event Cards that appear. Be the first player to collect 3 Makin' Shine Run Cards (3-4 players) or 5 Makin' Shine Run Cards (2 players) and you win.

Free Print and Play version here: http://shenanigangames.weebly.com/print-n-play.html

Description from the publisher:

Zombie Babies is a humorous character building card and dice game. The aim of the game is to get the most XP points, by completing quests which involves killing zombies, collecting items and building your characters stats.

Build your Hero - Improve on your skills and gain items to help you complete quests and defeat zombies.
Complete Quests - Choose to fight a zombie or choose a quest to find items and build on your 3 stats, to make future questing easier
Take a Nap - Too scared to venture forth? Regain your health or energy before embarking on your next quest.
Player Interaction - Use special cards to help yourself or make life harder for your opponents.
Each quest requests you to complete a set to skill tests by rolling dice to test your characters stats individually.

You roll the number of dice equal to your characters stat that matches the test requirement on the Quest card. Get that number of symbols on your dice and you get the rewards listed at the bottom of the card, these can be an Item, an increase to your characters stats or XP.

If you fail you might lose some stats you gained earlier in the game or health or energy.

Quest cards include a small funny story which players can choose to read.

However beware the other players might have a trick or two up their sleeve to leave you behind and fall prey to the many zombies wondering the nursery. Special cards spice up the game play and allow you to affect others turns making them weaker or help yourself overcome obstacles in your way.

At the end of each game a random Boss card will appear, all other quests will disappear and players will have to fight the Boss to the death to survive!

After completing quests and defeating zombies you keep the card and place it under your hero building your stats and XP. On the left hand side of the Hero card there are XP rewards to work towards. Be warned that if you fail to complete a quest or defeat a zombie you may need to loose a card decreasing one of your stats and any other benefits it gives!

"He" fell to Earth in the year 1972, but now it's time to go back to the ★. You, as a fifth dimension being, must connect the stars and guide "him" to ★.

Across the Universe — 星を渡る (Hoshi wo Wataru) — is focused and themed on many musicians who died in the year 2016, especially David Bowie and Prince. The game includes references to many songs and albums, including ★ (Blackstar, Bowie's last album before his death), the Byrds' album "Fifth Dimension", and the Beatles' song "Across the Universe".

The game lasts five rounds (years), and at the start of each year, star cards are dealt into the spring/summer/fall/winter slots. The number of star cards dealt to each season varies. The players bid for these star cards to catch them. Each player must decide how much to bid for each season and must place four cards from their hand, one card for each season, at the beginning of the year. Once the resolution starts, the cards used to bid may not be changed. When a player wins a star card, they discard the card they used to bid; for those who don't win, they can re-use their losing bids in different ways. A player can:


     Take it back to their hand to use again in a later year.
     Shift the bid card to the next season, adding this bid's value to what's already there.
     Move the bid card to the player's second tableau. The bid card is multi-purpose and also acts as an upper limit to your star card score.


Publisher's summary:

１９７２年、地球に落ちてきた「彼」が、とうとう★に還る日がやってきました。5次元人であるあなたは、５年間で星々に「ワタリ」と呼ばれる道筋をつけ、彼を★まで還しましょう！
このゲームは、２０１６年に亡くなったミュージシャンたち、特にデヴィッド・ボウイやプリンスに焦点を当て、テーマを付けました。
手札から4枚のカードをプロットして、星カードを競り落とします。ただし、競り負けると次の解決が有利になったり、優先順位が上がったり、最後に得点化できる星カードが増えます。上手に勝って上手に負ける。しかし、プログラミングのため、そこまでうまくいくかな？
publishers: シュピールディスオーダー (Spieldisorder)

boardgame: HOT 16: A Game of Dope Rhymes
description:
To play HOT 16 A Game of Dope Rhymes, you and your crew each provide a sample line that everyone needs to rhyme with for 1 bar (or line). Everyone writes 4 lines that rhyme - without seeing what anyone else is writing. Then you perform. How serious you are about the performance aspect is up to you. Sometimes the rhymes are silly and the songs are weird - sometimes it all comes together, and sounds like you're ready for the studio.

You perform your cleverly crafted masterpiece in rounds, with each rapper leading for a verse, and the rest following. The rapper who ends the current verse starts the next verse, and so on, until the song is done! Then you get hype!

HOT 16 A Game of Dope Rhymes is an old school rap crew party game. It's interactive, cooperative, and requires no musical talent whatsoever (though if you have some musical talent, it sure doesn't hurt).

Description from the publisher:

Short Order Hero is a devious, cut throat, challenge for friends wanting to flex their master culinary chops. The story behind the game is that you are one of five short order cooks working in a greasy diner. The owner of the diner tells the crew that at the end of the night he can can only keep one cook. You are working your rump roast off serving up the best you can, but your competition is doing their best to sabotage you. Can you win out and secure the job?

Find out if YOU can become the Short Order Hero!

Game Play:
Win the game by competing against other players to be the best short order cook. The Short Order Hero wins the game by scoring the most Recipe points at the end of the game.

Five recipes have been ordered, the tickets are available for each player/cook to make. Each cook is dealt 7 Ingredient cards. Cooks in turn, lay down all of the required ingredients, thus making the recipe. In addition a cook can add spices and seasonings to their food to make it better, OR add hairs and cockroaches to their opponent's made recipes to make their food worse.

Cooks can play as many cards from their hand as they wish, but their turn ends once they draw a card from the Ingredients deck.

Each recipe is worth different point values, the added ingredients can either increase or decrease the total point value.

The cook with the highest total recipe value from all their made food is the Short Order Hero!

User summary
Glasgow is an abstract game using glass tiles, for 3 players only. Some rules are adopted from Go, like placement restrictions and the Ko rule (hence the name Glass + Go = Glasgow). The designer feels the new rules make the game acceptable to advanced players.

The glass tiles are placed on a 6 x 6 grid. All tiles should be adjacent to other tiles orthogonally.
Up to 2 tiles of same color may be adjacent to each other; no blocks of 3 tiles may be made.

Whenever a tile is surrounded, the owner of the most surrounding tiles captures it. This scores the player 3 points. At the end of the game, each tile left on the 6 x 6 outer rim counts as 1 negative point.

The game ends when nobody can place any more tiles. Each player adds up their captured tiles, subtracts the tiles left on the outer rim, to get their final score. Most points wins.

The graphic design for the game was done by Decoct Design.

Publisher's summary
Glasgow（グラスゴー）はガラスタイルを使った3人専用アブストラクトです。

囲碁のルールにある着手禁止点やコウなどのルールを採用しました。

そこにマジョリティとバーストを混ぜた玄人向けの作品になったと思います。

タイルは６×６のエリアに置きます。
必ず他のタイルに接するように置かなければなりません。
同じ色のタイルは最大で２個までしか隣り合うことができません。

タイルの周囲４マス（２個連続したものなら６マス）が埋まるとマジョリティトップが内側のタイルを獲得します。
これは１枚１点となります。

また６×６の外周のマスは最終的に１枚マイナス１点になります。

全てのプレイヤーがタイルを置けなくなったらゲーム終了です。
獲得タイルから外周に置いたタイルの枚数を引いたものが得点となります。

Hex Shogi 41 is a Shogi variant adapted for play on hexagons in a rectangle, arranged in 9 rows each containing either 4 or 5 hexes.

Rules are similiar to Shogi, except that setup and movement is defined for the alternative board geometry, including additional restrictions on pawn drops.

Hex Shogi 81 is a Shogi variant adapted for play on hexagons in a 9x9 rhombus.

Rules are similiar to Shogi, except that setup and movement is defined for the alternative board geometry, including additional restrictions on pawn drops.

Hex Shogi 91 is a Shogi variant adapted for play on a hexagon of 91 hexagons, 6 per side.

Rules are similiar to Shogi, except that setup and movement is defined for the alternative board geometry, including additional restrictions on pawn drops.

Finally the 8th continent has been discovered! After years of hostility behind thick curtains of mist, hidden by Kushan pirates in attempt to keep its infinite resources guarded, the continent of infinity has been unveiled. With the guardians been made void and the curtains of secrecy now destroyed… more and more brave rulers set sail to this new and unexplored continent of Xadlagon.

Xadlagon, this vast continent is in need of leadership and who better to provide it than YOU? Be it as a righteous king or as a ruthless warlord, conquer trade and scheme your way to the top, because here on Xadlagon, YOU rule...Because you CAN!!! 
We present to you the a unique experience in tabletop board games, the all South African developed TradeWarz
T – Trade with allies
R – Raid your adversaries
A – Advance your economy
D – Deploy your armies and
E – Exploit the natural resources

The game TradeWarz was developed as one big fun challenge to create strategy board game where “Lady Luck” takes a back row seat to strategic planning and player cunning. This is the "first of its kind" to be developed in South Africa and can compete with the best the rest of the world has to offer with respects to board games in this genre....or any other genre for that matter.

TradeWarz can also serve as an educational tool to educate younger players how to take calculated risks in the game and also further on in life. Players young and old will have to weigh up the trade-offs between various risks and rewards while negotiating a better deal for themselves or their alliance in the game.

This game caters for every personality type as there are many ways to win be it by military might and aggression, expansive economic hoarding or a cautious defensive approach or a combination of these strategies…..you are the King and you decide!!!

description:
Mini Hexchess is an adaptation of the larger 91-cell "Hexagonal Chess" game by the same designer. (The designer notes that, although developed independently, the rules to his larger game are very similar to Glinski's Hexagonal Chess.) The mini game was created in response to a challenge posted on ChessVariants.com.

The game plays like orthodox chess, except the usual moves are interpreted as follows: Orthogonal moves cross hex edges. Diagonal moves follow hex corners, "jumping" over the connecting edges -- moving through spaces of the same color.

Unlike orthodox chess, this game has no castling, no pawn double-move, nor a corresponding en passant capture. This mini game has no queen, so a pawn is not allowed to promote to one.

Each player begins with 5 pawns, a king, a knight, a bishop, and a rook.

Description from the designer:

Monkey Wrench 2nd Edition:

Your company is firing employees; Can you get rid of the bad employee in time?

Monkey Wrench

Is a social deduction and manipulation game requiring a minimum of 7 people to play, (6 "Employees" & 1 Narrator). The concept of the game is rather simple. The company you work for, has suddenly been having problems with all of its production machines being sabotaged by an unknown person After hours. The Security footage shows that One of your fellow employees, (players) has been seen tampering with the machines, "After Hours". The problem is, the security system couldn't detect who it was. Worse, every employee has an alibi, and no one is willing to step forward with any information of evidence.

There for, the Board of Directors, (The Narrator(s)), have decided that one employee will be fired during each "Day Shift" via a 60% majority Employee vote, until the machines are no longer being tampered with each night. To help facilitate this, members of the local law enforcement (Undercover Cop) and the FBI (in larger games of over 15 players), as well as other law enforcement agencies (NSA 20 player game, CIA 25 player game ) to help catch the potential criminals involved. They are also working diligently around the clock to help your company find the culprit. Meeting in secret "After hours" to arrest potential suspects, while also masquerading as a regular employee. Their goal, is to help weed out the real problem person; The elusive The Monkey Wrench and their crew of saboteurs.

However, the members of the Monkey Wrench team have an edge: 
Once per "After hours" phase, they can secretly convert one of the other employee towards their goal. If they manage to convert a casual non titled "Employee", that person becomes a leader within their team, and will be given a "Monkey Wrench" card to show their loyalty. Additionally, depending on the size of the "company (game), other criminal elements can also be expected to try to infiltrate the entire organization. Everything from a simple, "Lunch Thief", to a "Cat Burglar", to even members of a Cult, as well as various types of "Hackers", Espionage experts and even a Corporate embezzler. Each with their own "After hours" abilities that can be used by the Monkey Wrench team, IF they can figure out who within the company is what. Which keeps the members of Law enforcement busy during the game, and helps ensure that the Monkey Wrench team will over throw the company.

Fortunately; the Law enforcement team, (Security guard, Undercover cop, FBI, CIA, NSA.. etc..), do have a extra tools in their wheel house to counter this influx of added trouble. For starters at the end of every "After hours" phase, the company "Security Guard" has the ability to The "sense" when another Employee can been involved with an illicit activity that evening. While the Undercover cop, once per After hours round can "Arrest" a single potential suspect. That player is then removed from game the next subsequent "Day shift". While the FBI agent, has the power to literally "Fire" anyone one person in the company. Either as a secret executive action during the "After hours" round, or by revealing themselves to the other players as a member of law Enforcement. Upon which they can then have executive power to remove the undercover CEO, or members of upper management and even overthrow their power position within the game. While the NSA has the power to make various "hacking' actions, while the CIA player, has the ability to be a spy in any group, and never reveal themselves, unless they want to. Each member of Law Enforcement, are thus granted unique abilities to help them thwart the dastardly goals of the criminal elements in game. However, in turn, each has their own requirements that they must fulfill in order to win the game.

However; the rest of the employees, and neutral parties can also use their individual abilities, as well as their natural powers of social deduction, interaction, and situational perceptions to determine who they should "vote" against during the "Day shift" round. That way they can work together to have 1 of their fellow employees (Players), removed from game as a group in hopes to help the Law Enforcement players remove threats from the game, and win by power of democratic unity.

So its up to you fellow employee…

Will you side with the Monkey Wrench and take down the company? 
Will you help the Corporate Security to weed out the problem person? 
Or... are you secretly the Monkey Wrench, all along? Manipulating everyone to help you shut down the company.

General game play:
Each player is given 4 cards at the beginning of the game:

1 - "Day shift" card, which denotes their public role within the company, that every other player is completely aware of. This card reflects that players normal operations within the company during the day, while they are working. It further operates as  each players "titled" position within a standard normal office environment. Each titled position, is sometimes granted certain abilities, that are usually made public to all players in play, during the "Day shift" phase.

1 - "After hours" card. which designates the given players roles, duties and Private activities when they are not at work, during the "After hours" phase of the game. During this phase of game play, some players are lumber into various secret shadow groups, that operate in secret. Some of them are trying to resolve the crime within the company. Others are trying to ruin the company, by either overthrowing it (Such as the Monkey Wrench), or corrupting the internal operations of the company. While others yet still, have absolutely little value, or influence upon the companies operations, and function as nothing more than benign red herrings, to offset the various groups investigations.

1 - "Vote/ Fire" card. This Operates as each individual players ability to either support another players choice of someone that they believe should be removed from play, (The "Vote" side of the card), by holding the card up above their head, showing that they cast a vote in support of the accuser against the one person they believe should be removed from game. Or on their "Current player" turn (When they have the "Current player" puck in hand), they may select one player in the room to be "Fired" from game, by pointing the card at the person they suspect is guilty, or should be removed from game. If they manage to gain a 65% majority supporting vote from the other remaining players in the game, the selected person is removed from game, revealing their "After hours" card(s). They then regardless of how the vote ended, had the card off to the next player to their Left.

1 - "Gestures" card. This card is a reference card of common hand gestures used during the "After hours" phase of the game. This helps players know what other players in the group they are part of are saying to them, and allows them to communicate in a sort of crude sign language with each other and the Narrators.



Average game time: 
15 minutes for a 7 to 10 person game
30 minutes for a 10 to 15 person game
45 minutes for a 15 to 20 person game
60 minutes for a 20 to 30 person gameIntended age of play:
This game is intended for persons 16 years or older. However, the production website says "18", only because the options, are 12 or 18. The content that is questionable, within the game, is sexually suggestive adult humor, found within 4 "After hours" cards. Which are not essential for general game play, and may be removed as needed for smaller games, of less than 25 persons playing, if persons playing the game are not age appropriate for the setting.

Number of players:
A basic game consists of at least 7 to 10 players:
Advanced games can handle 11 to 33 players.

Please note, however that for every 10 "Employee" players in the game, 1 "Board member" (Narrator), will be needed, as this will help the Narrator's to be able to manage remembering who is what position, has been fired, or changed roles within the game. A "Employee Roster" is provided within the game to help facilitate this, however larger games can get very confusing quickly, and to accommodate this, it's strongly advised to use 1 additional Narrator per 10 players.

Contents:
1 - 60 second timer 
1 - 120 second timer 
1 - pencil 
1 - "Employee Roster" score pad (Player role tracker) 
1 - "Current player" card 
30 - "Hand gesture" reference cards 
30 - "Voting/ "Fire" cards 
8 - Special effect cards (Used to reflect game modifiers, such as "Lunch Thief", Hacks, or other rule modifiers) 
37 - "Day Shift" - Character employment cards (These roles, Everyone in play will know about)
93 - "After hours" split between different "Hobbies", "Scandals", "Skill's", & "Moonlighting" jobs. 
1 - 40 page "Employee's Manuel" (Rule book) 
1 - 32 page "Occupational Directory" (Card Description book)
1 - Deluxe sized box. To hold all your expansions.

Get your copy of Monkey Wrench 2nd Edition today!

GeoMundi is an educational game that attempts to teach players about the countries of the world.

"Ten games that bring far away places home. Actual data of 229 countries and territories - wrapped in 10 games of action and strategy."

The game comes with 229 cards containing data about various countries. The rulebook contains rules for 10 different games which can be played with the included cards.

1) GeoMundi (2+ players)
This is essentially War. One player chooses a category on the cards (like Literacy). All players compare the value on their card. The player with the best value collects all the cards.

2) GeoMundi Double (2 players)
This is played like GeoMundi, but both players choose a category. One player must win both categories to win the cards. If neither player wins both categories, each draws a new card and chooses a new category. This repeats until a player wins both selected categories and that player then wins all the cards used up to that point.

3) GeoMundi Open (2+ players)
Played like GeoMundi but the lead player selects any of their cards and then chooses any category. Each player may then select any of their cards to use in the battle. Best value in the category wins the cards.

4) Handicap (4+ players on 2+ teams)
Teams take turns. One player on one team picks a card and then must give clues so that their team can guess the name of the country. The clue provider may not use any of the words on the card or give clues related to the country name, although rhymes are allowed.

5) Touch! (2+ players)
This game is similar to Slap Jack. Each player turns over a card on their turn. If two consecutively played cards are for countries which neighbor each other the first player to yell "Touch" wins all the cards in the pile. Islands are "wild" and are considered to touch all other countries.

6) Clue-in (2+ players)
Players decide on an order for the card categories to be used. Players take turns as the clue giver. That player reads the data on the card in the order decided upon. The first player to correctly guess the country name wins the card. Each player is only allowed one guess during each round and if they guess incorrectly the clue giver gets a random card from their stack.

7) Flag Wizz (2+ players)
This is played like Clue-in but only the flag is given as a clue.

8) The Capitals Game (2+ players)
This is played like Clue-in but the players must guess the country's capital city. Or the players can only use the capital city as the clue and the players must guess the country name.

9) Take Your Pick (2+ players)
This is played like Clue-in but the clue giver draws two cards, states the name of each country, and chooses a category. The players must guess which country has the better value or if they match.

10) Map-it
This is played like Clue-in. The clue giver states the name of the country and the first player to find the country on the included map and state the country number wins the card.

Publisher information:

"The Battle of South Caobang is a tactical/operational wargame of the Battle of Cao Bang during the Sino-Vietnamese War, 1979. This is the first game of Kuro Neko Design Workshop, Shanghai, China.

"Sino-Vietnamese War (also known as the Third Indochina War), usually called as self-defense counterattack against Vietnam in China, and is a small border conflict under the background of Sino-Soviet split.With the support of USSR, Vietnam invaded Cambodia,occupied Laos, and want to build Indochina Commonwealth. At the same time Vietnam increase the provocation on the Sino-Vietnamese border. China cannot tolerance this anymore and start this counterattack and start the 10 years border war.
Based on the historical background of Sino-Vietnamese War, on the basis of reference to a large number of detailed historical data, Bloody south border: Sino-Vietnamese War series wargame was born.
On Feb 17th 1979, under the call of “Fight back to defend the frontier”, PLA start its lightning strikes to Vietnam, and captured Cao Bang, Lao Cai, Lang Son and 20 other important cities and towns on north of Vietnam. After forced crossing KyCung river on south part of Lang Son, the way to capital of Vietnam is clear, mechanized troops can march to Hanoi within one day. Vietnam
immediately transferred the main force of the invasion of Kampuchea back to defend Hanoi, and this ease the pressure for Kampuchea. When PLA reached its strategic object, PLA start to retreat back to China. Main force of Vietnam didn’t dare to pursuit and only follow PLA troops from far distance. On March 16th, all PLA troops retreated back to China.
Followed by 10 years border conflict, PLA send troops from different military region and the fight spread among Luojiaping Mountain、Faka Hill、Koulin Hill、Lao Hill、Zheyin Hill. During this 10 years border conflict China took back large amount of territory back to China, collapsed Vietnam economy and completely break the dream of Vietnam to be the world's Third Military power.
We will complete the panoramic display of this battle with Bloody south border: Sino-Vietnamese War series. Totally 5 games will be in this series:

Battle of South Cao Bang 
Pursuit on Lang Son
Capture Lao Cai
Battle of North Cao Bang
Target Hanoi

"First game of this series will be Battle of South Cao Bang. The game system is Company Tactics System"
