boardgame: Simon's Cat Card Game: Promotional "0" Cards
description: Zero value cards for the characters in the game.

Civitas is a fast-paced hand management card game about different government forms. Become the President, General, or even King while you try to be the first to empty your hand of cards. Customize each game by mixing and matching the eight different government types (Monarchy, Military, Democracy, Communism, Theocracy, Plutocracy, Republic, and Anarchy).

Before starting the game, players choose which forms of government they want to use. Each governmental form features its own deck of color-coded number cards, event cards, law cards, wild cards, and government change cards. Combine the decks together and then deal each player 7 cards. The top card of the deck determines the starting government, and then on your turn, you play one card out of your hand that matches the color of the government in play. If it's a number card, play continues. If it's an event card, resolve the action on the card. Law cards have special actions that must be resolved according to the government in play (voting in a democracy or the first player deciding in a monarchy, for example). And government change cards force a shift to a new form of government. If you don't have a card to play, draw a new card and play passes to the next player. The game continues until one player has gotten rid of all his or her cards. You then score according to the number of cards left in your hand modified by the governments you led during the game.

Be sure to chose wisely when changing the governments because each one has special effects that can make it easier for you, or harder for your opponents, to win. Use the power of Communism to slow down the leader as you redistribute cards, or declare a Plutocracy and watch your lead get bigger with every turn. Move cards around in the deck in a Theocracy, and decide events together in a democracy. Build up other players' decks in a Military government, make other players lose turns in a Monarchy, and build coalitions in a Republic. But watch out for descent into Anarchy, where government leaders lose their power, and anything goes as wild cards take over game play.

Welcome to the Colortopia, a realm of happiness filled with colors where people like to put all kinds of colors on themselves and in their life, too. It's time for the Color Festival to come around! The Color Contest is one of the biggest days of the year; everyone is ready for it. Let's see who will win the title of the Color Master?

Colortopia is a real-time game of good sighting and some logical reasoning in which players rotate and flip their color cards to complete different color patterns based on color mixing and color contrast to earn color chips. The color chips can even be mixed to become a new color and get you more points! Whoever has the most points at the end is crowned the Color Master and wins!

A set of 9 extra cards with (regular) Dutch words for the Dutch version of Codenames.

The cards are:

     Amber/Zout
     Lavendel/Heet
     Zwart/Worst
     Blank/Koffie
     Jasmijn/Tong
     Marine/Spek
     Grijs/Beleg
     Zilver/Chili
     Zalm/Schuim

HexCON has a board of 4x4 hex-diamonds of 4 hex side length and a scoring hex-diamond of the same size. Each hex diamond is called a subboard. 2 sides of each subboard are associated with the color white and 2 with the color black. The subboards are alternating in the direction of these sides on the board.

Goal:have the biggest connected area of subboards
How:connect the sides of your color of a subboard to win it. Placement of stones in the subboard determines where your opponent can place the next stone on the board. When you win a subboard you get a bonus turn in which you can place your stone freely anywhere on the board. That stone determines the subboard for your opponents turn. It is possible to chain such wins. Is it impossible for a player to place a stone in a certain subboard, she can place her stone freely on the board. Won subboards are marked on the scoring board in player color.
Game end: as soon as one player controls an area on the scoring board that can not be surpassed in size.

Percy the Penguin is stuck in an igloo and can't get out.  By removing the ice blocks, one by one, you must find the one which will Save the Penguin.
The youngest player starts by inserting the trowel into the slot in any block f ice.  Make sure that the trowel is pushed in as far as it will go.  Carefully remove the trowel.  In doing so an ice block will also be removed.  If Percy does not jump out, that player's turn is over.  Play passes to the player on the left who repeats the action of inserting their trowel and removing an ice block.  The winner of the round is the player who removes the ice block which sets Percy free and so has Saved the Penguin.
Play an agreed number of rounds.
Note: There will be only 1 ice block which will Save the Penguin in each round.  To start the next round, replace all the ice blocks and make sure you rotate the Penguin.  Remember, a different ice block will release the Penguin each time you play.

Each player moves by a die roll. Should you be first to reach or pass a 'cross-road' can close it by putting a marker on it. Should another player now reach the same cross-road they must stop. The next turn(s), that player must roll a 6 or they cannot move. This continues in future turns until they roll the 6.
First to reach 'Home' wins.

Game found in the Toby Twirl 1953 Annual.

Vintage board game made in Germany about 1900. 
One player plays in Bear role with black pawn. Other players play in Bees role with yellow pawns. Bees are traveling around  the circle game board looking for new flowers and the Bear is trying to catch them and steal the honey. If the Bear catch all Bees - this player wins the game.

The whole title: "Würfelspiel "Heimatkunde" Hamburg - Altona". This vintage board game is about traveling in one of the biggest city of Germany - Hamburg. Players are taking cards and follow the instructions written on them. The first player who reach his final destination, wins the game.

A push your luck your luck card game where players compete to eliminate marks and collect bounties set by the Assassin's Guild.
The player with the biggest pile of gold at the end wins.

The game is a set of quick rounds, mark cards go up on the Guild notice board each with a variable bounty depending on which method is used to dispose of the target. Players have 4 cards representing different kill methods and 3 attempts per round to eliminate the marks.

Assassins play their attempt face down so everyone knows who your going for but not how. Using a gun is quicker but is worth little, while arranging an accident pays much better, but takes more time. The quickest assassin to get to a mark wins the bounty. Should two assassins use the same method however thing get awkward and neither gets the kill.

Players also compete to complete several missions laid out by the guild so that they an become assassin of the month and have the power to tip off the fuzz to harass their rivals.

A Victorian factory build founded on player trading that works!

In Meeple Factory, players use tile and worker placement to build up their factory room by room. The catch is players can only operate and expand their factories by trading resources with other players. Fortunately everyone needs your resources just as much so a rich market economy emerges.

Players win by ultimately having the most coin generated from selling products and expanding the factory.

Disposable Heroes 2 is a WWII platoon level historical miniature wargaming system. It is designed not just to put you in command of the troops and weapons of a WWII platoon but in the historical tactical situation of a platoon commander. It is your rifle squads that will carry your attack forward, or be the difference between holding the line or the enemy breaking through. Disposable Heroes 2 recreates a realistic tactical battlefield situation of a platoon commander.

Disposable Heroes 2 features:

Realistic ground scale and platoon frontage
Effective Fog of War system.

Accurate availability & deployment of platoons
Singular command and control system.

This is not just another platoon-level WWII system. Disposable Heroes 2 offers a unique tactical challenge with a focus on recreating the actual battlefield conditions that platoons fought in.

description:
Politics: The Debate is a card game for would-be political leaders. Players take on the role of politicians and try to convince each other they, and they alone, can solve socio-political issues. If you enjoy debating fiscal responsibility one turn, and the feasibility of solving homelessness by imprisoning the homeless the next, The Debate is the game for you.
Players begin by separating and shuffling the two decks – ISSUES and POLCIES. The issues deck should be placed face-down on the table, and each player takes five policy cards to form a hand. The youngest player starts as the dealer, and they turn over the top issue card. This represents a particular problem that all the players, as politicians, want to look like they can solve. Players do this by playing two Policy cards from their hand. Each player does this going clockwise around the group, starting with the player after the dealer. Each player plays his/her two cards and explains briefly (30ish seconds) how the two Policies they have played solve the particular issue.
After each player has played his/her two Policies, players debate each other in a calm and collected fashion, gently critiquing each other’s policies while defending their own. When that’s done, go for the jugular – mercilessly attack your opponent’s stupid policies, compare them unfavourably to your fiscally-sound utopian masterplan, and suggest they’re like Hitler somehow. Maybe bite someone. When this is done, vote for a winner – the player whose Policies were the best. Remember to vote for the player who did the best at explaining, justifying and defending their Policies – not the player who suggested Policies closest to your personal ideology.
Once you’ve voted, take a note of the score and continue. The player to the left of the dealer becomes the new dealer, he or she deals two new policy cards to the player (bringing their hand back up to five), and turns over a new issue card. The whole horrible mess goes on like this until players either run out of issues or, if they want to reshuffle the issues and keep playing, until a pre-defined end time (or when you’ve had enough). Then the player with the most points is declared winner. Players should take this time to congratulate him/her, and re-visit contentious debates from the game.

Berd is a new abstract strategy game for two players. The goal of the game is to collect more tokens than your opponent. Players collect token(s) when crossing the board starting from their base line and finishing - passing opponent’s baseline. On their path to finish players can capture opponent’s token(s)
Some definitions: 
1. Tower – is a stack of two or more tokens; 
2. Berd is a tower with two or more same color tokens on the top; 
3.Unprotected tower or unprotected token (UT) – a single token or a tower that is not Berd. Unprotected tower moves like a single token. 
4. Recovering a captured token – player can recover the captured token by landing on UT and by restacking the tower create a Berd.

At the beginning of the game players arrange their tokens on their baseline (the first row from player’s side) in any combination. Players move their UT (unprotected token or Unprotected tower) one square at a time forward or side. You can’t move your UT diagonally or backward. While you can move your UT laterally however you can’t do more than three consecutive side moves. Players can capture opponent’s token by landing on a square occupied by a single token or unprotected tower. You can’t capture or land on a square occupied by your opponent’s Berd. A player can recover his/her captured token from opponent’s unprotected tower by landing on that tower. In this case the player will restack tokens and place his/her tokens on the top creating a Berd. In order to play a token from the Berd you have to split the Berd and play a single token or unprotected tower. If there are captured opponent’s tokens in the Berd they must be attached at least under one of your token; -so basically you move UT from your Berd. The game is over when only one player has active token(s) left on the board.  Now players can stack their collected tokens and see whose tower is taller or who collected more tokens.

Variations of the game:
1. Berd strategy game can be played on different size boards (3x3; 4x4; 5x5; 6x6) and/or with various number of tokens. Two sided board supplied in this game with 3x3 and 4x4 sides. Recommended to play with 5 tokens per each player on 3x3 and max 6 tokens on 4x4 board. 
2. Game with color protection – Players can use colored squares on the board for protection from being captured by the opponent’s token. For example: Red color player’s UT (single token or unprotected tower) will be protected on the Red square. Other rules are identical to the basic Berd game.
3. Game with points – in this game each token assigned different weight/points (10; 20; 30; 40; 50 ..), shown on the token. This option of the game provides more possibilities for moves. The winner will be a player who collects higher total score. In order to play this version of the game you need to attach stickers with numbers. There are lines on the sides of the token for even attachment.
4. Game with hidden points – games #1; 2 and 3 can be played with tokens that have hidden numbers (numbers are not displayed outside, they are attached inside). This game is similar to game #3 with the difference that only a player who possesses a token can see the weight of that token. This feature introduces more intrigue and uncertainty about the end result of the game.

The zombies are shuffling though the spooky paths of the cemetery in search of brains. Get on this ghastly adventure and try to hoard the biggest amount of brains! But watch out for Dr Bizarre- he will try to steal your brains for his sinister experiments in his freaky lab! This game includes a puzzle-like game board that takes a different shape every time!

Players take turns spinning the spinner and if they get a colored lantern they move to the next lantern of that color in any direction. If that space is next to a brain token - pick it up and put it in your jar. The brain tokens have 1-3 brains on them, but is not revealed until the game ends. If they get Dr Bizarre they move him to the next purple lantern, and if he comes across any zombies that player loses all of their collected brains. The game ends when a player reaches the goal-space. Then the players turn their collected brain tokens over and the player with the most collected brains wins!

"Montecuccoli on the Raab" is the first wargame of the newborn "Montecuccoli Battles Series", designed by Paolo Carraro. 
The Battle of Saint Gotthard was fought on 1st August 1664. 
Montecuccoli led the League of the Rhine to a great victory over the Ottoman Empire, despite the lesser number of soldiers.
Montecuccoli was the military genius in the 17th century who won all the battles in which he was the commander.

description:
"Dark Ages Britannia" is the first game of a new wargame series about the tactical medieval warfare. In this card-driven game, players will fight 7 historical scenarios: 3 concerning the fight for the English throne in 1066 (Fulford, Stamford Bridge, Hastings) and 4 regarding the Scottish Independence War (Stirling Bridge, Falkirk, Bannockburn day 1 and 2). You can command your troops all over the battlefield, with no restrictions due to any division of the map into areas, and activate your units using cards or issuing specific tactical orders to them. Units can both attack or defend with 2 different parameters, allowing players to change their combat stance and tactical deployment.
The game system is very straightforward and does not require any combat table to be played: everything you need is on the beautifully illustrated blocks and cards.
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

description:
Riddler is an upside-down variation of charades. Each player gets five clue cards. A clue card has two icons on them - these icons represent certain features. Players then get a category card, which defines the frame of their word, and a starting clue card. Players then place one card from their hand with one icon visible under the starting clue card and pass their hand to the player to the left. Players do this until they run out of cards in hand. Then the real fun begins! Players have to come up with a thing that matches the category and most of their clue icons! Then player get points for this, according to the amount of used cards! The game lasts three rounds, player with the most points wins!

This game requires a lot of creativity and guarantees fun even with the least experienced players.

boardgame: Commissioner Victor: Case of Missing Alfred Worthapenny
description:
Another quiet day at the precinct — but not for long. A woman, covered in tears, bursts through the door, crying that her husband went missing. It seems that Mr. Alfred Worthapenny did not show himself at home for two days. He didn't show at work either, and friends report no sightings. After sending the woman home, you place a quick phone call to Commissioner Victor. After a short investigation, it turns out that the case is much more complicated that it seems. Lots of mutually exclusive evidence and a lack of connection between them — this looks strange. The Commissioner gathers his most trusted detectives. Time to solve the mystery of missing Alfred Worthapenny!

In Commissioner Victor: Case of Missing Alfred Worthapenny, players try to find out what happened to Mr. Alfred. They do so by placing evidence tiles in four colors on a grid. Each player has a different pattern to form from tiles of the same colors. Also, each player gets a tip that crosses out one possible outcome, which means that players cannot win with one of four colors.

The four extensions The New Buildings, The Fertile Land, The Fine Arts and The Bad Times can be played individually, all combined, or in any combination together with the basic game.

The New Buildings contains four new buildings(two green, two orange ones) which improve actions permanently.

The Fertile Land consists of six fertile land terrain tiles for easier access to resources. Each peasant on these terrain tiles can harvest or exploit both resources if the action tiles harvesting or exploiting are chosen. These terrain tiles do not have building sites.

The Fine Arts add the four gemstone mines to the terrain tiles of the basic game and four new buildings to the other buildings. For these a player can get additional victory points according to the gemstones.

The Bad Times consists of nine bad times tiles for experienced players. They offer new challenges as individual actions become temporarily worse.

Gok’s fate was sealed before he was even conceived. His harsh upbringing combined with his inability to learn, left him as a ideal recruit for the Cult of Yurei, that and the fact that he is quite formidable. His mutating mass, and he has a lot of it, allows Gok to adapt to best any opponent. The disease ridden, body filled cart he insists on hauling also serves as a grotesque distraction in battle for his opponents.

The large rat swarm proves the old saying of safety in numbers, where one rat falls another rises to take its place. The normally self-serving animals unite as one entity to bite and tear at opponents of the Cult. Armour does little to protect against the swarming mass of a plague of rats and samurai have been seen swamped while hacking numerous rats to pieces.

“Rotten to his core,” was how Shichiro’s own mother described him, even before he left with the last of the families meagre savings. Joining the cult wasn’t chance, it was inevitable for someone like him. He relishes killing but prefers a well-placed tanto in the back over the risk of direct confrontation.

Where she comes from, who she is or who she was will probably never be known. The chance of anything being left of the person she used to be seems unlikely. Unquestioning, she ambles along thoughtlessly, slowly and yet relentlessly to do the bidding of her dark masters. No one is safe from the depravities of the Cult.

A Samurais' honor is his life. Those foolish few to betray the ancient code are cursed. Trapped and tormented by their weakness they linger on, becoming twisted mockeries of their former selves. These evil spirits are a fearsome and tireless servants of Yurei.

How Mo Ises was drawn into the cult is unknown. He is though a deadly foe, often misjudged by his opponents they remain unaware of the threat he poses until they feel the cold of steel at their backs. The power of his words have been enhanced by whatever bargain he made with Yurei.

Harionago is a truly terrifying addition to the ranks of the Cult. An ancient evil that for generations has preyed on unwary travellers across the Jwar Isles, her new found purpose to further the goals of Yurei is a frightening prospect.

The Gaki are cursed, once greedy and conceited men, now damned to lurk in the dark. Constantly driven by the gnawing pain of a hunger that cannot be satisfied no matter to volume of meat this foul Yokai consumes. Those rare peasant who survive the steel like claws and razor sharp teeth talk of a cloak of dense, cold mist enveloping them before the assault.

Some say the Wanyudo are a curse of those that would break the tenants of Michi. I punishment for the Samurai who fail in their duty to their ancestors and a hex of the lands they survey. What is known is that these Wanyudo are the very vision of hell itself. Rampaging across the land, those brave enough to oppose the evil Yokai must endure the wicked flames that dance out from the flaming inferno charring flesh and melting metal, cooking the would be heroes alive.

Although thank fully rare, like all the Yokai of legend sitings are spoken of with greater frequency all over the Jwar Isles, further fuelling the unrest in the land. Some say that if the peasants of the land give up the lands lord the curse of the Wanyudo will move on.

boardgame: Yellow Sea (fan expansion for Ticket to Ride: Rails and Sails)
description:
This map is an unofficial fan made expansion. Symbols & Graphics Copyright © 2004-2015 Days of Wonder, Inc. Produced with permission of Days of Wonder, for non-commercial use only.

This follows the principles of the basic Ticket to Ride Rails and Sails game on a special board with special cards and rules. The object of the game is to gain the most points by travelling various routes and outwitting other players. You will need all the train and ship pieces, the train and ship cards and the rules from the base game.

There is an extra set of Explorer cards for the cities, each with a value to be earned by visiting that city. These cards are dealt/picked up alongwith tickets in the same quantity and with the same 'keep/dispose' rules.

There are also Challenger cards which may help or hinder you. The bonuses and the secret city card give you extra points.

There are many new options in the rules such as 'exchange all your cards at the start but keep all the new ones' and 'discard 5 cards and pick up 5 new ones'.

Little is known about the origins of Nezumi Kun, even the scholars of the Blue library could only guess at the occult rituals and black magic used to create this “Son of Nezumi”. The savage Yokai appears more wild and feral than its “father”, striking from nowhere, only a rank stench left to mark its passage before it scuttles away into the darkness. Waiting and lurking in the shadows waiting for instruction from a faceless unseen evil.

Description from the publisher:

Vs System 2PCG: The Predator Battles is a 200-card standalone expansion for Vs System 2PCG card game that features new characters and card mechanisms from the Predator universe! Players can play as heroes such as Dutch, Harrigan, and their allies or as some of the most deadly Predator hunters in existence. This set contains new main characters, supporting characters, equipment, and more across two new team factions.

description:
Description from the designer:

Friendly Farmers was created as an entry in the 2016 Children's Game PnP Design competition.

Friendly Farmers is a worker placement game. Each turn players assign the workers on their farm different tasks depending upon the weather and other factors influencing crop management. When crops are harvested they can be sold immediately or stockpiled for sale at a later stage.

In the basic game market prices are fixed and there is always a fully available labour pool.  In the advanced game the size of the labour pool and the value of each crop's market prices are subject to variation each turn.

Players race to grow and sell their crops and be the first to reach 50 points.

The philosopher’s stone sits atop a pedestal in the greatest temple of Elorim, bathing the chamber in green light.  Purportedly, the jewel fills the hearts of the people with peace,  protects the entire kingdom from evil, and even has the power to grant immortality. Sadly, the jewel can’t do any of those things, because it is a fake. Should a warrior such as yourself defeat the lost king and return the true philosopher’s stone, it might put a quick end to the blight of monsters.  It would be mighty impressive too!

The final Dungeon Dice expansion, adding dungeons, shrines, traps, and new potions to the all-dice game!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Dixon to Carrier: Scratch One Flattop
description:
Description from the publisher:

“All the tension on the carrier exploded the moment we heard Comdr. Dixon's voice come in strong and clear: ‘Scratch one flattop! Dixon to carrier: Scratch one flattop.’” -- Stanley Johnston, war correspondent for The Chicago Tribune, on the sinking of the Shōhō

The Japanese Empire invincibly thundered across the Pacific Ocean in the months after their surprise attack at Pearl Harbor. Scrambling in defense, the Americans gained intelligence of impending Japanese invasions across the Coral Sea, and moved two fleet carriers to oppose. The war's first great carrier battles ensued; the Americans would deliver the initial blow on 7 May 1942 when aircraft from the carrier Yorktown sank the Japanese light carrier Shōhō.

Dixon to Carrier: Scratch One Flattop! from designer Esteban Fernandez is a co-op game where each player takes control of one or more Douglas SBD Dauntless dive bombers from the USS Yorktown carrier. The objective of the game is to find, and sink, the Japanese aircraft carrier IJN Shōhō. The Japanese Navy wins if the players lose a certain number of planes or if the USS Yorktown is sunk.

Now you can attempt to recreate those fateful days in May. The stern days of Japanese naval supremacy must be challenged; will you succeed, or end in a watery grave?
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Description from the publisher:

Last fall, crackling fires wreathed the mountains. This spring, light-wielding thunderstorms roiled the valleys. Now, with summer come early and a glowing moon overhead, the soil is moist and black and delicious mushrooms are peeking up all over the forest. The Foray is on!

Morels Foray expands Morels with new items, characters, weather, a more dynamic forest, and unique systems enabling 2-4 players. 3p and 4p have slightly augmented decks, start players with a basket and sticks, and introduce "peek cards" placed beside the deck to show what's coming up in the forest (these cards enhance planning depth but are not immediately accessible).

Each player count also has unique facets. In 3p, players receive an optional (non-card acquisition) bonus action at the end of each turn. In 4p, a marker rotates clockwise to show who is the Priority Player on any given turn. Players select cards from the same forest simultaneously on a Compass, then reveal and resolve proceeding clockwise before resetting the forest and passing the Priority Marker. Deciphering playing styles and motives is helpful because having selected an option that is no longer available when you go to resolve requires adaptation, at a price. This style of simultaneous play keeps a peppy pace with no down time and resembles a 2p Morels hallmark in evaluating what your opponents are likely to do and adjusting for optimal foraging.

At its heart, Morels Foray varies both the social and tactical texture of Morels. The new cards present fresh decisions and the opportunity for new strategies.  Weather cards (Rainstorm and Forest Fire) can decimate existing forests or spring up new ones, Items (Map, Shiitake Log) grant power with proper timing, Events (Farmers Market, Eureka!) enable big plays, Characters (Thief, Farm Girl, Eccentric, Chef, Morchella) allow you to interact with the forest and your opponent(s) in new ways, and new mushrooms (Lion's Mane, Panther Cap) expand your foraging options in the ever beckoning forest.

Description from the publisher:

Magic Hat is a colourful memory game for the whole family!

What is it about?

Something disturbing happened in the Magic Kingdom! At night someone swapped all the hats in the castle! The Wizard looks ridiculously funny wearing Dragon’s horns on his head. We need to help the citizens of the Magic Kingdom with this mess!

Wow! How do we play?

The object for each player is to return the hats to their owners as fast as possible strategically moving the cards, bluffing and trying to mislead the opponents.

When it’s your turn, you have the following options: look up one hat card, swap two hat cards, open the matching hat card for the character.

If you open the wrong card you will have to take a penalty token and wait until your next turn to try and get that magic hat card that cancels the penalty!

Who is this game for?

Both kids and grown-ups will enjoy this game. Fairytale characters create a magical atmosphere and there is a hint of humour in each character.

What can we learn?

This game helps the kids to develop their memory, to learn how to control their emotions, to bluff and to think strategically.

Description from the designer:

TRIPLEX is three games of Tic Tac Toe, all played at once, on the same board with movable pieces. The goal is just as simple as that of Tic Tac Toe: to form alignments of three in a row. The goal is also to form one alignment of all three types of pieces all at the same time.

In turns, players play any one of their pieces into any open space. Once all pieces have been played onto the board they may then be moved from where they were played into any open space. The first player to form all three types of alignments at the same time wins.

TRIPLEX is a board game that designers have been trying to create for over a century. TRIPLEX is the worlds only easily comprehensible, highly enjoyable and truly three dimensional strategic board game.

Description from the designer:

Card Clash is a quick game that sees 2 players attacking and counterattacking with cards whose type preference beats the previously played card. If your opponent can't counter your attack with any of the 4 cards in their hand, you win the round! But each successive round adds a new set of stronger cards into both players' decks, so try to get the first attack in to knock out your opponent before they get a chance to play a devastating attack! The first to win 2 rounds wins the game.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
Family board game including 12 different games:


     Quiz!
     Sketch!
     Blind sketch!
     Sculpture!
     Pantomime!
     Stand up comedy!
     Song!
     Spelling!
     Scrabble!
     Hangman!
     Inverse reading!
     Vocabulary!

Description from the designer:

Moneytime is a basketball card game that aims to deliver the sensations of a basketball game.

Each player plays a team and plays each basketball possession to try to score baskets, and playing defense and offense alternatively (like basketball).

By playing his offensive cards against the defensive cards in the possessions, the player playing offense will try to build an advantage to score using basketball actions with the ball, and without the ball wisely to avoid turnovers.

Simple and fast paced, Moneytime is designed around the notion of time, where individual highlights and simple collective mechanics are key, while shots at the buzzer and crazy end games are not rare.

Sound from a basketball perceptive, it remains true to the spirit of the game and reflects how basketball is to be played.

Typically a two players game, It can also be played by up to six players.

boardgame: Red Dragon Storm: 1979 – China Vietnam War
description: Red Dragon Storm is a strategic wargame of the Sino-Vietnamese War, 1979.

boardgame: The Lord of the Rings: The Card Game – The Mountain of Fire
description:
Description from the publisher:

"We come now to the very brink, where hope and despair are akin. To waver is to fall."
     –Aragorn, The Return of the King

Dire, world-shattering stakes. Epic Multiplayer Mode. The fate of all Middle-earth… Mountain of Fire is the sixth and final The Lord of the Rings Saga Expansion for The Lord of the Rings: The Card Game!

Three epic scenarios grant you the opportunity to adventure alongside Aragorn and Frodo Baggins as they march to the Black Gate, carry the One Ring into the heart of Mordor, and climb the final steps to the fires of Mount Doom. These scenarios are inspired directly by The Return of the King, and their apocalyptic, world-altering stakes are reflected by the introduction of the dire keyword, which raises the threat elimination level to "99," and by rules that allow you play the final two scenarios simultaneously in Epic Multiplayer Mode, either in standalone play or as the climactic finale to your The Lord of the Rings Saga Campaign!

And it's not just the scenarios that are bigger and more fantastic than ever. Mountain of Fire is loaded with new heroes, allies, events, and attachments that truly convey the awesome resolve of those noble and daring Men, Elves, Dwarves, and Hobbits that stood at the end against the forces of Mordor.
family: Collectible: Living Card Game (Fantasy Flight Games)
Game: The Lord of the Rings – The Card Game (LCG)

description:
Description from the designer:

Wallet Civ in Space! is a 9 card pen and paper game. Print or write out the 9 instruction cards, and on the reverse of each card, you will create your space mining empire.

Each turn, shuffle the deck, set aside 2 of the cards not in play this turn, play out 4 cards "space" side up to build upon, and then the remaining 3 cards "instruction" side up to see what you can do! Then shuffle up the 9 cards again.

Please see https://www.boardgamegeek.com/thread/1577111/wallet-civ-9-card-pnp-game for rules.

VIXIV is a strategic card game based on ecology and the people who manage it. You are new interns with the Department of Wildlife Management. Tasked with maintaining a specific area, you must create a thriving ecosystem while simultaneously thwarting your peers as you compete for limited space and the coveted attention of professional mentors.

Once you have built your map and draw your cards, you begin laying out your species and building your ecosystem. As the game progresses, your species will grow and thrive, earning you points and hopefully the attention of a mentor. Be warned though, everything can change with frightening speed. In just a few moments you may find yourself combating wildfires, succumbing to disease, or butting heads with your rivals.

Things quickly grow competitive as free space shrinks and your peers force your hand with actions capable of affecting the entire board. If you stay dedicated to your goals and you may wind up leading the group. Stay too focused though, and you may find yourself falling behind as the changes overwhelm your carefully laid plans.

—description from the publisher

Description from the designer:

Debate (formerly known as Discussion) is a 18 cards microgame in which you try to create a coherent speech using your resources at hand and your memory, and forcing your opponent to flip its ideas.

Debate is being played with an 18 cards deck with different symbols on them. It is very important to differentiate between the front and back of each card. The backs contain only two symbols, 9 corresponding to one player (red) and 9 for the other (green). The hand of each player is always held with the backs facing the opposite player. The cards are played next to others on the table showing their back sides and triggering some effects. You win by forming a group of connected cards of your color greater than your opponent’s.

Description from the publisher:

Tugawords is a super fast and brain powered version of Tug of War in which players alternate turns spelling backwards towards themselves and the first one to reach the finish wins. Your not so easy task is to pull the Flag to the Finish by spelling longer words than your opponent while trying to avoid being eliminated by Missed Spells.

At an average of 2-20 minutes per game, Tugawords is quick, unpredictable, open-ended and never has a chance to get boring.

Imagine a speed chess + spelling bee + tennis volley + tug of war...

Now you have an idea of Tugawords.

No points to count, no special tiles, and no predictable outcomes. Just win or lose and you can always tell where you are in the game...

This action-packed game will have children racing to complete all the zany challenges to win the game! Players can also work in teams to complete missions to allow them to capture spaces on the grid. The team with the most spaces once the grid is full wins the game.

Description from the publisher:

Description from the publisher:

In LA BISTRO each player starts with a small stake that they use to build up their restaurant and then with profits decide how much to invest further, either in improvements to counter what the opposition are doing or to create a second restaurant.

Simultaneous actions to take in this game:

     Choose a building in one of six different types of neighborhoods to site your place. 
     Decide from one of eight types of cuisine.
     Set your prices. 
     Hire kitchen staff to improve your food quality. 
     Hire wait staff to improve your level of service.
     Renovate your space to improve your ambiance. 
     Advertise so that people know about you. 


Do these in any order you like, but do them better than everyone else, and quickly! Before you know it the first customers will be rolling in!

Features many details of the restaurant business including differing neighborhoods, cuisine types and a wide variety of employees and renovations.

Customer visits are determined by a new gaming mechanism in which players roll 15 dice and place them in a unique way to determine which restaurants the fickle public taste adores.

At the end of the year though, it's the planner who has the most money who wins.

The object of Pawsitively Historical is to be the first player to collect all historical pictures cards numbered from 1-14, in order. Roll the die and stretch your memory to find the hidden historical face cards in sequence. The 1st player to collect all 14 historical face cards in a row wins the game.

A fast & furious flipping card game. Quickly flip over the top card from your pile and place it onto the center pile. Slap the center pile when you spot one of three things - two of the same cards in a row, two of the same separated one that is different, or one of the Meteor Shower cards. The first player to slap the pile collects all the cards in the center to add to their pile until all have been collected.

Description from the publisher:

The Outrageous and Sneaky Bluffing Card Game. Get rid of the cards in your hand by laying them down in sequence. If you don't have the next card in the sequence, bluff by laying down another card. But don't get caught. If your opponents call your bluff and catch you in a lie, you get all the cards in the pile!

Description from the designer:

The Joust is a 2-player card game that uses a standard deck of cards along with the two jokers. You will also need pencil and paper or 4d6 [2- white, 1-red and 1-blue] used to track knight advantage and number of lances broken.

The design is component ready since it needs only a deck of cards and I will post the rules later today.

The Joust is played where two knights ride horses in a list with armor and lance trying to knock the other knight off his horse.

A win will be either unhorsing your opponent "which is an automatic win or breaking three lances.

As you ride down the list you will try to gain an advantage while also trying to center your lance on your opponents shield on impact.

You will use 8 cards to create the initial list. The jokers will represent each knight, and 9 cards [from each player] will represent each players shield.

Description from the publisher:

Peloponnes Heroes and Colonies will add Heroes with individual powers to the players and Colonies, which replaces the playerboards from the original game. All Colonies have different advantages and disadvantages.
A new "disaster" added with the Persians, who strikes twice in the game.
Gain strength while adding buildings and land to your civilisation to defend the Persians and other players.

Peloponnes Heroes and Colonies is a big new expansion for Peloponnes that comes in the same box size as the "Peloponnes Box".

Kingdom of Heaven: 2nd and 3rd Crusades is an issue game of War Drum Games' wargame books for beginners "Battle on the Table" #3. The game is solitaire. The player tries to defend the Latin Kingdom of Jerusalem against Muslim invasions. The system was inspired by Victory Point Games' Cruel Necessity.

boardgame: Twilight of the Reich: The Berlin Campaign 1945
description: Twilight of the Reich: The Berlin Campaign 1945 is an issue game of War Drum Games' wargame mook for beginners "Battle on the Table" #4. The game is basically played by two players. Soviet player tries to occupy the whole of Berlin, German player tries to prevent it.

Last Battle of Sanada Yukimura is an issue game of War Drum Games' wargame mook for beginners "Battle on the Table" #5. The theme is summer campaign of Siege of Osaka, 1615.
https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Siege_of_Osaka

The game is basically played by two players. One commands Tokugawa force, the other Toyotomi force.

This is the first game of Kilovolt Design's tactical mini wargame series "Winning or Losing". The theme is the Battle of Messines (1917) during World War I. The title is named after Australian war film Beneath Hill 60. The game is basically played by two players. British player tries to occupy Messines Ridge. German player tries to prevent it.

boardgame: Shadow of Wolves on a Moonlight Night
description: This is the second game of Kilovolt Design's tactical mini wargame series "Winning or Losing". The theme is U-boats attack on Convoy SC 104 during World War II. The game is basically played by two players. German player commands U-boats and tries to sink Allied transport ships. Allied player tries to prevent it.

This is the third game of Kilovolt Design's tactical mini wargame series "Winning or Losing". The theme is aerial combat in "MiG Alley" during Korean War. The game is basically played by two players. UN player commands USAF and tries to destroy a bridge over the Yalu. Communist player commands Soviet/Chinese MiGs and tries to defend the bridge.

"Lion of Sinai" is the fourth game of Kilovolt Design's tactical mini-wargame series "Winning or Losing". The theme is tank combat in the Sinai Peninsula, 14-16 October 1973 during Yom Kippur War. The game is basically played by two players.

description: This is the fifth game of Kilovolt Design's tactical mini wargame series "Winning or Losing". The theme is Battle of Vukovar during Croatian War of Independence. The game is 2 in 1: Battle of Vukovar and Battle of East Croatia.

This very early shopping game was published in 1879 as an advertising vehicle by Philadelphia department store Strawbridge & Clothier.

The board shows a circular track with 60 spaces. It is divided into four quadrants: blue, yellow, green and red. Each quadrant has spaces for three of the store's departments on the track, an elevator space inside the track, and a cashier's desk outside the track.

Players spin and move around the track. When they land on a department, they must go to that quadrant's elevator. There they stay until they spin one of three numbers, which then move them to a department either ahead or behind the one they left. A player landing on the same space as another bumps that player to the cashier's desk for the quadrant, from which he can move to the beginning of the quadrant with a spin of 3, or to the end of the quadrant with a 6.

No goods are actually bought or sold: the game is simply a race from the entrance to the exit with goods as distractions along the way.

The game board is of thin cardboard, and was given away in an envelope rather than a box.

Rage in Glory is a game based on old fashion sword fighting.

It is a two player board where both players have a battle board that they are dueling on.

You keep these boards secret from each other, and when you fight, you both set up your boards by putting attack and block tokens on the different numbers, when both players are ready, you reveal your boards.

If you attack a number and it wasn't blocked your opponent takes damage, but if the number that you attacked was block your opponent is unharmed.

The game ends when you are out of health.

Just remember to watch your back in this fast past action game.

The Letter Head Design Challenge invited Letter Head fans to submit their own games utilizing the innovative Letter Head deck. Here, then, are six brand new Letter Head games, running the gamut from deckbuilding to subterranean exploration. These games expand and change the way Letter Head is played, providing hours of additional enjoyment. And they're free to download from the Atlas Games website—all you need is a Letter Head deck to play.

Description:
This game has been published for the 1000th anniversary of the city of Fürth in a limited edition of 1000 copies.

Description of the author:
Get on your way and explore Fuerth in a very special way. Two to four players go on an exciting journey through the Kleeblattstadt. On 240 cards, questions await you to be answered and events want to be discovered. In addition to three possibilities for answering each question, you will find a brief explanation for each question. Let us invite you to test your knowledge of the city of Fürth and also to find out a lot about the medium-sized Franconian town.

boardgame: Dynasty: The Era of the Five Dynasties
description:
Richard Berg's Dynasty is a multi-player game that recreates the power politics and military struggles of tenth century China. One player begins the game as the Emperor, defending the realm from Khitan raiders, providing flood relief, and collecting taxes, so as to maintain the Mandate of Heaven and continue to rule. The other players are provincial governors seeking to weaken the Emperor's grasp on power and take it for themselves.

What's unique about this game is that the Emperor has a completely different set of actions and a completely different turn structure than his opponents. When one of the governors is able to overthrow the Emperor, he assumes that role, while the disgraced Emperor now finds himself a governor, and must scheme to retake the throne - alongside the other players.

Actions taken by the players include military expansion, army building, the collection of taxes, and the purchase or play of event cards.

"May you live in interesting times" is sometimes inaccurately attributed to an ancient Chinese curse, but regardless of where the phrase comes from, these are interesting times indeed. Petty warlords, rebellions, and natural disasters enter play via event cards, and create the chaotic tapestry upon which your struggles for power are woven. Not only must you deal with one darn thing after another, but you must work to turn them to your advantage. Every action you take has its cost, and creates opportunities for the other players.

Throughout the game and at the end, players will score Victory Points for their accomplishments - and earning a VP every turn is the Emperor, because staying in power for any length of time in the Five Dynasties era is an accomplishment! - and the player with the most Victory Points wins the game.

boardgame: Operation Unthinkable: Churchill's World War III
description:
Operation Unthinkable, designed by Ty Bomba, allows one or two players to explore the possible outcomes inherent in the campaign that would’ve resulted had Winston Churchill’s plan for starting World War III in July 1945 been put into effect. For that to have happened, two changes in the timeline need have taken place during the first half of that year.

First, Churchill would’ve had to put off the British general election until after Japan was defeated. He had the political leverage to do that, but against all good advice he chose not to do so. He mistakenly believed the recent victory over Nazi Germany was enough to sustain his coalition government in power.

Second, he would have had to convince US President Harry Truman, then only recently come into office after the death of FDR, that preemptive war against the Soviets was the best choice among a limited number of strategic options. A close study of the debate that went on historically within the White House and Pentagon at the time concerning the idea indicates that, by March 1946, those Washington powerbrokers had become convinced a new World War – with the USSR as the primary enemy – was indeed coming and coming soon. Had that conviction taken hold in Washington just eight months sooner – and it well might have, had Stalin or Tito been only a little more aggressive in their territorial and political demands – this would not be an alternative history design.

The general flow of the game inescapably has the Allies on the offensive at its start. On the Soviet side, that player is immediately faced with a decision to either “fight forward,” with Berlin as the central core of that gambit, or retreating east with the idea of slowly wearing down the Allied offensive and then counterattacking into its flanks late in the game. Further, though the Allies have the ability to make paradrops and amphibious invasions, they lack the dedicated manpower necessary to make such gambits automatically decisive. Most games are decided either in and around Berlin or somewhere east of the Oder-Neisse line.

Each hex on the map represents eight miles (13 km) from side to opposite side. Each full game turn represents one week. Each unit of maneuver represents a corps or army. There are 192 medium-size (9/16”) units. Complexity is low-intermediate, and a game can be completed in under four hours. The system is solitaire friendly.

An adventure is beginning. Only you can save Goldfish Kingdom. You have to accept the challenge and face countless monsters with skills. Unless you are a forgetful goldfish, there's no one who can defeat you. Come on! Our savior~~AMNESIA HEROES!

SETUP
1. Shuffle challenge cards and skill cards separately. Place these two decks in the middle of the table. 
2. In the beginning, draw a challenge card and place it in the middle of the table. 
3. Place damage marks and goldfish marks aside.
4. The player who looks like a goldfish goes first.

COURSE OF PLAY

There are four stages in this game. The leader in this turn executes the action.
 Learning skills: Through this stage, the leader deals the skill cards to each player. During the process, the leader has to remember which skill cards are in whose hands. It is the key to defeat the monster at the next stage.    
1. The leader deals the skill cards face-up to each player clockwise. 
2. Two options to deal cards:
A. Each time deal a skill card to each player clockwise.
B. Deal the skill cards to a player one time. Then deal the skill cards to the next player clockwise.      
Tip! No matter which option you choose, the process of dealing the skill cards can help you to find the best way to remember these cards. 
3. Each player chooses a skill card from your hands. Then places it face down in front of you as a skill you can't remember.

※ The leader doesn't have to execute this action.

Select a monster: The leader selects a challenge card from the table or the deck.

Attack:

According to the attack sequence shown on the challenge card, the leader has to play skill cards from the left to the right or use goldfish marks to attack the monster.

※ Remember the skill cards you dealt? Who has the skill card you need?!

boardgame: Sagrada: Promo 1 – Vitraux/Balboa Bay Window Pattern Card
description: A window pattern card for Sagrada that features a level 4 (Balboa Bay) and level 5 (Vitraux) pattern on opposing sides.

description:
菊花賽 (Kikkasai) is a two-player dice game from Japan that derives a lot from Riichi Mahjong, the Japanese version of Mahjong.

The name is a pun on the phononym 菊花祭 (Kikkasai), a flower festival where exhibitors show off their prize blooms. 菊 (Kiku) is a popular and lucky formal flower (花 hana, ka) in Japan, and 祭 (sai or matsuri) is a festival. Many flower farmers strive for a better Kiku. There are flower festivals where you can find and buy many flowers, and so a festival focused on Kiku is 菊花祭, especially with plants shown in competition. But 賽 (sai) is dice, so 菊花賽 is also Kikkasai, making a Flower Festival Dice game.

The game follows the style of Mahjong, drawing dice from a bag, rolling, discarding, trying to complete sets. In this game, you only handle 2 + 1 dice, so your target is to create one single set. Many of the rules match Mahjong; draw 1 discard 1, claiming the dice your opponent discards, or the rule of "trend" which gives you extra points (like the Dora rule in Mahjong).

Initially, one die is placed as the "trend". You get extra points for winning a hand by using a same colored die / same pip with the trend die. Then each player grabs 2 dice from the bag and rolls behind their screen. These dice are the players' hands.

Each turn, if you can use the last die discarded by your opponent to complete your set, you declare and win the round. If not, you grab a die from the bag and roll. If the 3 dice (2 in your hand and 1 just rolled) completes your hand, you win the round and score points. If not, you must discard 1 die, either from your hand, or the rolled one. Be careful not to toss out a die your opponent wants. Now the turn switches to your opponent.

The round ends when any player wins, or the bag is out of dice. Put all the dice back in the bag. If any player has 0 points or less, that player loses. Or else, start a new round, switching the starting player.

The publisher says:
A 2 player game of dice-Mahjong.

The game takes place in the Finals of a Flower show competition for prize Chrysanthemum. You aim for the victory by completing your set of Chrysanthemum dice. Both players start from 6 points, and you win by depleting your opponents' points to 0.

Basic game set is for 2, but combining 2 sets would allow you to play by 3 or 4 players.

Publisher's summary
２人用のダイス麻雀ゲームです。
舞台は菊の品評会の決勝戦。
３つの菊ダイスを揃えて優勝を目指します。
６ポイントずつ持ってスタートし、相手のポイントを０にすると勝ちです。
※基本２人用ですが、２セットあると３人プレイ、４人プレイをすることができます。

boardgame: Three Cheers for Master: Santa Claws
description:
4 promo unique cards for Three Cheers for Master.

These cards was released for Christmas. It's four times the same except the funny text which is different on each card.

Description from the publisher:

You’ve spent your life tramping across the fields of Terrinoth, facing danger time and again, testing your skills in battle against terrifying monsters, and living a life of unchecked adventure. You never know what you may discover beyond the horizon—a towering range of mountains, the lair of an evil beast, or the sun reflecting from the walls of one of the Free Cities. Yet throughout your travels, there’s always been a feeling of competition with other adventurers, driving you to fight harder and move faster than another so-called “hero.” Just maybe you’d prefer to work and fight together, once in a while?

Unbreakable Bonds is a new expansion for Runebound, and gives you the option to dramatically reshape the nature of the game. Rather than competing against up to three opponents, Unbreakable Bonds invites you to journey through the fantasy realm of Terrinoth as part of a group, or even to strike out on your own in a solo game! With a robust combat system to control the monsters, two new heroes, five cooperative / solo scenarios, and a wealth of new asset and skill cards for any game, Unbreakable Bonds pushes the borders of Terrinoth further than ever and leads you beyond the edge of the map.

boardgame: Three Cheers for Master: Phil A. Minion
description:
A promo card for three cheers for master

A new minion giveaway in conventions.

boardgame: To The Last Man: Rules for WWI Skirmish Combat
description:
The era of the First World War is sometimes seen as one of unimaginative mechanised slaughter with the humble infantryman no more than cannon fodder.

In reality, personal skill and courage were often more important than ever to the men in the front line. Cut off from their comrades in the wastes of no man's land, without the communications which soldiers take for granted today, small groups men struggled to stay alive and defeat their enemies in ferocious close range battles.

To The Last Man is a 32 page set of skirmish rules for the period which enables you to take command of a section or platoon in such a desperate situation and lead it to victory or bloody defeat. Designed for fast, enjoyable and tactically challenging games, the rules are intended not just for the Western Front, but for all the far flung theatres of conflict in the early decades of the 20th century - from the steppes of Siberia to the jungles of East Africa.

Morbid Curiosity is a unique party game that creates a delightfully dark evening with friends.

Using trivia and conversation cards, players explore and ignite their curiosity about the greatest mystery of all – death. Contains 162 cards... all of them about death. Takes only minutes to learn to play, provides hours of curious, deep, and irreverent conversation.

Morbid Curiosity is not a harsh look at your own mortality, or some delicate, awkward conversation about the end of life. Rather, it is a unique, well crafted game that lets people look at death while having a good time. So kick off your shoes, sit down, and get to know Death a little better.

On their turn a player (the reader) draws a card from either the conversation deck or the trivia deck and reads it aloud for the group to answer. Trivia cards are awarded to the player who gives the correct answer, for conversation cards the reader chooses their favourite answer. The first person to collect 7 cards wins.

description:
This is an 1884 trivia card game focused on American history. As with most such games, players are dealt an initial hand, and on their turn, ask a question of their neighbour to the left. If that person answers correctly, he keeps the card; if not, other players in turn have an opportunity, and if no one can answer, the questioning player keeps the card. The player with the most cards at the end of the game wins.

Most of the 180 cards in this game bear one or more title words (names, places, things or events) followed by two or more related facts. For these cards, the questioner states a title word or phrase, and the other players must state one relevant fact.

The remaining cards have specific questions, which players must answer completely. (For example: "Name the features of the Omnibus Bill. The 5-part answer: California should be a free state; Utah and New Mexico should be formed into territories; Texas should be paid $10 million to give up its claim to New Mexico; the Slave Trade should be prohibited in the District of Columbia; and Fugitive slaves should be returned to their owners.)

According to Annual Reports of the Department of the Interior, 1884 this game has 200 cards.

Orthoepy, which means the study of correct pronunciation, is an educational card game published in Chicago in 1899. It consists of two numbered decks each of 50 cards. One of these decks has Sentence cards, each with six numbered sentences; the other deck has the matching Master cards, showing the proper pronunciation of each difficult word in the sentences.

The rules provide several variations of the game. In the most basic, the Social Game, each deck is distributed evenly among the players. In turn, each player calls out the number of a Master card in his hand. The player holding the corresponding Sentence card then must slowly and distinctly read the sentence on that card of the Master's choice. If the Master player notices any mistakes, he must confirm what the Sentence player said, and then claims the pair of cards as a "book"; if he notices no mistakes, then the reading player gets the cards. Play then passes to the left, and the player with the most "books" when the cards run out is the winner.

The deck includes an Arbiter card, with whoever holds it being empowered to decide any disputes. In the Commercial game, the Arbiter card initially counts as a single point, and failed cards go to the Arbiter and count to his score. However, if the Arbiter makes a mistake, the Arbiter card goes to the successful Master player, and its value increases by one.

In the Professional game, the number of points won is equal to the number of test words correctly pronounced rather than the number of cards; a player missing even a single word scores nothing while the Master gets all the points for the sentence. This introduces an element of strategy for Masters: whether to challenge a given Reader with a long and difficult sentence that could mean a big swing one way or the other, or to choose a short and easier one with less risk and less reward. There are additional rules for Club play and Contests.

This is a roll-and-move game published to mark the U.S. entry into World War I. It allows up to six players, each representing an arm of the U.S. forces: Infantry, Cavalry, Artillery, Marine Corps, Engineers and Aviation Corps.

The board has a circular track and six spokes leading inward to the center of the board. Each player starts on the space representing the headquarters of his branch (Boston, Portland, Savannah, Charleston, Norfolk or New York), and must complete a circuit of the board "Dodging Submarines".

Upon return to base, he then heads inwards on a spoke, along spaces marked: In France; On Wotan Line; In Belgium; Before the Rhine; Across the Rhine; Through Germany; Before Berlin; and finally the central space In Berlin, with a caricature of Kaiser Wilhelm. The winner is the first to reach the center circle.

The board indicates "Patent pending" but does not name a publisher or copyright holder.

The gloves are off and the gauntlets are on! Implements of Destruction is the first expansion since Tools of Hero-Kind to feature Items, which attach to Heroes but can be claimed by Bosses. This set features 5 new Bosses and 24 new Items. It's playable with Boss Monster or Boss Monster 2, and it includes Explorer Items for your 5-6 player Crash Landing games!

User summary
Pacific Go is a game about the Pacific War. One player takes the role of the Japanese army, and the other is the Allied powers, and both struggle for their own forces' victory. The game is played in 10 rounds, playing time is about 90 - 120 minutes.

Both forces consume "Resource points (RP)", for both moving and attacking. The Japanese forces gain RP when they control certain resource production areas, whereas the Allies gain a fixed number of RP. The Japanese consume 1 RP per unit per area, whereas the Allies consume 1 RP per unit regardless of moved areas.

Some important areas have Victory Point values assigned. At the end of the game, the Allies win if they have gained enough VPs. Japan wins if they have successfully blocked the Allies from gaining VPs.

Publisher's summary
『PACIFIC GO』は、太平洋戦争を題材としたボードゲームです。一人は日本軍、もう一人は連合軍を担当して、自軍を勝利に導きます。
ゲームは全10ターンからなり、プレイタイムは90分から120分程度です。
両軍とも、移動と戦闘に「資源ポイント（RP）」を必要とします。日本軍は資源エリアを支配することで獲得し、連合軍は毎ターン規定数を獲得します。
日本軍は１ユニットが１エリア移動するたびに１RPを消費します。連合軍は何エリア移動しても１ユニットにつき１ポイントの消費です。
重要エリアにはVPが設定されていて、最終的に連合軍が規定数以上のVPを獲得していれば勝利。それを阻止すれば日本軍の勝利です。

description:
In a future where the solar system is ruled by a tyrannical government, a band of outcasts escapes to a star system light years away. There they discover an ancient human colony with a dark secret that threatens to destroy the Earth itself. You are all that stands between the Earth that abandoned you and its total destruction.

Take command of a ragtag space fleet and fight to save the Earth with nothing but a handful of outcasts and your ingenuity. Build, explore, and fight as you try to survive long enough to warn the Earth of the coming menace.

Centauri Saga: Abandoned is a legacy expansion for the sci-fi/4X co-operative board game for 1 to 4 players. All physical changes will be applied ONLY to expansion material itself.

This expansion works perfectly well with all variations of the game: Core, Arena & Legacy!

It allows players to enhance their characters with a special ability which is unlocked when you complete certain tasks.

The expansion also adds 2 extra commanders to your game!

Components

- 30 Special Ops Mission Cards
- 2 New Commanders
- 6 Player Markers (Punch Board Tokens)
- 18 Mission Markers (Punch Board Tokens)

boardgame: Quodd Heroes: Fringe Underground Map Pack
description: Beneath the town of Fringe, explorers have discovered ancient underground caverns filled with new challenges for our brave Heroes. This map pack comes with 3 large double-sided game boards, 1 large punch board with thematic tokens, and 2 of each Food, Item, Pet and Rune cards. 3 scenarios help you explore the deep, dark caves.

15 cards plus 2 cards of rules that add a Native Alliance power to each faction in the game. The ziploc also comes with an Alternative Setup "card" detailing two alternatives for setup steps 4 (forts) and 5 (muster markers) of the base game.

description:
The legendary treasure is here!
Use your brain to uncover the mystery of these five treasure maps.
You could get the treasure!

Goal of the game

Use 5 treasure maps to combine the same quantity of islands shown on clue cards. Then place the gold coin on the appointed location.

Game preparation

1. Each player uses a set of treasure map(5 maps per set)
2. Shuffle clue cards (your deck).
3. Place the coin aside.

Game round

Restriction:
Each treasure card must be shown at least an
island. Do not cover all of them.
All of the treasure maps must be used.
【Single Player Mode】
1. Draw a clue card from the deck .
2. Use 5 treasure cards to match the same quantity of island shown on the clue card by stacking and covering.
3. Place the coin on the appointed island.
【Multiple Player Mode:】
1. Prepare same quantity of copies according to the number of players.
2. Same rule as Single Player Mode.
3.The player who finds the treasure first grab the
clue card.
Correct O: The clue card turns into your point.
Wrong X: Return the clue card. Then remove one
4. Draw a new clue card and turn to next round.

Game End
The player who gets 3 clue cards wins the game.

Xiang-Yong was a type of regional army that emerged in the 1800s in the Qing dynasty army, which fought in most of China's wars after the Opium War and numerous rebellions exposed the ineffectiveness of the Manchu Eight Banners and Green Standard Army. The Xiang-Yong were created from the earlier tuanlian militias.

In the game Xiang-Yong, players play cards from their hands, trying to figure out which opponent might be able to help — but the one willing to help might not be without personal purposes for doing so. Choose your friends wisely, or you might not have the cards to join the game — and sometimes keeping yourself out of the other players' business is a better way to win this game...

Salt is a game of exploration, building, conflict, and magic in a constantly evolving landscape.

In Salt, you take control of a gang in an alternate reality, wild west-inspired setting. By exploring the world, charting new territory, gathering resources, building infrastructure, and acquiring weapons, you will gain control of the resources in the world. Figure out how to strike the most delicate balance of these elements to be the most dominant gang around.

Description from the publisher:

Laundry Day is a colourful game where you need to pair up socks after laundry. We will be choosing the socks for the friends from foreign countries!

Paired up socks: Myth or reality?

You are probably asking yourself this question! And yes! It is a reality! Every single patterned sock has a pair and this pair belongs to a one of the characters in the game!

How to do it?

Each player receives one character card. These characters come from six different foreign countries and dressed in national outfits. The object is to find four pairs of socks matching the pattern on your character’s dress. To win you will need to find all four pairs in three rounds of the game using one hand only!

Pick a sock!

If you need a challenge in this game ask for all four pairs of socks to have different toe and heel color. And the socks still have to match the pattern on the national dress!

There are six different cultures from different parts of the world and six different patterns from those cultures: Indian, Japanese, Mexican, Arabian, African and Russian! There is plenty of choice and topics for conversation!

Need another game variation?

You can spend a lot of time sorting out socks! Play classic memory game by placing the cards face down on the table.

Who is this game for?

For a family with preschool kids
    For those who works with kids
    For you can take this game with to a party

Description from the publisher:

The town of Devil's Burden is still under attack, and even more monsters are pouring in! Ingenious goblins, led by the mighty troll king and his retinue, are now laying devious traps all over the town to hamper the stalwart defenders. It is time to call for reinforcements — the long-time secret society within Devil's Burden: the Rogues' Guild.

Fantahzee: Rogue's Gallery adds rogues to the Fantahzee base game. Sneaky, clever, thieving heroes that can help you overcome the Horde. Adding rogues also means adding traps, which require rogues to overcome.

boardgame: Team Yankee: Bannon's Boys – American Spearhead Force
description:
Team Yankee, a heavy combat team, faces a Soviet invasion of West Germany. Outnumbered and outgunned, Captain Sean Bannon and his men will have to fight hard and they'll have to fight smart if they are going to survive.

Contains five plastic M1 Abrams Tanks, two plastic AH-1 Cobra Attack Helicopters, five resin tank commander figures, one resin objective marker, two decal sheets, five unit cards and one printed cardboard Artillery & Salvo template.

Now at the pinnacle of your career, you are sought after by two capital cities to build their city centers. Not only do you have to consider the natural features that were the reason for their founding, you also need to ensure the civic buildings are appropriately placed, and you must not forget to satisfy the citizens' demands along the way.

Between Two Cities: Capitals expands the base game by adding several new challenges: landscapes, districts, and civic building tiles. This is not a standalone expansion; you need the original Between Two Cities game to play. In more detail:

• Landscape mats give each city a unique layout to build on and around.
• Districts give players a majority bonus for connecting specific types of tiles.
• Civic building tiles are a new type of building tile; each unique civic building wants to be adjacent to two specific tile types (and not one specific tile type).
