boardgame: The Tears of Sango (fan expansion for T.I.M.E Stories)
description:
Tears of Sango is a science fiction story set 100 years in our future using the T.I.M.E. Stories base game system.  A global catastrophe is about to befall the Earth. Can TIME agents from even farther in the future save humanity (and themselves) from annihilation?

The scenario is played in two phases (two decks). Players must complete Phase 1 before continuing with Phase 2. Rules are provided to utilize two, three, or four players (receptacles), with eight receptacle cards to choose from. The Mission to save Earth is in your hands.

This is a downloadable print-and-play scenario for T.I.M.E. Stories (English language). The file is formatted for double-sided printing with 180 playable cards.

Revenge of the Blank is a light party game, where players write witty responses to prompts like:
An odd item that Batman keeps on his utility belt is ______.
A talking doll should NOT say “______” when you pull its string.
A fortune teller once told me I would live a life full of ______.

How To Play
1)Players are dealt a certain amount of Prompt cards depending on total number of players and desired game length.

2)One player "The Judge" reads a prompt off one of the cards they were dealt at the start of the game.

3)Everyone else then writes a response to the prompt.

4)Once all answers are submitted The Judge then selects their favorite and awards the player who wrote it the card with the prompt.

5)The player to the left of The Judge becomes The Judge and play continues like this until all the cards dealt out at the start of the game have been awarded to other players.

6)The player with the most cards wins.

Runicah is a Norse themed, competitive card game that uses set collection, resource management, blind trade, and self serving cooperation mechanics to bring players on a journey of back-stabbing, back-tracking and back-scratching.

Players will compete to be the first to complete their own unique set of runes. Players will almost certainly need to cooperate on some level if they wish to win, the key is how and when to help each other out.

The game consists of a single repeated round sequence until a winner is determined. Each player starts with a Character card; which gives them a unique player ability and a starting Rune drawn, all of which are randomly dealt. Players then all draw loot cards which make up the resource they use to complete trials and hopefully obtain more runes.

A round consists of players completing personal trials, trading of runes, and a group event. A player wins when they have completed a sets, either the same colour with one of each symbol, or the same symbol with one of each colour. The main focus comes from player interactions during the trials and trading parts to the round, with players deciding whether cooperation is personally beneficial enough to risk helping an opponent.

A game can last anywhere from 20 to 40 mins depending on group size, and is balanced in such a way as to count noone out until the very end.

boardgame: Star Trek: Attack Wing – Co-Pilot Resource
description:
The Co-Pilot Resource is a participation prize given to players who participated in the "Drive" Organized Play Event.

It costs 5 points and allows players to either
1) add +3 to their captains skill until the end of the round
2) reroll an attack or defense die
3) place an evade or battlestations token beside the ship
once a round. It can be used 5 times in total.

Mechanics: States of Siege style game
Player #: Solitaire
Game Time:  25-35+ min
Age:  12+

Synopsys: 
You are Robert Neville, a scientist who was unable to stop the spread of the terrible virus that was incurable and man-made. Immune, Neville is now the last human survivor in what is left of New York City and perhaps the world. Mutant victims of the plague -- The Infected -- lurk in the shadows... watching Neville's every move... waiting for him to make a fatal mistake.  For years Neville has search for a cure testing the blood of the infected and he is close to discovering that cure.  He only needs ten more samples of blood to create his antidote.  Perhaps mankind's last, best hope, Neville is driven by only one remaining mission: to find a way to reverse the effects of the virus using his own immune blood with the blood of the infected. He knows he is outnumbered... and quickly running out of time.  They are building in numbers and preparing for a major attack!  Can you survive long enough to find the cure?

I AM LEGEND: is a solitaire "States of Siege" game where you control Robert Neville who is trying to find a cure for the plague which has turned the living into vampires.  Each day you travel into the city looking for supplies, medicine, ammo or a blood sample from the infected.  Don't stay to late because once the day turns to night and you haven't made it back home, the infected prepare for their attack.  Each night the infected gather their forces and move closer to your home base; testing your barricades and looking for weaknesses.

Goal:
Search the city for [9] samples of blood to create the cure and reverse the plague before the vampires gather enough forces to penetrate your barricades and destroy you.  Also search for supplies to replenish ammo and materials to build up your barricades to fend off the vampire forces.

Victory conditions:  
Accomplish the above requirements before they vampires kill you.

Losing conditions:   
If the vampires gather enough forces to breach your barricades and kill you before you can figure out the cure; you lose!

COMPONENT LIST:
9-Cards, 9 d6 dice and 9 cubes make up the entire game component list.

CARDS
1 – Home Base card
4 - Vampire path cards – “with numbers descending from 5 or 6 to 2” double sided
4 - City Search cards - double sided

CUBES
2 – Green cubes –     “Blood sample”
2 – White cubes –     "Free choice" 
1 – Green cube –     “Virus Cure test track”
1 – Red cube –         “Vampire movement check”
1 – Black cube –     “Vampire movement check”
1 – Yellow cube –     “Vampire movement check”
1 – Blue cube –         “Vampire movement check”

DICE
4 – White d6 die -    "Supplies, Health, Ammo, and Barricades"
1 – Purple d6 die -     "Robert Neville "
1 – Red d6 die -         "Vampire movement tracker"
1 – Black d6 die -     "Vampire movement tracker"
1 – Yellow d6 die -     "Vampire movement tracker"
1 – Blue d6 die -        "Vampire movement tracker"

Synopsis:
DICE WARS is a fast paced, dice rolling game where two players try to gather the most cards through rolling dice and placing them on the card to control the cards. Players can roll as fast as possible to create roll combinations and control cards faster then their opponents. Once a card is controlled it can always be taken away from the opponent. Once a player controls four cards the game is over.

GAME PLAY:
7 cards are laid out in a row. each player takes their dice pool card and places it in front of them. Each player gets four d6 dice "white for player 1 and black for player 2". There is an extra die color red that can be gathered as cards are controlled and used to help create dice combination to control cards. This die will change ownership often. The players will roll their dice and place them on the cards they want to control. Once you roll the required dice combo you can control the card and turn it 90 degrees. Each card owned is worth victory points [1,1,3,3,5,5,10] at the end when four cards are controlled you add up your victory points and the high score wins. Tactics come in when you have to decide which cards you can control and earn enough points to win. You only need 1 point greater than your opponent to win and once you get your fourth card you need to make sure your points are higher than your opponent. Control changes a lot in this game and since each player is rolling their dice fast you have to keep an eye on which cards your opponent is placing dice on to try and control them. When a card is controlled a player can roll a small dice combination to turn the card back 90 degrees and remove your control but they don't control the card either until they roll the required combination.

In this board game, you play as a Syrian, Iraqi or Afghan migrant traveling towards Europe.

Finding yourself in Istanbul as an undocumented traveler you need to navigate a world of smugglers, police and unscrupulous employers. Piece together the fragments of information you get to plan your own Journey!

The objective of the game is to leave Istanbul. The players take turn to move around different neighborhoods of Istanbul. They have to be at the right place at the right times to earn money, avoid police raids and finally find a smuggler to take them out of the city. The events that takes place each round is determined by event cards which the players may gain information about as the game progress. Special circumstance cards provides each player with a personal quest that need to be solved before leaving Istanbul.

Access Card is a promo card for MoonQuake Escape.  It is an Equipment card and with it a player may "aim and use [a] Tractor Beam or Defense Gun, or Launch Rocket".

It was released as part of a three card promotion pack called the All Access Promo Pack, which included the Mug Shot card, Access Card card, and a bonus rules card.  All three cards were packaged in a single card sleeve.

13 cards can be arranged in over 6 Billion possible combinations ... 6,227,020,800 to be exact.

A version of this game is said to have been found at a UFO site in Texas back in the 1980s. With its mysterious origin, we are still unclear as to the strange effects of achieving the "13" result. Some say it sharpens intuition, improves focus, and intelligence.

But who knows....

"13" is primarily a solo card game, but more can play as well, using just 13 cards. It can be played by yourself just trying to get the highest score, break the code per say, or you could play it with two or more people as a betting game to see who can come up with the highest score over a three round match.

RULES:

The cards are mixed and dealt out randomly and face up in this configuration:

X X X X X 
    X X X
X X X X X 

Cards will have either an arrow/arrows showing in which direction they may affect another card. These arrows only affect cards immediately next to them, whether horizontally, vertically, or diagonally. The arrow on the activated card must be pointing to a card immediately adjacent to it, otherwise there is no effect. Some cards will have simple written instructions. When a card refers to “first position” or “last position” they are referring to the original dealt out top left position “1” or the original dealt out bottom right position “13” exactly. If the cards in those specific positions have been removed (the space is empty) then nothing happens.

So once the cards are shuffled and dealt out, you will “activate” one card and remove both the card activated and the card it affects from the tableau.

The objective is to remain with one card in the end, otherwise it’s an automatic loss. Remaining with one card in the end is usually possible, so avoiding mistakes is crucial. You’re shooting to remain with the highest value card in the end, preferably the “13”.

description: Rocket is casino style card game created by the makers of the game Pairs.  The game can be played with any Pairs playing card deck, but was specially designed and introduced with the Bar Maids deck.

Realistic football (soccer) simulation using magnetic board and pawns.
3 difficulty levels give a gameplay from a simple family game to real pedagogic and professional simulation.

With the first level of difficulty, each player gets a maximum of 5 actions per turn and must choose between attack, defense, shooting attempt, shooting, shooting defense and goalkeeper.

A special dice thrown give the result of each action and let the players know if it result with a goal or not after the succeded actions were simulated moving the pawns and the ball directely on the board.

The second and third levels of difficulty add more specifics and complex rules to the first ones by introducing real professional and international rules as corner, free-kick, offside, penalty and throw in.

Each player get 1 minute for its turn and the game can be set for 1 ou 2 45 minutes half times.

As a real soccer match, the best goal scored player is the winner.
Bonus card added to second issue of Kurier Planszowy.

Place your Posse Cards in front of your draw pile with the cowboy facing the opposite player.
Pull 3 randomly drawn cards from you number cards. Next roll the die to match the cards. If the card and die match, move that number card up to the hit area, reminding you that you have secured one shot. If you match 2 cards with one roll, you receive the Jam Card. (For one turn you only have 2 cards to roll to match.)

If at any time one player is ever 2 cards ahead, the opposing player "feels desperate" and takes the Quick Draw Card and goes twice for one turn. If no cards are matched, then replace any card of your choice with a randomly drawn card from the draw pile. When ever 4 cards have hit, then one Posse Card is turned so that the Grave is facing the opposite player.

In Dreamcatcher it is up to you to compete with your fellow gods of the Dream World to protect the minds of mortal man. Through luck, skill and the help of your dream power will you be able to acquire nightmares otherwise bound for the human realm.

Every round new nightmares are made available to bet on using your finite amount of dream power cards combined with action cards. Based on player order, rarity of nightmares currently available and who is running ahead multiple strategies, and a bit of luck, are needed to come out victorious.

Dreamcatcher is a fast paced bluffing deduction card game for 2-6 players.

description:
In Korocchi!, you try to find the correct card that is determined by two (or three) unique dice, and whoever touches the correct card first score points. Each of the two dice in the basic game has two pieces of information:

• Color die: Shows you the outside color and inside color.
• Shape die: Shows you the outside shape and the inside shape.

Three different creatures (cat, bat and obake) are depicted on the cards, with these creatures appearing in three colors. Each card depicts one large creature in one color holding a tiny creature in another color. By pairing the two dice, you know precisely which one card to touch from all those face up on the table.

For an additional challenge, you can roll the third die as well. The faces on this die might just show that you play as normal scoring one or two points, or it might show the shapes or colors being reversed — which means you need to look for the opposite card (sort of) instead.

boardgame: Wolf's Lair (fan expansion for T.I.M.E Stories)
description: A sequel to the Broken Peace fan expansion. Unlock the mysteries of a secret German base at the end of WWII.

Players travel around a world they created one card at a time. Players grab ants and race home before other players can garb the.m You can attack other aardvarks, build a home, and raid their other aardvark homes to steal ants. Be the first player to get three ants safely home and keep them there to win.

The cards in the game are used for all elements of play, building the map, moving the players, constructing houses, and attack others.

The raid itself had been swift and entirely unexpected. Nobody had seen much from the local Orcs for many years, and to suddenly find them so brazen, so strong. For years the hills had been a haven for a small number of Orcs; everyone knew that. Even so, there had never been more than a handful of them and they lacked any kind of organisation. How did they mount such a carefully planned attack and why?
This exciting expansion for Dungeon Saga: The Dwarf King's Quest includes a huge quantity of extra tiles to expand your game or create your own adventure and a brand new six-mission narrative campaign for the four original heroes from Dungeon Saga.

Vers is a freestyle rap based card game where the players compete against each other to see which can create the best freestyle rap, but it uses a template system that makes it accessible to anyone. The game's mission is to make freestyle rap accessible to anyone. From 7 year old girls to 71 year old grandparents, anyone can freestyle in this party game.

A player might get a category like "Taco" And then a base card like "Hate, Great, Appreciate"
And then one might rhyme: "There's a taco in my mouth and it's filled with hate, lettuce and cheese and it's looking great, but the beef is sour I cannot appreciate."

Each player would rap using a different category card and the player with the best rap would win, as chosen by the player judge. Then a new judge is chosen and a new battle commences.  The player winning the most battles at the end of the game, wins.

boardgame: Clockwork Memolion (クロックワークメモリオン)
description:
User summary
クロックワークメモリオン (Kurokkuwa-ku Memorion) Clockwork Memolion is a two player fantasy cardgame. You battle against your opponent by customizing your memory-lost characters by injecting various memories (words). Hence, the same character may be altered to execute different effects and abilities. First one to put your opponents' life to 0 wins the battle.

It's a Print and Play cardgame, supported by a custom website. A player can create a free account, and keep cards they gain on their personal page. Every 8 hours, each player receives a free Gacha giving a random card. But players can also buy a Premium Gacha giving five extra cards, including more rarites. Since the game is entirely PnP, buying cards is the only cost (an income model akin to mobile games) other than printing.

Gameplay is in quick 1 vs 1 deck-driven battles but the rules enable partnership games. Players tune up their decks with new cards they have collected and printed.

https://www.cwm-memories.com/

A Note on the Name: Japanese tend to use the L as an approximation to the R which they don't have. This is intentional, not a mistake. So this game would be spoken in Japanese more as Memorion than Memolion, which comes from the game's theme of lost memories.

Publisher's summary
記憶を失ったキャラクターに様々な記憶(ワード)を入れ能力をカスタマイズしながら戦うファンタジーカードゲームです。
同じカードでも違った効果、能力にすることができます。
先に相手のライフを0にしたプレイヤーの勝利です。

プリントして遊べるカードゲームです。
https://www.cwm-memories.com/
上のサイトに無料会員登録すれば8時間に1回無料でパックを引くことができ、それをマイページに保管することができます。
そこにアクセスすれば好きなカードが印刷できます。

In the year 1240 Princess Ikio proclaimed all men must find her the Super Unicorn. In order to lure the Super Unicorn, players will roll dice and vie for the special stars needed to complete their search.

In clockwise order, players take turns rolling all dice available, and must lock in dice on up to three different different stacks of cards available or on cards they have earned during the game. They may repeat this process until they want to pass. If they fail to roll a die or dice that can be used, their turn ends immediately. When the stack of cards on the highest rated card is is depleted, the player with the most stars on their face up cards wins the game.

boardgame: The Princess and the Dragon Game
description: The Princess and the Dragon Game is an educational game for children teaching reading and matching skills.  There are six different games, each of which involves collecting cards to fill the spaces on a board.  They involve combinations of matching pictures to words or words to pictures.  Some allow players to pick any card, others require them to pick off the top of a stack.  One game involves miming words, and another requires players to come up with synonyms.

Help Dory find her family and friends!  Dory and her friends are playing Hide and Seek.  Where are they hiding?  Be the first to find three, and you win.

The game is played on a large board printed 19" x 28" board printed with a pattern of dots designed to obscure pictures of the movie characters.  Each player has a diving-mask-shaped viewer designed to reveal the character's picture.  Players take turns turning over a card depicting a movie character.  Players then race to use their viewers to find that character on the board.  The player who finds the character claims the card.  First player to three cards wins.
Monroviaopoly is a city-themed Monopoly clone with a Monrovia, California theme.  Spaces on the board represent local businesses and landmarks.

Flying Tigers is game #4 in the Battle over Britain game series published by Minden Games.

Flying Tigers is an expansion module for the Battle over Britain game system.  It may be used with the standard rules contained in any game of the series.  It contains solitaire rules, optional rules, several new aircraft and scenarios.  You must own the core rues for Battle over Britain to use this module.  The rules and planes contained here may be used with any of the other titles in the game series.

Flying Tigers incorporates material from the out-of-print Battle over Britain: Solitaire Module and adds new content, particularly the planes and campaign involving the 1st American Volunteer Group fighting with the Chinese Air Force in 1941-42 against the Japanese.  The module also contains campaign game scenarios and planes for the Polish (1939) and Dutch (1940) campaigns.

To reach the peak of the fiery volcano, just identify the correct action words--but watch out for treacherous rock slides!

Verb Volcano is an educational game designed to teach verb forms and tenses.  Each player chooses a card with a sentence and several possible verbs that could complete it.  If they answer correctly, they spin a spinner to move up a track toward the top of the volcano.  Spaces along the track may move players forward or back or grant an extra spin.  The first player to reach the top of the volcano wins.

boardgame: The Order of Vampire Hunters: Nyx Expansion
description:
An optional Kickstarter exclusive crossover character from the Middara board game which comes with the following that can be used with The Order of Vampire Hunters game:

1 x Elder ID card
1 x Hunter ID card and weapons cards
Downloadable Hunt

Visions is a competitive game where the players collect cards that show clocks. They play the clocks to connect other cards via the hands of the clocks with their mental center. Those other cards represent different topics which belong to family Yates: ancestors, places where they lived, rooms where they used to be and their collection of jewels. While the players are visionary talented people, they try to help family Yates finding out what curse lies on them.

By connecting the family cards at the right time (represented by the clock cards) with their mental center the players gain victory points, visionary power and vision parts in different colors. Each player has got a vision card with a 3x3 pattern where they place the vision parts according to the rules. The players get victory points for full rows and columns.

The players may use the visionary power to change it into benefits, for example reducing the cost to connect family cards with the mental center or getting additional vision parts.
At a given time the player with the fewest victory points chooses a sort of visionary power and all benefits which are connected to this power will be more expensive for the rest of the game and all players have to deal with this limitation.

One family card lies at a special position and can't be connected directly with the mental center but it is possible to connect the card through the other family cards with the center to get the benefits and the chance to force other players to swap vision parts and/or visionary power.

The players also have to try to solve a master vision with the vision parts to get extra points and offer the best help for family Yates. Only the one with the most victory points is able to give them the chance to break the curse.

boardgame: Folklore: The Affliction – Dark Tales Expansion
description:
The Dark Tales expansion contains nine more stories, featuring new systems for dungeon exploration and open-world adventure.  Play as one of six new archetypes, or continue your campaign with your existing characters from the Folklore: The Affliction core game.  Be prepared: your quest in The Dark Tales expansion will take you to the heart of evil plaguing Kremel.  You'll need to gather your companions, train new abilities, and uncover powerful artifacts to strike back against the shadows seeping throughout the realm.

Features:

     Extend your campaign with nine new full-length stories - over 18 hours of replayable content
     Go looking for trouble with Rumor Cards: 22 free-play adventures throughout the land
     Explore the dark with new Dungeon Crawl Exploration scenarios
     Six new characters to choose from: the Butcher, Courtesan, Illusionist, Scientist, Slayer, and Woodsman
     New abilities, artifacts, afflictions, creatures, companions, maps, monsters, and items!

Lotus Dimension is a tabletop role-playing game (RPG).

Players have to solve all the challenges the 
game thrown at them using nonviolent means?
What if a game drew on amazing sci-fi and fantasy storytelling while also incorporating principles of nonviolence inspired by peaceful protests, historical leaders and the tenets of peaceful philosophical practices?

And what if it offered players a way to visualize and role-play solutions to wildly imagined problems, responding to and replacing violence and weaponry with empathy and ingenuity?

Be the first to complete your paper route by answering questions about facts and opinions!

Extra! Extra! is an educational children's game, designed to teach players to distinguish between fact and opinion.  Players start the game with five newspaper tokens each.  On a turn, they choose a card, read the passage on the card and then attempt to answer the associated question.  Correct answers allow the player to roll a die and move.  Wrong answers cause loss of a turn.  Players attempt to land on purple spaces, which are attached to houses and allow them to "deliver" one of their newspapers.  However, they may not deliver to a house that already has a paper.  A few spaces contain special instructions, such as extra turns or moves or loss of turns or taking a paper back.  The first player to deliver all his/her papers wins.

2-6 player, Ortus Regni variant.

The largest and most powerful shire in Mercia is before you. The Burhs (towns) of the shire are unique, and each can provide certain important services to its overlord. One Burgh is known for its great warriors, another for its persuasive lords and allies, another for its loyal and capable vassals. Take what Burhs you can, but choose wisely, with an eye on your own strengths and weaknesses.

2-6 player, Ortus Regni variantmat

The Danes left their Long Boats behind and settled in the North East of your nation. They crushed the Anglo-Saxon kingdom of Northumbria along the way, and now rule a kingdom of their own up north known as the Danelaw! See that they do not take over the whole of England, by keeping them in their place.

2-4 player, Ortus Regni variantmap

Before London was the center of England, before Canterbury was the seat of the English Church… Winchester was the capital of the Anglo-Saxon Kingdom of Wessex and its cathedral was the resting place for many of the first English kings. This is not an era of great urban centers, indeed, but Winchester rises above the other Burhs and fortified towns of the shires. It is the center of both political games and literacy, and hosts much of the pageantry of the kingdom.

The Fan Expansion Pack DOUBLES the number of characters in your deck by adding 15 new characters inspired by our Kickstarter fans! Each fan character is competitively balanced and ready to integrate into your Galatune Starter Deck, putting new gameplay options and battle strategies at your finger tips.

boardgame: Arkham Horror: The Card Game – The Path to Carcosa: Expansion
description:
"I cannot forget Carcosa where black stars hang in the heavens; where the shadow of men’s thoughts lengthen in the afternoon, when the twin suns sink into the lake of Hali; and my mind will bear for ever the memory of the Pallid Mask." –Robert W. Chambers, The King in Yellow

A new theater production is coming to Arkham all the way from Paris. Unsurprisingly, it's been all the rage in conversations among Arkham's socialites. But in your efforts to learn more about the play, you've uncovered a darker history. Disappearances. Suicides. Delusions. Insanity. Wherever The King in Yellow is performed, madness follows close behind…

In The Path to Carcosa, the second deluxe expansion for Arkham Horror: The Card Game, it becomes your task to uncover the secrets of this unusual play. The expansion's two scenarios kick off The Path to Carcosa campaign with a trip to the Ward Theatre and an invitation to the formal dinner party hosted by cast and crew. But with every answer you gain and every clue you uncover, you only find yourself falling deeper into mystery and madness.

Along with its new scenarios and encounter cards, The Path to Carcosa introduces six new investigators and sixty-two player cards (including a complete playset of each) that afford you new ways to explore and enjoy the game—even as they challenge you to overcome new weaknesses.

Empires is a drafting game, where each card has four quadrants.  When a player plays a card, he or she plays it in one of these four quadrants, and it has a different effect depending on which quadrant it is placed in.

There is a buying phase after the drafting phase each round, where players will purchase upgrades.

Game Bio:
Become the leader of a local church, and grow it into the largest, most influential church in the country. Bring in money, sell books, and build additions on to your building, all to attract more and more followers.
The player with the most followers and the largest empire at the end of the game is the winner!

boardgame: Mikan de Kyatto (みかんdeキャッと)
description:
User summary
みかんdeキャッと (Mikan de Kyatto) is MIKAN de CAT, a simple stacking game.

In Japan, the combination of oranges (Mikan) and cats is commonplace, and naturally understood. Kyatto is more of a pun on the sound 'kya' which is an exclamation, intended to sound like 'cat'. The game comes from the practise of always always keeping some oranges in Winter on your Kotatsu (a low table, heated underneath).

In this game, players use a cat spinner or cards, and stack oranges in the glass dish, placed on a table. The designer includes five different ways to play. The cat spinner shows a tomcat.

Publisher's summary
テーブルの上に置かれたガラスの器の中に、みかんを積んでいくバランスゲームです。
遊び方は5通りあり、カードを使ったり、ルーレットを使ったりします。

This is a small compilation of FlipFlop 3×3, FlipFlop 5×5, and FlipFour, all playable with the same board and components. The games of the compilation utilize the components as follows:

     FlipFlop 5×5 uses the entire 5×5 board, and each player uses 5 pieces.
     FlipFlop 3×3 uses just the inner 3×3 portion of the board, and each player uses 3 pieces.
     FlipFour uses the the entire 5×5 board, and each player uses 4 pieces.

boardgame: A Game of Thrones: The Card Game (Second Edition) – The Red Wedding
description:
Description from the publisher:

With The Red Wedding Chapter Pack for A Game of Thrones: The Card Game, you'll have the chance to continue the saga of A Storm of Swords with one of the most gut-wrenching and iconic moments of the entire series. Even as you walk among the fallen and the deadly power of House Frey continues to grow, you'll find new ways to spend and earn your gold, like the other expansions of the "Blood and Gold" cycle. With iconic characters like Ellaria Sand, Strong Belwas, Othell Yarwyck, and Walder Frey entering the game for the first time, The Red Wedding brings new horrors and schemes into every game.

boardgame: Wolfe Tone Rebellion: Irish Rebellion of 1798
description:
In 1796 the charismatic Irish rebel, Wolfe Tone, convinced the Revolutionary government of France and one of its finest generals, Lazare Hoche, that Ireland was poised for rebellion. Ireland would rise, he insisted, if France would only lend its support. That year, a force of 14,000 Frenchmen on 43 ships evaded the British fleet and sailed into Bantry Bay, Cork County, Ireland where rough weather forced them to abandon their operation and return to France.

Two years and three audiences with Napoleon later, Tone tried again. Three waves of French landings were planned to coordinate with a general rising of the United Irishmen (Protestant and Catholic alike) all across the island. Unfortunately, word leaked and key leaders were arrested in Dublin, triggering an early rising, quickly and brutally put down by a combination of professional troops, recently raised fencibles, local militia and orange order yeomen. The rebellion staggered on through September but only in County Wexford and later in County Mayo with minimal French support, did the rebellion meet with any success.

As for the French, the first wave of 1100 men under the able command of Jean Humbert, stormed ashore in County Mayo in August, 1798. A smaller force landed in Donegal in September under the command of Napper Tandy. Humbert enjoyed a few successes but soon surrendered to the overwhelming force Lord Cornwallis brought to bear. Soon after, Tandy abandoned the Emerald Isle when he realized he could no longer be of service to Humbert. A month later and further North off the coast of Donegal, a second wave of 3000 men under Jean Hardy and Wolfe Tone himself met with disaster.  Hardy’s fleet ran afoul of the British fleet and Tone was captured. Imprisoned and condemned to hang, Tone committed suicide. The third and largest wave of French soldiers never sailed.

Without determined French support the Irish rebellion of 1798 stood little chance of success. Historically it was a bloody great uncoordinated fiasco made worse by the early demise of Tone’s benefactor, General Hoche to consumption. History might have been different if the stars had aligned, if those key Irish leaders had not been captured and their plans revealed, if the rebels had received a sizeable number of French and British arms, if Hoche had survived. If a second and even a third wave of French regulars had landed, and if those French regulars had landed while the rebellion was still vibrant, then – then, as WOLFE TONE’S RISING reveals, England would have had a serious problem.

WOLFE TONE’S RISING puts the aforementioned what-ifs into play. A short game of seven turns pits English regulars and loyalist militia against the Irish rebels and their French allies. Here, the French play a shell game with the British attempting to land troops in Ireland not once but three times. Here, unknown morale plays a vital role to the outcome of every battle. Here, Local leaders are not cookie cut-outs but individuals who enjoy unique abilities in battle. And here, each battle is assisted by a battle board utilizing fresh mechanics that make each battle a mini-game unto itself.  A note on the leaders used: British and French leaders are key historical leaders, whereas Irish rebel leaders are largely representational.  Anonymous loyalist leaders represent a mixture of militia and yeomen. Loyalist hierarchy was either nonexistent or too confusing to identify specific names.

description:
Building a skyscraper is tough, but working together might be even tougher. Skyscrappers pits players against each other, only to force them to cooperate a few floors higher. Elevate your status when others fail to be the best person at NOT knocking down a tower.

A winner of our Nano Contest, this had us from the pitch, but the game ended up a blast.

In this game for 3-8 players, players will be picking cards simultaneously and stacking them on top of a tower, hopefully keeping it standing. Players could be placing cards on their own, or they could be working with the other players to stack a single card on the tower.

The player who is the best at keeping the tower standing wins!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Runewars Miniatures Game: Latari Elves – Army Expansion
description:
Description from the publisher:

Expand your Runewars Miniatures Game experience with the Latari Elves Army Expansion. This expansion not only introduces four new units and eight new figures to the Runewars Miniatures Game, but a brand-new faction in the form of the Latari Elves. Nimble and ferocious, the Latari Elves change the rules of war in the land of Terrinoth!

In Terminus Breach TD, a semi-cooperative tower defense board game for 1-4 players, you play as one of 12 Generals defending against 15 waves of enemy Drakka. Set up a map on the fully modular board, build your starting towers, and prepare for battle!

Each wave is a turn, and consists of five phases:

Phase 1 – ADVANCE the Drakka on the board, further along the path.
Phase 2 – DRAW new Drakka to the path as they enter the battlefield.
Phase 3 – DEFEND by using your towers to damage, poison, or slow Drakka.
Phase 4 – RESOLVE by collecting Bounty, petitioning the Emperor for aid, or receiving Imperial Favors and Sanctions.
Phase 5 – DEVELOP your defenses by building new towers and upgrading your existing towers.

Every 3 waves is a Battle, and at the end of each is a powerful Boss with unique abilities.

Use the Lull between Battles to collect Victory Points, receive benefits from War Chests and Spy Networks, and place the next Battle deck - each with five new, faster, stronger Drakka.

If enough Drakka get through your defenses to cause a widespread panic, the Emperor will recall the armies and the game ends.

Survive all 15 Waves and clear the board without that happening, and you've successfully defended your homeland!

Petrichor: Flowers is an expansion that emerged naturally during playtest and development that includes three new field types, the titular flowers, and enough components to play the game with five players.

Contains objective sets 265-269.

boardgame: Runewars Miniatures Game: Oathsworn Cavalry – Unit Expansion
description:
Description from the publisher:

Few sounds strike more fear into an enemy of Terrinoth than the thundering hooves of a column of armored destriers. Each mount is bred and trained for war. Each rider is a lifelong professional warrior or an avowed knight, sworn to a baron’s or Free City’s service until the rider’s final breath. Rider and mount work together on the battlefield in perfect synergy, charging into combat and crushing the enemy in a wave of steel.

With the Oathsworn Cavalry Unit Expansion, you gain four new Oathsworn Cavalry figures—enough to field a two-tray unit or increase the size of another unit. These fearsome knights are sworn to fight for the Daqan Lords and protect the realm, and they are first introduced in the Runewars Core Set. This unit expansion is a perfect way to quickly increase your cavalry and unlock new options with five new upgrade cards for any army.

The game re-themes Funny Bunny with groundhoges, who have to hide from an eagle. Groundhog-pawns are moved heading to the finish and number of spaces are determined by a deck of cards, just like in the original. What distinguishes this game is, that multiple pawns have to be brought to the end spaces, and that a speed element is added to the game. Whenever an eagle card is revealed, a button is pressed, which randomly might activate the eagle. Players are supposed to only hide their groundhogs, if the eagle takes off from behind a bush.

縁ノ日 Enishi no hi is a Japanese card game set during a Matsuri.

Players manage street stalls at the local shrine festival. Every turn you can acquire new stall cards and trade to collect stall cards of the same genre or numeric value. Business begins on the day of the festival and the sets of stalls you own will decide how much you earn in profits. When the final settling of accounts is over, the player in possession of the most money wins.
    
Publisher's summary
プレイヤーは縁日に出店する露店を取り仕切っています。毎ターン1枚ずつ露店カードが獲得できますので、それをジャンルか数値で同じものを揃えるようにトレードしましょう。縁日が開催されると決算が発生しますので、所有するセットを参照して収入を獲得してください。最後の決算が終了した時点で、一番所有金額の多いプレイヤーが勝利です。

boardgame: Last Night on Earth: Hero Pack Two

description:
Panzer Attack: War in Russia is a fast-paced, easy to learn, deck building, card-wargame that captures the epic scope of World War 2 combat on the Eastern Front.  The core game abstractly simulates the war between Germany and the USSR between 1941 and 1945, and the territory cards included allow players to fight along Army Group Center's path between Berlin and Moscow. Additional add-on modules add more cards for greater customization possibilities, and open up new campaign options.

The game uses deckbuilding aspects that allow players to customize their deck to fit their preferred play style, and features play that only requires the cards themselves in order to play a game.

The objective of the game is to either destroy all of your opponent's forces in play, or capture all territories on the board. This is done using troop and unit cards that represent the troops, equipment and historical units that fought in the war, in addition to action and leader cards that add variability and an extra layer of strategy to the game.

description:
In this deliciously gooey card game, you’ll build (or rebuild as needed) your body from a severed head, equip yourself with a motley collection of weapons and armor, and eventually behead the Chief of the Zombie Nest to take over leadership and win the game.

As a zombie, losing your head doesn’t kill you, but it sure can put a damper on your day. It’ll take time to forage and scavenge enough material to build a new body, but you’re determined to do it. Maybe you’ll get lucky and find some useful tools, maybe even a weapon or two.

It’s not going to be easy, because the other Players are all trying to do the same thing. Will you be lucky enough to find the chainsaw and the shotgun? Will some other zombie trick you at the last second into walking into helicopter blades? There’s only one way to find out!

Regardless, you’re not the kind of zombie that’s going to let a little beheading get between you and your ultimate goal: to rule the Zombie Tribe!
This expansion to the DC Universe Miniature game introduces Darkseid-the big bad of the DC Universe. This white metal kit comes ready to assemble and paint.

Description from the publisher:

Warforged: First Contact is a skirmish-level exploration game where the Iron Legionaries of the Terran Authority investigate a dimension-spanning shrine-portal of the alien Mantra. The Iron Legion will need to use tight coordination and clever tactics to overcome the limitless hordes of the Mantra and battle their way to the heart of the shrine-portal.


     2+ Players using Iron Legion versus the Mantra (additional players take command of separate Iron Legion squads; the Mantra player acts as "dungeon master").

     Unique and accessible command card activation system.

boardgame: Marvel Dice Masters: The Mighty Thor
description:
Marvel Dice Masters: The Mighty Thor is an expansion for any Dice Masters starter set. The new expansion will feature the heroic Asgardian himself, as well as a variety of heroes and villains that have crossed his path over the years, including Spider-Man, The Hulk, and Iron Man, with a total of more than 30 characters for use with the game.

Marvel Dice Master: The Mighty Thor' will be available in two formats:

Releasing in October, The Mighty Thor Countertop Display comes with eight Draft Packs, designed for use in draft tournaments but suitable for any Marvel Dice Masters game.  Each Draft Pack includes 2 Basic Action cards, 12 Character or Action cards, and 24 matching dice.

Then, in November, WizKids will release The Mighty Thor Gravity Feed display, which contains 90 randomly-filled foil packs.  Each foil pack comes with 2 cards and 2 matching dice.

You are an alchemist for "The Kingdom". In order to keep your job and become the Royal Alchemist you must make the most "Coin" for the Treasury. Starting with only Air, Earth, Fire, and Water as ingredients, play Formula cards from your hand to make valuable Materials. These Materials are worth Coin towards winning, but can also be used as ingredients for Formulas that steal, transform, destroy or regenerate cards.
      Alchenemy combines elements of resource management (cards that count towards winning can be discarded to perform actions), “take that!” (inevitably, someone breaks out the Aqua Regia and dissolves something you were really attached to), and a novel mechanic that gives players some control over when the game ends: you choose whether to draw from a common, limitless deck of basic resources or a limited, personal deck of more powerful cards – with the catch that the game ends when any personal stack is exhausted. Two, three, or four people may play and it should take first time players about an hour to finish.

A spelling card game that helps to improve your spelling, expand your vocabulary & have hours of fun competing with all your friends and families.

Somewhat in the style of a card based scrabble, each player takes turns taking 3 actions where they can either play a word, draw a card from the deck or a "spell field", or steal a card from another player's word.

The game ends when a player makes 5 words to which the final round is played. The winner is determined by the score from the words created. Words that uses 5 cards or more will score higher.

Players can also use various power cards which does various effects from drawing extra cards to stealing more than one cards  from another player

Economy Class is a stock-trading themed card game. The game is played over two phases. In the first phase players auction cards to build a hand. In the second they play half of their hand (through simultaneous card play) to manipulate the stock market before selling off the second half of the cards in their hand at the end of the game. The player with the most money at the end wins.

For the "Stadt, Land, Spielt"-Event in Germany and Austria designed mini-expansion for First Class.

You can Upgrade your trains with Coffee, Cake and Champagne and earn more scoring points in each phase.

Bottle Flip is a dexterity game where players attempt to flip a bottle onto a target according to different challenge cards. Players move along a scoring track after successfully completing flip challenges.

Types of Bottle Flip challenges include:
Individual Challenges
Face-To-Face “Flip Offs”
Group “All Out Flip Outs”
Create Your Own Challenges

Challenge examples:
-Flip and land a bottle using only your elbows
-1 attempt to land 6 bottle flips before you blink

The game is the various phases of a game of sport fishing. Advances on the path to the fisherman, choose the most favorable fishing spot, mount the barrel, choose the bait, the float, the toggle and line. After that it faces a real fishing action: if a fish bites can catch it only if you have just installed your fishing rod. In the end who will win to his credit as many kilograms of fish.
The first expansion to NecronomiCards. Match arcane symbols to summon elder gods, demons, & mythological monsters. Vol 2 adds new strategic elements & up to 8 player games.

INTERNATIONAL CAMPAIGN CURRENTLY ON KICKSTARTER!>>  https://www.kickstarter.com/projects/1460598061/warlords-of-terra-the-battlefield-faction-card-gam/description

Warlords of Terra  has been classified as BFCG or Battlefield Faction Card Game, due to its main difference in game mechanics: there exists a REAL BATTLEFIELD in which our troops will have to move and cross, for us to reach our opponent's lines and defeat them. Moreover "Faction" means that the product format is sold according to the different kingdoms or factions. New expansion sets come in new boxes containing FULL PLAYSET (not only 1-2 copies) of new cards for its corresponding faction or an alliance of 2 kingdoms. So, it's not necessary acquiring the full expansion if only an specific kingdom is wanted.

If you like classic Card Games mechanics but want something really NEW, and a true new strategic component which will make you develop your strategies at a higher level, this is your game.

Both players start with 20 life points, and our goal is reducing opponent's life to 0 or below. But to achieve that we need to cast our troops in our invocation line (first line)and cross with them all the battlefield, fighting enemy troops to reach their lines and "hit" them equal to our troops ATK value. As said, 20 points means victory. The fact troops need to move makes them have an unusual basic value: they have ATTACK/LIFE and SPEED (number of zones -cells- they can run in a turn).

But troops, objects and the rest of cards aren't played for free. It's fundamental to produce mana and gold for that. With this purpose, we will build "Place" cards with a cost and production amount. So, each turn we will add mana and gold equal to 1 plus the production indicated by our place cards. A good curve in resources production means a good match development, and that will make us stronger.

Important: place cards are separated from "battle cards" (the rest: troops, objects, spells...), for us to choose what we want to draw each turn.

In a players' phase, they prepare all "turned" (exhausted) cards, add resources, build places, invoke troops or forge objects, then go to tactical phase, in which they can move troops one by one, cast spells, play hero skills, equip objects, fight against enemy troops or hit enemy player.

Sudden spells can be also played in response to opponent's actions, in order to disable their strategy.

A 1vs1 game which would charm any card+strategy game lover.

We summarize it with a single sentence: 
END OF GAME. CHALLENGE BEGINS.
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
Up & Down is a trick-taking game which uses a traditional 52-card deck (1-13 in four suits), one special double-sided Up and Down Card as well as two dice.

Players compete over multiple rounds based on the number of players. Players are dealt a hand of cards. The number of cards depends on the number of players. The trump is determined by rolling the two dice (the trump is always between 2-12). Players win the trick if they play the highest or lowest card depending on the special Up & Down Card.

The player with the most tricks at the end of the game is declared the winner.

A casual and fast card game extracted from the Living Starship universe where each player is a ship captain vying to have their crew complete the most work to earn the admiral's attention.

The game is for 3-7 players and features a hexagonal deck of 98 cards that come in each of the seven Fleet Division colors (suits): Agency, Flight, Engineering, Health, Operations, Science and Tactical. Each suit has 14 cards - 7 officers and 7 enlisted.

Each round an increasing number of mission tokens (points) become available to be claimed by the crew cards. On your turn you play ONE CARD from hand and claim all the mission tokens of that color. If the mission tokens are as yet unclaimed, you take them. If another player has already claimed them and your card outranks the one they are using to hold the tokens, you steal them from that player!

The deck is seeded with 7 ALERT cards and the game ends when the last one is drawn. The player with the most points accumulated from claiming job tokens is the winner and is hailed by the admiralty as the most formidable captain in the Fleet.

boardgame: Great Western Trail: Promo Station Master Tiles
description:
Included in Great Western Trail: Rails to the North expansion


Two promotional Station Master Tiles enclosed to the French "Plato Magazine #95". 
(Note: A Dutch version also exists, provided by 999games. However, to date, the Dutch version has not been made available for purchase, it is only provided to the owners of the Dutch first edition of the base game.)Gameplay instructions:
Shuffle these new Station Master Tiles together with those of the base game. In step 2 of the setup, draw five Station Master Tiles from the seven randomly and allocate them onto the five station master places. The unused tiles are put back into the box.

Effects:
- Immediate action: Gain 12 dollars
- Two permanent certificates
These two tiles do not provide any victory points at the end of the game.

This promo is jointly offered by Gigamic and Plato Magazine.


Instructions de jeu:
Mélangez ces tuiles Gare avec celles du jeu de base. Lors de la phase 2 de la mise en place, piochez cinq tuiles Gare parmi les sept et répartissez-les sur les cinq emplacements dédiés. Les tuiles écartées sont remises dans la boite.Effets:
- Action immediate: gain de 12 dollars
- Deux certificats permanents
Ces deux tuiles ne rapportent pas de points de victoire.

Ce goodie vous est conjointement offert par Gigamic et Plato.

Rec-League Quidditch is an exciting quidditch-themed tile-placement game with some elements of skill-building and risk analysis for two players.  During the game, each player will control three characters on a team.  These characters will compete for control of the quidditch pitch, using the quaffle, bludger, and snitch to score points, as well as continually improving their strength, speed, and skill to win the match.

Each round is played by placing the character tiles in a line on the pitch, in between tiles representing the three quidditch balls.  The location on the line,as well as the particular ball that each character is adjacent to will determine the possible action they can take for the round.  Once all characters have been placed, they are activated by either using adjacent ball tiles to score points, or using them to build up their strength, speed, or skill.

Furthermore, one character on each team will be the designated seeker, and will be hunting down the snitch.  Catching the snitch will take not only skill, but a little luck as well, so players would be wise to make sure they time their attempts to catch the snitch when it would only be to their advantage, and not their opponent's.

Catching the snitch awards a team 50 points, and the game is over after the round ends in which either the snitch is caught, or one team has scored at least 150 points total.  The winner is the team with the most points at the end.

Arrange a unit of humanoid robots, select weapons, and edit your tactics!

CHRONO BLAZE'' (ク ロ ノ ブ レ イ ズ)" is a full-scale card game of the main theme computer!

Divide the 36 decks into two players, take their weapons with limited resources, repair the mechanics, and drive the battlefield!

The aircraft has a "Captain" and a "Wardrobe." The two players decide whether to use "Blind Type" or "Mighty Type" at the beginning of the game.

Place your equipment on your turn and use it.

If you destroy all of your opponent's gas, you win.

The key to winning and losing is a calm judgment and an accurate reading ... ... But as time comes "Luck (Ruck)" and "Unlucky (ア ン ラ ッ ク)" all swallow!

I will release a new sensory robot battle this spring as a model!

Contents

36 cards (for 2 people)

One instruction book


     The four-player competition is an extension rule, and two sets of these are required.



     The game requires two separate 6 pieces of dice (due to packing, it is not possible to bundle the dice that were predicted in the early days, but it will be accepted)

boardgame: Challenge Golden Bell OX Quiz game
description:
Proceed to the voice of the challenger Golden Bell TV host.
challenge mode and single mode support.

The game consists of three rounds in total.
Each round is made up of OX quiz.

Round 1: All Participation Quiz
Round 2: Individual Quiz
Round 3: Speed Quiz

The purpose of the game is to match the golden bell quiz. The 50th quiz.

At the end of Question 49, the person with the highest score challenges the Golden Bell Quiz.

There is no middle drop.
The winner of the Golden Bell quiz will win.

Masterwork
A beautiful game of creating prestigious artworks

In Masterwork, players are artists struggling to win prestige by creating famous artworks and participating in gallery exhibitions. Collect pigments to craft brilliant paints and use those paints to paint the greatest artworks of the High Renaissance. Get critiqued, build your portfolio, and exhibit your work to become the most prestigious artist of the period!


It's your turn
On your turn, if you haven’t started an artwork, you may select one from the gallery and move it to your easel, indicating that artwork is in progress. Now, only you can complete it. Next, use sets of pigments from your hand to produce paint, discarding the requisite cards indicated in the recipe area on the paint card. Move your new paint from the palette to your hand or place it on your easel, dedicating it to that artwork.

Each artwork requires a specific set of paints. When you have gathered them, in hand or on the easel, it’s time to submit your artwork for a critique. Roll the critique die; if the value of equal or higher to the prestige value of the artwork, you have successfully completed it! Move the artwork, along with all paints used to complete it, to the table, clearing your easel for your next artwork. If your roll came up short, take a feedback token indicating you’ve received useful advice from the critique. This token makes future critique attempts easier. Each subsequent artwork makes future critiques easier, representing the artist’s growing popularity.

All 24 artwork cards award prestige points, with addition prestige earned by participating in exhibitions. Exhibits award prestige points when players complete sets of artwork cards; such as: ‘Leonardo Exhibit’ (two artworks from Leonardo da Vinci) and ‘Portrait Exhibit’ (artworks featuring portrait images). Masterwork includes 16 exhibit cards, with a random set of three used each game, offering a great deal of replayability.


Mixing colors
Pairs of primary color (Red, Yellow, Blue) cards may mix to be played as a secondary card of the same type. Secondary colors (Orange, Green, Violet) and different card types (Pigment and Paint cards) do not mix. Mixing colors gives you creative options when producing Pigment and Paint cards. Remember: you must always mix the same type (Paint with Paint, etc.)


Ending and scoring
When a player completes a gold-edged Masterwork Artwork card, finish the current round so each player has an equal number of turns (the player to the right of the starting player gets the final play). Each player adds up the Prestige Points from their completed Artwork cards. The three Exhibit cards are evaluated one after another for each player, comparing their completed Artwork cards with the requirements of cards. For each completed Exhibit card, add the indicated Prestige Point value to the Player’s total. The player with the most Prestige Points wins!

boardgame: Ski Run: The Ultimate Skiing Board Game
description:
Ski Run is an action packed race across the mountain where you try to be the first player back to the resort for Apres Ski! A quick interactive game for all the family.

Pick your route across the piste map board by riding up lifts and racing down runs, whilst trying not to Wipe Out! Will you take the easy blue, the intermediate red or the more direct but difficult black run? Watch out for ice, whiteouts, jumps and the big freeze, whilst hopefully avoiding getting lost or colliding with your competitors. Chase the yeti to find his shortcuts and hire skidoos or heliski to catch up. Call a stop at a ski station to delay your rivals and keep your eye out for opportunities to jump the queues or follow the leader.

The lead changes constantly in this dynamic game of fluctuating fortunes, where you try to use the right cards at the right time to stay one step ahead of your opponents. A quick, brilliant, interactive game for all the family.

Contents: Piste map board, 16 ski run cards, 10 lift cards, 20 event cards, 4 special cards and 5 wipe out cards, 12 sided snowball dice, rules sheet, playing counters.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description: A racing and memory game - get your figure across the field by flipping pairs of tiles. Do not flip a tile with a Muckino, since that will not only end your turn, but also move you backward.

ego: emotions is a physically slightly smaller game in the EGO game series. It is a more compact and travel-friendly version that is ideal for taking on the move.

Features 200 brand new cards with people, things or events.

Each player starts with a set of playing pieces (smiley pieces). You have 3 smiley pieces (one positive, one neutral and one negative smiley). In addition, you get 10 chips that you will need to bet on the answers to the question cards. Depending on the number of participants involved in the game there will be 10 chips per player in one pile (the bank).

The youngest participant starts by dragging a card and then going on. The text on the card is read aloud to the other participants. The card is placed face up on the table so everyone can see it. The player who has drawn the card must now consider the card and evaluate if he or she is:

Positively voted for what's on the card (happy smiley)
Neutrally / Indifferent / Do not know (Neutral smiley)
Negative vote against what appears on the card (negative smiley)

When the player who has drawn the card has decided, he or she places the selected smiley face down on the table with the back up so the others can not see what the person thinks.

The other players place their smiley piece face down which they believe that player has selected. In addition, they must now bet between 1 or 3 chips on their guess. Smiley are then revaled.  Correct answer doubles your bid, wrong loses it. The active player receives as many chips as there are correct guesses from the bank

Turns continue in this manner. The game ends when the bank breaks. Goal is to get most chips.

A modular expansion for Paperback that adds 80 new cards, new abilities, typos, and 16 new covers.


     Lowercase Letter Cards We're introducing a whole new type of victory card. They're letter cards, not wilds, and they provide ¢ when played... and provide Fame depending on how many cards with the same letter you purchase throughout the game.
     New Starting Wilds Wild cards with a twist - they can be used as any letter, but if you use them as a specific vowel, they trigger abilities to enhance your strategy.
     New Common Cards Spice up the words you spell with the harder-to-use vowels U and Y.
     New Fame Cards 12 all new Fame cards (with new values) - each with brand new art from Ryan Goldsberry!
     Asterisk Cards New and easier way to get wilds into your deck, without sacrificing ¢ down the road.
     Typos Be careful not to write too quickly or you might end up hitting the wrong key.  These cards will eat up your victory cards if you don't get rid of them. 
     3-Letter Cards A card with 3 letters on it? They can often be harder to use, but help you spell even longer words.
     Dumpster Dive Recycle old letters from the trash pile, whether played by you or another player.
     Conditional Word Cards Further capturing the Scrabble-like feel that Paperback provides, we have new conditions to be met in order to receive a bonus - for example, be the second letter in a word, words with repeating letters, words that start with a vowel, words with no wild cards, and more!

A drawing game involving headbands.

You have a drawing-board on your forehead, and take a card which tells you what to draw on the board. Points for correct guesses to both drawing and guessing player.

boardgame: LittleStorm in all Kinds of Weather

description:
Description from the publisher:

Rush & Bash: Monster Chase is the second expansion for Rush & Bash, the game about a crazy race set in the same world of games like Vudù, GodZ, and the Super Fantasy series.

This expansion transforms the base game into a fully cooperative experience and the game heaviness switches from casual to strategy-oriented.

Instead of bashing each other out of the road, players must now join forces in order to exorcise a feral spirit that possessed an old, abandoned vehicle. The monster truck, jeep, motorcycle and locomotive are running amok across the dirty roads of Baruffus, destroying ancient temples, frightening the local fauna and devouring harmless nature spirits.
Are you able to momentary put away your grudges in order to chase and chastise the monster before it crosses the finishes lane?

Monster Chase comes with 4 new double-faced track tiles, a co-op version of the 8 existing characters, 4 different monsters and 2 decks of tarot-sized cards that determines the monster's behavior and supernatural tricks.

Next week civilization as we know is obliterated in a catelysmic series of events the survivors simply refer to as "The Meltdown".

You lead one of four factions of these survivors to battle for supremacy in the rising tides above your former home, Los Angeles.

Each player starts with three decks: the Vehicle, Fighter, and Action decks. You can use your faction specific player mat to organize your cards during game play. The faction specific counter is used to mark your score on the battle mat.

Unplayed vehicle cards can be organized above the player mat in the "docks" area either face up or face down (I play face down so my opponent has to remember which vehicles I've played). The unplayed action cards are shuffled and placed face down on the left side of the mat; you will randomly draw from this deck as you play. The fighter deck will usually be in your hand but you can set it in the middle of the mat to keep things organized. Lastly, each player will have 4 action cards in his/her hand, store these on the right side of the mat. The bottom portion of the mat will fill up with won or discarded cards as you play.

The player who initiates combat will choose 1 fighter card and 1 vehicle card from his player mat and place them in play near the battle mat.  Add the power scores from each card and place your faction's counter on the corresponding number on the battle mat. The next player will do the same, placing one fighter card and one vehicle card in play and adding his faction's counter to the mat. After all fighter and vehicle cards are played, in order, each player will place an action card face down. Once all action cards are committed to play they are flipped over to reveal their modifiers.

A children's game.

Each player has a board with six punched-out animal pieces.

On your turn, you spin the arrow and place your corresponding animal piece in your board. If you already have placed this animal, nothing happens.

First player to fill their board wins.

Cypher is a fast-paced math game for both children and adults. 5-15 minute rounds. The object of the game is to win the most cards.

Includes 55 cards high quality per deck. 44 Number Cards. 9 Operation Cards, 2 Times Table Cards, and Instructions.

A great supplement or alternative to flash cards.

Win cards by being the first to yell out the correct answer. Easy to learn and is adaptable to different children's abilities. Perfect for 3-6 players, Ages 8+

Operations in the game include addition, subtraction, and multiplication.

"All Quiet on the Potomac" is our Eastern Theater scenario supplement. This massive PDF covers every major battle fought in the east, from 1861-1865. Each historical scenario has been carefully researched, including color maps and Orders of Battle for the armies.

boardgame: Gibraltar of the West: The 1863 Vicksburg Campaign
description:
From the publisher:

It is early May 1863. Ulysses S Grant and over 40,000 Union troops have crossed the Mississippi and now threaten both Vicksburg and the state capital of Jackson. Will Grant cut free from his river-side supply base? Can Pemberton unite with the relief army assembling near Jackson?

"Gibraltar of the West" is a free campaign supplement, allowing players to re-fight the pivotal three weeks of the Vicksburg campaign. Players maneuver their divisions across a large theater map with the potential to fight a series of interconnected battles. Our simple rules are only 10 pages, but still include logistics, supply, weather, variable map movement, asymmetrical victory conditions, and historical random events. Did we mention it's free?

A color copy of the theater map is included in the rules, spread out across two pages, which you can cut out and tape together into an approximately 10"x 15" map.

The campaign requires the use of ten event cards, representing random events such as bad weather or new reinforcements. The cards are sized as standard poker cards, which could be slid into plastic protective sleeves if you so choose.

Stoner Loonacy is a rapid fire card game in which players race to be the first to empty their hand by matching one of two images on each card in their hand with the images on the face-up piles in front of them. The number of piles varies depending on the number of players, and if players ever reach a moment in which no one can play, everyone draws a card and adds it to their hand at the same time, then the game play resumes.

Recruit team members and gain experience before adventuring through the world.

You are the lowly Tinker. A strange wizard has charged you, the Sailor, the Soldier, and a Spy with defeating an evil that has taken over the world. He gave you 36 magic amulets for your journey. Your company of four will recruit new members and master events before leaving on your adventure ridding each location of evil.

Tinker, Sailor, Soldier, Spy is a solitaire Decktet game inspired by Friday.

boardgame: Runewars Miniatures Game: Daqan Infantry Command – Unit Upgrade Expansion
description:
Description from the publisher:

With polished armor glinting in the sunlight, the armies of the twelve baronies and the Free Cities are a stirring sight. Master wizards from the circles at Greyhaven and Nerekhall bolster their ranks, wielding destructive power and an arsenal of runebound shards to devastate their foes. Battle standards with histories as long as the Baronies of Daqan swirl in the wind, while the roar of lion horns stiffens the backs of wavering soldiers. Every company is led by a champion, one who has faced death time and again, yet has survived and now invites others to draw strength from his own stolid bravery.

The Daqan Infantry Command Unit Upgrade Expansion introduces four brand-new figures to the armies of the Daqan Lords—a Banner Guard, a Wizard, a Champion, and a Cornicen. Alongside these figures, you’ll find eighteen new upgrade cards, many of which include ways to bring these new figures into your armies. For instance, you may choose to support one of your units with the Lance Corporal upgrade card, implanting the Champion figure into the ranks of your unit. As long as the Champion figure is alive, you gain the benefit of the Lance Corporal card, which significantly boosts your tactical flexibility!

boardgame: Runewars Miniatures Game: Reanimate Archers – Unit Expansion
description:
Description from the publisher:

The hiss of Reanimates’ blighted arrows raining down from the skies sends even veteran soldiers scrambling for cover. Wounds inflicted by these arrows rapidly necrotize: flesh peels away, foulness spreads, and bones crumble and crack. Panic spreads as healthy warriors fall, rotted from within. Able to kill at range and afflict their targets with the blight of the Mistlands, Reanimate Archers inspire fear in even the most stalwart soldiers.

Fire poisoned arrows down on your enemies with the eight new Reanimate Archer figures included in the Reanimate Archers Unit Expansion! These eight figures give you the option to field another two-tray unit or increase the size of a larger unit—perhaps fielding a block of archers that will rain death on your foes. Like the Oathsworn Cavalry, these figures are identical to the figures included in the Runewars Core Set, and this expansion gives you an easy way to raise more undead to fight for Waiqar the Undying, while opening dark new paths with five new upgrade cards.

boardgame: Runewars Miniatures Game: Waiqar Infantry Command – Unit Upgrade Expansion
description:
Description from the publisher:

The undead are terrifying foes, known for their tireless strength and single-minded purpose, but no less terrifying is the fact that each of the champions leading Waiqar’s hosts possesses centuries of experience in military tactics and strategy. These military geniuses support their battle plans with the steady, inexorable beat of skeletal drummers and the sinister sigils raised by the gaunt standard-bearers. And perhaps worst of all, necromancers stalk the ranks of Waiqar’s army, raising the fallen to fight again in the name of their lord.

In the same fashion as the Daqan Infantry Command Unit Upgrade Expansion, this expansion pack offers four new figures that you may add to an infantry unit: a Standard-Bearer, a Champion, a Drummer, and a Necromancer. With eighteen new upgrade cards to increase your army’s power, you’ll find plenty of ways to add these figures to your units, perhaps adding a Marching Drummer who lets your unit turn freely, so long as the Drummer remains with the unit.
