boardgame: Mario Kart 7: Super Sound Racing Game
description: In this board game adaptation of the popular video game franchise, players take turns as they attempt to be the first player to cross the finish line, after completing two laps.  To do this, players take control of the steering wheel which acts as dice in the game.  Depending on which of three colors your playing piece is sitting on the game board, that indicates how many times you turn the steering wheel left or right.  The steering wheel will then tell you how many spaces to move your playing piece.  As in the video game, item boxes are scattered around the game board which grants items as players land on them.  Some are instant use items, while others are used later.The steering wheel is also used to determine if items are used successfully against opponents.  When hit by an item, players spin the steering wheel six times and the wheel will tell you if the outcome is successful or not.  Successful hits push the affected player to the edge of the game board marked by blue spaces.
family: Video Game Theme: Super Mario Bros.

description:
The objective is to create a tweet/response for the selected #hashtag.
For example, if the chosen #hashtag by the round leader is '#useless' players write something that is useless, such as 'Grandmas bra' or 'My husband's handyman skills'.
Your response can be anything, as long as it relates to the #hashtag; Tell the truth or make something up; Be creative, witty and funny.
Players get one point for each response they match with the correct author, and two points are awarded by the round leader to the player whose response they like best.
The game ends either when all players have been round leader, or after a predetermined number of rounds. The player with the most points at the end of the game wins.

StrongSword is a slightly quirky but simple-to-learn set of rules designed for playing tabletop fantasy wargames.

Many fantasy wargames place in your command of a huge army divided into tactical units or just a handful of models acting independently of one another. StrongSword places you at the head of a mob of ferocious frothing fighters, be that a large or small mob of them!

The style of play is quite straightforward, but the real challenge is working out how to use the simple rules to command your warriors in battle as effectively as possible. This is because, although all of your models act independently from one another, your whole force is guided by the single tactic or ‘Battle Stance’ you set each turn.

Your Battle Stance is a simple order that’s either shouted by leaders over the tumult of battle, indicated by flag waving, horn blowing, or similar battlefield communication. It tells each of your warriors how to act this turn – maybe they’ll be ordered to be defensive, or aggressive, stand back to catch their breath, or go completely nutty and charge at the nearest living enemy.

The key focus of commanding your warband in this arena of mob warfare is that you must choose the single Battle Stance that strengthens to your current situation – you can’t go giving differing, complex orders to different warriors under your command because you’re too busy fighting and simply don’t have that luxury of time. Instead, shout your commands as loudly as you’re able to and let your warriors do their best!

Combat and morale is kept pretty simple, as are the spells you can cast. This means that the game is driven by your Battle Stance choices, which presents players with the feeling of really commanding a smallish group of warriors on a low-tech battlefield.

The “Hattrick”  game presents one of the episodes from the Norticles universe, which took place during  prince Linnamen’s detective journeys. He was one of the private investigators secretly hired by the town authorities, and it was his conclusions that let the inhabitants of Loch Scarland inform the investigators from the capital city, where to look for the fugitive murderer. Karn did not mention this in his report, so as not to expose the inhabitants to repressions due to the illegal involvement of prince Linnamen. 
During the game of “Hattrick”, the players become the characters of this story. They may once again solve the duel between the two antagonists  (Karn and Milton), changing not only its progress, but also the final outcome. This variant is available also in  the “print and play” version, which can be found on www.norticles.com . 
In the box version, you can get to know other characters involved in the story, which will make it possible for you to play alternative scenarios. Among the characters, you will see prince Linnamen, the monks Rafael Gabriel and Augustine, and Mid Guns, the villain. All the characters come from the gamebook titled „The Wasteland”, where some of the events also take place in a wooden hut. The Hattrick game is therefore a perfect addition to prince Linnamen’s adventures in the Kaer-Nar-Nort wasteland.

http://norticles.com/wp-content/uploads/2017/08/Wasteland_demo.pdf

A challenge for your linguistic sense.

Form words in order to obtain a higher score. On your turn, arrange letters on the game board and try to cover more colored "bonus" squares. Words can be formed in two directions: horizontally and vertically. The game ends when all the letter chips are used or no other words can be formed. The player with the highest score wins.

In Jandwich (ジャンドイッチ), players each manage a sandwich shop and need to create sandwiches to satisfy guests. The card deck consists of different types of breads and fillings, and to create sandwiches, a player needs to pair a filling with matches types of bread. Players acquire cards by drawing from the deck and buying cards from the discard pile.

Publisher's summary
The players, as sandwich shop managers, make sandwiches to get and steal customers from other players. Using 12 different types of breads and fillings, form 2 melds of 3 cards from your hand of 6 cards.

There are 3 ways to meld each 3 cards, namely a "trio" (set of the same cards), "combo" (sequence with the same suit), or "sand" (2 same bread cards and 1 filling card). The game starts with each player holding 25 pt of Customer chips, and the player with the most Customer chips at the end of the game wins.

プレイヤーはそれぞれサンドイッチショップの店長となり、サンドイッチを作ってお客さんを取りあうゲームです。
パンと具材のサンドイッチカード12種類を使って、
6枚の手札を3枚組2セットの形にすると完成となります。
3枚組は同じカードを3枚である「トリオ」、同系統の3連番である「コンボ」、全て同数字のパン2枚と具材1枚の「サンド」の3種類の作り方があります。
25Pずつお客さんチップを持ってスタートし、最後に一番多くのお客さんチップを持っている人の勝ちです。

A naughty party game for naughty people! An adults only version of the classic party game.

Take turns acting out things like Horny Devil, Mr. Woodcock, and The Little Man in the Boat while teammates try to guess as many answers as possible within a minute. The winning team is the team with the most correct answers after each player has performed three times.

Each player puts a band on a cup, wrist, or bottle. Players hang out at a party as they normally would while also carrying out the dares printed on their Buzzed Bands. Keep following through with your dare until someone switches bands with you. Play continues until a player has received the Switch Bands for a third time.

Everyone wins when they play this game.  You get to drink while having a great time.  This game also acts as a wonderful ice breaker game to get to know new people at a party.
family: Admin: Outside the Scope of BGG

description:
Description from the publisher:

The game of wacky question and answer match-ups! Compete with other players to come up with the wackiest matching answers possible!

Players take turns asking questions or making fill-in-the-blank statements and other players submit hilarious answers. The one with the funniest answer wins! Hilarious entertainment to liven up any party!

boardgame: DC Universe Miniature Game: Parademon Invasion Force
description: This expansion introduces Parademons to the DC Universe Miniature Game. This white metal kit comes ready to assemble and paint.

boardgame: Carps & Angsty Manatee: Texas Edition (fan expansion for Cards Against Humanity)
description:
Texas: From the outside looking in, you can't understand it.

From the inside looking out, you can't explain it.

We dove deep into our Texas roots to create this raunchy, Texas-themed expansion pack. If you love Cards Against Humanity, you will love this unofficial expansion.

We took special care to ensure that all cards played well with other expansions and that the cards are the same professional size, finish, and quality as other decks. Carps & Angsty Manatee: Texas Edition includes 150 brand new, original cards (125 absurdly Texan white cards & 25 ridiculously Texan black cards).

100% compatible with CAH cards and other third party expansions.

boardgame: Batman Miniature Game: Falcone Crime Family
description: This expansion adds the Falcone Crime Family to the Batman Miniature Game. This white metal kit comes ready to assemble and paint.
This expansion adds Alberto Falcone to the Batman Miniature Game. This white metal kit comes ready to assemble and paint.

Description from the publisher:

Humanity Hates Trump: Expansion One is an unofficial expansion pack to other Party Games including Cards Against Humanity and the Humanity Hates Trump Base Set.

This set includes 110 cards (80 White Cards, 30 Black Cards) and 1 Ultimate Trump Card.

boardgame: Humanity Hates Hillary, Too: Expansion Two
description:
Humanity Hates Hillary, Too: Expansion Two is an unofficial expansion pack to other Party Games including Cards Against Humanity.

Includes 110 cards (80 White Cards, 30 Black Cards).

Description from the publisher:

Whether you celebrate Christmas, Hannukah or Chrismahanukwanzakah, most can agree that “the most wonderful time of the year,” is often the time we’re least thankful for. Between fighting families, re-gifted fruitcake and Black Friday frenzies, most of us end up on the naughty list once the holiday antics are over… Don’t get your tinsel in a tangle anymore! Introducing the gift that keeps on giving, Humanity Hates The Holidays: The Adult Party Game Santa Won’t Bring You.

Humanity Hates The Holidays is the perfect distraction that will take you away from holiday hell this year. Compatible with Humanity Hates Trump, Humanity Hates Hillary TOO!, Cards Against Humanity and other similar party games, this hilarious expansion pack will have the family in stitches. Humanity Hates The Holidays pokes fun at cliché Christmas music, Jewish Christmas traditions and Thanksgivings that even the Pilgrims would cringe over. Play it around the Thanksgiving table or at your holiday parties, heck, you may even bond with your crazy aunt over this!

Similarly to Cards Against Humanity, each player is dealt 10 white cards, each including a word or phrase. Each round, players take turns serving as the judge, drawing one black card containing a question or fill in the blank sentence. Players answer the question or fill in the blank sentence by choosing their favorite white card to fill it in. Each player takes turns judging, choosing their favorite option and the funniest answer wins the round.

Humanity Hates The Holidays includes one Ultimate Yule Log Card. The lucky player who has the card in their hand automatically wins the round.
An extra item card for Saltlands featuring an oil can.

Players place simultaneously triangle-tiles on the table so that the two adjacent fruit-symbols match. If circle is formed on the table player drops his token in the middle of the circle. First player who first drops all 3 of his tokens is the winner.

The game is simple -  be the first player to get from start to "finished". The first player to reach the “finished” space wins. Once someone wins, move everyone back to start, reshuffle cards and start playing your ridiculous hearts out again—your liver and a fuzzy selection of potentially bad decisions will be there to thank you later.

Each player rolls the die, moves their game piece and follows what the space on the board tells them to do.  The space can say anything from "take a drink", "give away a drink" or draw an individual or social card – and this is where things get interesting. Cards may instruct an individual to complete an activity, answer a question, or interact with other players in a specific way.  The goal being to get to know the players around you OR simply be entertained by your friends in new and hilarious ways.

Country versus Country.
Traditional game with different countries all around the world with information: Land area, population etc.
Game is played like Top Trump. Active player looks at his top card , chooses category (f.e. population) and all players compare the population from their top cards. Who has the biggest number gets all the cards. The last player in game is the winner.

Description from the rulebook:

It is the purpose of each player to buy at auction articles put up for sale by the auctioneer. Each article has a hidden value revealed after its purchase by the Highest Bidder. If the buyer purchases the article at less than the revealed value, he scores a profit. If he pays more than it's worth, he loses the difference. The winner of the game is the player with the highest amount of money remaining after all articles are auctioned off.

Before play begins each player secretly decides what each article being auctioned is worth and writes the dollar amount on a slip of paper and then inserts the paper into the article envelope. Each player begins with $1,179 and uses this money to bid on articles placed at auction.

The "auctioneer" title is passed from player to player and the number of items auctioned per auctioneer turn is determined by the number of players (articles should be divided up so each player has an equal number to auction off. The game instructions use groups of five). After each item is auctioned, the article envelope is opened and the slips are totaled. The auctioneer acts a the bank and either collects money from the bidder if the amount bid is more than the revealed value or pays the bidder if the revealed value is more than the amount bid.

boardgame: The Game of Abbie an' Slats
description: Simple children's race game based on comic strip characters. First player to reach the end by the exact number wins. The strip was written by Al Capp and drawn by Raeburn Van Buren and ran from 1937-1971.

A single punch board containing expansions for 4 different games - Elfenland, Zooloretto, Belfort, and Stone Age.

Expansions are:
Elfenland: Back to the Roads
Zooloretto: Tapir
Belfort: Guild Promo Pack #1
Stone Age: Casino

It was released for the Stadt Land Spielt event that took place on September 2013 throughout Germany.

boardgame: North Vs. South: A Civil War Card Game
description:
Description from the designer:

A card based strategy card game where players are trying to secure the most cities as possible before the fight between the North and South is over.

Players will obtain cities as they go and try to hold as many as possible by placing defense cards to protect and keep them.

Other players will use attack cards to attack the cities and acquire them, while drawing from the deck.  Drawing from the deck will allow players to build their army and obtain free cities to keep and defend.

The game ends when Abe Lincoln, the Emancipation Proclamation, Gettysburg address and Appomattox courthouse are drawn.  The players with the most cities at the end of the game wins.

User Summary:
運命力 (Unmeiryoku) is a dice-rolling game by the doujin circle カレーラーメン (in English: Curry Ramen'). It is based in the Project Touhou universe, with character cards representing various characters from the Project Touhou universe, drawn by many different artists (see below). 

The game box contains 8 dice, 54 characters cards (6 x 9 characters) and 6 rules summary cards.

The players all start with the Daiyousei character card. They take turns at rolling dice.

More specifically, a player's turn consists of:
1. You throw initially 3 dice
2. You may use as many character cards you have in your possession to tweak that initial roll. The effects could be to change the dice value or add more dice for instance.
3. After modification, if you achieve a specific condition you can get a new character card which you will be able to use at your next run. Now your turn ends.

The game continues until a player manages to get the Flandre character card.

Note: The rulebook is in Japanese but no Japanese is needed for playing the game as the cards only have symbols (dice sides and numbers).

Publisher's summary:

Twist your　'Fate' !

You think it's over? But it's not !
Add dice, remove dice, use spells to alter your luck and be the first to rejoin with Flandre !

運命を捻じ曲げろ！

賽は投げられた？関係ないね！
ダイス目を増やし、減らし、逆転させフランドールの元まで辿りつけ！

カレーラーメンのカードゲーム第二弾『運命力』！
今度はひたすらダイスを振りまくるゲームです。
